\chapter{封面}
\manualpdfbookmark{封面}{cover}

新东方 NEW ORIENTAL

韩国排名 No.1 的英语词汇书，畅销20余年！

英语词根词典 Vocabulary Workshop 2 （韩）金正基 编著

TOEFL、GRE、TOEIC、SAT、IELTS词汇，一本搞定！ 与《英语词缀词典》配合使用，效果加倍！

群言出版社 Qunyan Press

新东方大愚 英语学习丛书 www.dogwood.com.cn

本书以英语词源学理论为基础，以作者从英国、美国的大型书店和图书馆里多年搜集的信息为主要参考资料编写而成，体系完整，方法科学，内容全面，语言地道，版式美观，体例简约，兼具工具书的权威性与英语词汇书的实用性。

英语词根大容量：全面收录源自拉丁语、希腊语和盎格鲁-撒克逊语的2,000个英语词根，补充同义、反义、变形词根。查英语词根，选这一本就对了。

考试词汇全覆盖：本书收录30,000个词汇，全面覆盖20余年来TOEFL、GRE、TOEIC、SAT、IELTS考试真题中出现的词汇，同时也适用于国内的四六级、考研等英语考试。利用词源分析记忆法，举一反三记单词，有效扩充词汇量。

词频统计重科学：根据美国学校教科书以及美国教育学家兼心理学家桑戴克（Edward L. Thorndike）的词频统计著作《教师20,000词词汇书》（Teacher's Word Book of 20,000 Words），科学划分词汇等级、标注使用频率，有助于提高学习效率，避免浪费时间。

丰富实例好素材：本书包含10,000多个例句及词语搭配实例，内容时新、实用，主题包罗万象，可以为英语口语、写作积累实用素材。

词典、词汇书集一身：书中出现的所有词根和词汇均收录索引，查阅方便。查词典、背单词同步实现。背完单词可将本书作为工具书查阅使用，一举两得。

词根、词缀同步学：本书和《英语词缀词典》中1,000个主词条彼此重复，按照《英语词缀词典》里各Lecture中的词根顺序编写，再以此词根为基础拓展同根词。两本书可以同时学习使用。反复学习，加深记忆。

\chapter{前言}
\manualpdfbookmark{前言}{preface}

Preface 前言

20多年前，笔者倾注了极大的心血，编写并出版了《英语词缀词典》（Vocabulary Workshop 1）。如今，此书的销售量已突破百万，也已成为了大学生必备的词汇宝典和作者的自豪，乃至作者生命的一部分。此书出版后，广大读者纷纷提出要求，希望能有一本通过词根来学习词汇的书籍。为此，笔者编写了《英语词根词典》（Vocabulary Workshop 2）。

笔者于留学期间在英国伦敦和牛津、美国纽约和华盛顿的大型书店以及纽约州立大学图书馆搜集了大量的资料。根据这些资料以及词源学理论，本书归纳、讲解了近2,000个词根，涵盖常用基本词根及其同义、反义词根。这种利用词根来扩充词汇的学习方法效果极佳。例如，通过本书 Lecture 1 的词根 DUC 和 DUCT，可以同时掌握4个主词及5个派生词，31个补充词汇及10个派生词，共50个单词。

本书的一个重要特色是，根据美国小学、初中、高中、大学的教科书以及美国教育学家兼心理学家桑戴克博士（Edward L. Thorndike）的词频统计著作《教师的20,000词词汇书》（Teacher's Word Book of 20,000 Words），整理了单词的等级和使用频率，分别标示为：***→到美国4年级为止 / 5,000个单词；***→美国5年级～8年级 / 10,000个单词；**→美国9年级～11年级 / 20,000个单词；*→美国12年级 / 30,000个单词；*→美国大学以上 / 30,000个单词以上。科学地划分单词的等级并标注使用频率有助于提高学习效率，避免浪费时间。

本书的另一个重要特色是，列举了丰富的例句和词语搭配实例，并对基础词汇作了详细的阐释。国际化时代的到来要求我们具备出色的英语能力。本书收录的特别是以形容词为主的10,000多个例句，有助于提高英语会话和写作能力。会话和写作过程中最难把握的是形容词，这是因为人们往往分别掌握形容词及其修饰的名词。而事实上高效的方法应该是将形容词和名词结合起来学习。本书提供的大量搭配实例可以为英语会话和写作积累实用素材。基础词汇大体是中学时学过的非常简单的词汇，许多人觉

得英语很难，主要就是因为没有掌握好这些基础词汇。而英语里的大部分俗语和口语表达都是由这些基础词汇（基础动词110个，基础名词200个，基础介词50个）所构成，所以扎实掌握这些基础词汇是攻克英语的前提和捷径。

本书科学划分词汇等级，标注使用频率，读者可据此安排学习计划。全书200个LECTURE中出现的1,000个主词条应当全部掌握。其他词汇可按照星号标注的使用频率依次攻克：第一阶段→读者在以往考试中遇到的词汇；第二阶段→标有***和***的初级词汇；第三阶段→标有**的中级词汇；第四阶段→标有*的高级词汇；第五阶段→标有*的最高级词汇。其中，标有*的词汇使用频率很低，考试中基本不会出现；而标有***和**的词汇在考试中出现的几率很高，需重点掌握。

通过词根学习词汇的《英语词根词典》和通过前缀、后缀学习词汇的《英语词缀词典》里的1,000个主词条彼此重复。本书按照《英语词缀词典》里各 Lecture 中词条的词根顺序编写。建议两本书同时学习使用，反复学习，效果加倍！

最后，将本书献给这个时代的每一位生活实践者。

编者

\chapter{使用说明}
\manualpdfbookmark{使用说明}{usage}

本整理版以 `ocr.md` 为底本，在尽量保留原书词根体系、释义和例句材料的前提下，将正文重排为“词根章节 + 单词词条”的结构，方便连续阅读、人工校对与后续检索。

正文不再沿用原书的 Lecture 标题作为主体结构，而是直接以词根作为章节标题；附录、词根索引和单词索引保留在文末。

\begin{itemize}
  \item 一级结构按词根展开；同章并列词根用逗号分隔，方括号标示常见变形。
  \item 每个单词均使用 `word` 环境排版：左栏显示单词、星级和音标，右栏依次给出词根拆解、释义、同根词、搭配与例句。
  \item 星级沿用原书频率标记：`***`、`**`、`*` 依次表示由常见到较生僻的词汇层级。
  \item 附录保留补充词根材料；正文后的两组索引用于按词根和按单词反查页码。
\end{itemize}

常用顺序为 \verb|\wordbreakdown|、\verb|\wordsense|、\verb|\wordfamily|、\verb|\wordphrase|、\verb|\wordexample|。其中 \verb|\wordfamily| 用于同根词或派生词，\verb|\wordphrase| 只用于短语、固定搭配或词组。

例如：`DUC, DUCT[DU]` 一章以 `duct`/`duc` 为核心展开，并补充同义、近义或变形词根所关联的同根词，便于横向比较和集中记忆。

\chapter{必备前缀、后缀整理}
\manualpdfbookmark{必备前缀、后缀整理}{prefix-suffix}

\section*{前缀}

\begingroup
\small
\begin{description}[style=nextline,leftmargin=0pt,labelsep=0pt,font=\normalfont\bfseries]

\item[前缀 AB]
含义：
\begin{enumerate}[leftmargin=2.5em,nosep,topsep=2pt]
  \item away, from, off（分离，脱离；消失，减小，去除）
  \item intensive（强调）
\end{enumerate}
变形：
\begin{itemize}[leftmargin=2.5em,nosep,topsep=2pt]
  \item ABS：在辅音字母 c, t 之前
  \item A：在辅音字母 m, v 之前
\end{itemize}

\item[前缀 AD]
含义：
\begin{enumerate}[leftmargin=2.5em,nosep,topsep=2pt]
  \item to（方向；行为、动作、作用的对象；附加，添加；接近）
  \item intensive（强调）
\end{enumerate}
变形：
\begin{itemize}[leftmargin=2.5em,nosep,topsep=2pt]
  \item AC：在发“k”音的辅音字母 c, q 之前
  \item AS：在发“s”音的辅音字母 c, s 之前
  \item AF, AG, AL, AN, AP, AR, AT：在辅音字母 f, g, l, n, p, r, t 之前
  \item A：在辅音字母 b, m, p, v 及双辅音字母 sc, sp, st 之前
\end{itemize}

\item[前缀 AN]
含义：
\begin{enumerate}[leftmargin=2.5em,nosep,topsep=2pt]
  \item not（不是…的，不…的，不是…）
  \item without（没有…的，没有…地，不具备…的）
\end{enumerate}
变形：
\begin{itemize}[leftmargin=2.5em,nosep,topsep=2pt]
  \item A：在辅音字母之前
\end{itemize}

\item[前缀 COM]
含义：
\begin{enumerate}[leftmargin=2.5em,nosep,topsep=2pt]
  \item with（联合，伴随；持有，拥有；委托，管理；工具，手段；和……一致，适合于……；分离）
  \item together（一起，伴随，一块儿；总共；相互……；和……同时；完全地，彻底地）
  \item intensive（强调）
\end{enumerate}
变形：
\begin{itemize}[leftmargin=2.5em,nosep,topsep=2pt]
  \item COL：在辅音字母 l 之前
  \item CON：在辅音字母 c, f, g 之前
  \item COR：在辅音字母 r 之前
  \item CO：在元音字母 a, e, i, o, u 和送气音“h”以及辅音字母 g, v 之前
  \item COUN：添加元音字母 u
\end{itemize}

\item[前缀 DE]
含义：
\begin{enumerate}[leftmargin=2.5em,nosep,topsep=2pt]
  \item down（向下；降低地）
  \item from（脱离，使分离；使没有，…掉了的）
  \item intensive（强调）
\end{enumerate}
变形：无常见变形。

\item[前缀 DIS]
含义：
\begin{enumerate}[leftmargin=2.5em,nosep,topsep=2pt]
  \item apart（分离地，使分离；分别；远远地，脱离）
  \item not, reverse（否定，轻微否定）
  \item intensive（强调）
\end{enumerate}
变形：
\begin{itemize}[leftmargin=2.5em,nosep,topsep=2pt]
  \item DI：在辅音字母 g, l, m, r, v 之前
  \item DIF：在辅音字母 f 之前
\end{itemize}

\item[前缀 EN]
含义：
\begin{enumerate}[leftmargin=2.5em,nosep,topsep=2pt]
  \item in（在…里，在…中）
  \item make（使变成…；使成为…）
  \item give（授予）
  \item not（否定）
  \item intensive（强调）
\end{enumerate}
变形：
\begin{itemize}[leftmargin=2.5em,nosep,topsep=2pt]
  \item EM：在辅音字母 b, m, p 之前
\end{itemize}

\item[前缀 EX]
含义：
\begin{enumerate}[leftmargin=2.5em,nosep,topsep=2pt]
  \item out（在外边；向上；挑选；去除，使分离；彻底地，完全地）
  \item intensive（强调）
\end{enumerate}
变形：
\begin{itemize}[leftmargin=2.5em,nosep,topsep=2pt]
  \item E：在辅音字母 b, d, g, j, l, m, n, r, v 之前
  \item EF：在辅音字母 f 之前
  \item EC：在辅音字母 c, l, s, t, z 之前
  \item ES：在辅音字母 c 之前
  \item EX：`ex` 之后的辅音字母 s 脱落
\end{itemize}

\item[前缀 IN]
含义：
\begin{enumerate}[leftmargin=2.5em,nosep,topsep=2pt]
  \item in（在里边，以…的状态）
  \item on（在…上面，对…；对于；依靠…；针对…；进行中，继续；披在身上）
  \item not（否定）
  \item against（反对）
  \item to（向…，因…）
  \item intensive（强调）
\end{enumerate}
变形：
\begin{itemize}[leftmargin=2.5em,nosep,topsep=2pt]
  \item IL：在辅音字母 l 之前
  \item IM：在辅音字母 b, m, p 之前
  \item IR：在辅音字母 r 之前
  \item I：在辅音字母 g 之前
\end{itemize}

\item[前缀 INTER]
含义：
\begin{enumerate}[leftmargin=2.5em,nosep,topsep=2pt]
  \item between（在…之间，在…的中间；相互的）
\end{enumerate}
变形：
\begin{itemize}[leftmargin=2.5em,nosep,topsep=2pt]
  \item INTEL：在辅音字母 l 之前
  \item ENTER / ENTR：用于法语系统
\end{itemize}

\item[前缀 MAL]
含义：
\begin{enumerate}[leftmargin=2.5em,nosep,topsep=2pt]
  \item ill, bad（坏的）
\end{enumerate}
变形：
\begin{itemize}[leftmargin=2.5em,nosep,topsep=2pt]
  \item MAL：在辅音字母 d, f, v 之前
  \item MALI：在辅音字母 g, s 之前
\end{itemize}

\item[前缀 OB]
含义：
\begin{enumerate}[leftmargin=2.5em,nosep,topsep=2pt]
  \item against（逆着…，和…敌对，和…相反）
  \item to（向…；给…）
  \item in the way（妨碍地）
  \item over（越过；盖住）
  \item away（远远地，消失）
  \item intensive（强调）
\end{enumerate}
变形：
\begin{itemize}[leftmargin=2.5em,nosep,topsep=2pt]
  \item OC：在辅音字母 c 之前
  \item OF：在辅音字母 f 之前
  \item OP：在辅音字母 p 之前
  \item OS：在辅音字母 t 之前
  \item O：在辅音字母 m 之前
\end{itemize}

\item[前缀 PARA]
含义：
\begin{enumerate}[leftmargin=2.5em,nosep,topsep=2pt]
  \item beside（在…旁边，和…并肩）
  \item intensive（强调）
\end{enumerate}
变形：
\begin{itemize}[leftmargin=2.5em,nosep,topsep=2pt]
  \item PAR：在元音字母之前
\end{itemize}

\item[前缀 PER]
含义：
\begin{enumerate}[leftmargin=2.5em,nosep,topsep=2pt]
  \item through（贯通，通过）
  \item throughout（到处；从头到尾，一直，始终）
  \item falsely（虚假地）
  \item by（依据…）
  \item intensive（强调）
\end{enumerate}
变形：
\begin{itemize}[leftmargin=2.5em,nosep,topsep=2pt]
  \item PEL, POL：在辅音字母 l 之前
  \item PAR：在辅音字母 d, s 和元音字母 a 之前
  \item PIL：在辅音字母 g 之前
  \item POR：在辅音字母 t 之前
\end{itemize}

\item[前缀 PRE]
含义：
\begin{enumerate}[leftmargin=2.5em,nosep,topsep=2pt]
  \item before（先前，以前；前面；提前；向前）
\end{enumerate}
变形：无常见变形。

\item[前缀 PRO]
含义：
\begin{enumerate}[leftmargin=2.5em,nosep,topsep=2pt]
  \item forward, forth（向前）
  \item before（先前，提前，首先）
  \item for（赞同…，支持…）
  \item according to（跟随…）
  \item in place of（代替…）
\end{enumerate}
变形：
\begin{itemize}[leftmargin=2.5em,nosep,topsep=2pt]
  \item PUR：在辅音字母 c, p, s, v 之前
  \item POR：在辅音字母 t 之前
  \item PROD：在元音字母之前
  \item PR：在元音字母之前 o 脱落
\end{itemize}

\item[前缀 RE]
含义：
\begin{enumerate}[leftmargin=2.5em,nosep,topsep=2pt]
  \item back（向后；在后边；变成…；还给，作为答礼；藏在后边；抑制）
  \item again（重新）
  \item against（逆着）
  \item away（远远地，消失）
  \item mutually（相互）
  \item intensive（强调）
\end{enumerate}
变形：
\begin{itemize}[leftmargin=2.5em,nosep,topsep=2pt]
  \item RED：在元音字母之前
  \item REN：在辅音字母 d 之前
  \item R：在元音字母之前 e 脱落
\end{itemize}

\item[前缀 SUB]
含义：
\begin{enumerate}[leftmargin=2.5em,nosep,topsep=2pt]
  \item under（在…底下，在…下边）
  \item secondary（下位，副）
  \item slightly（一点，稍许）
  \item in place of（代替…）
  \item precisely（精确地）
\end{enumerate}
变形：
\begin{itemize}[leftmargin=2.5em,nosep,topsep=2pt]
  \item SUC：在辅音字母 c 之前
  \item SUF：在辅音字母 f 之前
  \item SUG：在辅音字母 g 之前
  \item SUM：在辅音字母 m 之前
  \item SUP：在辅音字母 p 之前
  \item SUR：在辅音字母 r 之前
  \item SUS：在辅音字母 c, p, t 之前
\end{itemize}

\item[前缀 SUPER]
含义：
\begin{enumerate}[leftmargin=2.5em,nosep,topsep=2pt]
  \item over（越过…，比…多，变得更加…；剩下，多余地，过多地；从上到下；超越；关于；全部；重复，再一次；结束）
  \item above（在上边）
  \item beyond（越过，超越）
\end{enumerate}
变形：
\begin{itemize}[leftmargin=2.5em,nosep,topsep=2pt]
  \item SUR
  \item SUPR
  \item SOPR
\end{itemize}

\item[前缀 TRANS]
含义：
\begin{enumerate}[leftmargin=2.5em,nosep,topsep=2pt]
  \item across（横穿，到那边；十字交叉；分叉；使理解）
  \item through（通过）
  \item over（越过）
  \item beyond（超越）
  \item change（变化）
  \item intensive（强调）
\end{enumerate}
变形：
\begin{itemize}[leftmargin=2.5em,nosep,topsep=2pt]
  \item TRAN：在辅音字母 q, s 之前
  \item TRA：在辅音字母 d, j, v 和元音字母 i 之前
  \item TRES, TREAS
\end{itemize}

\end{description}
\endgroup

\section*{后缀}

\begingroup
\small
\begin{description}[style=nextline,leftmargin=0pt,labelsep=0pt,font=\normalfont\bfseries]

\item[后缀 AGE]
含义：
\begin{enumerate}[leftmargin=2.5em,nosep,topsep=2pt]
  \item state, that which（状态，物）
  \item act（行为，动作）
  \item collective（集合）
  \item cost（费用）
  \item place, position（地点；地位，身份）
\end{enumerate}
变形：无常见变形。

\item[后缀 ITY]
含义：
\begin{enumerate}[leftmargin=2.5em,nosep,topsep=2pt]
  \item state, that which（状态，物）
  \item act（行为，动作）
  \item quality（性质）
\end{enumerate}
变形：
\begin{itemize}[leftmargin=2.5em,nosep,topsep=2pt]
  \item TY
  \item Y
\end{itemize}

\item[后缀 TION]
含义：
\begin{enumerate}[leftmargin=2.5em,nosep,topsep=2pt]
  \item state, that which（状态，物）
  \item act（行为，动作）
  \item result（结果）
\end{enumerate}
变形：
\begin{itemize}[leftmargin=2.5em,nosep,topsep=2pt]
  \item SION
  \item ION
\end{itemize}

\item[后缀 ABLE]
含义：
\begin{enumerate}[leftmargin=2.5em,nosep,topsep=2pt]
  \item capable of（能做…的，易做…的）
\end{enumerate}
变形：
\begin{itemize}[leftmargin=2.5em,nosep,topsep=2pt]
  \item IBLE
  \item ILE
\end{itemize}

\item[后缀 ANT, ENT]
含义：
\begin{enumerate}[leftmargin=2.5em,nosep,topsep=2pt]
  \item having the quality of（具有…性质的）
\end{enumerate}
变形：无常见变形。

\item[后缀 ARY, ORY]
含义：
\begin{enumerate}[leftmargin=2.5em,nosep,topsep=2pt]
  \item having the quality of（具有…性质的）
  \item related（有关系的）
\end{enumerate}
变形：无常见变形。

\item[后缀 IC, ICAL]
含义：
\begin{enumerate}[leftmargin=2.5em,nosep,topsep=2pt]
  \item nature of（具有…性质的）
\end{enumerate}
变形：无常见变形。

\item[后缀 OUS]
含义：
\begin{enumerate}[leftmargin=2.5em,nosep,topsep=2pt]
  \item full of, many（多的）
  \item having the quality of（具有…性质的）
  \item like（如同…）
\end{enumerate}
变形：无常见变形。

\item[后缀 ATE]
含义：
\begin{enumerate}[leftmargin=2.5em,nosep,topsep=2pt]
  \item make（使…）
  \item having the quality of（具有…性质的）
  \item person who, that which（人，…东西）
\end{enumerate}
变形：无常见变形。

\item[后缀 FY]
含义：
\begin{enumerate}[leftmargin=2.5em,nosep,topsep=2pt]
  \item make（使…）
\end{enumerate}
变形：无常见变形。

\end{description}
\endgroup

\chapter{目录}
\manualpdfbookmark{目录}{manual-contents}

本书采用按词根重组的正文结构，建议结合 PDF 书签或文末索引检索具体词条。章节安排如下：

\begin{itemize}
  \item 前置部分：前言、使用说明、必备前缀与后缀整理。
  \item 第一部分：利用前缀扩充词汇之词根篇，从 `DUC, DUCT[DU]` 起，至 `VEST` 止。
  \item 第二部分：利用后缀扩充词汇之词根篇，从 `HER` 起，至 `JUST` 止。
  \item 附录：补充词根。
  \item 索引：`I. 词根索引` 与 `II. 单词索引`。
\end{itemize}

如需查找具体单词，优先使用文末“单词索引”；如需回看同根词群，优先使用“词根索引”或 PDF 书签。

\mainmatter

\part{第一部分 利用前缀扩充词汇之词根篇}
\manualpdfbookmark{第一部分 利用前缀扩充词汇之词根篇}{part-one}

\chapter{DUC, DUCT[DU]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的词根 duc，duct 意为 to lead。du 是它们的变形。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 men[mean] 和来自希腊语的 agog。此外，还有意为 to drive 的来自拉丁语的词根 ag / ig / act，flig，pel / puls [peal] 等。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{abduct}{æb'dʌkt}
  \wordbreakdown{拉向远处 ab (= away) + duct (= lead)}
  \wordfamily{abduction 诱拐；abductor 诱拐者；induce 诱导}
  \wordsense{vt. 诱拐；绑架}
  \wordphrase{abduct a child}
  \wordexample{The gang tried to abduct a child outside the school.（那伙人试图在学校外绑架一名孩子。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 【解剖】使（肢体）外展}
  \wordphrase{abduct the arm}
  \wordexample{The muscle acts to abduct the arm away from the body.（这块肌肉的作用是使手臂外展远离躯体。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{conducive}{kən'dju:siv}
  \wordbreakdown{一起引向某处 con<com (= together) + duc (= lead)}
  \wordfamily{conduce 有助于；conduct 引导；conductive 传导的}
  \wordsense{a. 有助于……的；有利于……的}
  \wordphrase{be conducive to learning}
  \wordexample{The excessive cold in the classroom is not conducive to learning.（教室太冷会不利于学习。）}
  \wordsense{a. 促成……的；导致……的}
  \wordphrase{conducive to good health}
  \wordexample{Regular exercise is conducive to good health.（经常锻炼有助于身体健康。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{induce}{in'dju:s}
  \wordbreakdown{向里引导 in (= in, into) + duc (= lead)}
  \wordfamily{inducement 劝诱；induction 诱导；inductive 归纳的}
  \wordsense{vt. 引起；诱发}
  \wordphrase{induce sleep}
  \wordexample{The medicine can induce sleep within minutes.（这种药几分钟内就能催人入睡。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 劝诱；诱使（某人做某事）}
  \wordphrase{induce sb to invest}
  \wordexample{Nothing could induce her to change her mind.（什么都不能诱使她改变主意。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 【逻】归纳}
  \wordphrase{induce a general rule}
  \wordexample{Scientists induced a general rule from repeated experiments.（科学家从反复实验中归纳出一条普遍规律。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 【医】引产}
  \wordphrase{induce labor}
  \wordexample{Doctors sometimes induce labor when the pregnancy is overdue.（妊娠过期时，医生有时会进行引产。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{traduce}{trə'dju:s}
  \wordbreakdown{拉过头、拉偏 tra<trans (= over, across) + duc (= lead)}
  \wordfamily{traducer 诽谤者；adduce 引证；deduce 推论}
  \wordsense{vt. 中伤；诽谤}
  \wordphrase{traduce a rival}
  \wordexample{Stars are used to seeing their names traduced in the tabloids.（看见小报中伤自己的名誉，明星们已经习以为常了。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 歪曲；曲解}
  \wordphrase{traduce the truth}
  \wordexample{The article traduced the facts to suit a political agenda.（这篇文章歪曲事实以迎合某种政治目的。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{aqueduct}{'ækwidʌkt}
  \wordbreakdown{引水之道 aqu (= water) + duct (= lead)}
  \wordfamily{viaduct 高架桥；conductor 导体；conduct 传导}
  \wordsense{n. 沟渠；导水管；输水道}
  \wordphrase{a Roman aqueduct}
  \wordexample{The Roman aqueduct carried fresh water to the city.（这条罗马渡槽把淡水输送到城里。）}
  \wordsense{n. 【解剖】导管}
  \wordphrase{cerebral aqueduct}
  \wordexample{The cerebral aqueduct connects the third and fourth ventricles.（大脑导水管连接第三脑室和第四脑室。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{conduction}{kən'dʌkʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{共同传导 con<com (= together) + duct (= lead)}
  \wordfamily{conduct 传导；conductive 导电的；conductor 导体}
  \wordsense{n. （热的）传导}
  \wordphrase{heat conduction}
  \wordexample{Copper is widely used because of its excellent heat conduction.（铜因其优良的导热性而被广泛使用。）}
  \wordsense{n. （电流的）传导}
  \wordphrase{electrical conduction}
  \wordexample{Electrical conduction in metals depends on free electrons.（金属中的电传导依赖于自由电子。）}
  \wordsense{n. 【生理】（神经冲动的）传导}
  \wordphrase{nerve conduction}
  \wordexample{The test measures the speed of nerve conduction.（这项检查测量神经传导速度。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{conductor}{kən'dʌktə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{引导者 con<com (= together) + duct (= lead)}
  \wordfamily{conduct 指挥；conductive 导电的；semiconductor 半导体}
  \wordsense{n. 指挥家}
  \wordphrase{guest conductor}
  \wordexample{The guest conductor led the orchestra with great energy.（客席指挥充满激情地带领了乐团。）}
  \wordsense{n. 列车长；售票员}
  \wordphrase{train conductor}
  \wordexample{The conductor checked our tickets before departure.（列车员在发车前检查了我们的车票。）}
  \wordsense{n. 导体}
  \wordphrase{electrical conductor}
  \wordexample{Copper is an excellent conductor of electricity.（铜是极好的导电体。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{deduct}{di'dʌkt}
  \wordbreakdown{向下扣除 de (= down) + duct (= lead)}
  \wordfamily{deduction 扣除额；deductible 可扣除的；deductive 演绎的}
  \wordsense{vt. 扣除；减去}
  \wordphrase{deduct tax from wages}
  \wordexample{The company will deduct tax from your wages each month.（公司每个月都会从你的工资中扣税。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 演绎；推论}
  \wordphrase{deduct a conclusion}
  \wordexample{From these premises we can deduct a logical conclusion.（从这些前提中我们可以推导出一个合乎逻辑的结论。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{deductible}{di'dʌktəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{可被扣除的 de (= down) + duct (= lead) + -ible}
  \wordfamily{deduct 扣除；deduction 扣除额；tax-deductible 可抵税的}
  \wordsense{a. 可扣除的；可减免的}
  \wordphrase{deductible expense}
  \wordexample{Travel costs may be deductible expenses for the company.（差旅费用可能属于公司可扣除的支出。）}
  \wordsense{n. 【保险】免赔额}
  \wordphrase{a high deductible}
  \wordexample{The insurance plan has a high deductible of five thousand dollars.（这份保险计划有五千美元的高免赔额。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{deduction}{di'dʌkʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{扣除出来的结果 de (= down) + duct (= lead) + -ion}
  \wordfamily{deduct 扣除；deductible 可扣除的；deductive 演绎的}
  \wordsense{n. 扣除额；减除}
  \wordphrase{tax deduction}
  \wordexample{She claimed a tax deduction for her research expenses.（她为自己的研究支出申请了税额扣除。）}
  \wordsense{n. 推论；演绎}
  \wordphrase{logical deduction}
  \wordexample{Her conclusion was based on careful deduction rather than guesswork.（她的结论建立在谨慎推理而不是猜测之上。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{deductive}{di'dʌktiv}
  \wordbreakdown{向下推导的 de (= down) + duct (= lead) + -ive}
  \wordfamily{deduce 推论；deduction 推论；inductive 归纳的}
  \wordsense{a. 演绎的；推论的}
  \wordphrase{deductive reasoning}
  \wordexample{Sherlock Holmes is famous for his deductive reasoning.（夏洛克·福尔摩斯以演绎推理闻名。）}
  \wordsense{a. 推断出来的；由推理得出的}
  \wordphrase{deductive conclusion}
  \wordexample{The deductive conclusion followed logically from the premises.（这个推论性结论是从前提中合乎逻辑地得出的。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{ductile}{'dʌktail}
  \wordbreakdown{能够被拉伸的 duct (= lead, draw) + -ile}
  \wordfamily{ductility 延展性；inductile 不易延展的；conductor 导体}
  \wordsense{a. （金属）可延展的}
  \wordphrase{ductile metal}
  \wordexample{Gold is a highly ductile metal.（金是一种延展性极强的金属。）}
  \wordsense{a. 易教导的；顺从的}
  \wordphrase{a ductile mind}
  \wordexample{A ductile mind responds quickly to careful training.（善于引导的头脑会迅速回应细致训练。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{induct}{in'dʌkt}
  \wordbreakdown{引入其中 in (= into) + duct (= lead)}
  \wordfamily{induction 就任；inductive 归纳的；introduce 引入}
  \wordsense{vt. 使正式就任；使就职}
  \wordphrase{induct a president into office}
  \wordexample{The committee will induct the new president next week.（委员会将于下周正式安排新主席就职。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 吸收……为会员；使加入}
  \wordphrase{induct new members into the club}
  \wordexample{The society inducted ten new members this spring.（该学会今年春天吸收了十位新成员。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 使入门；使了解}
  \wordphrase{induct staff into company procedures}
  \wordexample{New hires are inducted into the safety rules on day one.（新员工第一天就会接受安全规定入门培训。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 【逻】归纳；【物】感应}
  \wordphrase{induct a current in the coil}
  \wordexample{The coil can induct a current in the wire nearby.（这个线圈能够在附近导线中感应出电流。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{induction}{in'dʌkʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{被引入的过程 in (= into) + duct (= lead) + -ion}
  \wordfamily{induct 使就任；inductive 归纳的；introduction 引入}
  \wordsense{n. 就职；入会仪式}
  \wordphrase{induction ceremony}
  \wordexample{Her induction into the academy took place on Friday.（她于周五正式加入了该学会。）}
  \wordsense{n. 入门培训}
  \wordphrase{induction course}
  \wordexample{All interns must complete a two-day induction course.（所有实习生都必须完成为期两天的入门培训。）}
  \wordsense{n. 归纳法}
  \wordphrase{induction in logic}
  \wordexample{The argument proceeds by induction rather than deduction.（这个论证采用的是归纳法而不是演绎法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 【物】感应}
  \wordphrase{electromagnetic induction}
  \wordexample{Faraday discovered electromagnetic induction in the nineteenth century.（法拉第在十九世纪发现了电磁感应现象。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{inductive}{in'dʌktiv}
  \wordbreakdown{具有引导性质的 in (= into) + duct (= lead) + -ive}
  \wordfamily{induct 使就任；induction 归纳法；deductive 演绎的}
  \wordsense{a. 归纳的}
  \wordphrase{inductive method}
  \wordexample{The scientist used an inductive method to form the hypothesis.（这位科学家用归纳法提出了假设。）}
  \wordsense{a. 诱导性的；感应的}
  \wordphrase{inductive sensor}
  \wordexample{The machine stops automatically when the inductive sensor is triggered.（当感应传感器被触发时，机器会自动停止。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{introduction}{ˌintrə'dʌkʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{引入内部 intro (= within) + duct (= lead) + -ion}
  \wordfamily{introduce 介绍；introductory 导言的；induction 入门培训}
  \wordsense{n. 介绍；引见}
  \wordphrase{make an introduction}
  \wordexample{May I make an introduction between you and Professor Li?（我可以把你介绍给李教授吗？）}
  \wordsense{n. 引进；采用}
  \wordphrase{the introduction of new technology}
  \wordexample{The introduction of new technology improved efficiency.（新技术的引进提高了效率。）}
  \wordsense{n. 绪论；前言；导论}
  \wordphrase{the introduction to the book}
  \wordexample{Read the introduction before you start chapter one.（开始第一章之前先读一下前言。）}
  \wordsense{n. 入门读物}
  \wordphrase{an introduction to economics}
  \wordexample{This text is a clear introduction to economics.（这本书是一本清晰易懂的经济学入门读物。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{introductory}{ˌintrə'dʌktəri}
  \wordbreakdown{起引导作用的 intro (= within) + duct (= lead) + -ory}
  \wordfamily{introduce 介绍；introduction 导论；intro 入门课程}
  \wordsense{a. 介绍性的；前言的}
  \wordphrase{introductory remarks}
  \wordexample{The dean began with a few introductory remarks.（院长先讲了几句开场白。）}
  \wordsense{a. 初步的；入门的}
  \wordphrase{introductory chapter}
  \wordexample{The course includes an introductory chapter on grammar.（这门课程包含一章语法入门内容。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{oviduct}{'əuvidʌkt}
  \wordbreakdown{输送卵子的管道 ovi (= egg) + duct (= lead)}
  \wordfamily{ovum 卵子；ova 卵；ovary 卵巢}
  \wordsense{n. 输卵管}
  \wordphrase{the bird's oviduct}
  \wordexample{The egg moves through the oviduct before the shell forms.（蛋壳形成之前，卵会先经过输卵管。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{product}{'prɔdʌkt}
  \wordbreakdown{向前引出的东西 pro (= forth) + duct (= lead)}
  \wordfamily{production 生产；productive 多产的；productivity 生产率}
  \wordsense{n. 产品；产物；产量}
  \wordphrase{agricultural / marine / forest products}
  \wordexample{The company exports agricultural and marine products worldwide.（这家公司向全球出口农产品和海产品。）}
  \wordsense{n. 结果；成果}
  \wordphrase{the product of years of research}
  \wordexample{The vaccine was the product of years of research.（这种疫苗是多年研究的成果。）}
  \wordsense{n. 国民生产总值中的“产值”义}
  \wordphrase{gross national product (GNP)}
  \wordexample{Economists compared the country's gross national product over a decade.（经济学家比较了该国十年间的国民生产总值。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{production}{prə'dʌkʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{向前产出的过程 pro (= forth) + duct (= lead) + -ion}
  \wordfamily{product 产品；productive 多产的；productivity 生产率}
  \wordsense{n. 生产；制造}
  \wordphrase{mass production}
  \wordexample{The factory relies on mass production to lower costs.（这家工厂依靠大规模生产来降低成本。）}
  \wordsense{n. 产量}
  \wordphrase{oil production}
  \wordexample{Oil production fell sharply after the storm.（暴风雨过后，石油产量大幅下降。）}
  \wordsense{n. 作品；演出}
  \wordphrase{a stage production}
  \wordexample{The school staged a new production of Hamlet.（学校上演了一版新的《哈姆雷特》。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{productive}{prə'dʌktiv}
  \wordbreakdown{能够产出的 pro (= forth) + duct (= lead) + -ive}
  \wordfamily{product 产品；production 生产；productivity 生产率}
  \wordsense{a. 多产的；生产性的}
  \wordphrase{productive writer}
  \wordexample{She remained a productive writer throughout her life.（她一生都是一位多产的作家。）}
  \wordsense{a. 肥沃的}
  \wordphrase{productive soil}
  \wordexample{The river valley has rich and productive soil.（这片河谷拥有肥沃而高产的土壤。）}
  \wordsense{a. 富有成效的；产生结果的}
  \wordphrase{productive discussion}
  \wordexample{We had a productive discussion about the budget.（我们就预算进行了一次富有成效的讨论。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{productivity}{ˌprɔdʌk'tivəti}
  \wordbreakdown{生产能力 pro (= forth) + duct (= lead) + -ivity}
  \wordfamily{product 产品；production 生产；productive 高效的}
  \wordsense{n. 生产率；生产力}
  \wordphrase{labor productivity}
  \wordexample{New software increased labor productivity.（新软件提高了劳动生产率。）}
  \wordsense{n. 工作效率；工作成效}
  \wordphrase{boost productivity}
  \wordexample{Short breaks can sometimes boost productivity.（短暂休息有时能够提升工作效率。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{seduction}{si'dʌkʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{把人拉离正轨 se (= apart) + duct (= lead) + -ion}
  \wordfamily{seduce 引诱；seductive 诱人的；seducer 勾引者}
  \wordsense{n. 诱惑；勾引}
  \wordphrase{the seduction of power}
  \wordexample{He resisted the seduction of easy money.（他抵制住了轻易得财的诱惑。）}
  \wordsense{n. 魅力；吸引力}
  \wordphrase{the seductions of suburban life}
  \wordexample{The novel explores the seductions of suburban life.（这部小说探讨了郊区生活的吸引力。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{seductive}{si'dʌktiv}
  \wordbreakdown{具有诱惑力的 se (= apart) + duct (= lead) + -ive}
  \wordfamily{seduce 引诱；seduction 诱惑；seducer 勾引者}
  \wordsense{a. 诱惑的；迷人的}
  \wordphrase{seductive smile}
  \wordexample{She gave him a seductive smile across the table.（她隔着桌子朝他露出一个迷人的微笑。）}
  \wordsense{a. 诱人的；极具吸引力的}
  \wordphrase{seductive offer}
  \wordexample{The company made a seductive offer to attract new clients.（公司开出了极具吸引力的条件来吸引新客户。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{reduction}{ri'dʌkʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{向回拉低 re (= back) + duct (= lead) + -ion}
  \wordfamily{reduce 减少；reductive 简化的；deduction 扣除额}
  \wordsense{n. 减少；削减；缩小}
  \wordphrase{reduction in costs}
  \wordexample{The plan aims at a reduction in costs.（这项计划旨在降低成本。）}
  \wordsense{n. 减价}
  \wordphrase{price reduction}
  \wordexample{The store announced a price reduction on winter coats.（商店宣布冬装降价。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{viaduct}{'vaiədʌkt}
  \wordbreakdown{道路上的引道 via (= way) + duct (= lead)}
  \wordfamily{aqueduct 输水道；conductor 导体；adduce 引证}
  \wordsense{n. 高架桥（尤指跨越山谷或低地的铁路桥）}
  \wordphrase{railway viaduct}
  \wordexample{The train crossed a stone viaduct above the river.（火车驶过河面上方的一座石质高架桥。）}
  \wordsense{n. 高架道路；立交桥}
  \wordphrase{urban viaduct}
  \wordexample{The new urban viaduct eased traffic congestion downtown.（新建的城市高架路缓解了市中心的交通拥堵。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{adduce}{ə'dju:s}
  \wordbreakdown{引到前面 ad (= to) + duce (= lead)}
  \wordfamily{deduce 推论；deducible 可推断的；induce 诱导}
  \wordsense{vt. （作为证据、示例）引用；举出；引证}
  \wordphrase{adduce evidence}
  \wordexample{The lawyer adduced fresh evidence in court.（律师在法庭上提出了新的证据。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{deduce}{di'dju:s}
  \wordbreakdown{向下推导 de (= down) + duce (= lead)}
  \wordfamily{deduction（推论）；deductive（演绎的）；deducible（可推断的）；abduct（诱拐）}
  \wordsense{vt. 演绎；推论}
  \wordphrase{deduce from the facts}
  \wordphrase{deduce the origin of a word}
  \wordexample{From the footprints, the detective deduced that two men had entered the room.（侦探从脚印推断出有两个人进过房间。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{deducible}{di'dju:səbl}
  \wordbreakdown{可以推导出来的 de (= down) + duce (= lead) + -ible}
  \wordfamily{deduce 推论；deduction 推论；deductive 演绎的}
  \wordsense{a. 可推论的；可演绎的}
  \wordphrase{logically deducible}
  \wordexample{The conclusion is logically deducible from the premises.（这个结论可以从前提中合乎逻辑地推导出来。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{Duce}{'du:tʃi}
  \wordbreakdown{引导者、领袖 duce (= lead)}
  \wordfamily{duke 公爵；dukedom 公爵领地；duchess 公爵夫人；duchy 公国}
  \wordsense{n. 领袖；元首（特指意大利法西斯时期对墨索里尼的称呼）}
  \wordphrase{Il Duce}
  \wordexample{The propaganda posters praised Mussolini as Il Duce.（宣传海报把墨索里尼吹捧为“领袖”。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{educational}{ˌedju'keiʃənl}
  \wordbreakdown{引导出来的教育性 e/ex (= out) + duc (= lead) + -ational}
  \wordfamily{educate 教育；education 教育；educator 教育家}
  \wordsense{a. 教育的}
  \wordphrase{educational policy}
  \wordexample{The report focuses on educational policy in rural areas.（这份报告聚焦于农村教育政策。）}
  \wordsense{a. 有教育意义的}
  \wordphrase{educational film}
  \wordexample{The museum prepared an educational film for children.（博物馆为孩子们准备了一部有教育意义的影片。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{educator}{'edjuˌkeitə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{把人引导出来的人 e/ex (= out) + duc (= lead) + -ator}
  \wordfamily{educate 教育；education 教育；educational 教育的}
  \wordsense{n. 教育家；教育工作者}
  \wordphrase{professional educator}
  \wordexample{She is a respected educator with thirty years of experience.（她是一位拥有三十年经验、备受尊敬的教育工作者。）}
  \wordsense{n. 教育理论家；教育学研究者}
  \wordphrase{progressive educator}
  \wordexample{John Dewey was a progressive educator who transformed American schools.（约翰·杜威是一位改变了美国学校的进步教育理论家。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{educe}{i'dju:s}
  \wordbreakdown{引导出来 e/ex (= out) + duce (= lead)}
  \wordfamily{educator 教育家；educational 教育的；deduce 推断}
  \wordsense{vt. 引出；使显现出（潜在的能力或性格）}
  \wordphrase{educe useful ideas}
  \wordexample{The discussion helped educe several useful ideas from the team.（这场讨论帮助团队引出了几个有用的想法。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{introduce}{ˌintrə'dju:s}
  \wordbreakdown{引入其中 intro (= within) + duce (= lead)}
  \wordfamily{introduction 介绍；introductory 入门的；induce 诱导}
  \wordsense{vt. （向某人）介绍；引见}
  \wordphrase{introduce A to B}
  \wordexample{Let me introduce you to our new manager.（让我把你介绍给我们的新经理。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 提出（议案、话题等）}
  \wordphrase{introduce a bill into Congress}
  \wordexample{The senator plans to introduce a bill next month.（这位参议员计划下个月提交一项法案。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 引进；采用}
  \wordphrase{introduce new technology}
  \wordexample{Potatoes were introduced into Europe from America.（马铃薯是从美洲引入欧洲的。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{produce}{prə'dju:s}
  \wordbreakdown{向前引出 pro (= forth, forward) + duce (= lead)}
  \wordfamily{product 产品；production 生产；productive 多产的；productivity 生产率}
  \wordsense{vt. 引起；招致}
  \wordphrase{produce a sensation}
  \wordexample{The film produced a sensation across Europe.（这部电影在欧洲引起了很大反响。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 制造；生产；制作}
  \wordphrase{produce high-quality steel}
  \wordexample{The plant can produce high-quality steel at low cost.（这家工厂能以较低成本生产高质量钢材。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 创作；上演；出版}
  \wordphrase{produce a new play}
  \wordexample{The theater will produce a new play this season.（剧院本季将上演一部新剧。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 拿出；出示}
  \wordphrase{produce a passport}
  \wordexample{Passengers must produce a passport at the border.（旅客在边境必须出示护照。）}
  \wordsense{n. 产品；农产品；产量}
  \wordphrase{fresh produce}
  \wordexample{The market sells fresh produce every morning.（这个市场每天早晨出售新鲜农产品。）}
\end{word}



\chapter{HOM, HUM}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 hom 意为 man，hum 意为 earth, ground。同义词根有来自希腊语的 anthrop 和来自盎格鲁-撒克逊语的 man。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{abominable}{ə'bɔminəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{脱离人的模样 ab (= away) + om(hom = man)}
  \wordfamily{abomination 厌恶；abominate 憎恶；abominator 憎恶者}
  \wordsense{a. 令人憎恶的；讨厌的}
  \wordphrase{an abominable habit}
  \wordexample{His abominable manners offended everyone at the table.（他那令人厌恶的举止冒犯了桌上的所有人。）}
  \wordsense{a. （天气、饮食等）令人不快的；糟糕的}
  \wordphrase{abominable weather}
  \wordexample{Her taste in clothing is abominable!（她的衣着品味很糟糕！）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{homage}{'hɔmidʒ}
  \wordbreakdown{对人表示敬重 hom (= man)}
  \wordfamily{humanity 人类；humane 仁慈的；Homo Sapiens 智人}
  \wordsense{n. 尊敬；敬意}
  \wordphrase{pay homage to}
  \wordexample{The writer paid homage to her mentor in the speech.（这位作家在演讲中向她的导师致敬。）}
  \wordsense{n. （封建时代对君主的）宣誓效忠}
  \wordphrase{swear homage to the king}
  \wordexample{The baron had to swear homage to the king.（这位男爵必须向国王宣誓效忠。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{homicide}{'hɔmisaid}
  \wordbreakdown{杀人 hom (= man) + cid (= kill)}
  \wordfamily{homicidal 杀人的；homicide detective 凶杀案侦探；homo 人}
  \wordsense{n. 杀人；杀人罪}
  \wordphrase{a homicide investigation}
  \wordexample{The coroner ruled the death a homicide.（验尸官裁定这起死亡案件属于他杀。）}
  \wordsense{n. 杀人犯}
  \wordphrase{homicide squad}
  \wordexample{The homicide squad caught the killer within a week.（重案组在一周内抓住了凶手。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{homicidal}{ˌhɔmi'saidl}
  \wordbreakdown{具有杀人性质的 hom (= man) + cid (= kill) + -al}
  \wordfamily{homicide 杀人；suicidal 自杀的；genocidal 种族灭绝的}
  \wordsense{a. 杀人的；凶残的}
  \wordphrase{a homicidal lunatic}
  \wordexample{The police were searching for a homicidal suspect.（警方正在搜捕一名有杀人嫌疑的嫌犯。）}
  \wordsense{a. 有杀人倾向的}
  \wordphrase{homicidal tendencies}
  \wordexample{The report mentioned his increasingly homicidal tendencies.（报告提到了他日益明显的杀人倾向。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{Homo Sapiens}{ˌhəuməu'seipiənz}
  \wordbreakdown{有智慧的人 homo (= man) + sap (= wise, judge)}
  \wordfamily{human 人类的；humanity 人类；humane 人道的}
  \wordsense{n. 智人}
  \wordphrase{early Homo Sapiens}
  \wordexample{Early Homo Sapiens spread across several continents.（早期智人扩散到了数个大洲。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{exhume}{eks'hju:m}
  \wordbreakdown{从土里掘出 ex (= out) + hum (= ground)}
  \wordfamily{inhume 埋葬；exhumation 掘尸；humus 腐殖质}
  \wordsense{vt. （从地里把尸体等）掘出；发掘}
  \wordphrase{exhume a body}
  \wordexample{Investigators obtained permission to exhume the body.（调查人员获得许可，将尸体掘了出来。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{exhumation}{ˌekshju:'meiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{掘出的行为 ex (= out) + hum (= ground) + -ation}
  \wordfamily{exhume 掘出；inhume 埋葬；humus 腐殖质}
  \wordsense{n. 掘尸；发掘}
  \wordphrase{the exhumation of the body}
  \wordexample{The court ordered the exhumation of the body for further tests.（法院下令掘尸，以便做进一步检验。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{humane}{hju:'mein}
  \wordbreakdown{像人一样温和 hom (= man)}
  \wordfamily{inhumane 不人道的；humanity 人道；humanitarian 人道主义者}
  \wordsense{a. 仁慈的；富于同情心的；亲切的}
  \wordphrase{a man of humane character}
  \wordexample{She is known as a doctor of humane character.（她以富有同情心的医生形象而闻名。）}
  \wordsense{a. （学科、制度等）促进文明或教化的；人文的}
  \wordphrase{humane education}
  \wordexample{The school promotes humane education as part of its mission.（这所学校把人文教育视为自身使命的一部分。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{humanism}{'hju:mənizəm}
  \wordbreakdown{以人为本的思想 human (= man) + -ism}
  \wordfamily{humanist 人文主义者；humanity 人类；humanitarian 人道主义者}
  \wordsense{n. 人道主义}
  \wordphrase{a spirit of humanism}
  \wordexample{The relief program was guided by a spirit of humanism.（这项救援计划以人道主义精神为指导。）}
  \wordsense{n. 人文主义}
  \wordphrase{Renaissance humanism}
  \wordexample{Renaissance humanism changed the study of literature and history.（文艺复兴时期的人文主义改变了文学和历史研究。）}
  \wordsense{n. 人本主义}
  \wordphrase{secular humanism}
  \wordexample{The essay compares existentialism with secular humanism.（这篇文章比较了存在主义与世俗人本主义。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{humanist}{'hju:mənist}
  \wordbreakdown{主张以人为本的人 human (= man) + -ist}
  \wordfamily{humanism 人文主义；humanity 人类；humanitarian 人道主义者}
  \wordsense{n. 人文学者}
  \wordphrase{a classical humanist}
  \wordexample{He was trained as a classical humanist before becoming a lawyer.（他在成为律师前受过古典人文学者的训练。）}
  \wordsense{n. 人道主义者；人本主义者}
  \wordphrase{a committed humanist}
  \wordexample{She describes herself as a committed humanist.（她说自己是一位坚定的人本主义者。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{humanitarian}{hju:ˌmæni'teəriən}
  \wordbreakdown{关怀人类的 human (= man) + -itarian}
  \wordfamily{humanity 人类；humanist 人道主义者；humanize 赋予人性}
  \wordsense{n. 人道主义者；慈善家}
  \wordphrase{a well-known humanitarian}
  \wordexample{The doctor became a well-known humanitarian after the war.（这位医生在战后成为著名的人道主义者。）}
  \wordsense{a. 人道主义的；博爱的}
  \wordphrase{humanitarian aid}
  \wordexample{Several countries sent humanitarian aid after the earthquake.（地震后，多个国家送来了人道主义援助。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{humanity}{hju:'mænəti}
  \wordbreakdown{人之整体 human (= man) + -ity}
  \wordfamily{human 人类的；humanitarian 人道主义者；humanize 赋予人性}
  \wordsense{n. 人类}
  \wordphrase{the future of humanity}
  \wordexample{Climate change threatens the future of humanity.（气候变化威胁着人类的未来。）}
  \wordsense{n. 人性；人道；慈爱}
  \wordphrase{show humanity to others}
  \wordexample{Even in war, we must show humanity to others.（即使在战争中，我们也必须对他人保有人性与仁爱。）}
  \wordsense{n. （pl.）人文学科}
  \wordphrase{the humanities}
  \wordexample{She studied the humanities at university.（她在大学里学习人文学科。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{humanize}{'hju:mənaiz}
  \wordbreakdown{使更有人性 human (= man) + -ize}
  \wordfamily{dehumanize 使失去人性；humanity 人性；humanitarian 人道主义的}
  \wordsense{vt. 赋予人性；使更人道}
  \wordphrase{humanize the prison system}
  \wordexample{The reform aims to humanize the prison system.（这项改革旨在让监狱制度更具人道精神。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 使通俗易懂；使平易近人}
  \wordphrase{humanize complex data}
  \wordexample{Good design can humanize complex data for ordinary users.（好的设计能让复杂数据对普通用户变得通俗易懂。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{humble}{'hʌmbl}
  \wordbreakdown{贴近土地 hum (= ground)}
  \wordfamily{humility 谦逊；humiliate 羞辱；humus 腐殖质}
  \wordsense{a. 谦逊的}
  \wordphrase{a humble person}
  \wordexample{Despite his fame, he remained humble and approachable.（尽管名气很大，他仍然谦逊而平易近人。）}
  \wordsense{a. 身份低贱的；出身卑微的}
  \wordphrase{a man of humble birth}
  \wordexample{She rose from humble beginnings to national office.（她从卑微出身一路走到了国家要职。）}
  \wordsense{a. （东西）粗陋的；简陋的}
  \wordphrase{a humble cottage}
  \wordexample{They spent the summer in a humble cottage by the sea.（他们在海边一间简陋的小屋里度过了夏天。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{humid}{'hju:mid}
  \wordbreakdown{像泥土一样潮湿 hum (= ground)}
  \wordfamily{humidity 湿度；humidify 使潮湿；dehumidify 除湿}
  \wordsense{a. 潮湿的；湿润的；湿气重的}
  \wordphrase{hot and humid}
  \wordexample{The air becomes hot and humid before the storm.（暴风雨来临前，空气会变得又热又潮。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{humidity}{hju:'midəti}
  \wordbreakdown{潮湿状态 hum (= ground) + -ity}
  \wordfamily{humid 潮湿的；humidify 使潮湿；dehumidify 除湿}
  \wordsense{n. 湿气；湿度}
  \wordphrase{high humidity}
  \wordexample{High humidity makes the room feel warmer than it is.（高湿度会让房间感觉比实际更热。）}
  \wordsense{n. 相对湿度（气象学术语）}
  \wordphrase{relative humidity}
  \wordexample{The relative humidity today is 85 percent.（今天的相对湿度为百分之八十五。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{humidify}{hju:'midifai}
  \wordbreakdown{使变得潮湿 humid (= moist) + -ify}
  \wordfamily{humidifier 加湿器；dehumidify 除湿；humidity 湿度}
  \wordsense{vt. 使潮湿；使湿润}
  \wordphrase{humidify the room}
  \wordexample{A small machine can humidify the room in winter.（冬天里，一台小机器就能让房间保持湿润。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{humiliate}{hju:'milieit}
  \wordbreakdown{把人压到地上 hum (= ground) + -iliate}
  \wordfamily{humiliation 羞辱；humility 谦逊；humble 谦卑的}
  \wordsense{vt. 羞辱；使丢脸；使蒙耻辱}
  \wordphrase{humiliate oneself}
  \wordexample{He refused to humiliate himself by begging for mercy.（他拒绝靠乞求怜悯来让自己蒙羞。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 使（对手）一败涂地}
  \wordphrase{humiliate the opposition}
  \wordexample{The team humiliated their rivals with a 7-0 victory.（这支球队以7比0大胜对手，令其颜面尽失。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{humiliation}{hju:ˌmili'eiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{受辱的状态 hum (= ground) + -iliation}
  \wordfamily{humiliate 羞辱；humility 谦逊；humble 谦卑的}
  \wordsense{n. 羞辱；丢脸}
  \wordphrase{suffer humiliation}
  \wordexample{He still remembered the humiliation of that public defeat.（他仍记得那次公开失败带来的羞辱。）}
  \wordsense{n. 令人感到屈辱的事}
  \wordphrase{endure daily humiliations}
  \wordexample{The prisoners endured daily humiliations at the hands of their captors.（囚犯们每天都要忍受看守的凌辱。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{humility}{hju:'miləti}
  \wordbreakdown{贴近土地的品格 hum (= ground) + -ility}
  \wordfamily{humble 谦逊的；humiliate 羞辱；humility 谦逊}
  \wordsense{n. 谦逊；谦卑}
  \wordphrase{show humility}
  \wordexample{Good leaders show humility when they make mistakes.（优秀的领导者在犯错时会表现出谦逊。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{humorist}{'hju:mərist}
  \wordbreakdown{富于幽默的人 humor + -ist}
  \wordfamily{humorous 幽默的；humor 幽默；humour 幽默（英）}
  \wordsense{n. 谈吐诙谐的人；滑稽演员；幽默作家}
  \wordphrase{a political humorist}
  \wordexample{The columnist became famous as a political humorist.（这位专栏作家因政治讽刺幽默而成名。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{humorous}{'hju:mərəs}
  \wordbreakdown{具有幽默性质的 humor + -ous}
  \wordfamily{humorist 幽默作家；humor 幽默；humour 幽默（英）}
  \wordsense{a. 幽默的；滑稽的；诙谐的}
  \wordphrase{humorous remarks}
  \wordexample{Her humorous remarks put the whole audience at ease.（她的诙谐发言让全场都放松了下来。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{humus}{'hju:məs}
  \wordbreakdown{土地中的腐殖层 hum (= ground)}
  \wordfamily{humble 谦卑的；humid 潮湿的；exhume 掘出}
  \wordsense{n. 腐殖质}
  \wordphrase{rich humus}
  \wordexample{Forest soil is dark because it contains rich humus.（森林土壤颜色深，是因为其中含有丰富的腐殖质。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{posthumous}{'pɔstjəməs}
  \wordbreakdown{死后、入土之后 post (= after) + hum (= ground)}
  \wordfamily{humus 腐殖质；exhume 掘出；inhume 埋葬}
  \wordsense{a. 遗腹的}
  \wordphrase{a posthumous child}
  \wordexample{The king's posthumous child was born months after his death.（国王去世数月后，他的遗腹子出生了。）}
  \wordsense{a. 作者死后出版的；追授的}
  \wordphrase{a posthumous work}
  \wordexample{The novel was published as a posthumous work.（这部小说作为遗著出版了。）}
  \wordsense{a. 死后的}
  \wordphrase{posthumous fame}
  \wordexample{Many artists receive posthumous fame.（许多艺术家在去世后才获得声誉。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{subhuman}{sʌb'hju:mən}
  \wordbreakdown{低于人的 sub (= under) + human (= man)}
  \wordfamily{humanity 人类；inhuman 非人的；humanize 赋予人性}
  \wordsense{a. 低于人类的；近乎非人的}
  \wordphrase{subhuman conditions}
  \wordexample{The report described the prisoners as living in subhuman conditions.（报告称囚犯生活在近乎非人的环境中。）}
  \wordsense{a. 类人般的}
  \wordphrase{subhuman creature}
  \wordexample{The novel imagines a subhuman creature living in the cave.（小说设想了一种生活在洞穴里的类人生物。）}
\end{word}



\chapter{TAIN,TEN[TIN]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根 TAIN, TEN[TIN] 来自拉丁语的 ten，意为 to hold。
  \item tain 是该词根经法语进入英语后的形式，tin 是元音弱化后的形式。
  \item abstain：abs<ab (= from) + tain (= hold)，`vi. from + sth` 戒除；弃权；有意回避。
  \item abstention 戒除；abstinence 节制，禁欲；abstainer 节制者，戒酒者；abstinent 节制的，禁欲的。
  \item In this age of AIDS, it's wise to abstain from unprotected sex.（在这个艾滋病盛行的年代，最好避免无保护措施的性行为。）
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}{abstain}{ab'stein}
  \wordbreakdown{抓住自己脱离某物 abs<ab (= from) + tain (= hold)}
  \wordfamily{abstention 戒除；abstinence 节制；abstainer 戒酒者；abstinent 节制的}
  \wordsense{vi. 戒除；戒绝}
  \wordphrase{abstain from alcohol}
  \wordexample{In this age of AIDS, it's wise to abstain from unprotected sex.（在这个艾滋病盛行的年代，最好避免无保护措施的性行为。）}
  \wordsense{vi. 弃权（投票时）}
  \wordphrase{abstain from voting}
  \wordexample{Three members abstained from voting on the resolution.（三名成员在该决议的投票中弃权。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[+*]{attainment}{e'teinmənt}
  \wordbreakdown{在近处拥有 at<ad (= to, near) + tain (= hold)}
  \wordfamily{attain 达到；attainable 可达到的；unattainable 不可达到的}
  \wordsense{n. 达到；获得}
  \wordphrase{attainment of a goal}
  \wordexample{His attainment of a Pulitzer Prize gave him a sense of satisfaction.（获得普利策奖使他感到满足。）}
  \wordsense{n. （pl.）学识；才能；造诣}
  \wordphrase{academic attainments}
  \wordexample{Her attainments in mathematics are remarkable for her age.（就她的年龄而言，她在数学方面的造诣非常出色。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{countenance}{'kauntənəns}
  \wordbreakdown{一起支撑 coun<com (= together) + ten (= hold)}
  \wordfamily{attainment 获得；malcontent 不满者；abstain 戒除；pertain 有关}
  \wordsense{vt. 支持；赞同；容忍}
  \wordphrase{countenance tardiness}
  \wordexample{Our teacher does not countenance tardiness in the classroom.（我们老师不允许上课迟到。）}
  \wordsense{n. 面容；脸色；表情}
  \wordphrase{a cheerful countenance}
  \wordexample{Her calm countenance betrayed no sign of fear.（她平静的面容没有流露出丝毫恐惧。）}
  \wordsense{n. 支持；赞同}
  \wordphrase{give countenance to}
  \wordexample{The government gave no countenance to the rebels' demands.（政府对叛乱者的要求不予支持。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{malcontent}{'mælkəntent}
  \wordbreakdown{不能一起、全部拥有 mal (= bad) + con<com (= together) + ten (= hold)}
  \wordfamily{discontent 不满；content 满足的；contentment 满足}
  \wordsense{n. 不满者；反抗者}
  \wordphrase{an old malcontent}
  \wordexample{Don't take his comments seriously; he's just an old malcontent.（不要太在意他的批评，他只不过是个心怀不满的老人。）}
  \wordsense{a. 不平的；不满的；反抗的}
  \wordphrase{malcontent citizens}
  \wordexample{The malcontent workers organized a strike.（心怀不满的工人们组织了一次罢工。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{pertain}{pə'tein}
  \wordbreakdown{抓牢 per (= intensive) + tain (= hold)}
  \wordfamily{pertinent 有关的；pertinence 恰当；impertinent 无礼的}
  \wordsense{vi. 有关；关于}
  \wordphrase{pertain to email}
  \wordexample{He wrote a report on current copyright law as it pertains to email.（他写了一份有关当前版权法的报告，主要涉及与电子邮件相关的内容。）}
  \wordsense{vi. 从属；附属}
  \wordphrase{privileges pertaining to the office}
  \wordexample{These rights pertain to all citizens equally.（这些权利平等地属于所有公民。）}
  \wordsense{vi. 适合；符合}
  \wordphrase{pertain to the situation}
  \wordexample{His remarks did not pertain to the matter under discussion.（他的话与正在讨论的事情不相干。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{pertinacious}{,pə:ti'neiʃəs}
  \wordbreakdown{抓牢的 per (= intensive) + tin(ten) (= hold)}
  \wordfamily{pertinacity 执拗；tenacious 顽强的；tenacity 坚韧}
  \wordsense{a. 顽固的；执拗的}
  \wordphrase{pertinacious interest groups}
  \wordexample{Anti-abortionists are one of the most pertinacious interest groups in America.（反堕胎主义者是美国最顽固的利益集团之一。）}
  \wordsense{a. 坚持不懈的；不屈不挠的}
  \wordphrase{pertinacious effort}
  \wordexample{Her pertinacious effort finally won her the scholarship.（她坚持不懈的努力最终为她赢得了奖学金。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{retentive}{ri'tentiv}
  \wordbreakdown{拿在后边的 re (= back, again) + ten (= hold)}
  \wordfamily{retain 保持；retention 保留；retainer 仆人；聘用定金}
  \wordsense{a. 记性好的；有保持力的}
  \wordphrase{a retentive memory}
  \wordexample{I used to have a retentive memory, but it's weakened with age.（我以前记忆力很好，可年龄越大，记性越差。）}
  \wordsense{a. 保持的；能蓄住的}
  \wordphrase{retentive soil}
  \wordexample{Clay is more retentive of moisture than sand.（黏土比沙子更能保持水分。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{sustain}{sə'stein}
  \wordbreakdown{在下面支撑 sus<sub (= under) + tain (= hold)}
  \wordfamily{sustenance 生计；sustainable 可持续的；sustained 持续的}
  \wordsense{vt. 支撑；维持}
  \wordphrase{sustain a family}
  \wordexample{During the lean years, egg sales alone sustained our farm.（年景不好时，我们只能靠卖鸡蛋来维持农场。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 遭受；蒙受（损失、伤害等）}
  \wordphrase{sustain injuries}
  \wordexample{The building sustained heavy damage in the earthquake.（这栋建筑在地震中遭受了严重损坏。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 【律】确认；认可}
  \wordphrase{objection sustained}
  \wordexample{The judge sustained the lawyer's objection.（法官认可了律师的异议。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{tenable}{'tenəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{可维持的或保持的 ten (= hold)}
  \wordfamily{tenability 站得住脚；untenable 站不住脚的；tenure 任期}
  \wordsense{a. （学术观点等）站得住脚的；合理的}
  \wordphrase{a tenable explanation}
  \wordexample{One tenable explanation is that your tiredness is caused by chronic fatigue syndrome.（一个合理解释是，你的疲劳是由慢性疲劳综合征引起的。）}
  \wordsense{a. （要塞、阵地等）可防守的}
  \wordphrase{a tenable position}
  \wordexample{The fortress was no longer tenable after the walls were breached.（城墙被攻破后，这座堡垒已无法防守。）}
  \wordsense{a. （官职或职位）可保持的；可任职的}
  \wordphrase{tenable for three years}
  \wordexample{The fellowship is tenable for three years.（这项研究员职位可任职三年。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[+]{container}{kən'teinə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{一起包住的 con<com (= together) + tain (= hold)}
  \wordfamily{contain 包含；containment 遏制；containerize 集装箱化}
  \wordsense{n. 容器}
  \wordphrase{a glass container}
  \wordexample{Store the liquid in a sealed container.（把液体存放在密封容器中。）}
  \wordsense{n. 集装箱（用于货物运输的大金属箱）}
  \wordphrase{shipping container}
  \wordexample{The port handles thousands of containers every day.（这个港口每天处理数千个集装箱。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[++*]{content}{'kɔntent}
  \wordbreakdown{一起包含的 con<com (= together) + ten (= hold)}
  \wordfamily{contented 满足的；discontent 不满；contentment 满足}
  \wordsense{n. （pl.）内容；目录}
  \wordphrase{table of contents}
  \wordexample{Check the table of contents before reading the book.（读书前先看看目录。）}
  \wordsense{n. 含量}
  \wordphrase{sugar content of milk}
  \wordexample{The moisture content of the gas must be measured carefully.（气体的湿度含量必须仔细测量。）}
  \wordsense{a. 满足的；满意的}
  \wordphrase{content with one's life}
  \wordexample{She seemed content with her simple life in the village.（她似乎对自己在村里的简朴生活很满足。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 使满足}
  \wordphrase{content oneself with}
  \wordexample{He contented himself with a glass of water.（他喝了杯水就满足了。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{continence}{'kɔntinəns}
  \wordbreakdown{一起约束着的 con<com (= together) + tin(ten) (= hold)}
  \wordfamily{continent 大陆；incontinent 失禁的；continual 持续的}
  \wordsense{n. 自制；节制（尤指性欲）}
  \wordphrase{practice continence}
  \wordexample{The monks practiced strict continence as part of their vows.（修士们严格禁欲，作为誓言的一部分。）}
  \wordsense{n. 【医】节制；自制力（对排泄的控制）}
  \wordphrase{urinary continence}
  \wordexample{The surgery helped restore urinary continence.（手术帮助恢复了排尿控制能力。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{continental}{,kɔnti'nentl}
  \wordbreakdown{连成一体的 con<com (= together) + tin(ten) (= hold)}
  \wordfamily{continent 大陆；intercontinental 洲际的；subcontinental 次大陆的}
  \wordsense{a. 大陆的；大陆性的}
  \wordphrase{continental climate}
  \wordexample{The region has a continental climate with hot summers and cold winters.（该地区属大陆性气候，夏热冬冷。）}
  \wordsense{a. （C-）欧洲大陆的}
  \wordphrase{continental breakfast}
  \wordexample{The hotel offers a continental breakfast of coffee and pastries.（酒店提供欧式早餐，有咖啡和糕点。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{continual}{kən'tinjuəl}
  \wordbreakdown{一直连着的 con<com (= together) + tin(ten) (= hold)}
  \wordfamily{continually 频繁地；continuous 连续不断的；continuity 连续性}
  \wordsense{a. 多次重复的；频繁的}
  \wordphrase{continual interruptions}
  \wordexample{The continual interruptions made it impossible to concentrate.（频繁的打扰使人无法集中注意力。）}
  \wordsense{a. 连续的；不间断的}
  \wordphrase{continual improvement}
  \wordexample{The company is committed to continual improvement of its products.（公司致力于产品的持续改进。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{continuance}{kən'tinjuəns}
  \wordbreakdown{持续保持 con<com (= together) + tin(ten) (= hold)}
  \wordfamily{continual 频繁的；continuation 续篇；continuity 连续性}
  \wordsense{n. 继续；持续}
  \wordphrase{continuance of prosperity}
  \wordexample{The continuance of prosperity depends on wise economic policy.（繁荣的持续取决于明智的经济政策。）}
  \wordsense{n. 持续的时间}
  \wordphrase{during the continuance of the war}
  \wordexample{During the continuance of the war, trade was severely disrupted.（战争期间，贸易受到严重破坏。）}
  \wordsense{n. 【律】延期审理}
  \wordphrase{request a continuance}
  \wordexample{The defense attorney requested a continuance to gather more evidence.（辩护律师请求延期审理以收集更多证据。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{continuation}{kəntinju'eiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{继续延展 con<com (= together) + tin(ten) (= hold)}
  \wordfamily{continuance 持续；continuity 连续性；continuous 连续的}
  \wordsense{n. 继续；持续}
  \wordphrase{continuation of the policy}
  \wordexample{The new president promised a continuation of the reform policy.（新总统承诺继续推行改革政策。）}
  \wordsense{n. 续篇；续集}
  \wordphrase{a continuation of the story}
  \wordexample{Fans eagerly awaited the exciting continuation of the story.（粉丝们热切期待着故事的精彩续篇。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{continuity}{,kɔnti'njuəti}
  \wordbreakdown{连续不断 con<com (= together) + tin(ten) (= hold)}
  \wordfamily{continuation 续篇；continuous 连续的；discontinuity 不连续}
  \wordsense{n. 连续（性）；连贯性}
  \wordphrase{continuity of subject}
  \wordexample{There is no continuity of subject in a dictionary.（字典里各主题之间没有连续性。）}
  \wordsense{n. （电影）分镜头剧本；场记}
  \wordphrase{continuity error}
  \wordexample{Sharp-eyed viewers spotted a continuity error in the film.（眼尖的观众发现了电影中的一处穿帮。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{continuous}{kən'tinjuəs}
  \wordbreakdown{不断连着的 con<com (= together) + tin(ten) (= hold)}
  \wordfamily{continuously 连续地；discontinuous 不连续的；continuity 连续性}
  \wordsense{a. 连续的；不断的；不间断的}
  \wordphrase{continuous rain}
  \wordexample{Three days of continuous rain caused flooding in the valley.（连续三天的降雨导致山谷发生洪水。）}
  \wordsense{a. 【语法】进行时态的}
  \wordphrase{present continuous tense}
  \wordexample{In English, the present continuous tense uses the -ing form.（英语中，现在进行时使用-ing形式。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{detain}{di'tein}
  \wordbreakdown{向下扣住 de (= down, away) + tain (= hold)}
  \wordfamily{detainee 被拘留者；detention 拘留；retain 保留}
  \wordsense{vt. 拘留；扣留}
  \wordphrase{detain a suspect}
  \wordexample{Police detained the suspect for questioning.（警方拘留了嫌疑人进行审问。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 留住；耽搁}
  \wordphrase{be detained by business}
  \wordexample{I was detained at the office and missed the train.（我被公事耽搁了，错过了火车。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{detainee}{,di:tei'ni:}
  \wordbreakdown{被扣住的人 de (= down, away) + tain (= hold)}
  \wordfamily{detain 拘留；detention 拘留；internee 被拘禁者}
  \wordsense{n. 被拘留者；在押人员}
  \wordphrase{political detainees}
  \wordexample{The organization campaigns for the release of political detainees.（该组织为释放政治犯而奔走呼号。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{detention}{di'tenʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{向下扣留 de (= down, away) + ten (= hold)}
  \wordfamily{detain 拘留；detainee 被拘留者；retention 保留}
  \wordsense{n. 拘留；扣留}
  \wordphrase{detention without trial}
  \wordexample{He was held in detention for three months without trial.（他未经审判被拘留了三个月。）}
  \wordsense{n. （学生的）课后留校处分}
  \wordphrase{get detention}
  \wordexample{The student got detention for talking in class.（那个学生因为上课说话被罚课后留校。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{entertain}{,entə'tein}
  \wordbreakdown{把人留在中间款待 enter<inter (= between) + tain (= hold)}
  \wordfamily{entertainer 演员；entertainment 娱乐；entertaining 有趣的}
  \wordsense{vt. 招待；款待}
  \wordphrase{entertain guests}
  \wordexample{They entertained their guests with a lavish dinner.（他们用丰盛的晚宴款待客人。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 使欢乐；使娱乐}
  \wordphrase{entertain the audience}
  \wordexample{The clown entertained the children for hours.（小丑让孩子们开心了好几个小时。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 怀有；抱有（想法、希望等）}
  \wordphrase{entertain doubts}
  \wordexample{She refused to entertain any doubts about his honesty.（她拒绝对他的诚实抱有任何怀疑。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{entertainer}{,entə'teinə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{使人愉快的人 enter<inter (= between) + tain (= hold)}
  \wordfamily{entertain 款待；entertainment 娱乐；entertaining 有趣的}
  \wordsense{n. 专业演员；表演者}
  \wordphrase{a street entertainer}
  \wordexample{He made his living as a street entertainer.（他靠做街头艺人谋生。）}
  \wordsense{n. 善于招待客人的人}
  \wordphrase{a born entertainer}
  \wordexample{She is a born entertainer who loves hosting parties.（她天生善于待客，喜欢举办聚会。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{entertainment}{,entə'teinmənt}
  \wordbreakdown{使人得到消遣 enter<inter (= between) + tain (= hold)}
  \wordfamily{entertain 款待；entertainer 演员；entertaining 有趣的}
  \wordsense{n. 娱乐；消遣}
  \wordphrase{entertainment industry}
  \wordexample{The entertainment industry generates billions of dollars annually.（娱乐产业每年创造数十亿美元的收入。）}
  \wordsense{n. 招待；款待}
  \wordphrase{entertainment expenses}
  \wordexample{The company's entertainment expenses exceeded the budget.（公司的招待费超出了预算。）}
  \wordsense{n. 文艺演出；演艺节目}
  \wordphrase{musical entertainment}
  \wordexample{The hotel provides live musical entertainment every evening.（酒店每晚提供现场音乐演出。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{lieutenant}{lef'tenənt}
  \wordbreakdown{代替其位的 lieu (= place) + tenant (= holder)}
  \wordfamily{tenancy 租赁；tenant 租户；tenure 任期}
  \wordsense{n. 中尉；少尉}
  \wordphrase{police lieutenant}
  \wordexample{The lieutenant led his platoon through the jungle.（中尉带领他的排穿越丛林。）}
  \wordsense{n. 代理官员；副职官员}
  \wordphrase{lieutenant governor}
  \wordexample{The lieutenant governor assumed office when the governor fell ill.（州长生病后，副州长代行职务。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{maintain}{mein'tein}
  \wordbreakdown{用手维持 main<man (= hand) + tain (= hold)}
  \wordfamily{maintenance 维护；maintainable 可维持的；maintainer 维护者}
  \wordsense{vt. 维持；保持}
  \wordphrase{maintain order}
  \wordexample{The police struggled to maintain order during the protest.（警方在抗议期间努力维持秩序。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 主张；坚持认为}
  \wordphrase{maintain one's innocence}
  \wordexample{He maintained his innocence throughout the trial.（他在整个审判过程中坚持自己无罪。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 赡养；扶养}
  \wordphrase{maintain a family}
  \wordexample{She works two jobs to maintain her family.（她打两份工来养家。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 维修；保养}
  \wordphrase{maintain the equipment}
  \wordexample{The building is expensive to maintain.（这栋楼的维护费用很高。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{maintenance}{'meintənəns}
  \wordbreakdown{维持住的状态 main<man (= hand) + tain (= hold)}
  \wordfamily{maintain 维持；maintainer 维护者；low-maintenance 低维护的}
  \wordsense{n. 维修；保养}
  \wordphrase{cost of maintenance}
  \wordexample{The cost of maintenance for old buildings is very high.（老建筑的维修费用非常高。）}
  \wordsense{n. 维持；保持}
  \wordphrase{maintenance of order}
  \wordexample{The maintenance of public order is a primary duty of the police.（维持公共秩序是警察的首要职责。）}
  \wordsense{n. 赡养费；生活费}
  \wordphrase{maintenance order}
  \wordexample{The court issued a maintenance order requiring him to pay child support.（法院下达了供养令，要求他支付子女抚养费。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{obtain}{əb'tein}
  \wordbreakdown{用力拿到 ob (= intensive) + tain (= hold)}
  \wordfamily{obtainable 可获得的；unobtainable 得不到的；maintenance 维护}
  \wordsense{vt. 得到；获得}
  \wordphrase{obtain permission}
  \wordexample{You must obtain permission before entering the restricted area.（进入限制区域前必须获得许可。）}
  \wordsense{vi. （规则、习俗等）流行；通行}
  \wordphrase{customs that still obtain}
  \wordexample{These traditions still obtain in rural areas.（这些传统在农村地区仍然流行。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{obtainable}{əb'teinəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{能够拿到的 ob (= intensive) + tain (= hold)}
  \wordfamily{obtain 获得；unobtainable 得不到的；attainable 可达到的}
  \wordsense{a. 可获得的；能得到的}
  \wordphrase{easily obtainable}
  \wordexample{Fresh vegetables are easily obtainable at the local market.（新鲜蔬菜在当地市场很容易买到。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{retinue}{'retinju:}
  \wordbreakdown{跟在后面的一群人 re (= again, back) + tin(ten) (= hold)}
  \wordfamily{retain 保留；suite 随从；entourage 随行人员}
  \wordsense{n. 随员；随从；侍从}
  \wordphrase{a large retinue}
  \wordexample{The king traveled with a large retinue of servants and guards.（国王带着一大群仆人和卫兵出行。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{tenacious}{tə'neiʃəs}
  \wordbreakdown{抓得紧的 ten (= hold)}
  \wordfamily{tenacity 坚韧；pertinacious 顽固的；retentive 有保持力的}
  \wordsense{a. 坚韧的；顽强的}
  \wordphrase{tenacious of life}
  \wordexample{Weeds are tenacious of life and hard to remove.（杂草生命力顽强，很难清除。）}
  \wordsense{a. 固执的；不放手的}
  \wordphrase{a tenacious foe}
  \wordexample{He is a tenacious negotiator who never gives up.（他是个不屈不挠的谈判者，从不放弃。）}
  \wordsense{a. （记忆力）强的}
  \wordphrase{a tenacious memory}
  \wordexample{She has a tenacious memory for names and faces.（她对名字和面孔有很强的记忆力。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{tenacity}{tə'næsəti}
  \wordbreakdown{抓得很紧的状态 ten (= hold)}
  \wordfamily{tenacious 坚韧的；pertinacity 执拗；retentive 有保持力的}
  \wordsense{n. 坚韧；顽强}
  \wordphrase{tenacity of purpose}
  \wordexample{Her tenacity of purpose helped her overcome every obstacle.（她意志的顽强帮助她克服了每一个障碍。）}
  \wordsense{n. 顽固；执拗}
  \wordphrase{cling with tenacity}
  \wordexample{He clung to his beliefs with remarkable tenacity.（他以惊人的执着坚守自己的信念。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{tenant}{'tenənt}
  \wordbreakdown{占住的人 ten (= hold)}
  \wordfamily{tenancy 租赁；tenement 公寓；landlord 房东}
  \wordsense{n. 承租人；房客}
  \wordphrase{evict a tenant}
  \wordexample{The landlord evicted the tenants for non-payment of rent.（房东因房客不交房租而将其赶走。）}
  \wordsense{n. 佃户；佃农}
  \wordphrase{tenant farmer}
  \wordexample{Tenant farmers worked the land but did not own it.（佃农耕种土地但并不拥有它。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{tenancy}{'tenənsi}
  \wordbreakdown{占有、租用的状态 ten (= hold)}
  \wordfamily{tenant 房客；tenement 公寓；tenure 任期}
  \wordsense{n. 租赁；租用}
  \wordphrase{during the tenancy}
  \wordexample{The landlord may not enter the property during the tenancy without notice.（租赁期间，房东不得未经通知进入房屋。）}
  \wordsense{n. 租期}
  \wordphrase{a short tenancy}
  \wordexample{The tenancy runs for twelve months.（租期为十二个月。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{tenement}{'tenəmənt}
  \wordbreakdown{可供占住的地方 ten (= hold)}
  \wordfamily{tenant 房客；tenancy 租赁；apartment 公寓}
  \wordsense{n. 廉价公寓；经济公寓}
  \wordphrase{tenement building}
  \wordexample{Immigrants crowded into tenement buildings on the Lower East Side.（移民们挤在下东区的廉价公寓楼里。）}
  \wordsense{n. 【律】保有物；不动产}
  \wordphrase{tenement and hereditament}
  \wordexample{The deed transferred all tenements and hereditaments to the buyer.（契约将所有不动产和可继承财产转让给了买方。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{tenet}{'tenit}
  \wordbreakdown{坚持持有的看法 ten (= hold)}
  \wordfamily{tenement 公寓；tenor 要旨；doctrine 教义}
  \wordsense{n. 信条；教义；原则}
  \wordphrase{basic tenets of democracy}
  \wordexample{Freedom of speech is one of the basic tenets of democracy.（言论自由是民主的基本信条之一。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{tenor}{'tenə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{整体保持的走向 ten (= hold)}
  \wordfamily{tenet 信条；tenure 任期；tenement 公寓}
  \wordsense{n. 进程；（生活的）一般趋向}
  \wordphrase{the even tenor of life}
  \wordexample{Nothing disturbed the even tenor of village life.（没有什么打扰到乡村生活的平静进程。）}
  \wordsense{n. 要旨；主旨；大意}
  \wordphrase{the tenor of the speech}
  \wordexample{I got the general tenor of what was being said.（我理解了所讲内容的大意。）}
  \wordsense{n. 男高音；男高音歌手}
  \wordphrase{tenor voice}
  \wordexample{He sang the aria in a clear tenor.（他用清亮的男高音唱了这首咏叹调。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{tenure}{'tenjuə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{占有的期间 ten (= hold)}
  \wordfamily{tenant 房客；tenancy 租赁；tenable 站得住脚的}
  \wordsense{n. 任期；任职期间}
  \wordphrase{during his tenure as president}
  \wordexample{During his tenure as president, the economy grew rapidly.（在他担任总统期间，经济快速增长。）}
  \wordsense{n. （大学教授等的）终身职位}
  \wordphrase{get tenure}
  \wordexample{After six years, she finally got tenure at the university.（六年后，她终于获得了大学的终身教职。）}
  \wordsense{n. （土地、财产的）保有权；占有}
  \wordphrase{land tenure}
  \wordexample{The system of land tenure varies from country to country.（土地保有制度因国而异。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{untenable}{ʌn'teinəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{无法维持的 un (= not) + ten (= hold)}
  \wordfamily{tenable 站得住脚的；indefensible 无法辩护的；tenure 任期}
  \wordsense{a. （论据等）站不住脚的；不能成立的}
  \wordphrase{an untenable argument}
  \wordexample{His position became untenable after the evidence was revealed.（证据公布后，他的立场变得站不住脚了。）}
  \wordsense{a. （阵地等）防守不住的；无法坚守的}
  \wordphrase{an untenable position}
  \wordexample{The military position was untenable under constant bombardment.（在持续轰炸下，这个军事阵地无法坚守。）}
\end{word}



\chapter{NORM}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根NORM 来自拉丁语的norm意为standard，rule。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{abnormal}{æb'nɔ:məl}
  \wordbreakdown{脱离标准的 ab (= from) + norm (= rule)}
  \wordfamily{abnormality 异常；abnormally 反常地；normal 正常的}
  \wordsense{a. 不正常的；异常的}
  \wordphrase{abnormal behavior}
  \wordexample{The calm before a thunderstorm sometimes seems abnormal.（暴风雨前的平静有时显得很异常。）}
  \wordsense{a. 变态的；畸形的}
  \wordphrase{abnormal psychology}
  \wordexample{She studied abnormal psychology at university.（她在大学学习变态心理学。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{enormous}{i'nɔ:məs}
  \wordbreakdown{超出标准的 e<ex (= out) + norm (= rule)}
  \wordfamily{enormity 穷凶极恶；enormously 极其；abnormal 异常的}
  \wordsense{a. 庞大的；巨大的}
  \wordphrase{an enormous amount}
  \wordexample{The project required an enormous amount of money.（这个项目需要巨额资金。）}
  \wordsense{a. 极大的；严重的}
  \wordphrase{enormous pressure}
  \wordexample{She was under enormous pressure to resign.（她面临着巨大的辞职压力。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[+]{enormity}{i'nɔ:məti}
  \wordbreakdown{超出常规到可怕的程度 e<ex (= out) + norm (= rule)}
  \wordfamily{enormous 巨大的；abnormality 异常；norm 标准}
  \wordsense{n. 穷凶极恶；滔天大罪}
  \wordphrase{the enormity of the crime}
  \wordexample{The enormity of the crime shocked the entire nation.（这桩滔天大罪震惊了全国。）}
  \wordsense{n. 巨大；严重性}
  \wordphrase{the enormity of the task}
  \wordexample{Only then did she realize the enormity of the task ahead.（直到那时她才意识到前方任务的艰巨。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{norm}{nɔ:m}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 NORM}
  \wordfamily{normal 正常的；normalize 使正常化；normative 规范的}
  \wordsense{n. 标准；规范}
  \wordphrase{social norms}
  \wordexample{Social norms vary greatly between cultures.（社会规范在不同文化之间差异很大。）}
  \wordsense{n. 常态；通常的情况}
  \wordphrase{above the norm}
  \wordexample{Temperatures this week are well above the norm.（本周气温远高于正常水平。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{normal}{'nɔ:məl}
  \wordbreakdown{合乎标准的 norm (= rule)}
  \wordfamily{normalcy 常态；normally 正常地；abnormal 异常的}
  \wordsense{a. 正常的；标准的；通常的}
  \wordphrase{normal temperature}
  \wordexample{Everything returned to normal after the storm.（暴风雨过后，一切恢复了正常。）}
  \wordsense{a. 师范的}
  \wordphrase{normal school}
  \wordexample{She graduated from a normal school and became a teacher.（她从师范学校毕业后成为了一名教师。）}
  \wordsense{n. 标准；常态}
  \wordphrase{above normal}
  \wordexample{His blood pressure is slightly above normal.（他的血压略高于正常值。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{normalize}{'nɔ:məlaiz}
  \wordbreakdown{使符合标准 norm (= rule)}
  \wordfamily{normalization 正常化；normal 正常的；norm 标准}
  \wordsense{vt. 使正常化；使标准化}
  \wordphrase{normalize relations}
  \wordexample{The two countries agreed to normalize diplomatic relations.（两国同意实现外交关系正常化。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 【数/统】归一化；规范化}
  \wordphrase{normalize the data}
  \wordexample{You need to normalize the data before running the analysis.（运行分析前需要对数据进行归一化处理。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[+]{normative}{'nɔ:mətiv}
  \wordbreakdown{作为规范标准的 norm (= rule)}
  \wordfamily{norm 标准；normal 正常的；normalize 使正常化}
  \wordsense{a. 规范的；标准的}
  \wordphrase{normative grammar}
  \wordexample{Normative grammar prescribes how language should be used.（规范语法规定语言应该如何使用。）}
  \wordsense{a. 【伦理学】规范性的}
  \wordphrase{normative ethics}
  \wordexample{Normative ethics asks what we ought to do.（规范伦理学探讨我们应该做什么。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{subnormal}{,sʌb'nɔ:məl}
  \wordbreakdown{低于标准的 sub (= under) + norm (= rule)}
  \wordfamily{abnormal 异常的；normal 正常的；supernormal 超常的}
  \wordsense{a. 正常值以下的；低于标准的}
  \wordphrase{subnormal temperatures}
  \wordexample{Subnormal temperatures persisted throughout the winter.（整个冬天气温一直低于正常值。）}
  \wordsense{a. 智力低下的；低能的}
  \wordphrase{educationally subnormal}
  \wordexample{The school provides special programs for educationally subnormal children.（这所学校为智力发育迟缓的儿童提供特殊课程。）}
\end{word}



\chapter{UND}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根UND 来自拉丁语的und意为wave。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{abound}{[ə'baʊnd]}
  \wordbreakdown{用力荡漾 ab (= intensive) + und (= wave)}
  \wordfamily{abundance 丰富；abundant 丰富的；abundantly 丰富地}
  \wordsense{vi. 大量存在；富于}
  \wordphrase{abound with restaurants}
  \wordexample{This section of town abounds with Italian restaurants.（这个城区有很多意大利餐厅。）}
  \wordsense{vi. 充满；到处都是}
  \wordphrase{abound in wildlife}
  \wordexample{The forest abounds in wildlife of every kind.（这片森林里到处都是各种野生动物。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{inundate}{['inʌndeit]}
  \wordbreakdown{在某物上荡漾 in (= on, in) + und (= wave)}
  \wordfamily{inundation 泛滥；flood 洪水；deluge 大洪水}
  \wordsense{vt. 淹没；泛滥}
  \wordphrase{inundate the village}
  \wordexample{In September of 1999, floods inundated many Nigerian villages.（1999年9月，尼日利亚的许多村庄被洪水淹没。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 压倒；使应接不暇}
  \wordphrase{be inundated with requests}
  \wordexample{The office was inundated with phone calls after the announcement.（公告发布后，办公室被电话淹没了。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{redundant}{[ri'dʌndənt]}
  \wordbreakdown{继续荡漾 red<re (= back, again) + und (= wave)}
  \wordfamily{redundancy 多余；surplus 过剩的；superfluous 多余的}
  \wordsense{a. 多余的；累赘的}
  \wordphrase{redundant words}
  \wordexample{Stop berating me! You're being redundant.（不要再责备我了！你太啰唆了。）}
  \wordsense{a. 被裁减的；失业的（英式用法）}
  \wordphrase{be made redundant}
  \wordexample{Two hundred workers were made redundant when the factory closed.（工厂关闭时，两百名工人被裁员。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{undulate}{['ʌndjuleit]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 UND}
  \wordfamily{undulation 波动；undulating 起伏的；wave 波浪}
  \wordsense{vi. 呈波浪形；起伏}
  \wordphrase{undulating hills}
  \wordexample{A field of wheat undulating in the breeze is a beautiful sight.（微风中起伏的麦田是一道美丽的风景。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 使波动；使起伏}
  \wordphrase{undulate the surface}
  \wordexample{The wind undulated the tall grass across the plain.（风吹得平原上的高草起伏不定。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{undulation}{[,ʌndju'leiʃn]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 UND}
  \wordfamily{undulate 起伏；wave 波浪；ripple 涟漪}
  \wordsense{n. 波动；起伏}
  \wordphrase{gentle undulations}
  \wordexample{The gentle undulations of the landscape made cycling pleasant.（地形的平缓起伏使骑行变得愉快。）}
  \wordsense{n. 波浪形；波状}
  \wordphrase{undulations of the road}
  \wordexample{The undulations of the road made driving difficult at night.（道路的起伏使夜间驾驶变得困难。）}
\end{word}



\chapter{DIC, DICT}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的词根 dic，dict 意为 to say，to declare，to allot（此含义比较少用）等。有时 “c”（k）不发音。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 fa / fam / fess，locut / loqu，nounc / nunci，or，parl，来自希腊语的 phras，pha / phe / phu 等。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{abdicate}{'æbdikeit}
  \wordbreakdown{说要脱离某物 ab (= off) + dic (= say, proclaim)}
  \wordfamily{abdication 退位；abdicator 退位者；dedicate 奉献}
  \wordsense{vt. 放弃（权力等）；退（位）}
  \wordphrase{abdicate the throne}
  \wordexample{Despite the scandal, the mayor did not abdicate his office.（尽管发生了丑闻，市长却没有辞职。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 推卸（责任）}
  \wordphrase{abdicate responsibility}
  \wordexample{Parents must not abdicate responsibility for their children's education.（父母不能推卸对子女教育的责任。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{edict}{'i:dikt}
  \wordbreakdown{大声说出 e<ex (= out) + dict (= say)}
  \wordfamily{abdicate 退位；indictment 起诉；verdict 裁决}
  \wordsense{n. 敕令；法令}
  \wordphrase{issue an edict}
  \wordexample{Historically, royal edicts had to be obeyed by a monarch's subjects.（历史上，臣民必须服从君主的命令。）}
  \wordsense{n. 命令；布告}
  \wordphrase{government edict}
  \wordexample{The government issued an edict banning all public gatherings.（政府发布命令禁止一切公共集会。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{indictment}{in'daitmənt}
  \wordbreakdown{逆着某人说 in (= against) + dict (= say)}
  \wordfamily{indict 起诉；indictable 可起诉的；prosecution 起诉}
  \wordsense{n. 起诉；控告}
  \wordphrase{face indictment}
  \wordexample{The financier was deeply depressed after his indictment.（那位金融家被起诉后变得非常消沉。）}
  \wordsense{n. 起诉书；公诉书}
  \wordphrase{hand down an indictment}
  \wordexample{The grand jury handed down an indictment on three counts of fraud.（大陪审团就三项欺诈罪名提出了起诉书。）}
  \wordsense{n. 控诉；谴责（比喻义）}
  \wordphrase{an indictment of the system}
  \wordexample{The report is a damning indictment of the healthcare system.（这份报告是对医疗体系的严厉控诉。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{vindicate}{'vindikeit}
  \wordbreakdown{使有力地说 vin (= force) + dic (= say)}
  \wordfamily{vindication 证明无罪；vindictive 报复的；avenge 报仇}
  \wordsense{vt. 证明……正确；证明……无辜}
  \wordphrase{vindicate one's claim}
  \wordexample{Various GAO studies have vindicated the new welfare reform laws.（美国总审计局的各种研究证明了新福利法修正案的正确性。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 维护；辩护}
  \wordphrase{vindicate one's rights}
  \wordexample{She went to court to vindicate her rights.（她上法庭维护自己的权利。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{jurisdiction}{ˌdʒuəris'dikʃən}
  \wordbreakdown{法律说 jur (= law, swear) + dict (= say)}
  \wordfamily{jurisdictional 管辖权的；judicial 司法的；jurisprudence 法学}
  \wordsense{n. 司法权；裁判权}
  \wordphrase{federal jurisdiction}
  \wordexample{Does this case have state or federal jurisdiction?（该案件属州法院管辖还是属联邦法院管辖？）}
  \wordsense{n. 管辖权；管辖范围}
  \wordphrase{within one's jurisdiction}
  \wordexample{This matter falls outside the jurisdiction of the local court.（此事不在地方法院的管辖范围内。）}
  \wordsense{n. 管辖区域}
  \wordphrase{different jurisdictions}
  \wordexample{Tax laws differ across jurisdictions.（各管辖区域的税法各不相同。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{predicament}{pri'dikəmənt}
  \wordbreakdown{先说 pre (= before) + dic (= say)}
  \wordfamily{predict 预言；predicate 断言；dilemma 困境}
  \wordsense{n. 困境；窘境}
  \wordphrase{in a predicament}
  \wordexample{The world is in a real predicament with the proliferation of nuclear weapons.（世界因核武器扩散而陷入困境。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{malediction}{ˌmæli'dikʃən}
  \wordbreakdown{说得很坏 male<mal (= bad) + dict (= say)}
  \wordfamily{benediction 祝福；malevolent 恶意的；curse 诅咒}
  \wordsense{n. 诅咒；咒骂}
  \wordphrase{heap maledictions upon}
  \wordexample{Heaping maledictions upon her ex-husband, she stormed from the room.（她一边咒骂着前夫，一边冲出房间。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{indicative}{in'dikətiv}
  \wordbreakdown{在里边说的 in (= in, to) + dic (= say)}
  \wordfamily{indicate 指示；indication 暗示；indicator 指标}
  \wordsense{a. 表示的；暗示的}
  \wordphrase{indicative of a problem}
  \wordexample{The white color of the dog's gums is indicative of a circulatory problem.（狗的牙龈发白，说明循环系统有问题。）}
  \wordsense{a. 【语法】陈述语气的}
  \wordphrase{indicative mood}
  \wordexample{Most everyday sentences are in the indicative mood.（大多数日常句子使用陈述语气。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{addict}{'ædikt}
  \wordbreakdown{向……说→宣判→使屈从 ad (= to) + dict (= say)}
  \wordfamily{addiction（沉溺，上瘾）；addictive（使人上瘾的）；addicted（上瘾的）；abdicate（放弃（权力等））}
  \wordsense{n. 有瘾的人；入迷的人}
  \wordphrase{a drug addict}
  \wordexample{The clinic$'$s main goal is to help addicts recover.（该诊所的主要目标是帮助成瘾者康复。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 使沉溺；使成瘾}
  \wordphrase{be addicted to}
  \wordexample{He is addicted to smoking and finds it impossible to quit.（他吸烟成瘾，觉得戒不掉。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{benediction}{ˌbeni'dikʃən}
  \wordbreakdown{说好话→祝福 bene (= good) + dict (= say)}
  \wordfamily{benedictory 祝福的；malediction 诅咒；benevolent 仁慈的}
  \wordsense{n. 祝福；赐福祈祷}
  \wordphrase{pronounce a benediction}
  \wordexample{The priest/(牧师) pronounced the benediction at the end of the service.（牧师在礼拜结束时宣读了赐福祈祷。）}
  \wordsense{n. （饭前或饭后的）感恩祷告}
  \wordphrase{say the benediction}
  \wordexample{Who would like to say the benediction before dinner?（谁愿意在晚餐前做感恩祷告？）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{contradict}{ˌkɔntrə'dikt}
  \wordbreakdown{反着说 contra (= against) + dict (= say)}
  \wordfamily{contradiction 矛盾；contradictory 矛盾的；self-contradictory 自相矛盾的}
  \wordsense{vt. 反驳；否认}
  \wordphrase{contradict a statement}
  \wordexample{The witness$'$s testimony contradicted the defendant$'$s earlier claims.（证人的证词与被告先前的说法相矛盾。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 与……矛盾；与……抵触}
  \wordphrase{contradict each other}
  \wordexample{The two reports contradict each other on several key points.（两份报告在几个关键点上相互矛盾。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{contradiction}{ˌkɔntrə'dikʃən}
  \wordbreakdown{反着说→矛盾 contra (= against) + dict (= say)}
  \wordfamily{contradict 反驳；contradictory 矛盾的；self-contradiction 自相矛盾}
  \wordsense{n. 矛盾；不一致}
  \wordphrase{a contradiction in terms}
  \wordexample{``Jumbo shrimp'' is a contradiction in terms.（"巨型小虾"是一个自相矛盾的说法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 反驳；否认}
  \wordphrase{in contradiction to}
  \wordexample{His actions are in contradiction to his stated beliefs.（他的行为与他声称的信念相矛盾。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{contradictory}{ˌkɔntrə'diktəri}
  \wordbreakdown{反着说的→矛盾的 contra (= against) + dict (= say)}
  \wordfamily{contradict 反驳；contradiction 矛盾；contradistinction 对比}
  \wordsense{a. 矛盾的；对立的}
  \wordphrase{contradictory statements}
  \wordexample{The witnesses gave contradictory accounts of the accident.（目击者对事故的描述相互矛盾。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{dedicate}{'dedikeit}
  \wordbreakdown{完全地说出→奉献 de (= completely) + dic (= say, allot)}
  \wordfamily{dedicated 献身的；dedication 奉献，题词；dedicatory 奉献的}
  \wordsense{vt. 把……奉献给；献身于}
  \wordphrase{dedicate oneself to}
  \wordexample{She dedicated her life to helping the poor.（她把一生奉献给了帮助穷人。）}
  \wordsense{vt. （给著作）题献词}
  \wordphrase{dedicate a book to sb.}
  \wordexample{He dedicated his first novel to his mother.（他把第一部小说题献给了母亲。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{diction}{'dikʃən}
  \wordbreakdown{说的方式 dict (= say) + ion (= noun suffix)}
  \wordfamily{dictional 措辞的；elocution 演说术；enunciation 发音}
  \wordsense{n. 用语；措辞；发音}
  \wordphrase{clear diction}
  \wordexample{A good singer must have clear diction so the audience can understand the lyrics.（好歌手必须发音清晰，让听众能听懂歌词。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{dictate}{dik'teit}
  \wordbreakdown{反复说→命令 dict (= say) + ate (= verb suffix)}
  \wordfamily{dictation（听写）；dictator（独裁者）；dictatorial（专横的）；abdicate（放弃（权力等））}
  \wordsense{vt. 口述；使听写}
  \wordphrase{dictate a letter}
  \wordphrase{dictate terms}
  \wordexample{She dictated the letter to her secretary.（她向秘书口述了这封信。）}
  \wordexample{The victors$'$ dictated the terms of the peace treaty.（胜利者强行规定了和平条约的条款。）}
  \wordsense{n. [pl.] 命令；指示}
  \wordphrase{the dictates of conscience}
  \wordexample{He always follows the dictates of common sense.（他总是遵从常识的指引。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{dictation}{dik'teiʃən}
  \wordbreakdown{口述→听写 dict (= say) + ation (= noun suffix)}
  \wordfamily{dictate 口述；dictator 独裁者；diction 措辞}
  \wordsense{n. 听写；口述}
  \wordphrase{take dictation}
  \wordexample{The secretary took dictation from the manager every morning.（秘书每天早上听经理口述记录。）}
  \wordsense{n. 命令；指示}
  \wordphrase{at the dictation of}
  \wordexample{He acted at the dictation of his superiors.（他按上级的指示行事。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{dictator}{dik'teitə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{发号施令者 dict (= say) + ator (= one who)}
  \wordfamily{dictatorial 独裁的；dictatorship 独裁政权；dictate 命令}
  \wordsense{n. 独裁者；专制者}
  \wordphrase{a military dictator}
  \wordexample{The dictator ruled the country with an iron fist for thirty years.（独裁者以铁腕统治了这个国家三十年。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{dictatorial}{ˌdiktei'tɔ:riəl}
  \wordbreakdown{独裁者的 dictator (= dictator) + ial (= adj suffix)}
  \wordfamily{dictator 独裁者；dictatorship 独裁政权；authoritarian 专制的}
  \wordsense{a. 独裁的；专政的}
  \wordphrase{a dictatorial regime}
  \wordexample{The country suffered under a dictatorial regime for decades.（这个国家在独裁政权下受苦了几十年。）}
  \wordsense{a. 专横的；盛气凌人的}
  \wordphrase{a dictatorial manner}
  \wordexample{His dictatorial manner made him unpopular with colleagues.（他专横的态度使他不受同事欢迎。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{dictatorship}{dik'teitəʃip}
  \wordbreakdown{独裁者的地位 dictator (= dictator) + ship (= state)}
  \wordfamily{dictator 独裁者；dictatorial 独裁的；autocracy 专制}
  \wordsense{n. 独裁；专政；独裁国家}
  \wordphrase{a military dictatorship}
  \wordexample{The country transitioned from a military dictatorship to a democracy.（该国从军事独裁过渡到了民主制度。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{dictum}{'diktəm}
  \wordbreakdown{所说的话 dict (= say) + um (= Latin noun suffix)}
  \wordfamily{verdict 裁决；edict 法令；interdict 禁令}
  \wordsense{n. 正式声明；权威意见}
  \wordphrase{a papal dictum}
  \wordexample{The court$'$s dictum on the matter was widely cited.（法院对此事的正式声明被广泛引用。）}
  \wordsense{n. 格言；警句}
  \wordphrase{the old dictum that...}
  \wordexample{He lived by the old dictum that actions speak louder than words.（他信奉"行胜于言"这句老话。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{interdict}{ˌintə'dikt}
  \wordbreakdown{在中间说→阻断 inter (= between) + dict (= say)}
  \wordfamily{interdiction 禁止；interdictory 禁止的；prohibit 禁止}
  \wordsense{vt. 禁止；阻止}
  \wordphrase{interdict trade}
  \wordexample{The government interdicted all trade with the hostile nation.（政府禁止了与敌对国家的一切贸易。）}
  \wordsense{n. 禁令}
  \wordphrase{place an interdict on}
  \wordexample{The court placed an interdict on the publication of the documents.（法院对文件的发布下达了禁令。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{predicate}{'predikət}
  \wordbreakdown{预先说出→断言 pre (= before) + dic (= say)}
  \wordfamily{predicative（谓语的）；predication（断言）；predicament（困境）；abdicate（放弃（权力等））}
  \wordsense{n. 【语法】谓语}
  \wordphrase{the predicate of a sentence}
  \wordexample{In The dog barked loudly,'' barked loudly'' is the predicate.（在"狗大声叫"中，"大声叫"是谓语。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 断言；断定}
  \wordphrase{predicate sth. on/upon}
  \wordexample{The theory is predicated on the assumption that people act rationally.（该理论建立在人们理性行事的假设之上。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{predicatory}{pri'dikətəri}
  \wordbreakdown{在前面说的→传道的 pre (= before) + dic (= say)}
  \wordfamily{predicate 断言；preach 传道；predicant 传道者}
  \wordsense{a. 传道的；说教的}
  \wordphrase{predicatory literature}
  \wordexample{The early church produced a large body of predicatory literature.（早期教会产生了大量传道文献。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{predict}{pri'dikt}
  \wordbreakdown{预先说→预言 pre (= before) + dict (= say)}
  \wordfamily{prediction 预言；predictable 可预测的；unpredictable 不可预测的}
  \wordsense{vt. 预言；预测；预报}
  \wordphrase{predict the outcome}
  \wordexample{No one could have predicted the outcome of the election.（没有人能预测选举的结果。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{prediction}{pri'dikʃən}
  \wordbreakdown{预先说出的→预言 pre (= before) + dict (= say)}
  \wordfamily{predict 预言；predictive 预测的；predictable 可预测的}
  \wordsense{n. 预言；预测；预报}
  \wordphrase{make a prediction}
  \wordexample{His prediction that the market would crash proved accurate.（他关于市场会崩盘的预测被证明是准确的。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{predictable}{pri'diktəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{可预先说出的 pre (= before) + dict (= say) + able}
  \wordfamily{unpredictable 不可预测的；predict 预言；predictably 可预见地}
  \wordsense{a. 可预测的；可预料的}
  \wordphrase{a predictable result}
  \wordexample{The ending of the movie was entirely predictable.（这部电影的结局完全在意料之中。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{syndicate}{'sindikət}
  \wordbreakdown{一起说→联合 syn (= together) + dic (= say)}
  \wordfamily{syndication 联合组织；syndicated 联合发行的；cartel 卡特尔}
  \wordsense{n. 辛迪加；企业联合组织}
  \wordphrase{a crime syndicate}
  \wordexample{The police broke up a major crime syndicate operating across three states.（警方捣毁了一个跨三个州运作的大型犯罪集团。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 联合组成辛迪加；（向多家报刊）同时出售}
