  \wordphrase{syndicate a column}
  \wordexample{Her comic strip is syndicated in over 200 newspapers.（她的连环漫画在200多家报纸上联合刊载。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{valediction}{ˌvæli'dikʃən}
  \wordbreakdown{说再见→告别 vale (= farewell, worth) + dict (= say)}
  \wordfamily{valedictory 告别的；valedictorian 致告别辞的学生代表；vale 再见}
  \wordsense{n. 告别；告辞；告别辞}
  \wordphrase{a formal valediction}
  \wordexample{His valediction to the company was brief but heartfelt.（他对公司的告别辞简短而真挚。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{valedictory}{ˌvæli'diktəri}
  \wordbreakdown{告别的 vale (= farewell) + dict (= say) + ory (= adj suffix)}
  \wordfamily{valediction 告别；valedictorian 致告别辞的学生代表；farewell 告别}
  \wordsense{a. 告别的}
  \wordphrase{a valedictory speech}
  \wordexample{She delivered a moving valedictory speech at the graduation ceremony.（她在毕业典礼上发表了感人的告别演说。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{valedictorian}{ˌvæliˌdik'tɔ:riən}
  \wordbreakdown{致告别辞的人 vale (= farewell) + dict (= say) + orian}
  \wordfamily{valedictory 告别的；valediction 告别；salutatorian 致欢迎辞的学生代表}
  \wordsense{n. （毕业典礼上）致告别辞的学生代表（通常为最优秀学生）}
  \wordphrase{class valedictorian}
  \wordexample{She graduated as class valedictorian with a perfect GPA.（她以满分绩点作为班级致告别辞的学生代表毕业。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{verdict}{'və:dikt}
  \wordbreakdown{真实地说→裁决 ver (= true) + dict (= say)}
  \wordfamily{dictum 声明；verity 真实；verify 证实}
  \wordsense{n. （陪审团的）裁决；判决}
  \wordphrase{a verdict of guilty}
  \wordexample{The jury returned a verdict of not guilty.（陪审团作出了无罪裁决。）}
  \wordsense{n. 意见；判断}
  \wordphrase{give one's verdict on}
  \wordexample{What$'$s your verdict on the new restaurant?（你对那家新餐厅的评价如何？）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{vindictive}{vin'diktiv}
  \wordbreakdown{以力量说→报复的 vin (= force) + dict (= say) + ive}
  \wordfamily{vindicate 证明无罪；vengeance 报仇；vindication 辩护}
  \wordsense{a. 怀恨在心的；报复心强的}
  \wordphrase{vindictive behavior}
  \wordexample{She became vindictive after being passed over for promotion.（她在晋升被忽略后变得心怀怨恨。）}
\end{word}



\chapter{COND}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 cond 意为 to hide。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 cel [ceal, cul]，cover / cur，tect / teg 和来自希腊语的 crypt。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{abscond}{əb'skɔnd}
  \wordbreakdown{悄悄隐藏起来 abs/ab (= from, away) + cond (= hide)}
  \wordfamily{abscondence 逃亡；absconder 潜逃者；flee 逃跑}
  \wordsense{vi. 潜逃；逃亡}
  \wordphrase{abscond with the funds}
  \wordexample{The treasurer absconded with the company$'$s funds.（财务主管携公司资金潜逃了。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{recondite}{'rekəndait}
  \wordbreakdown{藏回去→深藏的 re (= back) + cond (= hide)}
  \wordfamily{abscond 潜逃；esoteric 深奥的；arcane 神秘的}
  \wordsense{a. 深奥的；晦涩难懂的}
  \wordphrase{recondite knowledge}
  \wordexample{The professor$'$s lectures were filled with recondite references that few students understood.（教授的讲座充满了深奥的引用，很少有学生能理解。）}
\end{word}



\chapter{VERT, VERS}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 vert，vers 意为 to turn，同义词根有 verg，rot 和来自希腊语的 stroph 等，以及来自盎格鲁-撒克逊语的 tour。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{avert}{ə'və:t}
  \wordbreakdown{转开→避开 a/ab (= away, from) + vert (= turn)}
  \wordfamily{avertible 可避免的；aversion 厌恶；divert 转移}
  \wordsense{vt. 转移（目光等）；避开}
  \wordphrase{avert one's eyes}
  \wordexample{I usually avert my eyes if I see someone stumble in the street.（如果看见有人在路上摔倒，我大都会向别处看。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 防止；避免}
  \wordphrase{avert a disaster}
  \wordexample{Quick action by the pilot averted a major disaster.（飞行员的快速反应避免了一场重大灾难。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{averse}{ə'və:s}
  \wordbreakdown{转离→厌恶 a/ab (= away, from) + vers (= turn)}
  \wordfamily{aversion 厌恶；aversive 引起反感的；adverse 不利的}
  \wordsense{a. 厌恶的；反对的}
  \wordphrase{averse to risk}
  \wordexample{He is not averse to publicity, unlike many of his colleagues.（与许多同事不同，他并不反感公众关注。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{adverse}{'ædvə:s}
  \wordbreakdown{转向对面→不利的 ad (= to) + vers (= turn)}
  \wordfamily{adversity 逆境；adversary 对手；adversely 不利地}
  \wordsense{a. 不利的；有害的}
  \wordphrase{adverse effects}
  \wordexample{She had an adverse reaction to the penicillin shot.（她注射青霉素后出现了不良反应。）}
  \wordsense{a. 相反的；敌对的}
  \wordphrase{adverse conditions}
  \wordexample{The expedition continued despite adverse weather conditions.（尽管天气条件恶劣，探险队仍继续前进。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{inadvertent}{ˌinəd'və:tənt}
  \wordbreakdown{不注意转向的→疏忽的 in (= not) + ad (= to) + vert (= turn)}
  \wordfamily{inadvertence 疏忽；inadvertently 无意地；advertent 注意的}
  \wordsense{a. 无意的；疏忽的}
  \wordphrase{an inadvertent error}
  \wordexample{I assure you, my error was entirely inadvertent.（我向你保证，我的失误绝不是故意的。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{conversant}{kən'və:sənt}
  \wordbreakdown{一起转→熟悉的 con/com (= together) + vers (= turn)}
  \wordfamily{converse 谈话；conversation 会话；conversational 会话的}
  \wordsense{a. 精通的；熟知的}
  \wordphrase{conversant with}
  \wordexample{My uncle is conversant with most major European languages, though fluent in none.（我叔叔精通大部分欧洲语言，虽然哪门语言说得都不流利。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{controversy}{'kɔntrəvə:si}
  \wordbreakdown{反着转→争论 contro/contra (= against) + vers (= turn)}
  \wordfamily{controversial 有争议的；controvert 反驳；controversialist 争辩者}
  \wordsense{n. 争论；争议}
  \wordphrase{controversy over}
  \wordexample{There$'$s a lot of controversy over whether to build a new high school or not.（是否成立新高中的问题引发了很多争论。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{diversity}{dai'və:səti}
  \wordbreakdown{分开转→多样性 di/dis (= apart) + vers (= turn)}
  \wordfamily{diversify 使多样化；diverse 多样的；diversification 多样化}
  \wordsense{n. 多样性；差异}
  \wordphrase{cultural diversity}
  \wordexample{The social and cultural diversity in the San Francisco area is remarkable.（旧金山地区的社会、文化多样性非常显著。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{divert}{dai'və:t}
  \wordbreakdown{分开转→转移 di/dis (= apart) + vert (= turn)}
  \wordfamily{diversion 转移，消遣；diversionary 转移注意力的；diverting 有趣的}
  \wordsense{vt. 使转向；使改道}
  \wordphrase{divert traffic}
  \wordexample{Traffic was diverted to a detour while roadwork was completed.（道路施工完工之前，车辆都绕道走。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 使消遣；使娱乐}
  \wordphrase{divert oneself with}
  \wordexample{The children diverted themselves with games while waiting.（孩子们在等待时玩游戏消遣。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{perverse}{pə'və:s}
  \wordbreakdown{彻底转偏→乖张的 per (= thoroughly, wrongly) + vers (= turn)}
  \wordfamily{perversity 乖张；perversion 歪曲；pervert 使堕落}
  \wordsense{a. 故意作对的；倔强的}
  \wordphrase{perverse pleasure}
  \wordexample{He has a perverse character, always making problems for everybody.（他故意和人作对，常常给每个人制造麻烦。）}
  \wordsense{a. 有悖常情的；不正当的}
  \wordphrase{a perverse verdict}
  \wordexample{The jury reached what many considered a perverse verdict.（陪审团作出了许多人认为有悖常理的裁决。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{revert}{ri'və:t}
  \wordbreakdown{转回去→恢复 re (= back) + vert (= turn)}
  \wordfamily{reversion 回复；reversible 可逆的；reverse 颠倒}
  \wordsense{vi. 恢复；回复原状}
  \wordphrase{revert to}
  \wordexample{When his mother leaves on a trip, he reverts to his old, lazy self.（当他母亲出去旅行时，他就会恢复到自己以前的懒模样。）}
  \wordsense{vi. （财产、权利）归还；归属}
  \wordphrase{revert to the state}
  \wordexample{If the owner dies without heirs, the property reverts to the state.（如果业主去世时没有继承人，财产归还国家。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{versatile}{'və:sətail}
  \wordbreakdown{能向各方向转→多才多艺的 vers (= turn) + atile (= able)}
  \wordfamily{versatility（多才多艺）；avert（转移（目光等））；averse（厌恶的）}
  \wordsense{a. 多才多艺的}
  \wordphrase{a versatile performer}
  \wordphrase{a versatile tool}
  \wordexample{She$'$s a versatile performer who can sing, dance, and make people laugh.（她是一个多才多艺的演员，唱歌、跳舞、逗乐，无所不能。）}
  \wordexample{This versatile kitchen gadget can chop, blend, and puree.（这个多功能厨房小工具可以切碎、搅拌和打成泥。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{advert}{əd'və:t}
  \wordbreakdown{转向→注意 ad (= to) + vert (= turn)}
  \wordfamily{advertise 做广告；advertisement 广告；advertiser 广告商}
  \wordsense{vi. 提及；谈到}
  \wordphrase{advert to a topic}
  \wordexample{He adverted briefly to the problem of inflation in his speech.（他在演讲中简要提到了通货膨胀问题。）}
  \wordsense{vi. 注意；留意}
  \wordphrase{advert to the danger}
  \wordexample{She adverted to the risks involved before making her decision.（她在做决定前注意到了其中的风险。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{advertising}{'ædvətaiziŋ}
  \wordbreakdown{使人转向→引起注意→广告 ad (= to) + vert (= turn)}
  \wordfamily{advertise 做广告；advertisement 广告；advertiser 广告商}
  \wordsense{n. 广告（业）}
  \wordphrase{advertising agency}
  \wordexample{She works for a major advertising agency in New York.（她在纽约一家大型广告公司工作。）}
  \wordsense{a. 广告的}
  \wordphrase{advertising expenditure}
  \wordexample{The company doubled its advertising expenditure this year.（公司今年将广告费翻了一番。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{conversion}{kən'və:ʃən}
  \wordbreakdown{完全转变 con/com (= completely) + vers (= turn)}
  \wordfamily{convert 转变；convertible 可转换的；reconversion 再转换}
  \wordsense{n. 转变；转换}
  \wordphrase{conversion to digital}
  \wordexample{The conversion of the factory to automated production took two years.（工厂向自动化生产的转变花了两年时间。）}
  \wordsense{n. 改变信仰；皈依}
  \wordphrase{conversion to Islam}
  \wordexample{His conversion to Buddhism surprised his family.（他皈依佛教让家人感到惊讶。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{convert}{kən'və:t}
  \wordbreakdown{完全转变 con/com (= completely) + vert (= turn)}
  \wordfamily{conversion（转变）；convertible（可转换的）；converter（转换器）；avert（转移（目光等））}
  \wordsense{vt. 转换；转变}
  \wordphrase{convert into}
  \wordphrase{convert sb. to Christianity}
  \wordexample{They converted the old warehouse into luxury apartments.（他们把旧仓库改建成了豪华公寓。）}
  \wordexample{Missionaries tried to convert the local population to Christianity.（传教士试图让当地人皈依基督教。）}
  \wordsense{n. 皈依者；改变信仰者}
  \wordphrase{a convert to vegetarianism}
  \wordexample{She$'$s a recent convert to vegetarianism.（她最近才转变为素食主义者。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{convertible}{kən'və:təbl}
  \wordbreakdown{可完全转变的 con/com (= completely) + vert (= turn) + ible}
  \wordfamily{convert 转变；conversion 转换；inconvertible 不可兑换的}
  \wordsense{a. 可转换的；可兑换的}
  \wordphrase{convertible currency}
  \wordexample{The yuan is not yet a fully convertible currency.（人民币尚未成为完全可兑换的货币。）}
  \wordsense{n. 敞篷汽车}
  \wordphrase{drive a convertible}
  \wordexample{He drove a red convertible along the coastal highway.（他开着一辆红色敞篷车沿海岸公路行驶。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{inverse}{'invə:s}
  \wordbreakdown{向内转→相反的 in (= in, against) + vers (= turn)}
  \wordfamily{invert 颠倒；inversion 倒置；inversely 相反地}
  \wordsense{a. 相反的；反向的}
  \wordphrase{in inverse proportion to}
  \wordexample{Happiness is often in inverse proportion to wealth.（幸福往往与财富成反比。）}
  \wordsense{n. 反面；相反的事物}
  \wordphrase{the inverse of}
  \wordexample{Subtraction is the inverse of addition.（减法是加法的逆运算。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{invert}{in'və:t}
  \wordbreakdown{向内转→颠倒 in (= in, against) + vert (= turn)}
  \wordfamily{inverse 相反的；inversion 倒置；inverted 颠倒的}
  \wordsense{vt. 使颠倒；使倒转；使反向}
  \wordphrase{invert the order}
  \wordexample{Invert the cake onto a plate and let it cool.（把蛋糕倒扣在盘子上让它冷却。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{inversion}{in'və:ʃən}
  \wordbreakdown{颠倒的状态 in (= in, against) + vers (= turn) + ion}
  \wordfamily{invert 颠倒；inverse 相反的；inverted 倒置的}
  \wordsense{n. 颠倒；倒置；【语法】倒装}
  \wordphrase{temperature inversion}
  \wordexample{A temperature inversion trapped the smog over the city.（逆温层将雾霾困在了城市上空。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{introvert}{'intrəvə:t}
  \wordbreakdown{向内转→内向 intro (= within) + vert (= turn)}
  \wordfamily{introverted 内向的；introversion 内向；extrovert 外向的人}
  \wordsense{n. 内向的人}
  \wordphrase{a shy introvert}
  \wordexample{As an introvert, she prefers reading at home to attending parties.（作为一个内向的人，她更喜欢在家读书而不是参加聚会。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{obverse}{'ɔbvə:s}
  \wordbreakdown{面对着转→正面 ob (= toward) + vers (= turn)}
  \wordfamily{reverse 反面；converse 相反的事物；inverse 反面}
  \wordsense{n. （硬币、奖章的）正面}
  \wordphrase{the obverse of a coin}
  \wordexample{The obverse of the coin bears the portrait of the queen.（硬币正面印有女王的肖像。）}
  \wordsense{n. 对立面；对应物}
  \wordphrase{the obverse of the argument}
  \wordexample{Love is the obverse of hate.（爱是恨的对立面。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{subvert}{səb'və:t}
  \wordbreakdown{从下面翻转→颠覆 sub (= under) + vert (= turn)}
  \wordfamily{subversion 颠覆；subversive 颠覆性的；overthrow 推翻}
  \wordsense{vt. 颠覆；破坏（政权、制度等）}
  \wordphrase{subvert the government}
  \wordexample{They were accused of trying to subvert the democratically elected government.（他们被指控试图颠覆民选政府。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{subversive}{səb'və:siv}
  \wordbreakdown{从下面翻转的→颠覆性的 sub (= under) + vers (= turn) + ive}
  \wordfamily{subvert 颠覆；subversion 颠覆；seditious 煽动性的}
  \wordsense{a. 颠覆性的；破坏性的}
  \wordphrase{subversive activities}
  \wordexample{He was arrested for distributing subversive literature.（他因散发颠覆性文件而被捕。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{tergiversate}{tə:'dʒə:və:seit}
  \wordbreakdown{转过背去→变节 tergi (= the back) + vers (= turn) + ate}
  \wordfamily{tergiversation 变节；apostate 叛教者；equivocate 含糊其辞}
  \wordsense{vi. 变节；改变立场；搪塞}
  \wordphrase{tergiversate on the issue}
  \wordexample{The politician tergiversated on the issue, refusing to commit to either side.（那位政客在这个问题上含糊其辞，拒绝表明立场。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{transverse}{'trænzvə:s}
  \wordbreakdown{横着转→横向的 trans (= across) + vers (= turn)}
  \wordfamily{traverse 横越；transversal 横断的；longitudinal 纵向的}
  \wordsense{a. 横向的；横断的}
  \wordphrase{transverse section}
  \wordexample{A transverse section of the spinal cord reveals its internal structure.（脊髓的横截面揭示了其内部结构。）}
  \wordsense{n. 横向物；横轴}
  \wordphrase{the transverse of an ellipse}
  \wordexample{The transverse of the bridge spans 200 meters.（桥的横向跨度为200米。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{traverse}{'trævə:s}
  \wordbreakdown{横着转过→横越 tra/trans (= across) + vers (= turn)}
  \wordfamily{transverse 横向的；traversal 横越；traversable 可横越的}
  \wordsense{vt. 横越；横贯}
  \wordphrase{traverse the desert}
  \wordexample{The expedition traversed the entire Sahara Desert on foot.（探险队徒步横穿了整个撒哈拉沙漠。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 详细研究；仔细检查}
  \wordphrase{traverse the issues}
  \wordexample{The committee traversed every aspect of the proposal before voting.（委员会在投票前仔细审查了提案的每个方面。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 【法律】否认；反驳}
  \wordphrase{traverse an allegation}
  \wordexample{The defendant traversed the plaintiff's claims in court.（被告在法庭上否认了原告的主张。）}
  \wordsense{n. 横越；横断；横木}
  \wordphrase{a difficult traverse}
  \wordexample{The traverse across the glacier took three days.（横穿冰川花了三天时间。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{universal}{ˌju:ni'və:sl}
  \wordbreakdown{转向一体→普遍的 uni (= one) + vers (= turn) + al}
  \wordfamily{universe 宇宙；universality 普遍性；universally 普遍地}
  \wordsense{a. 普遍的；全世界的}
  \wordphrase{universal rules}
  \wordexample{The desire for freedom is a universal human trait.（对自由的渴望是人类普遍的特征。）}
  \wordsense{a. 通用的；万能的}
  \wordphrase{universal remote}
  \wordexample{This universal adapter works in any country.（这个万能适配器在任何国家都能用。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{versed}{və:st}
  \wordbreakdown{反复转→精通的 vers (= turn) + ed}
  \wordfamily{verse 诗；version 版本；versatile 多才多艺的}
  \wordsense{a. 精通的；熟练的}
  \wordphrase{well versed in}
  \wordexample{She is well versed in international law.（她精通国际法。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{version}{'və:ʃən}
  \wordbreakdown{转变→版本 vers (= turn) + ion}
  \wordfamily{versed 精通的；reversion 回复；aversion 厌恶}
  \wordsense{n. 版本}
  \wordphrase{a new version}
  \wordexample{A new version of the software will be released next month.（软件的新版本将于下月发布。）}
  \wordsense{n. 说法；描述}
  \wordphrase{his version of events}
  \wordexample{His version of events differs greatly from hers.（他对事件的说法与她的大相径庭。）}
  \wordsense{n. 改编形式；译本}
  \wordphrase{a film version of}
  \wordexample{The film version of the novel was a huge success.（这部小说的电影版大获成功。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{vertex}{'və:teks}
  \wordbreakdown{转向的最高点→顶点 vert (= turn) + ex (= noun suffix)}
  \wordfamily{vertical 垂直的；vertigo 眩晕；vertices 顶点（复数）}
  \wordsense{n. 顶点；最高点}
  \wordphrase{the vertex of a triangle}
  \wordexample{The vertex of the mountain was shrouded in clouds.（山顶笼罩在云雾中。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{vertical}{'və:tikl}
  \wordbreakdown{朝顶点方向的→垂直的 vert (= turn, top) + ical}
  \wordfamily{vertex 顶点；vertically 垂直地；horizontal 水平的}
  \wordsense{a. 垂直的；竖的}
  \wordphrase{a vertical line}
  \wordexample{The cliff rose in an almost vertical wall of rock.（悬崖几乎是一面垂直的岩壁。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{vertigo}{'və:tigəu}
  \wordbreakdown{头转→眩晕 vert (= turn) + igo (= condition)}
  \wordfamily{vertiginous 令人眩晕的；dizziness 头晕；giddiness 眩晕}
  \wordsense{n. 眩晕；头晕}
  \wordphrase{suffer from vertigo}
  \wordexample{She suffers from vertigo and cannot stand on high balconies.（她有眩晕症，无法站在高处的阳台上。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{vortex}{'vɔ:teks}
  \wordbreakdown{旋转→旋涡 vor/vert (= turn) + ex}
  \wordfamily{vertex 顶点；vortical 旋涡的；whirlpool 漩涡}
  \wordsense{n. 旋涡；漩涡}
  \wordphrase{the vortex of war}
  \wordexample{The country was drawn into the vortex of civil war.（这个国家被卷入了内战的旋涡。）}
\end{word}



\chapter{HOR}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的词根 hor 意为 to shudder，to bound，hour 等。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{abhor}{əb'hɔ:(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{因恐惧而远离 ab (= away, from) + hor (= shudder)}
  \wordfamily{abhorrence 憎恶；abhorrent 可恶的；loathe 厌恶}
  \wordsense{vt. 厌恶；憎恶}
  \wordphrase{abhor violence}
  \wordexample{The only character flaw I abhor is dishonesty.（我唯一憎恶的性格缺陷就是不诚实。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{horrible}{'hɔrəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{令人发抖的→可怕的 hor (= shudder) + ible (= able)}
  \wordfamily{horribly 可怕地；horror 恐怖；horrify 使恐惧}
  \wordsense{a. 可怕的；令人毛骨悚然的}
  \wordphrase{a horrible accident}
  \wordexample{The survivors described the horrible cruelty of the attackers.（幸存者描述了袭击者可怕的残忍行为。）}
  \wordsense{a. 糟透的；极不愉快的}
  \wordphrase{horrible weather}
  \wordexample{The food at that restaurant was absolutely horrible.（那家餐厅的食物简直糟透了。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{horrid}{'hɔrid}
  \wordbreakdown{令人发抖的 hor (= shudder) + id (= adj suffix)}
  \wordfamily{horrible 可怕的；horror 恐怖；horrify 使恐惧}
  \wordsense{a. 可怕的；恐怖的}
  \wordphrase{a horrid sight}
  \wordexample{The abandoned house had a horrid look about it.（那栋废弃的房子看起来阴森恐怖。）}
  \wordsense{a. 极讨厌的；令人不快的}
  \wordphrase{horrid weather}
  \wordexample{What horrid weather we$'$re having today!（今天的天气真糟糕！）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{horrific}{hɔ'rifik}
  \wordbreakdown{使人发抖的→恐怖的 hor (= shudder) + ific (= making)}
  \wordfamily{horrify 使恐惧；horrible 可怕的；terrific 极好的/可怕的}
  \wordsense{a. 极其可怕的；恐怖的；骇人听闻的}
  \wordphrase{a horrific crash}
  \wordexample{The horrific images from the war zone shocked the world.（来自战区的恐怖画面震惊了全世界。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{horrify}{'hɔrifai}
  \wordbreakdown{使人发抖 hor (= shudder) + ify (= make)}
  \wordfamily{horrifying 令人恐惧的；horrified 惊骇的；horror 恐怖}
  \wordsense{vt. 使恐惧；使惊骇}
  \wordphrase{horrified by the news}
  \wordexample{We were horrified to learn of the extent of the damage.（得知损失的程度后我们惊骇不已。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 使反感；使厌恶}
  \wordphrase{horrify the audience}
  \wordexample{His table manners horrified his hosts.（他的餐桌礼仪让主人感到反感。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{horror}{'hɔrə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{发抖→恐怖 hor (= shudder) + or (= noun suffix)}
  \wordfamily{horrible 可怕的；horrify 使恐惧；horrid 恐怖的}
  \wordsense{n. 恐怖；恐惧感}
  \wordphrase{a horror film}
  \wordexample{She stared in horror at the scene of the accident.（她惊恐地盯着事故现场。）}
  \wordsense{n. 令人恐怖的事物}
  \wordphrase{the horrors of war}
  \wordexample{The documentary revealed the horrors of the concentration camps.（纪录片揭示了集中营的恐怖。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{aphorism}{'æfərizəm}
  \wordbreakdown{划定界限→定义→格言 apo (= away, from) + hor (= bound)}
  \wordfamily{aphorist 格言作者；aphoristic 格言式的；maxim 格言}
  \wordsense{n. 箴言；格言；警句}
  \wordphrase{a well-known aphorism}
  \wordexample{``Less is more'' is a famous aphorism in architecture.（"少即是多"是建筑学中一句著名的格言。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{horizon}{hə'raizn}
  \wordbreakdown{界限→地平线 hor (= bound) + izon}
  \wordfamily{horizontal（水平的）；broaden（one's horizons 开阔视野）；abhor（厌恶）}
  \wordsense{n. 地平线}
  \wordphrase{on the horizon}
  \wordexample{A ship appeared on the horizon.（一艘船出现在地平线上。）}
  \wordsense{n. [pl.] 眼界；视野；范围}
  \wordphrase{broaden one's horizons}
  \wordexample{Travel broadens your horizons.（旅行能开阔你的视野。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{horizontal}{ˌhɔri'zɔntl}
  \wordbreakdown{地平线的→水平的 horizon (= horizon) + tal}
  \wordfamily{horizon 地平线；horizontally 水平地；vertical 垂直的}
  \wordsense{a. 水平的；地平线的}
  \wordphrase{a horizontal line}
  \wordexample{Draw a horizontal line across the page.（在页面上画一条水平线。）}
  \wordsense{n. 水平线；水平面}
  \wordphrase{above the horizontal}
  \wordexample{The plane climbed steeply above the horizontal.（飞机急剧爬升超过了水平面。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{horology}{hɔ'rɔlədʒi}
  \wordbreakdown{关于时间的学问 hor (= hour) + ology (= study of)}
  \wordfamily{horologist 钟表学家；horologe 时钟；chronology 年代学}
  \wordsense{n. 钟表学；钟表制造术}
  \wordphrase{a museum of horology}
  \wordexample{He devoted his career to horology and became a master watchmaker.（他毕生致力于钟表学，成为了一名制表大师。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{horoscope}{'hɔrəskəup}
  \wordbreakdown{观察时辰→占星 hor (= hour) + scope (= observe)}
  \wordfamily{horology 钟表学；telescope 望远镜；astrology 占星术}
  \wordsense{n. 星象；占星术；天宫图}
  \wordphrase{read one's horoscope}
  \wordexample{She reads her horoscope in the newspaper every morning.（她每天早上都看报纸上的星座运势。）}
\end{word}



\chapter{SOLV, SOLU}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 solv，solu 意为 to loosen，to free。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 franc，liber / liver，lys / lyt 等。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{absolve}{əb'zɔlv}
  \wordbreakdown{使脱离→免除 ab (= from) + solv (= loosen)}
  \wordfamily{absolution 赦免；absolute 绝对的；resolve 解决}
  \wordsense{vt. 使免除（责任、义务等）}
  \wordphrase{absolve sb. of blame}
  \wordexample{The investigation absolved him of all blame.（调查免除了他的一切责任。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 宽恕；赦免}
  \wordphrase{absolve sb. of sin}
  \wordexample{Children usually absolve their parents when they reach adulthood.（孩子们在长大成人之后大都会原谅自己的父母。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{irresolute}{i'rezəlu:t}
  \wordbreakdown{无法解开的→犹豫的 ir/in (= not) + re (= back) + solu (= loosen)}
  \wordfamily{irresolution 犹豫不决；resolute 坚决的；resolution 决心}
  \wordsense{a. 优柔寡断的；犹豫不决的}
  \wordphrase{an irresolute leader}
  \wordexample{This committee is frustratingly irresolute. Can$'$t we please just vote now?（委员会太优柔寡断了，我们不能现在就投票吗？）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{resolution}{ˌrezə'lu:ʃən}
  \wordbreakdown{完全解开→决心 re (= intensive) + solu (= loosen) + tion}
  \wordfamily{resolve 决定；resolute 坚决的；irresolution 犹豫}
  \wordsense{n. 决心；决定}
  \wordphrase{make a resolution}
  \wordexample{She made a New Year$'$s resolution to exercise more.（她下了新年决心要多锻炼。）}
  \wordsense{n. 解决；解答}
  \wordphrase{conflict resolution}
  \wordexample{If we work toward a resolution in a spirit of cooperation, I know we$'$ll succeed.（如果我们能够同心协力解决问题的话，我相信我们会成功的。）}
  \wordsense{n. 决议}
  \wordphrase{pass a resolution}
  \wordexample{The UN passed a resolution condemning the invasion.（联合国通过了一项谴责入侵的决议。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{solvent}{'sɔlvənt}
  \wordbreakdown{能溶解的 solv (= loosen) + ent (= adj/noun suffix)}
  \wordfamily{solvency 偿付能力；insolvent 破产的；solve 解决}
  \wordsense{a. 有偿付能力的}
  \wordphrase{remain solvent}
  \wordexample{Once I$'$m solvent, I promise to pay you everything I owe.（一旦我有了偿付能力，我承诺一定将我所亏欠的全部偿还给你。）}
  \wordsense{n. 溶剂}
  \wordphrase{an organic solvent}
  \wordexample{Use a solvent to remove the paint from the surface.（用溶剂去除表面的油漆。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{absolute}{'æbsəlu:t}
  \wordbreakdown{完全松开的→不受限制的 ab (= away) + solu (= loosen) + te}
  \wordfamily{absolutely 绝对地；absolutism 专制主义；absolve 赦免}
  \wordsense{a. 绝对的；完全的}
  \wordphrase{absolute power}
  \wordexample{Absolute power corrupts absolutely.（绝对的权力导致绝对的腐败。）}
  \wordsense{a. 专制的；独裁的}
  \wordphrase{an absolute monarch}
  \wordexample{Louis XIV was an absolute monarch who ruled France for 72 years.（路易十四是一位统治法国72年的专制君主。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{dissolve}{di'zɔlv}
  \wordbreakdown{分开松开→溶解 dis (= apart) + solv (= loosen)}
  \wordfamily{dissolution 解散；dissoluble 可溶解的；dissolute 放荡的}
  \wordsense{vt. 溶解；使融化}
  \wordphrase{dissolve in water}
  \wordexample{Dissolve the sugar in warm water before adding it to the mixture.（先将糖溶解在温水中，再加入混合物。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 解散；终止}
  \wordphrase{dissolve parliament}
  \wordexample{The president dissolved parliament and called for new elections.（总统解散了议会并宣布重新选举。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{dissoluble}{di'sɔljubl}
  \wordbreakdown{可分开松开的→可溶解的 dis (= apart) + solu (= loosen) + ble}
  \wordfamily{dissolve 溶解；indissoluble 不可溶解的；soluble 可溶的}
  \wordsense{a. 可溶解的；可分解的；可解散的}
  \wordphrase{a dissoluble compound}
  \wordexample{The contract is dissoluble by either party with thirty days$'$ notice.（任何一方提前三十天通知即可解除合同。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{dissolute}{'disəlju:t}
  \wordbreakdown{松散开→放纵 dis (= apart) + solu (= loosen) + -te}
  \wordfamily{dissolution 解散；dissolve 溶解；dissoluble 可溶解的}
  \wordsense{a. 放荡的，放纵的}
  \wordphrase{lead a dissolute life}
  \wordexample{He led a dissolute life after inheriting the fortune.（他继承遗产后过着放荡不羁的生活。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{dissolution}{ˌdisə'lu:ʃən}
  \wordbreakdown{松散开→分解 dis (= apart) + solu (= loosen) + -tion (名词后缀)}
  \wordfamily{dissolute 放荡的；dissolve 溶解；soluble 可溶的}
  \wordsense{n. 溶解；分解}
  \wordphrase{the dissolution of salt in water}
  \wordexample{The dissolution of calcium in acid produces hydrogen gas.（钙在酸中溶解会产生氢气。）}
  \wordsense{n. 解散；取消}
  \wordphrase{the dissolution of parliament}
  \wordexample{The prime minister announced the dissolution of parliament.（首相宣布解散议会。）}
  \wordsense{n. 瓦解；终结}
  \wordphrase{the dissolution of a marriage}
  \wordexample{The dissolution of their partnership was inevitable.（他们合伙关系的终结是不可避免的。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{insoluble}{in'sɔljubl}
  \wordbreakdown{不能松开→不能溶解 in (= not) + solu (= loosen) + -ble (= able)}
  \wordfamily{soluble 可溶的；dissolution 溶解；insolvent 破产的}
  \wordsense{a.（物质）不溶的，不易溶解的}
  \wordphrase{insoluble in water}
  \wordexample{Calcium carbonate is virtually insoluble in water.（碳酸钙几乎不溶于水。）}
  \wordsense{a.（问题）不可解决的；难以解释的}
  \wordphrase{an insoluble problem}
  \wordexample{The conflict seemed insoluble without outside intervention.（没有外部干预，这场冲突似乎无法解决。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{insolvent}{in'sɔlvənt}
  \wordbreakdown{不能偿还→破产 in (= not) + solv (= loosen, pay) + -ent}
  \wordfamily{insolvency 破产；solvent 有偿付能力的；dissolve 溶解}
  \wordsense{a. 无偿付能力的，破产的}
  \wordphrase{declare oneself insolvent}
  \wordexample{The company was declared insolvent after failing to pay its debts.（该公司因无力偿还债务而被宣布破产。）}
  \wordsense{n. 破产者，无力偿债者}
  \wordphrase{an insolvent debtor}
  \wordexample{The court appointed a trustee to manage the insolvent\textquotesingle s assets.（法院指定了一名受托人来管理破产者的资产。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{soluble}{'sɔljubl}
  \wordbreakdown{能松开→能溶解 solu (= loosen) + -ble (= able)}
  \wordfamily{insoluble 不溶的；solution 溶液；dissolve 溶解}
  \wordsense{a. 可溶解的}
  \wordphrase{soluble in water}
  \wordexample{Sugar is readily soluble in warm water.（糖很容易溶于温水。）}
  \wordsense{a. 可解决的，可解释的}
  \wordphrase{a soluble problem}
  \wordexample{With enough data, the mystery became soluble.（有了足够的数据，这个谜团变得可以解释了。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{solution}{sə'lu:ʃən}
  \wordbreakdown{松开→解开 solu (= loosen) + -tion (名词后缀)}
  \wordfamily{solve 解决；soluble 可溶的；dissolve 溶解}
  \wordsense{n. 解答，解决方法}
  \wordphrase{find a solution to the problem}
  \wordexample{Diplomacy is the best solution to a quarrel between two countries.（外交是解决两国纷争的最佳策略。）}
  \wordsense{n. 溶液；溶解}
  \wordphrase{a solution of salt in water}
  \wordexample{Stir until you get a complete solution of sugar in tea.（搅拌直到糖在茶中完全溶解。）}
\end{word}



\chapter{TOM[TEM]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自希腊语的 tom 意为 to cut。tom 里的元音字母 o 弱化成 e，变成了 tem。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 cis，cop / coup，scind / scis，sect，sever，tail，tons，trench 等。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{abstemious}{əb'sti:miəs}
  \wordbreakdown{砍断使分离→节制 abs (= away, from) + tem (= cut) + -ious}
  \wordfamily{abstemiousness 节制；temperance 节制；contempt 蔑视}
  \wordsense{a. 有节制的（尤指饮食）；节约的}
  \wordphrase{an abstemious lifestyle}
  \wordexample{Being abstemious, the old gentleman wasted little money on entertaining.（那位老绅士特别节俭，几乎没有在娱乐方面浪费过钱。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{dichotomy}{dai'kɔtəmi}
  \wordbreakdown{切成两部分 di (= two) + cho (= into) + tom (= cut) + -y}
  \wordfamily{anatomy 解剖学；atomic 原子的；atomize 使雾化}
  \wordsense{n. 两分法；对立；分裂}
  \wordphrase{the dichotomy between theory and practice}
  \wordexample{Playwright Tennessee Williams could never reconcile the spirit-flesh dichotomy.（剧作家田纳西·威廉斯始终无法调和精神和肉体的分裂。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{anatomy}{ə'nætəmi}
  \wordbreakdown{完全切开→解剖 ana (= up, completely) + tom (= cut) + -y}
  \wordfamily{anatomical（解剖的）；anatomist（解剖学者）；dichotomy（二分法）；abstemious（有节制的（尤指饮食））}
  \wordsense{n. 解剖学}
  \wordphrase{study anatomy}
  \wordphrase{the anatomy of a political scandal}
  \wordexample{Medical students must study anatomy in their first year.（医学生必须在第一年学习解剖学。）}
  \wordexample{This study provides a detailed anatomy of the financial crisis.（这本书对金融危机进行了详细剖析。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{atomic}{ə'tɔmik}
  \wordbreakdown{不可切割的→原子的 a (= not) + tom (= cut) + -ic}
  \wordfamily{atom 原子；atomize 使雾化；anatomy 解剖学}
  \wordsense{a. 原子的；原子能的}
  \wordphrase{atomic energy}
  \wordexample{The atomic bomb was first used in 1945.（原子弹于1945年首次使用。）}
  \wordsense{a. 极小的，微小的}
  \wordphrase{atomic particles}
  \wordexample{Scientists study atomic particles to understand the universe.（科学家研究微观粒子以理解宇宙。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{atomize}{'ætəmaiz}
  \wordbreakdown{使成原子→使分裂 a (= not) + tom (= cut) + -ize}
  \wordfamily{atomizer 喷雾器；atomic 原子的；atom 原子}
  \wordsense{v. 使分裂为原子；使成雾状}
  \wordphrase{atomize the liquid}
  \wordexample{The nozzle atomizes the fuel before it enters the combustion chamber.（喷嘴在燃料进入燃烧室之前将其雾化。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{appendectomy}{əˌpen'dektəmi}
  \wordbreakdown{切除附属物→阑尾切除 ap (= near) + pend (= hang) + ec (= out) + tom (= cut) + -y}
  \wordfamily{hysterotomy 子宫切开术；vasectomy 输精管切除术；anatomy 解剖学}
  \wordsense{n.【医】阑尾切除手术}
  \wordphrase{undergo an appendectomy}
  \wordexample{He had an emergency appendectomy last night.（他昨晚做了紧急阑尾切除手术。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{entomology}{ˌentə'mɔlədʒi}
  \wordbreakdown{切入→有切口的虫子→昆虫学 en (= into) + tom (= cut) + -ology (= study of)}
  \wordfamily{entomological 昆虫学的；entomologist 昆虫学者；anatomy 解剖学}
  \wordsense{n. 昆虫学}
  \wordphrase{study entomology}
  \wordexample{She devoted her career to entomology, specializing in butterflies.（她毕生致力于昆虫学，专攻蝴蝶研究。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{epitome}{i'pitəmi}
  \wordbreakdown{切在表面→摘要→缩影 epi (= on, upon) + tom (= cut) + -e}
  \wordfamily{epitomize 成为缩影；anatomy 解剖学；dichotomy 二分法}
  \wordsense{n. 缩影；典型；象征}
  \wordphrase{the epitome of elegance}
  \wordexample{She is the epitome of a loving mother.（她是慈母的典型。）}
  \wordsense{n.（书等的）摘要，梗概}
  \wordphrase{an epitome of world history}
  \wordexample{The pamphlet is an epitome of the full report.（这本小册子是完整报告的摘要。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{epitomize}{i'pitəmaiz}
  \wordbreakdown{epi (= on) + tom (= cut) + -ize (动词后缀)}
  \wordfamily{epitome 缩影；anatomy 解剖学；atomize 雾化}
  \wordsense{v. 成为……的缩影；是……的典型}
  \wordphrase{epitomize the spirit of the age}
  \wordexample{She epitomizes the modern working woman.（她是现代职业女性的典型。）}
  \wordsense{v. 写……的摘要}
  \wordphrase{epitomize a long report}
  \wordexample{He epitomized the three-volume work into a single chapter.（他将三卷本著作缩写成了一章。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{hysterotomy}{ˌhistə'rɔtəmi}
  \wordbreakdown{切开子宫 hystero (= womb) + tom (= cut) + -y}
  \wordfamily{appendectomy 阑尾切除术；vasectomy 输精管切除术；anatomy 解剖学}
  \wordsense{n.【医】子宫切开术}
  \wordphrase{perform a hysterotomy}
  \wordexample{The surgeon performed a hysterotomy to deliver the baby.（外科医生进行了子宫切开术来接生婴儿。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{vasectomy}{væ'sektəmi}
  \wordbreakdown{切除管道 vas (= vessel) + ec (= out) + tom (= cut) + -y}
  \wordfamily{hysterotomy 子宫切开术；appendectomy 阑尾切除术；anatomy 解剖学}
  \wordsense{n.【医】输精管切除术}
  \wordphrase{have a vasectomy}
  \wordexample{He decided to have a vasectomy after their third child.（第三个孩子出生后，他决定做输精管切除术。）}
\end{word}



\chapter{JECT, JAC[JET]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 ject，jac 意为 to throw，jet 是其变形。同义词根有来自希腊语的 bol [bl, bal] 和来自拉丁语的 lanc / launc，sip 等。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{abject}{'æbdʒekt}
  \wordbreakdown{扔掉使脱离→卑下 ab (= from) + ject (= throw)}
  \wordfamily{abjection 落魄；abjectly 悲惨地；eject 驱逐}
  \wordsense{a. 悲惨的，糟透的}
  \wordphrase{abject poverty}
  \wordexample{The prisoner of war was kept in abject circumstances.（战俘被关押在恶劣的环境中。）}
  \wordsense{a. 卑劣的；自卑的}
  \wordphrase{an abject apology}
  \wordexample{He made an abject apology for his rude behavior.（他为自己的粗鲁行为卑躬屈膝地道歉。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{adjacent}{ə'dʒeisnt}
  \wordbreakdown{扔得很近→邻近 ad (= to, near) + jac (= throw) + -ent}
  \wordfamily{adjacency 邻近；adjective 形容词；eject 驱逐}
  \wordsense{a. 邻近的；相邻的}
  \wordphrase{adjacent to the park}
  \wordexample{The chicken restaurant is adjacent to the dry cleaners.（那家鸡肉馆挨着干洗店。）}
  \wordsense{a. 【数学】（角、边）邻的}
  \wordphrase{adjacent angles}
  \wordexample{Adjacent angles share a common side and vertex.（邻角共用一条边和一个顶点。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{conjecture}{kən'dʒektʃə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{一起扔出→猜测 con (= together) + ject (= throw) + -ure}
  \wordfamily{conjectural 猜测的；inject 注射；project 投射}
  \wordsense{n. 猜测；推测；臆测}
  \wordphrase{a matter of conjecture}
  \wordexample{Without any scientific data, the best he could do was conjecture.（由于没有任何科学资料，他至多只能推测。）}
  \wordsense{v. 猜测；推测}
  \wordphrase{conjecture about the future}
  \wordexample{Scientists can only conjecture about what happened millions of years ago.（科学家只能推测数百万年前发生了什么。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{eject}{i:'dʒekt}
  \wordbreakdown{向外扔→驱逐 e (= out) + ject (= throw)}
  \wordfamily{ejection 逐出；ejector 喷射器；inject 注入}
  \wordsense{vt. 逐出，驱逐}
  \wordphrase{eject sb. from the stadium}
  \wordexample{After committing 5 fouls, a basketball player is ejected from the game.（篮球选手犯规五次就要被罚下赛场。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 喷射（液体等），吐出}
  \wordphrase{eject lava}
  \wordexample{The volcano ejected clouds of ash and molten rock.（火山喷出了大量火山灰和熔岩。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{adjective}{'ædʒiktiv}
  \wordbreakdown{附加扔上去→修饰词 ad (= to, near) + ject (= throw) + -ive}
  \wordfamily{adjacent 邻近的；adject 附加；inject 注射}
  \wordsense{n. 形容词}
  \wordphrase{descriptive adjective}
  \wordexample{In English, adjectives usually precede the nouns they modify.（在英语中，形容词通常放在所修饰名词的前面。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{deject}{di'dʒekt}
  \wordbreakdown{向下扔→使沮丧 de (= down) + ject (= throw)}
  \wordfamily{dejected 沮丧的；dejection 灰心；reject 拒绝}
  \wordsense{v. 使灰心，使沮丧}
  \wordphrase{be dejected by failure}
  \wordexample{Don\textquotesingle t let one failure deject you from trying again.（不要因为一次失败就灰心丧气不再尝试。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{dejection}{di'dʒekʃən}
  \wordbreakdown{de (= down) + ject (= throw) + -ion}
  \wordfamily{deject 使沮丧；dejected 沮丧的；reject 拒绝}
  \wordsense{n. 灰心，沮丧}
  \wordphrase{sink into dejection}
  \wordexample{After the defeat, the team sank into deep dejection.（失败之后，全队陷入了深深的沮丧。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{ejaculate}{i'dʒækjuleit}
  \wordbreakdown{向外投射 e (= out) + jacul (= throw) + -ate}
  \wordfamily{ejaculation 惊叹；inject 注射；eject 驱逐}
  \wordsense{v. 突然说出，惊叫}
  \wordphrase{ejaculate in surprise}
  \wordexample{``Good heavens!'' he ejaculated.（"天哪！"他惊叫道。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{ejaculation}{iˌdʒækju'leiʃən}
  \wordbreakdown{e (= out) + jacul (= throw) + -ation}
  \wordfamily{ejaculate 惊叫；exclamation 感叹；interjection 插话}
  \wordsense{n. 突然的喊叫；惊叹}
  \wordphrase{an ejaculation of surprise}
  \wordexample{His sudden ejaculation startled everyone in the room.（他突然的惊叫吓了屋里所有人一跳。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{inject}{in'dʒekt}
  \wordbreakdown{向里扔→注入 in (= into) + ject (= throw)}
  \wordfamily{injection 注射；eject 驱逐；project 投射}
  \wordsense{v. 注射（液体、药等）}
  \wordphrase{inject penicillin into the bloodstream}
  \wordexample{The nurse injected the vaccine into his arm.（护士将疫苗注射到他的手臂中。）}
  \wordsense{v. 注入（活力、资金等）}
  \wordphrase{inject new life into}
  \wordexample{The new CEO injected fresh energy into the company.（新任CEO为公司注入了新的活力。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{injection}{in'dʒekʃən}
  \wordbreakdown{in (= into) + ject (= throw) + -ion}
  \wordfamily{inject 注射；eject 驱逐；rejection 拒绝}
  \wordsense{n. 注射；注射剂}
  \wordphrase{give sb. an injection}
  \wordexample{The doctor gave him five injections of glucose.（医生给他注射了五支葡萄糖。）}
  \wordsense{n. 注入（资金等）}
  \wordphrase{a cash injection}
  \wordexample{The company needs a massive injection of capital to survive.（公司需要大量注入资金才能存活。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{interject}{ˌintə'dʒekt}
  \wordbreakdown{在中间扔入→插话 inter (= between) + ject (= throw)}
  \wordfamily{interjection 感叹词；inject 注射；eject 驱逐}
  \wordsense{v. 突然插话；插入（评论）}
  \wordphrase{interject a remark}
  \wordexample{``That\textquotesingle s not true!'' she interjected angrily.（"那不是真的！"她愤怒地插嘴道。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{jettison}{'dʒetisn}
  \wordbreakdown{扔出去→抛弃 jet (= throw) + -ison}
  \wordfamily{jetty 码头；eject 驱逐；project 投射}
  \wordsense{v. 投弃（货物以减轻船的负载）}
  \wordphrase{jettison the cargo}
  \wordexample{The crew jettisoned the cargo to keep the ship afloat.（船员们抛弃了货物以使船不沉没。）}
  \wordsense{v. 抛弃，丢弃（不需要的东西或想法）}
  \wordphrase{jettison old ideas}
  \wordexample{The company jettisoned its unprofitable divisions.（公司抛弃了不盈利的部门。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{jetty}{'dʒeti}
  \wordbreakdown{扔出去→突出物→码头 jet (= throw) + -ty}
  \wordfamily{jettison 抛弃；project 突出；eject 喷出}
  \wordsense{n. 防波堤；码头}
  \wordphrase{walk along the jetty}
  \wordexample{Fishermen sat on the jetty, casting their lines into the sea.（渔民们坐在码头上，把鱼线抛入大海。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{objective}{əb'dʒektiv}
  \wordbreakdown{扔在面前→对象→客观 ob (= toward) + ject (= throw) + -ive}
  \wordfamily{objectively 客观地；object 反对；subject 主观的}
  \wordsense{a. 客观的，不带偏见的}
  \wordphrase{an objective assessment}
  \wordexample{A judge must remain objective throughout the trial.（法官在整个审判过程中必须保持客观。）}
  \wordsense{n. 目标，目的}
  \wordphrase{achieve one\textquotesingle s objective}
  \wordexample{Our main objective is to increase market share.（我们的主要目标是增加市场份额。）}
\end{word}



\chapter{LU, LAV, LAU}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 lu，lav，lau 意为 to wash。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{ablution}{ə'blu:ʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{彻底洗净 ab (= away, off) + lu (= wash) + -tion}
  \wordfamily{abluent 洗净的；deluge 洪水；dilute 稀释}
  \wordsense{n.【宗】沐浴仪式；洗礼}
  \wordphrase{perform ablutions}
  \wordexample{The Aztecs often performed ritual ablution on victims prior to their sacrifice.（阿兹特克人在献上祭品之前往往要举行沐浴仪式。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{deluge}{'delju:dʒ}
  \wordbreakdown{完全冲洗掉→洪水 de (= off, completely) + lu (= wash) + -ge}
  \wordfamily{dilute 稀释；alluvial 冲积的；antediluvian 古老的}
  \wordsense{n. 洪水，暴雨；泛滥}
  \wordphrase{a deluge of rain}
  \wordexample{The deluge swept away the bridge, isolating the town.（洪水卷走了大桥，使城镇陷于孤立。）}
  \wordsense{n./vt. 蜂拥而至；使应接不暇}
  \wordphrase{be deluged with complaints}
  \wordexample{The office was deluged with phone calls after the announcement.（公告发布后，办公室电话蜂拥而至。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{alluvial}{ə'lu:viəl}
  \wordbreakdown{冲洗到→冲积 al (= to) + luv (= wash) + -ial}
  \wordfamily{alluvium 冲积层；deluge 洪水；dilute 稀释}
  \wordsense{a. 冲积的}
  \wordphrase{alluvial soil}
  \wordexample{The fertile alluvial soil along the river supports intensive farming.（河流沿岸肥沃的冲积土支撑着集约化农业。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{antediluvian}{ˌæntidi'lu:viən}
  \wordbreakdown{大洪水之前的→古老的 ante (= before) + di (= away) + luv (= wash) + -ian}
  \wordfamily{deluge 洪水；alluvial 冲积的；dilute 稀释}
  \wordsense{a. 大洪水前的；古老的，过时的}
  \wordphrase{antediluvian ideas}
  \wordexample{His views on women\textquotesingle s roles are positively antediluvian.（他对女性角色的看法简直是老古董。）}
  \wordsense{n. 年迈的人；老古董}
  \wordphrase{a real antediluvian}
  \wordexample{The professor was an antediluvian who still used a typewriter.（那位教授是个老古董，还在用打字机。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{dilute}{dai'lju:t}
  \wordbreakdown{冲洗掉→稀释 di (= away) + lu (= wash) + -te}
  \wordfamily{dilution 稀释；deluge 洪水；alluvial 冲积的}
  \wordsense{v. 稀释；冲淡}
  \wordphrase{dilute the juice with water}
  \wordexample{Dilute the concentrate with three parts water.（用三份水稀释浓缩液。）}
  \wordsense{v. 削弱；降低}
  \wordphrase{dilute the impact}
  \wordexample{Too many examples dilute the force of the argument.（例子太多反而削弱了论证的力度。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{laundry}{'lɔ:ndri}
  \wordbreakdown{lau (= wash) + -ndry (场所后缀)}
  \wordfamily{launder 洗涤；lavatory 洗手间；lave 冲洗}
  \wordsense{n. 送洗的衣物；洗好的衣物}
  \wordphrase{do the laundry}
  \wordexample{I need to do the laundry before the weekend.（我需要在周末前洗衣服。）}
  \wordsense{n. 洗衣房，洗衣店}
  \wordphrase{take clothes to the laundry}
  \wordexample{There is a laundry on the corner of the street.（街角有一家洗衣店。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{launder}{'lɔ:ndə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{lau (= wash) + -nder}
  \wordfamily{laundry 洗衣房；Laundromat 自助洗衣店；laundress 洗衣女工}
  \wordsense{v. 洗涤，洗熨}
  \wordphrase{freshly laundered shirts}
  \wordexample{She laundered and ironed all the bed linen.（她把所有床单都洗烫了。）}
  \wordsense{v. 洗钱（非法所得）}
  \wordphrase{launder money}
  \wordexample{He was arrested for laundering drug money through offshore accounts.（他因通过离岸账户洗钱而被捕。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{lava}{'lɑ:və}
  \wordbreakdown{lav (= wash, flow) + -a（流出之物→熔岩）}
  \wordfamily{lavatory 洗手间；lave 冲洗；launder 洗涤}
  \wordsense{n.【地】熔岩；火山岩}
  \wordphrase{a stream of lava}
  \wordexample{Rivers of lava flowed down the mountainside after the eruption.（火山喷发后，熔岩流顺着山坡流下。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{lavatory}{'lævətəri}
  \wordbreakdown{洗的地方→洗手间 lav (= wash) + -atory (= place)}
  \wordfamily{lava 熔岩；lave 冲洗；lavish 奢华的}
  \wordsense{n. 洗手间，卫生间，厕所}
  \wordphrase{public lavatory}
  \wordexample{Excuse me, where is the nearest lavatory?（请问最近的洗手间在哪里？）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{lave}{leiv}
  \wordbreakdown{lav (= wash) + -e}
  \wordfamily{lavish 奢华的；lavatory 洗手间；lava 熔岩}
  \wordsense{v. 洗，沐浴}
  \wordphrase{lave one\textquotesingle s face}
  \wordexample{She laved her face in the cool stream.（她在清凉的溪水中洗脸。）}
  \wordsense{v.（溪流等）冲刷（堤岸）}
  \wordphrase{waves laving the shore}
  \wordexample{Gentle waves laved the sandy shore.（柔和的波浪冲刷着沙滩。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{lavish}{'læviʃ}
  \wordbreakdown{大量冲洗→过度倾注 lav (= wash, pour) + -ish}
  \wordfamily{lave 冲洗；lavatory 洗手间；deluge 洪水}
  \wordsense{a. 非常慷慨的；极其丰富的}
  \wordphrase{a lavish banquet}
  \wordexample{They held a lavish wedding reception at the hotel.（他们在酒店举办了一场奢华的婚宴。）}
  \wordsense{v. 挥霍；慷慨给予}
  \wordphrase{lavish attention on sb.}
  \wordexample{She lavished care on her only child.（她对独子倾注了过多的关爱。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{undiluted}{ˌʌndai'lju:tid}
  \wordbreakdown{未稀释的 un (= not) + di (= away) + lu (= wash) + -ted}
  \wordfamily{dilute 稀释；dilution 稀释物；deluge 洪水}
  \wordsense{a. 未稀释的，未掺水的；纯粹的}
  \wordphrase{undiluted orange juice}
  \wordexample{He drank the undiluted whisky in one gulp.（他一口喝下了未掺水的威士忌。）}
\end{word}



\chapter{ROG}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 rog 意为 to ask。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 quir / quisit / quer / quest 等。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{abrogate}{'æbrəgeit}
  \wordbreakdown{要求废除→取消 ab (= away) + rog (= ask) + -ate}
  \wordfamily{abrogation 废除；arrogance 傲慢；interrogate 审问}
  \wordsense{vt. 废除（法律等）；取消}
  \wordphrase{abrogate a treaty}
  \wordexample{Congress abrogated many of the lax bankruptcy laws.（议会废除了许多不严谨的破产法。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{arrogance}{'ærəgəns}
  \wordbreakdown{向自己要求→自大 ar (= to) + rog (= ask) + -ance}
  \wordfamily{arrogant 傲慢的；arrogate 僭取；abrogate 废除}
  \wordsense{n. 傲慢，自大}
  \wordphrase{intellectual arrogance}
  \wordexample{In my book, dishonesty is a greater crime than arrogance.（在我看来，不诚实是比傲慢更大的罪过。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{arrogate}{'ærəgeit}
  \wordbreakdown{向自己要求→僭取 ar (= to) + rog (= ask) + -ate}
  \wordfamily{arrogation 僭取；arrogance 傲慢；prerogative 特权}
  \wordsense{vt. 冒称，僭越；没有正当理由地把……归属于自己}
  \wordphrase{arrogate power to oneself}
  \wordexample{A dictator, by definition, arrogates absolute power to himself.（根据定义，所谓独裁者就是指僭取绝对权力的人。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{derogatory}{di'rɔgətri}
  \wordbreakdown{向下要求→贬低 de (= down) + rog (= ask) + -atory}
  \wordfamily{derogate 贬损；derogation 贬低；arrogance 傲慢}
  \wordsense{a. 损害名声的；贬低的}
  \wordphrase{derogatory remarks}
  \wordexample{My boss seems to make only derogatory remarks about my work.（我的老板好像对我的工作只作贬低的评价。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{interrogate}{in'terəgeit}
  \wordbreakdown{在中间问→审问 inter (= between) + rog (= ask) + -ate}
  \wordfamily{interrogation 审问；interrogative 疑问的；interrogator 审问者}
  \wordsense{vt. 审问；质问}
  \wordphrase{interrogate a suspect}
  \wordexample{The customs agent interrogated us for a full five minutes.（海关人员整整审问了我们五分钟。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{prerogative}{pri'rɔgətiv}
  \wordbreakdown{率先提出要求→特权 pre (= before) + rog (= ask) + -ative}
  \wordfamily{interrogate 审问；abrogate 废除；arrogate 僭取}
  \wordsense{n. 特权}
  \wordphrase{the prerogative of the president}
  \wordexample{It\textquotesingle s the mayor\textquotesingle s prerogative to choose the 4th of July speaker each year.（选择每年独立纪念日的演讲者是市长的特权。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{rogation}{rəu'geiʃən}
  \wordbreakdown{rog (= ask) + -ation}
  \wordfamily{interrogate 审问；prerogative 特权；abrogate 废除}
  \wordsense{n. 法案的提出；祈祷}
  \wordphrase{Rogation Days}
  \wordexample{Rogation Days are traditionally a time for prayer and fasting.（祈祷日传统上是祈祷和斋戒的时间。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{rogue}{rəug}
  \wordbreakdown{rog (= ask) → 乞讨者→无赖}
  \wordfamily{roguery 欺骗；roguish 调皮的；surrogate 代理}
  \wordsense{n. 恶棍，无赖}
  \wordphrase{a lovable rogue}
  \wordexample{He was a rogue who cheated everyone he met.（他是个骗子，欺骗了遇到的每一个人。）}
  \wordsense{n. 调皮鬼；凶猛的离群兽}
  \wordphrase{a rogue elephant}
  \wordexample{The rogue elephant destroyed several villages.（那头离群的凶象毁坏了好几个村庄。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{roguish}{'rəugiʃ}
  \wordbreakdown{rogue (= 无赖) + -ish}
  \wordfamily{rogue 无赖；roguery 欺骗；surrogate 代理}
  \wordsense{a. 流氓的，无赖的；调皮的，淘气的}
  \wordphrase{a roguish smile}
  \wordexample{She gave him a roguish wink across the table.（她隔着桌子调皮地向他眨了眨眼。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{surrogate}{'sʌrəgət}
  \wordbreakdown{代替某人提出要求→代理 sur (= in place of) + rog (= ask) + -ate}
  \wordfamily{rogue 无赖；prerogative 特权；interrogate 审问}
  \wordsense{a. 代理的；代用的}
  \wordphrase{surrogate mother}
  \wordexample{She agreed to be a surrogate mother for her sister.（她同意为姐姐做代孕母亲。）}
  \wordsense{n. 替代物；代理人}
  \wordphrase{a surrogate for real experience}
  \wordexample{Television has become a surrogate for real human interaction.（电视已成为真实人际交往的替代品。）}
\end{word}



\chapter{TRUD, TRUS}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 trud，trus 意为 to thrust。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{abstruse}{əb'stru:s}
  \wordbreakdown{推开隐藏→深奥 abs (= away) + trus (= thrust) + -e}
  \wordfamily{abstruseness 深奥；obtrude 强加；intrude 侵入}
  \wordsense{a. 深奥的，难以理解的}
  \wordphrase{abstruse theories}
  \wordexample{For most people, the field of theoretical physics is decidedly abstruse.（对大多数人来说，理论物理学确实很深奥。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{obtrude}{əb'tru:d}
  \wordbreakdown{推向前方→强加 ob (= in the way) + trud (= thrust) + -e}
  \wordfamily{obtrusive 鲁莽的；obtrusion 强加；protrude 突出}
  \wordsense{vt. 强加，强制}
  \wordphrase{obtrude one\textquotesingle s opinions upon sb.}
  \wordexample{I\textquotesingle m sick of that guy obtruding his opinions upon us at every turn.（我对经常把自己的意见强加给我们的那个家伙感到厌烦。）}
  \wordsense{vi. 闯入，打扰}
  \wordphrase{obtrude upon sb.\textquotesingle s privacy}
  \wordexample{I didn\textquotesingle t want to obtrude upon their grief.（我不想打扰他们的悲伤。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{protrude}{prə'tru:d}
  \wordbreakdown{向前推→突出 pro (= forth) + trud (= thrust) + -e}
  \wordfamily{protrusion 突出；protrusive 突出的；obtrude 强加}
  \wordsense{v.（使）伸出，（使）突出}
  \wordphrase{protrude from the wall}
  \wordexample{The lavatory protrudes 22 inches from the bathroom wall.（洗脸池从洗手间的墙壁上突出来22英寸。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{extrude}{ik'stru:d}
  \wordbreakdown{向外推→挤出 ex (= out) + trud (= thrust) + -e}
  \wordfamily{extrusion 挤出；protrude 突出；intrude 侵入}
  \wordsense{v. 挤压成型；推出，逐出}
  \wordphrase{extrude metal into tubes}
  \wordexample{The machine extrudes plastic into thin sheets.（机器将塑料挤压成薄片。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{intrude}{in'tru:d}
  \wordbreakdown{向里推→侵入 in (= into) + trud (= thrust) + -e}
  \wordfamily{intruder 侵入者；intrusion 侵入；intrusive 侵入的}
  \wordsense{vi. 侵入；闯入}
  \wordphrase{intrude into a private area}
  \wordexample{The burglar intruded into the house through a back window.（窃贼从后窗闯入了房屋。）}
  \wordsense{vi. 打扰；干扰}
  \wordphrase{intrude on sb.\textquotesingle s privacy}
  \wordexample{I hope I am not intruding.（希望我没有打扰你。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 强加；把……强加于}
  \wordphrase{intrude one\textquotesingle s opinions on others}
  \wordexample{He always intrudes his opinions on others without being asked.（他总是不请自来地把自己的意见强加于人。）}
  \wordsense{vi. 【地质】侵入（岩层）}
  \wordphrase{magma intruding into rock}
  \wordexample{Molten magma intruded into the surrounding rock layers.（熔岩侵入了周围的岩层。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{intruder}{in'tru:də(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{in (= into) + trud (= thrust) + -er}
  \wordfamily{intrude 侵入；intrusion 侵入；extrude 挤出}
  \wordsense{n. 侵入者，闯入者}
  \wordphrase{detect an intruder}
  \wordexample{The alarm went off when an intruder entered the building.（入侵者进入大楼时警报响了。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{intrusion}{in'tru:ʒn}
  \wordbreakdown{in (= into) + trus (= thrust) + -ion}
  \wordfamily{intrude 侵入；intrusive 侵入的；intruder 侵入者}
  \wordsense{n. 侵入；打扰}
  \wordphrase{an intrusion on one\textquotesingle s privacy}
  \wordexample{She was angry at the numerous intrusions on her time.（她对屡次被打扰感到愤怒。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{thrust}{θrʌst}
  \wordbreakdown{trud/trus 词根本身→推}
  \wordfamily{thruster 推进器；intrude 侵入；protrude 突出}
  \wordsense{v. 用力推；（用刀等）刺}
  \wordphrase{thrust a knife into}
  \wordexample{He thrust the letter into her hand and walked away.（他把信塞到她手里就走了。）}
  \wordsense{n. 猛推，刺；要点}
  \wordphrase{the main thrust of the argument}
  \wordexample{The thrust of his speech was that taxes must be cut.（他演讲的要点是必须减税。）}
\end{word}



\chapter{VOC, VOK[VOUC, VOW, VOIC]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 voc，vok 意为 to call。因与拉丁语中的 vox，vocis 相关，所以也有 voice 的意思。vouc，vow，voic 是其变形。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{avocation}{ˌævəu'keiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{从正业叫走→副业 a (= away) + voc (= call) + -ation}
  \wordfamily{advocate 拥护；evoke 唤起；vocation 职业}
  \wordsense{n. 副业；业余爱好}
  \wordphrase{painting as an avocation}
  \wordexample{I am a poet, but only by avocation.（我是个诗人，不过只是业余的。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{advocate}{'ædvəkeit}
  \wordbreakdown{向人们呼喊→拥护 ad (= to) + voc (= call) + -ate}
  \wordfamily{advocacy（倡导）；advocator（拥护者）；avocation（副业）}
  \wordsense{v. 拥护；主张}
  \wordphrase{advocate tax reform}
  \wordexample{The candidate advocates deep tax cuts.（那位候选人主张大幅削减税金。）}
  \wordsense{n. 拥护者；辩护律师}
  \wordphrase{an advocate of free trade}
  \wordexample{She is a strong advocate for children\textquotesingle s rights.（她是儿童权利的坚定拥护者。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{evoke}{i'vəuk}
  \wordbreakdown{向外呼唤→唤起 e (= out) + vok (= call) + -e}
  \wordfamily{evocation 唤起；evocative 引起回忆的；invoke 祈求}
  \wordsense{vt. 引起，唤起（回忆、感情等）}
  \wordphrase{evoke memories of childhood}
  \wordexample{That song evokes many pleasant high school memories for me.（那首歌唤起了我许多高中时的美好回忆。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 引起（反应、回应）}
  \wordphrase{evoke a strong response}
  \wordexample{His speech evoked a strong emotional response from the audience.（他的演讲引起了听众强烈的情感反应。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 使人想起；使浮现}
  \wordphrase{evoke an image of}
  \wordexample{The painting evokes the atmosphere of a rainy Parisian street.（这幅画让人想起巴黎雨天街道的氛围。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{avowed}{ə'vaud}
  \wordbreakdown{向人宣告→公开的 a (= to) + vow (= call) + -ed}
  \wordfamily{avowal 公开声明；avow 坦白承认；avowedly 公然地}
  \wordsense{a. 坦率承认的；公开宣布的}
  \wordphrase{an avowed supporter}
  \wordexample{My father is an avowed supporter of the Republican Party.（我父亲坦然承认自己是共和党的支持者。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{disavow}{ˌdisə'vau}
  \wordbreakdown{否认声明 dis (= not) + a (= to) + vow (= call)}
  \wordfamily{disavowal 否认；avow 声明；avowed 公开的}
  \wordsense{vt. 否认；拒绝承认}
  \wordphrase{disavow responsibility}
  \wordexample{He put an ad in the newspaper disavowing any debts of his ex-wife.（他在报纸上登广告表示拒绝对前妻所欠的任何债务负责。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{invoke}{in'vəuk}
  \wordbreakdown{向内呼唤→祈求 in (= on, upon) + vok (= call) + -e}
  \wordfamily{invocation 祈祷；evoke 唤起；revoke 撤回}
  \wordsense{vt. 祈求（神的庇护）；祈祷}
  \wordphrase{invoke God\textquotesingle s blessing}
  \wordexample{People who say blessings at mealtime invoke the name of God with thanks.（就餐时祈祷的人心怀感激地呼唤上帝的名字。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 援引（法律、权利等）}
  \wordphrase{invoke the law}
  \wordexample{The government invoked emergency powers to deal with the crisis.（政府援引紧急权力来应对危机。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 引起；唤起（感情、想象等）}
  \wordphrase{invoke a sense of nostalgia}
  \wordexample{The old photographs invoked a deep sense of nostalgia.（那些老照片唤起了深深的怀旧之情。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 【计算机】调用（程序、函数）}
  \wordphrase{invoke a function}
  \wordexample{The system automatically invokes the backup procedure every night.（系统每晚自动调用备份程序。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{irrevocable}{i'revəkəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{不可叫回的→不可撤销的 ir (= not) + re (= back) + voc (= call) + -able}
  \wordfamily{revoke 撤回；revocable 可撤销的；invoke 祈求}
  \wordsense{a. 不能取消的；不可挽回的}
  \wordphrase{an irrevocable decision}
  \wordexample{The country club gave my father an irrevocable lifetime membership.（那个乡村俱乐部授予我父亲不可撤销的终身会员资格。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{provoke}{prə'vəuk}
  \wordbreakdown{向前呼唤→激怒 pro (= forth) + vok (= call) + -e}
  \wordfamily{provocation 挑衅；provocative 挑衅的；evoke 唤起}
  \wordsense{vt. 激怒；惹恼}
  \wordphrase{provoke sb. into doing sth.}
  \wordexample{My words provoked her so much that she stopped speaking to me.（我的话让她很生气，所以她没再跟我说话。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 诱发；激起（反应、情感）}
  \wordphrase{provoke a reaction}
  \wordexample{The announcement provoked widespread public outrage.（这一声明引发了公众的广泛愤怒。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 挑衅；挑动}
  \wordphrase{provoke a fight}
  \wordexample{He deliberately provoked the other boy into a fight.（他故意挑衅另一个男孩打架。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{revoke}{ri'vəuk}
  \wordbreakdown{叫回→撤回 re (= back) + vok (= call) + -e}
  \wordfamily{revocation 废止；revocable 可撤销的；irrevocable 不可撤销的}
  \wordsense{vt. 撤回；废止，取消}
  \wordphrase{revoke a license}
  \wordexample{His driving license was revoked after his DUI arrest.（他酒后驾车被捕后，驾照被吊销了。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{avouch}{ə'vautʃ}
  \wordbreakdown{向人呼唤→保证 a (= to) + vouc (= call)}
  \wordfamily{avow 声明；vouch 担保；advocate 拥护}
  \wordsense{v. 肯定；保证；承认}
  \wordphrase{avouch the truth of}
  \wordexample{He avouched the accuracy of the report.（他保证报告的准确性。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{convoke}{kən'vəuk}
  \wordbreakdown{一起呼唤→召集 con (= together) + vok (= call) + -e}
  \wordfamily{convocation 召集；invoke 祈求；provoke 激怒}
  \wordsense{v. 召集（会议）}
  \wordphrase{convoke a meeting}
  \wordexample{The president convoked an emergency session of Congress.（总统召集了国会紧急会议。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{convocation}{ˌkɔnvə'keiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{con (= together) + voc (= call) + -ation}
  \wordfamily{convoke 召集；invoke 祈求；avocation 副业}
  \wordsense{n.（会议的）召集；集会}
  \wordphrase{a convocation of scholars}
  \wordexample{The university held its annual convocation for graduating students.（大学为毕业生举行了年度集会。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{invoice}{'invɔis}
  \wordbreakdown{in (= in) + voic (= voice, call) → 发送通知→发货单}
  \wordfamily{convoke 召集；vocabulary 词汇；vocal 声音的}
  \wordsense{n. 发货单；发票}
  \wordphrase{issue an invoice}
  \wordexample{Please send us an invoice for the goods delivered.（请给我们寄一张已交货物的发票。）}
  \wordsense{v. 开发票；将……列入清单}
  \wordphrase{invoice the customer}
  \wordexample{We will invoice you at the end of the month.（我们将在月底给您开发票。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{unvoiced}{ʌn'vɔist}
  \wordbreakdown{un (= not) + voic (= voice, call) + -ed}
  \wordfamily{vocal 声音的；voice 声音；invoke 祈求}
  \wordsense{a.（想法等）未说出的；无声的}
  \wordphrase{unvoiced fears}
  \wordexample{Her unvoiced concerns were evident in her expression.（她未说出口的担忧从表情中显而易见。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{vocabulary}{və'kæbjələri}
  \wordbreakdown{voc (= call, voice) + -abul- + -ary (= place)}
  \wordfamily{vocal 声音的；vocational 职业的；invoke 祈求}
  \wordsense{n. 词汇；词汇量}
  \wordphrase{expand one\textquotesingle s vocabulary}
  \wordexample{A writer with a large vocabulary can express ideas more precisely.（词汇量大的作家能更精确地表达思想。）}
  \wordsense{n. 词汇表；词汇手册}
  \wordphrase{a vocabulary at the back of the textbook}
  \wordexample{There is a vocabulary at the back of the textbook for reference.（教科书后面附有词汇表供参考。）}
  \wordsense{n. （某学科或语言的）专业术语}
  \wordphrase{the vocabulary of science}
  \wordexample{The vocabulary of law is often difficult for ordinary people to understand.（法律术语对普通人来说往往很难理解。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{vocal}{'vəukl}
  \wordbreakdown{voc (= voice, call) + -al}
  \wordfamily{vocalize 发声；vocalist 歌手；vocally 口头地}
  \wordsense{a. 声音的；声乐的}
  \wordphrase{vocal cords}
  \wordexample{She trained her vocal cords for years to become an opera singer.（她训练声带多年才成为歌剧演员。）}
  \wordsense{a. 直言不讳的}
  \wordphrase{be vocal about}
  \wordexample{He has been very vocal about his opposition to the plan.（他一直直言不讳地反对这个计划。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{vocational}{vəu'keiʃənl}
  \wordbreakdown{voc (= call) + -ation + -al（天职→职业）}
  \wordfamily{vocation 职业；avocation 副业；vocal 声音的}
  \wordsense{a. 职业的}
  \wordphrase{vocational training}
  \wordexample{The school offers vocational training in plumbing and carpentry.（该校提供管道工和木工的职业培训。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{vocative}{'vɔkətiv}
  \wordbreakdown{voc (= call) + -ative（呼唤用的→呼格）}
  \wordfamily{vocal 声音的；invoke 祈求；evoke 唤起}
  \wordsense{a. 称呼的；【语】呼格的}
  \wordphrase{the vocative case}
  \wordexample{In Latin, the vocative case is used when addressing someone directly.（在拉丁语中，呼格用于直接称呼某人。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{vouch}{vautʃ}
  \wordbreakdown{vouc (= call, voice) → 呼唤作证→担保}
  \wordfamily{voucher 凭证；avouch 保证；avow 声明}
  \wordsense{v. 保证，担保；证明}
  \wordphrase{vouch for sb.}
  \wordexample{I can vouch for his honesty---he has never lied to me.（我可以为他的诚实担保——他从未对我撒过谎。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{voucher}{'vautʃə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{vouc (= call, voice) + -er（证明之物→凭证）}
  \wordfamily{vouch 担保；avouch 保证；invoice 发票}
  \wordsense{n. 收据，凭单；代金券}
  \wordphrase{a gift voucher}
  \wordexample{She received a \$50 gift voucher for her birthday.（她生日收到了一张50美元的礼券。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{vow}{vau}
  \wordbreakdown{vow (= call, voice) → 呼唤神灵→誓言}
  \wordfamily{avow 声明；disavow 否认；avowal 公开声明}
  \wordsense{n. 誓言，誓约}
  \wordphrase{marriage vows}
  \wordexample{They exchanged marriage vows in a small church.（他们在一座小教堂里交换了婚姻誓言。）}
  \wordsense{v. 发誓，立誓}
  \wordphrase{vow to do sth.}
  \wordexample{She vowed never to speak to him again.（她发誓再也不跟他说话。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{vowel}{'vauəl}
  \wordbreakdown{vow (= voice) + -el（有声音的→元音）}
  \wordfamily{vocal 声音的；vow 誓言；consonant 辅音}
  \wordsense{n. 元音}
  \wordphrase{long/short vowels}
  \wordexample{English has more vowel sounds than most other languages.（英语的元音比大多数其他语言都多。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{vox}{vɔks}
  \wordbreakdown{vox = 拉丁语"声音"（voc 词根的名词形式）}
  \wordfamily{vocal 声音的；voice 声音；vocabulary 词汇}
  \wordsense{n. 嗓音；声音}
  \wordphrase{Vox Populi}
  \wordexample{Vox Populi, Vox Dei---the voice of the people is the voice of God.（民之声即神之声。）}
\end{word}



\chapter{ERR[ARR]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 err 意为 to wander，arr 是它的变形。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 plan 和来自希腊语的 vag。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{aberration}{ˌæbə'reiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{脱离某物徘徊 ab (= from) + err (= wander)}
  \wordfamily{aberrant（脱离常规的，越轨的）；arrant（十足的）；err（犯错）}
  \wordsense{n. 脱离常规，越轨；像差}
  \wordphrase{aberration in the lens}
  \wordexample{An aberration in the lens caused the telescope to reveal a flawed image.（镜头的像差导致望远镜显示的图像有问题。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{arrant}{'ærənt}
  \wordbreakdown{arr (= wander) + -ant（到处游荡的→臭名昭著的）}
  \wordfamily{errant 漫游的；err 犯错；erratic 反复无常的}
  \wordsense{a. 十足的；臭名昭著的}
  \wordphrase{arrant nonsense}
  \wordexample{He is an arrant fool who believes everything he reads online.（他是个十足的傻瓜，网上看到什么都信。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{err}{ə:(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{err (= wander) → 偏离正道→犯错}
  \wordfamily{error 错误；errant 漫游的；erratic 反复无常的}
  \wordsense{v. 犯错；出差错}
  \wordphrase{to err is human}
  \wordexample{To err is human, to forgive divine.（犯错是人之常情，宽恕是神圣美德。）}
  \wordsense{v. 偏离正道；误入歧途}
  \wordphrase{err from the path of virtue}
  \wordexample{He erred from the path of righteousness in his youth.（他年轻时误入了歧途。）}
  \wordsense{v. 倾向于（某一方面）}
  \wordphrase{err on the side of caution}
  \wordexample{When in doubt, it is better to err on the side of caution.（拿不准时，宁可谨慎行事。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{errand}{'erənd}
  \wordbreakdown{err (= wander) + -and（外出游走→差事）}
  \wordfamily{err 犯错；errant 漫游的；arrant 十足的}
  \wordsense{n. 差事，跑腿}
  \wordphrase{run errands}
  \wordexample{She sent her son on an errand to the grocery store.（她让儿子去杂货店跑腿。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{errant}{'erənt}
  \wordbreakdown{err (= wander) + -ant（游荡的→误入歧途的）}
  \wordfamily{err 犯错；erratic 反复无常的；arrant 十足的}
  \wordsense{a. 犯错误的，误入歧途的；漫游的}
  \wordphrase{a knight errant}
  \wordexample{The errant husband begged his wife for forgiveness.（犯了错的丈夫恳求妻子原谅。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{erratic}{i'rætik}
  \wordbreakdown{err (= wander) + -atic（到处游荡的→无规律的）}
  \wordfamily{err 犯错；errant 漫游的；erroneous 错误的}
  \wordsense{a. 古怪的；无规律的；反复无常的}
  \wordphrase{erratic behavior}
  \wordexample{His erratic driving made the passengers nervous.（他反复无常的驾驶让乘客们紧张不安。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{erratum}{i'reitəm}
  \wordbreakdown{err (= wander) + -atum（偏离之物→错误）}
  \wordfamily{errata（复数）；erroneous 错误的；err 犯错}
  \wordsense{n. 书写错误，印刷错误（复数 errata）}
  \wordphrase{a list of errata}
  \wordexample{The publisher included a page of errata at the back of the book.（出版商在书末附了一页勘误表。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{erroneous}{i'rəuniəs}
  \wordbreakdown{err (= wander) + -oneous（偏离的→错误的）}
  \wordfamily{error 错误；err 犯错；erratum 印刷错误}
  \wordsense{a. 错误的，不正确的}
  \wordphrase{an erroneous assumption}
  \wordexample{The report was based on erroneous data.（该报告基于错误的数据。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{error}{'erə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{err (= wander) + -or（偏离→错误）}
  \wordfamily{erroneous 错误的；err 犯错；unerring 正确无误的}
  \wordsense{n. 错误，过失，失误}
  \wordphrase{an error of judgment}
  \wordexample{The accident was caused by human error.（事故是由人为失误造成的。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{unerring}{ʌn'ə:riŋ}
  \wordbreakdown{un (= not) + err (= wander) + -ing（不偏离的→正确无误的）}
  \wordfamily{err 犯错；error 错误；erroneous 错误的}
  \wordsense{a. 正确无误的；没有偏差的}
  \wordphrase{unerring accuracy}
  \wordexample{The archer hit the target with unerring aim.（弓箭手以精准无误的瞄准射中了靶心。）}
\end{word}



\chapter{ORI[ORT]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 ori 意为 to rise，to arise，变形是 ort。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{abortive}{ə'bɔ:tiv}
  \wordbreakdown{从产生处脱落→失败 ab (= from) + ort (= arise) + -ive}
  \wordfamily{abort 流产；abortion 堕胎；abortionist 堕胎者}
  \wordsense{a. 失败的；流产的}
  \wordphrase{an abortive attempt}
  \wordexample{The soldiers made an abortive attempt to rescue the hostages.（士兵们试图救出人质，但没有成功。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{aboriginal}{ˌæbə'ridʒənl}
  \wordbreakdown{从起源开始→原始的 ab (= from) + ori (= arise) + -ginal}
  \wordfamily{aborigines 土著居民；origin 起源；orient 东方}
  \wordsense{a.（人、生物）原始的；土著的}
  \wordphrase{aboriginal culture}
  \wordexample{Aboriginal culture dates back over 50,000 years.（土著文化可以追溯到五万多年前。）}
  \wordsense{n. 土著居民}
  \wordphrase{Australian Aboriginals}
  \wordexample{The aboriginal peoples of Australia have a rich oral tradition.（澳大利亚土著民族有丰富的口头传统。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{aborigines}{ˌæbə'ridʒəni:z}
  \wordbreakdown{ab (= from) + ori (= arise) + -gines（从起源就在的人→土著）}
  \wordfamily{aboriginal 土著的；Aborigine 澳洲土著；origin 起源}
  \wordsense{n. 土著居民（复数）；土生动植物}
  \wordphrase{the aborigines of the region}
  \wordexample{The aborigines of this island have lived here for thousands of years.（这个岛上的土著居民已在此生活了数千年。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{orient}{'ɔ:rient}
  \wordbreakdown{ori (= rise) + -ent（太阳升起的方向→东方）}
  \wordfamily{oriental 东方的；orientation 方向；origin 起源}
  \wordsense{v.（使）适应（环境等）；确定方向}
  \wordphrase{orient oneself to}
  \wordexample{It took her a while to orient herself to the new job.（她花了一段时间才适应新工作。）}
  \wordsense{n.（the Orient）东方}
  \wordphrase{the mysteries of the Orient}
  \wordexample{He spent years traveling in the Orient.（他在东方旅行了多年。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{oriental}{ˌɔ:ri'entl}
  \wordbreakdown{ori (= rise) + -ent + -al（东方的）}
  \wordfamily{orient 东方；orientalist 东方学者；origin 起源}
  \wordsense{a. 东方的；东方人的}
  \wordphrase{oriental art}
  \wordexample{She has a fine collection of oriental art and ceramics.（她收藏了精美的东方艺术品和陶瓷。）}
  \wordsense{n.（Oriental）东方人}
  \wordphrase{an Oriental}
  \wordexample{Oriental philosophy has influenced Western thought in many ways.（东方哲学在许多方面影响了西方思想。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{orientation}{ˌɔ:rien'teiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{ori (= rise) + -ent + -ation（确定方向→适应）}
  \wordfamily{orient 适应；orientate 定向；oriental 东方的}
  \wordsense{n. 情况介绍；（对环境的）适应；方向的确定}
  \wordphrase{freshman orientation}
  \wordexample{All new employees must attend a two-day orientation session.（所有新员工必须参加为期两天的入职培训。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{oriented}{'ɔ:rientid}
  \wordbreakdown{orient (= 定向) + -ed}
  \wordfamily{orient 适应；orientation 方向；origin 起源}
  \wordsense{a. 以……为目的的；重视……的}
  \wordphrase{export-oriented economy}
  \wordexample{This is a child-oriented home where everything revolves around the kids.（这是一个以孩子为中心的家庭，一切围绕孩子转。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{origin}{'ɔridʒin}
  \wordbreakdown{ori (= rise, arise) + -gin（升起之处→起源）}
  \wordfamily{original 原始的；originate 起源于；aboriginal 土著的}
  \wordsense{n. 起源，开端}
  \wordphrase{the origin of the universe}
  \wordexample{Darwin wrote \emph{The Origin of Species} in 1859.（达尔文于1859年写了《物种起源》。）}
  \wordsense{n. 出身；血统}
  \wordphrase{of humble origin}
  \wordexample{He was a man of humble origin who rose to become president.（他出身卑微，却当上了总统。）}
  \wordsense{n. 产地；来源}
  \wordphrase{country of origin}
  \wordexample{The country of origin must be clearly marked on the label.（产地必须在标签上清楚标明。）}
  \wordsense{n. 【数学】原点}
  \wordphrase{the origin of coordinates}
  \wordexample{The origin is the point where the x-axis and y-axis intersect.（原点是x轴和y轴的交点。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{original}{ə'ridʒənl}
  \wordbreakdown{ori (= rise) + -gin + -al（起源的→原始的→独创的）}
  \wordfamily{originally 最初；originality 独创性；origin 起源}
  \wordsense{a. 独创的，有创意的；最初的}
  \wordphrase{an original idea}
  \wordexample{She is an original writer whose style is unlike anyone else\textquotesingle s.（她是一位独创性的作家，风格独一无二。）}
  \wordsense{n. 原物，原作}
  \wordphrase{read in the original}
  \wordexample{I prefer to read Tolstoy in the original rather than in translation.（我更喜欢读托尔斯泰的原著而非译本。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{originality}{əˌridʒə'næləti}
  \wordbreakdown{original + -ity}
  \wordfamily{original 独创的；originate 起源于；origin 起源}
  \wordsense{n. 独创性，创造力，创意}
  \wordphrase{a work of great originality}
  \wordexample{His paintings show remarkable originality.（他的画作展现了非凡的独创性。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{originate}{ə'ridʒineit}
  \wordbreakdown{ori (= rise) + -gin + -ate}
  \wordfamily{originator 创始人；origin 起源；original 独创的}
  \wordsense{v. 起源于，来自于；创作，发明}
  \wordphrase{originate a new style}
  \wordexample{The custom originated in ancient Greece.（这个习俗起源于古希腊。）}
\end{word}



\chapter{RAD, RAS[RAZ]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 rad，ras 意为 to rub，to scrape。其变形为 raz。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{abrasive}{ə'breisiv}
  \wordbreakdown{磨掉→粗糙的 ab (= away) + ras (= scrape) + -ive}
  \wordfamily{abrasion 磨损；abrade 磨损；erase 擦掉}
  \wordsense{a. 有研磨作用的；粗暴的，伤人感情的}
  \wordphrase{abrasive personality}
  \wordexample{The texture of a cat\textquotesingle s tongue is remarkably abrasive.（猫舌头的肌理非常粗糙。）}
  \wordsense{n. 研磨料}
  \wordphrase{use an abrasive to clean}
  \wordexample{Use a mild abrasive to remove the stain.（用温和的研磨剂去除污渍。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{erase}{i'reiz}
  \wordbreakdown{向外刮掉→擦除 e (= out) + ras (= scrape) + -e}
  \wordfamily{eraser 橡皮；erasure 擦除；abrasive 研磨的}
  \wordsense{vt. 擦掉，抹去}
  \wordphrase{erase a mistake}
  \wordexample{She erased the wrong answer and wrote the correct one.（她擦掉错误答案，写上了正确的。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 删除（录音、数据等）}
  \wordphrase{erase a file from the computer}
  \wordexample{He accidentally erased all the data on the hard drive.（他不小心删除了硬盘上的所有数据。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 消除（记忆、印象等）}
  \wordphrase{erase the memory of}
  \wordexample{Nothing can erase the memory of that terrible day.（什么都无法消除对那可怕一天的记忆。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{eraser}{i'reizə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{erase (= 擦掉) + -r}
  \wordfamily{erase 擦掉；raze 夷平；abrasive 研磨的}
  \wordsense{n. 擦除器（如橡皮、黑板擦等）}
  \wordphrase{a pencil eraser}
  \wordexample{He used an eraser to remove the pencil marks.（他用橡皮擦掉了铅笔痕迹。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{raze / rase}{reiz}
  \wordbreakdown{raz (= scrape) + -e（刮平→夷平）}
  \wordfamily{erase 擦掉；abrasive 研磨的；rascal 恶棍}
  \wordsense{v. 拆毁，夷平}
  \wordphrase{raze a building to the ground}
  \wordexample{The city was razed by an earthquake.（城市被地震夷为平地。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{rascal}{'ræskl}
  \wordbreakdown{ras (= scrape) + -cal（被社会刮弃的人→恶棍）}
  \wordfamily{rascally 卑鄙的；raze 夷平；erase 擦掉}
  \wordsense{n. 恶棍；（开玩笑的）捣蛋鬼}
  \wordphrase{a lovable rascal}
  \wordexample{Come here, you little rascal!（过来，你这个小捣蛋鬼！）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{rascally}{'ræskəli}
  \wordbreakdown{rascal (= 恶棍) + -ly}
  \wordfamily{rascal 恶棍；raze 夷平；rash 鲁莽的}
  \wordsense{a. 流氓的，卑鄙的}
  \wordphrase{a rascally trick}
  \wordexample{That was a rascally trick to play on an old man.（对一个老人耍那种卑鄙的伎俩。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{rash}{ræʃ}
  \wordbreakdown{ras (= scrape) + -h（刮擦→急躁）}
  \wordfamily{rascal 恶棍；rasp 锉刀；abrasive 研磨的}
  \wordsense{n. 疹；短时间内爆发的一连串事物}
  \wordphrase{come out in a rash}
  \wordexample{The child came out in a rash after eating strawberries.（孩子吃了草莓后起了疹子。）}
  \wordsense{a. 急躁的；鲁莽的，轻率的}
  \wordphrase{a rash decision}
  \wordexample{It was rash of you to make such a promise.（你做出那样的承诺太轻率了。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{rasp}{rɑ:sp}
  \wordbreakdown{ras (= scrape) + -p（刮擦→锉刀）}
  \wordfamily{rasping 刺耳的；rash 鲁莽的；abrasive 研磨的}
  \wordsense{n. 粗锉刀；锉磨声}
  \wordphrase{the rasp of metal on stone}
  \wordexample{He could hear the rasp of metal on stone from the workshop.（他能听到车间里金属摩擦石头的刺耳声。）}
  \wordsense{v. 用粗锉刀锉；发出刺耳的声音}
  \wordphrase{rasp out orders}
  \wordexample{His voice rasped with irritation.（他的声音因恼怒而变得刺耳。）}
\end{word}



\chapter{CLAIM, CLAM}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 claim，clam 意为 to cry，to shout。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{acclaim}{ə'kleim}
  \wordbreakdown{向某人大声叫好→称赞 ac (= to) + claim (= cry out)}
  \wordfamily{acclamation 欢呼；proclaim 宣布；disclaim 否认}
  \wordsense{v. 欢呼，喝彩；极力称赞}
  \wordphrase{be acclaimed as}
  \wordexample{She was acclaimed as the greatest pianist of her generation.（她被誉为她那一代最伟大的钢琴家。）}
  \wordsense{n. 欢呼，喝彩；赞誉}
  \wordphrase{win critical acclaim}
  \wordexample{The film debuted to widespread acclaim.（那部电影首映时受到了广泛的好评。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{disclaim}{dis'kleim}
  \wordbreakdown{大喊说不要 dis (= not) + claim (= cry)}
  \wordsense{vt. 放弃；拒绝；否认}
  \wordfamily{disclaimer 放弃；放弃声明书；否认者}
  \wordphrase{disclaim any responsibility}
  \wordexample{I disclaim any responsibility in this woman's death.（我否认我对这个女人的死负有责任。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{proclaim}{prə'kleim}
  \wordbreakdown{向前喊→宣布 pro (= forth) + claim (= cry)}
  \wordfamily{proclamation 宣言；acclaim 称赞；disclaim 否认}
  \wordsense{vt. 宣布，公布，声明；显露}
  \wordphrase{proclaim independence}
  \wordexample{The mayor proclaimed this as ``Be Nice To Your Secretary Week''.（市长宣布这周为”热情对待秘书周”。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{claim}{kleim}
  \wordbreakdown{claim (= cry out) → 大声要求→主张}
  \wordfamily{claimant 原告；disclaim 否认；proclaim 宣布}
  \wordsense{v. 要求；声称；索赔}
  \wordphrase{claim compensation}
  \wordexample{He claims to have seen the accident happen.（他声称目睹了事故的发生。）}
  \wordsense{n. 主张；要求权；索赔}
  \wordphrase{lay claim to}
  \wordexample{Several countries lay claim to the disputed territory.（几个国家都声称对争议领土拥有主权。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{claimant}{'kleimənt}
  \wordbreakdown{claim (= cry) + -ant（要求者）}
  \wordfamily{claim 要求；disclaim 否认；counterclaim 反诉}
  \wordsense{n. 要求者；【律】原告}
  \wordphrase{the claimant in the case}
  \wordexample{The claimant filed a lawsuit against the company.（原告对该公司提起了诉讼。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{clamor}{'klæmə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{clam (= cry, shout) + -or（喊叫→喧嚣）}
  \wordfamily{clamorous 吵闹的；acclaim 称赞；exclaim 惊叫}
  \wordsense{n. 喧嚣，吵闹声}
  \wordphrase{a clamor for reform}
  \wordexample{There was a growing clamor for political reform.（要求政治改革的呼声越来越高。）}
  \wordsense{v. 喧嚷；吵闹着要求}
  \wordphrase{clamor for attention}
  \wordexample{The crowd clamored for the release of the prisoners.（人群吵嚷着要求释放囚犯。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{clamorous}{'klæmərəs}
  \wordbreakdown{clamor (= 喧嚣) + -ous}
  \wordfamily{clamor 喧嚣；acclaim 称赞；exclaim 惊叫}
  \wordsense{a. 吵闹的，喧哗的}
  \wordphrase{a clamorous mob}
  \wordexample{The clamorous crowd demanded answers from the officials.（喧哗的人群要求官员们给出答复。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{counterclaim}{ˌkauntə'kleim}
  \wordbreakdown{counter (= against) + claim (= cry)（反向要求→反诉）}
  \wordfamily{claim 要求；disclaim 否认；claimant 原告}
  \wordsense{n. 反要求；反诉}
  \wordphrase{file a counterclaim}
  \wordexample{The company filed a counterclaim alleging breach of contract.（公司提出反诉，指控对方违约。）}
  \wordsense{v. 提出反诉}
  \wordphrase{counterclaim for damages}
  \wordexample{The defendant filed a counterclaim for damages.（被告就赔偿金提出了反诉。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{declaim}{di'kleim}
  \wordbreakdown{向下喊→慷慨陈词 de (= down) + claim (= cry)}
  \wordfamily{declamation 朗诵；declamatory 雄辩的；proclaim 宣布}
  \wordsense{v. 慷慨激昂地演说；朗读，朗诵}
  \wordphrase{declaim against injustice}
  \wordexample{The senator declaimed against the new tax policy.（参议员慷慨激昂地抨击新税收政策。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{exclaim}{ik'skleim}
  \wordbreakdown{向外喊→惊叫 ex (= out) + claim (= cry)}
  \wordfamily{exclamation 惊叫；exclamatory 感叹的；declaim 演说}
  \wordsense{v. 惊叫，呼喊}
  \wordphrase{exclaim in surprise}
  \wordexample{``What a beautiful sunset!'' she exclaimed.（"多美的日落啊！"她惊叹道。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{exclamation}{ˌeksklə'meiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{ex (= out) + clam (= cry) + -ation}
  \wordfamily{exclaim 惊叫；exclamatory 感叹的；clamor 喧嚣}
  \wordsense{n. 惊叫；感叹词，感叹句}
  \wordphrase{exclamation mark (!)}
  \wordexample{She gave an exclamation of surprise when she saw the gift.（看到礼物时她发出了惊叹。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{exclamatory}{ik'sklæmətəri}
  \wordbreakdown{exclamation + -ory}
  \wordfamily{exclaim 惊叫；exclamation 惊叹；clamor 喧嚣}
  \wordsense{a. 叫喊的；感叹的}
  \wordphrase{an exclamatory sentence}
  \wordexample{``How wonderful!'' is an exclamatory sentence.（"多棒啊！"是一个感叹句。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{reclaim}{ri'kleim}
  \wordbreakdown{取回→开垦 re (= back) + claim (= cry out, call)}
  \wordfamily{reclamation 开垦；感化；回收；reclaimable 可开垦的；可回收的}
  \wordsense{vt. 开垦（荒地）；回收利用}
  \wordphrase{reclaim land from the sea}
  \wordexample{The Dutch have reclaimed thousands of acres of land from the sea.（荷兰人从海中开垦了数千英亩的土地。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 使悔改，使改过自新}
  \wordphrase{reclaim a man from vice}
  \wordexample{The program aims to reclaim young offenders from a life of crime.（该项目旨在使年轻罪犯改过自新。）}
\end{word}



\chapter{MOD}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 mod 意为 fit，manner，modern，measure，kind，way 等。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{accommodate}{ə'kɒmədeit}
  \wordbreakdown{使适合于某物 ac<ad (= to) + com (= together) + mod (= fit)}
  \wordfamily{accommodation 住宿；适应；和解；accommodating 乐于助人的；accommodative 适应的}
  \wordsense{vt. 容纳；提供住宿}
  \wordphrase{accommodate up to 500 guests}
  \wordexample{The hotel can accommodate up to 500 guests at a time.（这家酒店一次最多可容纳500位客人。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 使适应；满足（需要）}
  \wordphrase{accommodate oneself to new surroundings}
  \wordexample{She quickly accommodated herself to the new working environment.（她很快适应了新的工作环境。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{commodious}{kə'məudiəs}
  \wordbreakdown{和任何事物都相符的→宽敞的 com (= with) + mod (= fit, measure) + -ious}
  \wordfamily{commodiously（宽敞地）；commodiousness（宽敞）；accommodate（容纳）}
  \wordsense{a. 宽敞的；便利的}
  \wordphrase{a commodious apartment}
  \wordexample{The apartment is full of commodious closets.（那家公寓有很多宽敞的壁橱。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{modulate}{'mɒdjuleit}
  \wordbreakdown{使契合→调节 mod (= fit, measure) + -ul- + -ate}
  \wordfamily{modulation 调节；调制；变调；modulator 调节器；调制器}
  \wordsense{vt. 调整，调节；变换（音调等）}
  \wordphrase{modulate one's voice}
  \wordexample{Though furious, she modulated her voice to soothing tones to answer the phone.（虽然很生气，但她还是调整了一下声音，以平和的语调接了电话。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{commode}{kə'məud}
  \wordbreakdown{方便的东西 com (= together, intensive) + mod (= fit, manner)}
  \wordfamily{commodity 商品；commodious 宽敞的；accommodate 容纳}
  \wordsense{n. 衣柜，橱；室内便器}
  \wordphrase{an antique commode}
  \wordexample{The antique commode in the hallway dates back to the 18th century.（走廊里那个古董衣柜可以追溯到18世纪。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{commodity}{kə'mɒdəti}
  \wordbreakdown{适合使用的东西→商品 com (= together) + mod (= fit, measure) + -ity}
  \wordfamily{commodities（market 商品市场）；commoditize（商品化）；accommodate（容纳）}
  \wordsense{n. 日用品；商品}
  \wordphrase{staple commodities}
  \wordexample{Rice and wheat are the staple commodities in many Asian countries.（大米和小麦是许多亚洲国家的主要商品。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{démodé}{ˌdei'məu'dei}
  \wordbreakdown{脱离时尚的 de (= down, away) + mod (= manner, fashion) + -é}
  \wordfamily{mode 时尚；modish 流行的；outmoded 过时的}
  \wordsense{a. 过时的；老式的}
  \wordphrase{a démodé style}
  \wordexample{Her once-fashionable hat now looks completely démodé.（她那顶曾经时髦的帽子现在看起来完全过时了。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{mod}{mɒd}
  \wordbreakdown{mod (= manner, fashion) 的缩写形式}
  \wordfamily{modern 现代的；modernize 使现代化；mode 方式}
  \wordsense{a.（尤指穿着）新潮的}
  \wordphrase{mod fashion}
  \wordexample{The mod fashion of the 1960s featured bold colors and geometric patterns.（20世纪60年代的新潮时尚以大胆的色彩和几何图案为特色。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{modal}{'məudl}
  \wordbreakdown{mod (= manner, form) + -al}
  \wordfamily{modality 形式；形态；【医】疗法}
  \wordsense{a. 形态的，形式的；【语】情态的}
  \wordphrase{modal verb}
  \wordexample{In English, ``can,'' ``must,'' and ``should'' are modal verbs.（在英语中，can、must和should是情态动词。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{mode}{maud}
  \wordbreakdown{mod (= manner, way, measure)}
  \wordfamily{modal 形态的；model 模型；moderate 适度的；modish 流行的}
  \wordsense{n. 方式，做法；样式；流行，时尚}
  \wordphrase{a mode of transport}
  \wordexample{Walking is the oldest mode of transport known to mankind.（步行是人类已知的最古老的交通方式。）}
  \wordsense{n. 【音】调式}
  \wordphrase{the major mode}
  \wordexample{Most pop songs are written in the major mode.（大多数流行歌曲是用大调式写的。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{model}{'mɒdl}
  \wordbreakdown{mod (= measure, manner) + -el (小词缀)}
  \wordfamily{modeling 模型制作；模特职业；remodel 改造；role model 榜样}
  \wordsense{n. 模型；模范，典型；模特儿；样式}
  \wordphrase{a role model}
  \wordexample{She has always been a role model for young women in science.（她一直是年轻女性科学家的榜样。）}
  \wordsense{v. 做……的模型；当模特儿；模仿}
  \wordphrase{model oneself on}
  \wordexample{He modeled himself on his favorite author and began writing short stories.（他以自己最喜欢的作家为榜样，开始写短篇小说。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{moderate}{'mɒdərət}
  \wordbreakdown{有分寸的 mod (= measure, fit) + -er- + -ate}
  \wordfamily{moderation（适度）；moderator（主持人）；moderately（适度地）；accommodate（容纳）}
  \wordsense{a. 适度的；温和的；稳健的}
  \wordphrase{moderate exercise}
  \wordexample{Doctors recommend moderate exercise for at least thirty minutes a day.（医生建议每天至少进行三十分钟的适度运动。）}
  \wordsense{v. 使缓和；主持（讨论等）}
  \wordphrase{moderate a debate}
  \wordexample{She was invited to moderate the presidential debate.（她被邀请主持总统辩论。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{moderation}{ˌmɒdə'reiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{mod (= measure, fit) + -er- + -ation}
  \wordfamily{moderate 适度的；moderator 主持人；immoderate 过度的}
  \wordsense{n. 适度；节制；缓和}
  \wordphrase{in moderation}
  \wordexample{Red wine, drunk in moderation, is said to be good for the heart.（据说适量饮用红酒对心脏有益。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{moderator}{'mɒdəreitə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{mod (= measure, fit) + -er- + -ator (= one who)}
  \wordfamily{moderate 适度的；moderation 节制；immoderate 过度的}
  \wordsense{n. 会议主席；调停者；仲裁者}
  \wordphrase{act as moderator}
