  \wordexample{He was asked to act as moderator in the dispute between the two departments.（他被要求在两个部门的争端中担任调停者。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{modernism}{'mɒdənizəm}
  \wordbreakdown{mod (= manner, current) + -ern + -ism}
  \wordfamily{modernity 现代性；modernist 现代主义者；modernistic 现代主义的；postmodernism 后现代主义}
  \wordsense{n. 现代主义；现代思想}
  \wordphrase{literary modernism}
  \wordexample{Literary modernism emerged in the early 20th century as a break from traditional forms.（文学现代主义出现于20世纪初，是对传统形式的突破。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{modernize}{'mɒdənaiz}
  \wordbreakdown{mod (= manner, current) + -ern + -ize}
  \wordfamily{modernization 现代化；modern 现代的；modernism 现代主义}
  \wordsense{v. 使现代化}
  \wordphrase{modernize the infrastructure}
  \wordexample{The government invested billions to modernize the country's aging infrastructure.（政府投入数十亿资金使国家老化的基础设施现代化。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{modest}{'mɒdist}
  \wordbreakdown{有分寸的→谦逊的 mod (= measure, manner) + -est}
  \wordfamily{modesty 谦逊；immodest 不谦虚的；不端庄的}
  \wordsense{a. 谦逊的；适度的；朴素的；端庄的}
  \wordphrase{a modest proposal}
  \wordexample{Despite her great achievements, she remained modest about her success.（尽管取得了巨大成就，她对自己的成功仍然很谦逊。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{modesty}{'mɒdəsti}
  \wordbreakdown{mod (= measure, manner) + -esty}
  \wordfamily{modest 谦逊的；immodesty 不谦虚；不端庄}
  \wordsense{n. 谦逊，质朴；端庄；羞怯}
  \wordphrase{false modesty}
  \wordexample{Without false modesty, I can say that I am the best candidate for this position.（不是假谦虚，我可以说我是这个职位的最佳人选。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{modicum}{'mɒdikəm}
  \wordbreakdown{一小份→少量 mod (= measure) + -icum (拉丁语小词缀)}
  \wordfamily{moderate 适度的；modest 谦逊的；modify 修改}
  \wordsense{n. 少量，一点点}
  \wordphrase{a modicum of common sense}
  \wordexample{Anyone with a modicum of common sense would know not to touch a hot stove.（任何有一点常识的人都知道不要碰热炉子。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{modify}{'mɒdifai}
  \wordbreakdown{使合适→修改 mod (= fit, measure) + -ify (= make)}
  \wordfamily{modification 修改；modifier 修饰语；modified 改良的}
  \wordsense{v. 修改，变更；缓和；【语】修饰}
  \wordphrase{modify the design}
  \wordexample{We had to modify the original design to meet the new safety regulations.（我们不得不修改原始设计以满足新的安全法规。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{modification}{ˌmɒdifi'keiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{mod (= fit, measure) + -ific (= make) + -ation}
  \wordfamily{modify 修改；modifier 修饰语；unmodified 未修改的}
  \wordsense{n. 修改，变更；缓和}
  \wordphrase{make modifications to}
  \wordexample{Several modifications were made to the plan before it was approved.（该计划在获批之前经过了多次修改。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{modish}{'məudiʃ}
  \wordbreakdown{mod (= manner, fashion) + -ish}
  \wordfamily{mode 时尚；démodé 过时的；fashionable 时髦的}
  \wordsense{a. 流行的，时髦的}
  \wordphrase{modish clothes}
  \wordexample{She always wears modish clothes that attract attention wherever she goes.（她总是穿着时髦的衣服，走到哪里都引人注目。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{module}{'mɒdju:l}
  \wordbreakdown{mod (= measure) + -ule (小词缀)}
  \wordfamily{modular（模块化的）；modularize（使模块化）；accommodate（容纳）}
  \wordsense{n. 模块；组件；（宇宙飞船的）舱}
  \wordphrase{the lunar module}
  \wordexample{The lunar module separated from the spacecraft and descended to the moon's surface.（登月舱从宇宙飞船上分离，降落到月球表面。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{modus operandi}{ˌməudəs ɒpə'rændi:}
  \wordbreakdown{工作的方式 mod (= manner, way) + oper (= work) + -andi}
  \wordfamily{modus vivendi 生活方式；mode 方式；operate 操作}
  \wordsense{n. 工作方法；一贯手法，惯技}
  \wordphrase{the criminal's modus operandi}
  \wordexample{The detective recognized the criminal's modus operandi from a previous case.（侦探从以前的案件中认出了罪犯的惯用手法。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{modus vivendi}{ˌməudəs vi'vendi:}
  \wordbreakdown{生活的方式 mod (= manner, way) + viv (= life) + -endi}
  \wordfamily{modus operandi 工作方法；vivid 生动的；survive 生存}
  \wordsense{n. 生活方式；妥协，权宜之计}
  \wordphrase{reach a modus vivendi}
  \wordexample{After months of conflict, the two nations reached a modus vivendi.（经过数月的冲突，两国达成了妥协。）}
\end{word}



\chapter{QUI}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 qui 意为 quiet，to rest。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{acquit}{ə'kwit}
  \wordbreakdown{让某人心境平和 ac<ad (= to) + qui (= quiet)}
  \wordsense{vt. `sb + of + sth` 宣判……无罪；使（自己）作出某种表现；使（自己）履行（或完成）}
  \wordfamily{acquittal 宣判无罪；履行}
  \wordfamily{acquittance（债务的）清偿}
  \wordphrase{The jury acquitted the}
  \wordexample{The jury acquitted the prisoner after a short deliberation.（陪审团稍作审议，决定宣判犯人无罪。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{acquiesce}{ˌækwi'es}
  \wordbreakdown{面对某事变得安静 ac<ad (= to, intensive) + qui (= quiet)}
  \wordfamily{acquiescence（默认）；acquiescent（默认的）；acquit（sb + of + sth 宣判……无罪）}
  \wordsense{vi. to + sth 默认；默从}
  \wordphrase{I finally acquiesced to}
  \wordexample{I finally acquiesced to my parents' demands that I stop smoking.（最后我还是默从了我父母的要求，答应戒烟。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{disquieting}{dis'kwaiətiŋ}
  \wordbreakdown{不让心境平和、安宁的 dis (= not) + qui (= quiet, rest)}
  \wordsense{a. 使不安的，令人担忧的}
  \wordfamily{disquiet 使不安}
  \wordfamily{disquietude 不安，担忧}
  \wordfamily{disquietingly 不安地，担忧地}
  \wordphrase{disquieting sounds}
  \wordexample{The soldier could not sleep due to the disquieting sounds of battle in the distance.（从远处传来的令人不安的战斗声使那个士兵无法入睡。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{tranquility}{træŋ'kwiləti}
  \wordbreakdown{完全安静的、平稳的状态 tran<trans (= intensive) + qui (= quiet, rest)}
  \wordsense{n. 平静；平稳}
  \wordfamily{tranquil 平静的；平稳的}
  \wordfamily{tranquilizer 镇静剂}
  \wordfamily{tranquilize 使安静；使镇定}
  \wordphrase{tranquility here}
  \wordexample{The tranquility here at Loon Lake is marvelous.（卢恩湖非常平静。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{quiescent}{kwai'esnt}
  \wordbreakdown{qui (= quiet, rest) + -escent (= becoming)}
  \wordfamily{quiescence 静止；沉默；acquiesce 默认}
  \wordsense{a. 静止的，不动的；沉默的}
  \wordphrase{a quiescent volcano}
  \wordexample{The volcano has been quiescent for over a century, but scientists still monitor it closely.（这座火山已经沉寂了一个多世纪，但科学家们仍在密切监测。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{inquietude}{in'kwaiətju:d}
  \wordbreakdown{不安宁 in (= not) + qui (= quiet, rest) + -etude}
  \wordfamily{disquiet 使不安；quietude 宁静；tranquility 平静}
  \wordsense{n. 不安，担心}
  \wordphrase{a sense of inquietude}
  \wordexample{A growing sense of inquietude spread through the crowd as the storm approached.（随着暴风雨的临近，人群中弥漫着越来越强烈的不安感。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{quietism}{'kwaiətizəm}
  \wordbreakdown{qui (= quiet, rest) + -et- + -ism}
  \wordfamily{quietist 寂静主义者；quietude 宁静；quiescent 静止的}
  \wordsense{n. 【宗】寂静主义（天主教神修学派的主张）}
  \wordphrase{the doctrine of quietism}
  \wordexample{Quietism teaches that spiritual perfection is achieved through passive contemplation.（寂静主义教导人们通过被动的沉思来达到精神上的完美。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{quietude}{'kwaiətju:d}
  \wordbreakdown{qui (= quiet, rest) + -etude}
  \wordfamily{inquietude 不安；disquietude 不安；tranquility 平静}
  \wordsense{n. 寂静；宁静}
  \wordphrase{the quietude of the countryside}
  \wordexample{She moved to the countryside seeking the quietude that city life could never offer.（她搬到乡下，寻找城市生活永远无法提供的宁静。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{requiem}{'rekwiəm}
  \wordbreakdown{使灵魂安息 re (= again, intensive) + qui (= quiet, rest) + -em}
  \wordfamily{quietude 宁静；acquiesce 默认；tranquil 平静的}
  \wordsense{n.（天主教）安魂弥撒；安魂曲；挽歌}
  \wordphrase{Mozart's Requiem}
  \wordexample{Mozart's Requiem remains one of the most powerful pieces of sacred music ever composed.（莫扎特的安魂曲仍然是有史以来最有力的宗教音乐作品之一。）}
\end{word}



\chapter{HER, HES}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 her，hes 意为 to stick。同义词根有 clam，cleav，visc 等。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{adhere}{əd'hiə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{紧贴在某物上 ad (= to) + her (= stick)}
  \wordsense{vi. `to + sth` 黏附；坚持；追随}
  \wordfamily{adherence 附着；坚持；忠诚}
  \wordfamily{adhesion 附着，黏着；坚持}
  \wordfamily{adherent 黏着的；`(n.)` 追随者}
  \wordfamily{adhesive 带黏性的；`(n.)` 黏合剂}
  \wordphrase{adhere to the surface}
  \wordexample{Fried foods often adhere to the surface of a skillet.（油炸食品往往会粘在平底煎锅的表面上。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{coherent}{kəu'hiərənt}
  \wordbreakdown{粘贴在一起的 co<com (= together) + her (= stick)}
  \wordfamily{cohere（黏着）；cohesive（有黏着力的）；凝聚（性）；cohesion（黏着）；adhere（to + sth 黏附）}
  \wordsense{a. 黏着的；协调一致的；条理清楚的}
  \wordphrase{coherent}
  \wordexample{I felt drowsy after the operation, but mentally coherent.（手术之后我昏昏欲睡，不过意识是清醒的。）}
  \wordexample{coherent}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{hesitant}{'hezitənt}
  \wordbreakdown{her/hes (= stick, cling) + -it- + -ant（粘住不动→犹豫的）}
  \wordfamily{hesitantly 犹豫地；hesitance 踌躇；hesitate 犹豫}
  \wordsense{a. 犹豫的，踌躇的，迟疑的}
  \wordphrase{be hesitant about}
  \wordexample{She was hesitant about accepting the job offer.（她对是否接受这份工作犹豫不决。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{hesitate}{'heziteit}
  \wordbreakdown{her/hes (= stick, cling) + -it- + -ate（粘住不动→犹豫）}
  \wordfamily{hesitant 犹豫的；hesitation 犹豫；inherent 固有的}
  \wordsense{vi. 犹豫，踌躇}
  \wordphrase{hesitate to do sth.}
  \wordexample{Don\textquotesingle t hesitate to ask if you need any help.（如果需要帮助，请不要犹豫，尽管开口。）}
  \wordsense{vi. 顾虑；不愿意}
  \wordphrase{hesitate about / over}
  \wordexample{He hesitated over whether to accept the invitation.（他对是否接受邀请犹豫不决。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{hesitation}{ˌhezi'teiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{her/hes (= stick) + -it- + -ation（犹豫的状态）}
  \wordfamily{hesitate 犹豫；hesitant 犹豫的；inherent 固有的}
  \wordsense{n. 犹豫，踌躇}
  \wordphrase{without hesitation}
  \wordexample{She accepted the offer without hesitation.（她毫不犹豫地接受了这个提议。）}
  \wordsense{n. 口吃；结巴}
  \wordphrase{speak with hesitation}
  \wordexample{He spoke with some hesitation, searching for the right words.（他说话有些结巴，在寻找合适的措辞。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{incoherent}{ˌinkəu'hiərənt}
  \wordbreakdown{不黏在一起的→不连贯的 in (= not) + co<com (= together) + her (= stick) + -ent}
  \wordfamily{incoherence 不连贯；coherent 连贯的；coherence 连贯性}
  \wordsense{a. 不连贯的；无条理的；语无伦次的}
  \wordphrase{incoherent with rage}
  \wordexample{He was so drunk as to be quite incoherent when the police found him.（警察找到他时，他醉得语无伦次。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{inherent}{in'hiərənt}
  \wordbreakdown{黏在内部的→固有的 in (= in) + her (= stick) + -ent}
  \wordfamily{inherently 固有地；天性地；inhere 固有；存在于}
  \wordsense{a. 与生俱来的，固有的；内在的}
  \wordphrase{inherent in the system}
  \wordexample{Certain risks are inherent in the sport of mountain climbing.（某些风险是登山运动固有的。）}
\end{word}
\end{word}



\chapter{FLU}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 flu 意为 to flow。同义词根有来自希腊语的 rrh 和来自拉丁语的 lix，man 等。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{affluence}{'æfluəns}
  \wordbreakdown{不断流向→富裕 af<ad (= to, intensive) + flu (= flow) + -ence}
  \wordfamily{affluent 富裕的；丰富的；affluently 富裕地}
  \wordsense{n. 富饶，富有；丰富}
  \wordphrase{live in affluence}
  \wordexample{Affluence is a way of life for most American physicians.（大多数美国医生都过着富有的生活。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{fluctuate}{'flʌktʃueit}
  \wordbreakdown{流动 flu (= flow)}
  \wordsense{vi.（物价、温度等）上下变动，波动；动摇}
  \wordfamily{fluctuation 上下变动，波动；动摇}
  \wordphrase{fluctuates wildly sometimes}
  \wordexample{The exchange rate fluctuates wildly, sometimes as much as 10\% in one day.（汇率变动幅度很大，有时候甚至一天之内变动达 10\%。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{fluent}{'flu:ənt}
  \wordbreakdown{话从嘴中说出的 flu (= flow)}
  \wordsense{a. 流畅的，流利的}
  \wordfamily{fluency 流畅，流利}
  \wordfamily{fluently 流畅地，流利地}
  \wordphrase{fluent in both Chinese}
  \wordexample{He's fluent in both Chinese and Japanese.（他的汉语和日语讲得都很流利。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{confluence}{'kɒnfluəns}
  \wordbreakdown{流到一起 con<com (= together) + flu (= flow) + -ence}
  \wordfamily{confluent 汇合的；affluence 富裕；influence 影响}
  \wordsense{n.（江河的）交汇（处）；汇集}
  \wordphrase{the confluence of two rivers}
  \wordexample{The city was built at the confluence of the two great rivers.（这座城市建在两条大河的交汇处。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{effluent}{'efluənt}
  \wordbreakdown{流出的 ef<ex (= out) + flu (= flow) + -ent}
  \wordfamily{efflux 流出；affluent 富裕的；influent 流入的}
  \wordsense{n.（工厂、下水道等排出的）废水，污水}
  \wordphrase{industrial effluent}
  \wordexample{The factory was fined for discharging toxic effluent into the river.（这家工厂因向河中排放有毒废水而被罚款。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{efflux}{'eflʌks}
  \wordbreakdown{流出 ef<ex (= out) + flu (= flow) + -x}
  \wordfamily{effluent 废水；influx 涌入；flux 流动}
  \wordsense{n.（液体或气体的）流出；流出物}
  \wordphrase{an efflux of gas}
  \wordexample{An efflux of toxic gas from the pipe forced the evacuation of nearby residents.（管道中有毒气体的泄漏迫使附近居民撤离。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{flue}{flu:}
  \wordbreakdown{flu (= flow) → 气流通过的管道}
  \wordfamily{fluent 流利的；fluid 流体；flux 流动}
  \wordsense{n. 烟道；暖气管}
  \wordphrase{clean the flue}
  \wordexample{You should clean the flues of soot at least once a year to prevent chimney fires.（你应该每年至少清扫一次烟道中的烟灰，以防止烟囱起火。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{fluid}{'flu:id}
  \wordbreakdown{flu (= flow) + -id}
  \wordfamily{fluidity 流动性；易变性；fluids 液体}
  \wordsense{n. 流体（液体与气体的总称）}
  \wordphrase{body fluids}
  \wordexample{Doctors advised him to drink plenty of fluids to stay hydrated.（医生建议他多喝液体以保持水分。）}
  \wordsense{a. 流动的；不稳定的，易变的}
  \wordphrase{a fluid situation}
  \wordexample{The political situation remains fluid and could change at any moment.（政治局势仍然不稳定，随时可能发生变化。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{flume}{flu:m}
  \wordbreakdown{flu (= flow) + -me}
  \wordfamily{flue 烟道；fluid 流体；flush 冲洗}
  \wordsense{n. 水槽，水道（用来运送木材或转动水碓）}
  \wordphrase{a log flume}
  \wordexample{Logs were transported down the mountain through a wooden flume.（木材通过木制水槽从山上运下来。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{flurry}{'flʌri}
  \wordbreakdown{flu (= flow) + -rry (表示急促)}
  \wordfamily{flush 冲洗；flutter 飘动；fluster 使慌张}
  \wordsense{n. 阵风，阵雨，阵雪；慌张}
  \wordphrase{a flurry of activity}
  \wordexample{There was a flurry of activity as the guests arrived unexpectedly.（客人突然到来，引起了一阵忙乱。）}
  \wordsense{v. 使（某人）慌张}
  \wordphrase{get flurried}
  \wordexample{Don't get flurried; there's still plenty of time.（别慌，还有很多时间。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{flush}{flʌʃ}
  \wordbreakdown{flu (= flow) + -sh (表示急促动作)}
  \wordfamily{flushed 脸红的；兴奋的；blush 脸红}
  \wordsense{v.（脸）发红；用水冲洗；（水）奔流}
  \wordphrase{flush the toilet}
  \wordexample{The girl flushed when the boy spoke to her.（男孩跟女孩搭话时，女孩的脸红了。）}
  \wordsense{n. 红晕；激动，兴奋}
  \wordphrase{in the first flush of victory}
  \wordexample{In the first flush of victory, the team celebrated wildly.（在胜利的最初喜悦中，队伍疯狂庆祝。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{fluvial}{'flu:viəl}
  \wordbreakdown{flu (= flow) + -vi- + -al}
  \wordfamily{fluviatile 河流的；alluvial 冲积的；alluvium 冲积层}
  \wordsense{a. 河流的，生长在河流中的}
  \wordphrase{fluvial deposits}
  \wordexample{Geologists studied the fluvial deposits to understand the ancient river's course.（地质学家研究了河流沉积物以了解古河道的走向。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{flux}{flʌks}
  \wordbreakdown{flu (= flow) + -x}
  \wordfamily{influx 涌入；efflux 流出；reflux 回流}
  \wordsense{n. 流动；不断变动；涨潮；焊剂}
  \wordphrase{in a state of flux}
  \wordexample{The company has been in a state of flux since the new CEO took over.（自从新CEO上任以来，公司一直处于不断变动之中。）}
\end{word}



\chapter{LEG, LECT[LIG, LEAG]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 leg，lect 有 law，to gather，to choose，to read，to send 等多种含义。其变形有 lig，leag 等。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{allegiance}{ə'li:dʒəns}
  \wordbreakdown{朝向法的心 al<ad (= to) + leg (= law) + -iance}
  \wordfamily{allege 断言；allegation 指控；loyal 忠诚的}
  \wordsense{n. 忠诚；拥戴}
  \wordphrase{pledge allegiance to}
  \wordexample{I don't feel much allegiance to my company.（我对我们公司没有多少效忠之心。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{negligible}{'neglidʒəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{不值得收集的→可忽略的 neg (= not) + lig<leg (= gather) + -ible}
  \wordfamily{negligence 疏忽；negligent 疏忽的；neglect 忽视}
  \wordsense{a. 可以忽略的，微不足道的}
  \wordphrase{a negligible amount}
  \wordexample{A negligible amount of marijuana was discovered in his automobile.（在他的车内发现了极少量的大麻。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{diligent}{'dilidʒənt}
  \wordbreakdown{个别地选择→精心挑选→勤奋的 di<dis (= apart) + lig<leg (= choose) + -ent}
  \wordfamily{diligence 勤勉；diligently 勤奋地；industrious 勤劳的}
  \wordsense{a. 勤奋的；勤勉的}
  \wordphrase{a diligent student}
  \wordexample{She is a diligent worker though she lacks imagination.（她虽然缺乏想像力，但工作还是很勤奋的。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{eclectic}{i'klektik}
  \wordbreakdown{从中挑选的 ec<ex (= out) + lect (= choose) + -ic}
  \wordfamily{eclecticism 折中主义；eclectically 折中地；select 选择}
  \wordsense{a. 折中的；兼收并蓄的；不拘一格的}
  \wordphrase{eclectic taste}
  \wordexample{She has eclectic taste, as you can tell by her living room.（从她的客厅可以看出她不拘一格的品味。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{predilection}{ˌpri:di'lekʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{事先挑选出来→偏爱 pre (= before) + di (= apart) + lect (= choose) + -ion}
  \wordfamily{predispose 使倾向于；preference 偏好；eclectic 折中的}
  \wordsense{n. 嗜好，偏爱}
  \wordphrase{a predilection for}
  \wordexample{I have a strong predilection for sweets.（我特别偏爱甜食。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{legible}{'ledʒəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{能阅读的 leg (= read) + -ible}
  \wordfamily{legibility 易读性；illegible 难读的；readable 可读的}
  \wordsense{a.（字迹等）易读的；易辨认的}
  \wordphrase{barely legible}
  \wordexample{The postmark isn't legible. It might say ``New York.''（邮戳看不清，可能写的是”纽约”。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{illegible}{i'ledʒəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{不能阅读的 il<in (= not) + leg (= read) + -ible}
  \wordfamily{illegibility 不可辨认；legible 易读的；illegibly 难辨认地}
  \wordsense{a. 难读的，难辨认的}
  \wordphrase{illegible handwriting}
  \wordexample{The secretary couldn't type the letter because her boss's handwriting was illegible.（秘书无法录入那封信，因为老板的字迹难以辨认。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{allege}{ə'ledʒ}
  \wordbreakdown{用力向某人寄送→声称 al<ad (= to, intensive) + leg (= send)}
  \wordfamily{alleged 被断定的；未经证实的；allegation 指控；断言}
  \wordsense{vt.（无充分证据地）主张，断言；辩解}
  \wordphrase{allege that}
  \wordexample{He alleges that he was not speeding.（他辩解说自己没有超速行驶。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{relegate}{'religeit}
  \wordbreakdown{向后输送→降级 re (= back) + leg (= send) + -ate}
  \wordfamily{relegation 降级；放逐；delegate 委派；legate 使节}
  \wordsense{vt. 使降级；放逐；移交，委托}
  \wordphrase{relegate to a lower division}
  \wordexample{He relegated his stepson to the broken-down bedroom over the garage.（他把他的继子赶到车库上面那个破旧的卧室去了。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{intellectual}{ˌintə'lektʃuəl}
  \wordbreakdown{在中间选择→有辨别力的 intel<inter (= between) + lect (= choose, gather) + -ual}
  \wordfamily{intellect 智力；intelligence 智能；intelligentsia 知识分子阶层}
  \wordsense{n. 知识分子}
  \wordphrase{a public intellectual}
  \wordexample{While bright, I hardly think he qualifies as an intellectual.（他虽然很聪明，不过我并不认为他够格做一个知识分子。）}
  \wordsense{a. 智力的；智力超群的}
  \wordphrase{intellectual property}
  \wordexample{The company fiercely protects its intellectual property rights.（该公司极力保护其知识产权。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{legendary}{'ledʒəndri}
  \wordbreakdown{被传读下来的 leg (= read) + -end + -ary}
  \wordfamily{legend 传说；传奇人物；legendarily 传奇般地}
  \wordsense{a. 传说的；传奇的；著名的}
  \wordphrase{legendary figure}
  \wordexample{His appetite is legendary. He can actually eat 25 hot dogs at once!（他的胃口大是出了名的，他一次可以吃掉25个热狗！）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{legitimate}{li'dʒitimit}
  \wordbreakdown{合乎法律的 leg (= law) + -itim- + -ate}
  \wordfamily{legitimacy 合法性；legitimize 使合法化；illegitimate 非法的}
  \wordsense{a. 合法的；合理的；正统的；嫡出的}
  \wordphrase{a legitimate concern}
  \wordexample{Is corporal punishment a legitimate teaching method? I think not.（体罚是一种合理的教育方式吗？我认为不是。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{legal}{'li:gəl}
  \wordbreakdown{leg (= law) + -al}
  \wordfamily{legalism 守法主义；legally 合法地；illegal 非法的；legality 合法性}
  \wordsense{a. 法律的；法定的；合法的}
  \wordphrase{take legal action}
  \wordexample{If they refuse to pay, we will take legal action against them.（如果他们拒绝付款，我们将对他们提起诉讼。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{legality}{li:'gæləti}
  \wordbreakdown{leg (= law) + -ality}
  \wordfamily{legal 合法的；legalize 使合法化；illegal 非法的}
  \wordsense{n. 合法（性）}
  \wordphrase{question the legality of}
  \wordexample{Lawyers questioned the legality of the government's surveillance program.（律师们质疑政府监控项目的合法性。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{legalize}{'li:gəlaiz}
  \wordbreakdown{leg (= law) + -al + -ize}
  \wordfamily{legalization 合法化；legal 合法的；illegalize 使非法化}
  \wordsense{v. 使合法化}
  \wordphrase{legalize marijuana}
  \wordexample{Several states have voted to legalize the sale of marijuana.（几个州已投票通过使大麻销售合法化。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{legislate}{'ledʒisleit}
  \wordbreakdown{制定法律 leg (= law) + -is- + lat (= carry, produce) + -e}
  \wordfamily{legislation 立法；法律；legislator 立法者；legislature 立法机关}
  \wordsense{v. 制定法律}
  \wordphrase{legislate against}
  \wordexample{The government plans to legislate against discrimination in the workplace.（政府计划立法禁止工作场所的歧视。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{legislative}{'ledʒislətiv}
  \wordbreakdown{leg (= law) + -is- + lat (= carry) + -ive}
  \wordfamily{legislate 立法；legislation 法律；legislature 立法机关}
  \wordsense{a. 立法的；法律规定的}
  \wordphrase{the legislative branch}
  \wordexample{The legislative branch of government is responsible for making laws.（政府的立法部门负责制定法律。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{privilege}{'privilidʒ}
  \wordbreakdown{个人的法律→特权 priv (= single, peculiar) + i + leg (= law) + -e}
  \wordfamily{privileged 有特权的；underprivileged 贫困的；prerogative 特权}
  \wordsense{n. 特权；荣幸}
  \wordphrase{a privilege to do}
  \wordexample{It was a privilege to meet such a distinguished scientist.（能见到如此杰出的科学家是一种荣幸。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{privileged}{'privilidʒd}
  \wordbreakdown{priv (= single, peculiar) + i + leg (= law) + -ed}
  \wordfamily{privilege 特权；underprivileged 贫困的；overprivileged 享有过多特权的}
  \wordsense{a. 享有特权的}
  \wordphrase{the privileged few}
  \wordexample{Only the privileged few could afford such luxuries in those days.（在那个年代，只有少数特权阶层才能负担得起这样的奢侈品。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{analects}{'ænəlekts}
  \wordbreakdown{再次收集→选集 ana (= again, up) + lect (= gather) + -s}
  \wordfamily{collect 收集；select 选择；anthology 文选}
  \wordsense{n. 论集，文选}
  \wordphrase{the Confucian Analects}
  \wordexample{The Confucian Analects is one of the most influential works in Chinese philosophy.（《论语》是中国哲学中最有影响力的著作之一。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{collect}{kə'lekt}
  \wordbreakdown{聚集在一起 col<com (= together) + lect (= gather)}
  \wordfamily{collection 收藏；collector 收藏家；collected 镇定的；collective 集体的}
  \wordsense{vt. 收集；收藏}
  \wordphrase{collect stamps}
  \wordexample{He has been collecting rare stamps since he was a child.（他从小就一直在收集珍稀邮票。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 收取（税金、捐款、账单等）}
  \wordphrase{collect taxes}
  \wordexample{The government collects income tax from all working citizens.（政府向所有在职公民征收所得税。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 接走（某人）}
  \wordphrase{collect the children from school}
  \wordexample{I need to collect the children from school at three o\textquotesingle clock.（我需要三点钟去学校接孩子。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 使镇定；使集中（思想）}
  \wordphrase{collect oneself}
  \wordexample{She paused to collect herself before giving the speech.（她停顿了一下让自己镇定，然后开始演讲。）}
  \wordsense{a./ad. 由受话人付费的}
  \wordphrase{call collect}
  \wordexample{He called his parents collect from the airport.（他从机场打了一个对方付费的电话给父母。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{collective}{kə'lektiv}
  \wordbreakdown{col<com (= together) + lect (= gather) + -ive}
  \wordfamily{collectively 共同地；collectivism 集体主义；collectivize 使集体化}
  \wordsense{a. 集体的，共同的}
  \wordphrase{collective bargaining}
  \wordexample{The workers engaged in collective bargaining to negotiate better wages.（工人们进行集体谈判以争取更高的工资。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{colleague}{'kɒli:g}
  \wordbreakdown{一起聚集的人→同事 col<com (= together) + leag<leg (= gather)}
  \wordfamily{college 学院；collegiate 大学的；league 联盟}
  \wordsense{n. 同事}
  \wordphrase{a former colleague}
  \wordexample{I ran into a former colleague at the conference last week.（上周我在会议上碰到了一位前同事。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{college}{'kɒlidʒ}
  \wordbreakdown{聚集在一起的人→团体→学院 col<com (= together) + leg (= gather)}
  \wordfamily{collegiate 大学的；colleague 同事；collegial 学院的}
  \wordsense{n. 大学；学院；专科学校；团体，协会}
  \wordphrase{go to college}
  \wordexample{She was the first in her family to go to college.（她是家里第一个上大学的人。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{collegiate}{kə'li:dʒiət}
  \wordbreakdown{col<com (= together) + leg (= gather) + -iate}
  \wordfamily{college 学院；colleague 同事；intercollegiate 大学间的}
  \wordsense{a. 大学（生）的}
  \wordphrase{collegiate athletics}
  \wordexample{Collegiate athletics play a major role in American university culture.（大学体育在美国大学文化中扮演着重要角色。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{legion}{'li:dʒən}
  \wordbreakdown{聚集起来的人→军团 leg (= gather) + -ion}
  \wordfamily{legionary 军团的；legionnaire 军团士兵；legendary 传奇的}
  \wordsense{n. 古罗马军团；众多}
  \wordphrase{their numbers are legion}
  \wordexample{Their numbers are legion.（他们数量众多。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{neglect}{ni'glekt}
  \wordbreakdown{不收集→不关注→忽视 neg (= not) + lect (= gather)}
  \wordfamily{neglectful 疏忽的；negligence 过失；negligent 粗心的}
  \wordsense{vt. 忽视；忽略}
  \wordphrase{neglect one\textquotesingle s duties}
  \wordexample{He was fired for neglect of duty.（他因玩忽职守而被解雇。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 疏于照管；对……不够重视}
  \wordphrase{neglect one\textquotesingle s health}
  \wordexample{She neglected her health while working long hours.（她长时间工作，疏于照顾自己的健康。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 未能做到；遗漏}
  \wordphrase{neglect to mention}
  \wordexample{He neglected to mention that he had already resigned.（他没有提到自己已经辞职了。）}
  \wordsense{n. 忽视；疏忽；被忽视的状态}
  \wordphrase{fall into neglect}
  \wordexample{The old building had fallen into neglect over the years.（这座老建筑多年来一直处于无人照管的状态。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{negligence}{'neglidʒəns}
  \wordbreakdown{neg (= not) + lig<leg (= gather) + -ence}
  \wordfamily{negligent 粗心的；neglect 忽视；negligible 可忽略的}
  \wordsense{n. 过失（罪）；疏忽，粗心}
  \wordphrase{criminal negligence}
  \wordexample{The doctor was sued for negligence after the patient's condition worsened.（患者病情恶化后，医生因过失被起诉。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{negligent}{'neglidʒənt}
  \wordbreakdown{neg (= not) + lig<leg (= gather) + -ent}
  \wordfamily{negligence 过失；negligible 可忽略的；neglect 忽视}
  \wordsense{a. 不注意的，粗心大意的}
  \wordphrase{negligent driving}
  \wordexample{He was charged with negligent driving after the accident.（事故发生后，他被指控疏忽驾驶。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{dialect}{'daiəlekt}
  \wordbreakdown{通过选择→区分→方言 dia (= through) + lect (= choose)}
  \wordfamily{dialectal 方言的；dialectic 辩证的；idiolect 个人语言}
  \wordsense{n. 方言，地方话；行话}
  \wordphrase{a regional dialect}
  \wordexample{The novel is written in the dialect of rural southern China.（这部小说是用中国南方农村方言写的。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{dialectic}{ˌdaiə'lektik}
  \wordbreakdown{dia (= through) + lect (= choose) + -ic}
  \wordfamily{dialectician 辩证学家；dialectical 辩证的；dialect 方言}
  \wordsense{a. 辩证的}
  \wordphrase{dialectic reasoning}
  \wordexample{Hegel's dialectic philosophy influenced many later thinkers.（黑格尔的辩证哲学影响了许多后来的思想家。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{dialectical}{ˌdaiə'lektikəl}
  \wordbreakdown{dia (= through) + lect (= choose) + -ical}
  \wordfamily{dialectic 辩证的；dialect 方言；dialectics 辩证法}
  \wordsense{a. 辩证的；方言的}
  \wordphrase{dialectical materialism}
  \wordexample{Dialectical materialism is the philosophical basis of Marxism.（辩证唯物主义是马克思主义的哲学基础。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{elect}{i'lekt}
  \wordbreakdown{选出来 e<ex (= out) + lect (= choose)}
  \wordfamily{election 选举；elective 选修的；elector 选举人；electoral 选举的}
  \wordsense{v. 选举；选择，决定}
  \wordphrase{elect a president}
  \wordexample{The committee elected her as chairperson by a unanimous vote.（委员会一致投票选她为主席。）}
  \wordsense{a. 当选而尚未上任的}
  \wordphrase{the president-elect}
  \wordexample{The president-elect will take office in January.（当选总统将于一月就职。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{election}{i'lekʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{e<ex (= out) + lect (= choose) + -ion}
  \wordfamily{elect 选举；elective 选修的；elector 选举人}
  \wordsense{n. 选举；当选}
  \wordphrase{a general election}
  \wordexample{The general election will be held next month.（大选将于下个月举行。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{elective}{i'lektiv}
  \wordbreakdown{e<ex (= out) + lect (= choose) + -ive}
  \wordfamily{elect 选举；election 选举；electoral 选举的}
  \wordsense{a. 选修的；可以选择的；由选举产生的}
  \wordphrase{an elective course}
  \wordexample{Students must take at least three elective courses each semester.（学生每学期必须至少选修三门选修课。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{elector}{i'lektə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{e<ex (= out) + lect (= choose) + -or}
  \wordfamily{electorate 选民；electoral 选举的；elect 选举}
  \wordsense{n. 选举人}
  \wordphrase{presidential electors}
  \wordexample{In the US, presidential electors cast the official votes for president.（在美国，总统选举人投出选举总统的正式选票。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{electoral}{i'lektorəl}
  \wordbreakdown{e<ex (= out) + lect (= choose) + -or + -al}
  \wordfamily{elector 选举人；electorate 选民；election 选举}
  \wordsense{a. 选举（人）的}
  \wordphrase{the electoral college}
  \wordexample{The electoral college system is unique to the United States.（选举人团制度是美国独有的。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{elegant}{'eligənt}
  \wordbreakdown{精心挑选的→优雅的 e<ex (= out) + leg (= choose) + -ant}
  \wordfamily{elegance 优雅；inelegant 不雅的；elegantly 优雅地}
  \wordsense{a. 优雅的；精致的}
  \wordphrase{elegant manners}
  \wordexample{She moved with elegant grace across the ballroom floor.（她优雅地穿过舞厅。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{eligible}{'elidʒəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{能被选出的→合格的 e<ex (= out) + lig<leg (= choose) + -ible}
  \wordfamily{eligibility 资格；ineligible 不合格的；elect 选举}
  \wordsense{a. 合格的，有资格被选中的；合适的}
  \wordphrase{eligible for}
  \wordexample{You must be over 18 to be eligible to vote.（你必须年满18岁才有资格投票。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{intelligence}{in'telidʒəns}
  \wordbreakdown{在中间选择→辨别力→智力 intel<inter (= between) + lig<leg (= choose) + -ence}
  \wordfamily{intelligent 聪明的；intelligentsia 知识分子；intelligible 可理解的}
  \wordsense{n. 智力；智慧；聪明}
  \wordphrase{artificial intelligence}
  \wordexample{Artificial intelligence is transforming every industry.（人工智能正在改变每一个行业。）}
  \wordsense{n. 情报；谍报}
  \wordphrase{intelligence agency}
  \wordexample{The intelligence agency gathered information about enemy movements.（情报机构收集了关于敌军动向的信息。）}
  \wordsense{n. 情报人员；情报机构}
  \wordphrase{military intelligence}
  \wordexample{He worked in military intelligence during the war.（战争期间他在军事情报部门工作。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{intelligent}{in'telidʒənt}
  \wordbreakdown{intel<inter (= between) + lig<leg (= choose) + -ent}
  \wordfamily{intelligence 智力；intelligently 聪明地；unintelligent 不聪明的}
  \wordsense{a. 有才智的；聪明的}
  \wordphrase{an intelligent question}
  \wordexample{She gave intelligent answers to all the interview questions.（她对所有面试问题都给出了聪明的回答。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{intelligentsia}{inˌtelidʒent'siə}
  \wordbreakdown{intel<inter (= between) + lig<leg (= choose) + -entsia}
  \wordfamily{intelligent 聪明的；intelligence 智力；intellectual 知识分子}
  \wordsense{n. 【总称】知识分子（阶层）}
  \wordphrase{the intelligentsia}
  \wordexample{The intelligentsia played a key role in the country's reform movement.（知识分子阶层在国家的改革运动中发挥了关键作用。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{intelligible}{in'telidʒəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{intel<inter (= between) + lig<leg (= choose) + -ible}
  \wordfamily{intelligibility 易理解；unintelligible 难理解的；comprehensible 可理解的}
  \wordsense{a. 可理解的，明了的}
  \wordphrase{barely intelligible}
  \wordexample{His speech was barely intelligible through the thick accent.（由于浓重的口音，他的话几乎听不懂。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{select}{si'lekt}
  \wordbreakdown{分开选择→精选 se (= apart, away) + lect (= choose)}
  \wordfamily{selection 选择；selective 选择性的；selector 选择者}
  \wordsense{v. 选择，选拔，挑选}
  \wordphrase{select a candidate}
  \wordexample{The committee will select the best candidate for the position.（委员会将为该职位选出最佳候选人。）}
  \wordsense{a. 精选的；严格挑选的}
  \wordphrase{a select group}
  \wordexample{Only a select few were invited to the private reception.（只有精心挑选的少数人被邀请参加私人招待会。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{selection}{si'lekʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{se (= apart, away) + lect (= choose) + -ion}
  \wordfamily{select 选择；selective 选择性的；natural selection 自然选择}
  \wordsense{n. 选拔，选择；精选品；选集}
  \wordphrase{natural selection}
  \wordexample{Darwin's theory of natural selection revolutionized biology.（达尔文的自然选择理论彻底改变了生物学。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{selective}{si'lektiv}
  \wordbreakdown{se (= apart, away) + lect (= choose) + -ive}
  \wordfamily{selectivity 选择性；select 选择；selection 选择}
  \wordsense{a. 选择性的；有选择能力的}
  \wordphrase{selective about}
  \wordexample{She is very selective about the projects she takes on.（她对承接的项目非常挑剔。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{elegiac}{ˌeli'dʒaiək}
  \wordbreakdown{e<ex (= out) + leg (= read) + -iac → 读出来的哀伤}
  \wordfamily{elegy 挽歌；elegize 写挽歌；elegist 挽歌作者}
  \wordsense{a. 挽歌体的；哀伤的}
  \wordphrase{elegiac couplets}
  \wordexample{The poem has an elegiac tone that mourns the passing of youth.（这首诗有一种哀悼青春逝去的挽歌般的基调。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{elegy}{'elidʒi}
  \wordbreakdown{e<ex (= out) + leg (= read) + -y → 读出来的哀悼之辞}
  \wordfamily{elegiac 挽歌的；eulogy 颂词；dirge 挽歌}
  \wordsense{n.（为死者的）哀歌，挽歌}
  \wordphrase{write an elegy for}
  \wordexample{The poet wrote an elegy for his fallen comrades.（诗人为他牺牲的战友写了一首挽歌。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{lectern}{'lektən}
  \wordbreakdown{lect<leg (= read) + -ern → 读书的地方}
  \wordfamily{lecture 演讲；lector 朗读者；lesson 课}
  \wordsense{n.（教堂的）讲经台；演讲台}
  \wordphrase{stand at the lectern}
  \wordexample{The professor placed her notes on the lectern and began the lecture.（教授把笔记放在讲台上，开始了演讲。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{lecture}{'lektʃə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{lect (= read) + -ure → 读出来→演讲}
  \wordfamily{lecturer 讲师；lectureship 讲师职位；lectern 讲台}
  \wordsense{n. 演讲；训话}
  \wordphrase{give a lecture on}
  \wordexample{The professor gave a fascinating lecture on quantum physics.（教授做了一场关于量子物理的精彩演讲。）}
  \wordsense{v. 讲课；训诫}
  \wordphrase{lecture someone about}
  \wordexample{My mother always lectures me about eating healthier food.（我妈妈总是训诫我要吃更健康的食物。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{delegate}{'deligit}
  \wordbreakdown{派遣出去 de (= away, off) + leg (= send) + -ate}
  \wordfamily{delegation 代表团；delegacy 代表制度；relegate 降级}
  \wordsense{n. 代表}
  \wordphrase{send a delegate}
  \wordexample{Each country sent a delegate to the international conference.（每个国家都派了一名代表参加国际会议。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 委派为代表；授权}
  \wordphrase{delegate authority}
  \wordexample{A good manager knows how to delegate tasks effectively.（好的管理者知道如何有效地委派任务。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{delegation}{ˌdeli'geiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{de (= away, off) + leg (= send) + -ation}
  \wordfamily{delegate（代表）；delegacy（代表制度）；allegiance（忠诚）}
  \wordsense{n. 代表团；授权，委托}
  \wordphrase{a trade delegation}
  \wordexample{A delegation of business leaders visited the factory.（一个商界领袖代表团参观了工厂。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{legacy}{'legəsi}
  \wordbreakdown{leg (= send) + -acy → 送出去的东西→遗产}
  \wordfamily{legate 使节；legatee 遗产受赠人；delegate 代表}
  \wordsense{n. 遗产，遗赠；留给后人的东西}
  \wordphrase{leave a legacy}
  \wordexample{The war left a legacy of hatred that lasted for generations.（战争留下了延续数代人的仇恨遗产。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{legate}{'legət}
  \wordbreakdown{leg (= send) + -ate → 被派遣的人}
  \wordfamily{legacy 遗产；legation 公使馆；delegate 代表}
  \wordsense{n. 教皇的使节}
  \wordphrase{a papal legate}
  \wordexample{The papal legate arrived to negotiate peace between the warring kingdoms.（教皇使节到来，为交战的王国斡旋和平。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{legatee}{ˌlegə'ti:}
  \wordbreakdown{leg (= send) + -atee → 被送予遗产的人}
  \wordfamily{legacy 遗产；legate 使节；bequest 遗赠}
  \wordsense{n. 遗产受赠人}
  \wordphrase{the sole legatee}
  \wordexample{She was named as the sole legatee in her uncle's will.（她被指定为叔叔遗嘱中的唯一受赠人。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{legation}{li'geiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{leg (= send) + -ation}
  \wordfamily{legate 使节；delegate 代表；embassy 大使馆}
  \wordsense{n. 公使馆；公使馆全体人员}
  \wordphrase{the foreign legation}
  \wordexample{The legation was attacked during the uprising.（公使馆在起义期间遭到袭击。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{recollect}{ˌrekə'lekt}
  \wordbreakdown{重新收集→回想 re (= back, again) + col<com (= together) + lect (= gather)}
  \wordfamily{recollection 回忆；collect 收集；recall 回忆}
  \wordsense{v. 想起，回想}
  \wordphrase{recollect one's childhood}
  \wordexample{I can barely recollect what happened that night.（我几乎想不起那天晚上发生了什么。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{recollection}{ˌrekə'lekʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{re (= back, again) + col<com (= together) + lect (= gather) + -ion}
  \wordfamily{recollect 回想；memory 记忆；reminiscence 回忆}
  \wordsense{n. 回忆；记忆（力）；追忆的往事}
  \wordphrase{to the best of my recollection}
  \wordexample{To the best of my recollection, we met only once before.（据我回忆，我们之前只见过一次面。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{sacrilege}{'sækrəlidʒ}
  \wordbreakdown{偷取神圣之物→亵渎 sacr (= holy) + i + leg (= gather, take)}
  \wordfamily{sacrilegious 亵渎的；sacred 神圣的；sacrifice 牺牲}
  \wordsense{n. 亵渎神圣}
  \wordphrase{commit sacrilege}
  \wordexample{To many fans, changing the team's name would be sacrilege.（对许多球迷来说，更改球队名称就是亵渎。）}
\end{word}



\chapter{PAR[PAIR, PER, PIR, PEER]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 par 意为 to arrange, to appear, to produce, equal。其变形有 pair, per, pir, peer 等。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{apparel}{ə'pærəl}
  \wordbreakdown{靠近排列→穿戴整齐 ap<ad (= to, near) + par (= arrange) + -el}
  \wordfamily{apparatus 设备；prepare 准备；apparent 明显的}
  \wordsense{n. 衣着；服饰}
  \wordphrase{sports apparel}
  \wordexample{My teacher told me that shorts aren't appropriate apparel for class.（老师告诉我穿短裤来上课不合适。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{imperative}{im'perətiv}
  \wordbreakdown{在里面安排→命令 im<in (= in, intensive) + per<par (= arrange) + -ative}
  \wordfamily{imperious 专横的；emperor 皇帝；imperial 帝国的}
  \wordsense{a. 必需的；紧急的；命令式的}
  \wordphrase{it is imperative that}
  \wordexample{It's imperative to remove your shoes once you enter the temple.（进入寺院时，你必须脱掉鞋子。）}
  \wordsense{n. 命令；必要的事}
  \wordphrase{a moral imperative}
  \wordexample{Protecting the environment is a moral imperative for our generation.（保护环境是我们这一代人的道德责任。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{imperious}{im'piəriəs}
  \wordbreakdown{im<in (= in, intensive) + per<par (= arrange) + -ious → 发号施令的}
  \wordfamily{imperative 命令的；imperial 帝国的；imperialism 帝国主义}
  \wordsense{a. 飞扬跋扈的，傲慢的，专横的}
  \wordphrase{an imperious tone}
  \wordexample{She's an imperious little tot, always bossing around her young cousins.（她是一个专横的小女孩，经常对她的堂弟妹颐指气使。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{irreparable}{i'repərəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{无法重新整顿的 ir<in (= not) + re (= again) + par (= arrange) + -able}
  \wordfamily{repair 修理；reparation 赔偿；irreparably 不可挽回地}
  \wordsense{a. 无法修补的；无法挽回的}
  \wordphrase{irreparable damage}
  \wordexample{A broken teacup is irreparable, no matter what the glue package says.（无论胶水的外包装上怎么写，摔碎的茶杯都是无法修补的。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{apparition}{ˌæpə'riʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{出现在附近 ap<ad (= to, near) + par (= appear) + -ition}
  \wordfamily{apparent 明显的；appear 出现；disappear 消失}
  \wordsense{n. 幽灵，鬼怪；幻影}
  \wordphrase{a ghostly apparition}
  \wordexample{She claims to see apparitions in the graveyard at night.（她坚持说晚上在墓地看到幽灵。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{transparent}{træns'pærənt}
  \wordbreakdown{通过某物显现到另一边 trans (= through, across) + par (= appear) + -ent}
  \wordfamily{transparency 透明度；translucent 半透明的；opaque 不透明的}
  \wordsense{a. 透明的；清澈的；明了的}
  \wordphrase{transparent to the public}
  \wordexample{The transparent turquoise waters of the Caribbean are spectacular.（加勒比海透明的蓝绿色海水非常壮观。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{disparage}{dis'pæridʒ}
  \wordbreakdown{认为不平等→贬低 dis (= not) + par (= equal) + -age}
  \wordfamily{disparagement 贬低；disparagingly 轻蔑地；belittle 贬低}
  \wordsense{vt. 轻视，蔑视；贬低}
  \wordphrase{disparage someone's efforts}
  \wordexample{Insecure people often disparage the achievements of others.（不安的人总是会贬低别人的成绩。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{disparity}{dis'pærəti}
  \wordbreakdown{不平等 dis (= not) + par (= equal) + -ity}
  \wordfamily{disparage 贬低；parity 平等；comparable 可比较的}
  \wordsense{n. 不同，不等；差距}
  \wordphrase{income disparity}
  \wordexample{The disparity between rich and poor in Venezuela is startling.（委内瑞拉的贫富差距令人吃惊。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{impair}{im'peə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{使不平等→使变差 im<in (= not) + pair<par (= equal)}
  \wordfamily{impairment 损害；repair 修理；unimpaired 未受损的}
  \wordsense{vt. 损伤；削弱}
  \wordphrase{impair one's health}
  \wordexample{Alcohol had so impaired his faculties that he was unable to drive.（酒精极大地损伤了他的机能，以至于他开不了车了。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{apparatus}{ˌæpə'reitəs}
  \wordbreakdown{靠近安排好的→准备好的设备 ap<ad (= near) + par (= arrange) + -atus}
  \wordfamily{apparel 服饰；prepare 准备；repair 修理}
  \wordsense{n.（一套）装置，设备；器官；机构}
  \wordphrase{laboratory apparatus}
  \wordexample{The laboratory is equipped with the latest scientific apparatus.（实验室配备了最新的科学设备。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{empire}{'empaiə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{em<en (= intensive) + pir<par (= arrange) + -e → 统治安排→帝国}
  \wordfamily{emperor 皇帝；empress 皇后；imperial 帝国的}
  \wordsense{n. 帝国}
  \wordphrase{the Roman Empire}
  \wordexample{The Roman Empire lasted for over a thousand years.（罗马帝国延续了一千多年。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{imperial}{im'piəriəl}
  \wordbreakdown{im<in (= intensive) + per<par (= arrange) + -ial → 统治的}
  \wordfamily{empire 帝国；imperialism 帝国主义；emperor 皇帝}
  \wordsense{a. 帝国的；皇帝的；宏大的}
  \wordphrase{the Imperial Palace}
  \wordexample{The Imperial Palace in Beijing is one of the world's greatest architectural achievements.（北京的故宫是世界上最伟大的建筑成就之一。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{imperialism}{im'piəriəlizəm}
  \wordbreakdown{im<in (= intensive) + per<par (= arrange) + -ial + -ism}
  \wordfamily{imperialist 帝国主义者；imperialistic 帝国主义的；empire 帝国}
  \wordsense{n. 帝国主义；扩张主义}
  \wordphrase{cultural imperialism}
  \wordexample{Critics accused the company of cultural imperialism for imposing its values abroad.（批评者指责该公司在海外强加其价值观，是文化帝国主义。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{parade}{pə'reid}
  \wordbreakdown{par (= arrange) + -ade → 安排好的队列}
  \wordfamily{pare 削；prepare 准备；eparade 阅兵}
  \wordsense{n. 阅兵；游行；炫耀}
  \wordphrase{a military parade}
  \wordexample{Thousands lined the streets to watch the victory parade.（数千人站在街道两旁观看胜利游行。）}
  \wordsense{v. 列队行进；炫耀}
  \wordphrase{parade one's wealth}
  \wordexample{He likes to parade his knowledge in front of others.（他喜欢在别人面前炫耀自己的知识。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{pare}{peə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{par (= arrange) + -e → 整理→削去多余部分}
  \wordfamily{parings 削皮；prepare 准备；repair 修理}
  \wordsense{v. 削皮；修剪；消减}
  \wordphrase{pare down expenses}
  \wordexample{We need to pare down our expenses to stay within budget.（我们需要削减开支以保持在预算之内。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{prepare}{pri'peə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{预先安排好 pre (= before) + par (= arrange) + -e}
  \wordfamily{preparation 准备；preparedness 准备状态；preparatory 预备的}
  \wordsense{v. 准备}
  \wordphrase{prepare for an exam}
  \wordexample{You should prepare for the interview by researching the company.（你应该通过研究公司来为面试做准备。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{preparation}{ˌprepə'reiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{pre (= before) + par (= arrange) + -ation}
  \wordfamily{prepare 准备；preparatory 预备的；prepared 有准备的}
  \wordsense{n. 准备，预备；配制}
  \wordphrase{make preparations for}
  \wordexample{The team is making preparations for the upcoming tournament.（团队正在为即将到来的锦标赛做准备。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{preparatory}{pri'pærətri}
  \wordbreakdown{pre (= before) + par (= arrange) + -atory}
  \wordfamily{preparation 准备；prepare 准备；preliminary 初步的}
  \wordsense{a. 准备的，预备的}
  \wordphrase{preparatory school}
  \wordexample{He attended a preparatory school before entering university.（他在进入大学之前上了一所预备学校。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{repair}{ri'peə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{重新安排好→修理 re (= again) + pair<par (= arrange)}
  \wordfamily{repairable 可修理的；reparable 可补偿的；irreparable 无法修补的}
  \wordsense{v. 修理；修补}
  \wordphrase{repair the damage}
  \wordexample{It will take months to repair the damage caused by the flood.（修复洪水造成的损害需要数月时间。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{reparable}{'repərəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{re (= again) + par (= arrange) + -able}
  \wordfamily{repair 修理；reparation 赔偿；irreparable 无法修补的}
  \wordsense{a.（损失等）可补偿的；可修缮的}
  \wordphrase{reparable damage}
  \wordexample{Fortunately, the damage to the building is reparable.（幸运的是，建筑物的损坏是可以修复的。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{reparation}{ˌrepə'reiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{re (= again) + par (= arrange) + -ation}
  \wordfamily{reparable 可补偿的；repair 修理；irreparable 无法修补的}
  \wordsense{n. 补偿；赔偿；（pl.）（战败国的）赔款}
  \wordphrase{war reparations}
  \wordexample{Germany was required to pay war reparations after World War I.（第一次世界大战后，德国被要求支付战争赔款。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{separate}{'sepəreit}
  \wordbreakdown{分开安排 se (= away, apart) + par (= arrange) + -ate}
  \wordfamily{separation 分离；separately 分别地；separatist 分离主义者}
  \wordsense{v. 分离，分开；分散}
  \wordphrase{separate fact from fiction}
  \wordexample{It's important to separate fact from fiction when reading the news.（阅读新闻时，区分事实和虚构很重要。）}
  \wordsense{a. 分离的，分开的；单独的}
  \wordphrase{a separate issue}
  \wordexample{That is a separate issue and should be discussed later.（那是一个单独的问题，应该稍后讨论。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{separation}{ˌsepə'reiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{分开安排→分离 se (= apart, away) + par (= arrange) + -ation}
  \wordfamily{separate 分离；separable 可分离的；inseparable 不可分的}
  \wordsense{n. 分离；分开}
  \wordphrase{after a separation of five years}
  \wordexample{After a separation of five years, the twins were finally reunited.（分离五年后，这对双胞胎终于团聚了。）}
  \wordsense{n. 【政】分权；分立}
  \wordphrase{separation of powers}
  \wordexample{The separation of powers prevents any one branch from becoming too powerful.（三权分立防止任何一个部门权力过大。）}
  \wordsense{n. 【律】（夫妇）分居}
  \wordphrase{judicial separation}
  \wordexample{They agreed to a legal separation before filing for divorce.（他们在提出离婚之前同意了合法分居。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{separable}{'sepərəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{可以分开的 se (= apart) + par (= arrange) + -able}
  \wordfamily{separate 分离；separation 分离；inseparable 不可分的}
  \wordsense{a. 可分离的；可分开的}
  \wordphrase{separable from}
  \wordexample{In English, some phrasal verbs are separable and some are not.（在英语中，有些短语动词可分，有些不可分。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{apparent}{ə'pærənt}
  \wordbreakdown{出现在眼前的→明显的 ap<ad (= near) + par (= appear) + -ent}
  \wordfamily{apparently 显然地；appear 出现；appearance 外表}
  \wordsense{a. 明显的；显而易见的}
  \wordphrase{for no apparent reason}
  \wordexample{He left the meeting for no apparent reason.（他无缘无故地离开了会议。）}
  \wordsense{a. 表面上的；貌似的}
  \wordphrase{in spite of her apparent indifference}
  \wordexample{In spite of her apparent indifference, she was deeply hurt.（尽管她表面上冷淡，内心却深受伤害。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{appearance}{ə'piərəns}
  \wordbreakdown{出现在眼前 ap<ad (= near) + pear<par (= appear) + -ance}
  \wordfamily{appear 出现；apparent 明显的；apparently 显然地}
  \wordsense{n. 出现；露面}
  \wordphrase{make an appearance}
  \wordexample{The singer made a surprise appearance at the charity event.（那位歌手在慈善活动上意外亮相。）}
  \wordsense{n. 外观；外表；外貌}
  \wordphrase{judge by appearances}
  \wordexample{You should not judge people by their appearances.（你不应该以貌取人。）}
  \wordsense{n. 表象；假象}
  \wordphrase{to / by all appearances}
  \wordexample{To all appearances, he was a respectable businessman.（从外表看，他是一位体面的商人。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{compare}{kəm'peə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{放在一起看是否相等 com (= together) + par (= equal)}
  \wordfamily{comparison 比较；comparable 可比的；comparative 比较的}
  \wordsense{vt. 比较，对照}
  \wordphrase{compare A with B}
  \wordexample{Compare your answers with those at the back of the book.（把你的答案和书后面的答案对照一下。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 把……比作}
  \wordphrase{compare life to a journey}
  \wordexample{Shakespeare compared the world to a stage.（莎士比亚把世界比作一个舞台。）}
  \wordsense{vi. 相比；匹敌}
  \wordphrase{nothing compares with}
  \wordexample{Nothing compares with the taste of homemade bread.（没有什么比得上自制面包的味道。）}
  \wordsense{n. 比较；对照}
  \wordphrase{beyond compare}
  \wordexample{Her beauty is beyond compare.（她的美貌无与伦比。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{comparison}{kəm'pærisn}
  \wordbreakdown{放在一起比较 com (= together) + par (= equal) + -ison}
  \wordfamily{compare 比较；comparable 可比的；comparative 比较的}
  \wordsense{n. 比较，对照}
  \wordphrase{by / in comparison with}
  \wordexample{In comparison with other cities, this one is relatively safe.（与其他城市相比，这座城市相对安全。）}
  \wordsense{n. 比喻；类比}
  \wordphrase{draw a comparison between}
  \wordexample{He drew a comparison between the human brain and a computer.（他把人脑和计算机做了类比。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{comparable}{'kɒmpərəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{可以放在一起比较的 com (= together) + par (= equal) + -able}
  \wordfamily{compare 比较；comparison 比较；comparative 比较的}
  \wordsense{a. 可比较的；类似的}
  \wordphrase{comparable with the best}
  \wordexample{His achievements are comparable with the best.（他可以同成就最高的人相匹敌。）}
  \wordsense{a. 同等的；相当的}
  \wordphrase{comparable in size}
  \wordexample{The two houses are comparable in size but differ in price.（这两栋房子大小相当，但价格不同。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{comparative}{kəm'pærətiv}
  \wordbreakdown{用来比较的 com (= together) + par (= equal) + -ative}
  \wordfamily{comparatively 比较地；compare 比较；comparison 比较}
  \wordsense{a. 比较的；相对的}
  \wordphrase{comparative literature}
  \wordexample{She majored in comparative literature at university.（她大学主修比较文学。）}
  \wordsense{a. 【语法】比较级的}
  \wordphrase{the comparative form}
  \wordexample{``Better'' is the comparative form of ``good.''（"better"是"good"的比较级形式。）}
  \wordsense{n. 【语法】比较级}
  \wordphrase{form the comparative}
  \wordexample{Most one-syllable adjectives form the comparative by adding ``-er.''（大多数单音节形容词加"-er"构成比较级。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{compeer}{kɒm'piə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{同等的人 com (= together) + peer<par (= equal)}
  \wordfamily{compare 比较；comparable 可比的；peer 同辈}
  \wordsense{n. 地位相等的人；同辈}
  \wordphrase{among his compeers}
  \wordexample{He was respected among his compeers for his integrity.（他因正直而受到同辈的尊敬。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{disparate}{'dispərət}
  \wordbreakdown{不相等的→根本不同的 dis (= not) + par (= equal) + -ate}
  \wordfamily{disparity 不等；compare 比较；pair 一对}
  \wordsense{a. 根本不同的；无法比较的}
  \wordphrase{disparate groups}
  \wordexample{The committee brought together disparate groups with very different interests.（委员会把利益迥异的不同群体聚集在一起。）}
  \wordsense{n.（pl.）无法比较的事物}
  \wordphrase{a collection of disparates}
  \wordexample{The exhibition was a collection of disparates that somehow formed a coherent whole.（这个展览是一堆不相干事物的集合，却不知怎地构成了一个连贯的整体。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{pair}{peə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{相等的两个→一对 pair<par (= equal)}
  \wordfamily{par 同等；compare 比较；repair 修复}
  \wordsense{n. 一对，一双，一副}
  \wordphrase{a pair of shoes}
  \wordexample{She bought a new pair of shoes for the wedding.（她为婚礼买了一双新鞋。）}
  \wordsense{n. 一对（夫妻、情侣、搭档）}
  \wordphrase{the happy pair}
  \wordexample{The happy pair left for their honeymoon the next day.（这对幸福的新人第二天就去度蜜月了。）}
  \wordsense{v. 使成对；配对}
  \wordphrase{pair up with}
  \wordexample{Students were paired up for the group project.（学生们被配对进行小组项目。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{par}{pa:(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{par (= equal)（相等→标准→票面价值）}
  \wordfamily{parity 平等；pair 一对；compare 比较}
  \wordsense{n. 同等；标准水平}
  \wordphrase{on a par with}
  \wordexample{His cooking is on a par with that of a professional chef.（他的厨艺可以和专业厨师相媲美。）}
  \wordsense{n. 票面价值；平价}
  \wordphrase{above / below par}
  \wordexample{The stock is currently trading below par.（该股票目前低于票面价值交易。）}
  \wordsense{n. 【高尔夫】标准杆数}
  \wordphrase{under par}
  \wordexample{She finished the round two under par.（她以低于标准杆两杆的成绩完成了这一轮。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{parity}{'pærəti}
  \wordbreakdown{相等的状态 par (= equal) + -ity}
  \wordfamily{par 同等；pair 一对；disparity 不等}
  \wordsense{n. 同等，平等；对等}
  \wordphrase{gender parity}
  \wordexample{The country has achieved parity between men and women in education.（该国在教育方面实现了男女平等。）}
  \wordsense{n. 【经济】平价}
  \wordphrase{parity of exchange}
  \wordexample{The two currencies have reached parity for the first time.（两种货币首次达到了等值。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{peer}{piə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{peer<par (= equal)（同等的人→同辈）}
  \wordfamily{peerage 贵族；peerless 无与伦比的；parity 平等}
  \wordsense{n. 同辈；同等的人}
  \wordphrase{peer pressure}
  \wordexample{Teenagers are often influenced by peer pressure.（青少年经常受到同辈压力的影响。）}
  \wordsense{n. （英国的）贵族}
  \wordphrase{be raised to the peerage}
  \wordexample{He was raised to the peerage after years of public service.（他多年从事公共服务后被封为贵族。）}
  \wordsense{vi. 凝视；盯着看}
  \wordphrase{peer at / into / through}
  \wordexample{She peered through the window into the dark room.（她透过窗户凝视着黑暗的房间。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{peerless}{'piələs}
  \wordbreakdown{没有同等的人→无与伦比的 peer (= equal) + -less (= without)}
  \wordfamily{peer 同辈；peerage 贵族；parity 平等}
  \wordsense{a. 无与伦比的；无双的}
  \wordphrase{peerless beauty}
  \wordexample{The ballerina gave a peerless performance that moved the audience to tears.（那位芭蕾舞演员的表演无与伦比，令观众感动落泪。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{umpire}{'ʌmpaiə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{不偏不倚的人→裁判 um<un (= not) + pir<par (= equal)}
  \wordfamily{peer 同辈；peerless 无双的；empire 帝国}
  \wordsense{n. 仲裁人；（比赛的）裁判员}
  \wordphrase{act as umpire}
  \wordexample{He was asked to act as umpire in the dispute between the two companies.（他被请来在两家公司的纠纷中担任仲裁人。）}
  \wordsense{v. 仲裁；担任裁判}
  \wordphrase{umpire a match}
  \wordexample{She umpired the tennis match fairly and professionally.（她公正而专业地担任了这场网球比赛的裁判。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{vampire}{'væmpaiə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{源自斯拉夫语（吸血的怪物）}
  \wordfamily{umpire（裁判）；empire（帝国）；apparel（衣着）}
  \wordsense{n. 吸血鬼}
  \wordphrase{vampire legend}
  \wordphrase{vampire bat}
  \wordphrase{a financial vampire}
  \wordexample{Vampire legends have existed in many cultures for centuries.（吸血鬼传说在许多文化中已存在了几个世纪。）}
  \wordexample{Vampire bats feed on the blood of other animals.（吸血蝙蝠以其他动物的血液为食。）}
  \wordexample{The loan shark was a vampire who preyed on desperate people.（那个高利贷者是个专门剥削走投无路之人的吸血鬼。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{oviparous}{ˌəuvi'pərəs}
  \wordbreakdown{产卵的 ovi (= egg) + par (= produce) + -ous}
  \wordfamily{viviparous 胎生的；parental 父母的；parturition 分娩}
  \wordsense{a.（鸟、鱼、爬虫类）卵生的}
  \wordphrase{oviparous animals}
  \wordexample{Most birds and reptiles are oviparous, laying eggs that hatch outside the body.（大多数鸟类和爬行动物是卵生的，产卵后在体外孵化。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{parental}{pə'rentl}
  \wordbreakdown{父母的 par (= produce) + -ent + -al}
  \wordfamily{parent 父母；parentage 出身；parenthood 为人父母}
  \wordsense{a. 父母亲的；父母般的}
  \wordphrase{parental care}
  \wordexample{Children need parental guidance when using the internet.（孩子们上网时需要父母的指导。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{parricide}{'pærisaid}
  \wordbreakdown{杀父母 par (= parent) + -cid (= kill) + -e}
  \wordfamily{parental 父母的；homicide 杀人；suicide 自杀}
  \wordsense{n. 杀父（或母）罪；弑亲者}
  \wordphrase{commit parricide}
  \wordexample{In ancient Rome, parricide was considered one of the most heinous crimes.（在古罗马，弑亲被认为是最令人发指的罪行之一。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{parturition}{ˌpa:tjuə'riʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{生产 par (= produce) + -turition}
  \wordfamily{parental 父母的；oviparous 卵生的；viviparous 胎生的}
  \wordsense{n. 分娩，生产}
  \wordphrase{the process of parturition}
  \wordexample{The veterinarian assisted the cow during a difficult parturition.（兽医在母牛难产时进行了协助。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{viviparous}{vi'vipərəs}
  \wordbreakdown{活着生产的→胎生的 viv (= life) + par (= produce) + -ous}
  \wordfamily{oviparous 卵生的；vivid 生动的；parturition 分娩}
  \wordsense{a. 胎生的}
  \wordphrase{viviparous mammals}
  \wordexample{Most mammals are viviparous, giving birth to live young.（大多数哺乳动物是胎生的，产下活的幼崽。）}
\end{word}



\chapter{PEND, PENS[POND, PENC]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 pend, pens 意为 to hang, to weigh, to pay, to consider。其变形有 pond 和 penc。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{append}{ə'pend}
  \wordbreakdown{悬挂在某物之上 ap<ad (= to, add) + pend (= hang)}
  \wordsense{vt. sth + to + sth 附加，添加；悬挂}
  \wordfamily{appendix 附录}
  \wordfamily{appendage 附加物}
  \wordfamily{appendicitis 阑尾炎}
  \wordphrase{appends a postscript}
  \wordexample{She usually appends a postscript to her letters.（她通常在信上加一些附言。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{compendium}{kəm'pendiəm}
  \wordbreakdown{从某物上分离出来悬挂 com (= with) + pend (= hang, weigh)}
  \wordfamily{compendious（简洁的，简明的）；append（sth + to + sth 附加）；impending（迫近的）}
  \wordsense{n. 概论，概要；简编}
  \wordphrase{compendium of common French}
  \wordexample{This guidebook contains a compendium of common French idioms and expressions.（这本指南手册包括法语中常见的习惯用语和表达法的简编。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{impending}{im'pendiŋ}
  \wordbreakdown{悬挂在上面的 im<in (= on) + pend (= hang, weigh)}
  \wordfamily{compendium；compensate；append；propensity}
  \wordsense{a. 迫近的；悬挂的，悬置的}
  \wordphrase{impending tax audit}
  \wordexample{I'm dreading my impending tax audit with the Internal Revenue Service.（对国内收入署即将对我进行的税务审计，我感到很害怕。）}
  \wordexample{impending tax audit}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{compensate}{'kɒmpenseit}
  \wordbreakdown{用某物来偿还 com (= with) + pens (= pay, hang)}
  \wordfamily{compensation（赔偿，补偿）；compensatory（补偿的）；append（sth + to + sth 附加）}
  \wordsense{vt. sb + for + sth 补偿；赔偿}
  \wordphrase{compensate the issue}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of compensate.（此处展示 compensate 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{vi. for + sth 补偿；弥补}
  \wordphrase{I gave the girl}
  \wordexample{I gave the girl 100 dollars as compensation for her pain when my dog bit her.（我的狗咬了那个小女孩，作为对她所受痛苦的补偿，我给了她 100 美元。）}
  \wordexample{I gave the girl}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{propensity}{prə'pensəti}
  \wordbreakdown{预先挂好的 pro (= forth) + pens (= hang, weigh)}
  \wordsense{n. 倾向；习性}
  \wordfamily{compensate}
  \wordfamily{indispensable}
  \wordfamily{impending}
  \wordfamily{expend}
  \wordphrase{propensity to tap my}
  \wordexample{I have a propensity to tap my fingers when I'm nervous.（我在紧张的时候会弹手指。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{indispensable}{ˌindis'pensəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{不分开悬挂的 in (= not) + dis (= apart) + pens (= hang, weigh)}
  \wordfamily{indispensability（不可缺）；append（sth + to + sth 附加）；compendium（概论）}
  \wordsense{a. 不可缺少的，必需的}
  \wordphrase{indispensable writing tool}
  \wordexample{A thesaurus is an indispensable writing tool for any university student.（词典是所有大学生不可或缺的写作工具。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{expend}{ik'spend}
  \wordbreakdown{支付以使某物完尽 ex (= out) + pend (= pay, weigh)}
  \wordsense{vt. sth + on + sth 消费；耗尽，用光}
  \wordfamily{expenditure 支出，经费}
  \wordfamily{expense 费用，支出；牺牲}
  \wordfamily{expensive 昂贵的}
  \wordfamily{expendable 可花费的；可牺牲的}
  \wordphrase{expended on new acquisitions}
  \wordexample{About 28\% of the library's annual budget is expended on new acquisitions.（那家图书馆约 28\% 的年预算用来购置新书。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{recompense}{'rekəmpens}
  \wordbreakdown{用某种东西偿还 re (= back) + com (= with) + pens (= pay)}
  \wordsense{vt. 报答；赔偿；补偿}
  \wordfamily{expend}
  \wordfamily{depend}
  \wordfamily{indispensable}
  \wordfamily{dependant}
  \wordphrase{The insurance company recompensed}
  \wordexample{The insurance company recompensed the people injured in the auto accident.（保险公司对在车祸中受伤的人给予了赔偿。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{depend}{di'pend}
  \wordbreakdown{向下悬挂→依附 de (= down) + pend (= hang)}
  \wordfamily{dependable 可靠的；dependent 依赖的；dependence 依赖；independence 独立}
  \wordsense{vi. 依靠，依赖}
  \wordphrase{depend on one\textquotesingle s parents}
  \wordexample{Children depend on their parents for food and shelter.（孩子们依靠父母获得食物和住所。）}
  \wordsense{vi. 取决于}
  \wordphrase{it depends on the weather}
  \wordexample{Whether we go on a picnic depends on the weather.（我们是否去野餐取决于天气。）}
  \wordsense{vi. 信赖，指望}
  \wordphrase{depend on sb. to do sth.}
  \wordexample{You can always depend on him to keep his promises.（你总是可以信赖他会遵守承诺。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{dependant}{di'pendənt}
  \wordbreakdown{依附于人的→受抚养者 de (= down) + pend (= hang) + -ant}
  \wordfamily{depend 依靠；dependent 依赖的；dependence 依赖}
  \wordsense{n. 受抚养的家属；被赡养者}
  \wordphrase{tax dependants}
  \wordexample{You can claim tax relief for each dependant in your household.（你可以为家中每位受抚养人申请税收减免。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{dependent}{di'pendənt}
  \wordbreakdown{悬挂于下方的→依赖的 de (= down) + pend (= hang) + -ent}
  \wordfamily{depend 依靠；dependence 依赖；independence 独立；independent 独立的}
  \wordsense{a. 依赖的，依靠的}
  \wordphrase{dependent on his wife\textquotesingle s earnings}
  \wordexample{He is entirely dependent on his wife\textquotesingle s earnings.（他完全依靠妻子的收入。）}
  \wordsense{a. 取决于……的}
  \wordphrase{dependent upon weather}
  \wordexample{Whether we go is dependent upon the weather.（我们是否去取决于天气。）}
  \wordsense{n. 受抚养者（= dependant）}
  \wordphrase{dependents and beneficiaries}
  \wordexample{The insurance policy covers the employee and all dependents.（保险单涵盖员工及所有受抚养人。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{dependence}{di'pendəns}
  \wordbreakdown{依赖的状态 de (= down) + pend (= hang) + -ence}
  \wordfamily{depend 依靠；dependent 依赖的；independence 独立}
  \wordsense{n. 依靠，依赖}
  \wordphrase{dependence on foreign oil}
  \wordexample{The country is trying to reduce its dependence on foreign oil.（该国正在努力减少对外国石油的依赖。）}
  \wordsense{n. 信赖，信任}
  \wordphrase{place dependence on}
  \wordexample{You can place full dependence on his word.（你可以完全信赖他的话。）}
  \wordsense{n. （药物等的）依赖性；成瘾}
  \wordphrase{drug dependence}
  \wordexample{Drug dependence is a serious medical condition that requires treatment.（药物依赖是一种需要治疗的严重疾病。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{dependency}{di'pendənsi}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEND, PENS[POND, PENC]}
  \wordfamily{dependence；independence；dependent；independent；append（sth + to + sth 附加）}
  \wordsense{n. 附属国，附属地 *de (= down), pend (= hang)}
  \wordphrase{dependency of the USA}
  \wordexample{The island is no longer a dependency of the USA.（那座小岛不再是美国的附属地了。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{independence}{ˌindi'pendəns}
  \wordbreakdown{不依赖→独立 in (= not) + de (= down) + pend (= hang) + -ence}
  \wordfamily{independent 独立的；depend 依靠；dependence 依赖}
  \wordsense{n. 独立；自主；自立}
  \wordphrase{Independence Day}
  \wordexample{The country gained its independence from colonial rule in 1960.（该国于1960年从殖民统治下获得独立。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{independent}{ˌindi'pendənt}
  \wordbreakdown{不依赖的→独立的 in (= not) + de (= down) + pend (= hang) + -ent}
  \wordfamily{independently 独立地；independence 独立；depend 依靠}
  \wordsense{a. 独立的，自主的}
  \wordphrase{independent of}
  \wordexample{She is financially independent of her parents.（她在经济上不依赖父母。）}
  \wordsense{a. 独立进行的；不受影响的}
  \wordphrase{independent research}
  \wordexample{An independent investigation confirmed the findings.（一项独立调查证实了这些发现。）}
  \wordsense{n. 无党派人士}
  \wordphrase{vote for the independents}
  \wordexample{Vote for the independents at the next election.（在下一次选举中投票给无党派人士。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{penchant}{'pentʃənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEND, PENS[POND, PENC]}
  \wordfamily{independent；pendant；independence；pendent；append（sth + to + sth 附加）}
  \wordsense{n. 强烈的倾向；嗜好 *penc<pens (= hang)}
  \wordphrase{have a penchant for（嗜好……）}
  \wordexample{have a penchant for（嗜好……）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{pendant}{'pendənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEND, PENS[POND, PENC]}
  \wordfamily{penchant；pendent；independent；pending；append（sth + to + sth 附加）}
  \wordsense{n. 垂饰；吊灯 *pend (= hang)}
  \wordphrase{a pendant（垂饰）}
  \wordexample{a pendant（垂饰）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{pendent}{'pendənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEND, PENS[POND, PENC]}
  \wordfamily{pendant；pending；penchant；pendulous；append（sth + to + sth 附加）}
  \wordsense{a. 悬挂的，下垂的；向外伸出的；未决的，未定的 *pend (= hang)}
  \wordphrase{pendent rocks（向外突出的岩石）}
  \wordexample{pendent rocks（向外突出的岩石）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{pending}{'pendiŋ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEND, PENS[POND, PENC]}
  \wordfamily{pendent；pendulous；pendant；pendulum；append（sth + to + sth 附加）}
  \wordsense{a. 悬而未决的}
  \wordphrase{be pending}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of pending.（此处展示 pending 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{prep. 在……过程中，在……期间；在……之前 *pend (= hang)}
  \wordphrase{a patent pending（待批专利）}
  \wordphrase{pending the negotiations（在谈判之前）}
  \wordphrase{pending her decision（她作决定之前）}
  \wordexample{a patent pending（待批专利）}
  \wordexample{The lawsuit was then pending.（那次诉讼当时未作判决。）}
  \wordexample{pending the negotiations（在谈判之前）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{pendulous}{'pendjuləs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEND, PENS[POND, PENC]}
  \wordfamily{pending；pendulum；pendent；perpendicular；append（sth + to + sth 附加）}
  \wordsense{a. 悬垂的；摇摆的 *pend (= hang)}
  \wordphrase{the pendulous nests of weaver-birds（织巢鸟悬垂着的巢）}
  \wordexample{the pendulous nests of weaver-birds（织巢鸟悬垂着的巢）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{pendulum}{'pendjuləm}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEND, PENS[POND, PENC]}
  \wordfamily{pendulous；perpendicular；pending；suspend；append（sth + to + sth 附加）}
  \wordsense{n. （钟等的）摆；摇摆不定的人（或物） *pend (= hang)}
  \wordphrase{the swing of the pendulums（摇摆不定的舆论；（政党等的）胜负交替）}
  \wordexample{the swing of the pendulums（摇摆不定的舆论；（政党等的）胜负交替）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{perpendicular}{ˌpə:pən'dikjulə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEND, PENS[POND, PENC]}
  \wordfamily{append（sth + to + sth 附加）；compendium（概论）；impending（迫近的）}
  \wordsense{a. 成直角的，垂直的；直立的}
  \wordphrase{be perpendicular（成直角的，垂直的）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "be perpendicular".（常见搭配：成直角的，垂直的。）}
  \wordsense{n. 垂直面 *per (= completely), pend (= hang)}
  \wordphrase{out of (the)}
  \wordexample{out of (the)}
  \wordexample{be perpendicular（成直角的，垂直的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{suspend}{sə'spend}
  \wordbreakdown{从下面悬挂→暂停 sus<sub (= under) + pend (= hang)}
  \wordfamily{suspension 悬挂；suspense 悬念；suspended 悬浮的}
  \wordsense{vt. 悬挂；使悬浮}
  \wordphrase{lamps suspended from the ceiling}
  \wordexample{Crystal chandeliers were suspended from the high ceiling.（水晶吊灯悬挂在高高的天花板上。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 暂停；中止}
  \wordphrase{suspend payment}
  \wordexample{The company was forced to suspend operations during the investigation.（调查期间，公司被迫暂停运营。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 暂时取消（某人的职务、资格等）}
  \wordphrase{suspend a student from school}
  \wordexample{The player was suspended for three matches after the foul.（那名球员因犯规被停赛三场。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 暂缓作出（决定、判断）}
  \wordphrase{suspend judgment}
  \wordexample{Let us suspend judgment until all the facts are known.（让我们暂缓判断，等所有事实都清楚了再说。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{suspense}{sə'spens}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEND, PENS[POND, PENC]}
  \wordfamily{suspend；suspension；perpendicular；suspender；append（sth + to + sth 附加）}
  \wordsense{n. 担心，不安；紧张感；（小说、电影等的）悬念 *sus<sub (= under), pens (= hang)}
  \wordphrase{in great suspense（极为不安）}
  \wordexample{keep her in suspense for five days already（已经让她担心五天了）}
  \wordexample{in great suspense（极为不安）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{suspension}{sə'spenʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEND, PENS[POND, PENC]}
  \wordfamily{suspense；suspender；suspend；dispense；append（sth + to + sth 附加）}
  \wordsense{n. 悬挂；悬浮（状态）；悬而未决；暂令停职；中止 *sus<sub (= under), pens (= hang)}
  \wordphrase{suspension bridge（吊桥）}
  \wordphrase{suspension of a congressman（中止议员资格）}
  \wordexample{suspension bridge（吊桥）}
  \wordexample{suspension of a congressman（中止议员资格）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{suspender}{sə'spendə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEND, PENS[POND, PENC]}
  \wordfamily{suspension；dispense；suspense；dispensation；append（sth + to + sth 附加）}
  \wordsense{n. 《美》裤子背带；《英》吊袜带 *sus<sub (= under), pend (= hang)}
  \wordphrase{a suspender（《美》裤子背带）}
  \wordexample{a suspender（《美》裤子背带）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{dispense}{di'spens}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEND, PENS[POND, PENC]}
  \wordsense{v. 分配，分发；配药；（\textasciitilde{} with）免除 *dis (= apart, away), pens (= weigh)}
  \wordfamily{dispenser（分配者；药剂师；分配器（可以不打开盖子而直接抽取物质的容器））}
  \wordfamily{to dispense（分配，分发）}
  \wordphrase{to dispense（分配，分发）}
  \wordexample{to dispense（分配，分发）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{dispensation}{ˌdispen'seiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEND, PENS[POND, PENC]}
  \wordfamily{append（sth + to + sth 附加）；compendium（概论）；impending（迫近的）}
  \wordsense{n. 分配，分发；【宗】天意，天道；教规；处方 *dis (= apart, away), pens (= weigh)}
  \wordphrase{dispensation of food（食物分配）}
  \wordphrase{dispensation of providence（天意，上天的安排）}
  \wordexample{dispensation of food（食物分配）}
  \wordexample{dispensation of providence（天意，上天的安排）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{dispensable}{di'spensəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{可以分配出去的→非必要的 dis (= apart) + pens (= weigh) + -able}
  \wordfamily{indispensable 不可或缺的；dispense 分配；dispensary 药房}
  \wordsense{a. 非必要的；可有可无的}
  \wordphrase{easily dispensable}
  \wordexample{No employee should feel dispensable in a well-run company.（在管理良好的公司里，没有员工应该觉得自己可有可无。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{dispensary}{di'spensəri}
  \wordbreakdown{分配药物的地方 dis (= apart) + pens (= weigh) + -ary}
  \wordfamily{dispense 分配；dispensable 非必要的；dispensation 分配}
  \wordsense{n.（医院等的）药房；诊疗所}
  \wordphrase{hospital dispensary}
  \wordexample{The nurse collected the medicine from the dispensary.（护士从药房取了药。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{ponderable}{'pɒndərəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{可以称量的 pond<pend (= weigh) + -erable}
  \wordfamily{ponderous 沉重的；ponder 沉思；imponderable 不可估量的}
  \wordsense{a. 可称重的；可估量的}
  \wordphrase{ponderable evidence}
  \wordexample{The committee considered only ponderable evidence in making its decision.（委员会在做决定时只考虑了可衡量的证据。）}
  \wordsense{n.（pl.）可估量的事物}
  \wordphrase{the ponderables of the situation}
  \wordexample{We must weigh both the ponderables and imponderables before deciding.（我们在决定之前必须权衡可估量和不可估量的因素。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{ponderous}{'pɒndərəs}
  \wordbreakdown{有重量的→沉重的 pond<pend (= weigh) + -erous}
  \wordfamily{ponder 沉思；ponderable 可估量的；preponderance 优势}
  \wordsense{a. 沉重的；笨重的}
  \wordphrase{ponderous movements}
  \wordexample{The elephant made ponderous movements through the forest.（大象笨重地穿过森林。）}
  \wordsense{a. （文章、谈话等）沉闷的；冗长乏味的}
  \wordphrase{a ponderous speech}
  \wordexample{His ponderous speech put half the audience to sleep.（他沉闷的演讲让一半听众睡着了。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{preponderance}{pri'pɒndərəns}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEND, PENS[POND, PENC]}
  \wordfamily{ponderous；preponderant；ponderable；preponderate；append（sth + to + sth 附加）}
  \wordsense{n. （重量、力量方面的）优势，优越 *pre (= before), pond<pend (= weigh)}
  \wordphrase{preponderance theory（力量优势论）}
  \wordexample{preponderance theory（力量优势论）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{preponderant}{pri'pɒndərənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEND, PENS[POND, PENC]}
  \wordfamily{preponderance；preponderate；ponderous；inexpensive；append（sth + to + sth 附加）}
  \wordsense{a. （在重量、数量、力量等方面）占优势的，压倒的 *pre (= before), pond<pend (= weigh)}
  \wordphrase{be preponderant（占优势的，压倒的）}
  \wordexample{be preponderant（占优势的，压倒的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{preponderate}{pri'pɒndəreit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEND, PENS[POND, PENC]}
  \wordfamily{preponderant；inexpensive；preponderance；pension；append（sth + to + sth 附加）}
  \wordsense{v. （重量、数量、力量等）占优势 *pre (= before), pond<pend (= weigh)}
  \wordphrase{preponderate the issue}
  \wordexample{reasons that preponderate over other considerations（优先考虑的理由）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{inexpensive}{ˌinik'spensiv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEND, PENS[POND, PENC]}
  \wordfamily{preponderate；pension；preponderant；stipend；append（sth + to + sth 附加）}
  \wordsense{a. 便宜的，花费不多的 *in (= not), ex (= out), pens (= pay)}
  \wordphrase{be inexpensive（便宜的，花费不多的）}
  \wordexample{be inexpensive（便宜的，花费不多的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{pension}{'penʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEND, PENS[POND, PENC]}
  \wordfamily{pensionable（（根据职位、年龄等）有资格领退休金的）；pensioner（领退休金者）；append（sth + to + sth 附加）}
  \wordsense{n. 退休金；养老金}
  \wordphrase{the pension}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of pension.（此处展示 pension 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. 发退休金 *pens (= pay)}
  \wordphrase{old-age pension（养老金）}
  \wordphrase{draw one's pension（领取养老金）}
  \wordphrase{retire on a pension（拿着退休金退休）}
  \wordexample{old-age pension（养老金）}
  \wordexample{draw one's pension（领取养老金）}
  \wordexample{retire on a pension（拿着退休金退休）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{stipend}{'staipend}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEND, PENS[POND, PENC]}
  \wordfamily{stipendiary（领薪金的，有薪水的）；append（sth + to + sth 附加）；compendium（概论）}
  \wordsense{n. （学生定期领取的）生活津贴；（牧师等的）薪金，薪俸 *sti (= prick), pend (= pay)}
  \wordphrase{a stipend（生活津贴）}
  \wordexample{a stipend（生活津贴）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{pensive}{'pensiv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEND, PENS[POND, PENC]}
  \wordfamily{append（sth + to + sth 附加）；compendium（概论）；impending（迫近的）}
  \wordsense{a. 沉思的；忧心忡忡的 *pens (= consider)}
  \wordphrase{pensive looks（沉思的表情）}
  \wordexample{pensive looks（沉思的表情）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{pensée}{pɒn'sei}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEND, PENS[POND, PENC]}
  \wordfamily{pensive；ponder；stipend；prepense；append（sth + to + sth 附加）}
  \wordsense{n. 思想；（pl.）冥想录，随想录 *pens (= consider)}
  \wordphrase{Je pense donc je}
  \wordexample{Je pense, donc je suis.（我思故我在。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{ponder}{'pɒndə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEND, PENS[POND, PENC]}
  \wordfamily{prepense；pensive；stipend；append（sth + to + sth 附加）}
  \wordsense{v. 深思，沉思 *pond<pend (= consider)}
  \wordphrase{ponder on a difficulty（仔细思考困难）}
  \wordexample{ponder on a difficulty（仔细思考困难）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{prepense}{pri'pens}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEND, PENS[POND, PENC]}
  \wordfamily{ponder；pensive；stipend；append（sth + to + sth 附加）}
  \wordsense{a. 【用于名词后】预谋的，故意的，蓄意的 *pre (= before), pens (= consider)}
  \wordphrase{be prepense（预谋的，故意的，蓄意的）}
  \wordexample{be prepense（预谋的，故意的，蓄意的）}
\end{word}



\chapter{SPECT, SPEC[SPIC, SPI, SPY]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 spect, spec 意为 to look, to see。其变形为 spic, spi, spy 等。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 vid / vis 及变形 vic, view, voy, vey / vei, vy / vi, ud，来自拉丁语的 the, tu，来自希腊语的 orama, scop，以及来自盎格鲁-撒克逊语的 see 等。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{aspect}{'æspekt}
  \wordbreakdown{看的方向→方面 a<ad (= to) + spect (= look)}
  \wordfamily{perspective 视角；specious 似是而非的；conspicuous 显眼的}
  \wordsense{n. 方面}
  \wordphrase{aspects of the subject}
  \wordexample{I don\textquotesingle t understand all aspects of the subject.（我并不能理解这个主题的所有方面。）}
  \wordsense{n. 外观；面貌}
  \wordphrase{a gloomy aspect}
  \wordexample{The old house had a gloomy aspect in the rain.（老房子在雨中显得阴沉沉的。）}
  \wordsense{n. （建筑物的）朝向}
  \wordphrase{a southern aspect}
  \wordexample{The house has a southern aspect, so it gets plenty of sunlight.（这栋房子朝南，所以阳光充足。）}
  \wordsense{n. 【语法】体，时态}
  \wordphrase{the progressive aspect}
  \wordexample{The progressive aspect indicates an ongoing action.（进行体表示正在进行的动作。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{perspective}{pə'spektiv}
  \wordbreakdown{通过某物看 per (= through) + spect (= look)}
  \wordfamily{perspectively（依透视画法）；aspect（方面）；specious（外表美观的）}
  \wordsense{n. 展望；观点，看法；透视画法；透视图}
  \wordphrase{perspective on problems while}
  \wordexample{It's difficult to get a good perspective on problems while they're occurring.（人们很难对正在发生的问题有正确的看法。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{specious}{'spi:ʃəs}
  \wordbreakdown{外表上看见的 spec (= look)}
  \wordfamily{perspective；auspicious；aspect；conspicuous}
  \wordsense{a. 外表美观的，华而不实的；似是而非的，貌似有理的}
  \wordphrase{specious claim that he}
  \wordexample{Al Gore was roundly criticized for his specious claim that he invented the Internet.（阿尔·戈尔貌似有理地宣称自己发明了互联网，遭到了严厉的指责。）}
  \wordexample{specious claim that he}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{auspicious}{ɔ:'spiʃəs}
  \wordbreakdown{看鸟的 au<avi (= bird) + spic (= look) + ous (= having the quality of)}
  \wordfamily{auspices（资助，赞助）；aspect（方面）；perspective（展望）}
  \wordsense{a. 吉利的，吉祥的；顺利的}
  \wordphrase{auspicious moment presents itself}
  \wordexample{I plan to propose when an auspicious moment presents itself.（我打算在一个幸运的时刻来临之时求婚。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{conspicuous}{kən'spikjuəs}
  \wordbreakdown{看起来非常好的 con<com (= intensive) + spic (= look)}
  \wordfamily{conspicuously（显著地）；aspect（方面）；perspective（展望）}
  \wordsense{a. 显眼的；引人注目的；显著的}
  \wordphrase{conspicuous mole disfigures the}
  \wordexample{A conspicuous mole disfigures the beauty of her face.（一颗很显眼的痣破坏了她脸部的美。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{despicable}{di'spikəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{向下、向低处看的 de (= down) + spic (= look)}
  \wordfamily{despicably（卑劣地）；aspect（方面）；perspective（展望）}
  \wordsense{a. 卑鄙的，卑劣的}
  \wordphrase{despicable language}
  \wordexample{Apologize to your mother for your despicable language.（你要为你卑劣的言辞向你妈妈道歉。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{perspicacious}{ˌpə:spi'keiʃəs}
  \wordbreakdown{通过某事来透视的 per (= through) + spic (= look)}
  \wordfamily{perspicacity（敏锐，聪明）；aspect（方面）；perspective（展望）}
  \wordsense{a. 有洞察力的；聪明的，敏锐的}
  \wordphrase{perspicacious student}
  \wordexample{The chairman told me I was the most perspicacious student in his seminar.（主席告诉我，在参加研讨会的学生中，我最聪明。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{respite}{'respait}
  \wordbreakdown{在后边看或者拉开距离看 re (= back, away) + spi (= look)}

  \wordfamily{perspicacious}
  \wordfamily{circumspect}
  \wordfamily{despicable}
  \wordfamily{conspectus}
  \wordphrase{respite from the burning}
  \wordexample{The shady pines offered respite from the burning sun.（松树的绿荫为我们提供了一个避开灼热阳光的休息场所。）}
\end{word}

  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPECT, SPEC[SPIC, SPI, SPY]}
  \wordfamily{aspect（方面）；perspective（展望）；specious（外表美观的）}
  \wordsense{a. （叙述等）明确的，具体的；特有的；【医】有特效的；【生】种的}
  \wordphrase{be specific}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of specific.（此处展示 specific 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 特效药 *fic (= make)}
  \wordphrase{specific orders（明确的命令）}
  \wordphrase{specific remedy（特效药）}
  \wordphrase{specific purpose（明确的目的）}
  \wordexample{specific orders（明确的命令）}
  \wordexample{specific remedy（特效药）}
  \wordexample{specific purpose（明确的目的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{specification}{ˌspesifi'keiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPECT, SPEC[SPIC, SPI, SPY]}
  \wordfamily{specific；specify；species；specimen；aspect（方面）}
  \wordsense{n. 详述；（产品的）说明书；（pl.）明细（单）；规格 *fic (= make)}
  \wordphrase{the specification}
  \wordexample{technical specifications of a new car（新型汽车的技术规格）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{specify}{'spesifai}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPECT, SPEC[SPIC, SPI, SPY]}
  \wordfamily{specification；specimen；specific；speculate；aspect（方面）}
  \wordsense{v. 明确说明，载明；详述；把……列入计划或说明书 *fy (= make)}
  \wordphrase{to specify（明确说明，载明）}
  \wordexample{to specify（明确说明，载明）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{specimen}{'spesimən}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPECT, SPEC[SPIC, SPI, SPY]}
  \wordfamily{aspect（方面）；perspective（展望）；specious（外表美观的）}
  \wordsense{n. 样本，标本；（化验用的）抽样；家伙，怪人}
  \wordphrase{specimen he is}
  \wordexample{What a queer specimen he is!（他真是个怪人！）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{speculate}{'spekjuleit}
  \wordbreakdown{仔细看→深思→推测 spec (= look) + -ul- + -ate}
  \wordfamily{speculation 推测；speculative 推测的；speculator 投机者}
  \wordsense{vi. 推测；猜测}
  \wordphrase{speculate about / on}
  \wordexample{It is useless to speculate about what might have happened.（推测可能发生了什么是没有用的。）}
  \wordsense{vi. 投机；做投机买卖}
  \wordphrase{speculate in oil shares}
  \wordexample{He lost a fortune speculating in oil shares.（他做石油股票投机亏了一大笔钱。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{speculation}{ˌspekju'leiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{spec (= look) + -ul- + -ation（深思→推测）}
  \wordfamily{speculate 推测；speculative 推测的；speculator 投机者}
  \wordsense{n. 推测；猜测}
  \wordphrase{pure speculation}
  \wordexample{Reports that the company will merge are pure speculation.（关于公司将合并的报道纯属猜测。）}
  \wordsense{n. 思索；沉思}
  \wordphrase{philosophical speculation}
  \wordexample{He spent hours in philosophical speculation about the nature of reality.（他花了数小时思索现实的本质。）}
  \wordsense{n. 投机；投机买卖}
  \wordphrase{speculation in rice}
  \wordexample{Speculation in rice drove up prices during the famine.（饥荒期间大米投机推高了价格。）}
\end{word}

  \wordphrase{speculative stock（投机股票）}
  \wordphrase{speculative buying（投机购买）}
