  \wordsense{a. 明了的 *per (= completely, intensive)}
  \wordphrase{be perspicuous（明了的）}
  \wordexample{be perspicuous（明了的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{spice}{spais}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPECT, SPEC[SPIC, SPI, SPY]}
  \wordfamily{aspect（方面）；perspective（展望）；specious（外表美观的）}
  \wordsense{n. 调味品，香料；《喻》风味，情趣；香味}
  \wordphrase{a spice（调味品，香料）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "a spice".（常见搭配：调味品，香料。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 加香料于；使增添趣味}
  \wordphrase{a spice of humor（幽默情趣）}
  \wordexample{a spice of humor（幽默情趣）}
  \wordexample{a spice（调味品，香料）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{suspicion}{sə'spiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{从下面偷看→怀疑 sus<sub (= under) + pic<spic (= look) + -ion}
  \wordfamily{suspicious 可疑的；suspect 怀疑；unsuspecting 不起疑心的}
  \wordsense{n. 怀疑；猜疑}
  \wordphrase{above suspicion}
  \wordexample{His honesty is above suspicion.（他的诚实不容置疑。）}
  \wordsense{n. 嫌疑}
  \wordphrase{under suspicion}
  \wordexample{He was arrested on suspicion of murder.（他因涉嫌谋杀而被逮捕。）}
  \wordsense{n. 少量；一丝}
  \wordphrase{a suspicion of sadness}
  \wordexample{There was a suspicion of sadness in her voice.（她的声音里带着一丝伤感。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{suspicious}{sə'spiʃəs}
  \wordbreakdown{从下面偷看的→可疑的 sus<sub (= under) + pic<spic (= look) + -ious}
  \wordfamily{suspicion 怀疑；suspect 怀疑；despise 鄙视}
  \wordsense{a. 可疑的；引起怀疑的}
  \wordphrase{suspicious circumstances}
  \wordexample{The police ordered an inspection of all suspicious vehicles.（警方下令检查所有可疑车辆。）}
  \wordsense{a. 怀疑的；多疑的}
  \wordphrase{suspicious of strangers}
  \wordexample{She was always suspicious of people\textquotesingle s motives.（她总是怀疑别人的动机。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{despise}{di'spaiz}
  \wordbreakdown{向下看→鄙视 de (= down) + spis<spec (= look)}
  \wordfamily{despite 尽管；despicable 卑鄙的；suspicion 怀疑}
  \wordsense{vt. 蔑视，鄙视；看不起}
  \wordphrase{despise sb. for sth.}
  \wordexample{She despised him for his cowardice.（她因他的懦弱而鄙视他。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{despite}{di'spait}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPECT, SPEC[SPIC, SPI, SPY]}
  \wordfamily{despiteful（有恶意的）；aspect（方面）；perspective（展望）}
  \wordsense{n. 侮辱；损害；恶意}
  \wordphrase{a despite（侮辱）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "a despite".（常见搭配：侮辱。）}
  \wordsense{prep. 尽管 *de (= down)}
  \wordphrase{a despite（侮辱）}
  \wordexample{a despite（侮辱）}
  \wordexample{despite（尽管）}
\end{word}

  \wordphrase{an espionage（刺探）}
  \wordexample{an espionage（刺探）}

  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPECT, SPEC[SPIC, SPI, SPY]}
  \wordfamily{espionage；frontispiece；despite；spy；aspect（方面）}
  \wordsense{v. 发现；窥见 *e<ex (= out)}
  \wordphrase{to espy（发现）}
  \wordexample{to espy（发现）}
\end{word}

  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPECT, SPEC[SPIC, SPI, SPY]}
  \wordfamily{espy；spy；espionage；despite；aspect（方面）}
  \wordsense{n. （书的）卷首插画 *front (= forehead)}
  \wordphrase{a frontispiece（卷首插画）}
  \wordexample{a frontispiece（卷首插画）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{spy}{spai}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPECT, SPEC[SPIC, SPI, SPY]}
  \wordfamily{salt（和）；health（的意思）；aspect（方面）}
  \wordsense{n. 间谍；密探}
  \wordphrase{police spies（警方的密探）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "police spies".（常见搭配：警方的密探。）}
  \wordsense{v. 暗中监视；发现}
  \wordphrase{spy on the enemy's movements（侦察敌人的动态）}
  \wordphrase{spy satellite（间谍卫星）}
  \wordphrase{spy ship（间谍船）}
  \wordexample{spy on the enemy's movements（侦察敌人的动态）}
  \wordexample{spy satellite（间谍卫星）}
  \wordexample{spy ship（间谍船）}

\chapter{SAL[SUL, SIL, SAIL, SAUL]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 sal 意为 to leap，其变形有 sul, sil, sail, saul 等。此外，sal 还有 salt 和 health 的意思。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{assailant}{ə'seilənt}
  \wordbreakdown{向某人跳的人 as<ad (= to) + sail<sal (= leap)}
  \wordfamily{assail（攻击）；salient（显著的）；exult（at / in + sth 狂喜）}
  \wordsense{n. 攻击者}
  \wordphrase{assailants robbed me}
  \wordexample{Three assailants robbed me of my purse.（三个袭击者抢走了我的钱包。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{salient}{'seiliənt}
  \wordbreakdown{跳上去的 sal (= leap, salt)}
  \wordfamily{assailant；exult；resilient；salutary}
  \wordsense{a. 显著的，明显的，突出的；凸起的}
  \wordphrase{be salient（显著的，明显的，突出的）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "be salient".（常见搭配：显著的，明显的，突出的。）}
  \wordsense{n. 凸角；【军】战线或筑城的突出部}
  \wordphrase{salient point}
  \wordexample{In your composition, each paragraph should develop a single salient point.（写文章时，每一段落都应该有一个突出的论点。）}
  \wordexample{salient point}
  \wordexample{be salient（显著的，明显的，突出的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{exult}{ig'zʌlt}
  \wordbreakdown{用力跳跃 ex (= intensive) + ul<sul<sal (= leap)}
  \wordsense{vi. at / in + sth 狂喜，欢欣鼓舞}
  \wordfamily{exultation 狂喜}
  \wordfamily{exultant 狂喜的}
  \wordfamily{exultantly 狂喜地}
  \wordphrase{exults in her good}
  \wordexample{She exults in her good fortune to have triplets.（她非常高兴自己有幸生了三胞胎。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{resilient}{ri'ziliənt}
  \wordbreakdown{回跳、跳上的 re (= back) + sil<sal (= leap)}
  \wordsense{a. 有弹力的；有复原能力的；富有活力的}
  \wordfamily{resilience / resiliency 弹性，弹力；恢复力}
  \wordphrase{resilient and quickly recover}
  \wordexample{Most children are quite resilient and quickly recover from bumps and scrapes.（大部分孩子的恢复力都很强，磕碰、擦伤的地方很快就会好。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{salutary}{'sæljətri}
  \wordbreakdown{健康的 sal (= health)}
  \wordsense{a.（对身心）有益的}
  \wordfamily{resilient}
  \wordfamily{assault}
  \wordfamily{exult}
  \wordfamily{consult}
  \wordphrase{salutary advice Now you}
  \wordexample{The doctor gave you salutary advice. Now you need to follow it.（医生给了你有益健康的忠告，你要遵守。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{assault}{ə'sɒlt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SAL[SUL, SIL, SAIL, SAUL]}
  \wordfamily{consult；consultant；consultation；consultative；assailant（攻击者）}
  \wordsense{n. 突击；强攻}
  \wordphrase{the assault}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of assault.（此处展示 assault 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. 强攻；偷袭（阵地等） *as<ad (= to), saul<sal (= leap)}
  \wordphrase{assault and battery（暴力殴打）}
  \wordphrase{assault craft（突击舟）}
  \wordexample{assault and battery（暴力殴打）}
  \wordexample{assault craft（突击舟）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{consult}{kən'sʌlt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SAL[SUL, SIL, SAIL, SAUL]}
  \wordfamily{assault；consultant；consultation；consultative；assailant（攻击者）}
  \wordsense{v. 参考；与……商议 *con<com (= together), sul<sal (= leap)}
  \wordphrase{to consult（参考）}
  \wordexample{to consult（参考）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{consultant}{kən'sʌltənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SAL[SUL, SIL, SAIL, SAUL]}
  \wordfamily{consult；consultation；assault；consultative；assailant（攻击者）}
  \wordsense{n. 咨询者；（公司等的）顾问；会诊医生 *con<com (= together), sul<sal (= leap)}
  \wordphrase{consultant surgeon（外科会诊医生）}
  \wordphrase{firm of consultants（咨询公司）}
  \wordexample{consultant surgeon（外科会诊医生）}
  \wordexample{firm of consultants（咨询公司）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{consultation}{ˌkɒnsl'teiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SAL[SUL, SIL, SAIL, SAUL]}
  \wordfamily{consultant；consultative；consult；desultory；assailant（攻击者）}
  \wordsense{n. 商议，磋商；咨询；磋商会议 *con<com (= together), sul<sal (= leap)}
  \wordphrase{a consultation（商议，磋商）}
  \wordexample{a consultation（商议，磋商）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{consultative}{kən'sʌltətiv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SAL[SUL, SIL, SAIL, SAUL]}
  \wordfamily{consultation；desultory；consultant；exile；assailant（攻击者）}
  \wordsense{a. 咨询的；商议的，磋商的；顾问的 *con<com (= together), sul<sal (= leap)}
  \wordphrase{consultative committee（顾问委员会）}
  \wordexample{consultative committee（顾问委员会）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{desultory}{'desəltri}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SAL[SUL, SIL, SAIL, SAUL]}
  \wordfamily{consultative；exile；consultation；insult；assailant（攻击者）}
  \wordsense{a. 散漫的；无条理的；不连贯的 *de (= off), sul<sal (= leap)}
  \wordphrase{be desultory（散漫的）}
  \wordexample{be desultory（散漫的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{exile}{'eksail}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SAL[SUL, SIL, SAIL, SAUL]}
  \wordfamily{desultory；insult；consultative；result；assailant（攻击者）}
  \wordsense{n. 放逐；背井离乡，流亡；被放逐者；离乡者}
  \wordphrase{the exile}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of exile.（此处展示 exile 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. 放逐；使背井离乡 *ex (= out), il<sil<sal (= leap)}
  \wordphrase{place of exile（流放地）}
  \wordphrase{exiled for life（被终生流放的）}
  \wordexample{after an exile of ten years（流亡十年以后）}
  \wordexample{place of exile（流放地）}
  \wordexample{exiled for life（被终生流放的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{insult}{'insʌlt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SAL[SUL, SIL, SAIL, SAUL]}
  \wordfamily{insulting（侮辱的，无礼的）；assailant（攻击者）；salient（显著的）}
  \wordsense{n. 侮辱，无礼}
  \wordphrase{an insult（侮辱，无礼）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "an insult".（常见搭配：侮辱，无礼。）}
  \wordsense{v. 侮辱，羞辱 *in (= on, against), sul<sal (= leap)}
  \wordphrase{an insult（侮辱，无礼）}
  \wordexample{an insult（侮辱，无礼）}
  \wordexample{to insult（侮辱，羞辱）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{result}{ri'zʌlt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SAL[SUL, SIL, SAIL, SAUL]}
  \wordfamily{insult；resultant；exile；salacious；assailant（攻击者）}
  \wordsense{n. 结果}
  \wordphrase{a result（结果）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "a result".（常见搭配：结果。）}
  \wordsense{v. 发生；导致 *re (= back), sul<sal (= leap)}
  \wordphrase{a result（结果）}
  \wordexample{a result（结果）}
  \wordexample{to result（发生）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{resultant}{ri'zʌltənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SAL[SUL, SIL, SAIL, SAUL]}
  \wordfamily{result；salacious；insult；sally；assailant（攻击者）}
  \wordsense{a. 作为结果的；合成的}
  \wordphrase{be resultant}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of resultant.（此处展示 resultant 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 结果 *re (= back), sul<sal (= leap)}
  \wordphrase{resultant force（合力）}
  \wordphrase{resultant velocity（合速度）}
  \wordexample{resultant force（合力）}
  \wordexample{resultant velocity（合速度）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{salacious}{sə'leiʃəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SAL[SUL, SIL, SAIL, SAUL]}
  \wordfamily{resultant；sally；result；salmon；assailant（攻击者）}
  \wordsense{a. （言语、书、画等）猥亵的，淫秽的，下流的 *sal (= leap)}
  \wordphrase{be salacious（猥亵的，淫秽的，下流的）}
  \wordexample{be salacious（猥亵的，淫秽的，下流的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{sally}{'sæli}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SAL[SUL, SIL, SAIL, SAUL]}
  \wordfamily{salacious；salmon；resultant；sultry；assailant（攻击者）}
  \wordsense{n. （被包围军队的）突围，出击；俏皮话}
  \wordphrase{a sally（突围，出击）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "a sally".（常见搭配：突围，出击。）}
  \wordsense{v. （情感）迸发 *sal (= leap)}
  \wordphrase{a sally（突围，出击）}
  \wordexample{a sally（突围，出击）}
  \wordexample{to sally（迸发）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{salmon}{'sæmən}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SAL[SUL, SIL, SAIL, SAUL]}
  \wordfamily{sally；sultry；salacious；salad；assailant（攻击者）}
  \wordsense{n. 鲑鱼；鲑鱼肉；橙红色 *sal (= leap)}
  \wordphrase{a salmon（鲑鱼）}
  \wordexample{a salmon（鲑鱼）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{sultry}{'sʌltri}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SAL[SUL, SIL, SAIL, SAUL]}
  \wordfamily{salmon；salad；sally；salary；assailant（攻击者）}
  \wordsense{a. （天气）闷热的，酷热的；（人）易动感情的，热烈的 *sul<sal (= leap)}
  \wordphrase{be sultry（闷热的，酷热的）}
  \wordexample{be sultry（闷热的，酷热的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{salad}{'sæləd}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SAL[SUL, SIL, SAIL, SAUL]}
  \wordfamily{salad-dressing（色拉调料（由油、醋、香料等混合在一起制成））；salad-oil（色拉油）；assailant（攻击者）}
  \wordsense{n. 色拉，凉拌菜 *sal (= salt)}
  \wordphrase{salad days（少不更事的时期）}
  \wordexample{salad days（少不更事的时期）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{salary}{'sæləri}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SAL[SUL, SIL, SAIL, SAUL]}
  \wordfamily{salad；salaried；sultry；salmon；assailant（攻击者）}
  \wordsense{n. 薪金，薪水 *sal (= salt)}
  \wordphrase{the salary}
  \wordexample{salary of \$30,000 per annum（年薪 3 万美元）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{salaried}{'sælərid}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SAL[SUL, SIL, SAIL, SAUL]}
  \wordfamily{salary；salad；sultry；salmon；assailant（攻击者）}
  \wordsense{a. 有薪水的 *sal (= salt)}
  \wordphrase{salaried classes（工薪阶层）}
  \wordphrase{salaried posts（带薪职位）}
  \wordexample{salaried classes（工薪阶层）}
  \wordexample{salaried posts（带薪职位）}
\end{word}



\chapter{VENG}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 veng 意为 to punish。同义词根为 pun / pen。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{avenge}{ə'vendʒ}
  \wordbreakdown{对某人惩戒 a<ad (= to) + veng (= punish)}
  \wordfamily{avenger（复仇者，报复者）；avenging（报复的）；revenge（/）}
  \wordsense{vt. 复仇，报仇}
  \wordphrase{salaried classes（工薪阶层）}
  \wordexample{I avenged my low grade by toilet papering my teacher's house.（我用手纸把我们老师家围了起来，作为对他给我低分的报复。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{revenge}{ri'vendʒ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VENG}
  \wordfamily{revengeful；vengeance；vengeful；avenge（复仇）}
  \wordsense{n. /}
  \wordphrase{the revenge}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of revenge.（此处展示 revenge 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. 报仇，复仇 *re (= back)}
  \wordphrase{revenge the issue}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of revenge.（此处展示 revenge 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. 报仇，复仇）}
  \wordphrase{a revenge（/v. 报仇，复仇）}
  \wordexample{a revenge（/v. 报仇，复仇）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{revengeful}{ri'vendʒfl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VENG}
  \wordfamily{revenge；vengeance；vengeful；avenge（复仇）}
  \wordsense{a. 复仇的；报复的 *re (= back)}
  \wordphrase{be revengeful（复仇的）}
  \wordexample{be revengeful（复仇的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{vengeance}{'vendʒəns}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VENG}
  \wordsense{n. 报仇，报复}
  \wordfamily{revengeful}
  \wordfamily{vengeful}
  \wordfamily{revenge}
  \wordphrase{take vengeance on an enemy（向敌人报仇）}
  \wordphrase{with a vengeance（极度地；猛烈地）}
  \wordexample{take vengeance on an enemy（向敌人报仇）}
  \wordexample{with a vengeance（极度地；猛烈地）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{vengeful}{'vendʒfl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VENG}
  \wordfamily{vengeance；revengeful；revenge；avenge（复仇）}
  \wordsense{a. 复仇的，报复的}
  \wordphrase{be vengeful（复仇的，报复的）}
  \wordexample{be vengeful（复仇的，报复的）}
\end{word}



\chapter{CEL, CELER}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 cel, celer 意为 swift。其反义词根为 slo, tard，意为 slow。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{accelerate}{ək'seləreit}
  \wordbreakdown{使某物更快 ac<ad (= to, add) + celer (= swift)}
  \wordfamily{acceleration（加速）；accelerator（加速者，加速器）；celerity（迅速）}
  \wordsense{vt. 使加速；促进}
  \wordphrase{accelerate the issue}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of accelerate.（此处展示 accelerate 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{vi. 加快}
  \wordphrase{accelerate more each year}
  \wordexample{Global warming continues to accelerate more each year.（全球变暖每年持续加速。）}
  \wordexample{accelerate more each year}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{celerity}{si'lerəti}
  \wordbreakdown{快的状态 celer (= swift)}
  \wordsense{n. 迅速，敏捷}
  \wordfamily{accelerate}
  \wordfamily{accelerando}
  \wordfamily{decelerate}
  \wordfamily{excel}
  \wordphrase{celerity when we call}
  \wordexample{Our teenage son shows amazing celerity when we call him to dinner.（我们喊我那十几岁的儿子吃晚饭时，他的反应快得惊人。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{accelerando}{ækˌselə'rɑ:ndou}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CEL, CELER}
  \wordfamily{decelerate；excel；excellence；Excellency；accelerate（使加速）}
  \wordsense{a. /}
  \wordphrase{be accelerando}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of accelerando.（此处展示 accelerando 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{ad. 渐快的（地） *ac<ad (= to)}
  \wordphrase{be accelerando}
  \wordexample{be accelerando（/ad. 渐快的（地））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{decelerate}{di:'seləreit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CEL, CELER}
  \wordfamily{accelerando；excel；excellence；Excellency；accelerate（使加速）}
  \wordsense{v. 减速 *de (= down)}
  \wordphrase{to decelerate（减速）}
  \wordexample{to decelerate（减速）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{excel}{ik'sel}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CEL, CELER}
  \wordfamily{decelerate；excellence；accelerando；Excellency；accelerate（使加速）}
  \wordsense{v. 胜过，优于 *ex (= out)}
  \wordphrase{to excel（胜过，优于）}
  \wordexample{to excel（胜过，优于）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{excellence}{'eksələns}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CEL, CELER}
  \wordfamily{excel；Excellency；decelerate；accelerando；accelerate（使加速）}
  \wordsense{n. 卓越，优秀；优点，长处 *ex (= out)}
  \wordphrase{the excellence}
  \wordexample{prize for excellence in furniture design（家具设计优秀奖）}
  \wordexample{his excellence in all forms of sports（他在所有运动项目上的出色）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{Excellency}{'eksələnsi}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CEL, CELER}
  \wordfamily{accelerate（使加速）；celerity（迅速）；accelerando（/）}
  \wordsense{n. 阁下（对大使、总督等的尊称） *ex (= out)}
  \wordphrase{His / Her Excellency（间接提及时的称呼：阁下）}
  \wordphrase{sembl, simil / simul, par, ident 等和来自希腊语的}
  \wordexample{His / Her Excellency（间接提及时的称呼：阁下）}
\end{word}



\chapter{EQU[IQU]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 equ 意为 equal, same。其变形为 iqu。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 sembl, simil / simul, par, ident 等和来自希腊语的 tauto。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{adequate}{'ædikwət}
  \wordbreakdown{在相同的、同等的事物上添加的 ad (= to, add) + equ (= equal)}
  \wordsense{a. 适当的；足够的}
  \wordfamily{adequacy 适当；足够}
  \wordfamily{adequately 适当地；足够地}
  \wordphrase{adequate supply}
  \wordexample{My mother always reminds me of taking an adequate supply of socks to camp.（我妈妈常常提醒我：去野营时一定要带够袜子。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{equitable}{'ekwitəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{使能够同等的 equ (= equal, same)}
  \wordsense{a. 公正的，公平的；【律】衡平法的}
  \wordfamily{equitably 公正地，公平地}
  \wordfamily{equity 公正，公平；衡平法；（pl.）股息不定的普通股}
  \wordphrase{equitable property settlement}
  \wordexample{The divorcing couple negotiated an equitable property settlement.（那对离婚的夫妇就公平的财产分配达成了协议。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{equivocate}{i'kwivəkeit}
  \wordbreakdown{发出同样的声音 equ (= equal, same) + voc (= call, voice)}
  \wordsense{vi. 支吾，含糊其辞}
  \wordfamily{equivocal 歧义的；暧昧的，模糊的}
  \wordfamily{equivocation 含糊其辞}
  \wordfamily{equivocally 含糊地}
  \wordphrase{equivocating and get}
  \wordexample{Stop equivocating and get to the point!（别兜圈子了，快说重点！）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{inadequate}{in'ædikwət}
  \wordbreakdown{不是往同样的东西上添加的 in (= not) + ad (= to, add) + equ (= equal)}
  \wordsense{a. 不充分的；不适当的}
  \wordfamily{inadequacy 不充分；不适当}
  \wordfamily{inadequately 不适当地；不充分地}
  \wordphrase{inadequate for several semesters}
  \wordexample{His academic performance has been inadequate for several semesters running.（他的学术研究成绩连续几个学期都不合格。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{iniquity}{i'nikwəti}
  \wordbreakdown{不相同或不正确 in (= not) + iqu<equ (= equal, same)}
  \wordfamily{iniquitous（不公正的）；adequate（适当的）；equitable（公正的）}
  \wordsense{n. 不公正；邪恶；（pl.）不公正的行为；邪恶的行为}
  \wordphrase{iniquity}
  \wordexample{God banished Satan to hell for his iniquity.（上帝因撒旦的邪恶行径而将他放逐至地狱。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{equable}{'ekwəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 EQU[IQU]}
  \wordsense{a. 变化小的，稳定的；平和的}
  \wordfamily{equal}
  \wordfamily{equalitarian}
  \wordfamily{equality}
  \wordfamily{equalize}
  \wordphrase{equable climate / disposition / temper（变化很小的气候 / 平和的性格 / 温和的脾气）}
  \wordexample{equable climate / disposition / temper（变化很小的气候 / 平和的性格 / 温和的脾气）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{equal}{'i:kwəl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 EQU[IQU]}
  \wordfamily{equally（同样地，同等地）；adequate（适当的）；equitable（公正的）}
  \wordsense{a. 相同的，同等的；平等的；胜任的}
  \wordphrase{be equal}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of equal.（此处展示 equal 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 同等的人；可匹敌的事物}
  \wordphrase{the equal}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of equal.（此处展示 equal 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. 跟……相同；比得上}
  \wordphrase{equal opportunity（均等的机会）}
  \wordphrase{equal opportunity employer（提供公平机会的雇主）}
  \wordphrase{Equal Rights Amendment（（美国宪法的）平等权利修正案）}
  \wordexample{equal opportunity（均等的机会）}
  \wordexample{equal opportunity employer（提供公平机会的雇主）}
  \wordexample{She has no equal in cooking.（在烹饪方面没有谁能和她匹敌。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{equalitarian}{ˌi:kwɔli'teəriən}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 EQU[IQU]}
  \wordfamily{equal；equality；equable；equalize；adequate（适当的）}
  \wordsense{a. 平等主义的}
  \wordphrase{be equalitarian（平等主义的）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "be equalitarian".（常见搭配：平等主义的。）}
  \wordsense{n. 平等主义者}
  \wordphrase{be equalitarian（平等主义的）}
  \wordexample{be equalitarian（平等主义的）}
  \wordexample{an equalitarian（平等主义者）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{equality}{i:'kwɒləti}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 EQU[IQU]}
  \wordsense{n. 平等；同等；均等}
  \wordfamily{equalitarian}
  \wordfamily{equalize}
  \wordfamily{equal}
  \wordfamily{equate}
  \wordfamily{an equality（平等）}
  \wordphrase{an equality（平等）}
  \wordexample{an equality（平等）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{equalize}{'i:kwəlaiz}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 EQU[IQU]}
  \wordfamily{adequate（适当的）；equitable（公正的）；equivocate（支吾）}
  \wordsense{v. 使相等；使平等 *ize (= make)}
  \wordphrase{equalize tax burdens（均摊捐税负担）}
  \wordphrase{equalize income（使收入均等）}
  \wordexample{equalize tax burdens（均摊捐税负担）}
  \wordexample{equalize income（使收入均等）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{equate}{i:'kweit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 EQU[IQU]}
  \wordfamily{equalize；equation；equality；equator；adequate（适当的）}
  \wordsense{v. 等同；使相同 *ate (= make)}
  \wordphrase{equate two quantities（使两个数量相同）}
  \wordexample{equate the possession of wealth to happiness（认为拥有财富就等于幸福）}
  \wordexample{equate two quantities（使两个数量相同）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{equation}{i'kweiʒn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 EQU[IQU]}
  \wordsense{n.【数】方程式；等式；相等}
  \wordfamily{equate}
  \wordfamily{equator}
  \wordfamily{equalize}
  \wordfamily{equatorial}
  \wordfamily{an equation（方程式）}
  \wordphrase{an equation（方程式）}
  \wordexample{an equation（方程式）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{equator}{i'kweitə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 EQU[IQU]}
  \wordsense{n.（the ～）赤道}
  \wordfamily{equation}
  \wordfamily{equatorial}
  \wordfamily{equate}
  \wordfamily{equidistant}
  \wordfamily{an equator（赤道）}
  \wordphrase{an equator（赤道）}
  \wordexample{an equator（赤道）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{equatorial}{ˌekwə'tɔ:riəl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 EQU[IQU]}
  \wordsense{a. 赤道的；赤道附近的}
  \wordfamily{equator}
  \wordfamily{equidistant}
  \wordfamily{equation}
  \wordfamily{equilateral}
  \wordphrase{equatorial Africa（位于赤道附近的非洲）}
  \wordphrase{equatorial orbit（赤道轨道）}
  \wordphrase{Equatorial Guinea（赤道几内亚）}
  \wordexample{equatorial Africa（位于赤道附近的非洲）}
  \wordexample{equatorial orbit（赤道轨道）}
  \wordexample{Equatorial Guinea（赤道几内亚）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{equidistant}{ˌi:kwi'distənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 EQU[IQU]}
  \wordfamily{equatorial；equilateral；equator；equilibrium；adequate（适当的）}
  \wordsense{a. 等距的 *di<dis (= away), st (= stand)}
  \wordphrase{equidistant diplomacy（等距离外交）}
  \wordexample{equidistant diplomacy（等距离外交）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{equilateral}{ˌi:kwi'lætərəl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 EQU[IQU]}
  \wordfamily{equidistant；equilibrium；equatorial；equinox；adequate（适当的）}
  \wordsense{a. 等边的 *later (= side)}
  \wordphrase{equilateral triangle（等边三角形）}
  \wordexample{equilateral triangle（等边三角形）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{equilibrium}{ˌi:kwi'libriəm}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 EQU[IQU]}
  \wordfamily{equilateral；equinox；equidistant；equinoctial；adequate（适当的）}
  \wordsense{n. 平衡状态；均衡；（心情的）平静 *libr (= balance)}
  \wordphrase{in a state of equilibrium（处于平衡状态的）}
  \wordphrase{scales in equilibrium（平衡的天平）}
  \wordphrase{maintain / lose equilibrium（保持 / 失去平衡）}
  \wordexample{in a state of equilibrium（处于平衡状态的）}
  \wordexample{scales in equilibrium（平衡的天平）}
  \wordexample{maintain / lose equilibrium（保持 / 失去平衡）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{equinox}{'i:kwinɒks}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 EQU[IQU]}
  \wordfamily{equilibrium；equinoctial；equilateral；equipoise；adequate（适当的）}
  \wordsense{n. 昼夜平分时，春分或秋分 *nox (= night)}
  \wordphrase{autumnal / spring equinox（秋 / 春分）}
  \wordexample{autumnal / spring equinox（秋 / 春分）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{equinoctial}{ˌi:kwi'nɒkʃəl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 EQU[IQU]}
  \wordfamily{equinox；equipoise；equilibrium；equivalent；adequate（适当的）}
  \wordsense{a. 昼夜平分的，春分或秋分的 *noct (= night)}
  \wordphrase{equinoctial circle / line（天（球）赤道）}
  \wordphrase{equinoctial point（二分点）}
  \wordphrase{equinoctial gales / tides（二分点风暴 / 分点潮）}
  \wordexample{equinoctial circle / line（天（球）赤道）}
  \wordexample{equinoctial point（二分点）}
  \wordexample{equinoctial gales / tides（二分点风暴 / 分点潮）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{equipoise}{'ekwipɔiz}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 EQU[IQU]}
  \wordfamily{equinoctial；equivalent；equinox；equivalence（/ equivalency）；adequate（适当的）}
  \wordsense{n. 平衡，均衡；平衡物；秤锤 *pois<pos (= put)}
  \wordphrase{an equipoise（平衡，均衡）}
  \wordexample{an equipoise（平衡，均衡）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{equivalent}{i'kwivələnt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 EQU[IQU]}
  \wordfamily{adequate（适当的）；equitable（公正的）；equivocate（支吾）}
  \wordsense{a. 同等的}
  \wordphrase{noun equivalent（相当于名词的形式）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "noun equivalent".（常见搭配：相当于名词的形式。）}
  \wordsense{n. 同等物；相当物；意义相同的词（或符号、表达法等） *val (= worth)}
  \wordphrase{equivalent to an insult（等于辱骂的）}
  \wordexample{the Korean equivalent of “thank you”（和 “thank you” 相对应的韩语）}
  \wordexample{equivalent to an insult（等于辱骂的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{equivalence / equivalency}{i'kwivələns / -ənsi}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 EQU[IQU]}
  \wordfamily{equivalent；inequality；equipoise；inequitable；adequate（适当的）}
  \wordsense{n. 等同；等价 *val (= worth)}
  \wordphrase{High School Equivalency Exam（中学同等学力考试）}
  \wordexample{High School Equivalency Exam（中学同等学力考试）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{inequality}{ˌini'kwɒləti}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 EQU[IQU]}
  \wordfamily{equivalence（/ equivalency）；inequitable；equivalent；inequity；adequate（适当的）}
  \wordsense{n. 不平等；不同；不均衡；不等式 *in (= not)}
  \wordphrase{the inequality}
  \wordexample{the inequality between the rich and the poor（贫富间的不平等）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{inequitable}{in'ekwitəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 EQU[IQU]}
  \wordfamily{inequality；inequity；equivalence（/ equivalency）；unequal；adequate（适当的）}
  \wordsense{a. 不公平的，不公正的 *in (= not)}
  \wordphrase{inequitable division of the profits（不公平的收益分配）}
  \wordexample{inequitable division of the profits（不公平的收益分配）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{inequity}{in'ekwəti}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 EQU[IQU]}
  \wordfamily{inequitable；unequal；inequality；unequalled；adequate（适当的）}
  \wordsense{n. 不公平，不公正 *in (= not)}
  \wordphrase{an inequity（不公平，不公正）}
  \wordexample{an inequity（不公平，不公正）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{unequal}{ʌn'i:kwəl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 EQU[IQU]}
  \wordfamily{inequity；unequalled；inequitable；unequivocal；adequate（适当的）}
  \wordsense{a. 不同的；不公平的 *un (= not)}
  \wordphrase{be unequal（不同的）}
  \wordexample{be unequal（不同的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{unequalled}{ʌn'i:kwəld}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 EQU[IQU]}
  \wordfamily{unequal；unequivocal；inequity；inequitable；adequate（适当的）}
  \wordsense{a. 无与伦比的 *un (= not)}
  \wordphrase{be unequalled（无与伦比的）}
  \wordexample{be unequalled（无与伦比的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{unequivocal}{ˌʌni'kwivəkl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 EQU[IQU]}
  \wordfamily{unequalled；unequal；inequity；inequitable；adequate（适当的）}
  \wordsense{a. 明确的，不含糊的 *un (= not), voc (= voice, call)}
  \wordphrase{be unequivocal（明确的，不含糊的）}
  \wordexample{be unequivocal（明确的，不含糊的）}
\end{word}



\chapter{FLICT}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 flict 意为 to strike。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 bat, cuss, fend / fens[fenc, fest], lid / lis, plaud / plaus[plod, plos, plex] 和来自盎格鲁-撒克逊语的 sla 等。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{affliction}{ə'flikʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{向某人一击 af<ad (= to) + flict (= strike)}
  \wordfamily{afflict（使痛苦）；inflict（sth + on + sb 给予打击）；conflict（斗争）}
  \wordsense{n. 痛苦；苦事；痛苦的事由}
  \wordphrase{affliction that causes her}
  \wordexample{She has some affliction that causes her to walk with a limp.（她感到有些疼痛，走路一瘸一拐的。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{inflict}{in'flikt}
  \wordbreakdown{瞄准打 in (= on, against) + flict (= strike)}
  \wordfamily{affliction（痛苦）；conflict（斗争）；conflicting（抵触的）}
  \wordsense{vt. sth + on + sb 给予打击；使痛苦；加以处罚等}
  \wordphrase{inflicted severe damages}
  \wordexample{The tornado inflicted severe damages on the small town.（龙卷风给这个小城镇造成了严重的破坏。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{conflict}{'kɒnflikt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FLICT}
  \wordfamily{affliction（痛苦）；inflict（sth + on + sb 给予打击）；conflicting（抵触的）}
  \wordsense{n. 斗争，战斗；冲突}
  \wordphrase{wordy conflict（舌战）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "wordy conflict".（常见搭配：舌战。）}
  \wordsense{v. 冲突；矛盾 *con<com (= together)}
  \wordphrase{a conflict of interest（利益冲突）}
  \wordexample{the conflict between duty and desire（义务和欲望的冲突）}
  \wordexample{a conflict of interest（利益冲突）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{conflicting}{kən'fliktiŋ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FLICT}
  \wordfamily{conflict；affliction（痛苦）；inflict（sth + on + sb 给予打击）}
  \wordsense{a. 抵触的；冲突的 *con<com (= together)}
  \wordphrase{conflicting views（相互矛盾的观点）}
  \wordexample{conflicting views（相互矛盾的观点）}
\end{word}



\chapter{LIG[LI, LEAG, LY]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 lig 意为 to bind, to tie。变形有 li, leag, ly 等。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 cinct, nect / nex, strain / strict / string / stress 等和来自盎格鲁-撒克逊语的 bond。此外，意为 to join 的 junct / join / jug 也与此词根含义相似。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{alliance}{ə'laiəns}
  \wordbreakdown{捆得很近 al<ad (= to, near) + li<lig (= bind)}
  \wordfamily{ally（结盟）；allied（结盟的）；join（的）}
  \wordsense{n. 同盟；联姻}
  \wordphrase{cinct, nect / nex, strain / strict / string / stress 等和来自盎格鲁-撒克逊语的}
  \wordexample{In alliance with my mother, we convinced my father to buy us kids a car.（我们和妈妈联合起来说服爸爸给我们小孩子买了一辆汽车。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{obligate}{'ɒbligeit}
  \wordbreakdown{用力地捆 ob (= intensive) + lig (= bind)}
  \wordsense{vt. sb + to do 使负有责任、义务}
  \wordfamily{obligation 义务，责任}
  \wordfamily{obligatory 有义务的；必须的}
  \wordphrase{obligates the landlord}
  \wordexample{A typical rental lease obligates the landlord to make any needed repairs.（典型的租赁合同上都规定房东有义务负责所有必要的维修。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{liaison}{li'eizɒn}
  \wordbreakdown{起到捆的作用 li<lig (= bind)}
  \wordfamily{liaise（取得联系）；alliance（同盟）；obligate（sb + to do 使负有责任、义务）}
  \wordsense{n. 联络；私通}
  \wordphrase{liaison between top management}
  \wordexample{I work as a liaison between top management and the union leaders.（我负责最高管理层和工会领导人之间的联络工作。）}
  \wordexample{liaison between top management}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{liability}{ˌlaiə'biləti}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LIG[LI, LEAG, LY]}
  \wordfamily{liable；league；ligament；ligature；alliance（同盟）}
  \wordsense{n. 责任，义务；倾向；（pl.）债务；负债；不利（的事情） *li<lig (= bind)}
  \wordphrase{liability insurance（责任保险）}
  \wordphrase{liability for military service（兵役义务）}
  \wordphrase{liability to disease（易于患病的倾向）}
  \wordexample{liability insurance（责任保险）}
  \wordexample{liability for military service（兵役义务）}
  \wordexample{liability to disease（易于患病的倾向）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{liable}{'laiəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LIG[LI, LEAG, LY]}
  \wordfamily{liability；league；ligament；ligature；alliance（同盟）}
  \wordsense{a. 有责任的，有义务的；可能遭受……的；易患……的 *li<lig (= bind)}
  \wordphrase{be liable（有责任的，有义务的）}
  \wordexample{be liable（有责任的，有义务的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{league}{li:g}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LIG[LI, LEAG, LY]}
  \wordfamily{liable；ligament；liability；ligature；alliance（同盟）}
  \wordsense{n. 联盟，同盟；盟约；里格（长度单位，约为 3 英里、5 公里或 3 海里）}
  \wordphrase{a league（联盟，同盟）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "a league".（常见搭配：联盟，同盟。）}
  \wordsense{v. （使）结盟，联盟，（使）订立盟约 *leag<lig (= tie)}
  \wordphrase{a league（联盟，同盟）}
  \wordexample{a league（联盟，同盟）}
  \wordexample{to league（结盟，联盟，（使）订立盟约）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{ligament}{'ligəmənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LIG[LI, LEAG, LY]}
  \wordsense{n. 系带，纽带；韧带}
  \wordfamily{league}
  \wordfamily{ligature}
  \wordfamily{liable}
  \wordfamily{oblige}
  \wordfamily{a ligament（系带，纽带）}
  \wordphrase{a ligament（系带，纽带）}
  \wordexample{a ligament（系带，纽带）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{ligature}{'ligətʃə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LIG[LI, LEAG, LY]}
  \wordsense{n. 带子，绳索；绷带；结扎线}
  \wordfamily{ligament}
  \wordfamily{oblige}
  \wordfamily{league}
  \wordfamily{obliging}
  \wordfamily{a ligature（带子，绳索）}
  \wordphrase{a ligature（带子，绳索）}
  \wordexample{a ligature（带子，绳索）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{oblige}{ə'blaidʒ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LIG[LI, LEAG, LY]}
  \wordfamily{ligature；obliging；ligament；rally；alliance（同盟）}
  \wordsense{v. 迫使；施恩于；答应……的请求 *ob (= to)}
  \wordphrase{to oblige（迫使）}
  \wordexample{to oblige（迫使）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{obliging}{ə'blaidʒiŋ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LIG[LI, LEAG, LY]}
  \wordfamily{oblige；rally；ligature；reliable；alliance（同盟）}
  \wordsense{a. 乐于助人的 *ob (= to)}
  \wordphrase{obliging neighbors（乐于助人的邻居）}
  \wordexample{obliging neighbors（乐于助人的邻居）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{rally}{'ræli}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LIG[LI, LEAG, LY]}
  \wordfamily{alliance（同盟）；obligate（sb + to do 使负有责任、义务）；liaison（联络）}
  \wordsense{n. 大会，集会；（重新）集合}
  \wordphrase{political rally（政治集会）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "political rally".（常见搭配：政治集会。）}
  \wordsense{v. 重新集合、聚集；召集 *r<re (= again), al<ad (= near), ly<lig (= tie)}
  \wordphrase{a rally（大会，集会）}
  \wordexample{a rally（大会，集会）}
  \wordexample{to rally（重新集合、聚集）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{reliable}{ri'laiəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LIG[LI, LEAG, LY]}
  \wordfamily{reliability（可靠性，可信赖性）；alliance（同盟）；obligate（sb + to do 使负有责任、义务）}
  \wordsense{a. 可靠的；可依赖的 *re (= again), li<lig (= tie)}
  \wordphrase{reliable information（可靠的信息）}
  \wordexample{reliable information（可靠的信息）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{reliance}{ri'laiəns}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LIG[LI, LEAG, LY]}
  \wordfamily{alliance（同盟）；obligate（sb + to do 使负有责任、义务）；liaison（联络）}
  \wordsense{n. 信任，信赖；可依赖的人（或物） *re (= again), li<lig (= tie)}
  \wordphrase{a reliance（信任，信赖）}
  \wordexample{a reliance（信任，信赖）}

\begin{word}[***]{rely}{ri'lai}

  \wordsense{v. 依靠；信赖 *re (= again), li<lig (= tie)}

  \wordexample{to rely（依靠）}

\begin{word}[***]{religion}{ri'lidʒən}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LIG[LI, LEAG, LY]}
  \wordfamily{rely；religious；reliance；reliable；alliance（同盟）}
  \wordsense{n. 宗教；信仰；信条 *re (= again)}
  \wordphrase{freedom of religion（宗教信仰自由）}
  \wordexample{great religions of the world（世界主要宗教（Christianity, Judaism, Islam, Confucianism, Taoism, Buddhism, Hinduism, etc.））}
  \wordexample{freedom of religion（宗教信仰自由）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{religious}{ri'lidʒəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LIG[LI, LEAG, LY]}

  \wordsense{a. 宗教的，宗教上的；虔诚的；修道的；认真的}
  \wordphrase{be religious}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of religious.（此处展示 religious 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 修道士；修女；和尚；尼姑 *re (= again)}
  \wordphrase{religious rites（宗教仪式）}
  \wordphrase{religious life（修行生活）}
  \wordphrase{religious man（虔诚的人）}
  \wordexample{religious rites（宗教仪式）}
  \wordexample{religious life（修行生活）}
  \wordexample{religious man（虔诚的人）}

\chapter{VAL[VAIL]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 val 意为 worth, strong。变形为 vail。此外，来自拉丁语的 rob, fort / forc, firm, bil 等也有 strong 的意思。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{available}{ə'veiləbl}
  \wordbreakdown{对某人有价值的 a<ad (= to) + vail<val (= worth)}
  \wordfamily{avail（有助于）；ambivalent（（两种态度、意义等）对立的）；invalidate（使无效）}
  \wordsense{a. 可利用的，可使用的；有用的；可入住的；有空的；可见面的；可获得的}
  \wordphrase{religious rites（宗教仪式）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "religious rites".（常见搭配：宗教仪式。）}
  \wordsense{n. 修道士；修女；和尚；尼姑 *re (= again)}
  \wordphrase{rob, fort / forc, firm, bil 等也有}
  \wordexample{Many kinds of organically grown vegetables are available at this store.（在这家商店可以买到很多种有机蔬菜。）}
  \wordexample{rob, fort / forc, firm, bil 等也有}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{ambivalent}{æm'bivələnt}
  \wordbreakdown{价值站在双方的 ambi (= both) + val (= worth)}
  \wordfamily{ambivalence（矛盾情绪）；available（可利用的）；invalidate（使无效）}
  \wordsense{a. （两种态度、意义等）对立的，矛盾的}
  \wordphrase{ambivalent about his films}
  \wordexample{I'm ambivalent about his films. They're brilliant, but they're too violent!（我对他的电影持矛盾的态度，电影很精彩，但过于暴力！）}
  \wordexample{ambivalent about his films}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{invalidate}{in'vælideit}
  \wordbreakdown{使没有价值 in (= not) + val (= strong, worth)}
  \wordsense{vt. 使无效}
  \wordfamily{invalidation 使无效}
  \wordfamily{invalidity 无效}
  \wordfamily{invalidism 病弱，虚弱}
  \wordfamily{invalid 无效的}
  \wordphrase{invalid 无效的}
  \wordexample{invalid 无效的；（n./a.）['invəlid] 病人；有病的}
  \wordexample{Her research findings were invalidated by her strong personal bias.（她的研究成果因她顽固的个人偏见而变得毫无用处。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{prevail}{pri'veil}
  \wordbreakdown{力量领先 pre (= before) + vail<val (= strength, worth)}
  \wordsense{vi. 占优势，战胜；普及，盛行；奏效}
  \wordfamily{prevailing 占优势的；普及的，流行的；有效的}
  \wordfamily{prevalent 普及的，流行的}
  \wordfamily{prevalence 普及，流行}
  \wordfamily{prevailed}
  \wordphrase{prevailed}
  \wordexample{Although the team was down in the 3rd quarter, in the end they prevailed.（虽然那个队在第三局处于劣势，不过最终他们还是赢了。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{convalesce}{ˌkɒnvə'les}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VAL[VAIL]}
  \wordfamily{convalescent；countervail；devaluate；devaluation；available（可利用的）}
  \wordsense{v. （生病后渐渐地）恢复健康 *con<com (= with), esc (= becoming)}
  \wordphrase{convalesce after a long illness（久病之后逐渐康复）}
  \wordexample{convalesce after a long illness（久病之后逐渐康复）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{convalescent}{ˌkɒnvə'lesnt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VAL[VAIL]}
  \wordfamily{available（可利用的）；ambivalent（（两种态度、意义等）对立的）；invalidate（使无效）}
  \wordsense{a. （病）渐愈的；恢复中的}
  \wordphrase{be convalescent（渐愈的）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "be convalescent".（常见搭配：渐愈的。）}
  \wordsense{n. 康复期病人}
  \wordphrase{convalescent hospital（（为康复期病人提供的）康复院，疗养院）}
  \wordexample{convalescent hospital（（为康复期病人提供的）康复院，疗养院）}
  \wordexample{be convalescent（渐愈的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{countervail}{ˌkauntə'veil}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VAL[VAIL]}
  \wordfamily{available（可利用的）；ambivalent（（两种态度、意义等）对立的）；invalidate（使无效）}
  \wordsense{v. 与……势均力敌；抵消；补偿 *counter (= against), vail<val (= strong, strength)}
  \wordphrase{to countervail（与……势均力敌）}
  \wordexample{to countervail（与……势均力敌）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{devaluate}{di:'væljueit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VAL[VAIL]}
  \wordfamily{devalue（（使）贬值）；available（可利用的）；ambivalent（（两种态度、意义等）对立的）}
  \wordsense{v. 降低……的价值，（使）贬值 *de (= down)}
  \wordphrase{devaluate the dollar（使美元贬值）}
  \wordexample{devaluate the dollar（使美元贬值）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{devaluation}{diˌvælju'eiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VAL[VAIL]}
  \wordfamily{devaluate；equivalent；countervail；evaluate；available（可利用的）}
  \wordsense{n. 贬值 *de (= down)}
  \wordphrase{devaluation policy（贬值政策）}
  \wordexample{devaluation policy（贬值政策）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{equivalent}{i'kwivələnt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VAL[VAIL]}
  \wordfamily{devaluation；evaluate；devaluate；evaluation；available（可利用的）}
  \wordsense{a. 同等的}
  \wordphrase{be equivalent（同等的）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "be equivalent".（常见搭配：同等的。）}
  \wordsense{n. 同等物；相当物；意义相同的词（或符号、表达法等） *equ (= equal)}
  \wordphrase{be equivalent（同等的）}
  \wordexample{be equivalent（同等的）}
  \wordexample{an equivalent（同等物）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{evaluate}{i'væljueit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VAL[VAIL]}
  \wordfamily{equivalent；evaluation；devaluation；invalid；available（可利用的）}
  \wordsense{v. 评价；鉴定 *e<ex (= out)}
  \wordphrase{to evaluate（评价）}
  \wordexample{to evaluate（评价）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{evaluation}{iˌvælju'eiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VAL[VAIL]}
  \wordfamily{evaluate；invalid；equivalent；invaluable；available（可利用的）}
  \wordsense{n. 评价 *e<ex (= out)}
  \wordphrase{an evaluation（评价）}
  \wordexample{an evaluation（评价）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{invalid}{'invəlid}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VAL[VAIL]}
  \wordfamily{invalidity（无效）；invalidism（长期的病弱状态）；available（可利用的）}
  \wordsense{a. 无效的}
  \wordphrase{be invalid（无效的）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "be invalid".（常见搭配：无效的。）}
  \wordsense{n. 患者，病人}
  \wordphrase{be invalid（无效的）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "be invalid".（常见搭配：无效的。）}
  \wordsense{v. （因病或伤而）使退役 *in (= not)}
  \wordphrase{invalid excuses（无效的辩解）}
  \wordphrase{invalid will（无效遗嘱）}
  \wordexample{be invalided out of the army（因伤病而退伍）}
  \wordexample{invalid excuses（无效的辩解）}
  \wordexample{invalid will（无效遗嘱）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{invaluable}{in'væljuəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VAL[VAIL]}
  \wordfamily{invalid；revalue；evaluation；revaluation；available（可利用的）}
  \wordsense{a. 无法估价的，非常贵重的 *in (= intensive, not)}
  \wordphrase{be invaluable（无法估价的，非常贵重的）}
  \wordexample{be invaluable（无法估价的，非常贵重的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{revalue}{ri:'vælju:}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VAL[VAIL]}
  \wordfamily{invaluable；revaluation；invalid；unavailing；available（可利用的）}
  \wordsense{v. 对……再评价；对……再估价 *re (= again)}
  \wordphrase{to revalue（对……再评价）}
  \wordexample{to revalue（对……再评价）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{revaluation}{ˌri:vælju'eiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VAL[VAIL]}
  \wordfamily{available（可利用的）；ambivalent（（两种态度、意义等）对立的）；invalidate（使无效）}
  \wordsense{n. 再评价，再估价；币值的重新调整 *re (= again)}
  \wordphrase{revaluation of the German mark（重新调整德国马克的币值）}
  \wordexample{revaluation of the German mark（重新调整德国马克的币值）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{unavailing}{ˌʌnə'veiliŋ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VAL[VAIL]}
  \wordfamily{revaluation；undervalue；revalue；valence（/ valency）；available（可利用的）}
  \wordsense{a. 无效的；无益的；无用的 *un (= not), a<ad (= to), vail<val (= worth)}
  \wordphrase{be unavailing（无效的）}
  \wordexample{be unavailing（无效的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{undervalue}{ˌʌndə'value:}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VAL[VAIL]}
  \wordfamily{overvalue（高估）；available（可利用的）；ambivalent（（两种态度、意义等）对立的）}
  \wordsense{v. 低估}
  \wordphrase{to undervalue（低估）}
  \wordexample{to undervalue（低估）}
\end{word}

  \wordbreakdown{词根 VAL[VAIL]}

  \wordphrase{a valence / valency（原子价，化合价）}
  \wordexample{a valence / valency（原子价，化合价）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{valetudinarian}{ˌvælitju:di'neəriən}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VAL[VAIL]}
  \wordfamily{valence（/ valency）；valiant；undervalue；valid；available（可利用的）}
  \wordsense{a. 体弱多病的；过分担心健康的}
  \wordphrase{be valetudinarian（体弱多病的）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "be valetudinarian".（常见搭配：体弱多病的。）}
  \wordsense{n. 体弱多病的人；过分担心健康的人}
  \wordphrase{be valetudinarian（体弱多病的）}
  \wordexample{be valetudinarian（体弱多病的）}
  \wordexample{a valetudinarian（体弱多病的人）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{valiant}{'væliənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VAL[VAIL]}
  \wordsense{a. 勇敢的，英勇的}
  \wordfamily{valetudinarian}
  \wordfamily{valid}
  \wordfamily{valence / valency}
  \wordfamily{validity}
  \wordphrase{valiant soldier（勇敢的战士）}
  \wordexample{valiant soldier（勇敢的战士）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{valid}{'vælid}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VAL[VAIL]}
  \wordsense{a. 有效的；有根据的；正当的}
  \wordfamily{valiant}
  \wordfamily{validity}
  \wordfamily{valetudinarian}
  \wordfamily{valour}
  \wordphrase{valid for six months（六个月内有效）}
  \wordphrase{raise valid objections（提出有根据的异议）}
  \wordexample{valid for six months（六个月内有效）}
  \wordexample{raise valid objections（提出有根据的异议）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{validity}{və'lidəti}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VAL[VAIL]}
  \wordfamily{validate（使生效）；available（可利用的）；ambivalent（（两种态度、意义等）对立的）}
  \wordsense{n. 正当性；有效性；效力}
  \wordphrase{a validity（正当性）}
  \wordexample{a validity（正当性）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{valour}{'vælə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VAL[VAIL]}
  \wordsense{n.（特指人在战场上的）勇气；勇猛}
  \wordfamily{valorous（勇敢的，勇猛的）}

  \wordphrase{a valour（勇气）}

\begin{word}[***]{valuable}{'væljuəbl}

  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of valuable.（此处展示 valuable 的常见用法。）}

  \wordphrase{valuable friend（可贵的朋友）}
  \wordphrase{valuable discovery（有价值的发现）}
  \wordexample{valuable friend（可贵的朋友）}
  \wordexample{valuable discovery（有价值的发现）}

\chapter{COR, CORD[COUR]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 cor, cord 意为 heart。变形为 cour。同义词根为来自希腊语的 card。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{accord}{ə'kɔ:d}
  \wordbreakdown{心在近处 ac<ad (= to, near) + cord (= heart)}
  \wordfamily{accordance（一致，调和）；accordant（一致的，和谐的）；concord（一致）}
  \wordsense{vi. with + sb 一致，调和}
  \wordphrase{accord in practice}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of accord.（此处展示 accord 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 一致，调和；协议，协定}
  \wordphrase{accord with management}
  \wordexample{The union reached an accord with management at 4:30 this afternoon.（工会于今天下午 4 点 30 分和管理层达成了协议。）}
  \wordexample{accord with management}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{concord}{'kɒŋkɔ:d}
  \wordbreakdown{心一致 con<com (= with) + cord (= heart)}
  \wordsense{n. 一致；和谐}
  \wordfamily{concordance 一致；和谐；用语索引}
  \wordfamily{concordat 协定，协约}
  \wordfamily{concordant 一致的；和谐的}
  \wordphrase{concord}
  \wordexample{The marital rift was short lasting, ending in concord.（婚姻生活的不和谐持续了一小段时间，但最终还是归于和睦。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{cordial}{'kɔ:diəl}
  \wordbreakdown{出自内心的 cord (= heart)}
  \wordfamily{cordiality（热诚，诚挚）；cordially（热诚地，诚挚地）；accord（with + sb 一致）}
  \wordsense{a. 衷心的，诚恳的}
  \wordphrase{be cordial}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of cordial.（此处展示 cordial 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 甘露酒}
  \wordphrase{cordial welcome when we}
  \wordexample{We received a cordial welcome when we arrived at my uncle's home.（当我们到叔叔家时，受到了热烈的欢迎。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{discordant}{dis'kɔ:dənt}
  \wordbreakdown{心分开的 dis (= apart) + cord (= heart)}
  \wordsense{a. 不和的；不一致的；（声音）不和谐的；刺耳的}
  \wordfamily{discordance / discordancy 不调和；不一致}
  \wordfamily{discord 不一致；不和；（n.）不和；纷争}
  \wordphrase{discordant views were vigorously}
  \wordexample{A number of discordant views were vigorously expressed at the meeting.（会上人们踊跃地提出了很多不同的意见。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{core}{kɔ:(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 COR, CORD[COUR]}
  \wordfamily{courage；courageous；discourage；encore；accord（with + sb 一致）}
  \wordsense{n. 核心；重要部分，中心部分；果核，果心}
  \wordphrase{the core}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of core.（此处展示 core 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. 挖去……的果核}
  \wordphrase{Core English（核心英语）}
  \wordphrase{to the core（彻底）}
  \wordphrase{core an apple（挖出苹果的核）}
  \wordexample{Core English（核心英语）}
  \wordexample{to the core（彻底）}
  \wordexample{core an apple（挖出苹果的核）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{courage}{'kʌridʒ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 COR, CORD[COUR]}
  \wordfamily{Dutch（courage（酒后之勇）；accord（with + sb 一致）；concord（一致）}
  \wordsense{n. 勇气，胆量 *cour<cor (= heart)}
  \wordphrase{a courage（勇气，胆量）}
  \wordexample{a courage（勇气，胆量）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{courageous}{kə'reidʒəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 COR, CORD[COUR]}
  \wordfamily{courage；discourage；core；encore；accord（with + sb 一致）}
  \wordsense{a. 英勇的，勇敢的 *cour<cor (= heart)}
  \wordphrase{be courageous（英勇的，勇敢的）}
  \wordexample{be courageous（英勇的，勇敢的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{discourage}{dis'kʌridʒ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 COR, CORD[COUR]}
  \wordfamily{accord（with + sb 一致）；concord（一致）；cordial（衷心的）}
  \wordsense{v. 使丧失勇气，使沮丧；使打消念头；阻碍 *dis (= away)}
  \wordphrase{to discourage（使丧失勇气，使沮丧）}
  \wordexample{to discourage（使丧失勇气，使沮丧）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{encore}{'ɒŋkɔ:(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 COR, CORD[COUR]}
  \wordfamily{discourage；encourage；courageous；record；accord（with + sb 一致）}
  \wordsense{n. 请求再演}
  \wordphrase{the encore}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of encore.（此处展示 encore 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. 要求再演（法国喊 “bis”） *en (= intensive)}
  \wordphrase{get an encore（被要求再演）}
  \wordphrase{encore the pianist（要求那位钢琴演奏家再表演一曲）}
  \wordphrase{encore hours（（应观众要求的）重播时间）}
  \wordexample{get an encore（被要求再演）}
  \wordexample{encore the pianist（要求那位钢琴演奏家再表演一曲）}
  \wordexample{encore hours（（应观众要求的）重播时间）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{encourage}{in'kʌridʒ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 COR, CORD[COUR]}
  \wordfamily{accord（with + sb 一致）；concord（一致）；cordial（衷心的）}
  \wordsense{v. 激励，鼓舞；促进 *en (= make)}
  \wordphrase{to encourage（激励，鼓舞）}
  \wordexample{to encourage（激励，鼓舞）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{record}{'rekɔ:d}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 COR, CORD[COUR]}
  \wordfamily{record-breaking（打破纪录的）；accord（with + sb 一致）；concord（一致）}
  \wordsense{n. 记录；履历；唱片；（比赛等的）最高纪录}
  \wordphrase{good record（好的记录）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "good record".（常见搭配：好的记录。）}
  \wordsense{v. 记录；录音 *re (= again)}
  \wordphrase{off the record（非正式的；不得发表的）}
  \wordexample{off the record（非正式的；不得发表的）}
  \wordexample{a record（记录）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{recorder}{ri'kɔ:də(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 COR, CORD[COUR]}
  \wordsense{n. 记录员；记录器，录音机，录像机}
  \wordfamily{tape recorder（录音机）}
  \wordfamily{videotape recorder（磁带录像机）}
  \wordfamily{a recorder（记录员）}
  \wordphrase{a recorder（记录员）}
  \wordexample{a recorder（记录员）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{recording}{ri'kɔ:diŋ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 COR, CORD[COUR]}
  \wordfamily{recorder；record；encourage；encore；accord（with + sb 一致）}
  \wordsense{a. 记录的}
  \wordphrase{be recording（记录的）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "be recording".（常见搭配：记录的。）}
  \wordsense{n. 录音，录像 *re (= again)}
  \wordphrase{be recording（记录的）}
  \wordexample{be recording（记录的）}
  \wordexample{a recording（录音，录像）}
\end{word}



\chapter{APT[EPT, ATT]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 apt 意为 fit。变形为 ept 和 att。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{adapt}{ə'dæpt}
  \wordbreakdown{使适合于某物 ad (= to) + apt (= fit)}
  \wordfamily{adaptation（适应，顺应）；adaptability（适应性）；adaptable（可适应的）；adapted（合适的）；adaptive（合适的，适应的）}
  \wordsense{v. oneself + to + sth 使适应，使合适；改编；改造}
  \wordphrase{adapt the issue}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of adapt.（此处展示 adapt 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{vi. to + sth 适应}
  \wordphrase{adapt in practice}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of adapt.（此处展示 adapt 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 录音，录像 *re (= again)}
  \wordphrase{adapt to a new}
  \wordphrase{to adapt（oneself + to + sth 使适应，使合适）}
  \wordexample{They say it takes 6 weeks to adapt to a new town after you move.（人们说搬家之后需要 6 周的时间来适应新城市的生活。）}
  \wordexample{adapt to a new}
  \wordexample{to adapt（oneself + to + sth 使适应，使合适）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{adept}{ə'dept}
  \wordbreakdown{正好适合于某物的，合适的 ad (= to) + ept<apt (= fit)}
  \wordfamily{adapt；inept；apt；aptitude}
  \wordsense{a. 熟练的，娴熟的，精通的}
  \wordphrase{be adept（熟练的，娴熟的，精通的）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "be adept".（常见搭配：熟练的，娴熟的，精通的。）}
  \wordsense{n. 能手；内行}
  \wordphrase{adept at archery nearly}
  \wordexample{He's quite adept at archery, nearly always hitting the target dead on.（他很擅长射箭，几乎每次都能射中靶心。）}
  \wordexample{adept at archery nearly}
  \wordexample{be adept（熟练的，娴熟的，精通的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{inept}{i'nept}
  \wordbreakdown{不适当的 in (= not) + ept<apt (= fit)}
  \wordsense{a. 不适当的；不熟练的；愚笨的；无能的}
  \wordfamily{ineptitude 不熟练，不适宜；不当的言行}
  \wordfamily{ineptly 不适当地}
  \wordphrase{inept remark I never}
  \wordexample{I apologize for my inept remark; I never meant to offend you.（我为自己不恰当的言语道歉，我绝不是有意冒犯您。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{apt}{æpt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 APT[EPT, ATT]}
  \wordsense{a. 合适的，恰当的；聪明的；易于……的，有……倾向的}
  \wordfamily{aptly（适当地；巧妙地）}
  \wordfamily{aptness（适合性；倾向；才能）}
  \wordfamily{be apt（合适的，恰当的）}
  \wordphrase{be apt（合适的，恰当的）}
  \wordexample{be apt（合适的，恰当的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{aptitude}{'æptitju:d}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 APT[EPT, ATT]}
  \wordfamily{adapt（oneself + to + sth 使适应）；adept（熟练的）；inept（不适当的）}
  \wordsense{n. 恰当，适宜；天资；倾向}
  \wordphrase{aptitude test（能力倾向测验）}
  \wordphrase{Scholastic Aptitude Test（学习能力倾向测试，即}
  \wordexample{aptitude test（能力倾向测验）}
  \wordexample{Scholastic Aptitude Test（学习能力倾向测试，即}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{attitude}{'ætitju:d}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 APT[EPT, ATT]}
  \wordfamily{aptitude；attitudinize；apt；contraption；adapt（oneself + to + sth 使适应）}
  \wordsense{n. 态度；姿势 *att<apt (= fit)}
  \wordphrase{strike an attitude（装腔作势）}
  \wordexample{strike an attitude（装腔作势）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{attitudinize}{ˌæti'tju:dinaiz}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 APT[EPT, ATT]}
  \wordfamily{attitude；contraption；aptitude；periapt；adapt（oneself + to + sth 使适应）}
  \wordsense{v. 装腔作势 *att<apt (= fit)}
  \wordphrase{to attitudinize（装腔作势）}
  \wordexample{to attitudinize（装腔作势）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{contraption}{kən'træpʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 APT[EPT, ATT]}
  \wordfamily{attitudinize；periapt；attitude；aptitude；adapt（oneself + to + sth 使适应）}
  \wordsense{n. 奇妙的装置；新发明物 *contr<contra (= against)}
  \wordphrase{a contraption（奇妙的装置）}
  \wordexample{a contraption（奇妙的装置）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{periapt}{'periæpt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 APT[EPT, ATT]}
  \wordfamily{contraption；attitudinize；attitude；aptitude；adapt（oneself + to + sth 使适应）}
  \wordsense{n. 护身符 *peri (= around)}
  \wordphrase{a periapt（护身符）}
  \wordexample{a periapt（护身符）}
\end{word}



\chapter{LOT}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自盎格鲁-撒克逊语的 lot 意为 lot。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 sorc, sort 和来自希腊语的 cler。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{allot}{ə'lɒt}
  \wordbreakdown{给某物分份 al<ad (= to) + lot (= lot)}
  \wordfamily{allotment（分摊，分配）；lot（抽签）；lottery（抽签）}
  \wordsense{vt. sth + to + sb 分摊，分配}
  \wordphrase{The tour guide only}
  \wordexample{The tour guide only allotted us 20 minutes to see the pyramids.（导游只留给我们 20 分钟的时间去参观金字塔。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{lot}{lɒt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LOT}
  \wordsense{n. 抽签；份儿；运气，命运；（拍卖的）物品；某一类人；（特定用处的）一块土地；《美》电影摄制场；大量}
  \wordfamily{draw lots（抽签）}
  \wordfamily{bad lot（坏蛋）}
  \wordfamily{parking lot（停车场）}
  \wordfamily{building lot（建筑用地）}
  \wordphrase{cast in one's lot with（与……生死与共）}
  \wordphrase{Lot 46, six chairs（物品 46，6 把椅子）}
  \wordexample{cast in one's lot with（与……生死与共）}
  \wordexample{Lot 46, six chairs（物品 46，6 把椅子）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{lottery}{'lɒtəri}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LOT}
  \wordfamily{lot；lotto；allot（sth + to + sb 分摊）}
  \wordsense{n. 抽签，彩票；运气}
  \wordphrase{lottery wheel（摇奖机）}
  \wordphrase{lottery ticket（彩票）}
  \wordexample{lottery wheel（摇奖机）}
  \wordexample{lottery ticket（彩票）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{lotto}{'lɒtou}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LOT}
  \wordfamily{lottery；lot；allot（sth + to + sb 分摊）}
  \wordsense{n. 一种对号码的赌博游戏}
  \wordphrase{na, no, n 等）}
  \wordexample{na, no, n 等）}
  \wordexample{a lotto（一种对号码的赌博游戏）}
\end{word}



\chapter{NE[NA, NO, N]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 ne 意为 not，表示否定。其变形有 na, no, n 等。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{annul}{ə'nʌl}
  \wordbreakdown{使接近没有 an<ad (= to, near) + n<ne (= not) + ul (= any)}
  \wordfamily{annulment（取消，废止）；nefarious（恶毒的）；nescience（无知 *）}
  \wordsense{vt. 宣布……无效；废止，取消}
  \wordphrase{annul your employment contract}
  \wordexample{Failure to arrive to work on time will annul your employment contract.（如果你不能准时上班，将被解除聘用合同。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{nefarious}{ni'feəriəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 NE[NA, NO, N]}
  \wordfamily{nescience；neuter；neutral；neutrality；annul（宣布……无效）}
  \wordsense{a. 恶毒的，邪恶的 *fa (= say)}
  \wordphrase{be nefarious（恶毒的，邪恶的）}
  \wordexample{be nefarious（恶毒的，邪恶的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{nescience}{'neʃəns}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 NE[NA, NO, N]}
  \wordfamily{nescient（无知的）；annul（宣布……无效）；nefarious（恶毒的）}
  \wordsense{n. 无知 *sci (= know)}
  \wordphrase{a nescience（无知）}
  \wordexample{a nescience（无知）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{neuter}{'nju:tə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 NE[NA, NO, N]}
  \wordfamily{nescient（无知的）；annul（宣布……无效）；nefarious（恶毒的）}
  \wordsense{a. 中性的；（植物）无性的；生殖器发育不全的；不生育的}
  \wordphrase{neutered tomcat（阉割过的雄猫）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "neutered tomcat".（常见搭配：阉割过的雄猫。）}
  \wordsense{n. 【语】中性名词；中性；生殖器发育不全的动物；阉割过的动物}
  \wordphrase{neutered tomcat（阉割过的雄猫）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "neutered tomcat".（常见搭配：阉割过的雄猫。）}
  \wordsense{v. 阉割 *uter (= whether of the two)}
  \wordphrase{be neuter（中性的）}
  \wordexample{be neuter（中性的）}
  \wordexample{a neuter（中性名词）}
  \wordexample{to neuter（阉割）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{neutral}{'nju:trəl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 NE[NA, NO, N]}
  \wordfamily{annul（宣布……无效）；nefarious（恶毒的）；nescience（无知 *）}
  \wordsense{a. 中立的；中立国的；中性的；模糊的；略带灰色的；（齿轮）空档的}
  \wordphrase{neutered tomcat（阉割过的雄猫）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "neutered tomcat".（常见搭配：阉割过的雄猫。）}
  \wordsense{n. 中立者；中立国；（齿轮）空档 *utr<uter (= whether of the two)}
  \wordphrase{neutered tomcat（阉割过的雄猫）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "neutered tomcat".（常见搭配：阉割过的雄猫。）}
  \wordsense{v. 阉割 *uter (= whether of the two)}
  \wordphrase{neutral nations（中立国）}
  \wordphrase{neutral tints（暗淡色；灰色）}
  \wordexample{neutral nations（中立国）}
  \wordexample{neutral tints（暗淡色；灰色）}
  \wordexample{be neutral（中立的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{neutrality}{nju:'træləti}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 NE[NA, NO, N]}
  \wordfamily{annul（宣布……无效）；nefarious（恶毒的）；nescience（无知 *）}
  \wordsense{n. 中立状态 *utr<uter (= whether of the two)}
  \wordphrase{armed neutrality（武装中立）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "armed neutrality".（常见搭配：武装中立。）}
  \wordsense{a. 中立的；中立国的；中性的；模糊的；略带灰色的；（齿轮）空档的}
  \wordphrase{a neutrality（中立状态）}
  \wordexample{a neutrality（中立状态）}
  \wordexample{be neutrality（中立的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{neutralize}{'nju:trəlaiz}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 NE[NA, NO, N]}
  \wordfamily{neutral（tints（暗淡色）；annul（宣布……无效）；nefarious（恶毒的）}
  \wordsense{v. 使中立；使无效；使中和 *utr<uter (= whether of the two)}
  \wordphrase{neutralize a poison（中和毒素）}
  \wordexample{neutralize a poison（中和毒素）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{neutron}{'nju:trɒn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 NE[NA, NO, N]}
  \wordfamily{annul（宣布……无效）；nefarious（恶毒的）；nescience（无知 *）}
  \wordsense{n. 中子}
  \wordphrase{neutralize a poison（中和毒素）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "neutralize a poison".（常见搭配：中和毒素。）}
  \wordsense{v. 使中立；使无效；使中和 *utr<uter (= whether of the two)}
  \wordphrase{armed neutrality（武装中立）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "armed neutrality".（常见搭配：武装中立。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{naught / nought}{nɔ:t}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 NE[NA, NO, N]}
  \wordfamily{annul（宣布……无效）；nefarious（恶毒的）；nescience（无知 *）}
  \wordsense{n. 零；无 *ught (= thing), na, no<ne (= not)}
  \wordphrase{come to nought / naught（归于失败）}
  \wordphrase{point nought / naught one（零点零一（0.01））}
  \wordexample{come to nought / naught（归于失败）}
  \wordexample{point nought / naught one（零点零一（0.01））}
\end{word}



\chapter{PAC, PEAS}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 pac, peas 意为 peace。反义词根有意为 war 的来自拉丁语的 bell, mar 和来自法语的 guerr。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{appease}{ə'pi:z}
  \wordbreakdown{给某人以和平、平静、和解 ap<ad (= to) + peas (= peace)}
  \wordsense{vt. 安慰，使镇定；满足；让步}
  \wordfamily{appeasement 安慰；姑息；让步}
  \wordfamily{cf. unappeasable 难以满足的；无法平息的}
  \wordphrase{appease hungry children before}
  \wordexample{A cookie at 5:00 will appease hungry children before dinnertime.（晚饭之前孩子们会很饿，5 点钟的时候给他们一块饼干，可以哄住他们。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{pacify}{'pæsifai}
  \wordbreakdown{使变得平和 pac (= peace)}
  \wordfamily{pacification（和解）；appease（安慰）；pacific（和平的）}
  \wordsense{vt. 使平息；安抚；平定}
  \wordphrase{pacified the frightened children}
  \wordexample{The teacher pacified the frightened children with soothing words.（老师用抚慰的话语使受惊吓的孩子平静。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{pacific}{pə'sifik}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PAC, PEAS}
  \wordfamily{pacification；pacifism；appease（安慰）}
  \wordsense{a. 和平的；和解的；（大海等）平静的；温和的；太平的；太平洋的 *fic (= make)}
  \wordphrase{pacific era（和平年代）}
  \wordphrase{the Pacific Ocean（太平洋）}
  \wordphrase{the Pacific States（（美国）太平洋沿岸的州（加利福尼亚州、俄勒冈州、华盛顿州等））}
  \wordexample{pacific era（和平年代）}
  \wordexample{the Pacific Ocean（太平洋）}
  \wordexample{the Pacific States（（美国）太平洋沿岸的州（加利福尼亚州、俄勒冈州、华盛顿州等））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{pacification}{ˌpæsifi'keiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PAC, PEAS}
  \wordfamily{pacific；pacifism；appease（安慰）}
  \wordsense{n. 和解；平定 *fic (= make)}
  \wordphrase{a pacification（和解）}
  \wordexample{a pacification（和解）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{pacifism}{'pæsifizəm}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PAC, PEAS}
  \wordfamily{pacifist（和平主义者）；appease（安慰）；pacify（使平息）}
  \wordsense{n. 和平主义}
  \wordphrase{loc, pos / post / pon / pound, stal 等，还有来自希腊语的}
  \wordphrase{thes / thet / them, top。}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of pacifism.（此处展示 pacifism 的常见用法。）}
\end{word}



\chapter{SERT}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 sert 意为 to put, to place。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 loc, pos / post / pon / pound, stal 等，还有来自希腊语的 thes / thet / them, top。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{assert}{ə'sɜ:t}
  \wordbreakdown{强烈地提出、表达自己的主张 as<ad (= to, intensive) + sert (= put)}
  \wordsense{v. 断言；主张}
  \wordfamily{assertion 断言；主张}
  \wordfamily{assertiveness 自信}
  \wordfamily{assertive 断言的}
  \wordphrase{assert proudly that the}
  \wordexample{We assert proudly that the Chinese people have been most generous.（我们可以自豪地断言，中国人民是非常慷慨的。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{desert}{di'zɜ:t}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SERT}
  \wordfamily{assert（断言）；desertion（抛弃）；dissertation（论文 *dis (= apart, away)）}
  \wordsense{v. 逃跑；抛弃}
  \wordphrase{to desert（逃跑）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "to desert".（常见搭配：逃跑。）}
  \wordsense{n. （pl.）应得的赏罚；功过； 沙漠；荒野}
  \wordphrase{to desert（逃跑）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "to desert".（常见搭配：逃跑。）}
  \wordsense{a. 不毛的；无人居住的 *de (= away)}
  \wordphrase{according to one's deserts（根据某人的表现）}
  \wordphrase{the Sahara Desert（撒哈拉沙漠）}
  \wordexample{according to one's deserts（根据某人的表现）}
  \wordexample{the Sahara Desert（撒哈拉沙漠）}
  \wordexample{to desert（逃跑）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{desertion}{di'zɜ:ʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SERT}
  \wordfamily{desert；dissertation；exert；exertion；assert（断言）}
  \wordsense{n. 抛弃，遗弃；逃跑 *de (= away)}
  \wordphrase{a desertion（抛弃，遗弃）}
  \wordexample{a desertion（抛弃，遗弃）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{dissertation}{ˌdisə'teiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SERT}
  \wordfamily{desertion；exert；desert；exertion；assert（断言）}
  \wordsense{n. 论文 *dis (= apart, away)}
  \wordphrase{the dissertation}
  \wordexample{Ph. D. dissertation（博士论文）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{exert}{ig'zɜ:t}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SERT}
  \wordfamily{dissertation；exertion；desertion；insert；assert（断言）}
  \wordsense{v. 发挥，运用；尽（力） *ex (= out), ert<sert (= put)}
  \wordphrase{to exert（发挥，运用）}
  \wordexample{to exert（发挥，运用）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{exertion}{ig'zɜ:ʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SERT}
  \wordfamily{exert；insert；dissertation；insertion；assert（断言）}
  \wordsense{n. 努力；发挥 *ex (= out), ert<sert (= put)}
  \wordphrase{an exertion（努力）}
  \wordexample{an exertion（努力）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{insert}{in'sɜ:t}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SERT}
  \wordfamily{exertion；insertion；exert；dissertation；assert（断言）}
  \wordsense{v. 插入，嵌入 *in (= into)}
  \wordphrase{to insert（插入，嵌入）}
  \wordexample{to insert（插入，嵌入）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{insertion}{in'sɜ:ʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SERT}
  \wordfamily{insert；exertion；exert；dissertation；assert（断言）}
  \wordsense{n. 插入，嵌入；插入物；（报纸等）插入的广告 *in (= into)}
  \wordphrase{an insertion（插入，嵌入）}
  \wordexample{an insertion（插入，嵌入）}
\end{word}



\chapter{CED, CESS [CEED, CEAS]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 ced, cess 意为 to go。其变形为 ceed, ceas。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 grad / gress / gred, it, vad / vas，还有来自希腊语的 bat [bet, bit] 和来自盎格鲁-撒克逊语的 fare。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{access}{'ækses}
  \wordbreakdown{走近 ac<ad (= to, near) + cess (= go)}
  \wordfamily{accessibility（可接近）；accessible（可接近的）；accede（to + sth 同意）}
  \wordsense{n. 进入，接近；病的发作}
  \wordphrase{the access}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of access.（此处展示 access 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 【计】存取；接近}
  \wordphrase{access the building}
  \wordexample{You can only access the building by one entrance on weekends.（周末只能通过一个入口进入那座建筑。）}
  \wordexample{access the building}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{accede}{ək'si:d}
  \wordbreakdown{走近 ac<ad (= to, near) + ced (= go)}
  \wordsense{vi. to + sth 同意；就任；继承；正式加入}
  \wordfamily{accession 继承；就任；接近；加盟}
  \wordphrase{The police negotiator acceded}
  \wordexample{The police negotiator acceded to the kidnappers' demands.（警方谈判人员同意了绑架者的要求。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{concede}{kən'si:d}
  \wordbreakdown{带着某物走 con<com (= with) + ced (= go)}
  \wordfamily{concessive（让步的，让与的）；concession（让步，容忍）；租地营业（权）；access（进入）}
  \wordsense{vt. 承认；让步；给予}
  \wordphrase{concede that you are}
  \wordexample{As much as I hate losing an argument, I have to concede that you are correct.（我虽然很不喜欢在争论中输给别人，但我不得不承认你是对的。）}
  \wordexample{concede that you are}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{incessant}{in'sesnt}
  \wordbreakdown{不能走的 in (= not) + cess (= go)}
  \wordfamily{incessantly（不间断地）；access（进入）；accede（to + sth 同意）}
  \wordsense{a. 无休止的，不停的}
  \wordphrase{incessant complainer my present}
  \wordexample{My ex-wife was an incessant complainer; my present wife isn't.（我的前妻常无休止地抱怨，而我现在的妻子不这样。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{precedent}{'presidənt}
  \wordbreakdown{向前走 pre (= before) + ced (= go)}
  \wordsense{n. 前例，先例；判例}
  \wordfamily{precede 领先；先于……}
  \wordfamily{precedence 领先，优先，居先}
  \wordfamily{preceding 在前的}
  \wordfamily{precedented 有先例的}
  \wordphrase{precedent}
  \wordexample{Some say the government's decision to continue logging sets a dangerous precedent.（有人说政府决定继续伐木开了一个危险的先例。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{predecessor}{'pri:disesə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{领先往下走的人 pre (= before) + de (= down) + cess (= go)}
  \wordsense{n. 前任；祖先；被代替的原有事物}
  \wordfamily{precedent}
  \wordfamily{recede}
  \wordfamily{incessant}
  \wordfamily{intercede}
  \wordphrase{predecessor}
  \wordexample{The new Miss America was crowned by her predecessor.（前任美国小姐给新当选的美国小姐戴上了王冠。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{recede}{ri'si:d}
  \wordbreakdown{向后走 re (= back) + ced (= go)}
  \wordsense{vi. 退，后退}
  \wordfamily{recession 退，后退}
  \wordfamily{recess 假期；休会；休庭；幽深处}
  \wordfamily{recessive 后退的，逆行的}
  \wordfamily{recessional 后退的；《英》议会休会的；（n.）退场时唱的赞美诗}
  \wordphrase{recede within 36 hours}
  \wordexample{The flood waters are expected to recede within 36 hours.（预计暴涨的洪水将在 36 个小时内退去。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{intercede}{ˌintə'si:d}
  \wordbreakdown{走向其间 inter (= between) + ced (= go)}
  \wordsense{vi. in + sth; with + sb + for + sb 调停；仲裁；请愿，求情}
  \wordfamily{intercession 仲裁；调停；请求，请愿}
  \wordphrase{intercede when our son}
  \wordexample{We asked the consulate to intercede when our son was arrested in Panama.（儿子在巴拿马被捕时，我们请求领事馆予以调解。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{successive}{sək'sesiv}
  \wordbreakdown{连在底部、尾部走的 suc<sub (= under) + cess (= go)}
  \wordsense{a. 连续的；继承的}
  \wordfamily{succession 连续；继承（权）}
  \wordfamily{successor 继承人；后继人}
  \wordphrase{successive days}
  \wordexample{I was late to work on three successive days.（我连续三天上班迟到。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{abscess}{'æbses}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CED, CESS [CEED, CEAS]}
  \wordfamily{accessary；accessory；antecedent；cease；access（进入）}
  \wordsense{n. 脓肿，脓疮 *abs<ab (= away)}
  \wordphrase{abscesses on the gums（齿龈脓肿）}
  \wordexample{abscesses on the gums（齿龈脓肿）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{accessary}{ək'sesəri}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CED, CESS [CEED, CEAS]}
  \wordfamily{abscess；accessory；antecedent；cease；access（进入）}
  \wordsense{n. 帮凶，从犯 *ac<ad (= near)}
  \wordphrase{accessary to a crime（某犯罪行为的从犯）}
  \wordexample{accessary to a crime（某犯罪行为的从犯）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{accessory}{ək'sesəri}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CED, CESS [CEED, CEAS]}
  \wordfamily{accessary；antecedent；abscess；cease；access（进入）}
  \wordsense{n. 附属物；服饰搭配物（如钱包、手绢、胸针等）；从犯，帮凶}
  \wordphrase{the accessory}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of accessory.（此处展示 accessory 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{a. 附属的；帮凶的 *ac<ad (= near)}
  \wordphrase{accessories of a bicycle（自行车的附件）}
  \wordphrase{accessories of a woman's dress（女装的搭配物）}
  \wordexample{accessories of a bicycle（自行车的附件）}
  \wordexample{accessories of a woman's dress（女装的搭配物）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{antecedent}{'ænti'si:dənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CED, CESS [CEED, CEAS]}
  \wordfamily{antecedence（先行）；access（进入）；accede（to + sth 同意）}
  \wordsense{a. 在先的，先前的}
  \wordphrase{be antecedent}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of antecedent.（此处展示 antecedent 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 前情；祖先；经历；先行词 *ante (= before)}
  \wordphrase{accessories of a bicycle（自行车的附件）}
  \wordexample{accessories of a woman's dress（女装的搭配物）}
  \wordexample{accessories of a bicycle（自行车的附件）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{cease}{si:s}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CED, CESS [CEED, CEAS]}
  \wordfamily{ceaseless（不断的）；ceasefire（停战）；access（进入）}
  \wordsense{v. 停止，终止 *ceas<cess (= go)}
  \wordphrase{to cease（停止，终止）}
  \wordexample{to cease（停止，终止）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{cede}{si:d}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CED, CESS [CEED, CEAS]}
  \wordsense{v. 转让（权力），割让（土地）}

  \wordfamily{cessation}
  \wordfamily{antecedent}

  \wordphrase{to cede（转让（权力），割让（土地））}

\chapter{PLIC, PLY, PLI, PLE, PLEX, PLO}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 plic, ply, pli, ple, plex, plo 意为 to fold。意为 to fill, full 的词根 ple, plet, pli, ply, plen 等和这些词根形态相似，易于混淆，需要注意。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{accomplice}{ə'kʌmplis}
  \wordbreakdown{一起折得很近 ac<ad (= near) + com (= together) + plic (= fold)}
  \wordfamily{precedented（有先例的）；applicable（可应用的）；explicit（清楚的）}
  \wordsense{n. 共犯，同谋者}
  \wordphrase{the accomplice}
  \wordexample{The jewel thief was arrested with two younger accomplices.（那个偷宝石的小偷和两个年龄更小的共犯一起被逮捕了。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{applicable}{ə'plikəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{折得很近的 ap<ad (= to, near) + plic (= fold)}
  \wordsense{a. 可应用的；适当的}
  \wordfamily{applicant 申请者}
  \wordfamily{applied 适用的；应用的}
  \wordfamily{apply 适用；应用；申请}
  \wordfamily{application 适用；应用；申请（书）；敷用药}
  \wordfamily{applicability 适用性；应用性}
  \wordphrase{applicable construction laws when}
  \wordexample{It's necessary to obey all applicable construction laws when you build a house.（盖房子时需要遵守所有适用的建筑法。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{explicit}{ik'splisit}
  \wordbreakdown{折叠后放在外边的 ex (= out) + plic (= fold)}
  \wordfamily{explicate（阐明）；explication（阐明）；explicable（可说明的）；explicitly（明白地）；accomplice（共犯）}
  \wordsense{a. 清楚的，明白的，明确的；直率的}
  \wordphrase{explicitly 明白地}
  \wordexample{Her boss gave her an explicit warning not to come late again upon penalty of termination.（她的老板明确地警告她，如果再迟到，就要以解雇来惩罚她。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{imply}{im'plai}
  \wordbreakdown{折向里面 im<in (= in) + ply (= fold)}
  \wordsense{vt. 意味着；暗示；包含}
  \wordfamily{implication 牵连；暗示；蕴含}
  \wordfamily{implicit 暗示的；含蓄的}
  \wordfamily{implicitly 含蓄地；暗示地}
  \wordfamily{implicate 牵连}
  \wordphrase{imply that he's rude}
  \wordexample{I didn't mean to imply that he's rude, just that he's often busy.（我的意思不是说他很无礼，只是说他总是很忙。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{replica}{'replikə}
  \wordbreakdown{重新折到那个程度的东西 re (= again) + plic (= fold)}
  \wordsense{n.（尤指出于原作者之手的）复制品}
  \wordfamily{imply}
  \wordfamily{supplicate}
  \wordfamily{explicit}
  \wordfamily{pliable}
  \wordphrase{replica of the Hoover}
  \wordexample{Our school team won the prize for the best replica of the Hoover Dam.（我们学校参赛组制作的胡佛水坝模型最好，所以拿了奖。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{supplicate}{'sʌplikeit}
  \wordbreakdown{向下折叠 sup<sub (= under) + plic (= fold)}
  \wordsense{vt. 恳求，哀求}
  \wordfamily{supplication 恳求，哀求}
  \wordfamily{suppliant 恳求者，哀求者}
  \wordphrase{The immigrant supplicated the}
  \wordexample{The immigrant supplicated the border guard to please let him pass.（那个移民者恳求国境警卫让自己通过。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{pliable}{'plaiəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{易折的 pli (= fold)}
  \wordsense{a. 易弯的；易受影响的；顺从的；易适应的}
  \wordfamily{pliability / pliancy 柔软性；适应性}
  \wordfamily{cf. pliant (= pliable)}
  \wordphrase{pliable because it's so}
  \wordexample{The wood is actually pliable because it's so thin.（这木头很薄，所以实际上很易于弯曲。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{complicate}{'kɒmplikeit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PLIC, PLY, PLI, PLE, PLEX, PLO}
  \wordfamily{complicated；complication；complicity；duplicate；accomplice（共犯）}
  \wordsense{v. 使变复杂，使难对付 *com (= together)}
  \wordphrase{complicate the issue}
  \wordexample{This complicates matters.（这使事情变得复杂。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{complicated}{'kɒmplikeitid}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PLIC, PLY, PLI, PLE, PLEX, PLO}
  \wordfamily{complicate；complication；complicity；duplicate；accomplice（共犯）}
  \wordsense{a. 复杂的 *com (= together)}
  \wordphrase{complicated machine（复杂的机器）}
  \wordexample{complicated machine（复杂的机器）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{complication}{ˌkɒmpli'keiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PLIC, PLY, PLI, PLE, PLEX, PLO}
  \wordfamily{complicated；complicity；complicate；duplicate；accomplice（共犯）}
  \wordsense{n. 复杂；混乱；并发症 *com (= together)}
  \wordphrase{influenza with complications（带有并发症的流行性感冒）}
  \wordexample{influenza with complications（带有并发症的流行性感冒）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{complicity}{kəm'plisəti}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PLIC, PLY, PLI, PLE, PLEX, PLO}
  \wordfamily{complication；duplicate；complicated；duplication；accomplice（共犯）}
  \wordsense{n. 共谋，串通 *com (= together)}
  \wordphrase{a complicity（共谋，串通）}
  \wordexample{a complicity（共谋，串通）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{duplicate}{'dju:plikeit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PLIC, PLY, PLI, PLE, PLEX, PLO}
  \wordfamily{accomplice（共犯）；applicable（可应用的）；explicit（清楚的）}
  \wordsense{v. 复写；拷贝；复制；使加倍}
  \wordphrase{duplicating paper（复写纸）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "duplicating paper".（常见搭配：复写纸。）}
  \wordsense{a. 两重的；一对的；两倍的；完全相同的；复制的}
  \wordphrase{duplicating paper（复写纸）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "duplicating paper".（常见搭配：复写纸。）}
  \wordsense{n. 完全一样的东西；复制品；副本}
  \wordphrase{duplicate keys（复制的钥匙）}
  \wordphrase{duplicate copies（副本）}
  \wordexample{duplicate keys（复制的钥匙）}
  \wordexample{duplicate copies（副本）}
  \wordexample{to duplicate（复写）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{duplication}{ˌdju:pli'keiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PLIC, PLY, PLI, PLE, PLEX, PLO}
  \wordfamily{duplicator（复印机）；accomplice（共犯）；applicable（可应用的）}
  \wordsense{n. 复写；复制；复制品 *du (= two)}
  \wordphrase{a duplication（复写）}
  \wordexample{a duplication（复写）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{duplicity}{dju:'plisəti}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PLIC, PLY, PLI, PLE, PLEX, PLO}
