\chapter{JOURN}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根 journ 来自于拉丁语，意为 day。同义词根为 hemer.
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{adjourn}{ə'dʒə:n}
  \wordbreakdown{改到近日 ad (= to, near) + journ (= day)}
  \wordfamily{adjournment（延期）；journal（日报）；journalism（新闻业）}
  \wordsense{vt. 使（会议等）延期；休（会）；中止}
  \wordphrase{adjourn the issue}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of adjourn.（此处展示 adjourn 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{vi. to + sth 换地方；休会}
  \wordphrase{adjourned their deliberations}
  \wordexample{The jury adjourned their deliberations for lunch at 12:30.（12 点 30 分陪审团暂时中止审议，去用午餐。）}
  \wordexample{adjourned their deliberations}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{journal}{'dʒə:nl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JOURN}
  \wordfamily{adjourn（使（会议等）延期）；journalism（新闻业）；journalist（新闻工作者）}
  \wordsense{n. 日报；杂志；期刊；日志；日记}
  \wordphrase{Wall Street Journal（《华尔街日报》）}
  \wordphrase{Ladies' Home Journal（《妇女家庭杂志》）}
  \wordphrase{American Journal of Political Science（《美国政治学期刊》）}
  \wordexample{Wall Street Journal（《华尔街日报》）}
  \wordexample{Ladies' Home Journal（《妇女家庭杂志》）}
  \wordexample{American Journal of Political Science（《美国政治学期刊》）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{journalism}{'dʒə:nəlizəm}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JOURN}
  \wordfamily{journal；journalist；journalistic；journey；adjourn（使（会议等）延期）}
  \wordsense{n. 新闻业；新闻工作（包括写作、编辑、出版等）；【总称】报章杂志}
  \wordphrase{a journalism（新闻业）}
  \wordexample{a journalism（新闻业）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{journalist}{'dʒə:nəlist}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JOURN}
  \wordsense{n. 新闻工作者；新闻记者；报纸撰稿人}
  \wordfamily{journalism}
  \wordfamily{journalistic}
  \wordfamily{journal}
  \wordfamily{journey}
  \wordfamily{a journalist（新闻工作者）}
  \wordphrase{a journalist（新闻工作者）}
  \wordexample{a journalist（新闻工作者）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{journalistic}{ˌdʒə:nə'listik}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JOURN}
  \wordsense{a. 新闻业的；报刊特有的；新闻工作（者）的}
  \wordfamily{journalist}
  \wordfamily{journey}
  \wordfamily{journalism}
  \wordfamily{journeyman}
  \wordfamily{be journalistic（新闻业的）}
  \wordphrase{be journalistic（新闻业的）}
  \wordexample{be journalistic（新闻业的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{journey}{'dʒə:ni}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JOURN}
  \wordsense{n. 旅行（多指陆上的长途旅行）；旅程}
  \wordfamily{journalistic}
  \wordfamily{journeyman}
  \wordfamily{journalist}
  \wordfamily{sojourn}
  \wordfamily{a journey（旅行（多指陆上的长途旅行））}
  \wordphrase{a journey（旅行（多指陆上的长途旅行））}
  \wordexample{a journey（旅行（多指陆上的长途旅行））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{journeyman}{'dʒə:nimən}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JOURN}
  \wordsense{n.（旧时）学徒期满的工匠；熟练工；雇工}
  \wordfamily{journey}
  \wordfamily{sojourn}
  \wordfamily{journalistic}
  \wordfamily{journalist}
  \wordfamily{a journeyman（学徒期满的工匠）}
  \wordphrase{a journeyman（学徒期满的工匠）}
  \wordexample{a journeyman（学徒期满的工匠）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{sojourn}{'sɒdʒə:n}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JOURN}
  \wordfamily{journeyman；journey；journalistic；journalist；adjourn（使（会议等）延期）}
  \wordsense{n. /}
  \wordphrase{the sojourn}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of sojourn.（此处展示 sojourn 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. 逗留；旅居 *so<sub (= under)}
  \wordphrase{sojourn the issue}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of sojourn.（此处展示 sojourn 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. 逗留）}
  \wordphrase{a sojourn（/v. 逗留）}
  \wordexample{a sojourn（/v. 逗留）}
\end{word}



\chapter{VEN, VENT}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 ven, vent 意为 to come。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{advent}{'ædvent}
  \wordbreakdown{靠近 ad (= to, near) + vent (= come)}
  \wordsense{n. 到来；出现；（A\textasciitilde{}）基督降临}
  \wordfamily{adventitious}
  \wordfamily{convene}
  \wordfamily{covenant}
  \wordfamily{intervene}
  \wordphrase{advent of a new}
  \wordexample{The politician promised that his inauguration was the advent of a new era.（那位政治家承诺，他的就任将是一个新时代的到来。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{adventitious}{ˌædven'tiʃəs}
  \wordbreakdown{过来靠近的 ad (= to, near) + vent (= come)}
  \wordsense{a. 偶然的；外来的}
  \wordfamily{advent}
  \wordfamily{convene}
  \wordfamily{covenant}
  \wordfamily{intervene}
  \wordphrase{adventitious or genetically predetermined}
  \wordexample{It's difficult to know if certain personality traits are adventitious or genetically predetermined.（人们很难得知某种性格特征是偶然形成的还是遗传所决定的。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{convene}{kən'vi:n}
  \wordbreakdown{一起来 con<com (= together) + ven (= come)}
  \wordfamily{convention（会议，大会）；conventional（会议的）；advent（到来）}
  \wordsense{vt. 召集；传唤}
  \wordphrase{convene the issue}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of convene.（此处展示 convene 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{vi. 聚集，集会}
  \wordphrase{The chairman convened a}
  \wordexample{The chairman convened a city council meeting to decide on the controversy.（为了对这一争论作出决断，议长召集了市议会。）}
  \wordexample{The chairman convened a}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{covenant}{'kʌvənənt}
  \wordbreakdown{一起来 co<com (= together) + ven (= come)}
  \wordsense{n.（有法律约束力的）契约；盟约}
  \wordfamily{convene}
  \wordfamily{intervene}
  \wordfamily{adventitious}
  \wordfamily{supervene}
  \wordphrase{covenant}
  \wordexample{On July 4th, 1776, fifty-six men meeting at Philadelphia made an historic covenant.（1776 年 7 月 4 日，56 人会集在费城，签订了历史性的盟约。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{intervene}{ˌintə'vi:n}
  \wordbreakdown{来到其间 inter (= between) + ven (= come)}
  \wordsense{vi. in + sth 介入；干预，干涉；仲裁}
  \wordfamily{intervention 调停，仲裁；干涉，介入}
  \wordphrase{intervene in the disputes}
  \wordexample{I try not to intervene in the disputes among my children.（我尽量不介入孩子之间的争吵。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{supervene}{ˌsu:pə'vi:n}
  \wordbreakdown{从上往下来 super (= over) + ven (= come)}
  \wordsense{v. 意外发生；伴随发生}
  \wordfamily{intervene}
  \wordfamily{adventure}
  \wordfamily{covenant}
  \wordfamily{adventurous}
  \wordphrase{Paul Enders was dying}
  \wordexample{Paul Enders was dying of AIDS, so his fatal automobile accident only supervened the virus' attenuations of his life.（因患有艾滋病，保罗·恩德斯已生命垂危，那场致命的车祸只不过是在艾滋病毒侵蚀他身体后接着发生而已。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{adventure}{əd'ventʃə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VEN, VENT}
  \wordfamily{adventuress（女投机者（指用不正当手段谋求名利的女子））；advent（到来）；adventitious（偶然的）}
  \wordsense{n. 冒险（经历）；投机活动；女冒险者}
  \wordphrase{the adventure}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of adventure.（此处展示 adventure 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. 冒险 *ad (= to)}
  \wordphrase{story of adventure（冒险故事）}
  \wordexample{story of adventure（冒险故事）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{adventurous}{əd'ventʃərəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VEN, VENT}
  \wordfamily{adventure；avenue；circumvent；convenient；advent（到来）}
  \wordsense{a. 冒险的；喜欢冒险的；大胆的；充满危险和刺激的 *ad (= to)}
  \wordphrase{adventurous voyage（惊险的航海）}
  \wordexample{adventurous voyage（惊险的航海）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{avenue}{'ævənju:}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VEN, VENT}
  \wordfamily{advent（到来）；adventitious（偶然的）；convene（召集）}
  \wordsense{n. 大街，大道（avenue 为南北走向，street 为东西走向）；《喻》途径 *a<ad (= to)}
  \wordphrase{avenues to success（通往成功之路）}
  \wordexample{avenues to success（通往成功之路）}
  \wordexample{avenue*** n. 大街，大道（avenue 为南北走向，street 为东西走向）；《喻》途径 *a<ad (= to)}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{circumvent}{ˌsə:kəm'vent}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VEN, VENT}
  \wordfamily{avenue；convenient；adventurous；convenience；advent（到来）}
  \wordsense{v. 规避（法律、规则、困难等）；防止发生 *circum (= around)}
  \wordphrase{to circumvent（规避（法律、规则、困难等））}
  \wordexample{to circumvent（规避（法律、规则、困难等））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{convenient}{kən'vi:njənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VEN, VENT}
  \wordfamily{conveniently（便利地）；advent（到来）；adventitious（偶然的）}
  \wordsense{a. 便利的，方便的；适宜的 *con<com (= together)}
  \wordphrase{be convenient（便利的，方便的）}
  \wordexample{be convenient（便利的，方便的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{convenience}{kən'vi:niəns}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VEN, VENT}
  \wordfamily{convenient；convent；circumvent；contravene；advent（到来）}
  \wordsense{n. 便利，方便；方便的用具（或设备）；实利，好处 *con<com (= together)}
  \wordphrase{as a matter of convenience（为方便起见）}
  \wordphrase{at your earliest convenience（在你方便时尽早）}
  \wordphrase{marriage of convenience（基于利害关系的婚姻，权宜婚姻）}
  \wordexample{as a matter of convenience（为方便起见）}
  \wordexample{at your earliest convenience（在你方便时尽早）}
  \wordexample{marriage of convenience（基于利害关系的婚姻，权宜婚姻）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{convent}{'kɒnvənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VEN, VENT}
  \wordfamily{convenience；contravene；convenient；contravention；advent（到来）}
  \wordsense{n. 女修道会；女修道院；女隐修会 *con<com (= together)}
  \wordphrase{convent school（女隐修会学校）}
  \wordexample{go into / enter a convent（当修女）}
  \wordexample{convent school（女隐修会学校）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{contravene}{ˌkɒntrə'vi:n}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VEN, VENT}
  \wordfamily{convent；contravention；convenience；event；advent（到来）}
  \wordsense{v. 违反（法律、习惯等）；反驳（陈述、主张等）；与……相抵触，不一致 *contra (= against, opposite)}
  \wordphrase{to contravene（违反（法律、习惯等））}
  \wordexample{to contravene（违反（法律、习惯等））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{contravention}{ˌkɒntrə'venʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VEN, VENT}
  \wordfamily{contravene；event；convent；eventual；advent（到来）}
  \wordsense{n. （法律等的）违反，违背 *contra (= against, opposite)}
  \wordphrase{a contravention（违反，违背）}
  \wordexample{a contravention（违反，违背）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{event}{i'vent}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VEN, VENT}
  \wordfamily{contravention；eventual；contravene；eventful；advent（到来）}
  \wordsense{n. （重要）事件；后果，结果；情况；（体育运动的）比赛项目 *e<ex (= out)}
  \wordphrase{events cast their shadows}
  \wordexample{Coming events cast their shadows before.（山雨欲来风满楼。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{eventual}{i'ventʃuəl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VEN, VENT}
  \wordfamily{eventually（最后，终于）；advent（到来）；adventitious（偶然的）}
  \wordsense{a. 最终的；结果的 *e<ex (= out)}
  \wordphrase{be eventual}
  \wordexample{his foolish behavior and eventual failure（他愚蠢的行为及最终的失败）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{eventful}{i'ventfl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VEN, VENT}
  \wordsense{a. 变故多的}
  \wordfamily{eventual}
  \wordfamily{invent}
  \wordfamily{event}
  \wordfamily{invention}
  \wordphrase{eventful life}
  \wordexample{He has had an eventful life.（他的一生中有很多大事。）}
  \wordexample{The past year has been eventful.（去年是多事的一年。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{invent}{in'vent}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VEN, VENT}
  \wordfamily{advent（到来）；adventitious（偶然的）；convene（召集）}
  \wordsense{v. 发明，创造；捏造（谎言等），虚构 *in (= on)}
  \wordphrase{invent the issue}
  \wordexample{invent an excuse for being late（编造迟到的借口）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{invention}{in'venʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VEN, VENT}
  \wordfamily{invent；inventive；eventful；inventory；advent（到来）}
  \wordsense{n. 发明；创造；发明物；编造的故事；虚构；捏造 *in (= on)}
  \wordphrase{numerous inventions of Edison（爱迪生的众多发明）}
  \wordexample{newspapers that are full of inventions（充斥着虚假报道的报纸）}
  \wordexample{numerous inventions of Edison（爱迪生的众多发明）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{inventive}{in'ventiv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VEN, VENT}
  \wordfamily{invention；inventory；invent；parvenu；advent（到来）}
  \wordsense{a. 有发明才能的；善于创造的，有创造力的 *in (= on)}
  \wordphrase{inventive mind（富有创意的头脑）}
  \wordphrase{inventive powers（发明创造的能力）}
  \wordexample{inventive mind（富有创意的头脑）}
  \wordexample{inventive powers（发明创造的能力）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{inventory}{'invəntri}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VEN, VENT}
  \wordfamily{inventive；parvenu；invention；prevent；advent（到来）}
  \wordsense{n. 详细目录；（财产）目录；存货（总价）；存货盘存（报表）}
  \wordphrase{an inventory（详细目录）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "an inventory".（常见搭配：详细目录。）}
  \wordsense{v. 编制目录；盘存 *in (= on)}
  \wordphrase{an inventory（详细目录）}
  \wordexample{an inventory（详细目录）}
  \wordexample{to inventory（编制目录）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{parvenu}{'pa:vənju:}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VEN, VENT}
  \wordfamily{inventory；prevent；inventive；prevention；advent（到来）}
  \wordsense{n. 新贵；暴发户 *par<per (= completely)}
  \wordphrase{a parvenu（新贵）}
  \wordexample{a parvenu（新贵）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{prevent}{pri'vent}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VEN, VENT}
  \wordfamily{advent（到来）；adventitious（偶然的）；convene（召集）}
  \wordsense{v. 阻止，阻碍；预防 *pre (= before)}
  \wordphrase{to prevent（阻止，阻碍）}
  \wordexample{to prevent（阻止，阻碍）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{prevention}{pri'venʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VEN, VENT}
  \wordfamily{advent（到来）；adventitious（偶然的）；convene（召集）}
  \wordsense{n. 防止，阻碍；预防 *pre (= before)}
  \wordphrase{prevention of cruelty to animals（防止虐待动物）}
  \wordexample{Prevention is better than cure.（预防胜于治疗。）}
  \wordexample{prevention of cruelty to animals（防止虐待动物）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{preventive}{pri'ventiv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VEN, VENT}
  \wordfamily{preventative（预防药）；advent（到来）；adventitious（偶然的）}
  \wordsense{a. 阻止的；预防的 *pre (= before)}
  \wordphrase{preventive medicine / measures（预防药 / 预防措施）}
  \wordphrase{preventive detention / custody（【律】预防性拘留）}
  \wordphrase{preventive officer（《英》缉私官）}
  \wordexample{preventive medicine / measures（预防药 / 预防措施）}
  \wordexample{preventive detention / custody（【律】预防性拘留）}
  \wordexample{preventive officer（《英》缉私官）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{provenance}{'prɒvənəns}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VEN, VENT}
  \wordfamily{preventive；revenue；prevention；souvenir；advent（到来）}
  \wordsense{n. 起源，出处；（艺术品、手稿等的）历史，由来和发展 *pro (= forth)}
  \wordphrase{antique furniture of doubtful provenance（来历可疑的古旧家具（如不知是不是古董））}
  \wordexample{antique furniture of doubtful provenance（来历可疑的古旧家具（如不知是不是古董））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{revenue}{'revənju:}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VEN, VENT}
  \wordfamily{provenance；souvenir；preventive；venue；advent（到来）}
  \wordsense{n. 收入；（国家的）岁入，税收；（pl.）总收入 *re (= again, back)}
  \wordphrase{revenue and expenditure（收支）}
  \wordphrase{defraud the revenue（逃税）}
  \wordphrase{Internal Revenue Service（美国国内收入署（简称：IRS））}
  \wordexample{revenue and expenditure（收支）}
  \wordexample{defraud the revenue（逃税）}
  \wordexample{Internal Revenue Service（美国国内收入署（简称：IRS））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{souvenir}{ˌsu:və'niə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VEN, VENT}
  \wordfamily{revenue；venue；provenance；venture；advent（到来）}
  \wordsense{n. 纪念品 *sou<sub (= in place of)}
  \wordphrase{souvenir sheet（（集邮用语）小型张）}
  \wordexample{souvenir sheet（（集邮用语）小型张）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{venue}{'venju:}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VEN, VENT}
  \wordsense{n.（比赛、会议等的）地点；审判地（召集审判员进行审判的地点）}
  \wordfamily{souvenir}
  \wordfamily{venture}
  \wordfamily{revenue}
  \wordfamily{ventilate}
  \wordphrase{change the venue（（为保证审判的公平而）更换审判地）}
  \wordexample{Seoul was the venue of the 1988 Olympic Games.（首尔是 1988 年奥运会的主办地。）}
  \wordexample{change the venue（（为保证审判的公平而）更换审判地）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{venture}{'ventʃə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VEN, VENT}
  \wordfamily{advent（到来）；adventitious（偶然的）；convene（召集）}
  \wordsense{v. 冒险；斗胆提出（或说出）}
  \wordphrase{venture the issue}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of venture.（此处展示 venture 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 投机；（冒险）事业}
  \wordphrase{venture capital（冒险投入资本，风险资本）}
  \wordexample{Nothing venture, nothing have / gain / win.（不入虎穴，焉得虎子。）}
  \wordexample{venture capital（冒险投入资本，风险资本）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{ventilate}{'ventileit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VEN, VENT}
  \wordfamily{ventilator（换气 / 通风装置）；advent（到来）；adventitious（偶然的）}
  \wordsense{v. （给房间等）通风，换气；引起讨论}
  \wordphrase{venture capital（冒险投入资本，风险资本）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "venture capital".（常见搭配：冒险投入资本，风险资本。）}
  \wordsense{n. 投机；（冒险）事业}
  \wordphrase{ventilate a room（给房间通风）}
  \wordexample{ventilate a room（给房间通风）}
  \wordexample{The matter should be freely ventilated.（这个问题需要进行自由讨论。）}
  \wordexample{Nothing venture, nothing have / gain / win.（不入虎穴，焉得虎子。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{ventilation}{ˌventi'leiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VEN, VENT}
  \wordfamily{advent（到来）；adventitious（偶然的）；convene（召集）}
  \wordsense{n. 通风，换气}
  \wordphrase{the ventilation}
  \wordexample{the ventilation shaft of a coal mine（煤矿的通风井）}
\end{word}



\chapter{GRAV [GRIEV]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 grav 意为 heavy。其变形为 griev。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{aggravate}{'ægrəveit}
  \wordbreakdown{使更沉重 ag<ad (= to, add) + grav (= heavy)}
  \wordsense{vt. 使恶化；使恼火}
  \wordfamily{aggravation 恶化；恼火}
  \wordfamily{aggravating 恶化的}
  \wordphrase{aggravate my arthritis pain}
  \wordexample{Cold weather always seems to aggravate my arthritis pain.（好像冷天总会使我的关节炎疼痛加重。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{aggrieve}{ə'gri:v}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 GRAV [GRIEV]}
  \wordfamily{grave；gravitate；gravitation；gravity；aggravate（使恶化）}
  \wordsense{v. 【常用被动语态】使委屈；使悲痛 *ag<ad (= add), griev<grav (= heavy)}
  \wordphrase{to aggrieve（使委屈）}
  \wordexample{to aggrieve（使委屈）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{grave}{greiv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 GRAV [GRIEV]}
  \wordsense{n. 墓穴，坟墓}
  \wordfamily{gravedigger（掘墓人）}
  \wordfamily{gravestone（墓碑，墓石）}
  \wordfamily{graveyard（墓地）}
  \wordphrase{have one foot in the grave（离死期不远）}
  \wordexample{have one foot in the grave（离死期不远）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{gravitate}{'græviteit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 GRAV [GRIEV]}
  \wordsense{v. 受引力作用而运动；受吸引}
  \wordfamily{grave}
  \wordfamily{gravitation}
  \wordfamily{aggrieve}
  \wordfamily{gravity}
  \wordfamily{to gravitate（受引力作用而运动）}
  \wordphrase{to gravitate（受引力作用而运动）}
  \wordexample{to gravitate（受引力作用而运动）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{gravitation}{ˌgrævi'teiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 GRAV [GRIEV]}
  \wordsense{n. 引力；重力}
  \wordfamily{gravitate}
  \wordfamily{gravity}
  \wordfamily{grave}
  \wordfamily{grievance}
  \wordfamily{a gravitation（引力）}
  \wordphrase{a gravitation（引力）}
  \wordexample{a gravitation（引力）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{gravity}{'grævəti}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 GRAV [GRIEV]}
  \wordfamily{gravitation；grievance；gravitate；grieve；aggravate（使恶化）}
  \wordsense{n. 引力；重力；重量；严重性}
  \wordphrase{a gravity（引力）}
  \wordexample{a gravity（引力）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{grievance}{'gri:vəns}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 GRAV [GRIEV]}
  \wordfamily{gravity；grieve；gravitation；grievous；aggravate（使恶化）}
  \wordsense{n. 不满，不平；牢骚，抱怨 *griev<grav (= heavy)}
  \wordphrase{a grievance（不满，不平）}
  \wordexample{a grievance（不满，不平）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{grieve}{gri:v}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 GRAV [GRIEV]}
  \wordfamily{grievance；grievous；gravity；gravitation；aggravate（使恶化）}
  \wordsense{v. 使悲痛 *griev<grav (= heavy)}
  \wordphrase{to grieve（使悲痛）}
  \wordexample{to grieve（使悲痛）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{grievous}{'gri:vəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 GRAV [GRIEV]}
  \wordfamily{grieve；grievance；gravity；gravitation；aggravate（使恶化）}
  \wordsense{a. （令人）悲痛的；严重的 *griev<grav (= heavy)}
  \wordphrase{grievous railway accident（悲惨的铁路事故）}
  \wordphrase{grievous mistake（大错）}
  \wordphrase{grievous pain（极度的痛苦）}
  \wordexample{grievous railway accident（悲惨的铁路事故）}
  \wordexample{grievous mistake（大错）}
  \wordexample{grievous pain（极度的痛苦）}
\end{word}



\chapter{NEX, NECT}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 nex, nect 意为 to bind。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 cinct, lig, string / strict / strain / stress 等和来自希腊语的 bond，此外还有意为 to join 的 junct / jug / join，含义相似。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{annexation}{ˌænek'seiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{绑得很近 an<ad (= to, near) + nex (= bind)}
  \wordfamily{annex（合并）；join（的）；connect（连接）}
  \wordsense{n. 合并；被合并的领土；附加（物）}
  \wordphrase{grievous railway accident（悲惨的铁路事故）}
  \wordphrase{cinct, lig, string / strict / strain / stress 等和来自希腊语的}
  \wordexample{The annexation of Rapa Nui Island by Chile occurred in 1888.（智利吞并复活节岛一事发生在 1888 年。）}
  \wordexample{grievous pain（极度的痛苦）}
  \wordexample{junct / jug / join，含义相似。 annexation** n. 合并；被合并的领土；附加（物）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{connect}{kə'nekt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 NEX, NECT}
  \wordfamily{well-connected（与权贵有关系的）；annexation（合并）；connection（连接）}
  \wordsense{v. 连接；联想；联系；使联姻 *con<com (= with)}
  \wordphrase{to connect（连接）}
  \wordexample{to connect（连接）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{connection}{kə'nekʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 NEX, NECT}
  \wordfamily{connect；connective；disconnect；nexus；annexation（合并）}
  \wordsense{n. 连接；衔接；（人与人之间的）关系；客户 *con<com (= with)}
  \wordphrase{form useful connections（形成有利的关系）}
  \wordphrase{have connections with（与……有关系）}
  \wordexample{form useful connections（形成有利的关系）}
  \wordexample{have connections with（与……有关系）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{connective}{kə'nektiv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 NEX, NECT}
  \wordfamily{connection；disconnect；connect；nexus；annexation（合并）}
  \wordsense{a. 连接的}
  \wordphrase{be connective（连接的）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "be connective".（常见搭配：连接的。）}
  \wordsense{n. 【语】关联词 *con<com (= with)}
  \wordphrase{be connective（连接的）}
  \wordexample{be connective（连接的）}
  \wordexample{a connective（关联词）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{disconnect}{ˌdiskə'nekt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 NEX, NECT}
  \wordfamily{disconnected（（语言、文字等）不连贯的）；annexation（合并）；connect（连接）}
  \wordsense{v. 使分离；断开 *dis (= away), con<com (= with)}
  \wordphrase{to disconnect（使分离）}
  \wordexample{to disconnect（使分离）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{nexus}{'neksəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 NEX, NECT}
  \wordfamily{disconnect；connective；connection；connect；annexation（合并）}
  \wordsense{n. 关联，关系；【语】动词谓语结构}
  \wordphrase{a nexus（关联，关系）}
  \wordexample{a nexus（关联，关系）}
\end{word}



\chapter{NIHIL}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 nihil 意为 nothing。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{annihilate}{ə'naiəleit}
  \wordbreakdown{使接近没有 an<ad (= near) + nihil (= nothing)}
  \wordfamily{annihilation（歼灭）；nihilism（虚无主义）；nihilist（虚无主义者）}
  \wordsense{vt. 歼灭；彻底击溃；取消，废弃，使无效}
  \wordphrase{The soldiers annihilated their}
  \wordexample{The soldiers annihilated their enemy in the attack.（战士们在进攻中歼灭了敌人。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{nihilism}{'naiilizəm}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 NIHIL}
  \wordsense{n. 虚无主义；无政府状态；恐怖行为}
  \wordfamily{nihilist}
  \wordfamily{nihilistic}
  \wordfamily{a nihilism（虚无主义）}
  \wordphrase{a nihilism（虚无主义）}
  \wordexample{a nihilism（虚无主义）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{nihilist}{'naiilist}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 NIHIL}
  \wordsense{n. 虚无主义者；恐怖主义者}
  \wordfamily{nihilism}
  \wordfamily{nihilistic}
  \wordfamily{a nihilist（虚无主义者）}
  \wordphrase{a nihilist（虚无主义者）}
  \wordexample{a nihilist（虚无主义者）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{nihilistic}{ˌnaii'listik}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 NIHIL}
  \wordfamily{nihilist；nihilism；annihilate（歼灭）}
  \wordsense{a. 虚无主义的；恐怖主义的}
  \wordphrase{be nihilistic（虚无主义的）}
  \wordexample{be nihilistic（虚无主义的）}
\end{word}



\chapter{PORTION}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 portion 意为 share。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{apportion}{ə'pɔ:ʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{给某物分份 ap<ad (= to) + portion (= share)}
  \wordfamily{apportionment（分配）；disproportion（不均衡）；disproportionate（不均衡的）}
  \wordsense{vt. 分配；分摊}
  \wordphrase{apportioned the estate}
  \wordexample{The judge apportioned the estate in equal shares to the survivors.（法官将财产分给了幸存者。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{disproportion}{ˌdispra'pɔ:ʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PORTION}
  \wordfamily{disproportionate；proportion；proportional；portion；apportion（分配）}
  \wordsense{n. 不均衡，不均等 *dis (= not), pro (= according to)}
  \wordphrase{disproportion in age（年龄的不均衡）}
  \wordexample{disproportion in age（年龄的不均衡）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{disproportionate}{ˌdispra'pɔ:ʃənət}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PORTION}
  \wordfamily{disproportion；proportion；proportional；portion；apportion（分配）}
  \wordsense{a. 不均衡的，不成比例的 *dis (= not), pro (= according to)}
  \wordphrase{be disproportionate}
  \wordexample{pay that is disproportionate to the work done（与所做工作不成比例的薪水）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{proportion}{pra'pɔ:ʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PORTION}
  \wordfamily{disproportionate；proportional；disproportion；portion；apportion（分配）}
  \wordsense{n. 比例，比率；协调，均衡；部分，份儿；（pl.）面积；容积}
  \wordphrase{the proportion}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of proportion.（此处展示 proportion 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. 使均衡；使协调；使成比例 *pro (= according to)}
  \wordphrase{room of good proportions（布置得很协调的房间）}
  \wordphrase{a ship of majestic proportions（大容积轮船）}
  \wordphrase{direct / inverse proportion（正 / 反比例）}
  \wordexample{proportion of the expenditure to the income（支出占收入的比例）}
  \wordexample{large proportion of the earth's surface（地球表面的一大部分）}
  \wordexample{room of good proportions（布置得很协调的房间）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{proportional}{pra'pɔ:ʃənl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PORTION}
  \wordfamily{apportion（分配）；disproportion（不均衡）；disproportionate（不均衡的）}
  \wordsense{a. 成比例的；均衡的，协调的}
  \wordphrase{proportional representation（比例代表制）}
  \wordexample{proportional representation（比例代表制）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{portion}{'pɔ:ʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PORTION}
  \wordfamily{apportion（分配）；disproportion（不均衡）；disproportionate（不均衡的）}
  \wordsense{n. 一块；部分；份儿；（食物的）一人份；命运}
  \wordphrase{the portion}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of portion.（此处展示 portion 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. 划分，分配，分份}
  \wordphrase{a portion of land（一块土地）}
  \wordphrase{a portion of roast duck（一人份的烤鸭）}
  \wordphrase{prais, pris, pric, priz。}
  \wordexample{a portion of land（一块土地）}
  \wordexample{a portion of roast duck（一人份的烤鸭）}
\end{word}



\chapter{PRECI[PRAIS, PRIS, PRIC, PRIZ]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 preci 意为 price。其变形为 prais, pris, pric, priz。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{appreciable}{ə'pri:ʃəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{可以给某物定价的 ap<ad (= to) + preci (= price)}
  \wordsense{a. 可感知的；可观的；可估计的}
  \wordfamily{appreciation 感谢；评价；欣赏；重视}
  \wordfamily{appreciative 欣赏的；感激的}
  \wordfamily{appreciably 相当地；明显地；可察觉地}
  \wordfamily{appreciate 感谢；正确评价；欣赏；（充分）意识到}
  \wordphrase{appreciable amount}
  \wordexample{My uncle earned an appreciable amount of money in the stock market.（我叔叔在股市赚了一大笔钱。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{depreciation}{diˌpri:ʃi'eiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{价格向下、降低 de (= down) + preci (= price)}
  \wordsense{n. 降价，贬值；轻视；折旧}
  \wordfamily{depreciate 降价；轻视，蔑视}
  \wordfamily{depreciatory 降价的；轻视的}
  \wordphrase{depreciation}
  \wordexample{Capital equipment taxes are calculated after factoring in depreciation.（资本设备税的计算要先考虑折旧因素。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{precious}{'preʃəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PRECI[PRAIS, PRIS, PRIC, PRIZ]}
  \wordfamily{appraise；appraisal；praise；price；appreciable（可感知的）}
  \wordsense{a. 贵重的，珍贵的；《口》十足的；可爱的}
  \wordphrase{be precious}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of precious.（此处展示 precious 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{ad. 非常，很}
  \wordphrase{precious metal（贵金属）}
  \wordphrase{precious stone（宝石）}
  \wordphrase{precious fool（十足的傻瓜）}
  \wordexample{precious metal（贵金属）}
  \wordexample{precious stone（宝石）}
  \wordexample{precious fool（十足的傻瓜）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{appraise}{ə'preiz}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PRECI[PRAIS, PRIS, PRIC, PRIZ]}
  \wordfamily{appreciable（可感知的）；depreciation（降价）；precious（贵重的）}
  \wordsense{v. 评价；估价 *ap<ad (= to), prais<preci (= price)}
  \wordphrase{precious metal（贵金属）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "precious metal".（常见搭配：贵金属。）}
  \wordsense{ad. 非常，很}
  \wordphrase{appraise** v 评价；估价 *ap<ad}
  \wordexample{appraise** v 评价；估价 *ap<ad}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{appraisal}{ə'preizəl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PRECI[PRAIS, PRIS, PRIC, PRIZ]}
  \wordfamily{appreciable（可感知的）；depreciation（降价）；precious（贵重的）}
  \wordsense{n. 评价；估价 *ap<ad (= to), prais<preci (= price)}
  \wordphrase{an appraisal（评价）}
  \wordexample{an appraisal（评价）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{praise}{preiz}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PRECI[PRAIS, PRIS, PRIC, PRIZ]}
  \wordfamily{praiseworthy（值得称赞的，可嘉的）；appreciable（可感知的）；depreciation（降价）}
  \wordsense{v. /}
  \wordphrase{praise the issue}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of praise.（此处展示 praise 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 称赞，赞美 *prais<preci (= price)}
  \wordphrase{win high praise（赢得高度赞扬）}
  \wordphrase{in praise of（赞美……）}
  \wordexample{win high praise（赢得高度赞扬）}
  \wordexample{in praise of（赞美……）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{price}{prais}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PRECI[PRAIS, PRIS, PRIC, PRIZ]}
  \wordfamily{pricy（昂贵的）；appreciable（可感知的）；depreciation（降价）}
  \wordsense{v. 给……定价；问……的价格}
  \wordphrase{market price（市价）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "market price".（常见搭配：市价。）}
  \wordsense{n. 价格；代价；赏金}
  \wordphrase{price cartel（价格协定）}
  \wordphrase{price index（物价指数）}
  \wordphrase{price tag（贴在商品上的价格标签）}
  \wordexample{put a price on one's head（悬赏捉拿或处死某人）}
  \wordexample{price cartel（价格协定）}
  \wordexample{price index（物价指数）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{priceless}{'praisləs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PRECI[PRAIS, PRIS, PRIC, PRIZ]}
  \wordfamily{price；prize；praise；appraisal；appreciable（可感知的）}
  \wordsense{a. 贵重的，稀世珍宝的；《口》极荒唐的；极有趣的 *pric<preci (= price)}
  \wordphrase{priceless paintings（非常贵重的画）}
  \wordexample{priceless paintings（非常贵重的画）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{prize}{praiz}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PRECI[PRAIS, PRIS, PRIC, PRIZ]}
  \wordfamily{prizewinning（获奖的，得奖的）；appreciable（可感知的）；depreciation（降价）}
  \wordsense{n. 奖赏；奖品；赏金；捕获物，战利品；（竞争等的）目标}
  \wordphrase{consolation prizes（安慰奖）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "consolation prizes".（常见搭配：安慰奖。）}
  \wordsense{v. 评价；重视}
  \wordphrase{consolation prizes（安慰奖）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "consolation prizes".（常见搭配：安慰奖。）}
  \wordsense{a. 该得奖的；了不起的；得奖的；作为奖品的 *priz<preci (= price)}
  \wordphrase{prize for good conduct（善行奖）}
  \wordphrase{prize scholarship（成绩优良奖学金）}
  \wordphrase{prize winner（获奖者）}
  \wordexample{prize for good conduct（善行奖）}
  \wordexample{prize scholarship（成绩优良奖学金）}
  \wordexample{prize winner（获奖者）}
\end{word}



\chapter{SPIR}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 spir 意为 to breathe。此外，它还有 a coil 的意思。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{aspire}{ə'spaiə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{继续、不停地呼吸 a<ad (= to, intensive) + spir (= breathe)}
  \wordfamily{aspiration（热望）；aspirant（有志向的人）；aspiring（有志气的，有抱负的）；prizewinning（获奖的，得奖的）}
  \wordsense{vi. to + sth / do 向往；怀有大志}
  \wordphrase{prize for good conduct（善行奖）}
  \wordexample{A century ago, few little girls aspired to be doctors.（一个世纪以前，几乎没有女孩想当医生。）}
  \wordexample{a coil 的意思。 aspire*** vi. to + sth / do 向往；怀有大志}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{transpire}{træns'paiə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{通过某物呼吸或小声说话 tran<trans (= through) + spir (= breathe)}
  \wordfamily{transpiration（散发，蒸发）；aspire（to + sth / do 向往）；conspire（共谋）}
  \wordsense{vi. （秘密等）被发现，被知道}
  \wordphrase{transpire in practice}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of transpire.（此处展示 transpire 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{vt. （使）散发（水分等），使排出}
  \wordphrase{After an investigation it}
  \wordexample{After an investigation, it transpired that her own son-in-law had taken the watch.（调查表明，是她的女婿拿走了手表。）}
  \wordexample{After an investigation it}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{conspire}{kən'spaiə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPIR}
  \wordfamily{conspirator；conspiracy；expire；expiration；aspire（to + sth / do 向往）}
  \wordsense{v. 共谋；协力，共同促成 *con<com (= together)}
  \wordphrase{to conspire（共谋）}
  \wordexample{to conspire（共谋）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{conspirator}{kən'spirətə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPIR}
  \wordfamily{aspire（to + sth / do 向往）；transpire（（秘密等）被发现）；conspire（共谋）}
  \wordsense{n. 同谋者；阴谋家 *con<com (= together)}
  \wordphrase{a conspirator（同谋者）}
  \wordexample{a conspirator（同谋者）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{conspiracy}{kən'spirəsi}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPIR}
  \wordfamily{conspirator；expire；conspire；expiration；aspire（to + sth / do 向往）}
  \wordsense{n. 共谋；阴谋 *con<com (= together)}
  \wordphrase{conspiracy to overthrow the government（颠覆政府的阴谋）}
  \wordexample{conspiracy to overthrow the government（颠覆政府的阴谋）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{expire}{ik'spaiə(r)}

  \wordfamily{conspiracy；expiration；conspirator；expiry；aspire（to + sth / do 向往）}

  \wordphrase{to expire（期满）}
  \wordexample{to expire（期满）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{expiration}{ˌekspə'reiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPIR}
  \wordfamily{expire；expiry；conspiracy；inspire；aspire（to + sth / do 向往）}
  \wordsense{n. 期满；呼气 *ex (= out), pir<spir (= breathe)}
  \wordphrase{the expiration}
  \wordexample{at the expiration of the lease（在租期期满时）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{expiry}{ik'spairi}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPIR}
  \wordfamily{expiration；inspire；expire；inspiration；aspire（to + sth / do 向往）}
  \wordsense{n. 终结，期满 *ex (= out), pir<spir (= breathe)}
  \wordphrase{expiry of a driving license（驾驶执照到期）}
  \wordexample{expiry of a driving license（驾驶执照到期）}
\end{word}

  \wordsense{v. 赋予……灵感；鼓舞；（文章、话等）给以启示；授意发表（报道等） *in (= into)}
  \wordphrase{to inspire（赋予……灵感）}
  \wordexample{to inspire（赋予……灵感）}
\end{word}

  \wordphrase{have a sudden inspiration（突然想起一个好主意）}
  \wordexample{draw inspiration from nature（从自然中获取灵感）}
  \wordexample{have a sudden inspiration（突然想起一个好主意）}
\end{word}

  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPIR}

  \wordphrase{master spirit（大师）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "master spirit".（常见搭配：大师。）}
  \wordsense{v. 神秘地带走}
  \wordphrase{the abode of spirits（灵魂的安息处）}
  \wordphrase{the work of spirits（幽灵的所为）}
  \wordphrase{say in a kind spirit（亲切地说）}
  \wordexample{a glass of spirit and water（一杯掺了水的烈酒）}
  \wordexample{the abode of spirits（灵魂的安息处）}
  \wordexample{the work of spirits（幽灵的所为）}
\end{word}

  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPIR}

  \wordphrase{spirited conversation（热烈的对话）}
  \wordphrase{high-spirited / low-spirited（兴致勃勃的 / 没精打采的）}
  \wordexample{spirited attack / defense / reply（猛烈的攻击 / 勇敢的防御 / 热烈的回应）}
  \wordexample{spirited conversation（热烈的对话）}
  \wordexample{high-spirited / low-spirited（兴致勃勃的 / 没精打采的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{spiritual}{'spiritʃuəl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPIR}
  \wordfamily{spiritually（精神上）；Spiritualist（招魂论者）；aspire（to + sth / do 向往）}
  \wordsense{a. 精神上的，心灵的；宗教的}
  \wordphrase{be spiritual（精神上的，心灵的）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "be spiritual".（常见搭配：精神上的，心灵的。）}
  \wordsense{n. 《美》黑人灵歌}
  \wordphrase{be spiritual（精神上的，心灵的）}
  \wordexample{be spiritual（精神上的，心灵的）}
  \wordexample{a spiritual（《美》黑人灵歌）}
\end{word}

  \wordfamily{spiritual}
  \wordfamily{sprightly}
  \wordfamily{spirited}
  \wordfamily{spiral}
  \wordphrase{spirituous liquors（烈酒（如威士忌））}
  \wordexample{spirituous liquors（烈酒（如威士忌））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{sprightly}{'spraitli}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPIR}
  \wordfamily{spirituous；spiral；spiritual；spire；aspire（to + sth / do 向往）}
  \wordsense{a. 活泼的，生机勃勃的 *spr<spir (= breathe)}
  \wordphrase{be sprightly（活泼的，生机勃勃的）}
  \wordexample{be sprightly（活泼的，生机勃勃的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{spiral}{'spaiərəl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPIR}

  \wordphrase{inflationary spiral（螺旋形膨胀）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "inflationary spiral".（常见搭配：螺旋形膨胀。）}
  \wordsense{n. 螺旋；螺旋形的东西；螺旋弹簧；（物价等的）连续急剧上升或下降}
  \wordphrase{inflationary spiral（螺旋形膨胀）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "inflationary spiral".（常见搭配：螺旋形膨胀。）}
  \wordsense{v. 使成螺旋形；螺旋式移动；（物价等）不断急剧地上升或下降 *spir (= a coil)}
  \wordphrase{spiral staircase（螺旋形楼梯）}
  \wordphrase{spiral line（螺旋线）}
  \wordphrase{spiral down（螺旋式下降）}
  \wordexample{Prices are still spiraling.（物价还在急剧上升。）}
  \wordexample{spiral staircase（螺旋形楼梯）}
  \wordexample{spiral line（螺旋线）}
\end{word}

  \wordphrase{a spire（尖塔）}

\chapter{SIMIL[SIMUL]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 simil 意为 same, equal，元音弱化后变成了 simul。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 equ, par, sembl, ident 等和来自希腊语的 tauto。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{assimilate}{ə'simileit}
  \wordbreakdown{使几乎相同 as<ad (= to, near) + simil (= same)}
  \wordfamily{assimilation（同化）；dissimulate（掩饰（情感、动机等））；simulate（假装）}
  \wordsense{vt. 使同化；把……比作；消化；理解}
  \wordphrase{assimilation 同化；消化}
  \wordexample{It's difficult to assimilate so much information in a 1-hour lecture.（用一个小时的课程来消化这么多的信息是有难度的。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{dissimulate}{di'simjuleit}
  \wordbreakdown{使不相同 dis (= not) + simul<simil (= same)}
  \wordfamily{dissimulation（掩饰）；assimilate（使同化）；simulate（假装）}
  \wordsense{vt. 掩饰（情感、动机等）；装假}
  \wordphrase{dissimulate her feelings}
  \wordexample{Susan's efforts to dissimulate her feelings of inadequacy are a sign of immaturity.（苏珊试图掩饰自己无法胜任的感觉，这说明她还不够成熟。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{simulate}{'simjuleit}
  \wordbreakdown{使相同 simul<simil (= same)}
  \wordsense{vt. 假装；模仿；模拟}
  \wordfamily{simulation 假装；模仿；模拟}
  \wordfamily{simulator（为实验准备的）模拟装置}
  \wordphrase{simulate interest}
  \wordexample{I only simulate interest in her lectures because I think she's actually boring.（我只是假装对她的讲座很感兴趣，因为我觉得她实际上很无聊。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{facsimile}{fæk'siməli}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SIMIL[SIMUL]}
  \wordfamily{similarity；similar；simile；similitude；assimilate（使同化）}
  \wordsense{n. （笔迹、印刷品、画等的）摹本；【电信】电传真 *fac (= make)}
  \wordphrase{a facsimile（摹本）}
  \wordexample{a facsimile（摹本）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{similarity}{ˌsimə'lærəti}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SIMIL[SIMUL]}
  \wordsense{n. 类似（点），相似（处）}
  \wordfamily{facsimile}
  \wordfamily{similar}
  \wordfamily{simile}
  \wordfamily{similitude}
  \wordfamily{a similarity（类似（点），相似（处））}
  \wordphrase{a similarity（类似（点），相似（处））}
  \wordexample{a similarity（类似（点），相似（处））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{similar}{'similə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SIMIL[SIMUL]}
  \wordsense{a. 类似的}
  \wordfamily{similarly（类似地；一样地）}
  \wordfamily{be similar（类似的）}
  \wordphrase{be similar（类似的）}
  \wordexample{be similar（类似的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{simile}{'simili}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SIMIL[SIMUL]}
  \wordsense{n. 明喻}
  \wordfamily{similar}
  \wordfamily{similitude}
  \wordfamily{similarity}
  \wordfamily{simulacrum}
  \wordfamily{a simile（明喻）}
  \wordphrase{a simile（明喻）}
  \wordexample{a simile（明喻）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{similitude}{si'militju:d}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SIMIL[SIMUL]}
  \wordsense{n. 类似，相似}
  \wordfamily{simile}
  \wordfamily{simulacrum}
  \wordfamily{similar}
  \wordfamily{simultaneous}
  \wordfamily{a similitude（类似，相似）}
  \wordphrase{a similitude（类似，相似）}
  \wordexample{a similitude（类似，相似）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{simulacrum}{ˌsimju'leikram}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SIMIL[SIMUL]}
  \wordfamily{similitude；simultaneous；simile；similar；assimilate（使同化）}
  \wordsense{n. 幻影；假象 *acr (= sharp)}
  \wordphrase{a simulacrum（幻影）}
  \wordexample{a simulacrum（幻影）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{simultaneous}{ˌsiməl'teiniəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SIMIL[SIMUL]}
  \wordfamily{simultaneously（同时地）；assimilate（使同化）；dissimulate（掩饰（情感、动机等））}
  \wordsense{a. 同步的，同时发生的}
  \wordphrase{simultaneous interpreter（同声传译员）}
  \wordexample{simultaneous interpreter（同声传译员）}
\end{word}



\chapter{SUR}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 sur 意为 certain。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{assurance}{ə'ʃuərəns}
  \wordbreakdown{对于某事很确信 as<ad (= to) + sur (= certain)}
  \wordsense{n. 保证；表示保证的话；信心}
  \wordfamily{assure 使确信；使放心}
  \wordfamily{assured 确定的；有保证的；自信的}
  \wordfamily{assuredly 确信地；自信地}
  \wordphrase{assurance that my cancer}
  \wordexample{The doctor gave me every assurance that my cancer is curable.（医生使我确信我得的癌症是可以治愈的。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{ensure}{in'ʃuə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SUR}
  \wordfamily{insure；insurance；reassure；reassuring；assurance（保证）}
  \wordsense{v. 使确信，保证；使安全 *en (= make)}
  \wordphrase{to ensure（使确信，保证）}
  \wordexample{to ensure（使确信，保证）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{insure}{in'ʃuə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SUR}
  \wordfamily{assurance（保证）；ensure（使确信）；insurance（保险（业））}
  \wordsense{v. 确保，保证；投保 *in (= to)}
  \wordphrase{to insure（确保，保证）}
  \wordexample{to insure（确保，保证）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{insurance}{in'ʃuərəns}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SUR}
  \wordfamily{assurance（保证）；ensure（使确信）；insure（确保）}
  \wordsense{n. 保险（业）；赔偿费；保险金额；保险契约 *in (= to)}
  \wordphrase{insurance policy（保险契约，保险单）}
  \wordphrase{insurance premium（保险费）}
  \wordexample{insurance policy（保险契约，保险单）}
  \wordexample{insurance premium（保险费）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{reassure}{ˌri:ə'ʃuə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SUR}
  \wordfamily{reassurance（再保证）；assurance（保证）；ensure（使确信）}
  \wordsense{v. 使放心；再保证 *re (= again), as<ad (= to)}
  \wordphrase{to reassure（使放心）}
  \wordexample{to reassure（使放心）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{reassuring}{ˌri:ə'ʃuəriŋ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SUR}
  \wordfamily{reassure；insurance；insure；ensure；assurance（保证）}
  \wordsense{a. 令人安心的；安慰的 *re (= again), as<ad (= to)}
  \wordphrase{reassuring word / pat（令人安心的话 / 安慰的轻拍）}
  \wordexample{reassuring word / pat（令人安心的话 / 安慰的轻拍）}
\end{word}



\chapter{CLIM}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 clim 意为 ladder, slope。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{acclimate}{'ækləmeit}
  \wordbreakdown{向某事物倾斜 ac<ad (= to) + clim (= ladder, slope)}
  \wordfamily{acclimation（/ acclimatization 顺应）；climax（顶点）；climacteric（更年期）}
  \wordsense{vt. oneself + to + sth 使适应，使顺应}
  \wordphrase{acclimate the issue}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of acclimate.（此处展示 acclimate 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{vi. 习惯，适应}
  \wordphrase{reassuring word / pat（令人安心的话 / 安慰的轻拍）}
  \wordexample{It takes athletes several days to acclimate to high altitude venues.（运动员们需要几天的时间来适应高海拔的比赛地点。）}
  \wordexample{ladder, slope。 acclimate* vt. oneself + to + sth 使适应，使顺应}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{climax}{'klaimæks}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CLIM}
  \wordfamily{climacteric；climactic；climber；anticlimax；acclimate（oneself + to + sth 使适应）}
  \wordsense{n. 顶点，高潮；性高潮（= orgasm）}
  \wordphrase{a climax（顶点，高潮）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "a climax".（常见搭配：顶点，高潮。）}
  \wordsense{v. （使）达到高潮}
  \wordphrase{a climax（顶点，高潮）}
  \wordexample{a climax（顶点，高潮）}
  \wordexample{to climax（达到高潮）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{climacteric}{klai'mæktərik}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CLIM}
  \wordsense{n. 更年期；绝经期}
  \wordfamily{climax}
  \wordfamily{climactic}
  \wordfamily{climber}
  \wordfamily{anticlimax}
  \wordfamily{a climacteric（更年期）}
  \wordphrase{a climacteric（更年期）}
  \wordexample{a climacteric（更年期）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{climactic}{klai'mæktik}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CLIM}
  \wordsense{a. 顶点的，高潮的}
  \wordfamily{climacteric}
  \wordfamily{climber}
  \wordfamily{climax}
  \wordfamily{anticlimax}
  \wordfamily{be climactic（顶点的，高潮的）}
  \wordphrase{be climactic（顶点的，高潮的）}
  \wordexample{be climactic（顶点的，高潮的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{climber}{'klaimə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CLIM}
  \wordsense{n. 攀登者；《口》（在社会地位等方面）拼命向上爬的人，野心家}
  \wordfamily{climbing（攀登）}
  \wordfamily{climb-down（放弃原来坚持的论点（或立场）；让步）}
  \wordfamily{a climber（攀登者）}
  \wordphrase{a climber（攀登者）}
  \wordexample{a climber（攀登者）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{anticlimax}{ˌænti'klaimæks}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CLIM}
  \wordfamily{climber；climactic；climacteric；climax；acclimate（oneself + to + sth 使适应）}
  \wordsense{n. 【语】突降法；虎头蛇尾 *anti (= against, opposite)}
  \wordphrase{an anticlimax（突降法）}
  \wordexample{an anticlimax（突降法）}
\end{word}



\chapter{ALI, ALTER[ULTER, ALTRU]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 ali, alter 意为 other。其变形有 ulter, altru 等。同义词根有来自希腊语的 all。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{adulterate}{ə'dʌltəreit}
  \wordbreakdown{添加别的东西 ad (= to, add) + ulter<alter (= other)}
  \wordsense{vt. 掺杂，掺假}
  \wordfamily{adulteration 混合，掺和；掺假}
  \wordphrase{adulterate the drug}
  \wordexample{Cocaine dealers often adulterate the drug with harmless powder.（可卡因贩卖商常常将无害的粉末掺在可卡因中。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{alienate}{ˈeɪliəneit}
  \wordbreakdown{使具备其他性质 ali (= other)}
  \wordsense{vt. 使孤立，使疏远；【律】让渡}
  \wordfamily{alienation 孤立，疏远；让渡}
  \wordphrase{alienate a lot}
  \wordexample{You're likely to alienate a lot of customers with this price increase.（这样提高价格很可能会使你和很多顾客疏远。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{altercation}{ˌɔːltəˈkeiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{彼此意见不同 alter (= other)}
  \wordfamily{alienate；altruistic；adulterate；alien}
  \wordsense{n. 争论，激烈的争吵}
  \wordphrase{altercation in apartment 3D}
  \wordexample{The officers responded to reports of an altercation in apartment 3D.（警察们在接到 3D 号公寓内有口角冲突的报告后立即出动。）}
  \wordexample{altercation in apartment 3D}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{altruistic}{ˌæltruˈistik}
  \wordbreakdown{向着其他人的 altru(alter (= other))}
  \wordsense{a. 利他的}
  \wordfamily{altercation}
  \wordfamily{alien}
  \wordfamily{alienate}
  \wordfamily{inalienable}
  \wordphrase{altruistic and devotes himself}
  \wordexample{He's very altruistic and devotes himself to good works.（他总是想着别人，常做好事。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{alien}{ˈeɪliən}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ALI, ALTER[ULTER, ALTRU]}
  \wordsense{a. 外国的；性质不同的；（想法等）不相容的 n. 外国人}
  \wordfamily{inalienable}
  \wordfamily{alias}
  \wordfamily{alibi}
  \wordfamily{adultery}
  \wordfamily{be alien（外国的）}
  \wordphrase{be alien（外国的）}
  \wordexample{be alien（外国的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{inalienable}{ɪnˈeɪliənəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ALI, ALTER[ULTER, ALTRU]}
  \wordfamily{alien；alias；alibi；adultery；adulterate（掺杂）}
  \wordsense{a. 不能让与的 *in (= not)}
  \wordphrase{be inalienable（不能让与的）}
  \wordexample{be inalienable（不能让与的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{alias}{ˈeɪliəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ALI, ALTER[ULTER, ALTRU]}
  \wordsense{n.（为隐藏身份而使用的）化名，别名 ad. 化名为}
  \wordfamily{inalienable}
  \wordfamily{alibi}
  \wordfamily{alien}
  \wordfamily{adultery}
  \wordfamily{aliases}
  \wordphrase{aliases}
  \wordexample{The criminal had several aliases.（罪犯曾有多个化名。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{alibi}{ˈælɪbaɪ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ALI, ALTER[ULTER, ALTRU]}
  \wordfamily{alias；adultery；inalienable；adulterous；adulterate（掺杂）}
  \wordsense{n. 不在犯罪现场的证明；辩解 *bi (= place)}
  \wordphrase{alibi}
  \wordexample{The accused man was able to prove / establish / set up an alibi.（被告可以证明自己不在犯罪现场。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{adultery}{əˈdʌltəri}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ALI, ALTER[ULTER, ALTRU]}
  \wordfamily{adulterer（奸夫）；adulteress（奸妇）；adulterate（掺杂）}
  \wordsense{n. 通奸 *ad (= add), ulter<alter (= other)}
  \wordphrase{an adultery（通奸）}
  \wordexample{an adultery（通奸）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{adulterous}{əˈdʌltərəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ALI, ALTER[ULTER, ALTRU]}
  \wordfamily{adultery；alter；alibi；alteration；adulterate（掺杂）}
  \wordsense{a. 通奸的 *ad (= add), ulter<alter (= other)}
  \wordphrase{adulterous woman（通奸的女人）}
  \wordexample{adulterous woman（通奸的女人）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{alter}{ˈɔːltə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ALI, ALTER[ULTER, ALTRU]}
  \wordsense{v. 改变，变更；修改（成衣的）尺寸}
  \wordfamily{alterable（可改变的；可修改的）}
  \wordfamily{adulterous woman（通奸的女人）}
  \wordfamily{to alter（改变，变更）}
  \wordphrase{to alter（改变，变更）}
  \wordexample{to alter（改变，变更）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{alteration}{ˌɔːltəˈreiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ALI, ALTER[ULTER, ALTRU]}
  \wordfamily{adulterate（掺杂）；alienate（使孤立）；altercation（争论）}
  \wordsense{n. 变更；修改（成衣的）尺寸}
  \wordphrase{an alteration（变更）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "an alteration".（常见搭配：变更。）}
  \wordsense{v. 改变，变更；修改（成衣的）尺寸}
  \wordphrase{an alteration（变更）}
  \wordexample{an alteration（变更）}
  \wordexample{to alteration（改变，变更）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{alter ego}{ˌɔːltər ˈiːɡəʊ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ALI, ALTER[ULTER, ALTRU]}
  \wordfamily{alteration；alternate；alter；alternative；adulterate（掺杂）}
  \wordsense{n. 第二自我；至交 *ego (= self)}
  \wordphrase{an alter ego（第二自我）}
  \wordexample{an alter ego（第二自我）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{alternate}{ˈɔːltəneit; ɔːlˈtɜːnət}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ALI, ALTER[ULTER, ALTRU]}
  \wordfamily{adulterate（掺杂）；alienate（使孤立）；altercation（争论）}
  \wordsense{a. 轮流的，交替的；间隔的；（叶子）互生的；供选择的}
  \wordphrase{be alternate（轮流的，交替的）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "be alternate".（常见搭配：轮流的，交替的。）}
  \wordsense{n. 替补者；候补者；代理人}
  \wordphrase{be alternate（轮流的，交替的）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "be alternate".（常见搭配：轮流的，交替的。）}
  \wordsense{v. 交替；轮流}
  \wordphrase{on alternate days（隔一天）}
  \wordexample{Day alternates with night.（日夜交替。）}
  \wordexample{on alternate days（隔一天）}
  \wordexample{be alternate（轮流的，交替的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{alternative}{ɔːlˈtɜːnətɪv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ALI, ALTER[ULTER, ALTRU]}
  \wordfamily{alternatively（两者中择一地）；adulterate（掺杂）；alienate（使孤立）}
  \wordsense{n. 二择一；可选择的办法}
  \wordphrase{the alternative}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of alternative.（此处展示 alternative 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{a. 两者中择一的；供选择的}
  \wordphrase{be alternative}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of alternative.（此处展示 alternative 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. 交替；轮流}
  \wordphrase{alternative plan（供选择的计划）}
  \wordphrase{on alternate days（隔一天）}
  \wordphrase{son, thread, foul。}
  \wordexample{Day alternates with night.（日夜交替。）}
  \wordexample{alternative plan（供选择的计划）}
  \wordexample{on alternate days（隔一天）}
\end{word}



\chapter{FIL}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 fil 意为 son, thread, foul。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{affiliate}{əˈfɪlieit; əˈfɪliət}
  \wordbreakdown{形成近乎和儿子一样的关系 af<ad (= to, near) + fil (= son)}
  \wordfamily{affiliation（联系）；defile（弄脏）；filial（子女的）}
  \wordsense{vt. sb + with + sth 使形成紧密关系；使……加入，使隶属于……}
  \wordphrase{affiliate the issue}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of affiliate.（此处展示 affiliate 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{vi. with + sth 发生联系，参加}
  \wordphrase{affiliate in practice}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of affiliate.（此处展示 affiliate 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 分会，分公司；成员}
  \wordphrase{The state democratic party}
  \wordphrase{to affiliate（sb + with + sth 使形成紧密关系）}
  \wordexample{The state democratic party affiliated four local party groups last year.（民主党去年吸纳了四个地方党组织。）}
  \wordexample{The state democratic party}
  \wordexample{to affiliate（sb + with + sth 使形成紧密关系）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{defile}{dɪˈfaɪl}
  \wordbreakdown{使变得非常脏 de (= intensive) + fil (= foul)}
  \wordfamily{defilement（弄脏）；affiliate（sb + with + sth 使形成紧密关系）；filial（子女的）}
  \wordsense{vt. 弄脏；亵渎}
  \wordphrase{The mountain stream was}
  \wordexample{The mountain stream was defiled by the town's raw sewage.（该市未经处理的污水污染了山上流下来的溪水。）}
  \wordexample{The mountain stream was}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{filial}{ˈfɪliəl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FIL}
  \wordsense{a. 子女的；子代的；孝顺的}
  \wordfamily{filicide}
  \wordfamily{filament}
  \wordfamily{filature}
  \wordfamily{file}
  \wordphrase{filial duty（作为子女的义务）}
  \wordphrase{filial piety to parents（对父母的孝道）}
  \wordphrase{the first / second filial generation（杂交第一 / 二代）}
  \wordexample{filial duty（作为子女的义务）}
  \wordexample{filial piety to parents（对父母的孝道）}
  \wordexample{the first / second filial generation（杂交第一 / 二代）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{filicide}{ˈfɪlɪsaɪd}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FIL}
  \wordfamily{filial；filament；filature；file；affiliate（sb + with + sth 使形成紧密关系）}
  \wordsense{n. 杀害子女（者） *cid (= kill)}
  \wordphrase{a filicide（杀害子女（者））}
  \wordexample{a filicide（杀害子女（者））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{filament}{ˈfɪləmənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FIL}
  \wordfamily{filamentous（丝状的）；affiliate（sb + with + sth 使形成紧密关系）；defile（弄脏）}
  \wordsense{n. 细丝；单纤维；（雄蕊的）花丝；（电灯泡、电子管的）灯丝 *fil (= thread)}
  \wordphrase{a filament（细丝）}
  \wordexample{a filament（细丝）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{filature}{ˈfɪlətʃə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FIL}
  \wordfamily{filament；file；filicide；filings；affiliate（sb + with + sth 使形成紧密关系）}
  \wordsense{n. 缫丝；缫丝厂 *fil (= thread)}
  \wordphrase{a filature（缫丝）}
  \wordexample{a filature（缫丝）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{file}{faɪl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FIL}
  \wordfamily{affiliate（sb + with + sth 使形成紧密关系）；defile（弄脏）；filial（子女的）}
  \wordsense{n. 文件夹；档案；（排队的人、物的）列}
  \wordphrase{on file（存档）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "on file".（常见搭配：存档。）}
  \wordsense{v. 存档；排成纵列行进；提出（申请、抗议等）；提起（诉讼） *fil (= thread)}
  \wordphrase{file cabinet（文件柜）}
  \wordphrase{file number（文档编号）}
  \wordphrase{file punch（文件穿孔器）}
  \wordexample{file cabinet（文件柜）}
  \wordexample{file number（文档编号）}
  \wordexample{file punch（文件穿孔器）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{filings}{ˈfaɪlɪŋz}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FIL}
  \wordfamily{file；fillet；filature；profile；affiliate（sb + with + sth 使形成紧密关系）}
  \wordsense{n. 锉屑 *fil (= thread)}
  \wordphrase{a filings（锉屑）}
  \wordexample{a filings（锉屑）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{fillet}{ˈfɪlɪt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FIL}
  \wordfamily{affiliate（sb + with + sth 使形成紧密关系）；defile（弄脏）；filial（子女的）}
  \wordsense{n. （用做装饰的）带子；（去掉骨头的）肉片，鱼片}
  \wordphrase{filleted plaice（切成片的鲽鱼）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "filleted plaice".（常见搭配：切成片的鲽鱼。）}
  \wordsense{v. 把（鱼、肉）切成片 *fil (= thread)}
  \wordphrase{a fillet（带子）}
  \wordexample{a fillet（带子）}
  \wordexample{to fillet（把（鱼、肉）切成片）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{profile}{ˈprəʊfaɪl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FIL}
  \wordfamily{fillet；filth；filings；filthy；affiliate（sb + with + sth 使形成紧密关系）}
  \wordsense{n. 人物简介；侧影，侧面；轮廓}
  \wordphrase{the profile}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of profile.（此处展示 profile 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. 画出……的轮廓；勾画侧面图；写人物简介 *pro (= before), fil (= thread)}
  \wordphrase{portrait drawn in profile（侧影肖像）}
  \wordexample{line of hills profiled against the night sky（以夜空为背景的山的轮廓）}
  \wordexample{portrait drawn in profile（侧影肖像）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{filth}{fɪlθ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FIL}
  \wordfamily{profile；filthy；fillet；filings；affiliate（sb + with + sth 使形成紧密关系）}
  \wordsense{n. 污物，肮脏；淫猥 *fil (= foul)}
  \wordphrase{a filth（污物，肮脏）}
  \wordexample{a filth（污物，肮脏）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{filthy}{ˈfɪlθi}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FIL}
  \wordfamily{filth；profile；fillet；filings；affiliate（sb + with + sth 使形成紧密关系）}
  \wordsense{a. 肮脏的；淫猥的 *fil (= foul)}
  \wordphrase{filthy lucre（肮脏的钱财）}
  \wordexample{filthy lucre（肮脏的钱财）}
\end{word}



\chapter{LEV[LIEV]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 lev 意为 to raise, to lift, light, smooth 等。其变形为 liev。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{alleviate}{əˈliːvieit}
  \wordbreakdown{使变得更轻 al<ad (= to, add) + lev (= light, lift)}
  \wordfamily{alleviation（减轻，缓解）；irrelevant（不恰当的）；levity（轻率）}
  \wordsense{vt. 减轻（痛苦等），缓解}
  \wordphrase{filthy lucre（肮脏的钱财）}
  \wordexample{Aspirin usually alleviates moderate pain symptoms.（通常阿司匹林可以缓解轻度疼痛。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{irrelevant}{ɪˈreləvənt}
  \wordbreakdown{不能再举起的 ir<in (= not) + re (= again) + lev (= lift, light)}
  \wordsense{a. 不恰当的，不切题的；无关系的}
  \wordfamily{irrelevance / irrelevancy 不恰当，不对题；无关系}
  \wordphrase{irrelevant}
  \wordexample{My professor said the third paragraph of my essay is irrelevant.（教授说我论文的第三段与论文整体没有关联。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{levity}{ˈlevəti}
  \wordbreakdown{轻浮的行为 lev (= light, lift)}
  \wordfamily{irrelevant；relevant；alleviate；elevate}
  \wordsense{n. 轻率；轻浮}
  \wordphrase{levity It makes the}
  \wordexample{I enjoy our professor's levity. It makes the hour go faster.（我喜欢我们教授轻松的风格，因为这让时间过得很快。）}
  \wordexample{levity It makes the}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{relevant}{ˈreləvənt}
  \wordbreakdown{再次举起的 re (= again) + lev (= lift, light)}
  \wordsense{a. 有关系的，有关联的；恰当的}
  \wordfamily{relevance / relevancy 相关性；恰当}
  \wordfamily{relevantly 恰当地}
  \wordphrase{relevant to the subject}
  \wordexample{The bibliography contains items relevant to the subject of cardiopulmonary health.（参考文献目录中包含与心肺健康这一主题相关的条目。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{elevate}{ˈeliveit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LEV[LIEV]}
  \wordfamily{elevation；elevator；lever；leverage；alleviate（减轻（痛苦等））}
  \wordsense{v. 举起；提高 *e<ex (= out), lev (= lift)}
  \wordphrase{elevate the voice（提高嗓音）}
  \wordphrase{elevated railway / railroad（高架铁道）}
  \wordexample{elevate the voice（提高嗓音）}
  \wordexample{elevated railway / railroad（高架铁道）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{elevation}{ˌelɪˈveiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LEV[LIEV]}
  \wordfamily{elevate；elevator；lever；leverage；alleviate（减轻（痛苦等））}
  \wordsense{n. 高度，海拔；提高；高尚；（炮的）仰角；（建筑物的）立视图 *e<ex (= out), lev (= lift)}
  \wordphrase{elevation of thought（思想的高尚）}
  \wordexample{elevation of 3,000 meters（海拔 3,000 米）}
  \wordexample{elevation of thought（思想的高尚）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{elevator}{ˈeliveitə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LEV[LIEV]}
  \wordfamily{elevation；lever；elevate；leverage；alleviate（减轻（痛苦等））}
  \wordsense{n. 电梯；升降机 *e<ex (= out), lev (= lift)}
  \wordphrase{an elevator（电梯）}
  \wordexample{an elevator（电梯）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{lever}{ˈliːvə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LEV[LIEV]}
  \wordfamily{elevator；leverage；elevation；levitate；alleviate（减轻（痛苦等））}
  \wordsense{n. 杠杆；控制杆；手段}
  \wordphrase{a lever（杠杆）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "a lever".（常见搭配：杠杆。）}
  \wordsense{v. 用杠杆移动 *lev (= lift)}
  \wordphrase{a lever（杠杆）}
  \wordexample{a lever（杠杆）}
  \wordexample{to lever（用杠杆移动）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{leverage}{ˈliːvərɪdʒ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LEV[LIEV]}
  \wordfamily{alleviate（减轻（痛苦等））；irrelevant（不恰当的）；levity（轻率）}
  \wordsense{n. 杠杆作用；杠杆装置；（为达到某一目的而使用的）手段 *lev (= lift)}
  \wordphrase{a leverage（杠杆作用）}
  \wordexample{a leverage（杠杆作用）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{levitate}{ˈleviteit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LEV[LIEV]}
  \wordfamily{levitation（依靠超自然力量的空中飘浮）；alleviate（减轻（痛苦等））；irrelevant（不恰当的）}
  \wordsense{v. （使）飘浮；（使）升空 *lev (= lift)}
  \wordphrase{to levitate（飘浮）}
  \wordexample{to levitate（飘浮）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{levy}{ˈlevi}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LEV[LIEV]}
  \wordfamily{alleviate（减轻（痛苦等））；irrelevant（不恰当的）；levity（轻率）}
  \wordsense{v. 征收（税金等）；征集，召集；发动与……的战争（\textasciitilde{} on）；（依据法律）扣押}
  \wordphrase{capital levy（资产税）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "capital levy".（常见搭配：资产税。）}
  \wordsense{n. 课税，征税；征集；征兵 *lev (= light)}
  \wordphrase{capital levy（资产税）}
  \wordexample{levy a tax / a fine / a ransom on（向……征收税金 / 罚金 / 索取赎金）}
  \wordexample{levy on a person's property / estate（扣押某人的财产 / 不动产）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{relieve}{rɪˈliːv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LEV[LIEV]}
  \wordfamily{alleviate（减轻（痛苦等））；irrelevant（不恰当的）；levity（轻率）}
  \wordsense{v. 减轻（痛苦、负担等）；救济；换班；使解除；使突出，使醒目 *re (= again), liev<lev (= lift)}
  \wordphrase{relieve one's feelings（（哭喊或打闹以）发泄情绪）}
  \wordphrase{to laugh, to play。}
  \wordexample{relieve oneself / nature / the bowels（排泄，方便）}
  \wordexample{relieve one's feelings（（哭喊或打闹以）发泄情绪）}
\end{word}



\chapter{LUD, LUS}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 lud, lus 意为 to laugh, to play。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{allude}{əˈluːd}
  \wordbreakdown{对某事发笑 al<ad (= to) + lud (= laugh, play)}
  \wordfamily{allusion（暗指）；allusive（暗指的）；collusion（同谋）}
  \wordsense{vi. to + sth 暗指；间接提到}
  \wordphrase{allude to her profound}
  \wordexample{Her drooping eyes allude to her profound fatigue.（她双眼低垂，说明她非常疲倦。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{collusion}{kəˈluːʒn}
  \wordbreakdown{一起笑 col<com (= together) + lus (= laugh, play)}
  \wordfamily{collude（同谋）；collusive（同谋的）；allude（to + sth 暗指）}
  \wordsense{n. 同谋，勾结}
  \wordphrase{collusion in ticket pricing}
  \wordexample{The airlines are sometimes thought to be guilty of collusion in ticket pricing.（有时航空公司会被指责相互串通垄断机票价格。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{delude}{dɪˈluːd}
  \wordbreakdown{由于可笑而使某人脱离正常状态 de (= from) + lud (= laugh, play)}
  \wordfamily{delusion（迷惑，欺瞒）；delusive（欺骗的）；allude（to + sth 暗指）}
  \wordsense{vt. 欺骗；哄骗；迷惑}
  \wordphrase{deludes himself that he}
  \wordexample{He deludes himself that he is handsome.（他自欺地认为自己长得很帅。）}
  \wordexample{deludes himself that he}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{elude}{ɪˈluːd}
  \wordbreakdown{笑着从危险状况中摆脱出来 e<ex (= out) + lud (= laugh, play)}
  \wordsense{vt.（巧妙地）逃脱，摆脱}
  \wordfamily{elusive 逃避的}
  \wordfamily{elusion 回避；逃跑}
  \wordphrase{The criminal eluded the}
  \wordexample{The criminal eluded the cops by stealing a car and driving away.（犯人躲开警察偷了一辆车逃跑了。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{illusion}{ɪˈluːʒn}
  \wordbreakdown{头脑对某事物闹着玩 il<in (= on) + lus (= laugh, play)}
  \wordsense{n. 幻想；错觉；假象}
  \wordfamily{illusionist 魔术师；空想家}
  \wordfamily{illusive / illusory 幻影的；错觉的；迷惑人的}
  \wordphrase{illusion of perfect marriage}
  \wordexample{Many old maids hold the illusion of perfect marriage well into their fifties.（许多老处女在五十岁以后仍然幻想着拥有完美的婚姻。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{ludicrous}{ˈluːdɪkrəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LUD, LUS}
  \wordfamily{prelude；interlude；postlude；allude（to + sth 暗指）}
  \wordsense{a. 滑稽的，可笑的 *lud (= laugh)}
  \wordphrase{be ludicrous（滑稽的，可笑的）}
  \wordexample{be ludicrous（滑稽的，可笑的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{prelude}{ˈpreljuːd}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LUD, LUS}
  \wordfamily{ludicrous；interlude；postlude；allude（to + sth 暗指）}
  \wordsense{n. 前奏曲；序幕}
  \wordphrase{a prelude（前奏曲）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "a prelude".（常见搭配：前奏曲。）}
  \wordsense{v. 作为……的前奏（或序幕） *pre (= before), lud (= play)}
  \wordphrase{a prelude（前奏曲）}
  \wordexample{a prelude（前奏曲）}
  \wordexample{to prelude（作为……的前奏（或序幕））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{interlude}{ˈɪntəluːd}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LUD, LUS}
  \wordfamily{prelude；postlude；ludicrous；allude（to + sth 暗指）}
  \wordsense{n. 间奏；（话剧或歌剧等的）幕间；穿插事件 *inter (= between), lud (= play)}
  \wordphrase{interludes of bright weather（坏天气中间或有的晴天）}
  \wordexample{interludes of bright weather（坏天气中间或有的晴天）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{postlude}{ˈpəʊstluːd}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LUD, LUS}
  \wordfamily{interlude；prelude；ludicrous；allude（to + sth 暗指）}
  \wordsense{n. 后奏曲 *post (= after), lud (= play)}
  \wordphrase{a postlude（后奏曲）}
  \wordexample{a postlude（后奏曲）}
\end{word}



\chapter{PALL}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 pall 意为 pale。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{appall}{əˈpɔːl}
  \wordbreakdown{让某物变得苍白 ap<ad (= to) + pall (= pale)}
  \wordsense{vt. 使惊骇}
  \wordfamily{appalling 骇人听闻的}
  \wordfamily{appallingly 令人毛骨悚然地}
  \wordfamily{appalls me}
  \wordphrase{appalls me}
  \wordexample{The way he beats his dog appalls me.（他打狗的样子令我毛骨悚然。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{pallid}{ˈpælɪd}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PALL}
  \wordsense{a. 无血色的，苍白的}
  \wordfamily{pallor}
  \wordfamily{be pallid（无血色的，苍白的）}
  \wordphrase{be pallid（无血色的，苍白的）}
  \wordexample{be pallid（无血色的，苍白的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{pallor}{ˈpælə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PALL}
  \wordfamily{pallid；appall（使惊骇）；postlude（后奏曲 *post (= after), lud）}
  \wordsense{n. （脸的）苍白}
  \wordphrase{cap / capt / cept / ceiv[cip, cup, ceit], empt / em, rap / rav, sump / sumpt, lab / lep 等）}
  \wordexample{cap / capt / cept / ceiv[cip, cup, ceit], empt / em, rap / rav, sump / sumpt, lab / lep 等）}
\end{word}



\chapter{PREHEND, PREHENS[PREN, PREGN, PRIEV]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 prehend, prehens 意为 to take, to seize。其变形有 pren, pregn, priev 等。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 cap / capt / cept / ceiv[cip, cup, ceit], empt / em, rap / rav, sump / sumpt, lab / lep 等。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{apprehensive}{ˌæprɪˈhensɪv}
  \wordbreakdown{抓得很近的 ap<ad (= to, near) + prehens (= take)}
  \wordsense{a. 忧虑的，担心的；理解快的}
  \wordfamily{apprehension 忧虑；逮捕；理解}
  \wordfamily{apprehensible 可理解的}
  \wordfamily{apprehend 逮捕；忧虑；理解}
  \wordphrase{apprehensive about possible terrorist}
  \wordexample{Officials are apprehensive about possible terrorist attacks.（官员们担心可能发生的恐怖袭击。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{incomprehensible}{ˌɪnkɒmprɪˈhensəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{根本无法抓住的 in (= not) + com (= thoroughly) + prehens (= take)}
  \wordfamily{incomprehension（不了解）；apprehensive（忧虑的）；reprehensible（应受指责的）}
  \wordsense{a. 不能理解的}
  \wordphrase{incomprehensible}
  \wordexample{I've studied French for months, but I still find most conversations incomprehensible.（我已经学了好几个月法语了，但我发现对大部分对话仍无法理解。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{reprehensible}{ˌreprɪˈhensəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{向后、逆着抓住的 re (= back, against) + prehens (= take)}
  \wordsense{a. 应受指责的}
  \wordfamily{reprehend 责备，责难}
  \wordfamily{reprehension 非难，指责}
  \wordphrase{reprehensible behavior last night}
  \wordexample{You should be ashamed of your reprehensible behavior last night.（你应该为你昨晚应受指责的行为感到羞愧。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{misapprehension}{ˌmɪsæprɪˈhenʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{接受错 mis (= bad) + ap<ad (= intensive) + prehens (= take)}
  \wordfamily{misapprehend（误解，误会）；apprehensive（忧虑的）；incomprehensible（不能理解的）}
  \wordsense{n. 误解}
  \wordphrase{misapprehension that I give}
  \wordexample{I infer you're suffering from the misapprehension that I give a damn.（你好像误会我了，以为我是在谴责你。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{reprieve}{rɪˈpriːv}
  \wordbreakdown{抓住放在背后 re (= back) + priev<prehend (= take)}
  \wordfamily{misapprehension；comprehend；reprehensible；comprehensible；apprehensive（忧虑的）}
  \wordsense{n. 缓刑（令）；暂时缓解}
  \wordphrase{the reprieve}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of reprieve.（此处展示 reprieve 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 暂缓对……处刑；暂时缓解}
  \wordphrase{reprieve before the awful}
  \wordexample{Every death row prisoner dreams of a reprieve before the awful day.（所有的死刑犯在那个可怕的日子来临之前都梦想着能获得缓刑。）}
  \wordexample{reprieve before the awful}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{comprehend}{ˌkɒmprɪˈhend}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PREHEND, PREHENS[PREN, PREGN, PRIEV]}
  \wordfamily{comprehensible；comprehension；comprehensive；prehensile；apprehensive（忧虑的）}
  \wordsense{v. （充分）理解 *com (= completely)}
  \wordphrase{to comprehend（理解）}
  \wordexample{to comprehend（理解）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{comprehensible}{ˌkɒmprɪˈhensəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PREHEND, PREHENS[PREN, PREGN, PRIEV]}
  \wordfamily{comprehensibility（能理解）；apprehensive（忧虑的）；incomprehensible（不能理解的）}
  \wordsense{a. 能（充分）理解的 *com (= completely)}
  \wordphrase{be comprehensible（能（充分）理解的）}
  \wordexample{be comprehensible（能（充分）理解的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{comprehension}{ˌkɒmprɪˈhenʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PREHEND, PREHENS[PREN, PREGN, PRIEV]}
  \wordfamily{comprehensible；comprehensive；comprehend；prehensile；apprehensive（忧虑的）}
  \wordsense{n. 理解（力）；理解力测验 *com (= completely)}
  \wordphrase{a comprehension（理解（力））}
  \wordexample{a comprehension（理解（力））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{comprehensive}{ˌkɒmprɪˈhensɪv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PREHEND, PREHENS[PREN, PREGN, PRIEV]}
  \wordfamily{comprehension；prehensile；comprehensible；apprentice；apprehensive（忧虑的）}
  \wordsense{a. 理解的；综合的 *com (= completely)}
  \wordphrase{comprehensive description（全面的说明）}
  \wordphrase{man with a comprehensive mind（有理解力的人）}
  \wordphrase{comprehensive examination（综合考试）}
  \wordexample{comprehensive description（全面的说明）}
  \wordexample{man with a comprehensive mind（有理解力的人）}
  \wordexample{comprehensive examination（综合考试）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{prehensile}{prɪˈhensaɪl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PREHEND, PREHENS[PREN, PREGN, PRIEV]}
  \wordsense{a. 能抓住的}
  \wordfamily{comprehensive}
  \wordfamily{apprentice}
  \wordfamily{comprehension}
  \wordfamily{entrepreneur}
  \wordphrase{the prehensile trunk of an elephant（能抓住东西的大象鼻子）}
  \wordexample{the prehensile trunk of an elephant（能抓住东西的大象鼻子）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{apprentice}{əˈprentɪs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PREHEND, PREHENS[PREN, PREGN, PRIEV]}
  \wordfamily{apprehensive（忧虑的）；incomprehensible（不能理解的）；reprehensible（应受指责的）}
  \wordsense{n. 学徒；见习生；初学者}
  \wordphrase{an apprentice（学徒）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "an apprentice".（常见搭配：学徒。）}
  \wordsense{v. 当学徒 *ap<ad (= near), prent<prehend (= take)}
  \wordphrase{an apprentice（学徒）}
  \wordexample{an apprentice（学徒）}
  \wordexample{to apprentice（当学徒）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{entrepreneur}{ˌɒntrəprəˈnɜː(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PREHEND, PREHENS[PREN, PREGN, PRIEV]}
  \wordfamily{apprehensive（忧虑的）；incomprehensible（不能理解的）；reprehensible（应受指责的）}
  \wordsense{n. 企业家；创业者 *entr<inter (= between), pren<prehend (= take)}
  \wordphrase{an entrepreneur（企业家）}
  \wordexample{an entrepreneur（企业家）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{impregnable}{ɪmˈpreɡnəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PREHEND, PREHENS[PREN, PREGN, PRIEV]}
  \wordfamily{impregnability（攻不破）；apprehensive（忧虑的）；incomprehensible（不能理解的）}
  \wordsense{a. 攻不破的 *im<in (= not), pregn<prehend (= take)}
  \wordphrase{impregnable defenses（坚不可摧的防御）}
  \wordphrase{impregnable argument（无懈可击的辩论）}
  \wordexample{impregnable defenses（坚不可摧的防御）}
  \wordexample{impregnable argument（无懈可击的辩论）}
\end{word}



\chapter{CERT}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的词根 cert 意为 sure。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{ascertain}{ˌæsəˈtein}
  \wordbreakdown{使自己确信 as<ad (= to) + cert (= sure)}
  \wordfamily{ascertainable（可确定的）；ascertainment（确定）；impregnability（攻不破）}
  \wordsense{vt. 确认；查清}
  \wordphrase{impregnable defenses（坚不可摧的防御）}
  \wordexample{It was not possible to ascertain her whereabouts.（根本不可能查出她的行踪。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{certain}{ˈsɜːtn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CERT}
  \wordsense{a. 确信的，确凿的；某个；一些}
  \wordfamily{certainly}
  \wordfamily{certainty}
  \wordfamily{certify}
  \wordfamily{certified}
  \wordfamily{be certain（确信的，确凿的）}
  \wordphrase{be certain（确信的，确凿的）}
  \wordexample{be certain（确信的，确凿的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{certainly}{ˈsɜːtnli}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CERT}
  \wordsense{ad. 无疑地；必定；（用于回答）当然}
  \wordfamily{certain}
  \wordfamily{certainty}
  \wordfamily{certify}
  \wordfamily{certified}
  \wordphrase{Certainly not!（当然不是！）}
  \wordexample{Certainly not!（当然不是！）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{certainty}{ˈsɜːtnti}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CERT}
  \wordsense{n. 必定的事；确实性}
  \wordfamily{certainly}
  \wordfamily{certify}
  \wordfamily{certain}
  \wordfamily{certified}
  \wordfamily{a certainty（必定的事）}
  \wordphrase{a certainty（必定的事）}
  \wordexample{a certainty（必定的事）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{certify}{ˈsɜːtɪfaɪ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CERT}
  \wordsense{v. 证明；保证}
  \wordfamily{certainty}
  \wordfamily{certified}
  \wordfamily{certainly}
  \wordfamily{certifiable}
  \wordfamily{to certify（证明）}
  \wordphrase{to certify（证明）}
  \wordexample{to certify（证明）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{certified}{ˈsɜːtɪfaɪd}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CERT}
  \wordsense{a. 被证明的；有保证的}
  \wordfamily{certify}
  \wordfamily{certifiable}
  \wordfamily{certainty}
  \wordfamily{certificate}
  \wordphrase{certified check（保付支票）}
  \wordphrase{certified mail（挂号信）}
  \wordexample{certified check（保付支票）}
  \wordexample{certified mail（挂号信）}
  \wordexample{certified public accountant（执业会计师，缩写：C.P.A.）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{certifiable}{ˈsɜːtɪfaɪəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CERT}
  \wordfamily{certified；certificate；certify；certification；ascertain（确认）}
  \wordsense{a. 可证明的；可保证的；可证明有精神病的 *fic (= make)}
  \wordphrase{certifiable lunatic（被证明为精神错乱的）}
  \wordexample{certifiable lunatic（被证明为精神错乱的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{certificate}{səˈtɪfɪkət}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CERT}
  \wordfamily{ascertain（确认）；certain（确信的）；certainly（无疑地）}
  \wordsense{n. 证明书}
  \wordphrase{certificated teachers（正式教师）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "certificated teachers".（常见搭配：正式教师。）}
  \wordsense{v. 发证书给…… *fic (= make)}
  \wordphrase{certificate of attendance（在读证明）}
  \wordexample{certificate of attendance（在读证明）}
  \wordexample{a certificate（证明书）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{certification}{ˌsɜːtɪfɪˈkeiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CERT}
  \wordfamily{certificate；certitude；certifiable；certified；ascertain（确认）}
  \wordsense{n. 证明；保证 *fic (= make)}
  \wordphrase{a certification（证明）}
  \wordexample{a certification（证明）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{certitude}{ˈsɜːtɪtjuːd}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CERT}
  \wordfamily{certification；certificate；certifiable；certified；esthes（/ (a)）；pat（/ pass））；ascertain（确认）}
  \wordsense{n. 确实（性）}
  \wordphrase{a certitude（确实（性））}
  \wordexample{a certitude（确实（性））}
\end{word}



\chapter{SENT, SENS}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 sent, sens 意为 to feel。同义词根有来自希腊语的 path, (a)esthes / (a)esthet 和来自拉丁语的 pat / pass。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{assent}{əˈsent}
  \wordbreakdown{想法就在眼前 as<ad (= to, near) + sent (= feel)}
  \wordfamily{assentation（迎合，附和）；consensus（一致）；dissent（不赞成）}
  \wordsense{vi. to + sth 同意}
  \wordphrase{assent in practice}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of assent.（此处展示 assent 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 同意}
  \wordphrase{assent I'd like}
  \wordexample{With your assent, I'd like to close the shop early today.（如果您同意的话，今天我想早点关店门。）}
  \wordexample{assent I'd like}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{consensus}{kənˈsensəs}
  \wordbreakdown{想法一致 con<com (= with) + sens (= feel)}
  \wordfamily{consensual（在双方同意下成立的）；assent（to + sth 同意）；dissent（不赞成）}
  \wordsense{n. 一致；舆论}
  \wordphrase{consensus about cutting costs}
  \wordexample{The budget committee reached consensus about cutting costs by 10\% next year.（预算委员会达成一致意见，将明年的开支减少 10\%。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{dissent}{dɪˈsent}
  \wordbreakdown{想法分别不同 dis (= apart) + sent (= feel)}
  \wordfamily{dissension（意见分歧）；dissenter（不赞成者，反对者）；assent（to + sth 同意）}
  \wordsense{n. 不赞成，异议}
  \wordphrase{the dissent}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of dissent.（此处展示 dissent 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{vi. 持异议，不同意；不信奉英国国教}
  \wordphrase{dissenter 不赞成者，反对者；不信奉英国国教的人}
  \wordexample{Voices of dissent are welcomed in a true democracy.（真正的民主欢迎反对的声音。）}
  \wordexample{dissenter 不赞成者，反对者；不信奉英国国教的人}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{presentiment}{prɪˈzentɪmənt}
  \wordbreakdown{事先的感觉 pre (= before) + sens (= feel)}
  \wordsense{n. 预感}
  \wordfamily{dissent}
  \wordfamily{resentment}
  \wordfamily{consensus}
  \wordfamily{sententious}
  \wordphrase{presentiment all day that}
  \wordexample{I've had a presentiment all day that something extraordinary is going to happen.（我一整天都感觉有什么特别的事要发生。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{resentment}{rɪˈzentmənt}
  \wordbreakdown{再一次痛彻心扉的感受 re (= again, back, against) + sens (= feel)}
  \wordsense{n. 愤怒；怨恨}
  \wordfamily{resent 愤慨；怨恨}
  \wordfamily{resentful 愤慨的，怨恨的}
  \wordphrase{resentment when I was}
  \wordexample{I felt a lot of resentment when I was passed over again for a promotion.（又一次没能晋升，我很愤怒。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{sententious}{senˈtenʃəs}
  \wordbreakdown{使感受的 sens (= feel)}
  \wordfamily{sentence（句子）；assent（to + sth 同意）；consensus（一致）}
  \wordsense{a. 说教的；爱用警句的；简洁的}
  \wordphrase{sententious commencement speech nothing}
  \wordexample{She gave the standard sententious commencement speech, nothing special.（她的毕业典礼致词是标准的说教性的，毫无特别之处。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{consent}{kənˈsent}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SENT, SENS}
  \wordfamily{sense；senseless；sensible；sensitive；assent（to + sth 同意）}
  \wordsense{v. 同意，准许}
  \wordphrase{to consent（同意，准许）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "to consent".（常见搭配：同意，准许。）}
  \wordsense{n. 同意，准许 *con<com (= together)}
  \wordphrase{age of consent（承诺年龄，指法律上被认为可以进行性行为或结婚的年龄）}
  \wordexample{age of consent（承诺年龄，指法律上被认为可以进行性行为或结婚的年龄）}
  \wordexample{to consent（同意，准许）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{sense}{sens}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SENT, SENS}
  \wordfamily{assent（to + sth 同意）；consensus（一致）；dissent（不赞成）}
  \wordsense{n. 官能（指视觉、听觉、嗅觉、味觉、触觉）；辨别力；感觉；意识；意义，意思；（pl.）理智}
  \wordphrase{common sense（常识）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "common sense".（常见搭配：常识。）}
  \wordsense{v. 感觉到；察觉；意识到}
  \wordphrase{sense organ（感觉器官，如耳朵、眼睛等）}
  \wordphrase{a sense of humor（幽默感）}
  \wordphrase{come to one's senses（恢复知觉）}
  \wordexample{a sense of locality / direction（位置感 / 方向感）}
  \wordexample{sense organ（感觉器官，如耳朵、眼睛等）}
  \wordexample{a sense of humor（幽默感）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{senseless}{ˈsensləs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SENT, SENS}
  \wordfamily{sense；sensible；consent；sensitive；assent（to + sth 同意）}
  \wordsense{a. 愚蠢的；不省人事的，无意识的 *less (= without)}
  \wordphrase{senseless idea（愚蠢的想法）}
  \wordphrase{fall senseless to the ground（昏倒在地）}
  \wordexample{senseless idea（愚蠢的想法）}
  \wordexample{fall senseless to the ground（昏倒在地）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{sensible}{ˈsensəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SENT, SENS}
  \wordfamily{sensibly（明智地）；assent（to + sth 同意）；consensus（一致）}
  \wordsense{a. 明智的；适当的；实用的}
  \wordphrase{sensible ideas（明智的想法）}
  \wordphrase{sensible woman（明智的女子）}
  \wordexample{sensible ideas（明智的想法）}
  \wordexample{sensible woman（明智的女子）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{sensitive}{ˈsensətɪv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SENT, SENS}
  \wordsense{a. 敏感的；神经过敏的；易生气的；（仪表）灵敏度高的；（胶卷等）感光的}
  \wordfamily{sensitivity（敏感性；灵敏度）}
  \wordfamily{sensitize（使变得敏感；使具有感光性）}
  \wordfamily{cf. oversensitive（过分敏感的）}
  \wordphrase{sensitive to light（对光敏感的）}
  \wordphrase{sensitive skin（敏感性皮肤）}
  \wordphrase{sensitive nerve in a tooth（牙齿的敏感神经）}
  \wordphrase{sensitive to criticism（对批评敏感的）}
  \wordexample{sensitive to light（对光敏感的）}
  \wordexample{sensitive skin（敏感性皮肤）}
  \wordexample{sensitive nerve in a tooth（牙齿的敏感神经）}
  \wordexample{sensitive to criticism（对批评敏感的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{sensory}{ˈsensəri}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SENT, SENS}
  \wordsense{a. 感觉的；传递感觉的}
  \wordfamily{sensitive}
  \wordfamily{sensual}
  \wordfamily{sensible}
  \wordfamily{sensuous}
  \wordphrase{sensory organs（感觉器官）}
  \wordphrase{sensory nerves（感觉神经）}
  \wordexample{sensory organs（感觉器官）}
  \wordexample{sensory nerves（感觉神经）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{sensual}{ˈsenʃuəl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SENT, SENS}
  \wordsense{a. 肉体的；性感的；沉溺于感官享受的，耽于酒色的}
  \wordfamily{sensuality（好色；喜爱感官享受）}
  \wordfamily{sensualism（感觉论；肉欲主义）}
  \wordfamily{sensualist（好色之徒）}
  \wordphrase{sensual enjoyment（肉体的欢愉）}
  \wordphrase{sensual life（耽于酒色的生活）}
  \wordphrase{sensual lips（性感的嘴唇）}
  \wordexample{sensual enjoyment（肉体的欢愉）}
  \wordexample{sensual life（耽于酒色的生活）}
  \wordexample{sensual lips（性感的嘴唇）}

\begin{word}[**]{sensuous}{ˈsenʃuəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SENT, SENS}
  \wordsense{a. 感观的；给感官以快乐的}
  \wordfamily{sensual}
  \wordfamily{sensibility}
  \wordfamily{sensory}
  \wordfamily{sensation}
  \wordphrase{sensuous music（悦耳的音乐）}
  \wordphrase{sensuous paintings（悦目的绘画）}
  \wordexample{sensuous music（悦耳的音乐）}
  \wordexample{sensuous paintings（悦目的绘画）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{sensibility}{ˌsensəˈbɪləti}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SENT, SENS}
  \wordsense{n. 感觉（力）；敏感}
  \wordfamily{sensuous}
  \wordfamily{sensation}
  \wordfamily{sensual}
  \wordfamily{sensational}
  \wordphrase{sensibility of a poet（诗人的敏感）}
  \wordexample{sensibility of a poet（诗人的敏感）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{sensation}{senˈseiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SENT, SENS}
  \wordfamily{assent（to + sth 同意）；consensus（一致）；dissent（不赞成）}
  \wordsense{n. 引起轰动的事件，轰动效应；感觉（力）}
  \wordphrase{create a great sensation（引起很大的轰动）}
  \wordexample{create a great sensation（引起很大的轰动）}

\begin{word}[**]{sensational}{senˈseiʃənl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SENT, SENS}
  \wordfamily{sensationalism（追求轰动效应）；assent（to + sth 同意）；consensus（一致）}
  \wordsense{a. 引起轰动的；《贬》耸人听闻的；企图激起感情的}
  \wordphrase{sensational murder（耸人听闻的谋杀事件）}
  \wordphrase{sensational writer（采用耸人听闻手法的作家）}
  \wordphrase{sensational newspaper（追求轰动效应的报纸）}
  \wordexample{sensational murder（耸人听闻的谋杀事件）}
  \wordexample{sensational writer（采用耸人听闻手法的作家）}
  \wordexample{sensational newspaper（追求轰动效应的报纸）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{sentient}{ˈsentiənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SENT, SENS}

  \wordfamily{sensational}

  \wordfamily{sensation}
  \wordfamily{sensibility}

  \wordphrase{be sentient（有感觉力的，有知觉力的）}
  \wordexample{be sentient（有感觉力的，有知觉力的）}

\begin{word}[***]{sentiment}{ˈsentɪmənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SENT, SENS}
  \wordfamily{assent（to + sth 同意）；consensus（一致）；dissent（不赞成）}
  \wordsense{n. 感情，情绪，心情；伤感情绪；（pl.）意见，见解}

  \wordphrase{sentiment of pity（怜悯之情）}
