\chapter{SAT[SET]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的词根 sat 意为 enough，元音弱化后变成 set。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{asset}{ˈæset}
  \wordbreakdown{在近处充分拥有 as<ad (= to, near) + set<sat (= enough)}
  \wordsense{n.（pl.）资产，财产；宝贵的人（或物）}
  \wordfamily{insatiable}
  \wordfamily{satiate}
  \wordfamily{insatiate}
  \wordfamily{satisfaction}
  \wordphrase{asset to his company}
  \wordexample{Harold has been a real asset to his company.（哈罗德一直是他们公司的骨干。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{insatiable}{ɪnˈseiʃəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{不充分的 in (= not) + sat (= enough)}
  \wordsense{a. 不知足的；贪得无厌的}
  \wordfamily{asset}
  \wordfamily{satiate}
  \wordfamily{insatiate}
  \wordfamily{satisfaction}
  \wordphrase{insatiable appetites}
  \wordexample{The addictive personality invariably has insatiable appetites.（上瘾的人都有贪得无厌的欲望。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{satiate}{ˈseiʃieit}
  \wordbreakdown{使充分 sat (= enough)}
  \wordfamily{satiable（可满足的）；asset（（pl.）资产）；insatiable（不知足的）}
  \wordsense{vt. 使饱足；（因为太多而）使厌腻}
  \wordphrase{satiate me I'm a}
  \wordexample{Just a salad and crackers tend to satiate me. I'm a light eater.（我要一份沙拉和饼干就够了，我的胃口不大。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{insatiate}{ɪnˈseiʃiət}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SAT[SET]}
  \wordfamily{satisfaction；satisfactory；satisfy；saturate；asset（（pl.）资产）}
  \wordsense{a. 不知足的 *in (= not)}
  \wordphrase{be insatiate（不知足的）}
  \wordexample{be insatiate（不知足的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{satisfaction}{ˌsætɪsˈfækʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SAT[SET]}
  \wordfamily{insatiate；satisfactory；satisfy；saturate；asset（（pl.）资产）}
  \wordsense{n. 满足；乐事；赔偿 *fact (= make)}
  \wordphrase{a satisfaction（满足）}
  \wordexample{a satisfaction（满足）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{satisfactory}{ˌsætɪsˈfæktəri}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SAT[SET]}
  \wordfamily{satisfactorily（令人满意地）；asset（（pl.）资产）；insatiable（不知足的）}
  \wordsense{a. 令人满意的 *fact (= make)}
  \wordphrase{be satisfactory（令人满意的）}
  \wordexample{be satisfactory（令人满意的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{satisfy}{ˈsætɪsfaɪ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SAT[SET]}
  \wordsense{v. 使满意，使满足；使确信}
  \wordfamily{satisfying（令人满意的）}
  \wordfamily{to satisfy（使满意，使满足）}
  \wordphrase{to satisfy（使满意，使满足）}
  \wordexample{to satisfy（使满意，使满足）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{saturate}{ˈsætʃəreit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SAT[SET]}
  \wordsense{v. 浸透；（被湿气或潮湿的东西）浸湿；使饱享；充满；饱和}
  \wordfamily{satisfy}
  \wordfamily{saturation}
  \wordfamily{satisfactory}
  \wordfamily{satisfaction}
  \wordphrase{saturated with sunshine（尽情享受阳光的）}
  \wordphrase{saturated with learning（专心于学问的）}
  \wordphrase{saturated solution of salt（饱和盐溶液）}
  \wordexample{saturated with sunshine（尽情享受阳光的）}
  \wordexample{saturated with learning（专心于学问的）}
  \wordexample{saturated solution of salt（饱和盐溶液）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{saturation}{ˌsætʃəˈreiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SAT[SET]}
  \wordfamily{asset（（pl.）资产）；insatiable（不知足的）；satiate（使饱足）}
  \wordsense{n. 浸透；饱和（状态）}
  \wordphrase{saturation bombing（【军】饱和轰炸）}
  \wordphrase{saturation point（饱和点）}
  \wordphrase{st，stat，stit，sist 等。}
  \wordexample{saturation bombing（【军】饱和轰炸）}
  \wordexample{saturation point（饱和点）}
\end{word}



\chapter{SED, SESS[SID]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的词根 sed，sess 意为 to sit，元音弱化后变成 sid。反义词根有来自拉丁语的 st，stat，stit，sist 等。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{assiduous}{əˈsidjuəs}
  \wordbreakdown{一直坐着 as<ad (= to, intensive) + sid (= sit)}
  \wordsense{a. 勤奋的；周到的，竭诚的；一丝不苟的}
  \wordfamily{assiduity 勤奋，刻苦}
  \wordfamily{assiduously 勤奋地，刻苦地}
  \wordphrase{assiduous attention}
  \wordexample{Her assiduous attention to detail makes her a good editor.（她对细节一丝不苟的关注使她成为一名优秀的编辑。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{dissident}{ˈdisidənt}
  \wordbreakdown{分开坐 dis (= apart) + sid (= sit)}
  \wordfamily{dissidence（异议）；assiduous（勤奋的）；insidious（阴险的）}
  \wordsense{n. 持不同政见者；不赞成者}
  \wordphrase{the dissident}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of dissident.（此处展示 dissident 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{a. 持不同政见的；不赞成的}
  \wordphrase{dissident views}
  \wordexample{He isn't shy about expressing his habitually dissident views.（他从不怯于发表自己常持有的不同见解。）}
  \wordexample{dissident views}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{insidious}{inˈsidjəs}
  \wordbreakdown{坐在里面的 in (= in) + sid (= sit)}
  \wordsense{a. 阴险的；狡猾的；（疾病等）不知不觉间加剧的；潜伏的}
  \wordfamily{dissident}
  \wordfamily{residue}
  \wordfamily{assiduous}
  \wordfamily{subsidiary}
  \wordphrase{insidious and tend}
  \wordexample{Ugly rumors are insidious and tend to acquire a life of their own.（可恶的谣言不知不觉间愈演愈烈，而且有愈演愈真的倾向。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{residue}{ˈrezidju:}
  \wordbreakdown{重新、继续坐着 re (= again) + sid (= sit)}
  \wordsense{n. 剩余，残余；残余物；【律】剩余财产}
  \wordfamily{residual 剩余的，残余的}
  \wordfamily{residuary 残余的；剩余财产的}
  \wordphrase{residue that keeps}
  \wordexample{The insecticide leaves a residue that keeps on killing 2 weeks.（杀虫剂的残余物在两周内将继续起到杀虫作用。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{subsidiary}{səbˈsidiəri}
  \wordbreakdown{坐在下面 sub (= under) + sid (= sit)}
  \wordfamily{subsidize（给……补贴）；subsidy（补贴）；subsidiaries；assiduous（勤奋的）}
  \wordsense{a. 辅助的；附属的；津贴的}
  \wordphrase{be subsidiary}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of subsidiary.（此处展示 subsidiary 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 子公司}
  \wordphrase{subsidiaries}
  \wordexample{Multinational companies have a host of well-known subsidiaries.（跨国公司有许多著名的子公司。）}
  \wordexample{subsidiaries}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{supersede}{ˌsu:pəˈsi:d}
  \wordbreakdown{从上往下坐下 super (= over) + sed (= sit)}
  \wordsense{vt. 代替；取代}
  \wordfamily{supersession 取代}
  \wordfamily{AE-400X 取代}
  \wordfamily{AE-200。）}
  \wordphrase{supersedes Model AE-200}
  \wordexample{Model AE-400X supersedes Model AE-200.（模型}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{sedentary}{ˈsedntri}
  \wordbreakdown{与坐着的状态相关联的 sed (= sit) + tary (= relating to)}
  \wordsense{a. （工作等需要）坐着的；久坐的}
  \wordfamily{supersede}
  \wordfamily{assess}
  \wordfamily{subsidiary}
  \wordfamily{obsession}
  \wordphrase{sedentary lifestyle tend}
  \wordexample{People with a sedentary lifestyle tend to suffer from obesity.（有久坐习惯的人容易肥胖。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{assess}{əˈses}
  \wordbreakdown{靠近坐 as<ad (= to, near) + sess (= sit)}
  \wordfamily{assessment（评估）；assessor（评估员）；assiduous（勤奋的）}
  \wordsense{vt. sb/sth + at + sth 评估（财产）的价值；征（税）；处（罚金）}
  \wordphrase{assess the worth}
  \wordexample{I assess the worth of this pearl necklace at \$8,000.（这条珍珠项链我估价为 8000 美元。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{obsession}{əbˈseʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{妨碍地坐着 ob (= in the way) + sess (= sit)}
  \wordsense{n. 着魔；迷住}
  \wordfamily{obsessive 使人着迷的}
  \wordfamily{obsess（妄想、固有观念等）萦绕心头，不停地困扰}
  \wordphrase{obsession}
  \wordexample{Eating is my hobby. Shopping is my obsession!（吃东西是我的兴趣，购物让我着迷！）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{preside}{priˈzaid}
  \wordbreakdown{词根说明 pre (= before)}
  \wordsense{v. 担任会议主席，主持；掌管，统辖}
  \wordfamily{presider（主持者；主宰者）}
  \wordfamily{presiding officer（主持会议的官员）}
  \wordfamily{presiding judge（庭长，首席法官）}
  \wordfamily{to preside（担任会议主席，主持）}
  \wordphrase{to preside（担任会议主席，主持）}
  \wordexample{to preside（担任会议主席，主持）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{presidential}{ˌpreziˈdenʃl}

\chapter{BAS}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的词根 bas 意为 lower，bottom。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{abase}{əˈbeis}
  \wordbreakdown{降低到 a<ad (= to) + bas (= lower)}
  \wordfamily{abasement（屈尊）；debase（降低）；base（基部）}
  \wordsense{vt. 降低……的地位、身份、品格、名誉等}
  \wordphrase{abase yourself}
  \wordexample{Don't abase yourself by begging to get your job back.（不要为了要回工作而卑躬屈膝。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{debase}{diˈbeis}
  \wordbreakdown{使完全走向底部 de (= down, intensive) + bas (= bottom, lower)}
  \wordfamily{abase（降低……的地位、身份、品格、名誉等）；base（基部）；baseless（没有理由的）}
  \wordsense{vt. 降低，贬低（价值、品质、人格等）}
  \wordphrase{debase the Islamic faith}
  \wordexample{Many people feel that terrorists debase the Islamic faith.（许多人认为恐怖分子有损伊斯兰教信仰。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{base}{beis}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 BAS}
  \wordfamily{basement；basic；basis；basin；abase（降低……的地位、身份、品格、名誉等）}
  \wordsense{n. 基部；基础；基地；底边，底面；（棒球）垒}
  \wordphrase{the base}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of base.（此处展示 base 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. 以……为基础；把……建立在某种基础上}
  \wordphrase{base the issue}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of base.（此处展示 base 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{a. 不值钱的；卑劣的；不纯的}
  \wordphrase{air/naval base（空军/海军基地）}
  \wordphrase{base of operations（作战基地）}
  \wordphrase{base camp（探险队等的营地）}
  \wordexample{air/naval base（空军/海军基地）}
  \wordexample{base of operations（作战基地）}
  \wordexample{base camp（探险队等的营地）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{baseless}{ˈbeisləs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根说明 less (= without)}
  \wordsense{a. 没有理由的，没有根据的}
  \wordfamily{base}
  \wordfamily{basement}
  \wordfamily{debase}
  \wordfamily{basic}
  \wordphrase{baseless fears（莫名其妙的恐惧）}
  \wordexample{baseless fears（莫名其妙的恐惧）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{basement}{ˈbeismənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 BAS}
  \wordsense{n. 地下室}
  \wordfamily{basic}
  \wordfamily{base}
  \wordfamily{basis}
  \wordfamily{basin}
  \wordfamily{a basement（地下室）}
  \wordphrase{a basement（地下室）}
  \wordexample{a basement（地下室）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{basic}{ˈbeisik}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 BAS}
  \wordfamily{abase（降低……的地位、身份、品格、名誉等）；debase（降低）；base（基部）}
  \wordsense{a. 基础的；基本的}
  \wordphrase{basic principles（基本原则）}
  \wordphrase{Basic English（基础英语）}
  \wordexample{basic principles（基本原则）}
  \wordexample{Basic English（基础英语）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{basis}{ˈbeisis}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 BAS}
  \wordsense{n. 基础，根基，基部；基本；主要成分；最重要的部分}
  \wordfamily{basic}
  \wordfamily{basin}
  \wordfamily{basement}
  \wordfamily{base}
  \wordphrase{on a solid basis（在稳固的基础上）}
  \wordphrase{on an equal basis（在平等的基础之上）}
  \wordphrase{on the basis of（以……为基础）}
  \wordexample{on a solid basis（在稳固的基础上）}
  \wordexample{on an equal basis（在平等的基础之上）}
  \wordexample{on the basis of（以……为基础）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{basin}{ˈbeisn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 BAS}
  \wordfamily{abase（降低……的地位、身份、品格、名誉等）；debase（降低）；base（基部）}
  \wordsense{n. 水罐；盆；（喷泉等的）水池；（被陆地包围的）内湾，内港；船坞；盆地；流域}
  \wordphrase{a basin of water（一盆水）}
  \wordphrase{the Thames basin（泰晤士河流域）}
  \wordexample{a basin of water（一盆水）}
  \wordexample{the Thames basin（泰晤士河流域）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{bastard}{ˈba:stəd}
  \wordbreakdown{词根说明 ard (= person who)}
  \wordfamily{bastardy（私生）；abase（降低……的地位、身份、品格、名誉等）；debase（降低）}
  \wordsense{n. 庶子；私生子；家伙，坏蛋}
  \wordphrase{the bastard}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of bastard.（此处展示 bastard 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{a. 假冒的}
  \wordphrase{bastard child/son/daughter（私生子/私生女）}
  \wordphrase{bastard charity（伪善）}
  \wordphrase{to sing，song，元音弱化后变成}
  \wordexample{You heartless bastard!（你这个无情的家伙！）}
  \wordexample{bastard child/son/daughter（私生子/私生女）}
  \wordexample{bastard charity（伪善）}
\end{word}



\chapter{CENT, CANT[CHANT, CHANS]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的词根 cent，cant 意为 to sing，song，元音弱化后变成 chant，chans。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{accentuate}{əkˈsentʃueit}
  \wordbreakdown{用非常大的声音歌唱 ac<ad (= to, intensive) + cent (= sing)}
  \wordsense{vt. 重读；强调；使突出}
  \wordfamily{accentuation 重读；强调}
  \wordfamily{accent 重音；（v.）[əkˈsent] 加重音符号}
  \wordphrase{accentuate the deep blue}
  \wordexample{Your earrings accentuate the deep blue of your eyes.（你的耳环更加突出了你深蓝色的眼睛。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{enchant}{inˈtʃa:nt}
  \wordbreakdown{在里边歌唱 en (= in) + chant<cant (= sing)}
  \wordsense{vt. 使陶醉；使悦；对……施魔法}
  \wordfamily{enchantment 着迷；着魔}
  \wordfamily{enchanter 魔法师；使人着迷的人}
  \wordfamily{enchantress 女巫；迷人的女人}
  \wordfamily{enchanting 令人着迷的}
  \wordfamily{enchanted 入迷的，着魔的}
  \wordfamily{enchantingly 入迷地}
  \wordphrase{enchant my children}
  \wordexample{Fairy tales with trolls always enchant my children.（出现侏儒或巨人的童话总是能让我的孩子们很着迷。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{incentive}{inˈsentiv}
  \wordbreakdown{在里面唱歌 in (= in, on) + cent (= sing)}
  \wordsense{n. 刺激；鼓励；动机}
  \wordfamily{enchant}
  \wordfamily{recant}
  \wordfamily{accentuate}
  \wordfamily{chanson}
  \wordphrase{incentive to sell more}
  \wordexample{Salespeople usually receive commissions as an incentive to sell more.（销售人员常常收取回扣，这能刺激他们卖得更多。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{recant}{riˈkænt}
  \wordbreakdown{向后、使消失地唱歌 re (= back, away) + cant (= sing)}
  \wordfamily{recantation（撤回）；accentuate（重读）；enchant（使陶醉）}
  \wordsense{v. 撤回；放弃}
  \wordphrase{recanted her allegiance}
  \wordexample{After sessions with a deprogrammer, Iris recanted her allegiance to the cult.（和说服她放弃被灌输的思想的人见了几次面之后，艾里斯放弃了对异教的效忠。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{chanson}{ʃɔŋˈsɔŋ}
  \wordbreakdown{chans<cant (= sing)}
  \wordsense{n. 《法》歌曲}
  \wordfamily{recant}
  \wordfamily{chant}
  \wordfamily{incentive}
  \wordfamily{descant}
  \wordfamily{a chanson（《法》歌曲）}
  \wordphrase{a chanson（《法》歌曲）}
  \wordexample{a chanson（《法》歌曲）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{chant}{tʃa:nt}
  \wordbreakdown{chant<cant (= sing)}
  \wordfamily{chanson；descant；recant；incantation；accentuate（重读）}
  \wordsense{n. 歌曲；颂歌；吟唱}
  \wordphrase{a chant（歌曲）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "a chant".（常见搭配：歌曲。）}
  \wordsense{v. 唱（歌曲、颂歌）；吟唱}
  \wordphrase{a chant（歌曲）}
  \wordexample{a chant（歌曲）}
  \wordexample{to chant（唱（歌曲、颂歌））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{descant}{ˈdeskænt}
  \wordbreakdown{des<dis (= away, apart)}
  \wordfamily{chant；incantation；chanson；precentor；accentuate（重读）}
  \wordsense{n. 高音部（歌唱或演奏中高于主旋律的装饰性曲调或对位音）；详述}
  \wordphrase{to descant（详谈，详述）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "to descant".（常见搭配：详谈，详述。）}
  \wordsense{v. 详谈，详述}
  \wordphrase{to descant（详谈，详述}
  \wordexample{to descant（详谈，详述}
  \wordexample{a descant（高音部（歌唱或演奏中高于主旋律的装饰性曲调或对位音））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{incantation}{ˌinkænˈteiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根说明 in (= in)}
  \wordfamily{increase；accentuate（重读）；enchant（使陶醉）}
  \wordsense{n. 咒语；魔法}
  \wordphrase{an incantation（咒语）}
  \wordexample{an incantation（咒语）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{precentor}{priˈsentə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根说明 pre (= before)}
  \wordfamily{presidium；presidency；presidential；preside；cre（具有）；accentuate（重读）}
  \wordsense{n. （教堂圣歌的）领唱人}
  \wordphrase{to grow，to increase 等含义，元音弱化后变成}
  \wordphrase{to separate，to observe 的}
  \wordexample{to grow，to increase 等含义，元音弱化后变成}
  \wordexample{to separate，to observe 的}
\end{word}



\chapter{CRE[CRU]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 cre 具有 to grow，to increase 等含义，元音弱化后变成 cru。此外，意为 to separate，to observe 的 cret/creet/cre/cern 和此词根形态相似，需加以注意。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}{accrue}{əˈkru:}
  \wordbreakdown{更加增多 ac<ad (= to, add) + cru<cre (= grow, increase)}
  \wordfamily{accretion（增大，添加）；decrease（减少）；increment（增加）}
  \wordsense{vi. to + sb（通过自然增长、积累）产生；（利息等）自然积累而成}
  \wordphrase{accrues but just 4\%}
  \wordexample{Interest accrues, but just 4\% annually.（虽然也生息，但每年只有 4\%。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{decrease}{diˈkri:s}
  \wordbreakdown{对“增大、变大”的轻微否定 de (= reverse) + cre (= grow)}
  \wordfamily{decreasingly（逐渐减少地）；accrue（to + sb（通过自然增长、积累）产生）；increment（增加）}
  \wordsense{v. 减少；降低}
  \wordphrase{decrease the issue}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of decrease.（此处展示 decrease 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 减少；降低}
  \wordphrase{decrease property tax rates}
  \wordexample{The senate voted to decrease property tax rates yesterday.（参议院昨天投票决定降低财产税率。）}
  \wordexample{decrease property tax rates}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{increment}{ˈinkrəmənt}
  \wordbreakdown{在里面增长 in (= in, on) + cre (= grow)}
  \wordsense{n. 增加；增额}
  \wordfamily{decrease}
  \wordfamily{concrete}
  \wordfamily{accrue}
  \wordfamily{crescent}
  \wordphrase{increments until a mob}
  \wordexample{The street protest grew by increments until a mob had formed.（街上抗议的队伍不断扩大，最终演变成一场暴动。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{concrete}{ˈkɔnkri:t}
  \wordbreakdown{词根说明 con<com (= together)}
  \wordfamily{accrue（to + sb（通过自然增长、积累）产生）；decrease（减少）；increment（增加）}
  \wordsense{a. 具体的；实在的；有形的}
  \wordphrase{be concrete（具体的）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "be concrete".（常见搭配：具体的。）}
  \wordsense{n. 混凝土}
  \wordphrase{be concrete（具体的）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "be concrete".（常见搭配：具体的。）}
  \wordsense{v. 凝结；使凝固；用混凝土浇筑}
  \wordphrase{concrete proposals/evidence（具体的提案/确凿的证据）}
  \wordphrase{concrete jungle（水泥丛林（指竞争激烈、弱肉强食的城市））}
  \wordexample{concrete proposals/evidence（具体的提案/确凿的证据）}
  \wordexample{concrete jungle（水泥丛林（指竞争激烈、弱肉强食的城市））}
  \wordexample{be concrete（具体的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{crescent}{ˈkresnt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根说明 sc<esc (= becoming)}
  \wordsense{n. 新月}
  \wordfamily{concrete}
  \wordfamily{decrement}
  \wordfamily{increment}
  \wordfamily{increase}
  \wordfamily{a crescent（新月）}
  \wordphrase{a crescent（新月）}
  \wordexample{a crescent（新月）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{decrement}{ˈdekrimənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根说明 de (= down)}
  \wordsense{n. 减少（量）}
  \wordfamily{crescent}
  \wordfamily{increase}
  \wordfamily{concrete}
  \wordfamily{recruit}
  \wordfamily{a decrement（减少（量））}
  \wordphrase{a decrement（减少（量））}
  \wordexample{a decrement（减少（量））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{increase}{ˈinkri:s}
  \wordbreakdown{词根说明 in (= in)}
  \wordfamily{increasingly（渐增地，愈加）；accrue（to + sb（通过自然增长、积累）产生）；decrease（减少）}
  \wordsense{n. 增加}
  \wordphrase{an increase（增加）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "an increase".（常见搭配：增加。）}
  \wordsense{v. 增加，增大，增长}
  \wordphrase{an increase（增加）}
  \wordexample{an increase（增加）}
  \wordexample{to increase（增加，增大，增长）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{recruit}{riˈkru:t}
  \wordbreakdown{词根说明 re (= again)}
  \wordfamily{recruitment；residential；resident；residence；accrue（to + sb（通过自然增长、积累）产生）}
  \wordsense{v. 征募新兵；招收新会员或新职员；补充；恢复（体力等）}
  \wordphrase{to recruit（征募新兵）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "to recruit".（常见搭配：征募新兵。）}
  \wordsense{n. 征募新兵；招收，招生；补充；恢复体力}
  \wordphrase{to recruit（征募新兵）}
  \wordexample{to recruit（征募新兵）}
  \wordexample{a recruit（征募新兵）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{recruitment}{riˈkru:tmənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根说明 re (= again)}
  \wordfamily{recruit；residential；resident；residence；accrue（to + sb（通过自然增长、积累）产生）}
  \wordsense{n. 征募新兵；招收，招聘；补充}
  \wordphrase{recruitment advertising（招聘广告）}
  \wordphrase{recruitment training center（新兵训练所）}
  \wordphrase{dic / dict, locut / loqu, nounc / nunci, parl 等和来自希腊语的}
  \wordexample{recruitment advertising（招聘广告）}
  \wordexample{recruitment training center（新兵训练所）}
\end{word}



\chapter{FA,FAM,FESS}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 fa, fam, fess 意为 to say, to talk。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 dic / dict, locut / loqu, nounc / nunci, parl 等和来自希腊语的 phras, pha / phe / phu。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{affable}{ˈæfəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{靠近说话的 af<ad (= to, near) + fa (= say)}
  \wordfamily{affability（易于接近，亲切）；ineffable（说不出的）；prefatory（序言的）}
  \wordsense{a. 和蔼的，易于接近的，亲切的}
  \wordphrase{affable gentleman}
  \wordexample{My grandfather is a very affable gentleman.（我爷爷是个非常和蔼可亲的人。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{ineffable}{in'efəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{不能向外说的 in (= not) + ef<ex (= out) + fa (= say)}
  \wordsense{a. 说不出的；难以形容的}
  \wordfamily{affable}
  \wordfamily{prefatory}
  \wordfamily{defamation}
  \wordfamily{infamous}
  \wordphrase{ineffable joy}
  \wordexample{Nothing compares to the ineffable joy of listening to a Mozart symphony.（听莫扎特交响乐给人带来的愉悦不可言传，无与伦比。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{prefatory}{'prefətəri}
  \wordbreakdown{先说的 pre (= before) + fa (= say)}
  \wordfamily{preface（序言，前言）；affable（和蔼的）；ineffable（说不出的）}
  \wordsense{a. 序言的，前言的；位于前面的}
  \wordphrase{prefatory remarks}
  \wordexample{Before giving my speech, I'd like to make a few prefatory remarks.（在开始演讲之前，我想先说几句开场白。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{defamation}{ˌdefə'meiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{贬低地说某人 de (= down) + fam (= say)}
  \wordfamily{defame（诽谤）；defamatory（中伤的）；affable（和蔼的）}
  \wordsense{n. 诽谤，中伤}
  \wordphrase{defamation in the tabloid}
  \wordexample{Movie stars are often the victims of defamation in the tabloid press.（电影明星往往成为小报新闻中诽谤的对象。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{infamous}{'infəməs}
  \wordbreakdown{无法言说的 in (= not) + fa (= say)}
  \wordsense{a. 声名狼藉的，臭名昭著的，口碑不好的；邪恶的；无耻的}
  \wordfamily{infamy 声名狼藉；丑行，恶行}
  \wordphrase{infamous surprised attack}
  \wordexample{America will probably never forget Japan's infamous surprised attack on Pearl Harbor.（也许美国永远不会忘记臭名昭著的日本突袭珍珠港事件。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{profess}{prə'fes}
  \wordbreakdown{在众人面前吐露 pro (= forth) + fess (= say)}
  \wordsense{vt. 公开宣称，自称，冒充；把……作为职业}
  \wordfamily{profession 职业；宣布}
  \wordfamily{professionalism 专家气质；内行气派}
  \wordfamily{professor 教授}
  \wordfamily{professional 职业的；专家的；职业选手；专家；以特定职业谋生的人（如职业歌手、舞蹈家等）}
  \wordfamily{professed 公然的；冒充的}
  \wordfamily{professionally 职业地，专业地}
  \wordfamily{professedly 公然地；冒充地}
  \wordfamily{professes confidence}
  \wordphrase{professes confidence}
  \wordexample{The president professes confidence in the nation's economic recovery.（总统公开表示了对国家经济复苏的信心。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{confabulate}{kən'fæbjuleit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FA,FAM,FESS}
  \wordfamily{confabulation（谈论）；affable（和蔼的）；ineffable（说不出的）}
  \wordsense{v. 谈论 *con<com (= together, with)}
  \wordphrase{to confabulate（谈论）}
  \wordexample{to confabulate（谈论）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{fable}{'feibl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FA,FAM,FESS}
  \wordfamily{affable（和蔼的）；ineffable（说不出的）；prefatory（序言的）}
  \wordsense{n. 寓言；传说；神话}
  \wordphrase{a fable（寓言）}
  \wordexample{a fable（寓言）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{fabulous}{'fæbjələs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FA,FAM,FESS}
  \wordfamily{affable（和蔼的）；ineffable（说不出的）；prefatory（序言的）}
  \wordsense{a. 传说中的；难以置信的；惊人的}
  \wordphrase{fabulous heroes（传说中的英雄）}
  \wordphrase{fabulous wealth（巨额财富）}
  \wordexample{fabulous heroes（传说中的英雄）}
  \wordexample{fabulous wealth（巨额财富）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{infancy}{'infənsi}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FA,FAM,FESS}
  \wordfamily{fabulous；infant；fable；infantile；affable（和蔼的）}
  \wordsense{n. 婴儿期；（发展、成长的）初期 *in (= not)}
  \wordphrase{infancy of a nation（国家发展初期）}
  \wordexample{infancy of a nation（国家发展初期）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{infant}{'infənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FA,FAM,FESS}
  \wordfamily{infancy；infantile；fabulous；infantry；affable（和蔼的）}
  \wordsense{n. 婴儿 *in (= not)}
  \wordphrase{infant voices（婴儿的噪音）}
  \wordphrase{infant food（婴儿食品）}
  \wordphrase{infant industries（新兴 / 初创行业）}
  \wordexample{infant voices（婴儿的噪音）}
  \wordexample{infant food（婴儿食品）}
  \wordexample{infant industries（新兴 / 初创行业）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{infantile}{'infəntail}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FA,FAM,FESS}
  \wordfamily{infant；infantry；infancy；nefarious；affable（和蔼的）}
  \wordsense{a. 婴儿的；幼稚的 *in (= not)}
  \wordphrase{infantile pastimes（婴儿游戏）}
  \wordexample{infantile pastimes（婴儿游戏）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{infantry}{'infəntri}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FA,FAM,FESS}
  \wordfamily{infantile；nefarious；infant；fame；affable（和蔼的）}
  \wordsense{n. 【总称，用做单数】步兵 *in (= not)}
  \wordphrase{two regiments of infantry（两个步兵团）}
  \wordexample{two regiments of infantry（两个步兵团）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{nefarious}{ni'feəriəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FA,FAM,FESS}
  \wordfamily{infantry；fame；infantile；confess；affable（和蔼的）}
  \wordsense{a. 恶毒的，邪恶的 *ne (= not)}
  \wordphrase{nefarious activities（恶毒的行为）}
  \wordphrase{two regiments of infantry（两个步兵团）}
  \wordexample{nefarious activities（恶毒的行为）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{fame}{feim}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FA,FAM,FESS}
  \wordsense{n. 名誉；名声}
  \wordfamily{nefarious}
  \wordfamily{confess}
  \wordfamily{infantry}
  \wordfamily{confession}
  \wordfamily{a fame（名誉）}
  \wordphrase{a fame（名誉）}
  \wordexample{a fame（名誉）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{confess}{kən'fes}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FA,FAM,FESS}
  \wordfamily{fame；confession；nefarious；infantry；affable（和蔼的）}
  \wordsense{v. 坦白，供认，承认；表明信仰 *con<com (= together, with)}
  \wordphrase{to confess（坦白，供认，承认）}
  \wordexample{to confess（坦白，供认，承认）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{confession}{kən'feʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FA,FAM,FESS}
  \wordfamily{affable（和蔼的）；ineffable（说不出的）；prefatory（序言的）}
  \wordsense{n. 从实招认，供认；（信仰等的）告白 *con<com (= together)}
  \wordphrase{confession of faith（信仰告白）}
  \wordexample{confession of faith（信仰告白）}
\end{word}



\chapter{GRAND}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 grand 意为 great, large。同义词根有来自希腊语的 coloss, magn, maha, mega / megalo, metro 和来自拉丁语的 maj, max 等。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{aggrandize}{ə'grændaiz}
  \wordbreakdown{使更大 ag<ad (= to, add) + grand (= great)}
  \wordsense{vt. 增加，扩大，强化（权力、财产、地位等）；夸张}
  \wordfamily{aggrandizement 扩大；提高；强化；夸张}
  \wordphrase{aggrandize himself before his}
  \wordexample{He seeks to aggrandize himself before his superiors to advance his career.（他为了晋升而在上司面前过分地夸大自己。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{grand}{grænd}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 GRAND}
  \wordfamily{grandly（宏伟地）；aggrandize（增加）；grandeur（伟大）}
  \wordsense{a. 雄伟的，壮丽的；高贵的；愉快的；总的；完全的；崇高的}
  \wordphrase{grand jury（大陪审团）}
  \wordphrase{Grand Canyon（大峡谷（美国亚利桑那州科罗拉多河的大峡谷））}
  \wordphrase{Grand Old Party（老大党，指美国共和党，缩写：GOP）}
  \wordexample{grand jury（大陪审团）}
  \wordexample{Grand Canyon（大峡谷（美国亚利桑那州科罗拉多河的大峡谷））}
  \wordexample{Grand Old Party（老大党，指美国共和党，缩写：GOP）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{grandeur}{'grændʒə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 GRAND}
  \wordsense{n. 伟大，庄严；壮丽，壮观}
  \wordfamily{grand}
  \wordfamily{grandiloquent}
  \wordfamily{grandiose}
  \wordphrase{the grandeur of Napoleon Bonaparte（拿破仑·波拿巴的伟大）}
  \wordphrase{the grandeur of the Swiss Alps（瑞士阿尔卑斯山的壮观）}
  \wordphrase{the grandeur of his nature（他高贵的气质）}
  \wordexample{the grandeur of Napoleon Bonaparte（拿破仑·波拿巴的伟大）}
  \wordexample{the grandeur of the Swiss Alps（瑞士阿尔卑斯山的壮观）}
  \wordexample{the grandeur of his nature（他高贵的气质）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{grandiloquent}{ˌgræn'diləkwənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 GRAND}
  \wordfamily{grandiloquence（夸张之言）；aggrandize（增加）；grand（雄伟的）}
  \wordsense{a. 夸张的，夸大的；大言不断的 *loqu (= say)}
  \wordphrase{grandiloquent speaker（夸夸其谈的人）}
  \wordphrase{written in a grandiloquent style（以夸张的风格写作）}
  \wordexample{grandiloquent speaker（夸夸其谈的人）}
  \wordexample{written in a grandiloquent style（以夸张的风格写作）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{grandiose}{'grændiəus}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 GRAND}
  \wordfamily{grandiloquent；grandeur；grand；aggrandize（增加）}
  \wordsense{a. 宏伟的；浮夸的}
  \wordphrase{be grandiose（宏伟的）}
  \wordexample{be grandiose（宏伟的）}
\end{word}



\chapter{PROB, PROV}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 prob, prov 意为 to test, to try, to examine, good, proper。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{approbation}{ˌæprə'beiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{让某人尝试 ap<ad (= to) + prob (= try, test, good)}
  \wordfamily{approbate（认可）；probity（清廉）；reprove（责备）}
  \wordsense{n. 许可；认可；承认；赞赏}
  \wordphrase{the approbation}
  \wordexample{Most children behave well after receiving approbation.（大多数孩子都会在受到表扬后变得很乖。）}
  \wordexample{Most children behave well after receiving}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{probity}{ˈprəubiti}
  \wordbreakdown{经历考验的或优秀的性质 prob (= test, good, proper)}
  \wordsense{n. 清廉，高洁；诚实}
  \wordfamily{approbation}
  \wordfamily{reprove}
  \wordfamily{reprobate}
  \wordfamily{probe}
  \wordphrase{probity}
  \wordexample{My father is a man of unquestioned probity.（毫无疑问，我父亲是个诚实的人。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{reprove}{ri'pru:v}
  \wordbreakdown{重新进行试验 re (= again) + prov (= test)}
  \wordsense{vt. 责备，指责}
  \wordfamily{reproof 非难，指责，责备}
  \wordphrase{The judge reproved Microsoft}
  \wordexample{The judge reproved Microsoft for its obviously monopolistic business practices.（法官指责微软有明显的行业垄断行为。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{reprobate}{'reprəbeit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PROB, PROV}
  \wordfamily{reprobation（责备，非难）；approbation（许可）；probity（清廉）}
  \wordsense{v. 责备，非难}
  \wordphrase{to reprobate（责备，非难）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "to reprobate".（常见搭配：责备，非难。）}
  \wordsense{n. 恶棍，无赖 *re (= back, again, against)}
  \wordphrase{to reprobate（责备，非难）}
  \wordexample{to reprobate（责备，非难）}
  \wordexample{a reprobate（恶棍，无赖）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{probe}{prəub}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PROB, PROV}
  \wordfamily{reprobate；probable；probability；improbable；approbation（许可）}
  \wordsense{v. 探测；（用探针）探查；彻底调查（原因等）}
  \wordphrase{to probe（探测）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "to probe".（常见搭配：探测。）}
  \wordsense{n. 探针（医生为查明伤处等而使用的工具）；调查}
  \wordphrase{to probe（探测）}
  \wordexample{to probe（探测）}
  \wordexample{a probe（探针（医生为查明伤处等而使用的工具））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{probable}{'prɔbəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PROB, PROV}
  \wordfamily{probably（也许）；approbation（许可）；probity（清廉）}
  \wordsense{a. 可能会发生的}
  \wordphrase{be probable}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of probable.（此处展示 probable 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 很可能会被选中的人，可能获胜的人}
  \wordphrase{probable winner（可能获胜的人）}
  \wordphrase{probable result（可能出现的结果）}
  \wordexample{probable winner（可能获胜的人）}
  \wordexample{probable result（可能出现的结果）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{probability}{ˌprɔbə'biləti}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PROB, PROV}
  \wordsense{n. 可能性，或然性；可能出现的结果}
  \wordfamily{probable}
  \wordfamily{improbable}
  \wordfamily{probe}
  \wordfamily{probate}
  \wordfamily{a probability（可能性，或然性）}
  \wordphrase{a probability（可能性，或然性）}
  \wordexample{a probability（可能性，或然性）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{improbable}{im'prɔbəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PROB, PROV}
  \wordfamily{improbability（不可能有（或发生），不可能发生的事）；approbation（许可）；probity（清廉）}
  \wordsense{a. 不可能的，不可能有（或发生）的 *im<in (= not)}
  \wordphrase{be improbable（不可能的，不可能有（或发生）的）}
  \wordexample{be improbable（不可能的，不可能有（或发生）的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{probate}{'prəubeit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PROB, PROV}
  \wordfamily{improbable；probation；probability；probationer；approbation（许可）}
  \wordsense{n. 遗嘱的验证；经验证的遗嘱}
  \wordphrase{a probate（遗嘱的验证）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "a probate".（常见搭配：遗嘱的验证。）}
  \wordsense{v. 验证（遗嘱）}
  \wordphrase{a probate（遗嘱的验证）}
  \wordexample{a probate（遗嘱的验证）}
  \wordexample{to probate（验证（遗嘱））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{probation}{prə'beiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PROB, PROV}
  \wordsense{n. 试用，见习；见习期；试用期；鉴定；缓刑}
  \wordfamily{probationary（试用的；见习的；缓刑的）}
  \wordphrase{officer on probation（见习期公务员）}
  \wordphrase{three year's probation under suspended sentence of one year's imprisonment（一年徒刑，缓刑三年）}
  \wordphrase{probation system（缓刑制）}
  \wordphrase{probation officer（缓刑监护官）}
  \wordexample{officer on probation（见习期公务员）}
  \wordexample{three year's probation under suspended sentence of one year's imprisonment（一年徒刑，缓刑三年）}
  \wordexample{probation system（缓刑制）}
  \wordexample{probation officer（缓刑监护官）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{probationer}{prə'beiʃnə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PROB, PROV}
  \wordsense{n. 见习生；试用人员；缓刑犯}
  \wordfamily{probation}
  \wordfamily{approve}
  \wordfamily{probate}
  \wordfamily{approval}
  \wordfamily{a probationer（见习生）}
  \wordphrase{a probationer（见习生）}
  \wordexample{a probationer（见习生）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{approve}{ə'pru:v}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PROB, PROV}
  \wordfamily{probationer；approval；probation；disprove；approbation（许可）}
  \wordsense{v. 认可，赞成，同意；批准；满意 *ap<ad (= to)}
  \wordphrase{to approve（认可，赞成，同意）}
  \wordexample{to approve（认可，赞成，同意）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{approval}{ə'pru:vəl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PROB, PROV}
  \wordfamily{approve；disprove；probationer；disproof；approbation（许可）}
  \wordsense{n. 认可，赞成；同意；满意 *ap<ad (= to)}
  \wordphrase{goods on approval（供试用的货物（不满意可以退货））}
  \wordexample{goods on approval（供试用的货物（不满意可以退货））}
\end{word}

  \wordsense{v. 证明……不正确；反驳 *dis (= away, apart)}
  \wordphrase{to disprove（证明……不正确）}
  \wordexample{to disprove（证明……不正确）}

\begin{word}[*]{disproof}{'dispru:f}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PROB, PROV}
  \wordfamily{disprove；improve；approval；improvement；approbation（许可）}

\chapter{SCRIB, SCRIPT}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 scrib, script 意为 to write。同义词根有来自希腊语的 graph, gram。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{ascribe}{ə'skraib}
  \wordbreakdown{将某事写下来向某人告知 a<ad (= to) + scrib (= write)}
  \wordsense{vt. sth + to + sb/sth 将……归因（于）}
  \wordfamily{ascription 归因，归属}
  \wordfamily{ascribable 可归因于……的}
  \wordphrase{ascribe no malefic motive}
  \wordexample{I ascribe no malefic motive to his behavior.（我从他的行为里找不出任何恶意的动机。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{prescribe}{pri'skraib}
  \wordbreakdown{事先写 pre (= before) + scrib (= write)}
  \wordsense{vt. 规定，命令；开处方}
  \wordfamily{prescription 处方；命令，指示；规定，法规}
  \wordfamily{prescript 命令；规定，法令}
  \wordfamily{prescriptive 规定的，规范的；【律】依据时效的}
  \wordphrase{The doctor prescribed some}
  \wordexample{The doctor prescribed some tranquilizers and a few days of rest.（医生开了一些镇静药，要求休息几天。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{proscribe}{prə'skraib}
  \wordbreakdown{事先写 pro (= before) + scrib (= write)}
  \wordsense{vt. 禁止；剥夺公权；排斥；流放}
  \wordfamily{proscription 剥夺公权；流放；禁止}
  \wordphrase{The state senate proscribed}
  \wordexample{The state senate proscribed an expiration date on welfare benefits.（州议院禁止对福利救济金设置截止期限。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{transcribe}{træn'skraib}
  \wordbreakdown{写得使人明白，达到期望状态 tran<trans (= across) + scrib (= write)}
  \wordsense{v. 抄写；（播放）录音，（播放）录像；编曲}
  \wordfamily{transcript（学校的）成绩单；副本，誉本}
  \wordfamily{transcription 抄写；复本，誉本；播放录音或录像}
  \wordphrase{transcribe them}
  \wordexample{I record my professor's lectures and transcribe them at home.（教授讲课时我录下来，然后回家再听。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{circumscribe}{'sə:kəmskraib}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCRIB, SCRIPT}
  \wordfamily{ascribe（sth + to + sb/sth 将……归因（）；prescribe（规定）；proscribe（禁止）}
  \wordsense{v. 在……周围画线；限定，限制 *circum (= around)}
  \wordphrase{circumscribe one's interests（限制某人的爱好）}
  \wordexample{circumscribe one's interests（限制某人的爱好）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{conscript}{kən'skript}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCRIB, SCRIPT}
  \wordfamily{circumscribe；conscription；describe；description；ascribe（sth + to + sb/sth 将……归因（）}
  \wordsense{v. 征集，征召}
  \wordphrase{to conscript（征集，征召）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "to conscript".（常见搭配：征集，征召。）}
  \wordsense{n. 应征入伍者}
  \wordphrase{to conscript（征集，征召）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "to conscript".（常见搭配：征集，征召。）}
  \wordsense{a. 被征召的 *con<com (= together)}
  \wordphrase{conscript solider（应征入伍的士兵）}
  \wordexample{conscript solider（应征入伍的士兵）}
  \wordexample{to conscript（征集，征召）}
  \wordexample{a conscript（应征入伍者）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{conscription}{kən'skripʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCRIB, SCRIPT}
  \wordfamily{conscript；describe；circumscribe；description；ascribe（sth + to + sb/sth 将……归因（）}
  \wordsense{n. 征兵；征用（制度） *con<com (= together)}
  \wordphrase{conscription age（兵役年龄）}
  \wordphrase{conscription system（征兵制度）}
  \wordphrase{conscription of wealth（财产的征收；（战时向免服兵役者征收的）兵役税）}
  \wordexample{conscription age（兵役年龄）}
  \wordexample{conscription system（征兵制度）}
  \wordexample{conscription of wealth（财产的征收；（战时向免服兵役者征收的）兵役税）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{describe}{di'skraib}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCRIB, SCRIPT}
  \wordfamily{conscription；description；conscript；descriptive；ascribe（sth + to + sb/sth 将……归因（）}
  \wordsense{v. 描述，描写；把……说成 *de (= down, intensive)}
  \wordphrase{to describe（描述，描写）}
  \wordexample{to describe（描述，描写）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{description}{di'skripʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCRIB, SCRIPT}
  \wordfamily{describe；descriptive；conscription；inscribe；ascribe（sth + to + sb/sth 将……归因（）}
  \wordsense{n. 描述；描写；种类 *de (= down, intensive)}
  \wordphrase{description of the thief（对小偷的描述）}
  \wordphrase{vessels of every description（各种船舶）}
  \wordexample{description of the thief（对小偷的描述）}
  \wordexample{vessels of every description（各种船舶）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{descriptive}{di'skriptiv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCRIB, SCRIPT}
  \wordfamily{description；inscribe；describe；manuscript；ascribe（sth + to + sb/sth 将……归因（）}
  \wordsense{a. 叙述的；描写的 *de (= down, intensive)}
  \wordphrase{be descriptive（叙述的）}
  \wordexample{be descriptive（叙述的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{inscribe}{in'skraib}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCRIB, SCRIPT}
  \wordfamily{inscription（（纪念碑、货币等的）铭文）；ascribe（sth + to + sb/sth 将……归因（）；prescribe（规定）}
  \wordsense{v. 题写；雕刻；题赠 *in (= in, into)}
  \wordphrase{inscribed stock（记名股票）}
  \wordexample{inscribe a tomb with a name（在墓碑上刻名字）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{manuscript}{'mænjuskript}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCRIB, SCRIPT}
  \wordfamily{inscribe；nondescript；descriptive；postscript；ascribe（sth + to + sb/sth 将……归因（）}
  \wordsense{n. （书等的）原稿 *manu (= hand)}
  \wordphrase{poems still in manuscript（还没有付印的诗）}
  \wordexample{send a manuscript to the printers（把原稿发送给印刷工）}
  \wordexample{poems still in manuscript（还没有付印的诗）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{nondescript}{'nɔndiskript}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCRIB, SCRIPT}
  \wordfamily{manuscript；postscript；inscribe；rescript；ascribe（sth + to + sb/sth 将……归因（）}
  \wordsense{a. /}
  \wordphrase{be nondescript}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of nondescript.（此处展示 nondescript 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 难以归类的（人或物），无明显特征的（人或物）；无趣的 *non (= not), de (= down)}
  \wordphrase{poems still in manuscript（还没有付印的诗）}
  \wordexample{send a manuscript to the printers（把原稿发送给印刷工）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{postscript}{'pəustskript}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCRIB, SCRIPT}
  \wordfamily{nondescript；rescript；manuscript；subscribe；ascribe（sth + to + sb/sth 将……归因（）}
  \wordsense{n. （信件的）附言（缩写：P.S.） *post (= after)}
  \wordphrase{a postscript（附言（缩写：P.S.））}
  \wordexample{a postscript（附言（缩写：P.S.））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{rescript}{ri'skript}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCRIB, SCRIPT}
  \wordfamily{postscript；subscribe；nondescript；subscription；ascribe（sth + to + sb/sth 将……归因（）}
  \wordsense{n. 公告；（政府的）布告，敕令，法令；（罗马教皇的）解答敕令 *re (= again, back)}
  \wordphrase{a rescript（公告）}
  \wordexample{a rescript（公告）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{subscribe}{səb'skraib}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCRIB, SCRIPT}
  \wordfamily{ascribe（sth + to + sb/sth 将……归因（）；prescribe（规定）；proscribe（禁止）}
  \wordsense{v. 捐款；（在文件下面）署名；同意，赞成；订阅 *sub (= under)}
  \wordphrase{subscribe the issue}
  \wordexample{subscribe one's name to a petition（在请愿书上签名）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{subscription}{səb'skripʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCRIB, SCRIPT}
  \wordfamily{ascribe（sth + to + sb/sth 将……归因（）；prescribe（规定）；proscribe（禁止）}
  \wordsense{n. 捐款，认捐；订阅；签名；捐助金；订购款；会费 *sub (= under)}
  \wordphrase{subscription concert（联票音乐会（指预售联票本的系列音乐会中的一场））}
  \wordexample{subscription concert（联票音乐会（指预售联票本的系列音乐会中的一场））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{superscription}{ˌsu:pə'skripʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCRIB, SCRIPT}
  \wordfamily{subscription；scribe；subscribe；scribble；ascribe（sth + to + sb/sth 将……归因（）}
  \wordsense{n. 刻字，题字 *super (= above, over)}
  \wordphrase{a superscription（刻字，题字）}
  \wordexample{a superscription（刻字，题字）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{scribe}{skraib}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCRIB, SCRIPT}
  \wordfamily{superscription；scribble；subscription；scribbler；ascribe（sth + to + sb/sth 将……归因（）}
  \wordsense{n. （文牍、经文）抄写员 *-e (= person who)}
  \wordphrase{a scribe（抄写员）}
  \wordexample{a scribe（抄写员）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{scribble}{'skribl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCRIB, SCRIPT}
  \wordfamily{ascribe（sth + to + sb/sth 将……归因（）；prescribe（规定）；proscribe（禁止）}
  \wordsense{v. 潦草地写，乱涂，乱画 *-le (= small, little)}
  \wordphrase{to scribble（潦草地写，乱涂，乱画）}
  \wordexample{to scribble（潦草地写，乱涂，乱画）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{scribbler}{'skriblə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCRIB, SCRIPT}
  \wordsense{n. 写字潦草的人；无足轻重的作家}
  \wordfamily{scribble}
  \wordfamily{script}
  \wordfamily{scribe}
  \wordfamily{scripture}
  \wordfamily{a scribbler（写字潦草的人）}
  \wordphrase{a scribbler（写字潦草的人）}
  \wordexample{a scribbler（写字潦草的人）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{script}{skript}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCRIB, SCRIPT}
  \wordfamily{scripted（照稿念的）；ascribe（sth + to + sb/sth 将……归因（）；prescribe（规定）}
  \wordsense{n. 手迹，笔迹；手写体；脚本，底稿}
  \wordphrase{script writer（脚本作家，编剧）}
  \wordexample{script writer（脚本作家，编剧）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{scripture}{'skriptʃə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCRIB, SCRIPT}
  \wordfamily{ascribe（sth + to + sb/sth 将……归因（）；prescribe（规定）；proscribe（禁止）}
  \wordsense{n. （The (Holy) Scriptures）基督教《圣经》；（基督教以外的其他宗教的）圣典，经典}
  \wordphrase{the scripture}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of scripture.（此处展示 scripture 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{a. 引自圣经的，与圣经有关的}
  \wordphrase{scripture lesson（圣经课）}
  \wordexample{scripture lesson（圣经课）}
\end{word}



\chapter{SPERS[SPARS]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的词根 spers 意为 to sprinkle, to strew。spars 是其变形。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{aspersion}{ə'spə:ʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{向某人散播 a(ad (= to) + spers (= strew))}
  \wordsense{n. 洒圣水；诽谤，中伤}
  \wordfamily{asperse 洒圣水；诽谤，中伤}
  \wordphrase{aspersions on every director}
  \wordexample{The movie star's autobiography cast aspersions on every director she'd worked with.（那个电影演员在自传里诽谤了所有与她合作过的导演。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{disperse}{di'spə:s}
  \wordbreakdown{分别散播 di<dis (= away, apart) + spers (= strew)}
  \wordsense{vt. 驱散，使散开}
  \wordfamily{dispersal 散布，分散；解散}
  \wordfamily{dispersion 散布；分散}
  \wordphrase{The crowd of protesters}
  \wordexample{The crowd of protesters dispersed when the police arrived.（警察一到，示威者们就散开了。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{sparse}{spɑ:s}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPERS[SPARS]}
  \wordfamily{disperse；aspersion；scripture（（The (Holy) Scriptures）基）}
  \wordsense{a. 稀少的，稀疏的 *spars<spers (= strew)}
  \wordphrase{sparse population（稀少的人口）}
  \wordphrase{sparse beard（稀疏的胡须）}
  \wordexample{sparse population（稀少的人口）}
  \wordexample{sparse beard（稀疏的胡须）}
\end{word}



\chapter{TRIBUT}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的词根 tribut 意为 to give, to pay。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{attribute}{ə'tribju:t}
  \wordbreakdown{给某人某物 at(ad (= to) + tribut (= give))}
  \wordfamily{attributable（可归因于……的）；attributive（属性的）；attribution（归因，归属）}
  \wordsense{vt. sth + to + sth/sb 归因于……；认为……属于……}
  \wordphrase{sparse population（稀少的人口）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "sparse population".（常见搭配：稀少的人口。）}
  \wordsense{n. 属性，特征；象征}
  \wordphrase{attribute her extreme anger}
  \wordexample{To what do you attribute her extreme anger?（你认为是什么让她这么生气？）}
  \wordexample{to give, to pay。 attribute*** vt. sth + to + sth/sb 归因于……；认为……属于…… n. 属性，特征；象征}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{retribution}{ˌretri'bju:ʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{还、作为答谢还给 re (= again, back) + tribut (= give, pay)}
  \wordfamily{retributive（报答的）；attribute（sth + to + sth/sb 归因于……）；contribute（贡献）}
  \wordsense{n. 报答；报应}
  \wordphrase{the retribution}
  \wordexample{Retribution will quickly follow.（报应将很快随之降临。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{contribute}{kən'tribju:t}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TRIBUT}
  \wordfamily{attribute（sth + to + sth/sb 归因于……）；retribution（报答）；contribution（贡献）}
  \wordsense{v. 贡献，捐献；提供；投稿 *con<com (= with)}
  \wordphrase{to contribute（贡献，捐献）}
  \wordexample{to contribute（贡献，捐献）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{contribution}{ˌkɒntri'bju:ʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TRIBUT}
  \wordfamily{contribute；contributory；distribute；distribution；attribute（sth + to + sth/sb 归因于……）}
  \wordsense{n. 贡献，捐献；提供；投稿；捐助金；捐献的物品 *con<com (= with)}
  \wordphrase{contributions to the relief fund（给救济基金的捐款）}
  \wordphrase{contributions for the July issue（给七月刊的投稿）}
  \wordexample{contributions to the relief fund（给救济基金的捐款）}
  \wordexample{contributions for the July issue（给七月刊的投稿）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{contributory}{kən'tribjutəri}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TRIBUT}
  \wordfamily{contribution；distribute；contribute；distribution；attribute（sth + to + sth/sb 归因于……）}
  \wordsense{a. 贡献的；有助于……的；捐献的，义捐的；分担的 *con<com (= with)}
  \wordphrase{contributory negligence（【律】与有过失，共同过失）}
  \wordexample{contributory negligence（【律】与有过失，共同过失）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{distribute}{di'stribju:t}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TRIBUT}
  \wordfamily{attribute（sth + to + sth/sb 归因于……）；retribution（报答）；contribute（贡献）}
  \wordsense{v. 分配，分发；散布，分布；区分，分类 *dis (= apart, away)}
  \wordphrase{to distribute（分配，分发）}
  \wordexample{to distribute（分配，分发）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{distribution}{ˌdistri'bju:ʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TRIBUT}
  \wordfamily{distribute；distributive；contributory；tribute；attribute（sth + to + sth/sb 归因于……）}
  \wordsense{n. 分配，分发；分布 *dis (= apart, away)}
  \wordphrase{distribution of the profits（利益分配）}
  \wordphrase{distribution of wealth（财富的分配）}
  \wordexample{distribution of the profits（利益分配）}
  \wordexample{distribution of wealth（财富的分配）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{distributive}{di'stribjutiv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TRIBUT}
  \wordfamily{distribution；tribute；distribute；tributary；attribute（sth + to + sth/sb 归因于……）}
  \wordsense{a. 分配的；分布的 *dis (= apart, away)}
  \wordphrase{distributive trades（分配行业）}
  \wordexample{distributive trades（分配行业）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{tribute}{'tribju:t}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TRIBUT}
  \wordfamily{attribute（sth + to + sth/sb 归因于……）；retribution（报答）；contribute（贡献）}
  \wordsense{n. 定期的贡物；（表达尊敬、称赞的）行动、言语、礼物}
  \wordphrase{pay tribute to（向……进贡）}
  \wordexample{pay tribute to（向……进贡）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{tributary}{'tribjutəri}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TRIBUT}
  \wordfamily{tribute；distributive；distribution；distribute；attribute（sth + to + sth/sb 归因于……）}
  \wordsense{a. 进贡的；（河等）支流的}
  \wordphrase{be tributary（进贡的）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "be tributary".（常见搭配：进贡的。）}
  \wordsense{n. 进贡国，附庸国；支流}
  \wordphrase{be tributary（进贡的）}
  \wordexample{be tributary（进贡的）}
  \wordexample{a tributary（进贡国，附庸国）}
\end{word}



\chapter{VER}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的词根 ver 意为 true。此外 ver 还有 to feel awe 和 to fear 的意思。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{aver}{ə'və:(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{告诉某人是事实，没错 a<ad (= to) + ver (= true)}
  \wordfamily{verify；veritable；verily；verisimilitude}
  \wordsense{vt. 断言；证明……属实}
  \wordphrase{aver the issue}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of aver.（此处展示 aver 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 进贡国，附庸国；支流}
  \wordphrase{avers that the defendant}
  \wordexample{The prosecuting attorney avers that the defendant murdered his mother.（检察官断言被告谋杀了自己的母亲。）}
  \wordexample{avers that the defendant}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{verify}{'verifai}
  \wordbreakdown{使成为真实 ver (= true)}
  \wordfamily{verification（证实，证明）；verifiable（能证实的）；aver（断言）}
  \wordsense{vt. 核实（真实性或正确性）；证实，证明}
  \wordphrase{verify that Susan arrived}
  \wordexample{I can verify that Susan arrived late because her car had a flat tire.（我可以证明苏珊是因为车胎漏气才来晚的。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{veritable}{'veritəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{使能够成为事实的 ver (= true)}
  \wordsense{a. 真正的，名副其实的}
  \wordfamily{verity 真正，真实；确实存在的事物；真理}
  \wordfamily{verily 真实地；真正地}
  \wordphrase{veritable fortune selling her}
  \wordexample{She's earned a veritable fortune selling her cakes and cookies.（她靠卖糕点成了真正的富翁。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{verily}{'verili}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VER}
  \wordsense{ad. 真实地；真正地}
  \wordfamily{verisimilitude}
  \wordfamily{verity}
  \wordfamily{irreverent}
  \wordfamily{revere}
  \wordfamily{verily（真实地）}
  \wordphrase{verily（真实地）}
  \wordexample{verily（真实地）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{verisimilitude}{ˌverisi'militju:d}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VER}
  \wordfamily{verily；verity；irreverent；revere；aver（断言）}
  \wordsense{n. 貌似真实；逼真（的事物） *simil (= same)}
  \wordphrase{a verisimilitude（貌似真实）}
  \wordexample{a verisimilitude（貌似真实）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{verity}{'verəti}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VER}
  \wordfamily{verisimilitude；verily；irreverent；revere；aver（断言）}
  \wordsense{n. （言语等的）真实性；真理}
  \wordphrase{a verity（真实性）}
  \wordexample{a verity（真实性）}
\end{word}



\chapter{FAC, FACT, FEC, FECT, FIC, FICT[FEAS, FEIT, FEAT, FIT]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 fac, fact, fec / fect, fic / fict 意为 to make, to do。feas, feit, feat, fit 等是其变形。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{affectation}{ˌæfek'teiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{使靠近 af<ad (= to, near) + fect (= do, make)}
  \wordsense{n. 装模作样；假装}
  \wordfamily{affect 假装；影响}
  \wordfamily{affectionately 充满深情地}
  \wordfamily{affected 假装的；受影响的}
  \wordfamily{cf. affection 影响；爱情；感情}
  \wordphrase{affectation}
  \wordexample{His cigarette holder is merely an affectation.（他的烟斗只不过是装样子罢了。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{defect}{di'fekt}
  \wordbreakdown{做某物时漏掉一定要放入的材料 de (= from) + fect (= do, make)}
  \wordfamily{defection（脱离，脱党）；defector（叛离者，变节者）；defective（不完美的，有缺陷的）；affectation（装模作样）}
  \wordsense{n. 缺点，缺陷，不足之处}
  \wordphrase{the defect}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of defect.（此处展示 defect 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{vi. from + sth 脱离，逃跑}
  \wordphrase{defect}
  \wordexample{Almost everyone has at least one major character defect.（几乎所有的人都至少有一个较大的性格缺陷。）}
  \wordexample{defect}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{malefactor}{ˈmælifæktə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{做坏事的人 male<mal (= bad) + fact (= do, make)}
  \wordsense{n. 罪人；恶人}
  \wordfamily{malefaction 罪行，坏事}
  \wordfamily{maleficent 有害的，作恶的}
  \wordphrase{malefactor I'm dating now}
  \wordexample{My father always jokingly asks what kind of malefactor I'm dating now.（我父亲总是开玩笑地问我，最近在和一个什么样的坏小子约会。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{factual}{'fæktʃuəl}
  \wordbreakdown{实际所做或所制作的 fact (= do, make)}
  \wordsense{a. 事实上的；实际的}
  \wordfamily{malefactor}
  \wordfamily{facile}
  \wordfamily{defect}
  \wordfamily{beneficent}
  \wordphrase{factual and the fictional}
  \wordexample{TV docudramas are a blend of both the factual and the fictional.（纪实电视剧是事实与虚构的结合体。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{facile}{'fæsail}
  \wordbreakdown{易于做或制作的 fac (= do, make)}
  \wordfamily{factual；beneficent；malefactor；beneficial；affectation（装模作样）}
  \wordsense{a. 容易的，易得到的；做事不花力气的；熟练的；流畅的}
  \wordphrase{facile responses Really think}
  \wordexample{Class, don't give me facile responses. Really think about your answers.（同学们，不要给我那种很容易就能想到的答案，一定要好好思考一下再回答。）}
  \wordexample{facile responses Really think}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{beneficent}{bi'nefisnt}
  \wordbreakdown{做善事、亲切之事的 bene (= good) + fic (= do, make)}
  \wordsense{a. 做善事的，仁慈的；有益的}
  \wordfamily{beneficence 善行，慈善}
  \wordfamily{benefaction 善行，施舍}
  \wordfamily{benefactor 捐助人，捐赠者；恩人}
  \wordphrase{beneficent acts are answered}
  \wordexample{I generally find in life that beneficent acts are answered in kind.（我发现生活中总是善有善报。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{beneficial}{ˌbeni'fiʃl}
  \wordbreakdown{使有利的 bene (= good) + fic (= make, do)}
  \wordfamily{B（有益心脏健康）；affectation（装模作样）；defect（缺点）}
  \wordsense{a. 有益的，有帮助的}
  \wordphrase{beneficial for cardiac health}
  \wordexample{Taking a B-complex vitamin is proven to be beneficial for cardiac health.（已证实摄入复合维生素}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{efficacious}{ˌefi'keiʃəs}
  \wordbreakdown{做、制作得好的 ef<ex (= intensive) + fic (= do, make)}
  \wordsense{a. 灵验的；有效的}
  \wordfamily{efficacy 效力，功效}
  \wordfamily{efficaciously 有效地}
  \wordphrase{efficacious in preventing certain}
  \wordexample{A high fiber diet is efficacious in preventing certain kinds of cancer.（高纤维饮食可以有效预防某些癌症。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{munificent}{mju:'nifisnt}
  \wordbreakdown{使成为礼物的 mun (= gift, service) + fic (= make, do)}
  \wordfamily{munificence（慷慨）；affectation（装模作样）；defect（缺点）}
  \wordsense{a. 慷慨的，大方的；丰厚的}
  \wordphrase{munificent offer but I}
  \wordexample{The company made me a munificent offer, but I declined it.（那家公司的出价很慷慨，但我还是拒绝了。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{officiate}{ə'fiʃieit}
  \wordbreakdown{好好做事 of<ob (= intensive) + fic (= do, make)}
  \wordsense{vi. at + sth 行使职权；主持}
  \wordfamily{office 事务所；办公室}
  \wordfamily{officer 军官}
  \wordfamily{officiator 主婚人}
  \wordfamily{official 正式的；公务的；`(n.)` 公务员，官员}
  \wordphrase{A Unitarian minister officiated}
  \wordexample{A Unitarian minister officiated at his funeral.（一位论教派的牧师主持了他的葬礼。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{officious}{ə'fiʃəs}
  \wordbreakdown{使妨碍的 of<ob (= in the way) + fic (= do, make)}
  \wordsense{a. 好出主意的，多管闲事的}
  \wordfamily{officiate}
  \wordfamily{proficient}
  \wordfamily{munificent}
  \wordfamily{superficial}
  \wordphrase{officious translator}
  \wordexample{The government required us to use their somewhat officious translator.（政府要求我们用那个有点爱多管闲事的翻译官。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{proficient}{prə'fiʃnt}
  \wordbreakdown{首先做、制作 pro (= before) + fic (= make, do)}
  \wordfamily{proficiency（精通，熟练）；affectation（装模作样）；defect（缺点）}
  \wordsense{a. 精通的，熟练的}
  \wordphrase{be proficient}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of proficient.（此处展示 proficient 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 能手；专家}
  \wordphrase{proficient}
  \wordexample{I wouldn't say I'm a great saxophone player. I'm just barely proficient.（我没有说我是萨克斯演奏家，只是比较精通而已。）}
  \wordexample{proficient}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{superficial}{ˌsu:pə'fiʃl}
  \wordbreakdown{使登到上边 super (= above, over) + fic (= make, do)}
  \wordfamily{superficiality（表面性）；affectation（装模作样）；defect（缺点）}
  \wordsense{a. 表面的，外表的；肤浅的，没有深度的，浅薄的}
  \wordphrase{C-，老师说我的论文太肤浅。）}
  \wordexample{I got a C-minus. The teacher said my essay was superficial.（我得了}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{surfeit}{'sɜ:fit}
  \wordbreakdown{使过度 sur (= over, excessive) + feit<fact (= do, make)}
  \wordfamily{superficial；malfeasance；proficient；discomfit；affectation（装模作样）}
  \wordsense{n. 过量；饮食过度；腻烦}
  \wordphrase{the surfeit}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of surfeit.（此处展示 surfeit 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. （使）饮食过度；（使）腻烦}
  \wordphrase{surfeit of great recipes}
  \wordexample{There's a surfeit of great recipes on the Internet.（网上的精美食谱多得让人烦。）}
  \wordexample{surfeit of great recipes}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{malfeasance}{mæl'fi:zəns}
  \wordbreakdown{做坏事 mal (= bad) + feas<fac (= do, make)}
  \wordsense{n. 违法行为；（特指公务员的）不正当行为，渎职}
  \wordfamily{surfeit}
  \wordfamily{discomfit}
  \wordfamily{superficial}
  \wordfamily{confection}
  \wordphrase{malfeasance cost him the}
  \wordexample{The mayor's well-publicized malfeasance cost him the election.（市长的渎职行为已公之于众，他为此付出了代价，在选举中失利。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{discomfit}{dis'kʌmfit}
  \wordbreakdown{使完全做不了 dis (= not) + com (= thoroughly) + fit<fact (= make, do)}
  \wordfamily{discomfiture（挫败）；affectation（装模作样）；defect（缺点）}
  \wordsense{vt. 挫败；扰乱}
  \wordphrase{discomfited by the criminal}
  \wordexample{I'm discomfited by the criminal record of my daughter's fiance.（得知女儿未婚夫的前科后，我感到很惊慌。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{confection}{kən'fekʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC, FACT, FEC, FECT, FIC, FICT[FEAS, FEIT, FEAT, FIT]}
  \wordfamily{confectioner（糖果商）；affectation（装模作样）；defect（缺点）}
  \wordsense{n. 糖果，点心；混合，合成，调制；（一般指女士服装）成衣 *con<com (= together)}
  \wordphrase{a confection（糖果，点心）}
  \wordexample{a confection（糖果，点心）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{confectionery}{kən'fekʃənəri}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC, FACT, FEC, FECT, FIC, FICT[FEAS, FEIT, FEAT, FIT]}
  \wordfamily{affectation（装模作样）；defect（缺点）；malefactor（罪人）}
  \wordsense{n. 糖果类；糖果制造（业）；糖果店 *con<com (= together)}
  \wordphrase{a confectionery（糖果类）}
  \wordexample{a confectionery（糖果类）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{effect}{i'fekt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC, FACT, FEC, FECT, FIC, FICT[FEAS, FEIT, FEAT, FIT]}
  \wordfamily{no（effects（无存款（银行在空头支票上的批语，常写做）；affectation（装模作样）；defect（缺点）}
  \wordsense{n. 结果；效果，效能，效力；影响；印象；（pl.）财产}
  \wordphrase{take effect（见效，生效，起作用）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "take effect".（常见搭配：见效，生效，起作用。）}
  \wordsense{v. 招致；达到（目的）；实现 *ef<ex (= out, intensive)}
  \wordphrase{effect of heat upon metals（热对金属的影响）}
  \wordphrase{effect a cure（治愈）}
  \wordphrase{effect an insurance policy（投保）}
  \wordexample{effect of heat upon metals（热对金属的影响）}
  \wordexample{effect a cure（治愈）}
  \wordexample{effect an insurance policy（投保）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{effective}{i'fektiv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC, FACT, FEC, FECT, FIC, FICT[FEAS, FEIT, FEAT, FIT]}
  \wordfamily{effect；effectual；confectionery；infect；affectation（装模作样）}
  \wordsense{a. 有效的；给人深刻印象的；实际的，事实上的 *ef<ex (= out, intensive)}
  \wordphrase{effective measures（有效的手段）}
  \wordexample{effective measures（有效的手段）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{effectual}{i'fektʃuəl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC, FACT, FEC, FECT, FIC, FICT[FEAS, FEIT, FEAT, FIT]}
  \wordfamily{effective；infect；effect；infection；affectation（装模作样）}
  \wordsense{a. 奏效的，有效的 *ef<ex (= out, intensive)}
  \wordphrase{effectual remedy（有效的治疗）}
  \wordexample{effectual remedy（有效的治疗）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{infect}{in'fekt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC, FACT, FEC, FECT, FIC, FICT[FEAS, FEIT, FEAT, FIT]}
  \wordfamily{effectual；infection；effective；infectious；affectation（装模作样）}
  \wordsense{v. 【医】感染，传染；使受影响 *in (= into)}
  \wordphrase{infected with cholera（染上霍乱）}
  \wordexample{infected with cholera（染上霍乱）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{infection}{in'fekʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC, FACT, FEC, FECT, FIC, FICT[FEAS, FEIT, FEAT, FIT]}
  \wordfamily{infect；infectious；effectual；perfect；affectation（装模作样）}
  \wordsense{n. （通过空气、水传播病菌的）感染；传染（病）；影响 *in (= into)}
  \wordphrase{an infection（感染）}
  \wordexample{an infection（感染）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{infectious}{in'fekʃəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC, FACT, FEC, FECT, FIC, FICT[FEAS, FEIT, FEAT, FIT]}
  \wordfamily{infection；perfect；infect；perfection；affectation（装模作样）}

  \wordfamily{featureless（平淡无奇的，无特色的）；affectation（装模作样）；defect（缺点）}
  \wordsense{n. 特征；特色；（pl.）面貌，容貌；（报纸的）特写，特别报道；电影正片}
  \wordphrase{double features（【电影】两片连映）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "double features".（常见搭配：【电影】两片连映。）}
  \wordsense{v. 以……为特色 *feat<fact (= do, make)}
  \wordphrase{a man of handsome features（美男子）}
  \wordexample{unusual features in a political program（政治纲领与众不同的特点）}
  \wordexample{newspaper that makes a feature of sport（以体育报道为特色的报纸）}
  \wordexample{a man of handsome features（美男子）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{defeat}{di'fi:t}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC, FACT, FEC, FECT, FIC, FICT[FEAS, FEIT, FEAT, FIT]}
  \wordfamily{defeatism（失败主义）；defeatist（失败主义者）；affectation（装模作样）}
  \wordsense{v. 使失败，击败，使受挫；【律】使无效}
  \wordphrase{to defeat（使失败，击败，使受挫）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "to defeat".（常见搭配：使失败，击败，使受挫。）}
  \wordsense{n. 失败 *de (= down), feat<fact (= do, make)}
  \wordphrase{four victories and two defeats（四胜两败）}
  \wordexample{four victories and two defeats（四胜两败）}
  \wordexample{to defeat（使失败，击败，使受挫）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{benefit}{'benifit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC, FACT, FEC, FECT, FIC, FICT[FEAS, FEIT, FEAT, FIT]}
  \wordfamily{defeat；profit；feature；profitable；affectation（装模作样）}
  \wordsense{n. 益处；帮助；义演；义卖；补助费，救济金；（根据保险规定所得的）保险金}
  \wordphrase{the benefit}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of benefit.（此处展示 benefit 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. 有益于；得益 *bene (= good), fit<fact (= do, make)}
  \wordphrase{benefit performance / concert（义演 / 义演音乐会）}
  \wordphrase{medical / unemployment benefit（医疗津贴 / 失业救济金）}
  \wordexample{the benefits of a good education（优质教育的益处）}
  \wordexample{benefit performance / concert（义演 / 义演音乐会）}
  \wordexample{medical / unemployment benefit（医疗津贴 / 失业救济金）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{profit}{'prɔfit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC, FACT, FEC, FECT, FIC, FICT[FEAS, FEIT, FEAT, FIT]}
  \wordfamily{affectation（装模作样）；defect（缺点）；malefactor（罪人）}
  \wordsense{n. 利益；收益；利润}
  \wordphrase{the profit}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of profit.（此处展示 profit 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. 得益；有益于 *pro (= before), fit<fact (= do, make)}
  \wordphrase{profit margin（利润率）}
  \wordphrase{profit sharing（分红制）}
  \wordexample{profit margin（利润率）}
  \wordexample{profit sharing（分红制）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{profitable}{'prɔfitəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC, FACT, FEC, FECT, FIC, FICT[FEAS, FEIT, FEAT, FIT]}
  \wordfamily{profitably（有益地，有利地）；affectation（装模作样）；defect（缺点）}
  \wordsense{a. 有益的；有赢利的，有利的 *pro (= before), fit<fact (= do, make)}
  \wordphrase{profitable investment（有利润的投资）}
  \wordphrase{profitable instruction（有益的教导）}
  \wordexample{profitable investment（有利润的投资）}
  \wordexample{profitable instruction（有益的教导）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{profiteer}{ˌprɔfi'tiə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC, FACT, FEC, FECT, FIC, FICT[FEAS, FEIT, FEAT, FIT]}
  \wordfamily{profitable；profit；benefit；defeat；affectation（装模作样）}
  \wordsense{v. 牟取暴利}
  \wordphrase{to profiteer（牟取暴利）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "to profiteer".（常见搭配：牟取暴利。）}
  \wordsense{n. 奸商；牟取暴利者 *pro (= before), fit<fact (= do, make)}
  \wordphrase{to profiteer（牟取暴利）}
  \wordexample{to profiteer（牟取暴利）}
  \wordexample{a profiteer（奸商）}
\end{word}



\chapter{FRONT}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 front 意为 forehead, face。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{affront}{ə'frʌnt}
  \wordbreakdown{向某人凑过脸 af<ad (= to) + front (= face, forehead)}
  \wordfamily{affrontive（侮辱性的）；confront（面临）；effrontery（厚颜无耻（的行为））}
  \wordsense{vt. 公然侮辱；勇敢地面对}
  \wordphrase{affront the issue}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of affront.（此处展示 affront 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 公然侮辱}
  \wordphrase{affront to the church}
  \wordexample{Priests who marry are an affront to the church.（结婚的牧师对教会来说是一种侮辱。）}
  \wordexample{affront to the church}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{confront}{kən'frʌnt}
  \wordbreakdown{一起对顶额头 con<com (= together) + front (= forehead, face)}
  \wordfamily{confrontation（对抗）；affront（公然侮辱）；effrontery（厚颜无耻（的行为））}
  \wordsense{vt. 面临；勇敢地面对；对抗}
  \wordphrase{confront your fears rather}
  \wordexample{It's important to confront your fears rather than run from them.（重要的是，你应该勇敢地去面对你内心的恐惧，而不是逃避。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{effrontery}{i'frʌntəri}
  \wordbreakdown{将额头向外探 ef<ex (= out) + front (= forehead)}
  \wordsense{n. 厚颜无耻（的行为）}
  \wordfamily{confront}
  \wordfamily{frontage}
  \wordfamily{affront}
  \wordfamily{frontal}
  \wordphrase{effrontery to deny me}
  \wordexample{The doorman had the effrontery to deny me entrance to the hotel.（门卫厚颜无耻地阻止我进饭店。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{frontage}{'frʌntidʒ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FRONT}
  \wordsense{n.（建筑物等的）正面；临街地}
  \wordfamily{frontal}
  \wordfamily{frontier}
  \wordfamily{frontiersman}
  \wordfamily{frontispiece}
  \wordphrase{frontage road（沿街道路（与高速公路平行的辅助道路））}
  \wordexample{frontage road（沿街道路（与高速公路平行的辅助道路））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{frontal}{'frʌntl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FRONT}
  \wordfamily{frontage；frontier；frontiersman；frontispiece；affront（公然侮辱）}
  \wordsense{a. 正面的}
  \wordphrase{be frontal}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of frontal.（此处展示 frontal 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 额骨}
  \wordphrase{frontal attack / assault（正面攻击）}
  \wordphrase{full frontal nudity（全正面裸体）}
  \wordexample{frontal attack / assault（正面攻击）}
  \wordexample{full frontal nudity（全正面裸体）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{frontier}{'frʌntiə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FRONT}
  \wordsense{n. 边境，国境；（探索活动的）新领域}
  \wordfamily{frontal}
  \wordfamily{frontiersman}
  \wordfamily{frontage}
  \wordfamily{frontispiece}
  \wordphrase{town on the frontier（国境线上的城市）}
  \wordphrase{frontier disputes（边境纷争）}
  \wordphrase{frontier incidents（边境（冲突）事件）}
  \wordphrase{frontier spirit（开拓者精神）}
  \wordexample{town on the frontier（国境线上的城市）}
  \wordexample{frontier disputes（边境纷争）}
  \wordexample{frontier incidents（边境（冲突）事件）}
  \wordexample{frontier spirit（开拓者精神）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{frontiersman}{'frʌntiəzmən}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FRONT}
  \wordsense{n. 边远地区的居民}
  \wordfamily{frontier}
  \wordfamily{frontispiece}
  \wordfamily{frontal}
  \wordfamily{frontlet}
  \wordfamily{a frontiersman（边远地区的居民）}
  \wordphrase{a frontiersman（边远地区的居民）}
  \wordexample{a frontiersman（边远地区的居民）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{frontispiece}{'frʌntispi:s}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FRONT}
  \wordsense{n.（书的）卷首插画}
  \wordfamily{frontiersman}
  \wordfamily{frontlet}
  \wordfamily{frontier}
  \wordfamily{forefront}
  \wordfamily{a frontispiece（卷首插画）}
  \wordphrase{a frontispiece（卷首插画）}
  \wordexample{a frontispiece（卷首插画）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{frontlet}{'frʌntlit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FRONT}
  \wordfamily{frontispiece；forefront；frontiersman；frontier；affront（公然侮辱）}
  \wordsense{n. （丝带等）额饰 *let (= small)}
  \wordphrase{a frontlet（额饰）}
  \wordexample{a frontlet（额饰）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{forefront}{'fɔ:frʌnt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FRONT}
  \wordfamily{affront（公然侮辱）；confront（面临）；effrontery（厚颜无耻（的行为））}
  \wordsense{n. 最前面，最前方；最重要的位置 *fore (= before)}
  \wordphrase{serious, earnest, to cut。}
  \wordexample{in the forefront of the battle（在战斗的最前线）}
  \wordexample{in the forefront of my mind（在我心中最重要）}
\end{word}



\chapter{SEVER}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 sever 意为 serious, earnest, to cut。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{asseverate}{ə'sevəreit}
  \wordbreakdown{使变得非常深刻和严肃 as<ad (= to, intensive) + sever (= serious)}
  \wordfamily{asseveration（断言）；persevere（in + sth 坚持不懈）；severe（严重的）}
  \wordsense{vt. 断言}
  \wordphrase{The professor asseverated that}
  \wordexample{The professor asseverated that more than half the students had cheated.（教授断言一半以上的学生都作弊了。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{persevere}{ˌpɜ:sɪ'viə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{从头到尾始终非常严肃 per (= through, throughout) + sever (= serious)}
  \wordsense{vi. in + sth 坚持不懈；（辩论中）固执己见}
  \wordfamily{perseverance 忍耐，坚持}
  \wordfamily{persevering 有毅力的，锲而不舍的；固执的}
  \wordphrase{persevere especially when you}
  \wordexample{It's important to persevere, especially when you feel like quitting.（坚持不懈很重要，尤其是当你想放弃的时候。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{severe}{si'viə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SEVER}
  \wordfamily{dissever；sever；severance；asseverate（断言）}
  \wordsense{a. 严重的；剧烈的；严格的，苛刻的 *sever (= serious)}
  \wordphrase{be severe（严重的）}
  \wordexample{be severe（严重的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{dissever}{di'sevə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SEVER}
  \wordfamily{severe；sever；severance；asseverate（断言）}
  \wordsense{v. （使）分离；（使）分裂 *dis (= away), sever (= cut)}
  \wordphrase{to dissever（分离）}
  \wordexample{to dissever（分离）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{sever}{'sevə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SEVER}
  \wordfamily{dissever；severance；severe；asseverate（断言）}
  \wordsense{v. 切断，割断；中断；断绝；（使）分隔，（使）分离 *sever (= cut)}
  \wordphrase{to sever（切断，割断）}
  \wordexample{to sever（切断，割断）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{severance}{'sevərəns}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SEVER}
  \wordfamily{asseverate（断言）；persevere（in + sth 坚持不懈）；severe（严重的）}
  \wordsense{n. 割断，断绝；分离，隔离 *sever (= cut)}
  \wordphrase{severance payment（解雇金，离职金）}
  \wordexample{severance of diplomatic relations / of communications（断绝外交关系 / 通信中断）}
  \wordexample{severance payment（解雇金，离职金）}
\end{word}



\chapter{TENU}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 tenu 意为 thin。同义词根为 mac。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{attenuate}{ə'tenjuət}
  \wordbreakdown{使变得非常细 at<ad (= to, intensive) + tenu (= thin)}
  \wordfamily{attenuation（减弱）；extenuate（企图减轻（罪行等））；tenuous（稀薄的）}
  \wordsense{vt. 使减弱；使减少}
  \wordphrase{attenuates the severity}
  \wordexample{Aspirin normally attenuates the severity of a fever.（通常阿司匹林可以退高烧。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{extenuate}{ik'stenjueit}
  \wordbreakdown{脱离于罪，使罪变轻 ex (= out) + tenu (= thin)}
  \wordfamily{attenuate（使减弱）；tenuous（稀薄的）；severance（割断）}
  \wordsense{vt. 企图减轻（罪行等）}
  \wordphrase{extenuates her error}
  \wordexample{Her youth extenuates her error of judgment.（因为她年纪小，所以对她的判断失误减轻了处罚。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{tenuous}{'tenjuəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TENU}
  \wordfamily{attenuate（使减弱）；extenuate（企图减轻（罪行等））；severance（割断）}
  \wordsense{a. 稀薄的；纤细的；（区别）细微的，微妙的}
  \wordphrase{be tenuous}
  \wordexample{the tenuous web of a spider（蜘蛛的细丝网）}
\end{word}



\chapter{COL[CUL]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 col 意为 neck。另外，col 还有 to filter, large intestine, to till, glue 等含义。cul 是它的变形。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{accolade}{'ækəleid}
  \wordbreakdown{挂在脖子附近 ac<ad (= to, near) + col (= neck)}
  \wordfamily{collar；decollation；percolate}
  \wordsense{n. 称赞；赞赏；（the \textasciitilde{}）骑士爵位的授予典礼}
  \wordphrase{accolade from the Literary}
  \wordexample{The poet received a prestigious accolade from the Literary Society.（那位诗人获得了文学协会的极高赞誉。）}
  \wordexample{accolade from the Literary}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{collar}{'kɒlə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 COL[CUL]}
  \wordfamily{decollation；percolate；colander；accolade（称赞）}
  \wordsense{n. 领子；（狗、马等的）项圈；（连接两个管子、连杆、车轴等的）箍 *col (= neck)}
  \wordphrase{blue / white collar workers（蓝领 / 白领工作者）}
  \wordphrase{collar stud（领扣）}
  \wordphrase{collar bone（锁骨）}
  \wordexample{blue / white collar workers（蓝领 / 白领工作者）}
  \wordexample{collar stud（领扣）}
  \wordexample{collar bone（锁骨）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{decollation}{di:kə'leiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 COL[CUL]}
  \wordfamily{collar；percolate；colander；accolade（称赞）}
  \wordsense{n. 断头 *de (= down), col (= neck)}
  \wordphrase{a decollation（断头）}
  \wordexample{a decollation（断头）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{décolleté}{dei'kɒltei}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 COL[CUL]}
  \wordfamily{decollation；percolate；collar；colander；accolade（称赞）}
  \wordsense{a. 《法》（衣服）低领露肩的 *de (= down, away), col (= neck)}
  \wordphrase{be décolleté（《法》（衣服）低领露肩的）}
  \wordexample{be décolleté（《法》（衣服）低领露肩的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{percolate}{'pɜ:kəleit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 COL[CUL]}
  \wordfamily{percolator（（带有过滤器的）过滤式咖啡壶）；accolade（称赞）；collar（领子）}
  \wordsense{v. 过滤；（使）渗出；（使）浸透 *per (= through), col (= filter)}
  \wordphrase{percolate the issue}
  \wordexample{Water percolates through sand.（水从沙子里渗出来。）}
  \wordexample{I make coffee by percolating boiling water through ground coffee.（我用沸水滤过咖啡粉的方法煮咖啡。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{colander}{'kʌləndə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 COL[CUL]}
  \wordfamily{accolade（称赞）；collar（领子）；decollation（断头 *de (= down), col (=）}
  \wordsense{n. 过滤器；（厨房用）滤水器（碗状，有孔，用来过滤或淘洗食物） *col (= filter)}
  \wordphrase{a colander（过滤器）}
  \wordexample{a colander（过滤器）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{culvert}{'kʌlvət}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 COL[CUL]}
  \wordfamily{colander；colon；percolate；colic；accolade（称赞）}
  \wordsense{n. 阴沟；地下电缆管道 *cul<col (= filter)}
  \wordphrase{a culvert（阴沟）}
  \wordexample{a culvert（阴沟）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{colon}{'kəulən}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 COL[CUL]}
  \wordfamily{culvert；colic；colander；colitis；accolade（称赞）}
  \wordsense{n. 结肠 *col (= large intestine)}
  \wordphrase{colon cleanse（肠清洗剂）}
  \wordexample{colon cleanse（肠清洗剂）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{colic}{'kɒlik}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 COL[CUL]}
  \wordfamily{colon；colitis；culvert；colony；accolade（称赞）}
  \wordsense{n. （无腹泻的剧烈的）腹痛 *col (= large intestine)}
  \wordphrase{a colic（腹痛）}
  \wordexample{a colic（腹痛）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{colitis}{kəu'laitis}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 COL[CUL]}
  \wordfamily{colic；colony；colon；colonial；accolade（称赞）}
  \wordsense{n. 大肠炎 *col (= large intestine), itis (= inflammation)}
  \wordphrase{a colitis（大肠炎）}
  \wordexample{a colitis（大肠炎）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{colony}{'kɒləni}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 COL[CUL]}
  \wordfamily{colonist（殖民地开拓者）；accolade（称赞）；collar（领子）}
  \wordsense{n. 殖民地；侨民；群体；【生】群落 *col (= till)}
  \wordphrase{a colony of artists（一群美术家）}
  \wordphrase{a colony of ants（一群蚂蚁）}
  \wordexample{the South Korean colony in New York（纽约的韩国侨民）}
  \wordexample{a colony of artists（一群美术家）}
  \wordexample{a colony of ants（一群蚂蚁）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{colonial}{kə'ləuniəl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 COL[CUL]}
  \wordfamily{colonialist（殖民政策的支持者，殖民主义者）；accolade（称赞）；collar（领子）}
  \wordsense{a. 殖民地的}
  \wordphrase{be colonial（殖民地的）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "be colonial".（常见搭配：殖民地的。）}
  \wordsense{n. 殖民地居民（尤指开拓者的子孙） *col (= till)}
  \wordphrase{be colonial（殖民地的）}
  \wordexample{be colonial（殖民地的）}
  \wordexample{a colonial（殖民地居民（尤指开拓者的子孙））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{colonize}{'kɒlənaiz}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 COL[CUL]}
  \wordfamily{colonization（殖民）；colonizer（殖民地开拓者）；accolade（称赞）}
  \wordsense{v. 开拓殖民地 *col (= till)}
  \wordphrase{to colonize（开拓殖民地）}
  \wordexample{to colonize（开拓殖民地）}
\end{word}

\chapter{COST}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 cost 意为 rib, side。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{accost}{ə'kɒst}
  \wordbreakdown{走到某人身边 ac<ad (= to) + cost (= rib, side)}
  \wordfamily{costalgia；accosted（me）；protocol（（条约等的）草案）}
  \wordsense{vt. 走近与……搭话}
  \wordphrase{accosted me}
  \wordexample{My ex-wife accosted me in the driveway demanding an increase in alimony.（我的前妻从车道上过来跟我搭话，要求我提高赡养费。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{costalgia}{kɒs'tældʒiə}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 COST}
  \wordfamily{accost；protocol（（条约等的）草案）；collage（拼贴画（用纸、布、照片、金属等的小片粘贴拼合而成）}
  \wordsense{n. 肋骨痛}
  \wordphrase{a costalgia（肋骨痛）}
  \wordexample{a costalgia（肋骨痛）}
\end{word}



\chapter{JUD}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 jud 意为 to judge。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{adjudicate}{ə'dʒu:dikeit}
  \wordbreakdown{对某物进行判断 ad (= to) + jud (= judge)}
  \wordsense{v. 判决；宣告}
  \wordfamily{judicious}
  \wordfamily{judge}
  \wordfamily{judgment}
  \wordfamily{judicature}
  \wordphrase{adjudicates the fights I}
  \wordexample{My mother always adjudicates the fights I have with my sister.（每当我和我妹妹打架时，总是由妈妈来裁决。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{judicious}{dʒu:'diʃəs}
  \wordbreakdown{具有判断力的 jud (= judge)}
  \wordsense{a. 有判断力的；明智的}
  \wordfamily{adjudicate}
  \wordfamily{judge}
  \wordfamily{judgment}
  \wordfamily{judicature}
  \wordphrase{judicious use}
  \wordexample{The judicious use of antibiotics can prevent severe staphylococcus infections.（明智地使用抗生素可以预防葡萄球菌的严重感染。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{judge}{dʒʌdʒ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUD}
  \wordfamily{judgment；judicature；judicial；judiciary；adjudicate（判决）}

  \wordexample{A common collocation is "a judge".（常见搭配：裁判员。）}
  \wordsense{v. 判决，裁判；判断}
  \wordphrase{judge advocate（军法官；军事检察官）}
  \wordexample{judge advocate（军法官；军事检察官）}
  \wordexample{a judge（裁判员）}
\end{word}

  \wordphrase{in my judgment（以我之见）}
  \wordphrase{judgment seat（法官席；法庭）}

  \wordexample{in my judgment（以我之见）}
  \wordexample{judgment seat（法官席；法庭）}

  \wordphrase{be extrajudicial（司法程序以外的）}

  \wordsense{a. 缺乏判断力的，不明智的 *in (= not)}
  \wordphrase{injudicious remarks（不明智的话）}

\end{word}

  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUD}
  \wordfamily{prejudgment（不审而判）；adjudicate（判决）；judicious（有判断力的）}
  \wordsense{v. 过早判断，预先判断；【律】不审而判 *pre (= before)}
  \wordphrase{to prejudge（过早判断，预先判断）}
  \wordexample{to prejudge（过早判断，预先判断）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{prejudice}{'predʒudis}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUD}
  \wordfamily{prejudiced（抱有成见的，有偏见的）；adjudicate（判决）；judicious（有判断力的）}
  \wordsense{n. 偏见；【律】侵害，损害}
  \wordphrase{racial prejudice（种族偏见）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "racial prejudice".（常见搭配：种族偏见。）}
  \wordsense{v. 使抱偏见；使怀成见；损害 *pre (= before)}
  \wordphrase{a prejudice（偏见）}
  \wordexample{a prejudice（偏见）}
  \wordexample{to prejudice（使抱偏见）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{unprejudiced}{ʌn'predʒudist}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUD}
  \wordfamily{prejudice；prejudge；injudicious；extrajudicial；adjudicate（判决）}
  \wordsense{a. 无偏见的；公平的 *un (= not), pre (= before)}
  \wordphrase{be unprejudiced（无偏见的）}
  \wordexample{be unprejudiced（无偏见的）}
\end{word}



\chapter{JUNCT, JOIN[JUG]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 junct, join 意为 to join, to bind。jug 是它们的变形。此外，还有意为 to bind, to tie 的来自拉丁语的 lig [li, leag, ly], cinct, nect / nex, strict / string / stress 和来自盎格鲁-撒克逊语的 bond，含义相似。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{adjunct}{'ædʒʌŋkt}
  \wordbreakdown{另外再捆绑 ad (= to, add) + junct (= join, bind)}
  \wordfamily{enjoin；subjugate；conjunction；conjunctive}
  \wordsense{n. 附属物；助手，副手}
  \wordphrase{the adjunct}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of adjunct.（此处展示 adjunct 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{a. 附加的，辅助的}
  \wordphrase{adjunct to our print}
  \wordexample{A direct mail promotion will be an adjunct to our print advertising campaign.（以印刷广告活动为主，直接邮寄产品促销是辅助手段。）}
  \wordexample{adjunct to our print}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{enjoin}{in'dʒɔin}
  \wordbreakdown{用力捆绑 en (= intensive) + join (= join, bind)}
  \wordsense{vt. 命令；吩咐；禁止}
  \wordfamily{adjunct}
  \wordfamily{subjugate}
  \wordfamily{conjunction}
  \wordfamily{conjunctive}
  \wordphrase{enjoined from selling the}
  \wordexample{The store was enjoined from selling the inflammable children's clothing.（那家商店被禁止出售易燃性儿童服装。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{subjugate}{'sʌbdʒəgeit}
  \wordbreakdown{在下面捆 sub (= under) + jug (= bind, join)}
  \wordfamily{subjugation（征服）；adjunct（附属物）；enjoin（命令）}
  \wordsense{vt. 使服从，征服；使从属；使屈从}
  \wordphrase{subjugate women not only}
  \wordexample{Rapists subjugate women not only physically but also emotionally.（强奸犯不仅从身体上而且从精神上使女性屈从。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{conjunction}{kən'dʒʌŋkʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUNCT, JOIN[JUG]}
  \wordfamily{conjunctive；conjuncture；disjunctive；junction；adjunct（附属物）}
  \wordsense{n. 结合，连接；【语】连接词 *con<com (= together)}
  \wordphrase{in conjunction with（与……协力）}
  \wordphrase{a coordinate / subordinate conjunction（并列连词 / 从属连词）}
  \wordphrase{an unusual conjunction of circumstances（各种情况的奇特巧合）}
  \wordexample{in conjunction with（与……协力）}
  \wordexample{a coordinate / subordinate conjunction（并列连词 / 从属连词）}
  \wordexample{an unusual conjunction of circumstances（各种情况的奇特巧合）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{conjunctive}{kən'dʒʌŋktiv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUNCT, JOIN[JUG]}
  \wordfamily{conjunctiva（结膜）；conjunctivitis（结膜炎）；adjunct（附属物）}
  \wordsense{a. 结合的；连接的；【语】连接词的 *con<com (= together)}
  \wordphrase{conjunctive word（连接词）}
  \wordexample{conjunctive word（连接词）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{conjuncture}{kən'dʒʌŋktʃə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUNCT, JOIN[JUG]}
  \wordfamily{conjunctive；disjunctive；conjunction；junction；adjunct（附属物）}
  \wordsense{n. （事件的）共同发生；紧要关头 *con<com (= together)}
  \wordphrase{a conjuncture（共同发生）}
  \wordexample{a conjuncture（共同发生）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{disjunctive}{dis'dʒʌŋktiv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUNCT, JOIN[JUG]}
  \wordfamily{conjuncture；junction；conjunctive；juncture；adjunct（附属物）}
  \wordsense{a. 【语】转折的}
  \wordphrase{be disjunctive（转折的）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "be disjunctive".（常见搭配：转折的。）}
  \wordsense{n. 【语】转折连词 *dis (= away, apart)}
  \wordphrase{be disjunctive（转折的）}
  \wordexample{be disjunctive（转折的）}
  \wordexample{a disjunctive（转折连词）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{junction}{'dʒʌŋkʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUNCT, JOIN[JUG]}
  \wordsense{n. 连接，接合，汇合；（道路、铁道等的）会合处，交叉点；（铁路的）联轨站；（电路的）接点，中继}
  \wordfamily{disjunctive}
  \wordfamily{juncture}
  \wordfamily{conjuncture}
  \wordfamily{junta}
  \wordphrase{effect a junction with the allies（与联合军汇合）}
  \wordphrase{effect a junction with the Kyung-Bu Railway Line（与京釜铁路线汇合）}
  \wordphrase{junction circuit（中继电路）}

  \wordexample{effect a junction with the Kyung-Bu Railway Line（与京釜铁路线汇合）}

\begin{word}[**]{juncture}{'dʒʌŋktʃə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUNCT, JOIN[JUG]}
  \wordsense{n. 形势，情况，事态；危急关头；联合，接合；汇合}
  \wordfamily{junction}
  \wordfamily{junta}
  \wordfamily{disjunctive}

\begin{word}[*]{junta}{'dʒʌntə}
