\begin{word}[***]{string}{striŋ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 STRAIN, STRICT, STRING, STRESS[STRANG]}
  \wordfamily{pull（(the)）；strings（在幕后操纵）；string-puller（幕后操纵者）；constrain（迫使）}
  \wordsense{n. 绳（比 cord 细，比 thread 粗），线，细绳；一串东西，一连串；（乐器或弓等的）弦；（the strings）弦乐器；木偶牵线}
  \wordphrase{without strings（〈国家间的资金援助〉无附带条件的 / 无条件的）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "without strings".（常见搭配：〈国家间的资金援助〉无附带条件的 / 无条件的。）}
  \wordsense{v. 给（弓、小提琴等）上弦，调弦；用线串起（珍珠等）；用绳缚，系；排成一排前进}
  \wordphrase{a piece of string（一根线）}
  \wordphrase{a string of horses（一群马）}
  \wordexample{a string of beads / pearl（一串珠子 / 珍珠）}
  \wordexample{a string of abuses / curses（一连串的辱骂 / 诅咒）}
  \wordexample{a string of cars waiting at a red light（一连串等红灯的车）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{stringy}{ˈstriŋi}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 STRAIN, STRICT, STRING, STRESS[STRANG]}
  \wordfamily{string；strangle；astringent；strait；constrain（迫使）}
  \wordsense{a. 线状的，绳状的；多纤维的；有筋的；黏稠的；瘦而结实的}
  \wordphrase{stringy meat（有筋的肉）}
  \wordexample{stringy meat（有筋的肉）}
\end{word}



\chapter{TEMPOR}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 `tempor` 意为 `time, age, season`。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 `ev` 和来自希腊语的 `chron`。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{contemporary}{kənˈtemprəri}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TEMPOR}
  \wordfamily{contemporaneous（同时发生的，同时代的）；extemporary（即席的）；extempore（/）}
  \wordsense{a. 现代的，当代的；同时代的}
  \wordphrase{be contemporary}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of contemporary.（此处展示 contemporary 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. （pl.）同时代的人；当代人；同龄人}
  \wordphrase{contemporary poets can't make}
  \wordexample{Many contemporary poets can't make enough money to sustain them by writing poetry.（许多现代诗人都无法靠写诗来赚钱维持生计。）}
  \wordexample{contemporary poets can't make}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{extemporary}{ekˈstemprəri}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TEMPOR}
  \wordfamily{contemporary；extempore；extemporaneous；temporary}
  \wordsense{a. 即席的，即兴的 *ex (= out)}
  \wordphrase{be extemporary（即席的，即兴的）}
  \wordexample{be extemporary（即席的，即兴的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{extempore}{ekˈstempəri}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TEMPOR}
  \wordfamily{contemporary（现代的）；extemporary（即席的）；extemporaneous（即席的）}
  \wordsense{ad. /}
  \wordphrase{speak extempore（即兴发言）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "speak extempore".（常见搭配：即兴发言。）}
  \wordsense{a. 即席地（的），即兴地（的），无准备地（的） *ex (= out)}
  \wordphrase{speak extempore（即兴发言）}
  \wordexample{extempore（/a. 即席地（的），即兴地（的），无准备地（的））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{extemporaneous}{ekˌstempəˈreiniəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TEMPOR}
  \wordfamily{extempore；temporary；extemporary；temporal；contemporary（现代的）}
  \wordsense{a. 即席的，即兴的，无准备的；临时的；权宜之计的 *ex (= out)}
  \wordphrase{extemporaneous speech（即席讲话）}
  \wordphrase{extemporaneous death（当场死亡）}
  \wordphrase{extemporaneous shelter against a storm（为躲避暴风雨而临时搭建的避难所）}
  \wordexample{extemporaneous speech（即席讲话）}
  \wordexample{extemporaneous death（当场死亡）}
  \wordexample{extemporaneous shelter against a storm（为躲避暴风雨而临时搭建的避难所）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{temporary}{ˈtemprəri}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TEMPOR}
  \wordfamily{temporarily（暂时地，临时地）；contemporary（现代的）；extemporary（即席的）}
  \wordsense{a. 临时的，暂时的，一时的}
  \wordphrase{temporary employment（临时雇用）}
  \wordphrase{temporary job（临时工作）}
  \wordphrase{temporary measures（权宜之计）}
  \wordexample{temporary employment（临时雇用）}
  \wordexample{temporary job（临时工作）}
  \wordexample{temporary measures（权宜之计）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{temporal}{ˈtempərəl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TEMPOR}
  \wordfamily{temporary；temporality；extemporaneous；temporize；contemporary（现代的）}
  \wordsense{a. 时间的；世俗的，现世的；暂时的}
  \wordphrase{be temporal}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of temporal.（此处展示 temporal 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 颞部}
  \wordphrase{temporal power of the Pope（教皇处理世俗事务的俗权）}
  \wordphrase{temporal affairs（世俗之事）}
  \wordphrase{temporal prosperity（一时的繁荣）}
  \wordexample{temporal power of the Pope（教皇处理世俗事务的俗权）}
  \wordexample{temporal affairs（世俗之事）}
  \wordexample{temporal prosperity（一时的繁荣）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{temporality}{ˌtempəˈræləti}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TEMPOR}
  \wordfamily{temporalty（《古》〈总称〉俗人）；contemporary（现代的）；extemporary（即席的）}
  \wordsense{n. 暂时性；（pl.）宗教团体的不动产}
  \wordphrase{temporalities of the Church（教会的不动产）}
  \wordexample{temporalities of the Church（教会的不动产）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{temporize}{ˈtempəraiz}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TEMPOR}
  \wordsense{v. 拖延（决定、答复等）；（为争取时间而）敷衍；迎合潮流}
  \wordfamily{temporality}
  \wordfamily{temporizing}
  \wordfamily{temporal}
  \wordfamily{tempest}
  \wordfamily{to temporize（拖延（决定、答复等））}
  \wordphrase{to temporize（拖延（决定、答复等））}
  \wordexample{to temporize（拖延（决定、答复等））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{temporizing}{ˈtempəraiziŋ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TEMPOR}
  \wordsense{a.（为争取时间而）敷衍的；顺应时势的，迎合潮流的}
  \wordfamily{temporize}
  \wordfamily{tempest}
  \wordfamily{temporality}
  \wordfamily{tempestuous}
  \wordphrase{temporizing politicians / answer（敷衍的政客 / 回答）}
  \wordphrase{temporizing measures（权宜之计）}
  \wordexample{temporizing politicians / answer（敷衍的政客 / 回答）}
  \wordexample{temporizing measures（权宜之计）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{tempest}{ˈtempist}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TEMPOR}
  \wordfamily{temporizing；tempestuous；temporize；temporality；contemporary（现代的）}
  \wordsense{n. 暴风雨；骚乱，动乱 *temp(tempor (= time))}
  \wordphrase{tempest-swept / tempest-tossed（受暴风雨横扫的 / 被暴风雨摇弄、颠簸翻摇的）}
  \wordexample{tempest-swept / tempest-tossed（受暴风雨横扫的 / 被暴风雨摇弄、颠簸翻摇的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{tempestuous}{temˈpestʃuəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TEMPOR}
  \wordfamily{contemporary（现代的）；extemporary（即席的）；extempore（/）}
  \wordsense{a. 暴风雨的；暴风雨般的；剧烈的；骚动的}
  \wordphrase{in a tempestuous mood（心情激动的）}
  \wordphrase{tempestuous political meeting（喧闹的政治集会）}
  \wordexample{in a tempestuous mood（心情激动的）}
  \wordexample{tempestuous political meeting（喧闹的政治集会）}
\end{word}



\chapter{CID, CAD, CAS}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 `cid`，`cad`，`cas` 意为 `to fall`。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 `laps` 和来自希腊语的 `pto`。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{coincide}{ˌkəuinˈsaid}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CID, CAD, CAS}
  \wordsense{vi. with + sth 同时发生；（想法等）一致；重合}
  \wordfamily{coincidence 同时发生；恰巧一致}
  \wordfamily{coincident 一致的；同时发生的}
  \wordfamily{coincidental 恰巧一致的；同时发生的}
  \wordphrase{coincides with our dinner}
  \wordexample{Your arrival coincides with our dinner hour, so why don't you eat with us?（你到时恰巧是我们吃晚饭的时间，那你就和我们一起吃饭吧。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{decadence}{ˈdekədəns}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CID, CAD, CAS}
  \wordsense{n.（道德等的）堕落，颓废，衰退}
  \wordfamily{decay 腐烂，衰退；（n.）腐败，衰落}
  \wordfamily{decadent 堕落的，颓废的}
  \wordphrase{decadence}
  \wordexample{Conservative religions often preach against worldly decadence.（保守的宗教常常宣扬要抵制世俗的堕落。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{accident}{ˈæksidənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CID, CAD, CAS}
  \wordfamily{decadence；accidental；coincide；deciduous}
  \wordsense{n. （突发）事故；灾害，灾难；偶然（性） *ac<ad (= to, near)}
  \wordphrase{accident insurance（伤害 / 灾害保险）}
  \wordphrase{have an accident（遭遇事故）}
  \wordexample{Accidents will happen.（出事是难免的。）}
  \wordexample{accident insurance（伤害 / 灾害保险）}
  \wordexample{have an accident（遭遇事故）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{accidental}{ˌæksiˈdentl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CID, CAD, CAS}
  \wordfamily{accidentally（偶然地，意外地）；coincide（with + sth 同时发生）；decadence（（道德等的）堕落）}
  \wordsense{a. 偶然的，意外的 *ac<ad (= to, near)}
  \wordphrase{accidental death（意外死亡）}
  \wordphrase{accidental homicide（意外杀人）}
  \wordexample{accidental death（意外死亡）}
  \wordexample{accidental homicide（意外杀人）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{deciduous}{diˈsidʒuəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CID, CAD, CAS}
  \wordfamily{accidental；incident；accident；incidental；coincide（with + sth 同时发生）}
  \wordsense{a. （树木）落叶性的；（牙、角等）定期脱落的 *de (= down)}
  \wordphrase{deciduous tree（落叶树）}
  \wordphrase{deciduous teeth（乳齿，乳牙）}
  \wordexample{deciduous tree（落叶树）}
  \wordexample{deciduous teeth（乳齿，乳牙）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{incident}{ˈinsidənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CID, CAD, CAS}
  \wordfamily{deciduous；incidental；accidental；incidence；coincide（with + sth 同时发生）}
  \wordsense{n. 事情；（政治中的）事件，变故，事变}
  \wordphrase{the incident}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of incident.（此处展示 incident 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{a. 容易发生的；伴随的 *in (= into)}
  \wordphrase{be incident}
  \wordexample{Political Incident of the Year of Kapshin（甲申政变）}
  \wordexample{risks incident to the life of a test pilot（试飞员免不了要冒的生命危险）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{incidental}{ˌinsiˈdentl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CID, CAD, CAS}
  \wordfamily{coincide（with + sth 同时发生）；decadence（（道德等的）堕落）；accident（（突发）事故）}
  \wordsense{a. 伴随的，附带的；次要的；易发生的 *in (= into)}
  \wordphrase{incidental expenses（额外开支，杂费）}
  \wordexample{discomforts incidental to exploration in a wild country（在未开拓地区的探险过程中易碰到的不便之处）}
  \wordexample{incidental expenses（额外开支，杂费）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{incidence}{ˈinsidəns}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CID, CAD, CAS}
  \wordfamily{incidental；occident；incident；occidental；coincide（with + sth 同时发生）}
  \wordsense{n. 发生率；（事件的）影响 *in (= into)}
  \wordphrase{incidence of a disease（某种疾病的发病率）}
  \wordexample{incidence of a disease（某种疾病的发病率）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{occident}{ˈɔksidənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CID, CAD, CAS}

\chapter{SOLID[SOLD]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 `solid` 意为 `firm, whole`。注意勿与意为 `comfort, alone, sun, whole / entire` 的 `sol` 相混淆。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{consolidate}{kənˈsɔlideit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SOLID[SOLD]}
  \wordsense{vt. 合并，统一；变坚固，强化}
  \wordfamily{consolidation 强化；合并，统一}
  \wordphrase{The companies consolidated several}
  \wordexample{The companies consolidated several divisions after their merger.（那几家公司联合之后合并了几个部门。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{solid}{ˈsɔlid}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SOLID[SOLD]}
  \wordfamily{consolidate（合并）；solidarity（（源自于共同的利益关系或感情的）一致）；solidify（（使）坚固）}
  \wordsense{a. 固体的；（材料、结构）结实的，坚固的；可靠的，坚实的；全体一致的；团结的；连续的，不间断的}
  \wordphrase{be solid}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of solid.（此处展示 solid 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 立方体；固体}
  \wordphrase{solid fuels（固体燃料）}
  \wordphrase{Solid South（the \textasciitilde{}）（《美》〈传统上一贯支持民主党的〉南方诸州）}
  \wordexample{solid fuels（固体燃料）}
  \wordexample{Solid South（the \textasciitilde{}）（《美》〈传统上一贯支持民主党的〉南方诸州）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{solidarity}{ˌsɔliˈdærəti}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SOLID[SOLD]}
  \wordsense{n.（源自于共同的利益关系或感情的）一致，团结}
  \wordfamily{solid}
  \wordfamily{solidify}
  \wordfamily{consolidate}
  \wordfamily{solder}
  \wordphrase{national solidarity in the face of danger（面对危难全国人民团结一致）}
  \wordexample{national solidarity in the face of danger（面对危难全国人民团结一致）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{solidify}{səˈlidifai}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SOLID[SOLD]}
  \wordfamily{consolidate（合并）；solid（固体的）；solidarity（（源自于共同的利益关系或感情的）一致）}
  \wordsense{v. （使）坚固；（使）凝固；使一致，使团结}
  \wordphrase{to solidify（坚固）}
  \wordexample{to solidify（坚固）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{solder}{ˈsəuldə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SOLID[SOLD]}
  \wordfamily{consolidate（合并）；solid（固体的）；solidarity（（源自于共同的利益关系或感情的）一致）}
  \wordsense{n. 焊锡}
  \wordphrase{soldering iron（烙铁）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "soldering iron".（常见搭配：烙铁。）}
  \wordsense{v. 焊接 *sold<solid (= firm, whole)}
  \wordphrase{a solder（焊锡）}
  \wordexample{a solder（焊锡）}
  \wordexample{to solder（焊接）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{soldier}{ˈsəuldʒə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SOLID[SOLD]}
  \wordfamily{soldierly（/ soldierlike（军人般的）；consolidate（合并）；solid（固体的）}
  \wordsense{n. （陆军的）军人，士兵}
  \wordphrase{private soldier（普通兵）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "private soldier".（常见搭配：普通兵。）}
  \wordsense{v. 当兵 *sold<solid (= firm, whole)}
  \wordphrase{soldier of fortune（〈国家或个人的〉雇佣兵）}
  \wordexample{soldier of fortune（〈国家或个人的〉雇佣兵）}
  \wordexample{a soldier（军人，士兵）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{soldiery}{ˈsəuldʒəri}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SOLID[SOLD]}
  \wordfamily{consolidate（合并）；solid（固体的）；solidarity（（源自于共同的利益关系或感情的）一致）}
  \wordsense{n. ［总称］军人 *sold<solid (= firm, whole)}
  \wordphrase{soldiery}
  \wordexample{undisciplined soldiery（不遵守纪律的军人）}
\end{word}



\chapter{VICT, VINC}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根 VICT, VINC 来自拉丁语的 `vict`, `vinc`，意为 `to conquer`, `to show`。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{conviction}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{kən'vikʃn；完全征服 con<com (= intensive) + vict (= conquer)}
  \wordfamily{evince；invincible；evict；victim}
  \wordsense{n. 定罪，宣告有罪；确信，信念。convict：宣告有罪，使认罪；（}
  \wordphrase{It's my conviction that the defendant indeed committed the murder（我确信被告确实杀了人。）}
  \wordexample{It's my conviction that the defendant indeed committed the murder.（我确信被告确实杀了人。）}
  \wordsense{n. ）已决犯。It's my conviction that the defendant indeed committed the murder.（我确信被告确实杀了人。）}
  \wordphrase{the conviction}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of conviction.（此处展示 conviction 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 定罪，宣告有罪）}
  \wordphrase{the conviction}
  \wordexample{conviction（n. 定罪，宣告有罪）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{evince}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{i'vins；向外展示或完全征服 e<ex (= out, intensive) + vinc (= show, conquer)}
  \wordfamily{conviction；invincible；evict；victim}
  \wordsense{vt. 表明；显示出。evincible 可表明的；evincive 显示的。The young boy evinced no shame for the items he had stolen.（那个小男孩对偷盗物品没有表示出羞愧之意。）}
  \wordphrase{The young boy evinced no shame for the items he had stolen（那个小男孩对偷盗物品没有表示出羞愧之意。）}
  \wordexample{The young boy evinced no shame for the items he had stolen.（那个小男孩对偷盗物品没有表示出羞愧之意。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 表明）}
  \wordphrase{evince the issue}
  \wordexample{evince（vt. 表明）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{invincible}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{in'vinsəbl；无法征服的 in (= not) + vinc (= conquer)}
  \wordfamily{evince；evict；conviction；victim}
  \wordsense{a. 不能征服的；无敌的。invincibility 无敌，不可战胜；invincibly 无敌地。The North Vietnamese proved to be invincible, despite America's military might.（尽管美国军事力量强大，越南人民仍然证明了自己是不可战胜的。）}
  \wordphrase{The North Vietnamese proved to be invincible, despite America's military might（尽管美国军事力量强大，越南人民仍然证明了自己是不可战胜的。）}
  \wordexample{The North Vietnamese proved to be invincible, despite America's military might.（尽管美国军事力量强大，越南人民仍然证明了自己是不可战胜的。）}
  \wordsense{a. 不能征服的）}
  \wordphrase{be invincible}
  \wordexample{invincible（a. 不能征服的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{evict}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{i'vikt}
  \wordfamily{invincible；victim；evince；victimize；conviction（定罪）}
  \wordsense{v. 逐出。e<ex (= out)。eviction（逐出，驱逐）。He was evicted for not paying the rent.（他因为没交房租而被赶走了。）}
  \wordphrase{He was evicted for not paying the rent（他因为没交房租而被赶走了。）}
  \wordexample{He was evicted for not paying the rent.（他因为没交房租而被赶走了。）}
  \wordexample{evict（v. 逐出。e<ex (= out)。eviction（逐出，驱逐）。He was evicted for not paying the rent.（他因为没交房租而被赶走了。））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{victim}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{'viktim}
  \wordfamily{evict；victimize；invincible；victor；conviction（定罪）}
  \wordsense{n. 牺牲者，受害者，遇难者；（祭祀用的）牺牲。easy victim（容易受害的人）；victim of circumstance（环境的牺牲者，如受所处环境影响而犯罪的人）；the victims of war（战争的牺牲者）；the victims of the earthquake（震区灾民）}
  \wordphrase{easy victim（容易受害的人）}
  \wordphrase{victim of circumstance（环境的牺牲者，如受所处环境影响而犯罪的人）}
  \wordphrase{the victims of war（战争的牺牲者）}
  \wordexample{victim（n. 牺牲者，受害者，遇难者）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{victimize}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{'viktimaiz}
  \wordfamily{victim；victor；evict；Victoria；conviction（定罪）}
  \wordsense{v. 使牺牲。victimization（牺牲）。Trade union leaders were victimized.（工会的领导们牺牲了。）}
  \wordphrase{Trade union leaders were victimized（工会的领导们牺牲了。）}
  \wordexample{Trade union leaders were victimized.（工会的领导们牺牲了。）}
  \wordexample{victimize（v. 使牺牲。victimization（牺牲）。Trade union leaders were victimized.（工会的领导们牺牲了。））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{victor}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{'viktə(r)}
  \wordfamily{victimize；Victoria；victim；Victorian；conviction（定罪）}
  \wordsense{n. 胜利者，征服者}
  \wordphrase{victor nation（战胜国）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "victor nation".（常见搭配：战胜国。）}
  \wordsense{a. 胜利（者）的。victor nation（战胜国）}
  \wordphrase{be victor}
  \wordexample{victor（n. 胜利者，征服者）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{Victoria}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{vik'tɔ:riə}
  \wordfamily{victor；Victorian；victimize；victory；conviction（定罪）}
  \wordsense{n. 英国维多利亚女王（1819—1901）；维多利亚（女子名）；维多利亚城。Victoria Cross（维多利亚十字勋章，由维多利亚女王设立，授予有杰出功勋的军人，缩写：V.C.）；Victoria Falls（维多利亚瀑布）}
  \wordphrase{Victoria Cross（维多利亚十字勋章，由维多利亚女王设立，授予有杰出功勋的军人，缩写：V.C.）}
  \wordphrase{Victoria Falls（维多利亚瀑布）}
  \wordexample{Victoria（n. 英国维多利亚女王（1819—1901））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{Victorian}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{vik'tɔ:riən}
  \wordfamily{Victoria；victory；victor；victorious；conviction（定罪）}
  \wordsense{a. 维多利亚女王（时代）的；固执的，守旧的。the (Royal) Victorian Order（维多利亚勋章，由维多利亚女王设立，授予为国王立下杰出功勋之人，缩写：V.O.）；the Victorian age（维多利亚时代）；Victorian dress / manners / authors（维多利亚女王时代的服装、风俗、作者）}
  \wordphrase{Victorian Order（维多利亚勋章，由维多利亚女王设立，授予为国王立下杰出功勋之人，缩写：V.O.）}
  \wordphrase{the Victorian age（维多利亚时代）}
  \wordphrase{Victorian dress / manners / authors（维多利亚女王时代的服装、风俗、作者）}
  \wordexample{Victorian（a. 维多利亚女王（时代）的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{victory}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{'viktəri}
  \wordfamily{Victorian；victorious；Victoria；victual；conviction（定罪）}
  \wordsense{n. 胜利，战胜。gain / get / win a victory over the enemy（战胜敌人）；victory over difficulty（战胜困难）}
  \wordphrase{gain / get / win a victory over the enemy（战胜敌人）}
  \wordphrase{victory over difficulty（战胜困难）}
  \wordexample{gain / get / win a victory over the enemy（战胜敌人）}
  \wordexample{victory（n. 胜利，战胜。gain / get / win a victory over the enemy（战胜敌人））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{victorious}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{vik'tɔ:riəs}
  \wordfamily{victory；victual；Victorian；convince；conviction（定罪）}
  \wordsense{a. 获胜的，战胜的。victorious army（胜利军）}
  \wordphrase{victorious army（胜利军）}
  \wordexample{victorious（a. 获胜的，战胜的。victorious army（胜利军））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{victual}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{'vitl}
  \wordfamily{victorious；convince；victory；convincing；conviction（定罪）}
  \wordsense{n. （pl.）食物，粮食}
  \wordphrase{victual a ship（给船上提供粮食）}
  \wordphrase{The ship victualed at Pusan（那艘船在釜山储备粮食。）}
  \wordexample{The ship victualed at Pusan.（那艘船在釜山储备粮食。）}
  \wordsense{v. 提供食物，储备食物。victualer（专为船或部队供应食物者）；victual a ship（给船上提供粮食）。The ship victualed at Pusan.（那艘船在釜山储备粮食。）}
  \wordphrase{victual the issue}
  \wordexample{victual（n.（pl.）食物，粮食）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{convince}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{kən'vins}
  \wordfamily{victual；convincing；victorious；vincible；conviction（定罪）}
  \wordsense{v. 使确信，使信服。con<com (= thoroughly)。I am convinced of his honesty. / I am convinced that he is honest.（我相信他很正直。）}
  \wordphrase{convince the issue}
  \wordexample{I am convinced of his honesty. / I am convinced that he is honest.（我相信他很正直。）}
  \wordexample{convince（v. 使确信，使信服。con<com (= thoroughly)。I am convinced of his honesty. / I am convinced that he is honest.（我相信他很正直。））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{convincing}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{kən'vinsiŋ}
  \wordfamily{convince；vincible；victual；vanquish；conviction（定罪）}
  \wordsense{a. 令人信服的，有说服力的。convincingly（令人信服地，有说服力地）；convincing speaker / argument（令人信服的演说家、论据）}
  \wordphrase{convincing speaker / argument（令人信服的演说家、论据）}
  \wordexample{convincing（a. 令人信服的，有说服力的。convincingly（令人信服地，有说服力地））}
\end{word}

  \wordbreakdown{'vinsəbl}
  \wordfamily{convincing；vanquish；convince；victual；conviction（定罪）}

  \wordphrase{be vincible}
  \wordexample{vincible（a. 可征服的，可战胜的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{vanquish}{[]}

  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of vanquish.（此处展示 vanquish 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. 征服，击败）}
  \wordphrase{vanquish the issue}
  \wordexample{vanquish（v. 征服，击败）}
\end{word}



\chapter{LABOR}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根 LABOR 来自拉丁语的 `labor`，意为 `to work`。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 `op`。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{collaborate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{kə'læbəreit；一起工作 col<com (= with) + labor (= work)}
  \wordfamily{laborious；elaborate；belabor；labor}
  \wordsense{vi. with + sb; on + sth 合作；协作。collaboration 协作，合作；collaborator 通敌者；合作者；共同研究者。The conductor collaborated brilliantly with the members of the orchestra.（那位指挥和交响乐团的成员们合作得非常出色。）}
  \wordphrase{The conductor collaborated brilliantly with the members of the orchestra（那位指挥和交响乐团的成员们合作得非常出色。）}
  \wordexample{The conductor collaborated brilliantly with the members of the orchestra.（那位指挥和交响乐团的成员们合作得非常出色。）}
  \wordsense{vi. with + sb）}
  \wordphrase{collaborate in practice}
  \wordexample{collaborate（vi. with + sb）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{laborious}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{lə'bɔ:riəs；做很多事情的 labor (= work)}
  \wordfamily{collaborate；elaborate；belabor；labor}
  \wordsense{a. 努力的，辛苦的；费力的；生硬的；不流畅的。Restoring the ancient murals to their former splendor is a laborious task.（将古代壁画恢复到曾经的光彩是一项很费力气的工作。）}
  \wordphrase{Restoring the ancient murals to their former splendor is a laborious task（将古代壁画恢复到曾经的光彩是一项很费力气的工作。）}
  \wordexample{Restoring the ancient murals to their former splendor is a laborious task.（将古代壁画恢复到曾经的光彩是一项很费力气的工作。）}
  \wordsense{a. 努力的，辛苦的）}
  \wordphrase{be laborious}
  \wordexample{laborious（a. 努力的，辛苦的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{elaborate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{i'læbəreit}
  \wordfamily{laborious；belabor；collaborate；labor}
  \wordsense{v. 精心制作；详尽阐述}
  \wordphrase{Please elaborate your proposals a little（请详细说明一下你的提案。）}
  \wordphrase{elaborate design（精心的设计）}
  \wordphrase{elaborate plan（详细的计划）}
  \wordexample{Please elaborate your proposals a little.（请详细说明一下你的提案。）}
  \wordsense{a. 精心制作的；煞费苦心的；精巧的；复杂的。e<ex (= out)。elaboration（精心制作；精心之作；详尽的细节）。Please elaborate your proposals a little.（请详细说明一下你的提案。）elaborate design（精心的设计）；elaborate plan（详细的计划）；elaborate dinner（精心准备的晚餐）}
  \wordphrase{be elaborate}
  \wordexample{elaborate（v. 精心制作）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{belabor}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{bi'leibə(r)}
  \wordfamily{elaborate；labor；laborious；labored；collaborate（with + sb; on + sth 合作）}
  \wordsense{v. 毒打；痛骂。be (= make)}
  \wordphrase{belabor the issue}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of belabor.（此处展示 belabor 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. 毒打）}
  \wordphrase{belabor the issue}
  \wordexample{belabor（v. 毒打）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{labor}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{'leibə(r)}
  \wordfamily{belabor；labored；elaborate；laborer；collaborate（with + sb; on + sth 合作）}
  \wordsense{n. 劳动；〔总称〕劳工，工人；工作；努力；阵痛，分娩；（L-）（英国或英联邦国家的）工党}
  \wordphrase{hard labor（苦役；苦工）}
  \wordphrase{manual labor（体力劳动）}
  \wordphrase{division of labor（劳动分工）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "hard labor".（常见搭配：苦役；苦工。）}
  \wordsense{v. 劳动；工作；努力；费力地前进；苦恼；详细解释，详细论述；经受阵痛。hard labor（苦役；苦工）；manual labor（体力劳动）；division of labor（劳动分工）；cheap labor（廉价劳动力）；labor and capital（劳资）；labor and management（劳动者和管理者）；be in labor（分娩中）；a woman in labor（正在分娩的女子）}
  \wordphrase{labor the issue}
  \wordexample{labor（n. 劳动）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{labored}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{'leibəd}
  \wordfamily{labor；laborer；belabor；laboratory；collaborate（with + sb; on + sth 合作）}
  \wordsense{a. 吃力的；（言谈等）不自然的。labored breathing（费力的呼吸）}
  \wordphrase{labored breathing（费力的呼吸）}
  \wordexample{labored（a. 吃力的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{laborer}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{'leibərə(r)}
  \wordfamily{labored；laboratory；labor；belabor；collaborate（with + sb; on + sth 合作）}
  \wordsense{n. 体力劳动者。Laborite（工党党员，工党支持者）；agricultural laborer（农业劳动者）}
  \wordphrase{agricultural laborer（农业劳动者）}
  \wordexample{laborer（n. 体力劳动者。Laborite（工党党员，工党支持者））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{laboratory}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{lə'bɔrətri}
  \wordfamily{laborer；labored；labor；belabor；collaborate（with + sb; on + sth 合作）}
  \wordsense{n. 实验室，研究室；实验课。-ory (= place where)。chemical laboratory（化学实验室）；course with two lectures and one laboratory（讲两次课做一次实验的科目）}
  \wordphrase{chemical laboratory（化学实验室）}
  \wordphrase{course with two lectures and one laboratory（讲两次课做一次实验的科目）}
  \wordexample{course with two lectures and one laboratory（讲两次课做一次实验的科目）}
  \wordsense{n. 实验室，研究室）}
  \wordphrase{the laboratory}
  \wordexample{laboratory（n. 实验室，研究室）}
\end{word}



\chapter{MOT, MOV, MOB}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根 MOT, MOV, MOB 来自拉丁语的 `mot`, `mov`, `mob`，意为 `to move`。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 `migr` 和来自希腊语的 `kine / cinema`。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{commotion}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{kə'məuʃn；一起动 com (= together) + mot (= move)}
  \wordfamily{motivate；immobile；demote；emotion}
  \wordsense{n. 骚动；动乱；暴乱。There was a huge commotion in the hall when Aunt Betty dropped a tray of drinks.（贝蒂姑妈弄掉一个饮料盘，大厅里一阵混乱。）}
  \wordphrase{the commotion}
  \wordexample{There was a huge commotion in the hall when Aunt Betty dropped a tray of drinks.（贝蒂姑妈弄掉一个饮料盘，大厅里一阵混乱。）}
  \wordsense{n. 骚动）}
  \wordphrase{the commotion}
  \wordexample{commotion（n. 骚动）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{motivate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{'məutiveit；使感动，使心动 mot (= move)}
  \wordfamily{commotion；immobile；demote；emotion}
  \wordsense{vt. 给予……动机；刺激；激发。motivation 刺激，推动；motive 动机；（文艺作品的）主题；motiveless 毫无动机的。The picture of the fat lady on my refrigerator motivates me not to eat.（贴在电冰箱上的胖女人图片促使我节食。）}
  \wordphrase{The picture of the fat lady on my refrigerator motivates me not to eat（贴在电冰箱上的胖女人图片促使我节食。）}
  \wordexample{The picture of the fat lady on my refrigerator motivates me not to eat.（贴在电冰箱上的胖女人图片促使我节食。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 给予……动机）}
  \wordphrase{motivate the issue}
  \wordexample{motivate（vt. 给予……动机）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{immobile}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{i'məubail；不能移动的 im<in (= not) + mob (= move)}
  \wordfamily{motivate；demote；commotion；emotion}
  \wordsense{a. 不能移动的，不动的；固定的。immobilize 使不能移动；immobility 不动，静止；固定。A plaster cast holds a broken bone immobile, allowing it to heal.（石膏绷带固定住骨折的地方，以使其恢复。）}
  \wordphrase{A plaster cast holds a broken bone immobile, allowing it to heal（石膏绷带固定住骨折的地方，以使其恢复。）}
  \wordexample{A plaster cast holds a broken bone immobile, allowing it to heal.（石膏绷带固定住骨折的地方，以使其恢复。）}
  \wordsense{a. 不能移动的，不动的）}
  \wordphrase{be immobile}
  \wordexample{immobile（a. 不能移动的，不动的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{demote}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{di'məut}
  \wordfamily{immobile；emotion；motivate；emotional；commotion（骚动）}
  \wordsense{v. 使降级。de (= down)。demoted（降级的）}
  \wordphrase{demote the issue}
  \wordexample{demote（v. 使降级。de (= down)。demoted（降级的））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{emotion}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{i'məuʃn}
  \wordfamily{demote；emotional；immobile；locomotion；commotion（骚动）}
  \wordsense{n. 感情，情绪；激动。e<ex (= out)。betray one's emotion（流露感情）}
  \wordphrase{betray one's emotion（流露感情）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "betray one's emotion".（常见搭配：流露感情。）}
  \wordsense{n. 感情，情绪）}
  \wordphrase{the emotion}
  \wordexample{emotion（n. 感情，情绪）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{emotional}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{i'məuʃənl}
  \wordfamily{emotion；locomotion；demote；locomotive；commotion（骚动）}
  \wordsense{a. 感情的，情绪的；易动情的；激起感情的。e<ex (= out)。emotionally（情绪上，感情上）；emotive（感情的；情绪的）；emotionless（没有感情的，冷漠的）；emotional appeal（情感诉求）；emotional woman（易动情的女子）}
  \wordphrase{emotional appeal（情感诉求）}
  \wordphrase{emotional woman（易动情的女子）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "emotional appeal".（常见搭配：情感诉求。）}
  \wordsense{a. 感情的，情绪的）}
  \wordphrase{be emotional}
  \wordexample{emotional（a. 感情的，情绪的）}
\end{word}

  \wordfamily{emotional；locomotive；emotion；promote；commotion（骚动）}
  \wordsense{n. 运动，移动。loc (= to place)}
  \wordphrase{the locomotion}
  \wordexample{locomotion（n. 运动，移动。loc (= to place)）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{locomotive}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{ˌləukə'məutiv}
  \wordfamily{locomotion；promote；emotional；promoter；commotion（骚动）}
  \wordsense{n. 机车}
  \wordphrase{steam / diesel / electric locomotives（蒸汽、柴油、电力机车）}
  \wordphrase{locomotive engineer（火车司机）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "steam / diesel / electric locomotives".（常见搭配：蒸汽、柴油、电力机车。）}
  \wordsense{a. 移动的，有移动力的。loc (= to place)。steam / diesel / electric locomotives（蒸汽、柴油、电力机车）；locomotive engineer（火车司机）}
  \wordphrase{be locomotive}
  \wordexample{locomotive（n. 机车）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{promote}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{prə'məut}
  \wordfamily{locomotive；promoter；locomotion；promotion；commotion（骚动）}
  \wordsense{v. 使晋升，使晋级；推进，促进；发起。pro (= forward, forth)。He was promoted lieutenant colonel / to lieutenant colonel / to the rank of lieutenant colonel.（他晋升为陆军中校。）promote good feelings（增进好感）；promote a new business company（成立新公司）}
  \wordphrase{promote good feelings（增进好感）}
  \wordphrase{promote a new business company（成立新公司）}
  \wordexample{He was promoted lieutenant colonel / to lieutenant colonel / to the rank of lieutenant colonel.（他晋升为陆军中校。）}
  \wordsense{v. 使晋升，使晋级）}
  \wordphrase{promote the issue}
  \wordexample{promote（v. 使晋升，使晋级）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{promoter}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{prə'məutə(r)}
  \wordfamily{promote；promotion；locomotive；remote；commotion（骚动）}
  \wordsense{n. 促进者，助长者；提倡者；（企业的）发起人；（营业性体育比赛的）承办人。pro (= forward, forth)}
  \wordphrase{the promoter}
  \wordexample{promoter（n. 促进者，助长者）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{promotion}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{prə'məuʃn}
  \wordfamily{promoter；remote；promote；motif；commotion（骚动）}
  \wordsense{n. 晋级，晋升；发起；助长，推进；（通过宣传、广告等的）宣传，推销。pro (= forward, forth)。 win / gain promotion（晋级，晋升）；promotion of a new commercial product / a new book（新商品、新书的推销、宣传）；sales promotion（促销）}
  \wordphrase{win / gain promotion（晋级，晋升）}
  \wordphrase{promotion of a new commercial product / a new book（新商品、新书的推销、宣传）}
  \wordphrase{sales promotion（促销）}
  \wordexample{promotion of a new commercial product / a new book（新商品、新书的推销、宣传）}
  \wordexample{promotion（n. 晋级，晋升）}

\begin{word}[***]{remote}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{ri'məut}
  \wordfamily{promotion；motif；promoter；motion；commotion（骚动）}

  \wordphrase{remote control（遥控）}
  \wordphrase{remote sensing（遥感）}
  \wordphrase{remote from the subject we are discussing（和我们所议论的主题关系甚远）}
  \wordexample{remote from the subject we are discussing（和我们所议论的主题关系甚远）}
  \wordexample{remote（a. （时空上）遥远的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{motif}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{məu'ti:f}
  \wordfamily{remote；motion；promotion；motor；commotion（骚动）}
  \wordsense{n. （美术、音乐的）主题}
  \wordphrase{the motif}
  \wordexample{motif（n. （美术、音乐的）主题）}

\begin{word}[***]{motion}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{'məuʃn}
  \wordfamily{motif；motor；remote；motorist；commotion（骚动）}
  \wordsense{n. 运动，移动；动作，姿势（如手势、点头等）；动议，提议}
  \wordphrase{motion sickness（晕船〔或车〕）}
  \wordphrase{graceful motions（优雅的举止）}
  \wordphrase{The motion was adopted / carried / rejected by a majority of six（那项动议以六票的多数选票被采纳、通过、否决。）}
  \wordexample{on the motion of Mr. King（在金先生的动议下）}
  \wordexample{The motion was adopted / carried / rejected by a majority of six.（那项动议以六票的多数选票被采纳、通过、否决。）}
  \wordexample{He motioned to me to come nearer.（他示意我走近一点。）}
  \wordsense{v. 向……示意，向……打手势。motionless（不动的，静止的）；motion sickness（晕船〔或车〕）；graceful motions（优雅的举止）；on the motion of Mr. King（在金先生的动议下）；The motion was adopted / carried / rejected by a majority of six.（那项动议以六票的多数选票被采纳、通过、否决。）He motioned me out.（他示意我出去。）He motioned to me to come nearer.（他示意我走近一点。）}
  \wordphrase{motion the issue}
  \wordexample{motion（n. 运动，移动）}
\end{word}

  \wordphrase{motor the issue}
  \wordexample{motor（n. 发动机，马达）}

  \wordexample{motto（n. 座右铭，箴言，格言，警句）}

\begin{word}[***]{move}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{mu:v}
  \wordfamily{demobilize；movable；automobile；movement；commotion（骚动）}
  \wordsense{v. （使）移动；搬家；使感动；提议；进展，进行；行动，采取措施；使排泄，通便}
  \wordphrase{next month（我们下个月搬家。）}
  \wordphrase{move out / in（搬出去、搬进来）}
  \wordphrase{We were all moved by her entreaties（我们都被她的恳求感动了。）}
  \wordexample{We were all moved by her entreaties.（我们都被她的恳求感动了。）}
  \wordexample{It was a moving sight.（这是令人感动的一幕。）}
  \wordexample{Mr. Chairman, I move that the money be used for library books.（主席先生，我提议用这笔钱来为图书馆购买一些书籍。）}
  \wordsense{n. （位置的）移动，行动；措施；（国际象棋等的）棋子的走法。We are moving (house) next month.（我们下个月搬家。）move out / in（搬出去、搬进来）。We were all moved by her entreaties.（我们都被她的恳求感动了。）It was a moving sight.（这是令人感动的一幕。）be moved to tears（感动得流下了眼泪）。Mr. Chairman, I move that the money be used for library books.（主席先生，我提议用这笔钱来为图书馆购买一些书籍。）Large enemy forces are on the move.（敌人的大军正在转移。）move towards settling the strike（制止罢工的措施）。It's your move.（该你走棋了。）}
  \wordphrase{the move}
  \wordexample{move（v. （使）移动）}
\end{word}

\chapter{GER, GEST [GIST]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根 GER, GEST 来自拉丁语的 `ger`, `gest`，意为 `to carry`, `to bear`。其变形为 `gist`。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 `fer`, `lat`, `port`, `veh / vect` 和来自希腊语的 `phor / pher`。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{congest}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{kən'dʒest；一起搬运 con<com (= together) + gest (= carry, bear)}
  \wordfamily{belligerent；digest；digestion；digestive}
  \wordsense{vt. 使拥挤；使充满；使充血。congestion 拥挤，充血；congested 拥挤的，充血的。A number of large vehicles congested the bridge.（众多大型车辆使大桥拥堵。）}
  \wordphrase{A number of large vehicles congested the bridge（众多大型车辆使大桥拥堵。）}
  \wordexample{A number of large vehicles congested the bridge.（众多大型车辆使大桥拥堵。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 使拥挤）}
  \wordphrase{congest the issue}
  \wordexample{congest（vt. 使拥挤）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{belligerent}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{bə'lidʒərənt；带来战争的 bell (= war) + ger (= carry)}
  \wordfamily{congest；digest；digestion；digestive}
  \wordsense{a. 战争的，交战中的，好战的}
  \wordphrase{Son, I can't tolerate much more of your belligerent attitude（孩子，我实在不能忍受你好斗的态度。）}
  \wordexample{Son, I can't tolerate much more of your belligerent attitude.（孩子，我实在不能忍受你好斗的态度。）}
  \wordsense{n. 交战国；参加斗殴的人（或组织）。belligerency 交战状态，好战性；cf. bellicose 好战的。Son, I can't tolerate much more of your belligerent attitude.（孩子，我实在不能忍受你好斗的态度。）}
  \wordphrase{the belligerent}
  \wordexample{belligerent（a. 战争的，交战中的，好战的）}
\end{word}

  \wordphrase{food hard / easy of digestion（难、易于消化的食物）}

\begin{word}[***]{registered}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{'redʒistəd}
  \wordfamily{register；registrar；suggestive；registration；congest（使拥挤）}
  \wordsense{a. 注册的，已登记的。re (= back), gist<gest (= carry, bear)。registered design / trademark / letter / mail（注册图案 / 注册商标 / 挂号信 / 挂号邮件）；registered nurse（注册护士，有证书的护士，缩写：RN）}
  \wordphrase{registered design / trademark / letter / mail（注册图案 / 注册商标 / 挂号信 / 挂号邮件）}
  \wordphrase{registered nurse（注册护士，有证书的护士，缩写：RN）}
  \wordexample{registered design / trademark / letter / mail（注册图案 / 注册商标 / 挂号信 / 挂号邮件）}
  \wordexample{registered（a. 注册的，已登记的。re (= back), gist<gest (= carry, bear)。registered design / trademark / letter / mail（注册图案 / 注册商标 / 挂号信 / 挂号邮件））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{registrar}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{ˌredʒi'strɑ:(r)}
  \wordfamily{registered；registration；register；registry；congest（使拥挤）}
  \wordsense{n. （美国大学里从事学生注册、核发成绩证明书、学籍管理等工作的）注册主任；户籍员。re (= back), gist<gest (= carry, bear)。Registrar's Office（美国大学的学籍科）}
  \wordphrase{Registrar's Office（美国大学的学籍科）}
  \wordexample{registrar（n. （美国大学里从事学生注册、核发成绩证明书、学籍管理等工作的）注册主任）}

  \wordphrase{registration number（汽车牌照号码）}
  \wordphrase{registration fee（注册费，登记费）}

  \wordsense{n. 登记，注册）}

  \wordphrase{the registry}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of registry.（此处展示 registry 的常见用法。）}

  \wordexample{registry（n. 登记处，注册处）}

\chapter{VIA[VI], VEY, VOY}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 via 意为 way, road。via 里的 “a” 脱落后变成 vi。词根 vey，voy 意为 to carry, way。需要注意的是，purvey / survey，clairvoyant / voyeur 等单词里的 vey / voy 意为 to look。同义词根有来自希腊语的 od / hod。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{convey}{kən'vei}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VIA[VI], VEY, VOY；带着某物搬运、转达 con<com (= with) + vey (= carry, way)}
  \wordfamily{envoy；deviate；impervious；obviate}
  \wordsense{vt. sth + to + sb 搬运；运输；传达（意思或信息），传导；转让。conveyance 运输；传达；转让；conveyer / conveyor 运输装置；运输者；conveyancer（办理财产转让事务的）律师。Her expression conveyed anger, though her words were restrained.（虽然她言语克制，但她的表情表明她很生气。）}
  \wordphrase{Her expression conveyed anger, though her words were restrained（虽然她言语克制，但她的表情表明她很生气。）}
  \wordexample{Her expression conveyed anger, though her words were restrained.（虽然她言语克制，但她的表情表明她很生气。）}
  \wordexample{convey（vt. sth + to + sb 搬运）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{envoy}{'envɔi}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VIA[VI], VEY, VOY；向里边搬运 en (= in) + voy (= carry, way)}
  \wordfamily{convey；deviate；impervious；obviate}
  \wordsense{n. 外交使节，特使，公使。The U.S. appointed a special envoy to communicate with Afghan opposition troops.（美国指派了一名特使同阿富汗反对派军队谈判。）}
  \wordphrase{the envoy}
  \wordexample{The U.S. appointed a special envoy to communicate with Afghan opposition troops.（美国指派了一名特使同阿富汗反对派军队谈判。）}
  \wordexample{envoy（n. 外交使节，特使，公使。The U.S. appointed a special envoy to communicate with Afghan opposition troops.（美国指派了一名特使同阿富汗反对派军队谈判。））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{deviate}{'di:vieit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VIA[VI], VEY, VOY；偏离道路 de (= from) + vi<via (= road, way)}
  \wordfamily{envoy；impervious；convey；obviate}
  \wordsense{v. from + sth 偏离，背离。deviation 偏离，背离；deviationist（政党内）路线偏差（指“左”倾或右倾）分子；deviationism 偏向，偏离。The Corps of Engineers deviated the river's course to prevent flooding.（为了预防洪水，工程师们调整了河流的水道。）}
  \wordphrase{The Corps of Engineers deviated the river's course to prevent flooding（为了预防洪水，工程师们调整了河流的水道。）}
  \wordexample{The Corps of Engineers deviated the river's course to prevent flooding.（为了预防洪水，工程师们调整了河流的水道。）}
  \wordsense{v. from + sth 偏离，背离。deviation 偏离，背离）}
  \wordphrase{deviate the issue}
  \wordexample{deviate（v. from + sth 偏离，背离。deviation 偏离，背离）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{impervious}{im'pə:viəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VIA[VI], VEY, VOY；不能通过某物走出路的 im<in (= not) + per (= through) + vi<via (= road, way)}
  \wordfamily{deviate；obviate；envoy；trivial；convey（sth + to + sb 搬运）}
  \wordsense{a. （水等）不能渗透的；不受影响的，不为所动的。He seems to be impervious to fatigue, briskly running mile after mile.（他好像不知疲倦，精神抖擞地跑了一英里又一英里。）}
  \wordphrase{He seems to be impervious to fatigue, briskly running mile after mile（他好像不知疲倦，精神抖擞地跑了一英里又一英里。）}
  \wordexample{He seems to be impervious to fatigue, briskly running mile after mile.（他好像不知疲倦，精神抖擞地跑了一英里又一英里。）}
  \wordexample{impervious（a.（水等）不能渗透的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{obviate}{'ɔbvieit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VIA[VI], VEY, VOY；把某物搬得远远地，使消失 ob (= away) + vi<via (= carry, way)}
  \wordfamily{impervious；trivial；deviate；convoy；convey（sth + to + sb 搬运）}
  \wordsense{vt. 排除（危险、困难等），清除；使……成为不必要；避免。Satellite TV obviates the need for an outside TV antenna.（有了卫星电视，就用不着室外电视天线了。）}
  \wordphrase{Satellite TV obviates the need for an outside TV antenna（有了卫星电视，就用不着室外电视天线了。）}
  \wordexample{Satellite TV obviates the need for an outside TV antenna.（有了卫星电视，就用不着室外电视天线了。）}
  \wordexample{obviate（vt. 排除（危险、困难等），清除）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{trivial}{'triviəl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VIA[VI], VEY, VOY；三岔路的 tri (= three) + vi(a) (= road, way)}
  \wordfamily{obviate；convoy；impervious；voyage；convey（sth + to + sb 搬运）}
  \wordsense{a. 无价值的，琐碎的；平凡的，普通的；轻薄的，浅薄的。trivialize 使无价值；triviality 无价值；普通。I only earned a trivial amount of money in tips.（我只赚到了很少的一点小费。）}
  \wordphrase{I only earned a trivial amount of money in tips（我只赚到了很少的一点小费。）}
  \wordexample{I only earned a trivial amount of money in tips.（我只赚到了很少的一点小费。）}
  \wordsense{a. 无价值的，琐碎的）}
  \wordphrase{be trivial}
  \wordexample{trivial（a. 无价值的，琐碎的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{convoy}{'kɔnvɔi}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VIA[VI], VEY, VOY}
  \wordfamily{trivial；voyage；obviate；devious；convey（sth + to + sb 搬运）}
  \wordsense{v. （军舰）护航，护卫}
  \wordphrase{The supply ships sailed under convoy（供给船被一路护送着航行。）}
  \wordphrase{The convoy was attacked by submarines（护航船队遭到了潜水艇的攻击。）}
  \wordexample{The supply ships sailed under convoy.（供给船被一路护送着航行。）}
  \wordexample{The convoy was attacked by submarines.（护航船队遭到了潜水艇的攻击。）}
  \wordsense{n. 护送，护卫；护航舰队（部队），受护送的船只（船队）。*con<com (= together)。The supply ships sailed under convoy.（供给船被一路护送着航行。）The convoy was attacked by submarines.（护航船队遭到了潜水艇的攻击。）}
  \wordphrase{the convoy}
  \wordexample{to convoy（护航，护卫。n. 护送，护卫）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{voyage}{'vɔiidʒ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VIA[VI], VEY, VOY}
  \wordsense{n.（长时间的）航海，航行；（pl.）游记；航海日记。v. 航海，航行。voyager（探索未知海洋的）航海者，航行者。The Voyages of Marco Polo（《马可·波罗游记》）。}
  \wordfamily{convoy}
  \wordfamily{devious}
  \wordfamily{trivial}
  \wordfamily{obvious}
  \wordfamily{a voyage（航海，航行）}
  \wordphrase{a voyage（航海，航行）}
  \wordexample{a voyage（航海，航行）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{devious}{'di:viəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VIA[VI], VEY, VOY}
  \wordfamily{voyage；obvious；convoy；previous；convey（sth + to + sb 搬运）}
  \wordsense{a. 曲折的，迂回的；离开正道的。*de (= away, from), vi<via (= way, road)。take a devious route to avoid busy streets（为避开繁忙路段而选择迂回的路线） get rich by devious ways（利用不正当的手段致富）}
  \wordphrase{take a devious route to avoid busy streets（为避开繁忙路段而选择迂回的路线）}
  \wordphrase{get rich by devious ways（利用不正当的手段致富）}
  \wordphrase{be devious（曲折的，迂回的）}
  \wordexample{take a devious route to avoid busy streets（为避开繁忙路段而选择迂回的路线）}
  \wordexample{be devious（曲折的，迂回的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{obvious}{'ɔbviəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VIA[VI], VEY, VOY}
  \wordfamily{devious；previous；voyage；via；convey（sth + to + sb 搬运）}
  \wordsense{a. 明显的，显而易见的。*ob (= in the way), vi<via (= way, road)。obviously（明显地）}
  \wordphrase{be obvious（明显的，显而易见的。*ob (= in the way), vi<via (= way, road)。obviously（明显地））}
  \wordexample{be obvious（明显的，显而易见的。*ob (= in the way), vi<via (= way, road)。obviously（明显地））}

  \wordphrase{previous conviction（前科）}
  \wordphrase{without previous notice（不预先通知）}
  \wordphrase{be previous（前的，以前的）}
  \wordexample{be previous（前的，以前的）}

\begin{word}[***]{via}{'vaiə}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VIA[VI], VEY, VOY}

  \wordbreakdown{词根 VIA[VI], VEY, VOY}

  \wordphrase{viable policy（可行的政策）}
  \wordphrase{be viable（可实行的）}

\chapter{BAT}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 bat 意为 to strike, to beat。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 flict，cuss，fend / fens [fenc, fest]，lid / lis，plaud / plaus [plod, plos, plex] 等和来自盎格鲁-撒克逊语的 sla。此外，bat 还有 to go 的含义。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{combatant}{'kɔmbətənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 BAT；互相打 com (= together) + bat (= strike)}
  \wordfamily{abate；bat；baton；battalion}
  \wordsense{n. 参加战斗者}
  \wordphrase{Several combatants were seen on the city's streets（人们在街上看见了几名战士。）}
  \wordexample{Several combatants were seen on the city's streets.（人们在街上看见了几名战士。）}
  \wordsense{a. 作战的，战斗的。combat 战斗；和……作战；combative 好斗的。Several combatants were seen on the city's streets.（人们在街上看见了几名战士。）}
  \wordphrase{be combatant}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of combatant.（此处展示 combatant 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{a. 作战的，战斗的。combat 战斗）}
  \wordphrase{be combatant}
  \wordexample{combatant（n. 参加战斗者。a. 作战的，战斗的。combat 战斗）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{abate}{ə'beit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 BAT}
  \wordfamily{combatant；bat；baton；battalion}
  \wordsense{v. （风、洪水、痛苦等）减轻，减少；废除。*a (= off)。abatement（减少，减弱）}
  \wordphrase{to abate（减轻，减少）}
  \wordexample{to abate（减轻，减少）}
\end{word}

  \wordfamily{abate}

\begin{word}[***]{battle}{'bætl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 BAT}
  \wordfamily{batting；battlement；battery；batty；combatant（参加战斗者）}
  \wordsense{n. 战斗；战役；（the\textasciitilde{}）胜利，成功}
  \wordphrase{sham battle（模拟战）}
  \wordphrase{battle of life（生存竞争）}
  \wordphrase{die in battle（战死）}
  \wordexample{The battle is to the strong.（胜利属于强者。）}
  \wordsense{v. 作战；斗争。battlefield（战场）；sham battle（模拟战）；battle of life（生存竞争）；die in battle（战死） The battle is to the strong.（胜利属于强者。）battle formation（战斗阵形）}
  \wordphrase{a battle（战斗）}
  \wordexample{a battle（战斗）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{battlement}{'bætlmənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 BAT}
  \wordsense{n.（pl.）（塔、城堡等）设有枪炮眼的城垛}
  \wordfamily{battle}
  \wordfamily{batty}
  \wordfamily{batting}
  \wordfamily{debate}
  \wordfamily{a battlement（（塔、城堡等）设有枪炮眼的城垛）}
  \wordphrase{a battlement（（塔、城堡等）设有枪炮眼的城垛）}
  \wordexample{a battlement（（塔、城堡等）设有枪炮眼的城垛）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{batty}{'bæti}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 BAT}
  \wordsense{a. 神经错乱的，疯狂的}
  \wordfamily{battlement}
  \wordfamily{debate}
  \wordfamily{battle}
  \wordfamily{debatable}
  \wordfamily{be batty（神经错乱的，疯狂的）}
  \wordphrase{be batty（神经错乱的，疯狂的）}
  \wordexample{be batty（神经错乱的，疯狂的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{debate}{di'beit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 BAT}
  \wordfamily{batty；debatable；battlement；battle；combatant（参加战斗者）}
  \wordsense{n. 讨论；争论}
  \wordphrase{debating society（辩论社团）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "debating society".（常见搭配：辩论社团。）}
  \wordsense{v. 讨论；争论；辩论。*de (= down)。debater（讨论者；辩论者）；debating society（辩论社团）}
  \wordphrase{a debate（讨论）}
  \wordexample{a debate（讨论）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{debatable}{di'beitəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 BAT}
  \wordfamily{debate；batty；battlement；battle；combatant（参加战斗者）}
  \wordsense{a. 可争议的；成问题的；有争议的，未决的。*de (= down)。debatable ground（国境等存在争议的土地）}
  \wordphrase{debatable ground（国境等存在争议的土地）}
  \wordphrase{be debatable（可争议的）}
  \wordexample{be debatable（可争议的）}
\end{word}



\chapter{CIS[CAS, CHIS]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根 CIS[CAS, CHIS] 来自拉丁语的 `cis`，意为 `to cut`。其变形为 `cas` 和 `chis`。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 `tom[tem]`、`scind/scis`、`sect`、`sever`、`tail`、`tons`、`trench`、`cop/coup` 等。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}{concise}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{kən'sais}
  \wordfamily{conciseness（简洁）；concisely（简洁地）；excise（国内消费税）}
  \wordsense{a. 简洁的；简明的。【好好地切的：con<com (= intensive) + cis (= cut)】}
  \wordphrase{concise answers}
  \wordexample{Class, please write concise answers to the essay questions.（同学们，请简要写出这篇文章问题的答案。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{excise}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{ik'saiz}
  \wordfamily{excision（切除）；concise（简洁的）；incisive（锐利的）}
  \wordsense{n. 国内消费税，货物税。【切掉：ex (= out) + cis (= cut)】}
  \wordphrase{He insisted that the}
  \wordexample{He insisted that the violent scenes be excised from the film.（他坚持应删除电影中的暴力场面。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{incisive}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{in'saisiv}
  \wordsense{a. 锐利的；尖锐的；激烈的；敏感的；果断的。【砍向里面或内部的：in (= in, into) + cis (= cut)】}
  \wordfamily{incision 切割，切开；切口。}
  \wordfamily{incisor 门牙。}
  \wordfamily{incise 切割；雕刻，铭刻。}
  \wordphrase{incisive editor and cuts}
  \wordexample{She's an incisive editor and cuts right to the bone.（她是一个果断的编辑，修改删节大刀阔斧。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{circumcision}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{ˌsə:kəm'siʒn}
  \wordfamily{concise（简洁的）；excise（国内消费税）；incisive（锐利的）}
  \wordsense{n. （尤指犹太教等为男婴施行的）割礼；割包皮。circum (= around)}
  \wordphrase{He was never circumcised}
  \wordexample{He was never circumcised, and I feel his uncircumcised penis is a turn off to women.（他没有做过包皮切除手术，我想女人们因此而离开他。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{decisive}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{di'saisiv}
  \wordsense{a. 决定性的；决定的；坚决的；确定的。de (= down)}
  \wordfamily{decisively（决定性地）。}
  \wordfamily{v. 概括…的大意，为…写摘要。pre (= before, intensive)}
  \wordphrase{decisive battle（决战）。}
  \wordphrase{decisive evidence（确证）。}
  \wordphrase{decisive ballots（决选投票）。}
  \wordexample{decisive battle（决战）。}
  \wordexample{decisive evidence（确证）。}
  \wordexample{decisive ballots（决选投票）。 précis ['preisi:] n. 《法》（演讲、文章的）大意，摘要}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{precise}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{pri'sais}
  \wordsense{a. 精确的；确切的；细致的；严格的；刻板的。pre (= before, intensive)}
  \wordfamily{decisive}
  \wordfamily{precisely}
  \wordfamily{circumcision}
  \wordfamily{chisel}
  \wordphrase{precise measurements（精确的测量）。}
  \wordphrase{a very precise man（非常刻板的人）。}
  \wordexample{precise measurements（精确的测量）。}
  \wordexample{a very precise man（非常刻板的人）。}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{precisely}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{pri'saisli}
  \wordfamily{precise（measurements（精确的测量））；concise（简洁的）；excise（国内消费税）}
  \wordsense{ad. 精确地；一丝不苟地；（用于肯定回答）对，确实如此。pre (= before, intensive)}
  \wordphrase{a very precise man}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of precisely.（此处展示 precisely 的常见用法。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{chisel}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{'tʃizl}
  \wordsense{n. 凿子，凿刀；v. 用凿子凿、刻、雕。chis(cis (= cut))}
  \wordfamily{precisely}
  \wordfamily{castration}
  \wordfamily{precise}
  \wordfamily{decisive}
  \wordfamily{a chisel（凿子，凿刀）}
  \wordphrase{a chisel（凿子，凿刀）}
  \wordexample{a chisel（凿子，凿刀）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{castration}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{kæ'streiʃn}
  \wordfamily{concise（简洁的）；excise（国内消费税）；incisive（锐利的）}
  \wordsense{n. 阉割；删改。cas(cis (= cut))}
  \wordphrase{the castration}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of castration.（此处展示 castration 的常见用法。）}
\end{word}



\chapter{GEN[GN]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根 GEN[GN] 来自希腊语的 `gen`，意为 `birth, creation, race, kind`。`gn` 是它的变形。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}{congenial}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{kən'dʒi:niəl}
  \wordfamily{congeniality（情投意合）；congenially（情投意合地）；cognate（同源的）}
  \wordsense{a. 性格相同的，情投意合的；惬意的。【带着某物一同出生的：con<com (= with) + gen (= birth)】}
  \wordphrase{congenial atmosphere}
  \wordexample{I like working here because of the congenial atmosphere.（我喜欢在这里工作，因为这里的环境很舒适。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{cognate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{'kɒgneit}
  \wordfamily{cognation（亲族）；congenial（性格相同的）；disingenuous（不诚实的）}
  \wordsense{a. 同源的；同族的；【语】同源词的；同语系的}
  \wordphrase{be cognate}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of cognate.（此处展示 cognate 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 同源词。【一起、一同出生：co<com (= together) + gn<gen (= birth)】}
  \wordphrase{cognate languages}
  \wordexample{German and English are cognate languages.（德语和英语属同一语系。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{disingenuous}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{ˌdisin'dʒenjuəs}
  \wordfamily{disingenuously（不诚实地）；disingenuousness（不诚实）；congenial（性格相同的）}
  \wordsense{a. 不诚实的；不正直的；阴险的。【非出生状态的：dis (= not) + in (= in) + gen (= birth)】}
  \wordphrase{disingenuous flattery sets my}
  \wordexample{Her disingenuous flattery sets my teeth on edge.（她那毫无诚意的恭维让我很不舒服。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{engender}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{in'dʒendə(r)}
  \wordsense{vt. 使发生，引起，诱发，造成。【使诞生：en (= make) + gen (= birth, creation)】}
  \wordfamily{disingenuous}
  \wordfamily{generate}
  \wordfamily{cognate}
  \wordfamily{genial}
  \wordphrase{engender considerable conflict}
  \wordexample{Unkind words, often repeated, may ultimately engender considerable conflict.（刻薄的话如果频繁重复，终究会引起大矛盾。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{generate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{'dʒenəreit}
  \wordsense{vt. 生育；产生；发出（光、热、电等）；引起，导致。【使诞生，使出生：gen (= birth, creation)】}
  \wordfamily{generation 产出，发生；世代。}
  \wordfamily{generator 发电机，发生器。}
  \wordfamily{generative 有生产力的；生成的。}
  \wordphrase{generate such destructive results}
  \wordexample{How do earthquakes generate such destructive results?（地震是怎样造成如此破坏性的后果的？）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{genial}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{'dʒi:niəl}
  \wordsense{a. （天气等）适宜生长的，温和的，温暖的；亲切的，和蔼的。【出生、诞生的：gen (= birth) + i + al (= nature of)】}
  \wordfamily{geniality 温和，亲切；和蔼的行为。}
  \wordphrase{genial home atmosphere is}
  \wordexample{A genial home atmosphere is the most important ingredient to a child's development.（温暖的家庭氛围是孩子成长过程中最重要的因素。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{ingenuity}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{ˌindʒə'nju:əti}
  \wordfamily{ingenious（机灵的）；congenial（性格相同的）；cognate（同源的）}
  \wordsense{n. 机灵；才干，才能；创造力，独创性。【诞生的状态：in (= in) + gen (= birth)】}
  \wordphrase{ingenuity kept the company}
  \wordexample{His ingenuity kept the company running even during the cash shortfall.（他的才能使公司在资金短缺的状况下也能正常运转。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{ingenuous}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{in'dʒenjuəs}
  \wordsense{a. 坦白的；直率的；天真的；纯真的；自然的。【诞生的状态的：in (= in) + gen (= birth, creation)】}
  \wordfamily{ingenuity}
  \wordfamily{indigenous}
  \wordfamily{genial}
  \wordfamily{malignant}
  \wordphrase{ingenuous country girl when}
  \wordexample{She was an ingenuous country girl when she first arrived in the city.（初到城里之时，她还是一个天真无邪的乡村姑娘。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{indigenous}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{in'didʒənəs}
  \wordsense{a. 本土的；固有的。【在里边、内部出生的：indi (= within) + gen (= birth)】}
  \wordfamily{ingenuous}
  \wordfamily{malignant}
  \wordfamily{ingenuity}
  \wordfamily{congenital}
  \wordphrase{indigenous people make decisions}
  \wordexample{As a general rule, indigenous people make decisions communally.（一般情况下，土著居民会共同作出决定。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{malignant}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{mə'lignənt}
  \wordsense{a. 怀有恶意的，恶毒的；（疾病）恶性的，致命的；极有害的。【使产生坏状态的、恶性的：mali<mal (= bad) + gn<gen (= birth)】}
  \wordfamily{malignity 恶意；恶性。}
  \wordfamily{malignancy 恶性。}
  \wordfamily{malign a. 极有害的，恶性的}
  \wordfamily{v. 中伤，诽谤。}
  \wordphrase{malignant}
  \wordexample{Unfortunately, laboratory tests showed the mass to be malignant.（不幸的是，化验显示肿块为恶性。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{congenital}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{kən'dʒenitəl}
  \wordsense{a. （疾病）先天的。con<com (= with)}
  \wordfamily{malignant}
  \wordfamily{degenerate}
  \wordfamily{indigenous}
  \wordfamily{eugenics}
  \wordphrase{congenital idiocy/deformity（先天弱智/残疾）。}
  \wordexample{congenital idiocy/deformity（先天弱智/残疾）。}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{degenerate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{di'dʒenəreit}
  \wordsense{v. 变质，堕落；退步；退化。a. 堕落的；退化的。de (= down)}
  \wordfamily{degeneration（退步，退化；堕落）。}
  \wordfamily{degeneracy（退步，退化；堕落）。}
  \wordfamily{degenerating}
  \wordphrase{degenerating}
  \wordexample{The South Korean people of today are degenerating.（当今韩国人正在逐步堕落。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{eugenics}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{ju:'dʒeniks}
  \wordfamily{degenerate；homogeneous；congenital；homogenize；congenial（性格相同的）}
  \wordsense{n. 优生学。eu (= good)}
  \wordphrase{an eugenics（优生学。eu (= good)）}
  \wordexample{an eugenics（优生学。eu (= good)）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{homogeneous}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{ˌhɒmə'dʒi:niəs}
  \wordfamily{congenial（性格相同的）；cognate（同源的）；disingenuous（不诚实的）}
  \wordsense{a. 同种的，同质的。homo (= same)}
  \wordphrase{homogeneous population}
  \wordexample{homogeneous population of South Korea（由单一民族构成的韩国人口）。}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{homogenize}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{hə'mɒdʒənaiz}
  \wordfamily{homogenized（milk（均脂牛奶））；congenial（性格相同的）；cognate（同源的）}
  \wordsense{v. 使均匀，使均质（尤指使牛奶中的油脂颗粒均匀分布）。homo (= same)}
  \wordphrase{to homogenize（使均匀，使均质（尤指使牛奶中的油脂颗粒均匀分布）。homo (= same)）}
  \wordexample{to homogenize（使均匀，使均质（尤指使牛奶中的油脂颗粒均匀分布）。homo (= same)）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{heterogeneous}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{ˌhetərə'dʒi:niəs}
  \wordsense{a. 异种的，异质的。hetero (= other)}
  \wordfamily{homogenize}
  \wordfamily{pregnant}
  \wordfamily{homogeneous}
  \wordfamily{pregnancy}
  \wordphrase{heterogeneous population}
  \wordexample{the heterogeneous population of the United States（由不同民族构成的美国人口）。}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{pregnant}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{'pregnənt}
  \wordsense{a. 怀孕的；（言语、行动）意味深长的，含蓄的；富有的；充满的。pre (= before), gn<gen (= birth)}
  \wordfamily{heterogeneous}
  \wordfamily{pregnancy}
  \wordfamily{homogenize}
  \wordfamily{progenitor}
  \wordphrase{I am pregnant/expecting.（我怀孕了。）}
  \wordexample{I am pregnant/expecting.（我怀孕了。）}
  \wordexample{political events pregnant with consequences（具有重要意义的政治事件）。}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{pregnancy}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{'pregnənsi}
  \wordsense{n. 怀孕；丰富；深奥，意味深长。pre (= before), gn<gen (= birth)}
  \wordfamily{pregnant}
  \wordfamily{progenitor}
  \wordfamily{heterogeneous}
  \wordfamily{progeny}
  \wordphrase{pregnancies in six years}
  \wordexample{She's had eight pregnancies in six years.（她六年中怀孕八次。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{progenitor}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{prəu'dʒenitə(r)}
  \wordsense{n. （人、动植物的）祖先；前辈。pro (= before)}
  \wordfamily{pregnancy}
  \wordfamily{progeny}
  \wordfamily{pregnant}
  \wordfamily{primogeniture}
  \wordphrase{the great progenitor（伟大的祖先）。}
  \wordexample{the great progenitor（伟大的祖先）。}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{progeny}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{'prɒdʒəni}
  \wordsense{n. 子孙，后裔；结果，产物。pro (= forth)}
  \wordfamily{progenitor}
  \wordfamily{primogeniture}
  \wordfamily{pregnancy}
  \wordfamily{regenerate}
  \wordfamily{a progeny（子孙，后裔）}
  \wordphrase{a progeny（子孙，后裔）}
  \wordexample{a progeny（子孙，后裔）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{primogeniture}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{ˌpraiməu'dʒenitʃə(r)}
  \wordfamily{congenial（性格相同的）；cognate（同源的）；disingenuous（不诚实的）}
  \wordsense{n. 长子（的身份）；长子继承（权）。prim (= first)}
  \wordphrase{a primogeniture（长子（的身份））}
  \wordexample{a primogeniture（长子（的身份））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{regenerate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{ri'dʒenəreit}
  \wordsense{v. 洗心革面；刷新；再生。a. 新生的。re (= again)}
  \wordfamily{regeneration（新生，刷新，再生）。}
  \wordfamily{to regenerate（洗心革面）}
  \wordphrase{to regenerate（洗心革面）}
  \wordexample{to regenerate（洗心革面）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{gender}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{'dʒendə(r)}
  \wordsense{n. 【语】性。}
  \wordfamily{regenerate}
  \wordfamily{gene}
  \wordfamily{primogeniture}
  \wordfamily{genealogy}
  \wordfamily{a gender（性）}
  \wordphrase{a gender（性）}
  \wordexample{a gender（性）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{gene}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{dʒi:n}
  \wordsense{n. 基因。}
  \wordfamily{gender}
  \wordfamily{genealogy}
  \wordfamily{regenerate}
  \wordfamily{genealogical}
  \wordphrase{gene bank（基因文库）。}
  \wordphrase{gene pool（基因库）。}
  \wordexample{gene bank（基因文库）。}
  \wordexample{gene pool（基因库）。}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{genealogy}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{ˌdʒi:ni'ælədʒi}
  \wordsense{n. 家系，血统；系统，系谱；系谱学。}
  \wordfamily{genealogist（系谱学者）。}
  \wordfamily{a genealogy（家系，血统）}
  \wordphrase{a genealogy（家系，血统）}
  \wordexample{a genealogy（家系，血统）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{genealogical}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{ˌdʒi:niə'lɒdʒikəl}
  \wordsense{a. 系谱的，家系的；表示系统的。}
  \wordfamily{genealogy}
  \wordfamily{general}
  \wordfamily{gene}
  \wordfamily{generality}
  \wordphrase{genealogical tree（家系图；系统树）。}
  \wordphrase{genealogical table（族谱）。}
  \wordexample{genealogical tree（家系图；系统树）。}
  \wordexample{genealogical table（族谱）。}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{general}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{'dʒenrəl}
  \wordsense{a. 一般的，普通的；大体的；概括的，综合的，全体的，总体的；（用于职位后）总…，长…。n. 陆军上将，将军；战略家，战术家。}
  \wordfamily{attorney general（《美》司法部长；《英》检察总长）。}
  \wordfamily{surgeon general（《美》卫生局局长）。}
  \wordfamily{postmaster general（邮政部长）。}
  \wordfamily{inspector general（检察长）。}
  \wordphrase{General Accounting Office（审计院，总审计局）。}
  \wordphrase{general affairs（总务）。}
  \wordphrase{General Assembly（联合国大会）。}
  \wordphrase{general attack（总攻）。}
  \wordphrase{general anesthesia（全身麻醉）。}
  \wordphrase{general hospital（综合医院）。}
  \wordphrase{general manager（总经理）。}
  \wordphrase{general orders（【军】一般命令）。}
  \wordphrase{general practitioner（全科医生，普通医生）。}
  \wordphrase{general strike（总罢工）。}
  \wordphrase{general war（全面战争）↔ local war（局部战争）。}
  \wordexample{General Accounting Office（审计院，总审计局）。}
  \wordexample{general affairs（总务）。}
  \wordexample{General Assembly（联合国大会）。}
  \wordexample{general attack（总攻）。}
  \wordexample{general anesthesia（全身麻醉）。}
  \wordexample{general hospital（综合医院）。}
  \wordexample{general manager（总经理）。}
  \wordexample{general orders（【军】一般命令）。}
  \wordexample{general practitioner（全科医生，普通医生）。}
  \wordexample{general strike（总罢工）。}
  \wordexample{general war（全面战争）↔ local war（局部战争）。}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{generality}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{ˌdʒenə'ræləti}
  \wordsense{n. 一般性，普遍性；（the \textasciitilde{} of）多数，大部分。}
  \wordfamily{general}
  \wordfamily{generalize}
  \wordfamily{genealogical}
  \wordfamily{generalization}
  \wordphrase{from generalities to particularities（从一般到个别）。}
  \wordexample{from generalities to particularities（从一般到个别）。}
  \wordexample{The generality of Swedes are blonde.（大多数瑞典人都是金发。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{generalize}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{'dʒenrəlaiz}
  \wordsense{v. 使一般化，推广；概括（事实等），归纳；普及。}
  \wordfamily{generality}
  \wordfamily{generalization}
  \wordfamily{general}
  \wordfamily{generally}
  \wordphrase{generalize the use}
  \wordexample{generalize the use of a new invention（推广新发明的使用）。}
\end{word}

  \wordbreakdown{ˌdʒenrəlai'zeiʃn}
  \wordsense{n. 一般化，概括，归纳；〈贬〉判断。}

  \wordfamily{a generalization（一般化，概括，归纳）}
  \wordphrase{a generalization（一般化，概括，归纳）}
  \wordexample{a generalization（一般化，概括，归纳）}
\end{word}
