\chapter{SOL}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根 SOL 来自拉丁语的 `sol`，意为 `alone/single`、`whole/entire`、`sun`、`comfort`。此外，注意不要将其与意为 `firm/whole` 的来自拉丁语的 `solid` 相混淆。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}{console}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{kən'səul}
  \wordfamily{consolation（安慰）；consolatory（慰藉的，慰问的）；consolable（可安慰的，可慰藉的）}
  \wordsense{n. 控制台，操纵台，仪表板；落地式电视机柜；落地式收音机。【拥有可以用来安慰的东西：con<com (= with) + sol (= comfort)】}
  \wordphrase{console her after her}
  \wordexample{Nothing could console her after her daughter's death.（她女儿死之后，没有什么能够抚慰她。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{disconsolate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{dis'kɒnsələt}
  \wordfamily{disconsolately（拒绝接受安慰地，忧愁地）；console（控制台）；solicitous（担心的）}
  \wordsense{a. 因失去…而郁闷的；拒绝接受安慰的；伤心的；忧愁的。【没有安慰的：dis (= not) + con<com (= with) + sol (= comfort)】}
  \wordphrase{disconsolate after her miscarriage}
  \wordexample{The woman was disconsolate after her miscarriage.（那个女子在流产之后一直郁郁寡欢。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{solicitous}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{sə'lisitəs}
  \wordsense{a. 担心的；惦念的。【全身心地大喊的：sol (= entire, alone) + cit (= call)】}
  \wordfamily{solicitude 焦虑，焦急；挂念，担心。}
  \wordphrase{solicitous and I really}
  \wordexample{The nurses have been very solicitous, and I really appreciate it.（护士们一直很挂念我，这让我很感激。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{inconsolable}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{ˌinkən'səuləbl}
  \wordsense{a. 无法慰藉的，极度伤心的。in (= not), con<com (= with), sol (= comfort)}
  \wordfamily{solicitous}
  \wordfamily{solace}
  \wordfamily{disconsolate}
  \wordfamily{solatium}
  \wordphrase{inconsolable grief（无法慰藉的悲伤）。}
  \wordexample{inconsolable grief（无法慰藉的悲伤）。}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{solace}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{'sɒləs}
  \wordsense{n. 安慰（物）；v. 给…以安慰。sol (= comfort)}
  \wordfamily{inconsolable}
  \wordfamily{solatium}
  \wordfamily{solicitous}
  \wordfamily{desolate}
  \wordphrase{find solace in music（在音乐中寻找慰藉）。}
  \wordphrase{solace myself with Carlsberg（用嘉士伯酒慰藉自己）。}
  \wordexample{find solace in music（在音乐中寻找慰藉）。}
  \wordexample{solace myself with Carlsberg（用嘉士伯酒慰藉自己）。}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{solatium}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{sə'leiʃiəm}
  \wordfamily{solace；desolate；inconsolable；desolation；console（控制台）}
  \wordsense{n. 慰问金。sol (= comfort)}
  \wordphrase{a solatium（慰问金。sol (= comfort)）}
  \wordexample{a solatium（慰问金。sol (= comfort)）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{desolate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{'desələt}
  \wordfamily{solatium；desolation；solace；sole；console（控制台）}
  \wordsense{v. 使荒废；使凄凉。de (= away), sol (= alone)}
  \wordphrase{desolate-looking girl（看起来楚楚可怜的女孩）。}
  \wordphrase{desolate life（孤独的生活）。}
  \wordexample{desolate wind-swept moorland area（荒凉的、大风肆虐的荒野）。}
  \wordexample{desolate-looking girl（看起来楚楚可怜的女孩）。}
  \wordexample{desolate life（孤独的生活）。}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{desolation}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{ˌdesə'leiʃn}
  \wordsense{n. 荒废；荒凉，凄凉；废墟。de (= away), sol (= alone)}
  \wordfamily{desolate}
  \wordfamily{sole}
  \wordfamily{solatium}
  \wordfamily{soliloquy}
  \wordphrase{desolation caused by war（战争留下的废墟）。}
  \wordexample{desolation caused by war（战争留下的废墟）。}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{sole}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{səul}
  \wordsense{a. 唯一的，独一无二的，单独的；独占的。n. （袜子、鞋等的）底，底面。v. 给（鞋等）装底。sol (= alone)}
  \wordfamily{solely（单独地，只是）。}
  \wordfamily{solely responsible（独自负责的）。}
  \wordphrase{rubber-soled boots（橡胶底的靴子）。}
  \wordphrase{thin-soled shoes（薄底的鞋）。}
  \wordexample{sole cause of the accident（引发事故的唯一原因）。}
  \wordexample{have the sole right of selling the article（拥有该产品的独家销售权）。}
  \wordexample{rubber-soled boots（橡胶底的靴子）。}
  \wordexample{thin-soled shoes（薄底的鞋）。}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{soliloquy}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{sə'liləkwi}
  \wordsense{n. 自言自语；（歌剧中的）独白。sol (= alone), loqu (= say)}
  \wordfamily{soliloquize（自言自语；独白）。}
  \wordfamily{a soliloquy（自言自语）}
  \wordphrase{a soliloquy（自言自语）}
  \wordexample{a soliloquy（自言自语）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{solitary}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{'sɒlitri}
  \wordfamily{console（控制台）；disconsolate（因失去…而郁闷的）；solicitous（担心的）}
  \wordsense{a. 孤独的；独居的；唯一的；人迹罕至的。sol (= alone)}
  \wordphrase{solitary traveler（孤独的旅行者）。}
  \wordphrase{solitary life（独居生活）。}
  \wordphrase{solitary confinement（单独监禁）。}
  \wordexample{solitary traveler（孤独的旅行者）。}
  \wordexample{solitary life（独居生活）。}
  \wordexample{solitary confinement（单独监禁）。}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{solitude}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{'sɒlitju:d}
  \wordsense{n. 孤独，寂寞，独居；荒凉之地。sol (= alone)}
  \wordfamily{solitary}
  \wordfamily{solo}
  \wordfamily{soliloquy}
  \wordfamily{solemn}
  \wordphrase{live in solitude（独自生活）。}
  \wordphrase{not fond of solitude（不喜欢孤独的）。}
  \wordexample{live in solitude（独自生活）。}
  \wordexample{not fond of solitude（不喜欢孤独的）。}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{solo}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{'səuləu}
  \wordsense{n. 独唱（曲），独奏（曲）；单飞；独角戏。a. 单独的。ad. 单独地。sol (= alone)}
  \wordfamily{soloist（独唱者，独奏者）。}
  \wordfamily{fly solo（单飞）。}
  \wordphrase{violin/piano solo（小提琴/钢琴独奏曲）。}
  \wordphrase{his first solo flight（他的首次单飞）。}
  \wordexample{violin/piano solo（小提琴/钢琴独奏曲）。}
  \wordexample{his first solo flight（他的首次单飞）。}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{solemn}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{'sɒləm}
  \wordfamily{console（控制台）；disconsolate（因失去…而郁闷的）；solicitous（担心的）}
  \wordsense{a. 严肃的；庄严的，庄重的；宗教上的；正式的。sol (= whole, entire, sun), emn<enn<ann (= year)}
  \wordphrase{solemn silence/look/sight（肃静/严肃的表情/隆重的场面）。}
  \wordexample{solemn silence/look/sight（肃静/严肃的表情/隆重的场面）。}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{solemnity}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{sə'lemnəti}
  \wordsense{n. 严肃；庄严；隆重的仪式。sol (= whole, entire, sun), emn<enn<ann (= year)}
  \wordfamily{solemn}
  \wordfamily{solicit}
  \wordfamily{solo}
  \wordfamily{solicitor}
  \wordfamily{solemnities}
  \wordphrase{solemnities}
  \wordexample{The Queen was crowned with all the proper solemnities.（经过一系列的庄严仪式后，女王登上了王位。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{solicit}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{sə'lisit}
  \wordsense{v. 向…恳求，寻求（某物），央求；（尤指妓女在公共场合）拉客。sol (= whole, entire), cit (= call)}
  \wordfamily{solicitation（恳求；拉客）。}
  \wordfamily{to solicit（向…恳求，寻求（某物），央求）}
  \wordphrase{to solicit（向…恳求，寻求（某物），央求）}
  \wordexample{to solicit（向…恳求，寻求（某物），央求）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{solicitor}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{sə'lisitə(r)}
  \wordsense{n. 揽客；《英》初级律师（负责法庭律师和诉讼人之间事务联系的律师）；《美》（政府部门或某一城市负责法律事务的）法务官。sol (= whole, entire), cit (= call)}
  \wordfamily{solicit}
  \wordfamily{solar}
  \wordfamily{solemnity}
  \wordfamily{solarium}
  \wordphrase{Solicitor General（《英》副检察长）。}
  \wordexample{Solicitor General（《英》副检察长）。}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{solar}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{'səulə(r)}
  \wordsense{a. 太阳的，利用太阳光的。sol (= sun)}
  \wordfamily{solicitor}
  \wordfamily{solarium}
  \wordfamily{solicit}
  \wordfamily{solstice}
  \wordphrase{solar eclipse（日食）。}
  \wordphrase{solar energy（太阳能）。}
  \wordphrase{solar house（太阳能住宅）。}
  \wordphrase{solar power（太阳能）。}
  \wordphrase{solar system（太阳系）。}
  \wordexample{solar eclipse（日食）。}
  \wordexample{solar energy（太阳能）。}
  \wordexample{solar house（太阳能住宅）。}
  \wordexample{solar power（太阳能）。}
  \wordexample{solar system（太阳系）。}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{solarium}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{sə'leəriəm}
  \wordfamily{solar；solstice；solicitor；solicit；console（控制台）}
  \wordsense{n. （用玻璃围起来的医疗用的）日光浴室。sol (= sun), arium (= place where)}
  \wordphrase{a solarium（日光浴室。sol (= sun), arium (= place where)）}
  \wordexample{a solarium（日光浴室。sol (= sun), arium (= place where)）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{solstice}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{'sɒlstis}
  \wordfamily{console（控制台）；disconsolate（因失去…而郁闷的）；solicitous（担心的）}
  \wordsense{n. 至，至点。sol (= sun), stic (= stand, stop)}
  \wordphrase{summer/winter solstice（夏至/冬至）。}
  \wordexample{summer/winter solstice（夏至/冬至）。}
\end{word}



\chapter{TEND, TENS, TENT}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 tend, tens, tent，意为 to stretch。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{contend}{kən'tend}
  \wordbreakdown{相互拉扯的 con<com (= together) + tend (= stretch)}
  \wordfamily{contention（争辩，争论）；contentious（有争议的）；extensive（广大的）}
  \wordsense{vi. for / with + sth 争辩；争论；竞争}
  \wordphrase{contend in practice}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of contend.（此处展示 contend 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 主张}
  \wordphrase{contended she was only}
  \wordexample{Despite what the officer said, the driver contended she was only driving at 30 mph.（不管警察说什么，那个司机都争辩说自己的驾车时速只有 30 英里。）}
  \wordexample{contended she was only}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{extensive}{ik'stensiv}
  \wordbreakdown{向外伸展的 ex (= out) + tens (= stretch)}
  \wordsense{a. 广大的；广泛的}
  \wordfamily{extend 扩展，扩大；伸（手、脚等）}
  \wordfamily{extension 延长，扩大}
  \wordfamily{extent 宽度，程度，范围；【律】【英】扣押令}
  \wordfamily{extensively 广大地，广泛地}
  \wordphrase{extensive system}
  \wordexample{An extensive system of underground aquifers provides water in the Midwest.（大范围的地下蓄水层为中西部地区提供用水。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{intensive}{in'tensiv}
  \wordbreakdown{在……里拉长的 in (= in) + tens (= stretch)}
  \wordsense{a. 强烈的；集中的；精深的；透彻的；加强语气的，强调的}
  \wordfamily{intensify 加强}
  \wordfamily{intensification 激化，强化}
  \wordfamily{intensity 强烈；极度}
  \wordfamily{intension 强度}
  \wordfamily{intense 强烈的，激烈的；热情的}
  \wordphrase{intensive course}
  \wordexample{I took a 2-day intensive course on how to use my new computer.（为学会使用我的新计算机，我参加了一个为期两天的强化培训课程。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{ostensible}{ɔ'stensəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{向上拉长的 os<ob (= over, against) + tens (= stretch)}
  \wordfamily{ostensibly（表面上）；contend（for / with + sth 争辩）；extensive（广大的）}
  \wordsense{a. 表面上的，外表上的；面子上的}
  \wordphrase{ostensible reason}
  \wordexample{The ostensible reason for their divorce was irreconcilable differences''.（他们离婚的表面理由是“性格不和”。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{ostentatious}{,ɔsten'teiʃəs}
  \wordbreakdown{向某物的反方向拉长的 os<ob (= against, over) + tent (= stretch)}
  \wordsense{a. 卖弄的，夸耀的；豪华的，铺张的}
  \wordfamily{ostentation 夸耀，卖弄；虚饰}
  \wordfamily{ostentatiously 卖弄地}
  \wordphrase{ostentatious wedding}
  \wordexample{We want an elegant but not ostentatious wedding.（我们希望办一场高雅而不铺张的婚礼。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{pretentious}{pri'tenʃəs}
  \wordbreakdown{向前伸展的 pre (= before) + tent (= stretch)}
  \wordsense{a. 炫耀的，做作的；狂妄的}
  \wordfamily{pretend 做作，自负}
  \wordfamily{pretense 虚假；做作}
  \wordfamily{pretension 自命，自称；做作；假装；借口；托词}
  \wordfamily{pretender 妄提要求者；伪装者}
  \wordphrase{pretentious at all They're}
  \wordexample{The people at the country club aren't pretentious at all. They're very nice!（那个乡村俱乐部的人一点都不狂妄，他们很亲切！）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{attend}{ə'tend}
  \wordbreakdown{向着某处伸展 at<ad (= to) + tend (= stretch)}
  \wordsense{v. 出席，参加；（\textasciitilde{}to）致力于；护理；陪同，伴随；侍奉}
  \wordfamily{pretentious}
  \wordfamily{attendance}
  \wordfamily{ostentatious}
  \wordfamily{attention}
  \wordphrase{attend to what others are saying（注意听别人说的话）}
  \wordphrase{have the honor of attending upon the Prince（很荣幸侍奉王子）}
  \wordexample{The lectures were well attended.（听演讲的人很多。）}
  \wordexample{attend to what others are saying（注意听别人说的话）}
  \wordexample{have the honor of attending upon the Prince（很荣幸侍奉王子）}
  \wordexample{My good luck attends you!（祝你好运！）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{attendance}{ə'tendəns}
  \wordbreakdown{到场、侍候的状态 at<ad (= to) + tend (= stretch)}
  \wordsense{n. 出席，出勤；出席人数；侍奉，照料}
  \wordfamily{attend}
  \wordfamily{attention}
  \wordfamily{pretentious}
  \wordfamily{attendant}
  \wordphrase{certificate of attendance（在学证明书）}
  \wordphrase{prize for regular attendance（全勤奖）}
  \wordphrase{attendance book（出勤簿）}
  \wordphrase{large / small attendance（出席人数多 / 少）}
  \wordphrase{in attendance upon the Queen（陪同女王）}
  \wordexample{certificate of attendance（在学证明书）}
  \wordexample{prize for regular attendance（全勤奖）}
  \wordexample{attendance book（出勤簿）}
  \wordexample{large / small attendance（出席人数多 / 少）}
  \wordexample{in attendance upon the Queen（陪同女王）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{attention}{ə'tenʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{把心神伸向某处 at<ad (= to) + tent (= stretch)}
  \wordsense{n. 注意，注意力；（pl.）殷勤；照料；立正姿势；治疗}
  \wordfamily{Attention, please!（各位请注意！）}
  \wordfamily{Attention!（〔口令〕立正）}
  \wordphrase{Attention, Mr. Roberts（〔用于商务文书、公文中〕罗伯茨先生亲启）}
  \wordphrase{attract / draw attention（引起注意）}
  \wordphrase{listen with attention（倾听）}
  \wordphrase{pay one's attentions to a lady（讨一位小姐的欢心）}
  \wordphrase{receive immediate attention（得到紧急救治）}
  \wordexample{Attention, Mr. Roberts（〔用于商务文书、公文中〕罗伯茨先生亲启）}
  \wordexample{attract / draw attention（引起注意）}
  \wordexample{listen with attention（倾听）}
  \wordexample{pay one's attentions to a lady（讨一位小姐的欢心）}
  \wordexample{receive immediate attention（得到紧急救治）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{attendant}{ə'tendənt}
  \wordbreakdown{伴随而至的 at<ad (= to) + tend (= stretch)}
  \wordfamily{attendee（出席者）；contend（for / with + sth 争辩）；extensive（广大的）}
  \wordsense{n. 侍从；出席者；（pl.）随行人员}
  \wordphrase{the attendant}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of attendant.（此处展示 attendant 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{a. 伴随的；侍候的，护理的}
  \wordphrase{the Prince and his attendants（王子及其侍从）}
  \wordphrase{famine and its attendant diseases（饥荒和紧随其至的疾病）}
  \wordexample{old age and its attendant evils（老龄及紧随其至的麻烦）}
  \wordexample{the Prince and his attendants（王子及其侍从）}
  \wordexample{famine and its attendant diseases（饥荒和紧随其至的疾病）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{attentive}{ə'tentiv}
  \wordbreakdown{向着某处专注伸去 at<ad (= to) + tent (= stretch)}
  \wordfamily{attentively（注意地）；contend（for / with + sth 争辩）；extensive（广大的）}
  \wordsense{a. 注意的；留意的；专心的；体贴的}
  \wordphrase{attentive to one's studies（专心致志学习的）}
  \wordphrase{attentive to her young brother（关心她弟弟的）}
  \wordphrase{attentive audience（倾听的观众）}
  \wordexample{attentive to one's studies（专心致志学习的）}
  \wordexample{attentive to her young brother（关心她弟弟的）}
  \wordexample{attentive audience（倾听的观众）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{detente}{dei'tɑ:nt}
  \wordbreakdown{向下松开 de (= down) + tent (= stretch)}
  \wordsense{n. <法> 紧张关系的缓和}
  \wordfamily{attentive}
  \wordfamily{distend}
  \wordfamily{attendant}
  \wordfamily{hypertension}
  \wordfamily{a detente（<法> 紧张关系的缓和）}
  \wordphrase{a detente（<法> 紧张关系的缓和）}
  \wordexample{a detente（<法> 紧张关系的缓和）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{distend}{di'stend}
  \wordbreakdown{向外拉开 dis (= away) + tend (= stretch)}
  \wordsense{v. 扩张，扩大；使膨胀；膨胀}
  \wordfamily{distension / distention 膨胀}
  \wordfamily{to distend（扩张，扩大）}
  \wordphrase{to distend（扩张，扩大）}
  \wordexample{to distend（扩张，扩大）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{hypertension}{haipə'tenʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{过度拉紧 hyper (= over, above, excessive) + tens (= stretch)}
  \wordsense{n. 高血压}
  \wordfamily{distend}
  \wordfamily{hypotension}
  \wordfamily{detente}
  \wordfamily{intend}
  \wordfamily{a hypertension（高血压）}
  \wordphrase{a hypertension（高血压）}
  \wordexample{a hypertension（高血压）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{hypotension}{haipəu'tenʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{拉力偏低 hypo (= under, less) + tens (= stretch)}
  \wordsense{n. 低血压}
  \wordfamily{hypertension}
  \wordfamily{intend}
  \wordfamily{distend}
  \wordfamily{intent}
  \wordfamily{a hypotension（低血压）}
  \wordphrase{a hypotension（低血压）}
  \wordexample{a hypotension（低血压）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{intend}{in'tend}
  \wordbreakdown{向里伸去 in (= into) + tend (= stretch)}
  \wordsense{v. 想要；计划，打算；意指}
  \wordfamily{intended 预谋的，故意的；〔n.〕已订婚者}
  \wordfamily{intended}
  \wordphrase{intended}
  \wordexample{Let me introduce you to my intended.（我向你介绍我的未婚妻。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{intent}{in'tent}
  \wordbreakdown{向里绷紧的 in (= into, intensive) + tent (= stretch)}
  \wordfamily{intently（专心地，专注地）；contend（for / with + sth 争辩）；extensive（广大的）}
  \wordsense{n. 目标；意图；意味，意思}
  \wordphrase{the intent}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of intent.（此处展示 intent 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{a. （表情）热切的；（\textasciitilde{}on）专心致志的}
  \wordphrase{shoot with intent to kill（故意开枪杀人）}
  \wordphrase{with good / evil (malicious)}
  \wordphrase{to all intents and purposes（事实上）}
  \wordexample{shoot with intent to kill（故意开枪杀人）}
  \wordexample{with good / evil (malicious)}
  \wordexample{to all intents and purposes（事实上）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{intention}{in'tenʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{向内伸去的目标 in (= into, intensive) + tent (= stretch)}
  \wordfamily{contend（for / with + sth 争辩）；extensive（广大的）；intensive（强烈的）}
  \wordsense{n. 意向，意图，目标，目的}
  \wordphrase{with good intentions（善意地）}
  \wordphrase{with the intention of \textasciitilde{}ing（打算做……）}
  \wordphrase{well-intentioned / ill-intentioned（善意的 / 恶意的）}
  \wordexample{with good intentions（善意地）}
  \wordexample{with the intention of \textasciitilde{}ing（打算做……）}
  \wordexample{well-intentioned / ill-intentioned（善意的 / 恶意的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{intentional}{in'tenʃənl}
  \wordbreakdown{有意伸向某处的 in (= into, intensive) + tent (= stretch)}
  \wordsense{a. 故意的，蓄谋的}
  \wordfamily{intention}
  \wordfamily{portend}
  \wordfamily{intent}
  \wordfamily{portent}
  \wordfamily{be intentional（故意的，蓄谋的）}
  \wordphrase{be intentional（故意的，蓄谋的）}
  \wordexample{be intentional（故意的，蓄谋的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{portend}{pɔ:'tend}
  \wordbreakdown{向前贯穿地伸出 por<per (= through) + tend (= stretch)}
  \wordsense{v. 预示（未来）；预先警告}
  \wordfamily{intentional}
  \wordfamily{portent}
  \wordfamily{intention}
  \wordfamily{superintend}
  \wordphrase{portend a storm}
  \wordexample{Black clouds portend a storm.（乌云预示着暴风雨即将来临。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{portent}{'pɔ:tənt}
  \wordbreakdown{预先伸出的征兆 por<per (= through) + tent (= stretch)}
  \wordsense{n.（不祥的、大事件的）征兆，预兆；迹象；异常的人（或事物）}
  \wordfamily{portentous 不祥的，凶兆的；令人惊讶的；自命不凡的}
  \wordfamily{a portent（征兆，预兆）}
  \wordphrase{a portent（征兆，预兆）}
  \wordexample{a portent（征兆，预兆）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{superintend}{,su:pərin'tend}
  \wordbreakdown{在上面向内管着 super (= over) + in (= into) + tend (= stretch)}
  \wordsense{v. 主管；监督；指挥}
  \wordfamily{superintendence 主管；监督}
  \wordfamily{to superintend（主管）}
  \wordphrase{to superintend（主管）}
  \wordexample{to superintend（主管）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{superintendent}{,su:pərin'tendənt}
  \wordbreakdown{在上面统管的人 super (= over) + in (= into) + tend (= stretch)}
  \wordsense{n. 监督者，管理者；（企业、机关等的）负责人}
  \wordfamily{superintend}
  \wordfamily{tend}
  \wordfamily{portent}
  \wordfamily{tendency}
  \wordphrase{superintendent of schools（教育局局长）}
  \wordphrase{superintendent of a revenue office（税务局局长）}
  \wordexample{superintendent of schools（教育局局长）}
  \wordexample{superintendent of a revenue office（税务局局长）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{tend}{tend}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TEND, TENS, TENT}
  \wordsense{v. 看管，照料；倾向，趋向}
  \wordfamily{superintendent}
  \wordfamily{tendency}
  \wordfamily{superintend}
  \wordfamily{tendentious}
  \wordfamily{to tend（看管，照料）}
  \wordphrase{to tend（看管，照料）}
  \wordexample{to tend（看管，照料）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{tendency}{'tendənsi}
  \wordbreakdown{伸向某个方向的倾向 tend (= stretch)}
  \wordsense{n. 倾向，趋势}
  \wordfamily{tend}
  \wordfamily{tendentious}
  \wordfamily{superintendent}
  \wordfamily{tender}
  \wordphrase{strong upward tendency in oil shares（石油类股票的强劲上涨趋势）}
  \wordexample{strong upward tendency in oil shares（石油类股票的强劲上涨趋势）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{tendentious}{ten'denʃəs}
  \wordbreakdown{带有某种倾向的 tend (= stretch)}
  \wordsense{a. 倾向性的，有偏见的；宣传性的}
  \wordfamily{tendency}
  \wordfamily{tender}
  \wordfamily{tend}
  \wordfamily{tendon}
  \wordphrase{tendentious / tendencious reports（宣传性报道）}
  \wordexample{tendentious / tendencious reports（宣传性报道）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{tender}{'tendə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{拉得很细致、很柔和 tend (= stretch)}
  \wordfamily{tenderloin（（牛或猪）腰部的嫩肉）；tender-hearted（心肠软的，富于恻隐之心的）；contend（for / with + sth 争辩）}
  \wordsense{a. （色彩、光线等）柔和的；柔弱的；年幼的，未成熟的；敏感的，容易受到伤害的，一触就痛的；棘手的；（肉）易咀嚼的，嫩的；亲切的，温柔的，体贴的}
  \wordphrase{be tender}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of tender.（此处展示 tender 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{ad. 温柔地，亲切地}
  \wordphrase{be tender}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of tender.（此处展示 tender 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 看护人员；补给船；投标；法定货币；提出，提供}
  \wordphrase{the tender}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of tender.（此处展示 tender 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. 提出，提供；投标}
  \wordphrase{tender green（嫩绿）}
  \wordphrase{tender skin（娇嫩的皮肤）}
  \wordphrase{tender subject（棘手的问题，微妙的问题）}
  \wordexample{Love me tender.（温柔地爱我。）}
  \wordexample{tender his resignation to the president（向总统递交辞呈）}
  \wordexample{tender for the construction of a new highway（投标承建新的高速公路）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{tendon}{'tendən}
  \wordbreakdown{拉住肌肉与骨骼的组织 tend (= stretch)}
  \wordsense{n. 筋，腱}
  \wordfamily{tender}
  \wordfamily{tense}
  \wordfamily{tendentious}
  \wordfamily{tensile}
  \wordfamily{a tendon（筋，腱）}
  \wordphrase{a tendon（筋，腱）}
  \wordexample{a tendon（筋，腱）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{tense}{tens}
  \wordbreakdown{被拉紧的 tens (= stretch)}
  \wordfamily{tendon；tensile；tender；tension；contend（for / with + sth 争辩）}
  \wordsense{a. 拉紧的；紧张的}
  \wordphrase{be tense}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of tense.（此处展示 tense 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. 使拉紧，使绷紧；使紧张}
  \wordphrase{tense the issue}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of tense.（此处展示 tense 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 【语】时态}
  \wordphrase{faces tensed with anxiety（焦虑、紧张的面孔）}
  \wordphrase{tense atmosphere（紧张的气氛）}
  \wordexample{faces tensed with anxiety（焦虑、紧张的面孔）}
  \wordexample{tense atmosphere（紧张的气氛）}
  \wordexample{He tensed his muscles for the effort.（他将自己的肌肉绷起来发力。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{tensile}{'tensail}
  \wordbreakdown{具有张力的 tens (= stretch)}
  \wordsense{a. 张力的，紧张的；可伸长的，可拉长的}
  \wordfamily{tense}
  \wordfamily{tension}
  \wordfamily{tendon}
  \wordfamily{tender}
  \wordphrase{measure the tensile strength of wire（测定金属丝的张力）}
  \wordexample{measure the tensile strength of wire（测定金属丝的张力）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{tension}{'tenʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{拉紧的状态 tens (= stretch)}
  \wordsense{n. 紧张状态、程度，紧绷；张力；紧张；（人、团体、国家等之间的）紧张关系}
  \wordfamily{tensile}
  \wordfamily{tense}
  \wordfamily{tendon}
  \wordfamily{tender}
  \wordfamily{a tension（紧张状态、程度，紧绷）}
  \wordphrase{a tension（紧张状态、程度，紧绷）}
  \wordexample{a tension（紧张状态、程度，紧绷）}
\end{word}



\chapter{AG, ACT, IG}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 ag, act, ig，意为 to drive, to do。当 ag 之前为元音时，ag 中的 a 脱落变成 g。这几个词根用作 to drive 之意时，其同义词根有来自拉丁语的 flig, pel/puls [peal]。此外，来自拉丁语的词根 duct/duc [du], men [mean] 及来自希腊语的 agog 意为 to lead，含义相似。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{cogent}{'kəudʒənt}
  \wordbreakdown{共同驱动的 co<com (= together) + g<ag (= drive, do)}
  \wordfamily{cogency（说服力）；agitation（动摇）；ambiguous（引起歧义的）}
  \wordsense{a. 有说服力的}
  \wordphrase{cogent lecture}
  \wordexample{Our physics professor presented a very cogent lecture on quarks.（我们的物理学教授关于夸克理论的讲座十分有说服力。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{agitation}{,ædʒi'teiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{开走、带动起来的 ag (= do, drive)}
  \wordsense{n. 动摇，不安；激烈的辩论；煽动；（社会的）混乱}
  \wordfamily{agitate 使动摇，煽动}
  \wordfamily{agitator 煽动者，鼓动者}
  \wordfamily{agitated 激动的；焦虑的}
  \wordfamily{agitating 使不安的，使焦虑的}
  \wordphrase{agitation she feels is}
  \wordexample{The agitation she feels is due to a run-in she had with a teacher today.（她情绪激动是因为今天与老师进行了一场争论。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{ambiguous}{æm'bigjuəs}
  \wordbreakdown{转圈开的 amb<ambi (= around, both) + ig (= drive)}
  \wordsense{a. 引起歧义的；暧昧的，模糊的，意图不明确的}
  \wordfamily{ambiguity 暧昧，模糊，含糊不清；可作两种（或多种）解释}
  \wordphrase{ambiguous and contain many}
  \wordexample{The instructions are somewhat ambiguous and contain many grammatical mistakes.（使用说明书有些意思模糊，而且其中语法错误百出。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{exigency}{'eksidʒənsi}
  \wordbreakdown{赶到外边 ex (= out) + ig (= drive)}
  \wordfamily{exigent（紧急的，迫切的）；cogent（有说服力的）；agitation（动摇）}
  \wordsense{n. 危急，紧急状态，当务之急}
  \wordphrase{exigency}
  \wordexample{He put his house up for sale for reasons of financial exigency.（他因经济紧张而拍卖了房子。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{intransigent}{in'trænsidʒənt}
  \wordbreakdown{不能交叉成十字形拉走的 in (= not) + trans (= across) + ig (= drive)}
  \wordfamily{intransigence（不妥协的态度）；cogent（有说服力的）；agitation（动摇）}
  \wordsense{a. （尤指政治上）不妥协的}
  \wordphrase{be intransigent}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of intransigent.（此处展示 intransigent 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 不妥协的人}
  \wordphrase{intransigent about not letting}
  \wordexample{My parents are intransigent about not letting me drive until I'm 18.（我父母坚决不让我在我 18 岁之前开车。）}
  \wordexample{intransigent about not letting}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{prodigal}{'prɔdigl}
  \wordbreakdown{向前赶的 prod<pro (= forth) + ig (= drive)}
  \wordsense{a. 浪费的，挥霍的；放荡的；慷慨的；丰富的}
  \wordfamily{prodigality 挥霍，浪费}
  \wordfamily{prodigally 浪费地，挥霍地}
  \wordphrase{prodigal spending left him}
  \wordexample{My father's prodigal spending left him bankrupt after a few years.（我父亲的挥霍无度让他在几年后破产了。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{agency}{'eidʒənsi}
  \wordbreakdown{发挥作用的机构 ag (= do)}
  \wordsense{n. 代理，代理处，中介机构；（the \textasciitilde{} of）……的作用；政府的专门机构}
  \wordfamily{prodigal}
  \wordfamily{agenda}
  \wordfamily{intransigent}
  \wordfamily{agent}
  \wordphrase{the agency of water（水的作用）}
  \wordexample{the agency of water（水的作用）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{agenda}{ə'dʒendə}
  \wordbreakdown{待做之事 ag (= do)}
  \wordsense{n. 待议诸事项；议程；日常工作事项}
  \wordfamily{agency}
  \wordfamily{agent}
  \wordfamily{prodigal}
  \wordfamily{agile}
  \wordphrase{agenda}
  \wordexample{item No. 5 on the agenda（议程第五项）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{agent}{'eidʒənt}
  \wordbreakdown{做事的人或媒介 ag (= do)}
  \wordsense{n. 代理人，代理商；特工；原因，动因；【化】剂}
  \wordfamily{FBI agent（美国联邦调查局特工）}
  \wordfamily{chemical agents（化学药剂）}
  \wordfamily{an agent（代理人，代理商）}
  \wordphrase{an agent（代理人，代理商）}
  \wordexample{an agent（代理人，代理商）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{agile}{'ædʒail}
  \wordbreakdown{驱动得快的 ag (= drive)}
  \wordsense{a. 敏捷的，轻快的，灵活的}
  \wordfamily{agent}
  \wordfamily{stratagem}
  \wordfamily{agenda}
  \wordfamily{litigate}
  \wordfamily{be agile（敏捷的，轻快的，灵活的）}
  \wordphrase{be agile（敏捷的，轻快的，灵活的）}
  \wordexample{be agile（敏捷的，轻快的，灵活的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{stratagem}{'strætədʒəm}
  \wordbreakdown{驱动军队的办法 strat (= army) + ag (= drive)}
  \wordsense{n. 战略，对策}
  \wordfamily{agile}
  \wordfamily{litigate}
  \wordfamily{agent}
  \wordfamily{litigation}
  \wordfamily{a stratagem（战略，对策）}
  \wordphrase{a stratagem（战略，对策）}
  \wordexample{a stratagem（战略，对策）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{litigate}{'litigeit}
  \wordbreakdown{把争端推动到法庭 lit (= dispute) + ig (= drive)}
  \wordsense{v. 诉讼}
  \wordfamily{stratagem}
  \wordfamily{litigation}
  \wordfamily{agile}
  \wordfamily{navigable}
  \wordfamily{to litigate（诉讼）}
  \wordphrase{to litigate（诉讼）}
  \wordexample{to litigate（诉讼）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{litigation}{,liti'geiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{推动争端进入诉讼 lit (= dispute) + ig (= drive)}
  \wordfamily{litigant（诉讼当事人）；cogent（有说服力的）；agitation（动摇）}
  \wordsense{n. 诉讼}
  \wordphrase{a litigation（诉讼）}
  \wordexample{a litigation（诉讼）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{navigable}{'nævigəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{可被驱船航行的 nav (= ship, sail) + ig (= drive)}
  \wordsense{a.（河、海等）可航行的，适于航行的；可操纵的}
  \wordfamily{litigation}
  \wordfamily{navigate}
  \wordfamily{litigate}
  \wordfamily{navigation}
  \wordphrase{not in a navigable condition（不具备航海条件的）}
  \wordexample{not in a navigable condition（不具备航海条件的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{navigate}{'nævigeit}
  \wordbreakdown{驾驶船只前行 nav (= ship, sail) + ig (= drive)}
  \wordsense{v. 航海，航空；操纵}
  \wordfamily{navigable}
  \wordfamily{navigation}
  \wordfamily{litigation}
  \wordfamily{navigator}
  \wordfamily{to navigate（航海，航空）}
  \wordphrase{to navigate（航海，航空）}
  \wordexample{to navigate（航海，航空）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{navigation}{,nævi'geiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{航行、驾驶 nav (= ship, sail) + ig (= drive)}
  \wordsense{n. 航海，航空；航行}
  \wordfamily{navigate}
  \wordfamily{navigator}
  \wordfamily{navigable}
  \wordfamily{counteract}
  \wordphrase{Navigation Acts（航海条例）}
  \wordexample{Navigation Acts（航海条例）}

\begin{word}[***]{navigator}{'nævigeitə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{驾驶船只或飞机的人 nav (= ship, sail) + ig (= drive)}

  \wordfamily{navigation}
  \wordfamily{counteract}
  \wordfamily{navigate}
  \wordfamily{enact}
  \wordfamily{a navigator（航海者，航海家）}
  \wordphrase{a navigator（航海者，航海家）}
  \wordexample{a navigator（航海者，航海家）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{counteract}{,kauntər'ækt}
  \wordbreakdown{反着去做 counter (= against) + act (= do)}
  \wordfamily{cogent（有说服力的）；agitation（动摇）；ambiguous（引起歧义的）}
  \wordsense{v. 和……相抵消，对……起反作用；中和}
  \wordphrase{counteract his bad influence（消除他的不良影响）}
  \wordexample{counteract his bad influence（消除他的不良影响）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{enact}{i'nækt}
  \wordbreakdown{做成、制定 en (= make) + act (= do)}
  \wordsense{v. 制定（法律），颁布（法案）；上演，演出}
  \wordfamily{enactment（法的）制定，立法；法规，法令}
  \wordphrase{as by law enacted（按照法律规定）}
  \wordexample{as by law enacted（按照法律规定）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{exact}{ig'zækt}
  \wordbreakdown{逼着做出来 ex (= out, intensive) + act (= do)}
  \wordfamily{exactitude（正确）；勒索（之物）；cogent（有说服力的）}
  \wordsense{a. 正确的；准确的；精密的}
  \wordphrase{be exact（正确的）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "be exact".（常见搭配：正确的。）}
  \wordsense{v. 索取；强求；要求}
  \wordphrase{be exact（正确的）}
  \wordexample{the exact meaning of the word（这个词的准确意思）}
  \wordexample{be exact（正确的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{exactly}{ig'zæktli}
  \wordbreakdown{准确地做出来 ex (= out, intensive) + act (= do)}
  \wordfamily{Exactly!（一点不错！）；cogent（有说服力的）；agitation（动摇）}
  \wordsense{ad. 准确地，确切地；（肯定的回答）一点不错，正是}
  \wordphrase{exactly what I said}
  \wordexample{That's exactly what I said.（这正是我所说的。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{exacting}{ig'zæktiŋ}
  \wordbreakdown{不断强求的 ex (= out, intensive) + act (= do)}
  \wordsense{a. 苛求的，苛刻的，严格的；吃力的}
  \wordfamily{exactly}
  \wordfamily{inaction}
  \wordfamily{exact}
  \wordfamily{inactive}
  \wordphrase{exacting job（吃力的工作）}
  \wordexample{exacting job（吃力的工作）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{inaction}{in'ækʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{不去做 in (= not) + act (= do)}
  \wordsense{n. 不活动，无行动；懒惰}
  \wordfamily{exacting}
  \wordfamily{inactive}
  \wordfamily{exactly}
  \wordfamily{inactivate}
  \wordfamily{an inaction（不活动，无行动）}
  \wordphrase{an inaction（不活动，无行动）}
  \wordexample{an inaction（不活动，无行动）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{inactive}{in'æktiv}
  \wordbreakdown{不活动的 in (= not) + act (= do)}
  \wordsense{a. 不活动的，无行动的}
  \wordfamily{inaction}
  \wordfamily{inactivate}
  \wordfamily{exacting}
  \wordfamily{interact}
  \wordfamily{be inactive（不活动的，无行动的）}
  \wordphrase{be inactive（不活动的，无行动的）}
  \wordexample{be inactive（不活动的，无行动的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{inactivate}{in'æktiveit}
  \wordbreakdown{使之不能活动 in (= not) + act (= do)}
  \wordsense{v. 使无法活动}
  \wordfamily{inactive}
  \wordfamily{interact}
  \wordfamily{inaction}
  \wordfamily{interaction}
  \wordphrase{inactivate a virus（阻止病毒活动〔繁殖〕）}
  \wordexample{inactivate a virus（阻止病毒活动〔繁殖〕）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{interact}{,intər'ækt}
  \wordbreakdown{在彼此之间起作用 inter (= between) + act (= do)}
  \wordsense{v. 相互作用，相互影响}
  \wordfamily{inactivate}
  \wordfamily{interaction}
  \wordfamily{inactive}
  \wordfamily{overact}
  \wordfamily{to interact（相互作用，相互影响）}
  \wordphrase{to interact（相互作用，相互影响）}
  \wordexample{to interact（相互作用，相互影响）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{interaction}{,intər'ækʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{彼此之间的作用 inter (= between) + act (= do)}
  \wordsense{n. 相互作用，相互影响}
  \wordfamily{interactive 相互作用／影响的}
  \wordfamily{an interaction（相互作用，相互影响）}
  \wordphrase{an interaction（相互作用，相互影响）}
  \wordexample{an interaction（相互作用，相互影响）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{overact}{,əuvər'ækt}
  \wordbreakdown{做得过头 over (= excessive) + act (= do)}
  \wordsense{v. 行为过火；夸张地表演}
  \wordfamily{interaction}
  \wordfamily{radioactive}
  \wordfamily{interact}
  \wordfamily{react}
  \wordfamily{to overact（行为过火）}
  \wordphrase{to overact（行为过火）}
  \wordexample{to overact（行为过火）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{radioactive}{,reidiəu'æktiv}
  \wordbreakdown{带着射线活动的 radio (= ray) + act (= do)}
  \wordsense{a. 放射性的}
  \wordfamily{radioactivate 使带放射性}
  \wordfamily{radioactivity 放射能，放射现象}
  \wordphrase{radioactive substance（放射性物质）}
  \wordexample{radioactive substance（放射性物质）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{react}{ri'ækt}
  \wordbreakdown{反过来起作用 re (= back, again, against) + act (= do)}
  \wordsense{v.（\textasciitilde{}to）反应，起反应；（\textasciitilde{}against）反抗；（\textasciitilde{}on）起反作用；起（化学）反应}
  \wordfamily{radioactive}
  \wordfamily{reaction}
  \wordfamily{overact}
  \wordfamily{reactionary}
  \wordfamily{to react（反应，起反应）}
  \wordphrase{to react（反应，起反应）}
  \wordexample{to react（反应，起反应）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{reaction}{ri'ækʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{反过来的作用 re (= back, again, against) + act (= do)}
  \wordsense{n. 反应；反作用；倒退，复古；反动}
  \wordfamily{react}
  \wordfamily{reactionary}
  \wordfamily{radioactive}
  \wordfamily{reactor}
  \wordphrase{action and reaction（作用和反作用）}
  \wordexample{action and reaction（作用和反作用）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{reactionary}{ri'ækʃənri}
  \wordbreakdown{带有反作用倾向的 re (= back, again, against) + act (= do) + ary (= person who, pertaining to)}
  \wordfamily{reaction；reactor；react；retroactive；cogent（有说服力的）}
  \wordsense{n. 反动势力}
  \wordphrase{a reactionary（反动势力）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "a reactionary".（常见搭配：反动势力。）}
  \wordsense{a. 反动的；复古的}
  \wordphrase{a reactionary（反动势力）}
  \wordexample{a reactionary（反动势力）}
  \wordexample{be reactionary（反动的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{reactor}{ri'æktə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{起反应的人或物 re (= back, again, against) + act (= do)}
  \wordfamily{cogent（有说服力的）；agitation（动摇）；ambiguous（引起歧义的）}
  \wordsense{n. 起反应的人（或物）}
  \wordphrase{a reactor（起反应的人（或物））}
  \wordexample{a reactor（起反应的人（或物））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{retroactive}{,retrəu'æktiv}
  \wordbreakdown{向后发生作用 retro (= backward) + act (= do)}
  \wordfamily{retroactively（有追溯效力地）；cogent（有说服力的）；agitation（动摇）}
  \wordsense{a. （法律、工资支付等）具有追溯效力的}
  \wordphrase{retroactive to July 11（追溯至 7 月 11 日）}
  \wordexample{retroactive to July 11（追溯至 7 月 11 日）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{transact}{træn'zækt}
  \wordbreakdown{穿过去做 trans (= through, change) + act (= do)}
  \wordsense{v. 处理（事务），执行；交易}
  \wordfamily{retroactive}
  \wordfamily{transaction}
  \wordfamily{reactor}
  \wordfamily{underact}
  \wordfamily{to transact（处理（事务），执行）}
  \wordphrase{to transact（处理（事务），执行）}
  \wordexample{to transact（处理（事务），执行）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{transaction}{træn'zækʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{处理、办理、交易的过程 trans (= through, change) + act (= do)}
  \wordfamily{cogent（有说服力的）；agitation（动摇）；ambiguous（引起歧义的）}
  \wordsense{n. 办理，处理；交易；业务；（pl.）（学会等的）议事录；会报}
  \wordphrase{the transaction of business（事务处理）}
  \wordexample{the transaction of business（事务处理）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{underact}{,ʌndər'ækt}
  \wordbreakdown{做得不够 under (= under, inferior) + act (= do)}
  \wordsense{v. 不卖力地表演，不充分地表演}
  \wordfamily{transaction}
  \wordfamily{act}
  \wordfamily{transact}
  \wordfamily{action}
  \wordfamily{to underact（不卖力地表演，不充分地表演）}
  \wordphrase{to underact（不卖力地表演，不充分地表演）}
  \wordexample{to underact（不卖力地表演，不充分地表演）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{act}{ækt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ACT}
  \wordfamily{cogent（有说服力的）；agitation（动摇）；ambiguous（引起歧义的）}
  \wordsense{n. 行为，行动；法令，条例；（戏剧的）幕；（马戏、杂耍表演中）连续的很短暂的一段；假装}
  \wordphrase{heroic act（英勇的行为）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "heroic act".（常见搭配：英勇的行为。）}
  \wordsense{v. 行动，做；（\textasciitilde{}upon）（药）对……起作用；（\textasciitilde{}as）扮演……的角色；（\textasciitilde{}for）代理；（\textasciitilde{}up）捣乱，胡闹；起作用，尽职责}
  \wordphrase{Acts of Congress（国会法案）}
  \wordexample{Acts of Congress（国会法案）}
  \wordexample{Don't act the fool / ass / idiot.（别做傻事。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{action}{'ækʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{做出来的动作或行为 act (= do)}
  \wordsense{n. 行动，行为；动作，姿态；作用，机能；诉讼；战斗；（钢琴、枪等的）机械装置}
  \wordfamily{actionable 可以提起诉讼的}
  \wordfamily{take action（采取行动）}
  \wordfamily{chemical action（化学作用）}
  \wordphrase{civil / criminal action（民事 / 刑事诉讼）}
  \wordphrase{Clear for action!（准备作战！）}
  \wordexample{Actions speak louder than words.（行动比语言更有说服力。）}
  \wordexample{civil / criminal action（民事 / 刑事诉讼）}
  \wordexample{Clear for action!（准备作战！）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{acting}{'æktiŋ}
  \wordbreakdown{正在做、代做的 act (= do)}
  \wordfamily{action；active；act；activity；cogent（有说服力的）}
  \wordsense{a. 代理的，临时的；表演的，表演用的}
  \wordphrase{be acting}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of acting.（此处展示 acting 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 演技；装腔作势}
  \wordphrase{Acting President}
  \wordexample{The Acting President of Harvard University（哈佛大学代理校长）}
  \wordexample{Acting President}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{active}{'æktiv}
  \wordbreakdown{在活动中的 act (= do)}
  \wordfamily{actively（活泼地）；cogent（有说服力的）；agitation（动摇）}
  \wordsense{a. 活动的；有活力的；现行的；在活动中的；主动的}
  \wordphrase{active volcano（活火山）}
  \wordphrase{active market（生机勃勃的市场）}
  \wordphrase{on active service（现役）}
  \wordexample{active volcano（活火山）}
  \wordexample{active market（生机勃勃的市场）}
  \wordexample{on active service（现役）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{activity}{æk'tivəti}
  \wordbreakdown{活动的状态 act (= do)}
  \wordsense{n. 活动；活跃；（pl.）（某一团体的）活动；活动能力；活力}
  \wordfamily{active}
  \wordfamily{activist}
  \wordfamily{acting}
  \wordfamily{activate}
  \wordphrase{school / classroom / outdoor activities（校内活动 / 教室内活动 / 室外活动）}
  \wordexample{school / classroom / outdoor activities（校内活动 / 教室内活动 / 室外活动）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{activist}{'æktivist}
  \wordbreakdown{主张行动的人 act (= do)}
  \wordsense{n.（政治运动中的）行动主义者；（政党等的）激进分子}
  \wordfamily{activity}
  \wordfamily{activate}
  \wordfamily{active}
  \wordfamily{actual}
  \wordphrase{Republican / Democratic party activist（《美》共和党 / 民主党激进分子〔尤其指积极参与地方政党活动，在地区管理及活动中表现活跃的分子〕）}
  \wordexample{Republican / Democratic party activist（《美》共和党 / 民主党激进分子〔尤其指积极参与地方政党活动，在地区管理及活动中表现活跃的分子〕）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{activate}{'æktiveit}
  \wordbreakdown{使之活动 act (= do)}
  \wordsense{v. 使活动；使活化}
  \wordfamily{activist}
  \wordfamily{actual}
  \wordfamily{activity}
  \wordfamily{actually}
  \wordfamily{to activate（使活动）}
  \wordphrase{to activate（使活动）}
  \wordexample{to activate（使活动）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{actual}{'æktʃuəl}
  \wordbreakdown{真正做出来的 act (= do)}
  \wordfamily{actuality（现实性）；cogent（有说服力的）；agitation（动摇）}
  \wordsense{a. 实际的，事实上的；现实的}
  \wordphrase{actual condition（实际状况）}
  \wordphrase{the actual state of Iraq（伊拉克的实际情况）}
  \wordexample{actual condition（实际状况）}
  \wordexample{the actual state of Iraq（伊拉克的实际情况）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{actually}{'æktʃuəli}
  \wordbreakdown{实际上、真正地 act (= do)}
  \wordsense{ad. 实际上，事实上；（表示强调或惊讶）竟然，居然，令人意外地；如今}
  \wordfamily{actual}
  \wordfamily{actuary}
  \wordfamily{activate}
  \wordfamily{actuate}
  \wordphrase{the political party actually in power（执政党）}
  \wordexample{the political party actually in power（执政党）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{actuary}{'æktʃuəri}
  \wordbreakdown{从事精算工作的人 act (= do)}
  \wordfamily{cogent（有说服力的）；agitation（动摇）；ambiguous（引起歧义的）}
  \wordsense{n. 保险精算师}
  \wordphrase{an actuary（保险精算师）}
  \wordexample{an actuary（保险精算师）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{actuate}{'æktʃueit}
  \wordbreakdown{驱使去做 act (= do)}
  \wordsense{v. 激励；开动}
  \wordfamily{actuary}
  \wordfamily{actually}
  \wordfamily{actual}
  \wordfamily{activate}
  \wordphrase{A great statesman is}
  \wordexample{A great statesman is actuated by love of his country, not by love of power.（一个伟大的政治家应该在爱国心而非权力欲望的驱使下行动。）}
\end{word}



\chapter{PRIS}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 pris 意为 to take, to seize。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 cap / capt / cept / ceiv[cip, cup, ceit], empt / em, rap / rav, sum / sumpt, lab / lep 等。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{comprise}{kəmˈpraiz}
  \wordbreakdown{一起抓 com (= together) + pris (= take)}
  \wordsense{vt. 由……组成；包含，包括}
  \wordfamily{apprise}
  \wordfamily{enterprising}
  \wordfamily{reprisal}
  \wordfamily{imprison}
  \wordphrase{comprises fifty states}
  \wordexample{The American Union comprises fifty states.（美联邦由 50 个州组成。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{apprise}{əˈpraiz}
  \wordbreakdown{把某物带给某人 ap<ad (= to) + pris (= take)}
  \wordsense{vt. sb + of + sth 告知}
  \wordfamily{comprise}
  \wordfamily{enterprising}
  \wordfamily{reprisal}
  \wordfamily{imprison}
  \wordphrase{The army apprised the}
  \wordexample{The army apprised the woman of her husband's death in military action.（部队告知那个女子她的丈夫在战斗中牺牲了。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{enterprising}{ˈentəpraiziŋ}
  \wordbreakdown{在其间夺取，占据的 enter<inter (= between) + pris (= take)}
  \wordsense{a. 有事业心的；进取的；有冒险精神的}
  \wordfamily{enterprise 事业；企业；进取精神，冒险精神}
  \wordphrase{enterprising nature gives her}
  \wordexample{Her enterprising nature gives her a high probability for success as a CEO.（她积极进取的性格使她很有可能成为一名成功的首席执行官。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{reprisal}{riˈpraizl}
  \wordbreakdown{因报答、回报而抓住 re (= back) + pris (= take)}
  \wordsense{n. 报仇，报复}
  \wordfamily{enterprising}
  \wordfamily{imprison}
  \wordfamily{apprise}
  \wordfamily{imprisonment}
  \wordfamily{reprisals}
  \wordphrase{reprisals}
  \wordexample{She didn't report her husband's abuse for fear of reprisals.（她因为害怕遭到报复而没有揭发丈夫的虐待行为。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{imprison}{imˈprizn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PRIS}
  \wordfamily{imprisonment；prison；prisoner；surprise；comprise（由……组成）}
  \wordsense{v. 囚禁，关押 *im<in (= into)}
  \wordphrase{to imprison（囚禁，关押）}
  \wordexample{to imprison（囚禁，关押）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{imprisonment}{imˈpriznmənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PRIS}
  \wordsense{n. 关押，囚禁}
  \wordfamily{imprison}
  \wordfamily{prison}
  \wordfamily{prisoner}
  \wordfamily{surprise}
  \wordphrase{one year's imprisonment（一年监禁）}
  \wordexample{one year's imprisonment（一年监禁）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{prison}{ˈprizn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PRIS}
  \wordfamily{comprise（由……组成）；apprise（sb + of + sth 告知）；enterprising（有事业心的）}
  \wordsense{n. 监狱，牢狱；拘留所；监禁，囚禁，关押}
  \wordphrase{prison breaking（越狱）}
  \wordphrase{be released from prison（被从监狱释放）}
  \wordexample{prison breaking（越狱）}
  \wordexample{be released from prison（被从监狱释放）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{prisoner}{ˈpriznə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PRIS}
  \wordsense{n. 囚犯，犯人；俘虏}
  \wordfamily{prison}
  \wordfamily{surprise}
  \wordfamily{imprisonment}
  \wordfamily{surprising}
  \wordphrase{prisoner of conscience（政治犯；宗教犯）}
  \wordexample{prisoner of conscience（政治犯；宗教犯）}
  \wordexample{prisoner of war（战俘〔缩写：P.O.W.〕）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{surprise}{səˈpraiz}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PRIS}
  \wordfamily{prisoner；surprising；prison；imprisonment；comprise（由……组成）}
  \wordsense{n. 惊奇；惊人之事，意外之事；突袭，奇袭}
  \wordphrase{the surprise}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of surprise.（此处展示 surprise 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. 使惊奇；突袭}
  \wordphrase{surprise the issue}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of surprise.（此处展示 surprise 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{a. 令人惊讶的，感到意外的 *sur (= above, over)}
  \wordphrase{surprise party（惊喜聚会〈秘密策划的庆祝活动〉）}
  \wordphrase{be taken by surprise（遭到突袭）}
  \wordphrase{surprise attack / visit（突然袭击 / 突访）}
  \wordexample{surprise party（惊喜聚会〈秘密策划的庆祝活动〉）}
  \wordexample{be taken by surprise（遭到突袭）}
  \wordexample{surprise attack / visit（突然袭击 / 突访）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{surprising}{səˈpraiziŋ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PRIS}
  \wordfamily{surprisingly（惊讶地，意外地）；comprise（由……组成）；apprise（sb + of + sth 告知）}
  \wordsense{a. 令人惊讶的，意外的 *sur (= above, over)}
  \wordphrase{stinct / sting / stig / sti。}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of surprising.（此处展示 surprising 的常见用法。）}
\end{word}



\chapter{PUNCT, PUNG[PUNC, POUNC, POIGN, PON, POINT]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 punct, pung 意为 to prick。punc, pounc, poign, pon, point 等是它们的变形。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 stinct / sting / stig / sti。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{compunction}{kəmˈpʌŋkʃən}
  \wordbreakdown{用力地、狠狠地扎 com (= intensive) + punct (= prick)}
  \wordsense{n. 良心的谴责；悔恨}
  \wordfamily{compunctious 内疚的，后悔的}
  \wordfamily{compunctiously 后悔地}
  \wordphrase{compunction at beating their}
  \wordexample{Some parents feel no compunction at beating their disobedient children.（有些父母因为孩子不听话而打孩子时，从不感到内疚。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{punctilio}{pʌŋkˈtiliəu}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PUNCT, PUNG[PUNC, POUNC, POIGN, PON, POINT]}
  \wordsense{n. （仪式等的）细枝；拘泥细节}
  \wordfamily{punctilious}
  \wordfamily{punctual}
  \wordfamily{punctuality}
  \wordfamily{punctuate}
  \wordphrase{stand upon punctilios（过分拘泥于细节）}
  \wordexample{stand upon punctilios（过分拘泥于细节）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{punctilious}{pʌŋkˈtiliəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PUNCT, PUNG[PUNC, POUNC, POIGN, PON, POINT]}
  \wordsense{a. 谨小慎微的；拘泥于细节的}
  \wordfamily{punctiliously（谨小慎微地；拘泥于细节地）}
  \wordfamily{be punctilious（谨小慎微的）}
  \wordphrase{be punctilious（谨小慎微的）}
  \wordexample{be punctilious（谨小慎微的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{punctual}{ˈpʌŋktʃuəl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PUNCT, PUNG[PUNC, POUNC, POIGN, PON, POINT]}
  \wordfamily{punctually（严守时间地）；compunction（良心的谴责）；punctilio（（仪式等的）细枝）}
  \wordsense{a. 严守时间的，准时的}
  \wordphrase{punctual as the clock（〈像钟表般〉准时的）}
  \wordphrase{punctual to the minute（分秒不差，准时）}
  \wordexample{be punctual for the lecture / in payment of one's rent（准时上课 / 按时交纳房租）}
  \wordexample{punctual as the clock（〈像钟表般〉准时的）}
  \wordexample{punctual to the minute（分秒不差，准时）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{punctuality}{ˌpʌŋktʃuˈæləti}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PUNCT, PUNG[PUNC, POUNC, POIGN, PON, POINT]}
  \wordsense{n. 严守时间}
  \wordfamily{punctual}
  \wordfamily{punctuate}
  \wordfamily{punctilious}
  \wordfamily{punctuation}
  \wordfamily{a punctuality（严守时间）}
  \wordphrase{a punctuality（严守时间）}
  \wordexample{a punctuality（严守时间）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{punctuate}{ˈpʌŋktʃueit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PUNCT, PUNG[PUNC, POUNC, POIGN, PON, POINT]}
  \wordsense{v. 在……间加标点；不时打断}
  \wordfamily{punctuality}
  \wordfamily{punctuation}
  \wordfamily{punctual}
  \wordfamily{puncture}
  \wordphrase{speech punctuated with cheers（不时被叫喝彩声打断的演说）}
  \wordphrase{punctuate one's remarks with thumps on the table（时不时敲击桌子打断某人的话）}
  \wordexample{speech punctuated with cheers（不时被叫喝彩声打断的演说）}
  \wordexample{punctuate one's remarks with thumps on the table（时不时敲击桌子打断某人的话）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{punctuation}{ˌpʌŋktʃuˈeiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PUNCT, PUNG[PUNC, POUNC, POIGN, PON, POINT]}
  \wordsense{n. 标点}
  \wordfamily{punctuate}
  \wordfamily{puncture}
  \wordfamily{punctuality}
  \wordfamily{expunge}
  \wordphrase{punctuation mark（标点）}
  \wordexample{punctuation mark（标点）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{puncture}{ˈpʌŋktʃə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PUNCT, PUNG[PUNC, POUNC, POIGN, PON, POINT]}
  \wordfamily{punctuation；expunge；punctuate；pungent；compunction（良心的谴责）}
  \wordsense{v. 穿孔，扎，刺}
  \wordphrase{puncture the issue}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of puncture.（此处展示 puncture 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. （用尖的东西扎出的）小孔}
  \wordphrase{the puncture}
  \wordexample{puncture an abscess / her husband's ego（刺破脓肿 / 刺伤她丈夫的自尊心）}
  \wordexample{Two of my tires punctured while I was on that stony road.（经过满是石子的马路时，我的两个车胎被扎破了。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{expunge}{ikˈspʌndʒ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PUNCT, PUNG[PUNC, POUNC, POIGN, PON, POINT]}
  \wordfamily{puncture；pungent；punctuation；pounce；compunction（良心的谴责）}
  \wordsense{v. 擦去；抹去；删除 *ex (= out)}
  \wordphrase{to expunge（擦去）}
  \wordexample{to expunge（擦去）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{pungent}{ˈpʌndʒənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PUNCT, PUNG[PUNC, POUNC, POIGN, PON, POINT]}
  \wordsense{a. （气味、味道）辛辣的；刺激性的；（言语等）尖锐的，辛辣的，尖刻的}
  \wordfamily{pungently（辛辣地；刺激地）}
  \wordfamily{pungency（辛辣；尖锐；刺激性）}
  \wordphrase{pungent sauce（辛辣的酱汁）}
  \wordphrase{pungent criticism / remarks / satire（尖刻的批评 / 话 / 讽刺）}
  \wordexample{pungent sauce（辛辣的酱汁）}
  \wordexample{pungent criticism / remarks / satire（尖刻的批评 / 话 / 讽刺）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{pounce}{pauns}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PUNCT, PUNG[PUNC, POUNC, POIGN, PON, POINT]}
  \wordfamily{compunction（良心的谴责）；punctilio（（仪式等的）细枝）；punctilious（谨小慎微的）}
  \wordsense{v. 猛扑；突袭 *pounc<pung (= prick)}
  \wordphrase{The tiger pounced savagely}
  \wordexample{The tiger pounced savagely on a mouse.（那只老虎猛地扑向一只老鼠。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{punch}{pʌntʃ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PUNCT, PUNG[PUNC, POUNC, POIGN, PON, POINT]}
  \wordfamily{compunction（良心的谴责）；punctilio（（仪式等的）细枝）；punctilious（谨小慎微的）}
  \wordsense{n. 打孔；打孔机，穿孔机，冲压机；猛击，用拳头击打；力量；活力；潘趣酒（酒、果汁、香料等混合而成的饮料）}
  \wordphrase{punched cards（打孔卡）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "punched cards".（常见搭配：打孔卡。）}
  \wordsense{v. （用打孔机）打孔、冲孔；用拳头猛击 *punc<pung (= prick)}
  \wordphrase{punch him on the chin（对他的下巴猛地一击）}
  \wordphrase{face I'd like to punch（欠揍的脸）}
  \wordphrase{Punch-and-Judy show（英国滑稽木偶剧〈主人公潘趣杀死了孩子和妻子朱迪，最终被判处绞刑〉） (as)}
  \wordexample{give him a punch on the nose（对他的鼻子猛地一击）}
  \wordexample{punch him on the chin（对他的下巴猛地一击）}
  \wordexample{face I'd like to punch（欠揍的脸）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{poignant}{ˈpɔinjənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PUNCT, PUNG[PUNC, POUNC, POIGN, PON, POINT]}
  \wordfamily{compunction（良心的谴责）；punctilio（（仪式等的）细枝）；punctilious（谨小慎微的）}
  \wordsense{a. 痛切的；伤心的；辛酸的 *poign<pung (= prick)}
  \wordphrase{be poignant}
  \wordexample{poignant sorrow / regret / memories（彻骨的伤痛 / 深深的懊悔 / 痛苦的回忆）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{poniard}{ˈpɒnjəd}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PUNCT, PUNG[PUNC, POUNC, POIGN, PON, POINT]}
  \wordfamily{poignant；point；punch；pointed；compunction（良心的谴责）}
  \wordsense{n. 短剑，匕首}
  \wordphrase{a poniard（短剑，匕首）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "a poniard".（常见搭配：短剑，匕首。）}
  \wordsense{v. 以匕首刺 *pon<pung (= prick)}
  \wordphrase{a poniard（短剑，匕首）}
  \wordexample{a poniard（短剑，匕首）}
  \wordexample{to poniard（以匕首刺）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{point}{pɔint}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PUNCT, PUNG[PUNC, POUNC, POIGN, PON, POINT]}
  \wordfamily{compunction（良心的谴责）；punctilio（（仪式等的）细枝）；punctilious（谨小慎微的）}
  \wordsense{v. (\textasciitilde{} to) 指；显示，表示，表明；(\textasciitilde{} at) 向……瞄准；指向……；(\textasciitilde{} out) 指出；削尖（铅笔等）；强调}
  \wordphrase{turning point（转折点）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "turning point".（常见搭配：转折点。）}
  \wordsense{n. 要点，要义，论点；项目，细目；特点；瞬间，刹那；（钉子、铅笔、刀的）尖头，尖端；（作为符号的）点；（空间、时间上的或想法的）点；（温度计、指南针等的）度；得分，评分；学分 *point<pung (= prick)}
  \wordphrase{point a pencil（把铅笔削尖）}
  \wordphrase{miss the point（没抓住要点）}
  \wordphrase{good / bad points（优点 / 缺点）}
  \wordexample{What's the point?（重点是什么？）}
  \wordexample{at the point of the sword / bayonet（剑拔弩张）}
  \wordexample{the boiling / freezing / melting point（沸点 / 冰点 / 熔点）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{pointed}{ˈpɔintid}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PUNCT, PUNG[PUNC, POUNC, POIGN, PON, POINT]}
  \wordfamily{point；point-blank；poniard；pointer；compunction（良心的谴责）}
  \wordsense{a. 有针对性的（明确指向某人或行为）；尖锐的 *point<pung (= prick)}
  \wordphrase{pointed reproof（尖锐的指责）}
  \wordexample{show pointed attentions to the glamorous movie star（十分关注那位迷人的电影明星）}
  \wordexample{pointed reproof（尖锐的指责）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{point-blank}{ˌpɔintˈblæŋk}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PUNCT, PUNG[PUNC, POUNC, POIGN, PON, POINT]}
  \wordfamily{pointed；pointer；point；pointless；compunction（良心的谴责）}
  \wordsense{a. （射击）近距离平射的；直截了当的}
  \wordphrase{be point-blank}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of point-blank.（此处展示 point-blank 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{ad. 近距离平射地；直截了当地 *blank (= white), point<pung (= prick)}
  \wordphrase{fire at point-blank range（近距离射击）}
  \wordphrase{point-blank refusal（直截了当的拒绝）}
  \wordexample{fire at point-blank range（近距离射击）}
  \wordexample{point-blank refusal（直截了当的拒绝）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{pointer}{ˈpɔintə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PUNCT, PUNG[PUNC, POUNC, POIGN, PON, POINT]}
  \wordfamily{point-blank；pointless；pointed；appoint；compunction（良心的谴责）}
  \wordsense{n. 指针；短毛大猎狗 *point<pung (= prick)}
  \wordphrase{a pointer（指针）}
  \wordexample{a pointer（指针）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{pointless}{ˈpɔintləs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PUNCT, PUNG[PUNC, POUNC, POIGN, PON, POINT]}
  \wordfamily{pointer；appoint；point-blank；appointed；compunction（良心的谴责）}
  \wordsense{a. 无意义的；没有目标的，无目的的；未得分的 *point<pung (= prick), less (= without)}
  \wordphrase{pointless draw（零比零）}
  \wordexample{pointless draw（零比零）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{appoint}{əˈpɔint}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PUNCT, PUNG[PUNC, POUNC, POIGN, PON, POINT]}
  \wordfamily{appointee（被任命者）；compunction（良心的谴责）；punctilio（（仪式等的）细枝）}
  \wordsense{v. 任命，委任；指定（时间、地点等）；约定；给……提供装备 *ap<ad (= to), point<pung (= prick)}
  \wordphrase{to appoint（任命，委任）}
  \wordexample{to appoint（任命，委任）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{appointed}{əˈpɔintid}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PUNCT, PUNG[PUNC, POUNC, POIGN, PON, POINT]}
  \wordfamily{appoint；appointment；pointless；disappoint；compunction（良心的谴责）}
  \wordsense{a. 指定的，指派的；约定的；设备好的 *ap<ad (= to), point<pung (= prick)}
  \wordphrase{at the appointed time（在约定的时间）}
  \wordphrase{his appointed task（指派给他的任务）}
  \wordphrase{well-appointed / badly-appointed library（设备好的 / 简陋的图书馆）}
  \wordexample{at the appointed time（在约定的时间）}
  \wordexample{his appointed task（指派给他的任务）}
  \wordexample{well-appointed / badly-appointed library（设备好的 / 简陋的图书馆）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{appointment}{əˈpɔintmənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PUNCT, PUNG[PUNC, POUNC, POIGN, PON, POINT]}
  \wordfamily{appointed；disappoint；appoint；disappointment；compunction（良心的谴责）}
  \wordsense{n. 指定；任命，任用；约会；职位，职务；（pl.）设备，陈设 *ap<ad (= to), point<pung (= prick)}
  \wordphrase{the appointment}
  \wordexample{make / keep / break an appointment with him（约好和他见面 / 遵守约定与他见面 / 失约没去和他见面）}
  \wordexample{get a good appointment in a business firm（在公司里谋求到一个好职位）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{disappoint}{ˌdisəˈpɔint}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PUNCT, PUNG[PUNC, POUNC, POIGN, PON, POINT]}
  \wordfamily{disappointed（失望的）；disappointing（令人失望的，辜负期望的）；disappointedly（失望地，灰心地）；compunction（良心的谴责）}
  \wordsense{v. 使失望；挫败（计划等）；使（希望等）破灭 *dis (= away, apart), ap<ad (= to), point<pung (= prick)}
  \wordphrase{to disappoint（使失望）}
  \wordexample{to disappoint（使失望）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{disappointment}{ˌdisəˈpɔintmənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PUNCT, PUNG[PUNC, POUNC, POIGN, PON, POINT]}
  \wordfamily{compunction（良心的谴责）；punctilio（（仪式等的）细枝）；punctilious（谨小慎微的）}
  \wordsense{n. 失望；令人失望的人（或事物） *dis (= away, apart), ap<ad (= to), point<pung (= prick)}
  \wordphrase{to my great disappointment（令我极其失望的是）}
  \wordphrase{suffer many disappointments in love（经历多次失恋）}
  \wordexample{to my great disappointment（令我极其失望的是）}
  \wordexample{suffer many disappointments in love（经历多次失恋）}
\end{word}



\chapter{CLUD, CLUS[CLOS, CLOIS, CLAUS, CLOT]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 clud, clus 意为 to shut。clos, clois, claus, clot 是它们的变形。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{conclusive}{kənˈklu:siv}
  \wordbreakdown{一起关闭的 con<com (= together) + clus (= shut)}
  \wordsense{a. 决定性的；确凿的；最终的}
  \wordfamily{conclude 下结论}
  \wordfamily{conclusion 结论}
  \wordfamily{conclusively 决定性地，最终地}
  \wordphrase{conclusive evidence that the}
  \wordexample{The investigator presented conclusive evidence that the criminal was guilty.（那位调查员出示了确凿的证据，证明那个犯人有罪。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{preclude}{priˈklu:d}
  \wordbreakdown{事先关上、关押起来 pre (= before) + clud (= shut)}
  \wordsense{vt. 阻止，妨碍；排除；防止}
  \wordfamily{preclusion 排除；妨碍；防止}
  \wordfamily{preclusive 排除的；妨碍的；防止的}
  \wordphrase{preclude getting facial cosmetic}
  \wordexample{Some medical conditions preclude getting facial cosmetic surgery.（某些医疗条件妨碍脸部整形手术的进行。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{recluse}{riˈklu:s}
  \wordbreakdown{不露面，躲在里面过着封闭的生活 re (= back) + clus (= shut)}
  \wordfamily{preclude；seclusion；conclusive；exclude}
  \wordsense{n. 脱离世俗的人；隐遁者}
  \wordphrase{the recluse}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of recluse.（此处展示 recluse 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{a. 脱离世俗的，隐遁的}
  \wordphrase{recluse}
  \wordexample{My aunt is agoraphobic and something of a recluse.（我姑姑有广场恐惧症，活得像个隐士。）}
  \wordexample{recluse}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{seclusion}{siˈklu:ʒn}
  \wordbreakdown{脱离，过着封闭的生活 se (= away) + clus (= shut)}
  \wordsense{n. 隔绝；隐退，隐遁}
  \wordfamily{seclude 隔绝；使隐退}
  \wordfamily{secluded 隔绝的；隐退的；偏僻的}
  \wordphrase{seclusion after the traffic}
  \wordexample{She went into seclusion after the traffic death of her toddler.（自从她那蹒跚学步的孩子死于车祸后，她便过起了隐居的生活。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{exclude}{ikˈsklu:d}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CLUD, CLUS[CLOS, CLOIS, CLAUS, CLOT]}
  \wordfamily{conclusive（决定性的）；preclude（阻止）；recluse（脱离世俗的人）}
  \wordsense{v. 拒绝（接纳）；排斥；排除（可能性） *ex (= out)}
  \wordphrase{exclude all possibility of doubt（排除一切怀疑的可能性）}
  \wordexample{exclude all possibility of doubt（排除一切怀疑的可能性）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{exclusive}{ikˈsklu:siv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CLUD, CLUS[CLOS, CLOIS, CLAUS, CLOT]}
  \wordfamily{conclusive（决定性的）；preclude（阻止）；recluse（脱离世俗的人）}
  \wordsense{a. 排除的，除外的；孤傲的；（集团、团体）排他的；（商品）独家经营的；独占的，唯一的 *ex (= out)}
  \wordphrase{exclusive economic zone（专属经济区〔缩写：EEZ〕）}
  \wordphrase{exclusive right（专有权）}
  \wordexample{exclusive economic zone（专属经济区〔缩写：EEZ〕）}
  \wordexample{exclusive right（专有权）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{include}{inˈklu:d}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CLUD, CLUS[CLOS, CLOIS, CLAUS, CLOT]}
  \wordfamily{inclusion（包含，算入）；including（six）；conclusive（决定性的）}
  \wordsense{v. 包含，算入 *in (= in)}
  \wordphrase{including six}
  \wordexample{This atlas contains fifty maps, including six of North America.（这本地图册共由 50 幅地图组成，其中包括 6 张北美地图。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{including}{inˈklu:diŋ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CLUD, CLUS[CLOS, CLOIS, CLAUS, CLOT]}
  \wordfamily{include；inclusive；exclusive；disclose；conclusive（决定性的）}
  \wordsense{prep. 包含，包括 *in (= in)}
  \wordphrase{including myself}
  \wordexample{There are seven of us including myself.（把我算在内共 7 个人。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{inclusive}{inˈklu:siv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CLUD, CLUS[CLOS, CLOIS, CLAUS, CLOT]}
  \wordfamily{inclusively（包括地，在内地）；conclusive（决定性的）；preclude（阻止）}
  \wordsense{a. (\textasciitilde{} of) 包括的，包含的 *in (= in)}
  \wordphrase{five inclusive of the driver（包括司机共 5 个人）}
  \wordexample{five inclusive of the driver（包括司机共 5 个人）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{disclose}{disˈklauz}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CLUD, CLUS[CLOS, CLOIS, CLAUS, CLOT]}
  \wordfamily{inclusive；disclosure；including；enclose；conclusive（决定性的）}
  \wordsense{v. 揭露，暴露，揭开，透露；公开 *dis (= not)}
  \wordphrase{disclose the issue}
  \wordexample{refuse to disclose one's name and address（拒绝透露姓名和地址）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{disclosure}{disˈklauʒə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CLUD, CLUS[CLOS, CLOIS, CLAUS, CLOT]}
  \wordfamily{disclose；enclose；inclusive；enclosure；conclusive（决定性的）}
  \wordsense{n. 暴露，透露；公开；被揭发的事 *dis (= not)}
  \wordphrase{a disclosure（暴露，透露）}
  \wordexample{a disclosure（暴露，透露）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{enclose}{inˈklauz}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CLUD, CLUS[CLOS, CLOIS, CLAUS, CLOT]}
  \wordfamily{disclosure；enclosure；disclose；close；conclusive（决定性的）}
  \wordsense{v. 围绕，（用围墙或栅栏等）围起来；封（信）；放入（纸袋、包裹），封入 *en (= in)}
  \wordphrase{A check for 50}
  \wordexample{A check for 50 dollars is enclosed.（附上了一张 50 美元的支票。）}
  \wordexample{Enclosed please find a check for 50 dollars.（请收下附上的 50 美元支票。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{enclosure}{inˈklauʒə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CLUD, CLUS[CLOS, CLOIS, CLAUS, CLOT]}
  \wordsense{n. 围栏；围绕；（特指随信一起）封入；封入之物}
  \wordfamily{enclose}
  \wordfamily{close}
  \wordfamily{disclosure}
  \wordfamily{closed}
  \wordphrase{enclosure of common land（用围栏圈起一块公有土地）}
  \wordexample{enclosure of common land（用围栏圈起一块公有土地）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{close}{klauz}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CLUD, CLUS[CLOS, CLOIS, CLAUS, CLOT]}
  \wordfamily{enclosure；closed；enclose；closing；conclusive（决定性的）}
  \wordsense{v. 闭（眼）；关（门、窗等）；收起（伞）；合上（书）；封闭（通道、入口、小孔）；关闭（店铺等）；结束，终止；商定（交易）；靠拢，密集}
  \wordphrase{close the issue}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of close.（此处展示 close 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. （期限的）结束；（活动等的）终止；（四周围住的）场地；院子；死胡同}
  \wordphrase{the close}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of close.（此处展示 close 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{a. （空间、时间上）接近的；（关系）密切的，亲密的；（在比赛中）势均力敌的，差异微小的；密集的；缜密的，周密的；吝啬的；（天气）闷热的；（房间等）通风不畅的，（空气）污浊的；隐藏的，非公开的}
  \wordphrase{be close}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of close.（此处展示 close 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{ad. 接近地；紧密地；紧紧地}
  \wordphrase{close a deal（成交）}
  \wordphrase{close cut（捷径）}
  \wordphrase{close friend（密友）}
  \wordexample{close call / thing / shave（侥幸脱险）}
  \wordexample{close election / contest / match / finish（势均力敌的选举 / 竞争 / 比赛 / 结局）}
  \wordexample{You are very close.（你已经很接近正确答案了。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{closed}{klauzd}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CLUD, CLUS[CLOS, CLOIS, CLAUS, CLOT]}
  \wordfamily{closed-door（秘密的，非公开的） ↔ open-door（公开的）；conclusive（决定性的）；preclude（阻止）}
  \wordsense{a. 关闭的，密闭的，密封的；非公开的；排他性的}
  \wordphrase{closed primary（仅由一个政党的成员参加的预选）}
  \wordexample{Closed today.（今日停业。）}
  \wordexample{closed primary（仅由一个政党的成员参加的预选）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{closing}{ˈkləuziŋ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CLUD, CLUS[CLOS, CLOIS, CLAUS, CLOT]}
  \wordfamily{closed；closely；close；closeness；conclusive（决定性的）}
  \wordsense{a. 结尾的，结束的；闭会的}
  \wordphrase{be closing}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of closing.（此处展示 closing 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 密封，封闭；终结，结束；结算}
  \wordphrase{closing date（截止时间）}
  \wordphrase{closing address（闭幕词）}
  \wordexample{closing date（截止时间）}
  \wordexample{closing address（闭幕词）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{closely}{ˈkləusli}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CLUD, CLUS[CLOS, CLOIS, CLAUS, CLOT]}
  \wordfamily{conclusive（决定性的）；preclude（阻止）；recluse（脱离世俗的人）}
  \wordsense{ad. 密集地，紧紧地；接近地；精密地；严密地}
  \wordphrase{closely contested election（势均力敌的选举）}
  \wordexample{closely contested election（势均力敌的选举）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{closeness}{ˈkləusnəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CLUD, CLUS[CLOS, CLOIS, CLAUS, CLOT]}
  \wordsense{n. 接近；密集；亲密}
  \wordfamily{closely}
  \wordfamily{closure}
  \wordfamily{closing}
  \wordfamily{closet}
  \wordphrase{the closeness of a resemblance（非常相像）}
  \wordexample{the closeness of a resemblance（非常相像）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{closure}{ˈkləuʒə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CLUD, CLUS[CLOS, CLOIS, CLAUS, CLOT]}
  \wordfamily{conclusive（决定性的）；preclude（阻止）；recluse（脱离世俗的人）}
  \wordsense{n. 结束；终止；封闭，关闭}
  \wordphrase{a closure（结束）}
  \wordexample{a closure（结束）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{closet}{ˈklɒzit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CLUD, CLUS[CLOS, CLOIS, CLAUS, CLOT]}
  \wordfamily{closure；cloture；closeness；cloister；conclusive（决定性的）}
  \wordsense{n. 壁橱；（旧用法）私室；书斋；厕所}
  \wordphrase{the closet}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of closet.（此处展示 closet 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{a. 私人的；空谈的；关起门来的；秘密的}
  \wordphrase{be closet}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of closet.（此处展示 closet 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. 把……引进私室密谈 *et (= small)}
  \wordphrase{closet thinker / strategist（纸上谈兵的空想家 / 战略家）}
  \wordphrase{closet theory（空谈理论）}
  \wordexample{closet thinker / strategist（纸上谈兵的空想家 / 战略家）}
  \wordexample{closet theory（空谈理论）}
  \wordexample{He was closeted with his advisor for two hours.（他和他的导师密谈了两小时。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{cloture}{ˈkləutʃə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CLUD, CLUS[CLOS, CLOIS, CLAUS, CLOT]}
  \wordfamily{closet；cloister；closure；clause；conclusive（决定性的）}
  \wordsense{n. 结束；截止}
  \wordphrase{a cloture（结束）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "a cloture".（常见搭配：结束。）}
  \wordsense{v. 结束辩论 *clot<clos (= shut)}
  \wordphrase{a cloture（结束）}
  \wordexample{a cloture（结束）}
  \wordexample{to cloture（结束辩论）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{cloister}{ˈklɔistə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CLUD, CLUS[CLOS, CLOIS, CLAUS, CLOT]}
  \wordfamily{cloture；clause；closet；claustrophobia；conclusive（决定性的）}
  \wordsense{n. 修道院；（修道院、大教堂等的后院围成四角形的）有房顶的回廊；（the \textasciitilde{}）隐居生活}
  \wordphrase{a cloister（修道院）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "a cloister".（常见搭配：修道院。）}
  \wordsense{v. 使居于修道院中 *clois<clos (= shut)}
  \wordphrase{live a cloistered life（过修道院的隐居生活）}
  \wordexample{live a cloistered life（过修道院的隐居生活）}
  \wordexample{a cloister（修道院）}
  \wordexample{to cloister（使居于修道院中）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{clause}{klɔ:z}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CLUD, CLUS[CLOS, CLOIS, CLAUS, CLOT]}
  \wordfamily{conclusive（决定性的）；preclude（阻止）；recluse（脱离世俗的人）}
  \wordsense{n. （合约、法律文书等的）条目，条款；【语】从句}
  \wordphrase{clause by clause（逐条地）}
  \wordexample{clause by clause（逐条地）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{claustrophobia}{ˌklɔ:strəˈfəubiə}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CLUD, CLUS[CLOS, CLOIS, CLAUS, CLOT]}
  \wordfamily{clause；mare（clausum）；cloister；cloture；conclusive（决定性的）}
  \wordsense{n. 幽闭恐怖（症） *phob (= fear)}
  \wordphrase{a claustrophobia（幽闭恐怖（症））}
  \wordexample{a claustrophobia（幽闭恐怖（症））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{mare clausum}{ˈma:rei ˈklɔ:səm}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CLUD, CLUS[CLOS, CLOIS, CLAUS, CLOT]}
  \wordfamily{claustrophobia；clause；cloister；cloture；conclusive（决定性的）}
  \wordsense{n. 领海（↔ mare liberum 公海） *mar (= sea)}
  \wordphrase{a mare clausum（领海（↔ mare liberum 公海））}
  \wordexample{a mare clausum（领海（↔ mare liberum 公海））}
\end{word}



\chapter{DOL}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根 DOL 来自拉丁语的 dol 意为 to grieve，同义词根为来自拉丁语的 mourn。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{condolence}{kən'dəuləns}
  \wordbreakdown{带走伤心的话 con<com (= with) + dol (= grieve)}
  \wordsense{n.（pl.）吊唁，慰问}
  \wordfamily{condole 吊唁，慰问}
  \wordfamily{condolatory 吊唁的，哀悼的}
  \wordphrase{condolence to the widow}
  \wordexample{We sent flowers and a note of condolence to the widow.（我们给那位遗孀寄去了花和吊唁信。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{indolent}{'indələnt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 DOL}
  \wordfamily{indolence（倦怠，懒惰）；condolence（（pl.）吊唁）；dole（（\textasciitilde{} out）发）}
  \wordsense{a. 倦怠的，懒惰的；无行动的 *in (= not)}
  \wordphrase{be indolent（倦怠的，懒惰的）}
  \wordexample{be indolent（倦怠的，懒惰的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{dole}{dəul}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 DOL}
  \wordfamily{indolent；doleful；dolor；dolorous；condolence（（pl.）吊唁）}
  \wordsense{v. （\textasciitilde{} out）发放赈济物}
  \wordphrase{dole the issue}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of dole.（此处展示 dole 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 施舍品，义捐品；施舍；（the \textasciitilde{}）（英）失业救济金；（古诗）悲哀，悲叹}
  \wordphrase{The small meal was}
  \wordexample{The small meal was doled out to the hungry crew.（饥饿的人们分到了少量的食物。）}
  \wordexample{The small meal was}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{doleful}{'dəulful}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 DOL}
  \wordsense{a. 沉浸于悲痛中的}
  \wordfamily{dole}
  \wordfamily{dolor}
  \wordfamily{indolent}
  \wordfamily{dolorous}
  \wordfamily{be doleful（沉浸于悲痛中的）}
  \wordphrase{be doleful（沉浸于悲痛中的）}
  \wordexample{be doleful（沉浸于悲痛中的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{dolor}{'dɔlə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 DOL}
  \wordsense{n.《诗》伤心，悲哀}
  \wordfamily{doleful}
  \wordfamily{dolorous}
  \wordfamily{dole}
  \wordfamily{indolent}
  \wordfamily{a dolor（《诗》伤心，悲哀）}
  \wordphrase{a dolor（《诗》伤心，悲哀）}
  \wordexample{a dolor（《诗》伤心，悲哀）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{dolorous}{'dɔlərəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 DOL}
  \wordfamily{dolor；doleful；dole；indolent；condolence（（pl.）吊唁）}

\end{word}



\chapter{FLAGR, FLAM}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根 FLAGR, FLAM 来自拉丁语的 flagr, flam 意为 to burn。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 arid, caust/caut, torr，来自希腊语的 ether，以及来自盎格鲁-撒克逊语的 burn/bran。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{conflagration}{ˌkɔnflə'greiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{彻底地燃烧 con<com (= thoroughly, intensive) + flagr (= burn)}
  \wordsense{n. 火灾}
  \wordfamily{inflammable}
  \wordfamily{flagrant}
  \wordfamily{flambeau}
  \wordfamily{flamboyant}
  \wordphrase{conflagration destroyed thousands}
  \wordexample{A devastating conflagration destroyed thousands of acres of forest.（一场毁灭性的火灾烧毁了数千英亩森林。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{inflammable}{in'flæməbl}
  \wordbreakdown{在某物上面燃烧的 in (= on) + flam (= burn)}
  \wordsense{a. 易燃的；易激动的，易怒的}
  \wordfamily{inflame 点火；激怒；使发炎}
  \wordfamily{inflammation 发炎；点火}
  \wordfamily{inflammatory 激动的；发炎的}
  \wordphrase{Inflammable children's clothes have}
  \wordexample{Inflammable children's clothes have been outlawed for sale in America.（在美国，出售易燃性儿童服装是违法的。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{flagrant}{'fleigrənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FLAGR, FLAM}
  \wordfamily{conflagration（火灾）；inflammable（易燃的）；flambeau（火把）}
  \wordsense{a. （罪行、罪犯等）穷凶极恶的，明目张胆的；凶恶的，恶名昭著的}
  \wordphrase{flagrant offenses / sinners（凶恶的罪行／罪犯）}
  \wordexample{flagrant offenses / sinners（凶恶的罪行／罪犯）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{flambeau}{'flæmbəu}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FLAGR, FLAM}
  \wordfamily{flagrant；flamboyant；flamingo；flame；conflagration（火灾）}
  \wordsense{n. 火把，装饰用的大烛台 *beau (= pretty)}
  \wordphrase{a flambeau（火把，装饰用的大烛台）}
  \wordexample{a flambeau（火把，装饰用的大烛台）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{flamboyant}{flæm'bɔiənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FLAGR, FLAM}
  \wordfamily{flamboyance（绚烂，艳丽）；conflagration（火灾）；inflammable（易燃的）}
  \wordsense{a. 绚烂的，华丽的，艳丽的；炫耀的 *boy (= float)}
  \wordphrase{be flamboyant（绚烂的，华丽的，艳丽的）}
  \wordexample{be flamboyant（绚烂的，华丽的，艳丽的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{flamingo}{flə'miŋgəu}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FLAGR, FLAM}
  \wordsense{n. 火烈鸟，红鹤}
  \wordfamily{flamboyant}
  \wordfamily{flame}
  \wordfamily{flambeau}
  \wordfamily{flaming}
  \wordfamily{a flamingo（火烈鸟，红鹤）}
  \wordphrase{a flamingo（火烈鸟，红鹤）}
  \wordexample{a flamingo（火烈鸟，红鹤）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{flame}{fleim}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FLAGR, FLAM}
  \wordfamily{conflagration（火灾）；inflammable（易燃的）；flagrant（（罪行、罪犯等）穷凶极恶的）}
  \wordsense{n. （pl.）火舌，火焰；耀眼的光辉，光芒；热情；《俚》情人}
  \wordphrase{in flames（燃烧着）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "in flames".（常见搭配：燃烧着。）}
  \wordsense{v. 燃烧；勃然大怒；使成火红色}
  \wordphrase{flame projector / thrower（火焰喷射器）}
  \wordphrase{the flames of sunset（落日的余晖）}
  \wordexample{flame of anger / indignation / enthusiasm（怒火／愤怒／如火的热情）}
  \wordexample{hillsides flaming with the colors of fall（被秋色染红的山坡）}
  \wordexample{His anger flamed out.（他怒火冲天。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{flaming}{'fleimiŋ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FLAGR, FLAM}
  \wordfamily{pi（以及来自希腊语的）；conflagration（火灾）；inflammable（易燃的）}
  \wordsense{a. 着火的，燃烧的；灼热的；极端的，惊人的}
  \wordphrase{flaming sun（灼人的太阳）}
  \wordexample{You flaming idiot!（你这个大笨蛋！）}
  \wordexample{flaming sun（灼人的太阳）}
\end{word}



\chapter{SECR, SACR, SANCT[SACER, SAINT]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根 SECR, SACR, SANCT[SACER, SAINT] 来自拉丁语的 secr, sacr, sanct 意为 holy，sacer，saint 是它们的变形。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 pi 以及来自希腊语的 hagi。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{consecrate}{'kɔnsikreit}
  \wordbreakdown{使和神圣在一起 con<com (= together) + secr (= holy)}
  \wordsense{vt. 使神圣；献给，奉献}
  \wordfamily{consecration 神圣化；奉献；（圣职）授任仪式}
  \wordphrase{consecrates matzo and wine}
  \wordexample{A priest consecrates matzo and wine prior to a communion ceremony.（牧师在圣餐仪式之前将无酵饼和葡萄酒作为祭品。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{desecrate}{'desikreit}
  \wordbreakdown{贬低神圣 de (= down) + secr (= holy)}
  \wordfamily{desecration（亵渎神圣）；consecrate（使神圣）；execrable（极坏的）}
  \wordsense{vt. 亵渎神圣，玷污神圣}
  \wordphrase{The Satanists desecrated the}
  \wordexample{The Satanists desecrated the church sanctuary with animal blood.（那些崇拜撒旦的人用动物的鲜血来玷污教会圣所的神圣。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{execrable}{'eksikrəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{脱离神圣的 ex (= down) + ecr<secr (= holy)}
  \wordsense{a. 极坏的；恶劣的}
  \wordfamily{execrate 诅咒；憎恶}
  \wordfamily{execration 诅咒；憎恶}
  \wordphrase{execrable that a man}
  \wordexample{How execrable, that a man guilty of murder should go free.（杀人犯居然逍遥法外，真是太可恶了。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{sanctimonious}{ˌsæŋkti'məuniəs}
  \wordbreakdown{如圣者般行事的 sanct (= holy)}
  \wordsense{a. 伪装虔诚的，假装神圣的}
  \wordfamily{execrable}
  \wordfamily{sacred}
  \wordfamily{desecrate}
  \wordfamily{sacrifice}
  \wordphrase{sanctimonious religious right would}
  \wordexample{The sanctimonious religious right would outlaw everything fun if they could.（伪装虔诚的宗教保守主义势力总是尽他们所能禁止一切娱乐活动。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{sacred}{'seikrid}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SECR, SACR, SANCT[SACER, SAINT]}
  \wordfamily{consecrate（使神圣）；desecrate（亵渎神圣）；execrable（极坏的）}
  \wordsense{a. 神圣的；神的；与宗教相关的；庄严的；不可侵犯的}
  \wordphrase{sacred cow（（印度的）圣牛；《谑》神圣不可侵犯的事物）}
  \wordphrase{sacred writings（宗教经典〔《古兰经》《圣经》等〕）}
  \wordphrase{sacred music（宗教音乐）}
  \wordexample{sacred cow（（印度的）圣牛；《谑》神圣不可侵犯的事物）}
  \wordexample{sacred writings（宗教经典〔《古兰经》《圣经》等〕）}
  \wordexample{sacred music（宗教音乐）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{sacrifice}{'sækrifais}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SECR, SACR, SANCT[SACER, SAINT]}
  \wordfamily{sacrificial（牺牲的）；consecrate（使神圣）；desecrate（亵渎神圣）}
  \wordsense{n. 牺牲；（供奉的）祭品；牺牲行为；牺牲品}
  \wordphrase{a sacrifice（牺牲）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "a sacrifice".（常见搭配：牺牲。）}
  \wordsense{v. （向神）献祭；牺牲 *fic (= make)}
  \wordphrase{a sacrifice（牺牲）}
  \wordexample{a sacrifice（牺牲）}
  \wordexample{to sacrifice（献祭）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{sacrilege}{'sækrəlidʒ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SECR, SACR, SANCT[SACER, SAINT]}
  \wordfamily{sacrilegious（亵渎神圣的）；consecrate（使神圣）；desecrate（亵渎神圣）}
  \wordsense{n. 亵渎神圣 *leg (= send)}
  \wordphrase{a sacrilege（亵渎神圣）}
  \wordexample{a sacrilege（亵渎神圣）}

  \wordfamily{sacristy}
  \wordfamily{sacrifice}
  \wordfamily{sacrosanct}
  \wordfamily{a sacristan（《古》（天主教）教堂看守人，圣器保管人）}
  \wordphrase{a sacristan（《古》（天主教）教堂看守人，圣器保管人）}
  \wordexample{a sacristan（《古》（天主教）教堂看守人，圣器保管人）}

\begin{word}[*]{sacristy}{'sækristi}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SECR, SACR, SANCT[SACER, SAINT]}
  \wordsense{n.（天主教教堂的）圣器收藏室}
  \wordfamily{sacristan}
  \wordfamily{sacrosanct}
  \wordfamily{sacrilege}
  \wordfamily{sacrament}
  \wordfamily{a sacristy（圣器收藏室）}
  \wordphrase{a sacristy（圣器收藏室）}
