\begin{word}[*]{sacrosanct}{'sækrəusæŋkt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SECR, SACR, SANCT[SACER, SAINT]}
  \wordfamily{sacristy；sacrament；sacristan；sacramental；consecrate（使神圣）}
  \wordsense{a. 神圣不容侵犯的 *sanct (= holy)}
  \wordphrase{sacrosanct}
  \wordexample{He regards his privileges as sacrosanct.（他认为自己的特权是不容侵犯的。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{sacrament}{'sækrəmənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SECR, SACR, SANCT[SACER, SAINT]}
  \wordsense{n. 圣事，圣礼（包括洗礼、坚信礼、圣体、告解、终傅、神品、婚配）}
  \wordfamily{sacrosanct}
  \wordfamily{sacramental}
  \wordfamily{sacristy}
  \wordfamily{sacerdotal}
  \wordphrase{the Blessed / Holy Sacrament（圣餐礼）}
  \wordexample{the Blessed / Holy Sacrament（圣餐礼）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{sacramental}{ˌsækrə'mentl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SECR, SACR, SANCT[SACER, SAINT]}
  \wordsense{a. 圣事的，圣餐（礼）的}
  \wordfamily{sacrament}
  \wordfamily{sacerdotal}
  \wordfamily{sacrosanct}
  \wordfamily{sanctify}
  \wordphrase{sacramental wine（圣餐用葡萄酒）}
  \wordexample{sacramental wine（圣餐用葡萄酒）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{sacerdotal}{ˌsæsə'dəutl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SECR, SACR, SANCT[SACER, SAINT]}
  \wordfamily{sacerdotalism（祭司制度）；consecrate（使神圣）；desecrate（亵渎神圣）}
  \wordsense{a. 圣职的，祭司的 *sacer<sacr (= holy), dot (= give)}
  \wordphrase{be sacerdotal（圣职的，祭司的）}
  \wordexample{be sacerdotal（圣职的，祭司的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{sanctify}{'sæŋktifai}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SECR, SACR, SANCT[SACER, SAINT]}
  \wordfamily{sanctification（神圣化）；consecrate（使神圣）；desecrate（亵渎神圣）}
  \wordsense{v. 使神圣 *ify (= make)}
  \wordphrase{to sanctify（使神圣）}
  \wordexample{to sanctify（使神圣）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{sanction}{'sæŋkʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SECR, SACR, SANCT[SACER, SAINT]}
  \wordfamily{sanctify；sanctity；sacerdotal；sanctuary；consecrate（使神圣）}
  \wordsense{n. 批准，认可，承认，同意；（一般指数个国家共同对违反国际法的国家所实施的）国际制裁}
  \wordphrase{the sanction}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of sanction.（此处展示 sanction 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. 批准，认可，承认，同意}
  \wordphrase{punitive / vindicatory sanction（刑罚／惩罚）}
  \wordexample{a book that was translated without the sanction of the author and publisher（未经作者和出版人同意而翻译的书）}
  \wordexample{apply economic / arms sanctions against an aggressor country（对侵略国实施经济／军事制裁）}
  \wordexample{punitive / vindicatory sanction（刑罚／惩罚）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{sanctity}{'sæŋktəti}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SECR, SACR, SANCT[SACER, SAINT]}
  \wordsense{n. 圣洁；神圣；（pl.）神圣的事物（如义务、感情等）}
  \wordfamily{sanction}
  \wordfamily{sanctuary}
  \wordfamily{sanctify}
  \wordfamily{sanctum}
  \wordphrase{violate the sanctity of an oath（亵渎誓言的神圣）}
  \wordphrase{the sanctities of the home（家庭的神圣义务）}
  \wordexample{violate the sanctity of an oath（亵渎誓言的神圣）}
  \wordexample{the sanctities of the home（家庭的神圣义务）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{sanctuary}{'sæŋktʃuəri}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SECR, SACR, SANCT[SACER, SAINT]}
  \wordfamily{consecrate（使神圣）；desecrate（亵渎神圣）；execrable（极坏的）}
  \wordsense{n. 圣所，教堂，寺庙；内殿；祭坛；避难所，庇护所；（鸟兽等的）禁猎区 *ary (= place where)}
  \wordphrase{seek / take sanctuary（寻找避难所／逃往圣所）}
  \wordphrase{sanctuary of political refugees（政治流亡人士的避难所）}
  \wordphrase{sanctuary privilege（庇护权）}
  \wordexample{seek / take sanctuary（寻找避难所／逃往圣所）}
  \wordexample{sanctuary of political refugees（政治流亡人士的避难所）}
  \wordexample{sanctuary privilege（庇护权）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{sanctum}{'sæŋktəm}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SECR, SACR, SANCT[SACER, SAINT]}
  \wordfamily{sanctuary；saint；sanctity；saintly；consecrate（使神圣）}
  \wordsense{n. 圣所；密室 *um (= place where)}
  \wordphrase{a sanctum（圣所）}
  \wordexample{a sanctum（圣所）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{saint}{seint}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SECR, SACR, SANCT[SACER, SAINT]}
  \wordsense{n. 圣人；（死后）进入天国的人；天使；道德崇高的人，似圣徒般的人；（S\textasciitilde{}）圣（在人名、教堂名、职位名等之前多写作 St.） *saint<sanct (= holy)}
  \wordfamily{sainted（神圣的；追封为圣徒的）}
  \wordfamily{sainthood（圣徒身份；［总称］诸圣）}
  \wordfamily{the (blessed)}
  \wordfamily{Saints（上天的各位圣人）}
  \wordfamily{St. Luke（圣人卢克）}
  \wordphrase{I'm no saint.（我不是圣人。）}
  \wordexample{I'm no saint.（我不是圣人。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{saintly}{'seintli}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SECR, SACR, SANCT[SACER, SAINT]}
  \wordfamily{saintliness（神圣，圣洁）；saintlike（神圣的，圣人般的）；consecrate（使神圣）}
  \wordsense{a. 神圣的；圣人般的 *saint<sanct (= holy), ly (= pertaining to)}
  \wordphrase{saintly expression on his face（他脸上圣人般的表情）}
  \wordexample{saintly expression on his face（他脸上圣人般的表情）}
\end{word}



\chapter{ROD, ROS}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根 ROD, ROS 来自拉丁语的 rod, ros 意为 to gnaw。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{corrosive}{kə'rəusiv}
  \wordbreakdown{用力抓挠，腐蚀 cor<com (= intensive) + ros (= gnaw)}
  \wordfamily{corrode（使腐蚀）；corrosion（腐蚀）；erode（腐蚀）}
  \wordsense{a. 腐蚀的；腐蚀性的}
  \wordphrase{be corrosive}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of corrosive.（此处展示 corrosive 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 腐蚀物，腐蚀剂}
  \wordphrase{corrosive and damages the}
  \wordexample{Salt air is somewhat corrosive and damages the paint on automobiles over time.（含有盐分的空气多少具有一些腐蚀性，时间久了会损坏汽车的喷漆。）}
  \wordexample{corrosive and damages the}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{erode}{i'rəud}
  \wordbreakdown{咬断 e<ex (= out, off) + rod (= gnaw)}
  \wordfamily{erosion（腐蚀，侵蚀）；corrosive（腐蚀的）；rodent（啮齿动物（如老鼠、兔子、松鼠、海狸等））}
  \wordsense{vt. 腐蚀，侵蚀}
  \wordphrase{erode and become very}
  \wordexample{Over centuries, sharp rocks erode and become very smooth.（经过几个世纪的侵蚀后，尖锐的石头变得非常平滑。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{rodent}{'rəudnt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ROD, ROS}
  \wordfamily{rodenticide；corrosive（腐蚀的）；erode（腐蚀）}
  \wordsense{n. 啮齿动物（如老鼠、兔子、松鼠、海狸等）}
  \wordphrase{a rodent（啮齿动物（如老鼠、兔子、松鼠、海狸等））}
  \wordexample{a rodent（啮齿动物（如老鼠、兔子、松鼠、海狸等））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{rodenticide}{rəu'dentisaid}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ROD, ROS}
  \wordfamily{rodent；corrosive（腐蚀的）；erode（腐蚀）}
  \wordsense{n. 老鼠药 *cid (= kill)}
  \wordphrase{a rodenticide（老鼠药）}
  \wordexample{a rodenticide（老鼠药）}
\end{word}



\chapter{ALESC[ESC, ALIT, OLESC]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根 ALESC[ESC, ALIT, OLESC] 来自拉丁语的 alesc 意为 becoming, growing。esc, alit, olesc 是它的变形。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{coalition}{ˌkəuə'liʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{变成一起 co<com (= together) + alit<alesc (= becoming)}
  \wordfamily{coalesce（联合）；adolescence（青春期 *ad (= to, toward),）；adolescent（青春期的）}
  \wordsense{n. 联合；（政党间的）联盟}
  \wordphrase{coalition of nations attacked}
  \wordexample{A coalition of nations attacked Iraq in 1991.（多国联军于 1991 年攻击了伊拉克。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{adolescence}{ˌædə'lesns}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ALESC[ESC, ALIT, OLESC]}
  \wordfamily{adolescent；florescence；fluorescent；phosphorescence；coalition（联合）}
  \wordsense{n. 青春期 *ad (= to, toward), olesc<alesc (= becoming)}
  \wordphrase{adolescence has fascinated people}
  \wordexample{The period of adolescence has fascinated people of all ages.（青春期阶段总是使各个年龄的人都着迷。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{adolescent}{ˌædə'lesnt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ALESC[ESC, ALIT, OLESC]}
  \wordfamily{adolescence；florescence；fluorescent；phosphorescence；coalition（联合）}
  \wordsense{a. 青春期的}
  \wordphrase{be adolescent}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of adolescent.（此处展示 adolescent 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 青少年 *ad (= to, toward), olesc<alesc (= becoming)}
  \wordphrase{adolescent as being "high-minded"}
  \wordexample{Aristotle characterized the adolescent as being "high-minded", but somewhat put this down to lack of experience.（亚里士多德认为青少年的特点是“高洁的”，但也认为这在某种程度上源于缺乏经验。）}
  \wordexample{adolescent as being "high-minded"}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{florescence}{flɔ:'resns}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ALESC[ESC, ALIT, OLESC]}
  \wordfamily{florescent（开花的，花盛开的）；coalition（联合）；adolescence（青春期 *ad (= to, toward),）}
  \wordsense{n. 开花，盛开；花期，全盛期 *flor (= flower), esc<alesc (= becoming)}
  \wordphrase{a florescence（开花，盛开）}
  \wordexample{a florescence（开花，盛开）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{fluorescent}{fluə'resnt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ALESC[ESC, ALIT, OLESC]}
  \wordfamily{fluorescence（荧光性）；coalition（联合）；adolescence（青春期 *ad (= to, toward),）}
  \wordsense{a. 发荧光的，荧光性的 *fluor (= bloom), esc<alesc (= becoming)}
  \wordphrase{fluorescent lamps / lighting（荧光灯 / 荧光照明）}
  \wordexample{fluorescent lamps / lighting（荧光灯 / 荧光照明）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{phosphorescence}{ˌfɔsfə'resns}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ALESC[ESC, ALIT, OLESC]}
  \wordfamily{phosphorescent（发磷光的，磷光性的）；phor（/ pher）；coalition（联合）}
  \wordsense{n. 发磷光；磷光 *phos (= light), phor (= carry, produce), esc<alesc (= becoming)}
  \wordphrase{to carry, to bear，与}
  \wordphrase{ger / gest，port，veh / vect 和来自希腊语的}
  \wordexample{to carry, to bear，与}
\end{word}



\chapter{LAT}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根 LAT 来自拉丁语的 lat 意为 to carry, to bear，与 fer 来源相同。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 ger / gest，port，veh / vect 和来自希腊语的 phor / pher。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{collate}{kə'leit}
  \wordbreakdown{一起搬运 col<com (= together) + lat (= carry)}
  \wordfamily{collation（对照）；dilate（使膨胀）；elated（喜气洋洋的）}
  \wordsense{vt. 对照}
  \wordphrase{collating the data sets}
  \wordexample{After collating the data sets, the scientist realized the experimental medicine was ineffective.（对照了数据之后，科学家得知那种实验药物不起作用。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{dilate}{dai'leit}
  \wordbreakdown{分别、远远地搬运 di<dis (= apart, away) + lat (= carry)}
  \wordfamily{dilation（膨胀，扩大）；collate（对照）；elated（喜气洋洋的）}
  \wordsense{vt. 使膨胀，使扩大}
  \wordphrase{dilate the issue}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of dilate.（此处展示 dilate 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{vi. 使扩大，膨胀；详述，细说}
  \wordphrase{The ophthalmologist dilated my}
  \wordexample{The ophthalmologist dilated my pupils for the eye exam.（为了检查视力，眼科医生为我做了散瞳。）}
  \wordexample{The ophthalmologist dilated my}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{elated}{i'leitid}
  \wordbreakdown{向外、向上搬运的 e<ex (= out, up) + lat (= carry)}
  \wordsense{a. 喜气洋洋的，兴高采烈的}
  \wordfamily{elation 喜气洋洋}
  \wordfamily{elate 使兴奋，使兴高采烈}
  \wordphrase{elated to learn I'd}
  \wordexample{I was elated to learn I'd been accepted at the university of my choice.（得知我被报考的大学录取之后，我十分高兴。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{latent}{'leitnt}
  \wordbreakdown{抱着的 lat (= carry, bear)}
  \wordfamily{latency（潜在，潜伏）；collate（对照）；dilate（使膨胀）}
  \wordsense{a. 隐蔽的，隐匿的；潜在的，潜伏的}
  \wordphrase{latent hostility}
  \wordexample{Freud said infants may harbor latent hostility for their mothers when they stop nursing.（弗罗伊德曾说过，幼儿断奶后会对他们的母亲怀有一种潜在的敌意。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{dilatory}{'diləteri}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LAT}
  \wordfamily{oblate；oblation；prelate；relate；collate（对照）}
  \wordsense{a. 缓慢的；拖延的 *di (= apart, away)}
  \wordphrase{dilatory campaigns}
  \wordexample{The filibuster is an effective tool in dilatory campaigns.（以冗长的演说〔在议会中〕阻碍议事是一种有效的拖延手段。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{oblate}{'ɔbleit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LAT}
  \wordfamily{dilatory；oblation；prelate；relate；collate（对照）}
  \wordsense{a. 扁圆的 *ob (= to, toward)}
  \wordphrase{oblate sphere}
  \wordexample{The earth is an oblate sphere.（地球是一个扁圆形的球体。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{oblation}{əu'bleiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LAT}
  \wordfamily{oblate；prelate；dilatory；relate；collate（对照）}
  \wordsense{n. 向神供奉；奉献物 *ob (= to, toward)}
  \wordphrase{an oblation（向神供奉）}
  \wordexample{an oblation（向神供奉）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{prelate}{'prelət}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LAT}
  \wordfamily{oblation；relate；oblate；relation；collate（对照）}
  \wordsense{n. 高级教士（如 archbishop, bishop, metropolitan, patriarch） *pre (= before)}
  \wordphrase{archbishop, bishop, metropolitan, patriarch））}
  \wordexample{archbishop, bishop, metropolitan, patriarch））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{relate}{ri'leit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LAT}
  \wordfamily{prelate；relation；oblation；relationship；collate（对照）}
  \wordsense{v. 叙述，讲；使联系；有关 *re (= again)}
  \wordphrase{I am not related}
  \wordexample{I am not related to him in any way.（我和他没有任何关系。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{relation}{ri'leiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LAT}
  \wordfamily{collate（对照）；dilate（使膨胀）；elated（喜气洋洋的）}
  \wordsense{n. 陈述，叙述；关系，关联；（pl.）（人、团体、国家等的）关系；联系；交往 *re (= again)}
  \wordphrase{public relations officer（公共关系人员〔缩写：PRO〕）}
  \wordexample{public relations officer（公共关系人员〔缩写：PRO〕）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{relationship}{ri'leiʃnʃip}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LAT}
  \wordfamily{relation；relative；relate；correlate；collate（对照）}
  \wordsense{n. 关系；血缘关系；感情关系 *re (= again)}
  \wordphrase{degree of relationship（亲等）}
  \wordexample{His affair with Susan has developed into a lasting relationship.（他和苏珊的恋情已经发展为持久的感情关系。）}
  \wordexample{degree of relationship（亲等）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{relative}{'relətiv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LAT}
  \wordfamily{relatively（比较地，相对地）；relativity（〔爱因斯坦的〕相对论）；collate（对照）}
  \wordsense{a. 比较的，相对的；（\textasciitilde{}to）和……有关系的}
  \wordphrase{be relative}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of relative.（此处展示 relative 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 【语】关系词；亲戚 *re (= again)}
  \wordphrase{in relative comfort（相对舒适地）}
  \wordphrase{papers relative to the case（和案件相关的文件）}
  \wordexample{in relative comfort（相对舒适地）}
  \wordexample{papers relative to the case（和案件相关的文件）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{correlate}{'kɔrəleit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LAT}
  \wordfamily{relative；correlation；relationship；correlative；collate（对照）}
  \wordsense{v. 关联；使相互关联 *cor<com (= together), re (= again)}
  \wordphrase{correlate psychology with political science（把心理学和政治学联系起来）}
  \wordexample{Her research correlates with his.（她的研究和他的有一定关联。）}
  \wordexample{correlate psychology with political science（把心理学和政治学联系起来）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{correlation}{ˌkɔrə'leiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LAT}
  \wordfamily{correlate；correlative；relative；interrelate；collate（对照）}
  \wordsense{n. 相互关系；相关性 *cor<com (= together), re (= again)}
  \wordphrase{correlation between climate and vegetation（气候和植被间的相互关系）}
  \wordexample{correlation between climate and vegetation（气候和植被间的相互关系）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{correlative}{kə'relətiv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LAT}
  \wordfamily{correlation；interrelate；correlate；interrelation；collate（对照）}
  \wordsense{a. 相互关联的，相关的}
  \wordphrase{be correlative}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of correlative.（此处展示 correlative 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 【语】关联词 *cor<com (= together), re (= again)}
  \wordphrase{correlative words（关联词）}
  \wordphrase{correlative conjunction（关联连词）}
  \wordexample{correlative words（关联词）}
  \wordexample{correlative conjunction（关联连词）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{interrelate}{ˌintəri'leit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LAT}
  \wordfamily{collate（对照）；dilate（使膨胀）；elated（喜气洋洋的）}
  \wordsense{v. （使）相互联系 *inter (= between), re (= again)}
  \wordphrase{to interrelate（相互联系）}
  \wordexample{to interrelate（相互联系）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{interrelation}{ˌintəri'leiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LAT}
  \wordfamily{interrelationship（相互关系）；collate（对照）；dilate（使膨胀）}
  \wordsense{n. 相互关系 *inter (= between), re (= again)}
  \wordphrase{an interrelation（相互关系）}
  \wordexample{an interrelation（相互关系）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{superlative}{su'pə:lətiv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LAT}
  \wordfamily{interrelation；translate；interrelate；translation；collate（对照）}
  \wordsense{a. 最高的，最好的，最佳的；【语】最高级的}
  \wordphrase{be superlative}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of superlative.（此处展示 superlative 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 最好的人（或物）；顶峰；【语】最高级 *super (= above)}
  \wordphrase{superlative degree（最高级）}
  \wordphrase{speak in superlatives（说话夸张）}
  \wordexample{superlative degree（最高级）}
  \wordexample{speak in superlatives（说话夸张）}
  \wordexample{The subject of all these superlatives was plain old Miller.（受到最高赞誉的人是平凡的老米勒。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{translate}{træns'leit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LAT}
  \wordfamily{translatable（可译的）；translator（翻译者）；collate（对照）}
  \wordsense{v. 翻译；解释；调动；（圣经中）肉体不死而升天 *trans (= change, through)}
  \wordphrase{to translate（翻译）}
  \wordexample{to translate（翻译）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{translation}{træns'leiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LAT}
  \wordfamily{translate；legislate；superlative；latitude；collate（对照）}
  \wordsense{n. 翻译；解释 *trans (= change, through)}
  \wordphrase{errors in translation（翻译错误）}
  \wordphrase{literal / close translation（直译）}
  \wordphrase{read Milton in translation（阅读米尔顿作品的译本）}
  \wordexample{errors in translation（翻译错误）}
  \wordexample{literal / close translation（直译）}
  \wordexample{read Milton in translation（阅读米尔顿作品的译本）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{legislate}{'ledʒisleit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LAT}
  \wordfamily{legislator（立法者，国会议员）；collate（对照）；dilate（使膨胀）}
  \wordsense{v. 制定法律 *leg (= law)}
  \wordphrase{legislate against gambling（制定禁赌法案）}
  \wordexample{legislate against gambling（制定禁赌法案）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{latitude}{'lætitju:d}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LAT}
  \wordfamily{latitudinal（纬度的）；collate（对照）；dilate（使膨胀）}
  \wordsense{n. 纬度（↔ longitude 经度）；（pl.）地区，地带；（活动、见解、思想等的）范围，自由；（胶片）曝光宽容度，曝光时限 *lat (= carry, wide)}
  \wordphrase{north / south latitude（北纬 / 南纬）}
  \wordphrase{high / low latitudes（高 / 低纬度地区）}
  \wordphrase{cold / warm latitude（寒带 / 温带地区）}
  \wordexample{north / south latitude（北纬 / 南纬）}
  \wordexample{high / low latitudes（高 / 低纬度地区）}
  \wordexample{cold / warm latitude（寒带 / 温带地区）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{latitudinarian}{ˌlætitju:di'neəriən}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LAT}
  \wordfamily{latitude；legislate；translation；translate；collate（对照）}
  \wordsense{a. /}
  \wordphrase{be latitudinarian}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of latitudinarian.（此处展示 latitudinarian 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. （特指在宗教信仰、教理方面）不拘泥于教条的（人），宽容的（人） *lat (= carry, wide)}
  \wordphrase{the latitudinarian}
  \wordexample{be latitudinarian（/ n.（特指在宗教信仰、教理方面）不拘泥于教条的（人），宽容的（人））}
\end{word}



\chapter{MISER}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根 MISER 来自拉丁语的 miser 意为 to pity, wretched。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{commiserate}{kə'mizəreit}
  \wordbreakdown{带有同情之情 com (= with, intensive) + miser (= pity)}
  \wordfamily{commiseration（怜悯，同情）；miser（守财奴）；miserable（可怜的）}
  \wordsense{vi. with + sb 怜悯；同情}
  \wordphrase{The patrolman commiserated with}
  \wordexample{The patrolman commiserated with the parents whose child died in the accident.（巡警很同情在事故中失去孩子的父母。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{miser}{'maizə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MISER}
  \wordsense{n. 守财奴，吝啬鬼}
  \wordfamily{miserly（守财奴似的，吝啬的）}
  \wordfamily{a miser（守财奴，吝啬鬼）}
  \wordphrase{a miser（守财奴，吝啬鬼）}
  \wordexample{a miser（守财奴，吝啬鬼）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{miserable}{'mizrəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MISER}
  \wordsense{a. 可怜的，悲惨的，不幸的；使人难受的；糟糕的}
  \wordfamily{miser}
  \wordfamily{miserably}
  \wordfamily{misery}
  \wordphrase{miserable slums（条件极差的贫民窟）}
  \wordphrase{What a miserable meal!（这饭菜太糟糕了！）}
  \wordexample{miserable slums（条件极差的贫民窟）}
  \wordexample{What a miserable meal!（这饭菜太糟糕了！）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{miserably}{'mizrəbli}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MISER}
  \wordfamily{commiserate（with + sb 怜悯）；miser（守财奴）；miserable（可怜的）}
  \wordsense{ad. 悲惨地}
  \wordphrase{miserably poor / underpaid（穷得可怜的 / 薪水少得可怜的）}
  \wordexample{miserably poor / underpaid（穷得可怜的 / 薪水少得可怜的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{misery}{'mizəri}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MISER}
  \wordfamily{commiserate（with + sb 怜悯）；miser（守财奴）；miserable（可怜的）}
  \wordsense{n. 悲惨；贫困；（精神、肉体上的）痛苦；《口》爱发牢骚的人}
  \wordphrase{living in misery and need（生活穷困潦倒）}
  \wordphrase{the miseries of mankind（人类的不幸）}
  \wordphrase{to cook, to mature。}
  \wordexample{Misery loves company.（同病相怜。）}
  \wordexample{living in misery and need（生活穷困潦倒）}
  \wordexample{the miseries of mankind（人类的不幸）}
\end{word}



\chapter{COC, COCT}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根 COC, COCT 来自拉丁语的 coc, coct 意为 to cook, to mature。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{concoct}{kən'kɔkt}
  \wordbreakdown{放在一起做饭、烹饪 con<com (= together) + coct (= cook)}
  \wordsense{vt. 调制；调和；编造；图谋}
  \wordfamily{concoction 混合（物），调和（物）；编造}
  \wordphrase{concoct hilarious excuses}
  \wordexample{Policemen say that speeders often concoct hilarious excuses for speeding.（警察说超速驾驶的人往往会编造出一些可笑的借口。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{precocious}{pri'kouʃəs}
  \wordbreakdown{早一点成熟的 pre (= before) + coc (= mature, cook)}
  \wordfamily{precocity（早熟）；concoct（调制）；decoct（煎）}
  \wordsense{a. 早熟的}
  \wordphrase{precocious tot She could}
  \wordexample{She's a precocious tot. She could already read at 3.（她是个早慧的孩子，三岁的时候就会识字读书了。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{decoct}{di'kɔkt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 COC, COCT}
  \wordfamily{decoction；concoct（调制）；precocious（早熟的）}
  \wordsense{v. 煎，熬 *de (= intensive, down)}
  \wordphrase{decoction}
  \wordexample{decoction（煎；煎制的药剂）}
\end{word}



\chapter{STERN[STREN]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根 STERN[STREN] 来自拉丁语的 stern 意为 rigid，它的变形为 stren。stern 也有 chest, breast 的意思，但此种用法不多见。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{consternation}{kənstə'neiʃən}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 STERN[STREN]}
  \wordfamily{stern；sternum；strenuous}
  \wordsense{n. 吃惊，惊愕；惊慌失措 [身体完全变硬 con<com (= intensive) + stern (= rigid)]}
  \wordphrase{the consternation}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of consternation.（此处展示 consternation 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 吃惊，惊愕；惊慌失措}
  \wordphrase{consternation at seeing three}
  \wordexample{Imagine my consternation at seeing three hoods with guns approaching me.（想像一下，当看到三个强盗持枪向我走来时我是多么惊慌失措。）}
  \wordexample{consternation at seeing three}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{stern}{stə:n}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 STERN[STREN]}
  \wordfamily{sternly（严格地）；sternness（严格）；consternation（吃惊）}
  \wordsense{a. 严格的；苛刻的，严厉的；坚定的}
  \wordphrase{be stern}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of stern.（此处展示 stern 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 船尾}
  \wordphrase{stern treatment / rebukes（严厉的对待／严厉的指责）}
  \wordphrase{stern taskmaster / face（严厉的工头／板着的面孔）}
  \wordphrase{stern looks（严厉的表情）}
  \wordexample{stern treatment / rebukes（严厉的对待／严厉的指责）}
  \wordexample{stern taskmaster / face（严厉的工头／板着的面孔）}
  \wordexample{stern looks（严厉的表情）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{sternum}{'stə:nəm}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 STERN[STREN]}
  \wordfamily{stern；strenuous；consternation}
  \wordsense{n. 胸骨 *stern (= breast, chest)}
  \wordphrase{a sternum（胸骨）}
  \wordexample{a sternum（胸骨）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{strenuous}{'strenjuəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 STERN[STREN]}
  \wordfamily{palp（和）；consternation（吃惊）；stern（严格的）}
  \wordsense{a. 奋发的；艰苦的 *stren<stern (= rigid)}
  \wordphrase{make strenuous efforts（竭尽全力）}
  \wordphrase{strenuous work（艰苦的工作）}
  \wordphrase{strenuous workers（勤奋的工人）}
  \wordexample{make strenuous efforts（竭尽全力）}
  \wordexample{strenuous work（艰苦的工作）}
  \wordexample{strenuous workers（勤奋的工人）}
\end{word}



\chapter{TANG, TACT, TING[TIG, TAG]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根 TANG, TACT, TING[TIG, TAG] 来自拉丁语的 tang, tact, ting 以及它们的变形 tig, tag，意为 to touch。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 palp 和 tegr。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{contiguous}{kən'tigjuəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TANG, TACT, TING[TIG, TAG]}
  \wordfamily{contiguity（接触）；disentangle（解开）；intangible（不能抚摸的）}
  \wordsense{a. 接触的；毗邻的；近处的 [和某种事物相接触的 con<com (= with) + tig<ting (= touch)]}
  \wordphrase{be contiguous}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of contiguous.（此处展示 contiguous 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{a. 接触的；毗邻的；近处的}
  \wordphrase{contiguous United States}
  \wordexample{TV weather broadcasters usually only offer forecasts for the 48 contiguous United States.（电视天气预报通常只预报美国本土的 48 个州的天气情况。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{disentangle}{ˌdisin'tæŋgl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TANG, TACT, TING[TIG, TAG]}
  \wordfamily{disentanglement（解开）；contiguous（接触的）；intangible（不能抚摸的）}
  \wordsense{vt. 解开；解决纠纷}
  \wordphrase{to disentangle（解开）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "to disentangle".（常见搭配：解开。）}
  \wordsense{vi. 解开；松开 [对“使被纠缠”的轻微否定 dis (= reverse) + en (= make) + tang (= touch)]}
  \wordphrase{to disentangle（解开）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "to disentangle".（常见搭配：解开。）}
  \wordsense{vi. 解开；松开}
  \wordphrase{disentangle the 4 witnesses'}
  \wordexample{The detective managed to disentangle the 4 witnesses' conflicting stories.（那位侦探从四位证人相互矛盾的叙述中了解了事实真相。）}
  \wordexample{disentangle the 4 witnesses'}
  \wordexample{to disentangle（解开）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{intangible}{in'tændʒəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TANG, TACT, TING[TIG, TAG]}
  \wordfamily{intangibility（不可触知性）；contiguous（接触的）；disentangle（解开）}
  \wordsense{a. 不能抚摸的；无形的；难以掌握的 [无法接触或抚摸的 in (= not) + tang (= touch)]}
  \wordphrase{be intangible}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of intangible.（此处展示 intangible 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{a. 不能抚摸的；无形的；难以掌握的}
  \wordphrase{intangible}
  \wordexample{Some of the most important gifts we receive from parents are intangible.（我们从父母那里得到的一些最重要的礼物是无形的财富。）}
  \wordexample{intangible}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{tangible}{'tændʒəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TANG, TACT, TING[TIG, TAG]}
  \wordfamily{tangibility（可触知）；tangibly（可触知地）；contiguous（接触的）}
  \wordsense{a. 通过触摸可以感知的；有形的；实际的；确凿的 [可触摸的 tang (= touch)]}
  \wordphrase{be tangible}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of tangible.（此处展示 tangible 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{a. 通过触摸可以感知的；有形的；实际的；确凿的}
  \wordphrase{tangible evidence}
  \wordexample{We can only go to court with tangible evidence.（我们只有掌握了确凿的证据才可以起诉。）}
  \wordexample{tangible evidence}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{contingent}{kən'tindʒənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TANG, TACT, TING[TIG, TAG]}
  \wordfamily{tangible；contingency；intangible；contagion；contiguous（接触的）}
  \wordsense{a. 可能（但不确定）的，偶然的；（\textasciitilde{} to）附带的；（\textasciitilde{} upon）以……为条件的}
  \wordphrase{be contingent}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of contingent.（此处展示 contingent 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 分遣队；代表团 *con<com (= with), ting (= touch)}
  \wordphrase{contingent advantage（可能的优势）}
  \wordphrase{fee contingent on success（成功后才能得到的报酬）}
  \wordexample{contingent advantage（可能的优势）}
  \wordexample{Such risks are contingent to the trade.（贸易中伴随着这样的风险。）}
  \wordexample{fee contingent on success（成功后才能得到的报酬）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{contingency}{kən'tindʒənsi}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TANG, TACT, TING[TIG, TAG]}
  \wordfamily{contingent；contagion；tangible；contagious；contiguous（接触的）}
  \wordsense{n. （事件的）偶然性，可能性；偶然事件；（紧随某一事件之后的）附带事件 *con<com (= with), ting (= touch)}
  \wordphrase{prepared for all contingencies（准备应付所有偶然事件）}
  \wordphrase{result that depends upon contingencies（事件偶然性所带来的结果）}
  \wordphrase{contingency arrangements / plans（应对偶然事件的安排／计划）}
  \wordexample{prepared for all contingencies（准备应付所有偶然事件）}
  \wordexample{result that depends upon contingencies（事件偶然性所带来的结果）}
  \wordexample{contingency arrangements / plans（应对偶然事件的安排／计划）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{contagion}{kən'teidʒən}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TANG, TACT, TING[TIG, TAG]}
  \wordfamily{contingency；contagious；contingent；entangle；contiguous（接触的）}
  \wordsense{n. （由接触引发的疾病）感染，传染；传染病；（思想、情绪、传闻等的）感染 *con<com (= with), tag<ting (= touch)}
  \wordphrase{contagion of fear swept}
  \wordexample{A contagion of fear swept through the crowd.（恐惧在人群中蔓延开来。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{contagious}{kən'teidʒəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TANG, TACT, TING[TIG, TAG]}
  \wordfamily{contagiously（传染地）；contiguous（接触的）；disentangle（解开）}
  \wordsense{a. （疾病）接触传染性的；（人）携带传染病原的；易传染的，易感染的 *con<com (= with), tag<ting (= touch)}
  \wordphrase{contagious laughter / enthusiasm（感染旁人的笑容／热情）}
  \wordexample{Yawning is contagious.（打哈欠是会传染的。）}
  \wordexample{contagious laughter / enthusiasm（感染旁人的笑容／热情）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{entangle}{in'tæŋgl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TANG, TACT, TING[TIG, TAG]}
  \wordfamily{contagious；entanglement；contagion；tangle；contiguous（接触的）}
  \wordsense{v. 缠住；使卷入；连累 *en (= make)}
  \wordphrase{My fishing line got}
  \wordexample{My fishing line got entangled in weeds.（我的钓鱼线被杂草缠住了。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{entanglement}{in'tæŋglmənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TANG, TACT, TING[TIG, TAG]}
  \wordfamily{contiguous（接触的）；disentangle（解开）；intangible（不能抚摸的）}
  \wordsense{n. 纠缠；（pl.）（阻止敌人前进的）铁丝网 *en (= make)}
  \wordphrase{entanglements with rogues（和无赖纠缠）}
  \wordexample{entanglements with rogues（和无赖纠缠）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{tangle}{tæŋgl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TANG, TACT, TING[TIG, TAG]}
  \wordfamily{contiguous（接触的）；disentangle（解开）；intangible（不能抚摸的）}
  \wordsense{n. （线、毛等）纠结，纠缠，混乱状态}
  \wordphrase{tangled hair（乱糟糟的头发）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "tangled hair".（常见搭配：乱糟糟的头发。）}
  \wordsense{v. 使缠结，纠缠；使混乱；（with）和某人争论，吵架}
  \wordphrase{tangled hair（乱糟糟的头发）}
  \wordexample{make a tangle of my ball of wool（使我的毛线团缠在一起）}
  \wordexample{a tangle（纠结，纠缠，混乱状态）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{tangent}{'tændʒənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TANG, TACT, TING[TIG, TAG]}
  \wordsense{n.【数】切线，正切}
  \wordfamily{tangle}
  \wordfamily{tact}
  \wordfamily{entanglement}
  \wordfamily{tactful}
  \wordphrase{go / fly off at a tangent（突然离开原来的思路；突然离题）}
  \wordexample{go / fly off at a tangent（突然离开原来的思路；突然离题）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{tact}{tækt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TANG, TACT, TING[TIG, TAG]}
  \wordfamily{contiguous（接触的）；disentangle（解开）；intangible（不能抚摸的）}
  \wordsense{n. 机智，机敏；老练}
  \wordphrase{the tact}
  \wordexample{have great tact in dealing with people（在和人打交道方面很有一套）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{tactful}{'tæktful}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TANG, TACT, TING[TIG, TAG]}
  \wordsense{a. 有才智的，机智的，机敏的；老练的}
  \wordfamily{tactfully（机智地）}
  \wordfamily{be tactful（有才智的，机智的，机敏的）}
  \wordphrase{be tactful（有才智的，机智的，机敏的）}
  \wordexample{be tactful（有才智的，机智的，机敏的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{tactics}{'tæktiks}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TANG, TACT, TING[TIG, TAG]}
  \wordfamily{contiguous（接触的）；disentangle（解开）；intangible（不能抚摸的）}
  \wordsense{n. （sing.）战术，兵法；（pl.）策略，手法}
  \wordphrase{grand / minor tactics（高级／初级战术）}
  \wordphrase{win by surprise tactics（出奇制胜）}
  \wordexample{grand / minor tactics（高级／初级战术）}
  \wordexample{win by surprise tactics（出奇制胜）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{tactical}{'tæktikəl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TANG, TACT, TING[TIG, TAG]}
  \wordsense{a. 战术的；作战的；善于用策略的}
  \wordfamily{tactically（战术性地）}
  \wordfamily{tactician（战术家，兵法家；谋士）}
  \wordphrase{the Tactical Air Command（战略空军司令部〔缩写：TAC〕）}
  \wordphrase{tactical unit（作战部队）}
  \wordphrase{tactical nuclear force / command ship（战术核力量／战术指挥舰）}
  \wordphrase{tactical target / weapon（战略目标／战略武器）}
  \wordphrase{tactical error（战略错误）}
  \wordexample{the Tactical Air Command（战略空军司令部〔缩写：TAC〕）}
  \wordexample{tactical unit（作战部队）}
  \wordexample{tactical nuclear force / command ship（战术核力量／战术指挥舰）}
  \wordexample{tactical target / weapon（战略目标／战略武器）}
  \wordexample{tactical error（战略错误）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{tactile}{'tæk.tail}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TANG, TACT, TING[TIG, TAG]}
  \wordsense{a. 触觉的；可触知的}
  \wordfamily{tactical}
  \wordfamily{contact}
  \wordfamily{tactics}
  \wordfamily{intact}
  \wordphrase{tactile organ / reflex（触觉器官／反射）}
  \wordexample{tactile organ / reflex（触觉器官／反射）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{contact}{'kɒntækt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TANG, TACT, TING[TIG, TAG]}
  \wordfamily{tactile；intact；tactical；tactics；contiguous（接触的）}
  \wordsense{n. 接触，联系，联络；熟人}
  \wordphrase{a contact（接触，联系，联络）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "a contact".（常见搭配：接触，联系，联络。）}
  \wordsense{v. （和某人）接触，联系 *con<com (= with)}
  \wordphrase{a contact（接触，联系，联络）}
  \wordexample{a contact（接触，联系，联络）}
  \wordexample{to contact（接触，联系）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{intact}{in'tækt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TANG, TACT, TING[TIG, TAG]}
  \wordfamily{contiguous（接触的）；disentangle（解开）；intangible（不能抚摸的）}
  \wordsense{a. 原封不动的，未受损伤的，完整无缺的 *in (= not)}
  \wordphrase{be intact}
  \wordexample{He lived on the interest and kept his capital intact.（他不动本金，只靠利息维持生计。）}
\end{word}



\chapter{ROB}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根 ROB 来自拉丁语的 rob 意为 strong, strength。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 fort / forc, firm, bil 等，以及来自拉丁语的意为 strong, worth 的 val。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{corroborate}{kə'rɒbəreit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ROB}
  \wordfamily{corroboration（确证）；corroborative（确证的）；corroborant（滋补的）}
  \wordsense{vt. 确证 [使非常有力 cor<com (= intensive) + robor (= strength)]}
  \wordphrase{corroborate the issue}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of corroborate.（此处展示 corroborate 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 确证}
  \wordphrase{The bartender corroborated the}
  \wordexample{The bartender corroborated the suspect's alibi for his whereabouts on the night of the murder.（酒吧男招待证实了谋杀发生当晚嫌疑人不在犯罪现场。）}
  \wordexample{The bartender corroborated the}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{robot}{'rəubɒt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ROB}
  \wordsense{n. 机器人；机械呆板的人}
  \wordfamily{corroborate}
  \wordfamily{robust}
  \wordfamily{a robot（机器人）}
  \wordphrase{a robot（机器人）}
  \wordexample{a robot（机器人）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{robust}{rəu'bʌst}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ROB}
  \wordfamily{corroborate（确证 [使非常有力 cor<com (= int）；robot（机器人）；intact（原封不动的）}
  \wordsense{a. 强健的；健全的；结实的}
  \wordphrase{robust young man / appetite（健壮的年轻人／旺盛的食欲）}
  \wordexample{robust young man / appetite（健壮的年轻人／旺盛的食欲）}
\end{word}



\chapter{PLAC[PLAIS]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根 PLAC[PLAIS] 来自拉丁语的 plac 意为 to please, to soothe, peace，它的变形为 plais。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{complaisant}{kəm'pleizənt}
  \wordbreakdown{使完全快乐的 com (= intensive) + plais<plac (= please)}
  \wordfamily{complaisance（彬彬有礼）；implacable（不能安抚的）；placate（安抚）}
  \wordsense{a. 讨好的；彬彬有礼的；顺从的}
  \wordphrase{complaisant service}
  \wordexample{The very rich often receive exceptionally complaisant service from their factotums.（勤杂工对富人的服务往往格外殷勤。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{implacable}{im'plækəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{无法安抚的 im<in (= not) + plac (= soothe, peace, please)}
  \wordfamily{complaisant；placate；complacent；complacence}
  \wordsense{a. 不能安抚的，难以平息的；毫不宽容的}
  \wordphrase{implacable jealousy}
  \wordexample{She could never quiet the implacable jealousy of her husband.（她无法平息丈夫的妒忌之心。）}
  \wordexample{implacable jealousy}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{placate}{plə'keit}
  \wordbreakdown{使变得和平 plac (= peace)}
  \wordsense{vt. 安抚，平息}
  \wordfamily{implacable}
  \wordfamily{complacent}
  \wordfamily{complaisant}
  \wordfamily{complacence}
  \wordphrase{placate an enraged customer}
  \wordexample{It's not easy to placate an enraged customer.（平息一位顾客的怒火并不是件容易的事。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{complacent}{kəm'pleisnt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PLAC[PLAIS]}
  \wordfamily{complacence；placid；placebo；complaisant（讨好的）}
  \wordsense{a. 自我满足的 *com (= intensive, completely)}
  \wordphrase{be complacent}
  \wordexample{with a complacent smile / air（面带着满意的微笑／满意的态度）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{complacence}{kəm'pleisns}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PLAC[PLAIS]}
  \wordfamily{complaisant（讨好的）；implacable（不能安抚的）；placate（安抚）}
  \wordsense{n. 自我满足，沾沾自喜 *com (= intensive, completely)}
  \wordphrase{a complacence（自我满足，沾沾自喜）}
  \wordexample{a complacence（自我满足，沾沾自喜）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{placid}{'plæsid}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PLAC[PLAIS]}
  \wordfamily{complaisant（讨好的）；implacable（不能安抚的）；placate（安抚）}
  \wordsense{a. 宁静的；温和的；沉着的 *id (= having the quality of)}
  \wordphrase{placid lake（宁静的湖水）}
  \wordexample{placid lake（宁静的湖水）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{placebo}{plə'si:bəu}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PLAC[PLAIS]}
  \wordfamily{placid；complacence；complacent；complaisant（讨好的）}
  \wordsense{n. 安慰剂（不含药性，仅为加强病人康复的希望）}
  \wordphrase{a placebo（安慰剂（不含药性，仅为加强病人康复的希望））}
  \wordexample{a placebo（安慰剂（不含药性，仅为加强病人康复的希望））}
\end{word}



\chapter{DIGN[DAIN, DEIGN]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根 DIGN[DAIN, DEIGN] 来自拉丁语的 dign 意为 worthy，它的变形为 dain, deign。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{condign}{kən'dain}
  \wordbreakdown{足以受罚的 con<com (= intensive) + dign (= worthy)}
  \wordsense{a.（尤指惩罚）应得的；适当的}
  \wordfamily{disdain}
  \wordfamily{indignant}
  \wordfamily{indignation}
  \wordfamily{indignity}
  \wordphrase{condign}
  \wordexample{For many conservatives, capital punishment for capital crimes is merely condign.（对许多保守主义者来说，犯死罪的人被判死刑不过是罪有应得。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{disdain}{dis'dein}
  \wordbreakdown{不是有价值的行为 dis (= not) + dain<dign (= worthy)}
  \wordfamily{disdainful（轻蔑的）；disdainfully（轻蔑地）；disdains（riding）；condign（（尤指惩罚）应得的）}
  \wordsense{vt. to do / \textasciitilde{}ing 蔑视；不屑}
  \wordphrase{disdain the issue}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of disdain.（此处展示 disdain 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 轻蔑，蔑视}
  \wordphrase{disdains riding}
  \wordexample{The CEO disdains riding in coach and insists on first class.（那位首席执行官不屑于坐经济舱，坚持要坐头等舱。）}
  \wordexample{disdains riding}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{indignant}{in'dignənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 DIGN[DAIN, DEIGN]}
  \wordfamily{indignation；indignity；dignify；dignity；condign（（尤指惩罚）应得的）}
  \wordsense{a. 愤慨的，愤怒的 *in (= not)}
  \wordphrase{indignant at a false accusation（对不当的指责感到愤慨的）}
  \wordphrase{indignant with a cruel man（对残暴的人感到义愤填膺的）}
  \wordexample{indignant at a false accusation（对不当的指责感到愤慨的）}
  \wordexample{indignant with a cruel man（对残暴的人感到义愤填膺的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{indignation}{ˌindiɡ'neiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 DIGN[DAIN, DEIGN]}
  \wordfamily{indignant；indignity；dignify；dignity；condign（（尤指惩罚）应得的）}
  \wordsense{n. 愤慨，愤怒，义愤 *in (= not)}
  \wordphrase{arouse the indignation of people（引起人们的愤慨）}
  \wordphrase{feel strong indignation against congressmen（对国会议员感到强烈的愤慨）}
  \wordexample{arouse the indignation of people（引起人们的愤慨）}
  \wordexample{feel strong indignation against congressmen（对国会议员感到强烈的愤慨）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{indignity}{in'dignəti}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 DIGN[DAIN, DEIGN]}
  \wordfamily{indignation；dignify；indignant；dignity；indignities；condign（（尤指惩罚）应得的）}
  \wordsense{n. 侮辱，轻蔑；无礼；（pl.）侮辱性的言行 *in (= not)}
  \wordphrase{indignities}
  \wordexample{The hijackers subjected us to all sorts of indignities.（劫机犯们对我们进行了各种侮辱。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{dignify}{'dignifai}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 DIGN[DAIN, DEIGN]}
  \wordfamily{condign（（尤指惩罚）应得的）；disdain（to do / \textasciitilde{）；indignant（愤慨的）}
  \wordsense{v. 使有尊严；抬高……的身价 *ify (= make)}
  \wordphrase{dignified old lady（高贵的老妇人）}
  \wordexample{dignified old lady（高贵的老妇人）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{dignity}{'dignəti}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 DIGN[DAIN, DEIGN]}
  \wordfamily{dignitary（显贵，要人）；condign（（尤指惩罚）应得的）；disdain（to do / \textasciitilde{）}
  \wordsense{n. 尊严；尊贵，高贵；高位}
  \wordphrase{stand upon one's dignity（保持尊严）}
  \wordexample{stand upon one's dignity（保持尊严）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{dainty}{'deinti}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 DIGN[DAIN, DEIGN]}
  \wordfamily{daintily（优雅地，雅致地）；condign（（尤指惩罚）应得的）；disdain（to do / \textasciitilde{）}
  \wordsense{a. （人）秀美的，优美的，优雅的；（东西）精美的，精致的；易损坏的；（对衣食）过分讲究的；（食物）美味的}
  \wordphrase{be dainty（秀美的，优美的，优雅的）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "be dainty".（常见搭配：秀美的，优美的，优雅的。）}
  \wordsense{n. （pl.）美味的食物，珍馐佳肴 *dain<dign (= worthy)}
  \wordphrase{daintily dressed young lady（衣着高雅的年轻女士）}
  \wordphrase{dainty little lady（秀美的少女）}
  \wordexample{daintily dressed young lady（衣着高雅的年轻女士）}
  \wordexample{dainty little lady（秀美的少女）}
  \wordexample{be dainty（秀美的，优美的，优雅的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{deign}{dein}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 DIGN[DAIN, DEIGN]}
  \wordfamily{marc（/ mark，来自希腊语的）；beck（/ beac）；condign（（尤指惩罚）应得的）}
  \wordsense{v. （\textasciitilde{}to do）降低身份；屈尊俯就；施恩惠于 *deign<dign (= worthy)}
  \wordphrase{deign to listen}
  \wordexample{He would not deign to listen to you.（他是不会放下架子听你的话的。）}
\end{word}



\chapter{SIGN}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 sign 意为 mark, sign。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 marc / mark，来自希腊语的 sem，以及来自盎格鲁-撒克逊语的 beck / beac。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{consign}{kən'sain}
  \wordbreakdown{和标志一起发送 con<com (= with, together) + sign (= sign, mark)}
  \wordsense{vt. sb / sth + to + sb / sth 托运，寄送；托付，委托}
  \wordfamily{consignment 委托；托运}
  \wordfamily{consigner / consignor 委托人；发货人}
  \wordfamily{consignee 受托人；收货人}
  \wordphrase{consigned their comrade}
  \wordexample{Dropping the shrouded body into the sea, the sailors consigned their comrade to the deep.（船员们将身穿寿衣的尸体扔到海中，将这名同伴托付给了大海。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{designate}{ˈdezɪgneit}
  \wordbreakdown{表示从某物掉出去 de (= from) + sign (= mark)}
  \wordfamily{designation（指出）；consign（sb / sth + to + sb / sth）；signify（表明）}
  \wordsense{vt. 标明，指出；命名；指派，任命}
  \wordphrase{designate the issue}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of designate.（此处展示 designate 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{a. 指派的}
  \wordphrase{designate campgrounds}
  \wordexample{Green triangles on the map designate campgrounds.（地图上标出的绿色三角形表示野营地。）}
  \wordexample{designate campgrounds}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{signify}{ˈsignifai}
  \wordbreakdown{使表示出 sign (= mark, sign)}
  \wordsense{vt. 表明，表示；意味着；有重要性}
  \wordfamily{signification 意味，意义；表示}
  \wordfamily{signifies yes}
  \wordphrase{signifies yes}
  \wordexample{Moving the head up and down signifies yes.（点头表示赞同。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{assign}{ə'sain}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SIGN；*as<ad (= to)}
  \wordfamily{consign（sb / sth + to + sb / sth）；designate（标明）；signify（表明）}
  \wordsense{v. 分配，分派，指派（任务等）；指定（时间、地点）；把……归于；任命，委任；转让（财产、权力等），让渡}
  \wordphrase{assigned to us}
  \wordexample{Those rooms have been assigned to us.（那些房间分配给我们了。）}
  \wordexample{assigned to us}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{assignment}{ə'sainmənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SIGN；*as<ad (= to)}
  \wordfamily{assign；cosigner；countersign；ensign；consign（sb / sth + to + sb / sth）}
  \wordsense{n. 分配；指派；（分配的）任务；作业}
  \wordphrase{assignment（*as<ad (= to)）}
  \wordexample{assignment（*as<ad (= to)）}
  \wordexample{an assignment（分配）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{cosigner}{ˌkəu'sainə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SIGN；*co<com (= together)}
  \wordfamily{cosign（连署）；consign（sb / sth + to + sb / sth）；designate（标明）}
  \wordsense{n. 连署者}
  \wordphrase{a cosigner（连署者）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "a cosigner".（常见搭配：连署者。）}
  \wordsense{a. ）连署的）}
  \wordphrase{cosigner（*co<com (= together)）}
  \wordexample{cosigner（*co<com (= together)）}
  \wordexample{a cosigner（连署者）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{countersign}{ˈkauntəsain}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SIGN；*counter (= against)}
  \wordfamily{countersignature（副署，连署）；consign（sb / sth + to + sb / sth）；designate（标明）}
  \wordsense{n. 【军】（通过岗哨时必须给出的）暗号，口令，答令；副署}
  \wordphrase{the countersign}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of countersign.（此处展示 countersign 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. 副署，连署}
  \wordphrase{countersign.”}
  \wordexample{“Advance and give the countersign.”（“出列并说出口令。”）}
  \wordexample{countersign.”}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{ensign}{ˈensən}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SIGN；*en (= on, in)}
  \wordfamily{consign（sb / sth + to + sb / sth）；designate（标明）；signify（表明）}
  \wordsense{n. （海军的）舰旗；《美》海军少尉}
  \wordphrase{the ensign}
  \wordexample{white / red / blue ensign（（英国皇家海军使用的）白旗 / （英国商船使用的）红旗 / （海军预备舰队使用的）蓝旗）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{insignia}{inˈsigniə}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SIGN；*in (= on)}
  \wordfamily{ensign；insignificant；countersign；resign；consign（sb / sth + to + sb / sth）}
  \wordsense{n. （表示级别或成员的）佩章、衣饰等}
  \wordphrase{insignia of mourning（丧章）}
  \wordexample{insignia of mourning（丧章）}
  \wordexample{insignia（*in (= on)）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{insignificant}{ˌinsigˈnifikənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SIGN；*in (= not), fic (= make)}
  \wordfamily{insignia；resign；ensign；resigned；consign（sb / sth + to + sb / sth）}
  \wordsense{a. 无意义的，无关紧要的，不值一提的，微小的}
  \wordphrase{insignificant talk（不重要的谈话）}
  \wordphrase{insignificant-looking little man（看起来微不足道的小人物）}
  \wordexample{insignificant talk（不重要的谈话）}
  \wordexample{insignificant-looking little man（看起来微不足道的小人物）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{resign}{riˈzain}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SIGN；*re (= back)}
  \wordfamily{re-sign（再签署）；consign（sb / sth + to + sb / sth）；designate（标明）}
  \wordsense{v. 辞职；放弃；听从}
  \wordphrase{resign（*re (= back)）}
  \wordexample{resign（*re (= back)）}
  \wordexample{to resign（辞职）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{resigned}{riˈzaind}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SIGN；*re (= back)}
  \wordfamily{resignedly（听从地，顺从地）；consign（sb / sth + to + sb / sth）；designate（标明）}
  \wordsense{a. 顺从的，听天由命的，甘愿的；辞职的，退休的}
  \wordphrase{with a resigned look（带着听天由命的表情）}
  \wordphrase{resigned post（空缺）}
  \wordphrase{be resigned to one's fate（听天由命）}
  \wordexample{with a resigned look（带着听天由命的表情）}
  \wordexample{resigned post（空缺）}
  \wordexample{be resigned to one's fate（听天由命）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{resignation}{ˌrezigˈneiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SIGN；*re (= back)}
  \wordfamily{resigned；undersign；resign；significant；consign（sb / sth + to + sb / sth）}
  \wordsense{n. 辞职，辞任；辞呈；听从，甘愿}
  \wordphrase{the resignation}
  \wordexample{offer / give in / tender / send in / hand in one's resignation（递交辞呈）}
  \wordexample{resignation（*re (= back)）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{undersign}{ˌʌndəˈsain}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SIGN}
  \wordfamily{consign（sb / sth + to + sb / sth）；designate（标明）；signify（表明）}
  \wordsense{v. 在……下面签名，在末尾签名}
  \wordphrase{undersign the issue}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of undersign.（此处展示 undersign 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. ）（文书的）署名者）}
  \wordphrase{the undersign}
  \wordexample{I, the undersigned（我，签署人）}
  \wordexample{We, the undersigned（我们，签署人）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{significant}{sigˈnifikənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SIGN；*fic (= make)}
  \wordfamily{significantly（意味深长地）；consign（sb / sth + to + sb / sth）；designate（标明）}
  \wordsense{a. 有意义的，意味深长的；重大的，重要的}
  \wordphrase{significant speech（有意义的演讲）}
  \wordexample{significant speech（有意义的演讲）}
  \wordexample{significant（*fic (= make)）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{significance}{sigˈnifikəns}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SIGN；*fic (= make)}
  \wordfamily{significant；sign；undersign；signal；consign（sb / sth + to + sb / sth）}
  \wordsense{n. 意义；重大；重要（性）；意味深长}
  \wordphrase{of little / no significance（无足轻重的 / 无关紧要的）}
  \wordexample{with a look / word of great significance（以极为意味深长的表情 / 话语）}
  \wordexample{of little / no significance（无足轻重的 / 无关紧要的）}
\end{word}

  \wordexample{sign a letter（在信上签名）}

\chapter{SUMM}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 summ 意为 highest, total, sum。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{consummate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SUMM；a.}
  \wordfamily{consummation（完成）；sum（合计）；summing-up（（法官对证据、辩论等的）总结）}
  \wordsense{v.}
  \wordphrase{consummate the issue}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of consummate.（此处展示 consummate 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{a. [拥有高潮 con<com (= with) + summ (= sum, highest point)]}
  \wordphrase{be consummate}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of consummate.（此处展示 consummate 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 实现；完成}
  \wordphrase{consummate the issue}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of consummate.（此处展示 consummate 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{a. 完美的；精通的}
  \wordphrase{My mercenary dreams were}
  \wordexample{My mercenary dreams were consummated the day I opened my own business.（我的发财梦在我的小店开张那天实现了。）}
  \wordexample{My mercenary dreams were}
  \wordexample{to consummate（v. [kənˈsʌmeit]）}
\end{word}
