\begin{word}[**]{summa cum laude}{ˌsumə kʌm ˈlaʊdi}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SUMM；*cum (= with), laude (= praise)}
  \wordfamily{contumacy（顽抗）；tumultuously（喧闹地，骚乱地）；convolve（卷，缠绕）；convolute（卷绕）；convoluted（缠绕的，回旋的，卷绕的）；convoluting（卷绕的，回旋的）；evolutionary（/ evolutional 展开的）；volubility（健谈，喋喋不休）}
  \wordsense{ad. 最优等地，以最优异的学业成绩（指在美国大学以第一名成绩毕业）}
  \wordphrase{magna cum laude（以优异成绩（指在美国大学以第二名成绩毕业））}
  \wordphrase{evolution 展开，进展；进化；evolutionism 进化论；evolutionist 进化论者}
  \wordphrase{revolving bookstand / chair（旋转书架／转椅）}
  \wordexample{scenes that revolted all who saw them（让所有见到的人都不快的场景）}
  \wordexample{revolting to our ideas of morality（违背我们的道德观念的）}
  \wordexample{John graduated summa cum laude from the State University of New York at Stony Brook.（约翰以最优异的成绩从位于石溪的纽约州立大学毕业。）}
  \wordsense{a. 傲慢的，无礼的。con<com (= intensive)。 **contumely**}
  \wordphrase{The student is exceptionally contumacious（那个学生太不服管教了。）}
  \wordexample{The student is exceptionally contumacious.（那个学生太不服管教了。）}
  \wordsense{n. 傲慢，无礼；侮辱性的言行。con<com (= intensive)。 **tumescent**}
  \wordphrase{tummy button（肚脐）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "tummy button".（常见搭配：肚脐。）}
  \wordsense{a. 肿胀的。esc (= becoming)。tumescence（肿胀） **tumid**}
  \wordphrase{tummy button（肚脐）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "tummy button".（常见搭配：肚脐。）}
  \wordsense{a. （身体的一部分）肿胀的；（文体等）夸张的}
  \wordphrase{tummy button（肚脐）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "tummy button".（常见搭配：肚脐。）}
  \wordsense{n. 肚子}
  \wordphrase{tummy button（肚脐）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "tummy button".（常见搭配：肚脐。）}
  \wordsense{a. 吵闹的，喧嚣的；骚乱的，无序的}
  \wordphrase{the tumult of battle（战争的混乱）}
  \wordphrase{in a tumult（心烦意乱地；激动地）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "the tumult of battle".（常见搭配：战争的混乱。）}
  \wordsense{n. （古墓的）坟头}
  \wordphrase{tumultuous welcome（热烈的欢迎）}
  \wordexample{The convolutions of the small intestines are remarkable.（小肠缠绕在一起的样子十分惊人。） **evolve** [i'vɔlv] 【滚出外边 e<ex (= out) + volv (= roll)】vi. into + sth 慢慢地发展，逐渐地变化；进化。vt. 使发展，使展开；进化形成。}
  \wordexample{Many theologians believe the human race is evolving spiritually.（许多神学者都相信人类在精神上是不断进化的。） **voluble** ['vɔljubl] 【卷舌的 volu (= roll)】a. 爱说话的，喋喋不休的；健谈的；流利的。}
  \wordexample{Our voluble tour guide gave us lots of information that, frankly, was boring.（我们健谈的导游为我们提供了不少信息，但说实话也挺让人烦的。） **voluminous** [və'lu:minəs] 【滚动很多的 volu (= roll)】a. （书）卷数多的；（作家）多产的；容积大的，体积大的；长篇大论的，冗长的。}
  \wordsense{v. 把（任务、职责）移交给……，委托。de (= down)}
  \wordphrase{tummy button（肚脐）}
  \wordexample{Shockingly, the FBI kept voluminous files on Rev. Martin Luther King, Jr.（令人震惊的是，美国联邦调查局居然保存着有关马丁·路德·金牧师的大量资料。）}
  \wordexample{When the president is ill, his duties devolve upon the vice-president.（总统生病期间，他的职责移交给副总统。）}
  \wordsense{a. 错综复杂的，纠缠不清的；【植】内卷的。in (= into)}
  \wordphrase{tummy button（肚脐）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "tummy button".（常见搭配：肚脐。）}
  \wordsense{v. 旋转；细想，反复思考。re (= again)}
  \wordphrase{an involved plot（复杂的情节）}
  \wordphrase{involved sentence（复杂的句子）}
  \wordexample{The earth revolves on its axis.（地球以地轴为中心自转。）}
  \wordsense{n. 连发左轮手枪；转炉。re (= again)}
  \wordphrase{revolving seasons（循环往复的季节）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "revolving seasons".（常见搭配：循环往复的季节。）}
  \wordsense{n. 反革命。counter (= against), re (= against)}
  \wordphrase{The use of atomic energy will revolutionize the lives of coming generations（原子能的使用将引起未来人们生活方式上的巨大变革。）}
  \wordexample{The use of atomic energy will revolutionize the lives of coming generations.（原子能的使用将引起未来人们生活方式上的巨大变革。）}
  \wordsense{a. 令人厌恶的，令人恶心的；反叛的。re (= against), volt<volu (= roll)}
  \wordphrase{revolts against oppressions（对压迫的反抗）}
  \wordexample{voluted sea shell（涡形的贝壳）}
\end{word}



\chapter{VIV, VIT}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根 VIV, VIT 来自拉丁语的 `viv`, `vit`，意为 `to live, life`。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 `vig / veg`、`anim` 以及来自希腊语的 `bio`。
  \item **convivial** [kən'viviəl] 【一起有意思地度过的 `con<com (= together) + viv (= live)`】`a.` 快乐而随和的；爱交际的；宴会的。`conviviality` 酒宴；欢乐；愉快。The restaurant hostess is quite convivial, offering a warm smile to all customers.（那家餐厅的女主人十分开朗，对所有的顾客都笑脸相迎。）
  \item **vivacious** [vi'veiʃəs] 【活着的 `viv (= live, life)`】`a.` 活泼的，快活的；有生气的。`vivacity` 快活，活泼；生机勃勃；有生气。She's an attractive, bright, vivacious young woman.（她是一个有魅力的、聪明、活泼的姑娘。）
  \item **devitalize** [di:'vaitəlaiz] 【使失去生命力 `de (= away) + vit (= live)`】`vt.` 夺走生命；使衰弱。`devitalization` 衰弱。The September 11 World Trade Center tragedy devitalized the world economy.（9月11日世界贸易中心的悲剧使世界经济丧失了活力。）
  \item **vital** ['vaitl] 【如同存活的东西一般的 `vit (= live, life)`】`a.` 至关重要的，必需的；生命的；维持生命所必需的；致命的；`n.`（pl.）维持生命所必需的器官；要害，核心。`vitalize` 注入活力，赋予生命；`vitality` 生命力，活力；生气；`vitalism` 生机论；活力论；`vitalist` 活力论者。I lost all my vital records in the tornado, including my passport.（在那场龙卷风中我丢失了我的全部重要档案，包括护照。）
  \item **revive** [ri'vaiv] 【再次活 `re (= again) + viv (= live, life)`】`v.`（使）苏醒，（使）复兴，（使）复活。`revival` 苏醒，复活；恢复；【宗】信仰复兴；`revivalist`【宗】信仰复兴运动者。We revived mother with a cool cloth after she fainted in church.（妈妈在教堂里晕倒了，我们用凉的布为她冷敷使她苏醒。）
  \item **revivify** [ri:'vivifai] `v.` 使苏醒；使振奋精神。`re (= again)`。
  \item **survive** [sə'vaiv] `v.` 存活；幸免于；比……活得长。`sur (= over)`。
  \item **survival** [sə'vaivl] `n.` 存在，残存；残存物，幸存者。`sur (= over)`。`survival after death`（死里逃生）；`the survival of the fittest`（适者生存）；`survival kit`（救生包，装有紧急情况所需的必备品；救生背包）。
  \item **survivor** [sə'vaivə(r)] `n.` 幸存者，生还者，残存物。`sur (= over)`。`survivors of the earthquake`（地震的幸存者）；`survivor guilt`（生存者的自责〔罪恶感〕）；`survivor syndrome`（幸存者综合征，战争、灾害的幸存者所表现出的内疚、紧张、精神崩溃等精神状况）。
  \item **viva** ['vi:və] `n.` 万岁。
  \item **viva voce** ['vaivə 'voutʃi] `ad.` 用口头；`a.` 口头的；`n.` 口试。`voc (= voice, call)`。`viva (= viva voce)`；`viva voce examination`（口试）。
  \item **vivid** ['vivid] `a.` 活泼的；生气勃勃的；（光、颜色等）鲜明的，明亮的；强烈的；（记忆、印象、描述等）生动的，清晰的；逼真的，栩栩如生的。`id (= having the quality of)`。`vividly`（生动地；活泼地；朝气蓬勃地）；`vividness`（活泼；鲜明）；`vivid imagination`（栩栩如生的想像）；`vivid in my memory`（在我的记忆中清晰的）。
  \item **viviparous** [vi'vipərəs] `a.` 胎生的；母体发芽的。`par (= produce)`。`viviparous seeds`（胎萌种子）。
  \item **vivisect** [ˌvivi'sekt] `v.` 活体解剖。`sect (= cut)`。
  \item **vivisection** [ˌvivi'sekʃn] `n.` 活体解剖。`sect (= cut)`。`vivisectionist`（活体解剖〔论〕者）。
  \item **vitamin** ['vitəmin] `n.` 维生素（生物进行正常生理活动时所必需的有机化合物）。`multivitamin`（多种维生素）；`vitamin A`（维生素A）；`illnesses caused by vitamin deficiency`（因缺乏维生素而引起的疾病）；`vitamin tablets`（维生素片）。
\end{itemize}

\chapter{MENS}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根 MENS 来自拉丁语的 `mens`，意为 `to measure`，同义词根有来自拉丁语的 `meter`, `metr`。
  \item **commensurate** [kə'menʃərət] 【大小、尺寸相互一致的 `com (= with) + mens (= measure)`】`a.` 成比例的；均衡的，相应的；同量的。`commensurable` 可用同一单位度量的。Salary for the position will be commensurate with prior experience and ability.（这个职位的薪水将与以往的工作经验和能力成比例。）
  \item **immense** [i'mens] 【无法测量的 `im<in (= not) + mens (= measure)`】`a.` 广大的，巨大的；无限的；极妙的，优秀的。`immensity` 无限；广大；巨大；`immensely` 无限地；非常。There is an immense gulf of enmity between my brother and me.（我哥哥和我之间矛盾很深。）
  \item **commensurable** [kə'menʃərəbl] `a.` 可度量的。`com (= with)`。Their achievements are not commensurable.（他们的成就不可相提并论。）
  \item **dimension** [dai'menʃn] `n.` （长、宽、厚、高等的）尺寸；（pl.）大小，规模；范围；重要性；（口）（女性的）胸围，腰围，臀围；【数】维。`di<dis (= away, apart)`。What are the dimensions of the room?（这个房间有多大？）`building of great dimensions`（庞大的建筑物）；`dimensions of the problem`（问题的重要性）；`of one dimension`（一维的，线性的）；`of two dimensions`（二维的，平面的）；`of three dimensions`（三维的，立体的）。
  \item **dimensional** [dai'menʃənl] `a.` ……维的；尺寸的。`di<dis (= away, apart)`。`3D (three-dimensional) figures`（三维图形）；`two-dimensional space`（二维空间）；`three-dimensional films / picture`（立体电影／三维图像）。
\end{itemize}

\chapter{TUM}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根 TUM 来自拉丁语的 `tum`，意为 `to swell`，同义词根有来自希腊语的 `bry`，以及来自盎格鲁-撒克逊语的 `bel`, `bla` 等。
  \item **contumacious** [ˌkɔntju:'meiʃəs] 【肿、激动得非常厉害的 `con<com (= intensive) + tum (= swell)`】`a.` 反抗的，桀骜不驯的；固执的。(1) `contumacy` 顽抗；蓄意藐视法庭 (2) The student is exceptionally contumacious.（那个学生太不服管教了。）
  \item **contumelious** [ˌkɔntju:'mi:liəs] `a.` 傲慢的，无礼的。`con<com (= intensive)`。
  \item **contumely** ['kɔntju:mli] `n.` 傲慢，无礼；侮辱性的言行。`con<com (= intensive)`。
  \item **tumescent** [tju:'mesnt] `a.` 肿胀的。`esc (= becoming)`。`tumescence`（肿胀）
  \item **tumid** ['tju:mid] `a.` （身体的一部分）肿胀的；（文体等）夸张的。(1) `tumidity`（肿胀；夸张）
  \item **tummy** ['tʌmi] `n.` 肚子。(1) `tummy button`（肚脐）
  \item **tumour** ['tju:mə(r)] `n.` 肿瘤，肿块。(1) `benign / malignant tumor`（良性／恶性肿瘤）
  \item **tumult** ['tju:mʌlt] `n.` 喧嚣，骚动，骚乱。(1) `the tumult of battle`（战争的混乱）`in a tumult`（心烦意乱地；激动地）
  \item **tumultuous** [tju:'mʌltʃuəs] `a.` 吵闹的，喧嚣的；骚乱的，无序的。(1) `tumultuously`（喧闹地，骚乱地）(2) `tumultuous welcome`（热烈的欢迎）
  \item **tumulus** ['tju:mjuləs] `n.` （古墓的）坟头
\end{itemize}

\chapter{VOLV, VOLU}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 `volv`, `volu` 意为 `to roll, to turn`。
  \item **convolution** [ˌkɔnvə'lu:ʃn] 【一起滚 `con<com (= together) + volu (= roll, turn)`】`n.` 回旋，卷绕。(1) `convolve` 卷，缠绕；`convolute` 卷绕 (2) `convoluted` 缠绕的，回旋的，卷绕的；错综复杂的；`convoluting` 卷绕的，回旋的 (3) The convolutions of the small intestines are remarkable.（小肠缠绕在一起的样子十分惊人。）
  \item **evolve** [i'vɔlv] 【滚出外边 `e<ex (= out) + volv (= roll)`】`vi.` `into + sth` 慢慢地发展，逐渐地变化；进化。`vt.` 使发展，使展开；进化形成。(1) `evolution` 展开，进展；进化；`evolutionism` 进化论；`evolutionist` 进化论者 (2) `evolutionary / evolutional` 展开的；进化的；发展的 (3) Many theologians believe the human race is evolving spiritually.（许多神学者都相信人类在精神上是不断进化的。）
  \item **voluble** ['vɔljubl] 【卷舌的 `volu (= roll)`】`a.` 爱说话的，喋喋不休的；健谈的；流利的。(1) `volubility` 健谈，喋喋不休 (2) `volubly` 流利地；爱说话地；喋喋不休地 (3) Our voluble tour guide gave us lots of information that, frankly, was boring.（我们健谈的导游为我们提供了不少信息，但说实话也挺让人烦的。）
  \item **voluminous** [və'lu:minəs] 【滚动很多的 `volu (= roll)`】`a.` （书）卷数多的；（作家）多产的；容积大的，体积大的；长篇大论的，冗长的。(1) `volume` 卷；体积；量；音量；大量 (2) Shockingly, the FBI kept voluminous files on Rev. Martin Luther King, Jr.（令人震惊的是，美国联邦调查局居然保存着有关马丁·路德·金牧师的大量资料。）
  \item **devolve** [di'vɔlv] `v.` 把（任务、职责）移交给……，委托。`de (= down)`。(1) When the president is ill, his duties devolve upon the vice-president.（总统生病期间，他的职责移交给副总统。）The work devolved upon him.（这项工作委托给他了。）
  \item **devolution** [ˌdi:və'lu:ʃn] `n.` （权力的）转移，委任；权力下放。`de (= down)`。
  \item **involute** ['invəlu:t] `a.` 错综复杂的，纠缠不清的；【植】内卷的。`in (= into)`。(1) `involution`（错综复杂；纠缠；卷入）
  \item **involve** [in'vɔlv] `v.` 牵连，连累；使陷入（困难等）；使成为必要条件或结果；包含。`in (= into)`。(1) `involvement`（牵连，连累，卷入；包含）
  \item **involved** [in'vɔlvd] `a.` 不易懂的；复杂的。`in (= into)`。(1) `an involved plot`（复杂的情节）`involved sentence`（复杂的句子）
  \item **revolve** [ri'vɔlv] `v.` 旋转；细想，反复思考。`re (= again)`。(1) The earth revolves on its axis.（地球以地轴为中心自转。）revolve a problem in one's mind（反复思考一个问题）
  \item **revolving** [ri'vɔlviŋ] `a.` （周期性）循环的；旋转式的。`re (= again)`。(1) revolving bookstand / chair（旋转书架／转椅）revolving fund / stage（周转资金／旋转舞台）revolving seasons（循环往复的季节）
  \item **revolver** [ri'vɔlvə(r)] `n.` 连发左轮手枪；转炉。`re (= again)`。(1) reach for one's revolver（伸手掏左轮手枪）
  \item **revolution** [ˌrevə'lu:ʃn] `n.` 革命，大变革；旋转；【天】公转。`re (= again)`。(1) the English Revolution（英国〔光荣〕革命，1688）the American Revolution（美国独立战争）the French Revolution（法国大革命）the Chinese Revolution（中国辛亥革命）the Russian (October) Revolution（俄国十月革命）
  \item **revolutionary** [ˌrevə'lu:ʃənəri] `a.` 革命的，革命性的；引起大变革的。`n.` 革命者。`re (= against)`。(1) revolutionary ideas（革命思想）revolutionary army（革命军）Bolshevik revolutionary（布尔什维克革命者）
  \item **revolutionize** [ˌrevə'lu:ʃənaiz] `v.` 使革命化；彻底地改革。`re (= again)`。(1) The use of atomic energy will revolutionize the lives of coming generations.（原子能的使用将引起未来人们生活方式上的巨大变革。）
  \item **counterrevolution** [ˌkauntəˌrevə'lu:ʃn] `n.` 反革命。`counter (= against), re (= against)`。(1) counterrevolutionary（反革命分子）
  \item **revolt** [ri'vəult] `v.` 叛乱；（使）不快，（使）厌恶。`n.` 叛乱，反抗。`re (= against), volt<volu (= roll)`。(1) revolt against their oppressors（反抗压迫者）scenes that revolted all who saw them（让所有见到的人都不快的场景）stir the people to revolt（鼓动人民反抗）a period of revolt（反抗期）revolts against oppressions（对压迫的反抗）
  \item **revolting** [ri'vəultiŋ] `a.` 令人厌恶的，令人恶心的；反叛的。`re (= against), volt<volu (= roll)`。(1) revoltingly（讨厌地）(2) revolting behavior（令人厌恶的行为）revolting to our ideas of morality（违背我们的道德观念的）revoltingly dirty room（脏得让人恶心的屋子）
  \item **volute** [və'lu:t] `n.`（特指爱奥尼亚、科林斯柱顶的）涡形花样，涡形饰
  \item **voluted** [və'lu:tid] `a.` 有涡形装饰的，涡形的。(1) voluted sea shell（涡形的贝壳）
\end{itemize}

\chapter{PICT[PIG]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 pict 意为 to paint，它的变形为 pig。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{depict}{diˈpikt}
  \wordbreakdown{非常生动地画、描写 de (= intensive) + pict (= paint)}
  \wordsense{vt. 描画；描写；描述}
  \wordfamily{depiction 描画；描写，描述}
  \wordphrase{depicts a placid woman}
  \wordexample{The portrait depicts a placid woman, secure in the bosom of her family.（这幅肖像画里画的是一个安详的女人，她被家人紧紧搂着。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{pictograph}{'piktəgrɑ:f}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PICT[PIG]}
  \wordfamily{pictorial；picture；picturedom；picturesque；depict（描画）}
  \wordsense{n. 古代石壁画；象形文字；统计图表 *graph (= write)}
  \wordphrase{a pictograph（古代石壁画）}
  \wordexample{a pictograph（古代石壁画）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{pictorial}{pikˈtɔ:riəl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PICT[PIG]}
  \wordfamily{pictograph；picture；picturedom；picturesque；depict（描画）}
  \wordsense{a. 画的；有插图的；图示的}
  \wordphrase{be pictorial}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of pictorial.（此处展示 pictorial 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 有插图的杂志、报纸、画报}
  \wordphrase{pictorial record of the wedding（记录婚礼的图片）}
  \wordphrase{pictorial magazine（画报）}
  \wordphrase{pictorial puzzle（画谜）}
  \wordexample{pictorial record of the wedding（记录婚礼的图片）}
  \wordexample{pictorial magazine（画报）}
  \wordexample{pictorial puzzle（画谜）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{picture}{'piktʃə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PICT[PIG]}
  \wordfamily{pictorial；picturedom；pictograph；picturesque；depict（描画）}
  \wordsense{n. 图画，绘画，照片；景色；美丽如画的物（或人）；相似的形象；（生动的）描述；状况；图像，画面；（the pictures）电影}
  \wordphrase{the picture}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of picture.（此处展示 picture 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. 描述；想像}
  \wordphrase{picture frame / mounting（画框 / 装裱）}
  \wordphrase{draw a picture（画画）}
  \wordexample{picture frame / mounting（画框 / 装裱）}
  \wordexample{draw a picture（画画）}
  \wordexample{May I take your picture?（可以为你照张相吗？）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{picturedom}{'piktʃədəm}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PICT[PIG]}
  \wordfamily{picture；picturesque；pictorial；pigment；depict（描画）}
  \wordsense{n. 电影界 *dom (= house)}
  \wordphrase{a picturedom（电影界）}
  \wordexample{a picturedom（电影界）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{picturesque}{ˌpiktʃəˈresk}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PICT[PIG]}
  \wordfamily{picturedom；pigment；picture；pigmentation；depict（描画）}
  \wordsense{a. 如画的，美丽的；别具一格的；生动的，栩栩如生的 *esque (= like)}
  \wordphrase{picturesque village（美丽的乡村）}
  \wordphrase{picturesque language（生动的语言）}
  \wordphrase{picturesque Indian（奇特的印度人）}
  \wordexample{picturesque village（美丽的乡村）}
  \wordexample{picturesque language（生动的语言）}
  \wordexample{picturesque Indian（奇特的印度人）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{pigment}{'pigmənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PICT[PIG]}
  \wordsense{n. 绘画颜料，涂料；色素}
  \wordfamily{picturesque}
  \wordfamily{pigmentation}
  \wordfamily{picturedom}
  \wordfamily{picture}
  \wordphrase{pigment cell（色素细胞）}
  \wordexample{pigment cell（色素细胞）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{pigmentation}{ˌpigmenˈteiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PICT[PIG]}
  \wordfamily{pigment；picturesque；picturedom；picture；depict（描画）}
  \wordsense{n. 染色，着色；色素沉着}
  \wordphrase{a pigmentation（染色，着色）}
  \wordexample{a pigmentation（染色，着色）}
\end{word}



\chapter{TERR}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根 TERR 来自拉丁语的 `terr`，意为 `to frighten, earth`。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{deter}{di't3:(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TERR；使吃惊而把手从某物上拿开 de (= from) + ter(terr (= frighten)}
  \wordfamily{deterrence（威慑）；deterrent（制止的）；terrible（骇人的）}
  \wordsense{vt. sb + from + \textasciitilde{}ing 制止，阻止，抑制；使不敢}
  \wordphrase{deter speeding significantly}
  \wordexample{The police say speed traps deter speeding significantly.（警方称汽车超速监视区有效地防止了超速驾驶。）}
  \wordexample{deter speeding significantly}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{terrible}{'terabl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TERR}
  \wordfamily{deter（sb + from + \textasciiti）；terribly（非常）；terrific（可怕的）}
  \wordsense{a. 骇人的，可怕的；极度的；极坏的，糟透的 *terr (= frighten)}
  \wordphrase{terrible crime / sight（骇人的罪行／令人毛骨悚然的景象）}
  \wordphrase{terrible winter（严冬）}
  \wordphrase{terrible food（极糟糕的食物）}
  \wordexample{His performance is terrible.（他的演技很糟糕。）}
  \wordexample{terrible crime / sight（骇人的罪行／令人毛骨悚然的景象）}
  \wordexample{terrible winter（严冬）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{terribly}{'terabli}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TERR}
  \wordfamily{terrible；terrific；deter；terrify}
  \wordsense{ad. 非常，十分，极其；可怕地 *terr (= frighten)}
  \wordphrase{be terribly}
  \wordexample{It's terribly cold.（非常冷。）}
  \wordexample{How terribly boring he is!（他实在太无聊了！）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{terrific}{ta'rifik}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TERR}
  \wordfamily{deter（sb + from + \textasciiti）；terrible（骇人的）；terribly（非常）}
  \wordsense{a. 可怕的，吓人的，恐怖的；惊人的，极度的，极大的，极端的；（美俚）非常好的，很棒的，了不起的 *terr (= frighten), fic (= make)}
  \wordphrase{at a terrific pace（以惊人的速度）}
  \wordphrase{terrific vacation（很棒的假期）}
  \wordphrase{terrific party（极好的晚会）}
  \wordexample{at a terrific pace（以惊人的速度）}
  \wordexample{terrific vacation（很棒的假期）}
  \wordexample{terrific party（极好的晚会）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{terrify}{'terifai}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TERR}
  \wordfamily{terrific；terror；terribly；deteriorate；deter（sb + from + \textasciiti）}
  \wordsense{v. 使害怕 *terr (= frighten), ify (= make)}
  \wordphrase{terrify the issue}
  \wordexample{You terrify me!（你吓死我了！）}
  \wordexample{What a terrifying experience!（真是一段恐怖的经历！）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{terror}{'terə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TERR}
  \wordfamily{terrorism（恐怖主义，恐怖行为）；terrorist（恐怖分子，恐怖主义者）；deter（sb + from + \textasciiti）}
  \wordsense{n. 恐怖，令人恐怖的人（或事）；（口）极讨厌的人 *terr (= frighten)}
  \wordphrase{the Reign of Terror（（法国大革命中的）恐怖政治）}
  \wordphrase{terror-struck / terror-stricken（吓坏的，吓得心惊胆战的）}
  \wordexample{the Reign of Terror（（法国大革命中的）恐怖政治）}
  \wordexample{terror-struck / terror-stricken（吓坏的，吓得心惊胆战的）}
  \wordexample{This child is a perfect terror.（这个孩子真令人讨厌。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{deteriorate}{di'tiəriəreit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TERR}
  \wordfamily{deterioration（恶化，（质量）下降，堕落）；deter（sb + from + \textasciiti）；terrible（骇人的）}
  \wordsense{v. （使）恶化；（使）退化；变质，变坏 *de (= down), ter<terr (= earth)}
  \wordphrase{deteriorates in a hot}
  \wordexample{Leather quickly deteriorates in a hot, damp climate.（皮革在闷热、潮湿的环境中很快就会变质。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{esoteric}{esa'terik}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TERR}
  \wordfamily{deteriorate；extraterrestrial；terror；extraterritorial；deter（sb + from + \textasciiti）}
  \wordsense{a. 秘传的，秘密的；难解的，深奥的 *eso (= within), ter<terr (= earth)}
  \wordphrase{be esoteric（秘传的，秘密的）}
  \wordexample{be esoteric（秘传的，秘密的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{extraterrestrial}{ekstrata'restrial}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TERR}
  \wordfamily{esoteric；extraterritorial；deteriorate；interment；deter（sb + from + \textasciiti）}
  \wordsense{n. 外星人，天外来客}
  \wordphrase{the extraterrestrial}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of extraterrestrial.（此处展示 extraterrestrial 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{a. 地球外的 *extra (= outside), ter<terr (= earth)}
  \wordphrase{be extraterrestrial}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of extraterrestrial.（此处展示 extraterrestrial 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{a. 地球外的）}
  \wordphrase{be extraterrestrial}
  \wordexample{an extraterrestrial（外星人，天外来客 a. 地球外的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{extraterritorial}{'ekstrateri'torial}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TERR}
  \wordfamily{extraterritoriality（治外法权）；deter（sb + from + \textasciiti）；terrible（骇人的）}
  \wordsense{a. 治外法权的 *extra (= outside), ter<terr (= earth)}
  \wordphrase{be extraterritorial（治外法权的）}
  \wordexample{be extraterritorial（治外法权的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{interment}{in't3:mənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TERR}
  \wordfamily{extraterritorial；Mediterranean；extraterrestrial；subterranean；deter（sb + from + \textasciiti）}
  \wordsense{n. 埋葬，土葬 *in (= in), ter<terr (= earth)}
  \wordphrase{an interment（埋葬，土葬）}
  \wordexample{an interment（埋葬，土葬）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{Mediterranean}{meditə'reinian}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TERR}
  \wordsense{a. 地中海的；（小写）被陆地包围的 n.（the \textasciitilde{}）地中海 *medi (= middle), ter<terr (= earth)}
  \wordfamily{interment}
  \wordfamily{subterranean}
  \wordfamily{extraterritorial}
  \wordfamily{terrace}
  \wordfamily{be Mediterranean（地中海的）}
  \wordphrase{be Mediterranean（地中海的）}
  \wordexample{be Mediterranean（地中海的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{subterranean}{sʌbtə'reinian}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TERR}
  \wordfamily{Mediterranean；terrace；interment；terra（firma）；deter（sb + from + \textasciiti）}
  \wordsense{a. 地下的}
  \wordphrase{be subterranean}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of subterranean.（此处展示 subterranean 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 地下洞穴 *sub (= under), ter<terr (= earth)}
  \wordphrase{subterranean passage / railway (railroad)（地下通道／地铁）}
  \wordphrase{subterranean water（地下水）}
  \wordphrase{subterranean maneuver（地下活动）}
  \wordexample{subterranean passage / railway (railroad)（地下通道／地铁）}
  \wordexample{subterranean water（地下水）}
  \wordexample{subterranean maneuver（地下活动）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{terrace}{'terəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TERR}
  \wordfamily{deter（sb + from + \textasciiti）；terrible（骇人的）；terribly（非常）}
  \wordsense{n. 梯田；倾斜的平地，台地；斜坡上房屋前的街巷；（道路中央的）狭长形小园地区}
  \wordphrase{terraced fields（梯田）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "terraced fields".（常见搭配：梯田。）}
  \wordsense{v. 使成梯形地，使成为台地 *ter<terr (= earth)}
  \wordphrase{a terrace（梯田）}
  \wordexample{a terrace（梯田）}
  \wordexample{to terrace（使成梯形地，使成为台地）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{terra firma}{'terə 'f3:mə}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TERR}
  \wordfamily{terrace；terra（incognita）；subterranean；terrain；deter（sb + from + \textasciiti）}
  \wordsense{n. 陆地，大地（有别于海洋、河流） *ter<terr (= earth), firm (= strong)}
  \wordphrase{the terra firma}
  \wordexample{glad to be on terra firma again（很高兴能重返陆地）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{terra incognita}{'terə in'kɔgnita}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TERR}
  \wordfamily{terra（firma）；terrain；terrace；terrestrial；deter（sb + from + \textasciiti）}
  \wordsense{n. 未探明地区，未知领域 *ter<terr (= earth), in (= not), co<com (= thoroughly), gn<gno (= know)}
  \wordphrase{a terra incognita（未探明地区，未知领域）}
  \wordexample{a terra incognita（未探明地区，未知领域）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{terrain}{tə'rein}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TERR}
  \wordfamily{terra（incognita）；terrestrial；terrier；deter（sb + from + \textasciiti）}
  \wordsense{n. 地形，地势；地带，地域；领域，范围 *terr (= earth)}
  \wordphrase{the terrain}
  \wordexample{difficult terrain for heavy armored vehicles（重装甲车难以行进的地形）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{terrestrial}{tə'restrial}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TERR}
  \wordfamily{deter（sb + from + \textasciiti）；terrible（骇人的）；terribly（非常）}
  \wordsense{a. 地球的，地上的；人间的；陆地的；陆栖的}
  \wordphrase{be terrestrial}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of terrestrial.（此处展示 terrestrial 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 地球上的居民；陆生生物 *ter<terr (= earth)}
  \wordphrase{terrestrial globe（地球仪）}
  \wordphrase{terrestrial heat（地热）}
  \wordphrase{terrestrial parts of the world（地球的陆地部分）}
  \wordexample{terrestrial globe（地球仪）}
  \wordexample{terrestrial heat（地热）}
  \wordexample{terrestrial parts of the world（地球的陆地部分）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{terrier}{'teriə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TERR}
  \wordsense{n. 一种猎狗（小而活泼，会为寻找食物而挖洞）}
  \wordfamily{terrestrial}
  \wordfamily{territorial}
  \wordfamily{terrain}
  \wordfamily{territory}
  \wordfamily{a terrier（一种猎狗（小而活泼，会为寻找食物而挖洞））}
  \wordphrase{a terrier（一种猎狗（小而活泼，会为寻找食物而挖洞））}
  \wordexample{a terrier（一种猎狗（小而活泼，会为寻找食物而挖洞））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{territorial}{teri'tɔ:rial}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TERR}
  \wordsense{a. 领土的；（T\textasciitilde{}）美国准州的，美国领土的 *ter<terr (= earth)}
  \wordfamily{terrier}
  \wordfamily{territory}
  \wordfamily{terrestrial}
  \wordfamily{terrain}
  \wordphrase{territorial waters / air / seas（领水／领空／领海）}
  \wordphrase{territorial issue / expansion（领土问题／领土扩张）}
  \wordphrase{territorial integrity / sovereignty（领土完整／领土主权）}
  \wordphrase{territorial possessions（土地财产）}
  \wordphrase{Territorial laws（美国领地的法律）}
  \wordexample{territorial waters / air / seas（领水／领空／领海）}
  \wordexample{territorial issue / expansion（领土问题／领土扩张）}
  \wordexample{territorial integrity / sovereignty（领土完整／领土主权）}
  \wordexample{territorial possessions（土地财产）}
  \wordexample{Territorial laws（美国领地的法律）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{territory}{'terətri}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TERR}
  \wordfamily{deter（sb + from + \textasciiti）；terrible（骇人的）；terribly（非常）}
  \wordsense{n. 领土；地域，地区；（动物的）领地，活动范围；（科学等的）领域，范围；美国的准州（未正式成为州的地区） *ter<terr (= earth)}
  \wordphrase{South Korean territory（韩国领土）}
  \wordphrase{territory of biochemistry（生物化学领域）}
  \wordexample{South Korean territory（韩国领土）}
  \wordexample{Much territory in Africa is desert.（非洲的大片地区是沙漠。）}
  \wordexample{territory of biochemistry（生物化学领域）}
\end{word}



\chapter{TRA, TRACT[TREAT]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根 TRA, TRACT[TREAT] 来自拉丁语的 tra, tract 意为 to draw，变形为 treat。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{detract}{di'trækt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TRA, TRACT[TREAT]}
  \wordfamily{detraction（诽谤，责难）；detractor（责难者，诽谤者）；detractive（诽谤的，责难的）}
  \wordsense{v. from + sth; sth + from + sth 降低，贬低（名声、价值等） *de (= down) + tract (= draw)}
  \wordphrase{detracts from her natural}
  \wordexample{Her excess of makeup detracts from her natural beauty.（她的浓妆有损她的自然美。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{intractable}{in'træktəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TRA, TRACT[TREAT]}
  \wordfamily{intractability（难以控制）；detract（from + sth; sth + from +）；retractable（可以取消的）}
  \wordsense{a. 不听话的，倔强的；难以控制的，难以驾驭的 *in (= not) + tract (= draw)}
  \wordphrase{intractable cancer}
  \wordexample{He died at 39, a victim of an intractable cancer.（他因癌症不治而死去，终年39岁。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{retractable}{ri'træktəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TRA, TRACT[TREAT]}
  \wordsense{a. 可以取消的，可以撤回的；伸缩自如的，可以蜷缩的，可以塞进里面的}
  \wordfamily{retract 撤回，取消；使一下子进去}
  \wordfamily{retraction 回收，撤回；蜷缩}
  \wordfamily{retractile 可以收缩的}
  \wordphrase{retractable awnings}
  \wordexample{I bought a set of retractable awnings for all my home's windows.（我为我们家所有窗户买了一套伸缩遮阳篷。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{entreat}{in'tri:t}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TRA, TRACT[TREAT]}
  \wordfamily{entreaty（恳求，哀求）；entreating（恳求的，乞求的）；entreatingly（恳求地）；detract（from + sth; sth + from +）}
  \wordsense{vt. 恳请；乞求 *en (= in) + treat<tract (= draw)}
  \wordphrase{entreated the suspect}
  \wordexample{The policeman entreated the suspect to surrender without violence.（警察恳请嫌疑人自首投降，不要暴力反抗。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{maltreat}{,mæl'tri:t}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TRA, TRACT[TREAT]}
  \wordfamily{maltreatment（虐待）；detract（from + sth; sth + from +）；intractable（不听话的）}
  \wordsense{vt. 虐待，粗暴地对待；滥用 *mal (= bad) + treat<tract (= draw)}
  \wordphrase{maltreat the children entrusted}
  \wordexample{Occasionally, day care workers maltreat the children entrusted to their care.（托儿所的员工有时会粗暴地对待入托的孩子。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{abstract}{'æbstrækt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TRA, TRACT[TREAT]}
  \wordfamily{abstracted（心不在焉的，发呆的）；abstractedly（心不在焉地，走神地）；detract（from + sth; sth + from +）}
  \wordsense{n. 摘要，摘录；抽象概念}
  \wordphrase{the abstract}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of abstract.（此处展示 abstract 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{a. 抽象的}
  \wordphrase{be abstract}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of abstract.（此处展示 abstract 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. 抽出，提取；偷 *abs<ab (= away)}
  \wordphrase{of a sermon（布道摘选）}
  \wordphrase{abstract metal from ore（从矿石中提炼金属）}
  \wordexample{abstract a wallet from his pocket（从他的口袋里偷走钱包）}
  \wordexample{of a sermon（布道摘选）}
  \wordexample{abstract metal from ore（从矿石中提炼金属）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{abstraction}{æb'strækʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TRA, TRACT[TREAT]}
  \wordfamily{abstract；attract；maltreat；attraction；detract（from + sth; sth + from +）}
  \wordsense{n. 抽象；抽象概念；心不在焉 *abs<ab (= away)}
  \wordphrase{with an air of abstraction（一副心不在焉的样子）}
  \wordphrase{in a moment of abstraction（出神的瞬间）}
  \wordexample{with an air of abstraction（一副心不在焉的样子）}
  \wordexample{in a moment of abstraction（出神的瞬间）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{attract}{ə'trækt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TRA, TRACT[TREAT]}
  \wordfamily{abstraction；attraction；abstract；attractive；detract（from + sth; sth + from +）}
  \wordsense{v. 吸引，引起（注意、兴趣）；引诱 *at<ad (= near)}
  \wordphrase{attract attention（引起注意）}
  \wordexample{attract attention（引起注意）}
  \wordexample{Do you feel attracted to her?（你对她有好感吗？）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{attraction}{ə'trækʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TRA, TRACT[TREAT]}
  \wordfamily{attract；attractive；abstraction；contract；detract（from + sth; sth + from +）}
  \wordsense{n. 吸引力；引力；吸引人的东西 *at<ad (= near)}
  \wordphrase{attraction of a pretty girl（美女的魅力）}
  \wordphrase{attractions of a big city（大都市的各种诱惑）}
  \wordexample{attraction of a pretty girl（美女的魅力）}
  \wordexample{attractions of a big city（大都市的各种诱惑）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{attractive}{ə'træktiv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TRA, TRACT[TREAT]}
  \wordfamily{attraction；contract；attract；contraction；detract（from + sth; sth + from +）}
  \wordsense{a. 吸引人的，有魅力的 *at<ad (= near)}
  \wordphrase{attractive personality（有魅力的个性）}
  \wordphrase{a most attractive girl（非常吸引人的女孩）}
  \wordphrase{attractive price（吸引人的价格）}
  \wordexample{attractive personality（有魅力的个性）}
  \wordexample{a most attractive girl（非常吸引人的女孩）}
  \wordexample{attractive price（吸引人的价格）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{contract}{kən'trækt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TRA, TRACT[TREAT]}
  \wordfamily{contractual（合同（上）的）；detract（from + sth; sth + from +）；intractable（不听话的）}
  \wordsense{v. （使）（肌肉等）收缩；缩小，缩减；患（病）；负（债）；沾染上（不好的习惯等）；约定，订合同}
  \wordphrase{contractible / contractile（减少的，收缩性的）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "contractible / contractile".（常见搭配：减少的，收缩性的。）}
  \wordsense{n. 契约，合同；承包合同 *con<com (= together)}
  \wordphrase{contract a muscle（使肌肉收缩）}
  \wordphrase{sign a contract（在合同上签字）}
  \wordphrase{conditions of contract（合同条件）}
  \wordexample{contract with a firm for 1,000 tons of cement（与一家公司签订1,000吨水泥的销售合同）}
  \wordexample{social / verbal / written contract（社会契约／口头／书面合同）}
  \wordexample{contract a muscle（使肌肉收缩）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{contraction}{kən'trækʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TRA, TRACT[TREAT]}
  \wordfamily{contract；contractor；attractive；extract；detract（from + sth; sth + from +）}
  \wordsense{n. 收缩，缩短，缩小；收缩的东西；缩略形式 *con<com (= together)}
  \wordphrase{the contraction of a muscle（肌肉收缩）}
  \wordexample{the contraction of a muscle（肌肉收缩）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{contractor}{kən'træktə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TRA, TRACT[TREAT]}
  \wordfamily{detract（from + sth; sth + from +）；intractable（不听话的）；retractable（可以取消的）}
  \wordsense{n. 立契约者；承包人 *con<com (= together)}
  \wordphrase{a contractor（立契约者）}
  \wordexample{a contractor（立契约者）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{extract}{ik'strækt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TRA, TRACT[TREAT]}
  \wordfamily{contractor；extraction；contraction；protract；detract（from + sth; sth + from +）}
  \wordsense{v. （通常指用力）拔出；榨取（果汁等）；提取；设法得到（信息等）；（从书籍、演讲中）摘录，选用}
  \wordphrase{extract the issue}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of extract.（此处展示 extract 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 提炼物，浓缩物，浓缩汁，精；摘录，选萃 *ex (= out)}
  \wordphrase{oil extracted from olives（从橄榄中提炼出的油）}
  \wordphrase{vanilla / beef extract（香草精／浓缩牛肉汁）}
  \wordphrase{extract of malt（麦芽油）}
  \wordexample{extract a tooth / a cork from the bottle / a bullet from a wound（拔牙／从瓶中拔出软木塞／从伤口处取出子弹）}
  \wordexample{extract money / information from him（设法从他那儿弄到钱／情报）}
  \wordexample{oil extracted from olives（从橄榄中提炼出的油）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{extraction}{ik'strækʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TRA, TRACT[TREAT]}
  \wordfamily{extract；protract；contractor；protractor；detract（from + sth; sth + from +）}
  \wordsense{n. 拔出，抽出；血统，出身 *ex (= out)}
  \wordphrase{extraction of a tooth（拔牙）}
  \wordexample{an American of South Korean extraction（韩裔美国人）}
  \wordexample{extraction of a tooth（拔牙）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{protract}{prə'trækt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TRA, TRACT[TREAT]}
  \wordfamily{protraction（拖延，延长）；detract（from + sth; sth + from +）；intractable（不听话的）}
  \wordsense{v. 延长，拖延（时间） *pro (= forward)}
  \wordphrase{protracted visit / argument（持久的访问／争论）}
  \wordexample{protracted visit / argument（持久的访问／争论）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{protractor}{prə'træktə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TRA, TRACT[TREAT]}
  \wordfamily{protract；subtract；extraction；subtraction；detract（from + sth; sth + from +）}
  \wordsense{n. 量角器，分度规 *pro (= forward)}
  \wordphrase{a protractor（量角器，分度规）}
  \wordexample{a protractor（量角器，分度规）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{subtract}{səb'trækt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TRA, TRACT[TREAT]}
  \wordfamily{protractor；subtraction；protract；tract；detract（from + sth; sth + from +）}
  \wordsense{v. 减去，扣除 *sub (= under)}
  \wordphrase{subtract the issue}
  \wordexample{6 subtracted from 9 gives 3.（9减6等于3。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{subtraction}{səb'trækʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TRA, TRACT[TREAT]}
  \wordfamily{subtract；tract；protractor；tractable；detract（from + sth; sth + from +）}
  \wordsense{n. 减去，减法 *sub (= under)}
  \wordphrase{subtraction}
  \wordexample{Two from five is a simple subtraction.（5减2是很简单的减法。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{tract}{trækt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TRA, TRACT[TREAT]}
  \wordfamily{detract（from + sth; sth + from +）；intractable（不听话的）；retractable（可以取消的）}
  \wordsense{n. （山林、农田等）广阔土地；【解】管；（特指涉及宗教、政治等的）小册子，短文}
  \wordphrase{digestive / respiratory tract（消化道／呼吸道）}
  \wordexample{wide tracts of desert in North Africa（北非广阔的沙漠地带）}
  \wordexample{digestive / respiratory tract（消化道／呼吸道）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{tractable}{'træktəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TRA, TRACT[TREAT]}
  \wordsense{a. 易驾驭的；易处理的}
  \wordfamily{tract}
  \wordfamily{traction}
  \wordfamily{subtraction}
  \wordfamily{tractor}
  \wordfamily{be tractable（易驾驭的）}
  \wordphrase{be tractable（易驾驭的）}
  \wordexample{be tractable（易驾驭的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{traction}{'trækʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TRA, TRACT[TREAT]}
  \wordsense{n. 拖拉，牵引；牵引力}
  \wordfamily{tractable}
  \wordfamily{tractor}
  \wordfamily{tract}
  \wordfamily{distrait}
  \wordphrase{electric / steam traction（电力／蒸汽牵引（力））}
  \wordphrase{traction engine（牵引机车）}
  \wordexample{electric / steam traction（电力／蒸汽牵引（力））}
  \wordexample{traction engine（牵引机车）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{tractor}{'træktə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TRA, TRACT[TREAT]}
  \wordsense{n. 拖拉机，牵引车（牵引农业机械或其他载重装备）}
  \wordfamily{traction}
  \wordfamily{distrait}
  \wordfamily{tractable}
  \wordfamily{distraught}
  \wordfamily{a tractor（拖拉机，牵引车（牵引农业机械或其他载重装备））}
  \wordphrase{a tractor（拖拉机，牵引车（牵引农业机械或其他载重装备））}
  \wordexample{a tractor（拖拉机，牵引车（牵引农业机械或其他载重装备））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{distrait}{di'strei}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TRA, TRACT[TREAT]}
  \wordfamily{tractor；distraught；traction；entrails；detract（from + sth; sth + from +）}
  \wordsense{a. 心不在焉的，精神恍惚的 *dis (= away)}
  \wordphrase{be distrait（心不在焉的，精神恍惚的）}
  \wordexample{be distrait（心不在焉的，精神恍惚的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{distraught}{di'strɔ:t}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TRA, TRACT[TREAT]}
  \wordfamily{distrait；entrails；tractor；entrain；detract（from + sth; sth + from +）}
  \wordsense{a. 心烦意乱的；几乎发狂的 *dis (= away)}
  \wordphrase{distraught with grief（悲痛欲狂）}
  \wordexample{distraught with grief（悲痛欲狂）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{entrails}{'entreilz}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TRA, TRACT[TREAT]}
  \wordfamily{distraught；entrain；distrait；portray；detract（from + sth; sth + from +）}
  \wordsense{n. 内脏；肠；（物体的）内部 *en (= into)}
  \wordphrase{an entrails（内脏）}
  \wordexample{an entrails（内脏）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{entrain}{in'trein}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TRA, TRACT[TREAT]}
  \wordfamily{entrails；portray；distraught；portrait；detract（from + sth; sth + from +）}
  \wordsense{v. 使（军队）上火车 *en (= into)}
  \wordphrase{to entrain（使（军队）上火车）}
  \wordexample{to entrain（使（军队）上火车）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{portray}{pɔ:'trei}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TRA, TRACT[TREAT]}
  \wordfamily{detract（from + sth; sth + from +）；intractable（不听话的）；retractable（可以取消的）}
  \wordsense{v. 画，为...画像；（用语言）描写；（在戏剧中）饰演...角色 *por<pro (= forth, forward)}
  \wordphrase{to portray（画，为...画像）}
  \wordexample{to portray（画，为...画像）}
\end{word}

  \wordbreakdown{词根 TRA, TRACT[TREAT]}

  \wordphrase{a portrait（肖像（画），画像，相片）}
  \wordexample{a portrait（肖像（画），画像，相片）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{trace}{treis}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TRA, TRACT[TREAT]}
  \wordsense{n. 足迹，痕迹，行踪，踪迹；微量，极少量；（马车的）缰绳，挽绳 v. 查出...的踪迹，跟踪；探出；画轮廓；描绘；（用绘图工具）刻印；复写；（费力、细心地）写（字）；追溯，探索来由}
  \wordfamily{traceable（可描画的，可描摹的；可追溯的；可追踪的）}
  \wordfamily{tracing（描摹；追踪）}
  \wordphrase{traces of an ancient civilization（古代文明的踪迹）}
  \wordphrase{mere trace of a smile（淡淡的微笑）}
  \wordphrase{trace of fear（一丝恐惧）}
  \wordphrase{trace element（微量元素）}
  \wordexample{traces of an ancient civilization（古代文明的踪迹）}
  \wordexample{mere trace of a smile（淡淡的微笑）}
  \wordexample{trace of fear（一丝恐惧）}
  \wordexample{trace element（微量元素）}
  \wordexample{Archaeologists have traced many Roman roads in Britain.（考古学家们在英国发掘出很多古罗马人留下的道路。）}
  \wordexample{His fear of dogs traces back to a childhood experience.（他对狗的恐惧源于他童年的经历。）}
\end{word}

  \wordphrase{roses trailing over the walls（蔓过围墙的蔷薇）}
  \wordexample{trail a wild animal / criminal（追踪野兽／罪犯）}

  \wordexample{Smoke trailed from the chimney.（烟雾从烟囱里袅袅飘出。）}

\begin{word}[***]{treat}{tri:t}

  \wordexample{treat a case of rheumatism（医治风湿病患者）}
  \wordexample{treat her friends to oysters and champagne（用牡蛎和香槟招待她的朋友们）}
  \wordexample{It is your treat today.（今天该你请客了。）}
  \wordexample{This is to be my treat.（这次我请客。）}
  \wordexample{It is a treat to see you.（很高兴见到你。）}
\end{word}

\chapter{VAC[VAS]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根 VAC[VAS] 来自拉丁语的 vac 意为 empty，它的变形为 vas。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 van，void 以及来自盎格鲁-撒克逊语的 wan。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{devastate}{'devasteit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VAC[VAS]}
  \wordfamily{devastation（破坏）；vacate（腾出屋子）；vacillate（犹豫不决）}
  \wordsense{v. 蹂躏；使荒芜 *de (= intensive) + vas(vac (= empty)}
  \wordphrase{The tornado devastated a}
  \wordexample{The tornado devastated a 4-block area of the small town.（那场龙卷风使小村庄的四个街区遭到了破坏。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{vacate}{və'keit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VAC[VAS]}
  \wordfamily{vacancy（空房，空地，空白）；vacant（空的）；devastation（破坏）；devastate（蹂躏）}
  \wordsense{vt. 腾出屋子，搬出；退位；退出，让出 *vac (= empty)}
  \wordphrase{vacate the issue}
  \wordexample{The school was vacated for 2 hours while the police searched for a bomb.（在警方搜寻炸弹期间，学校清场两小时。）}
  \wordexample{The tornado devastated a 4-block area of the small town.（那场龙卷风使小村庄的四个街区遭到了破坏。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{vacillate}{'væsəleit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VAC[VAS]}
  \wordfamily{vacillation（踌躇，犹豫）；devastate（蹂躏）；vacate（腾出屋子）}
  \wordsense{vi. 犹豫不决，摇摆，踌躇 *vac (= empty)}
  \wordphrase{During my teenage years}
  \wordexample{During my teenage years my singing voice vacillated between baritone and tenor.（在我十几岁的时候，我的嗓音总是在男中音和男高音之间变换不定。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{evacuate}{i'vækjueit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VAC[VAS]}
  \wordfamily{排泄（物））；evacuee（避难民，被疏散者）；devastate（蹂躏）}
  \wordsense{v. （特指军人）从...撤退；使疏散，使避难；排空（胃或肠） *e(ex (= out)}
  \wordphrase{evacuate a fort（撤离要塞）}
  \wordexample{evacuate a fort（撤离要塞）}
  \wordexample{The children were evacuated to the country.（孩子们被送到乡下避难。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{vacancy}{'veikənsi}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VAC[VAS]}
  \wordsense{n. 空；空间；空虚；空白；空房，空地；心不在焉（的状态）；空职，空缺；空额}
  \wordfamily{fill (up)}
  \wordfamily{a vacancy（填补空缺）}
  \wordfamily{evacuation（撤离，撤退；疏散；排泄（物））}
  \wordfamily{evacuee（避难民，被疏散者）}
  \wordfamily{vacancy*** n. 空；空间；空虚；空白；空房，空地；心不在焉（的状态）；空职，空缺；空额}
  \wordphrase{look into vacancy（失神地望着）}
  \wordphrase{good vacancies for typists and clerks（优厚的打字员和办事员的空缺）}
  \wordphrase{three vacancies in the apartment（公寓里的三间空房）}
  \wordphrase{evacuate a fort（撤离要塞）}
  \wordexample{look into vacancy（失神地望着）}
  \wordexample{good vacancies for typists and clerks（优厚的打字员和办事员的空缺）}
  \wordexample{three vacancies in the apartment（公寓里的三间空房）}
  \wordexample{I'd like to make a reservation for tonight. Do you have any vacancies?（我想今晚订一间房，请问还有空房吗？）}
  \wordexample{The children were evacuated to the country.（孩子们被送到乡下避难。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{vacant}{'veikənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VAC[VAS]}
  \wordsense{a. 空白的，空的，空着的；（时间）空闲的，清闲的；（心灵）空虚的；（眼神）茫然的}
  \wordfamily{vacancy}
  \wordfamily{vacation}
  \wordfamily{evacuate}
  \wordfamily{vacuous}
  \wordphrase{vacant space（未使用的空间）}
  \wordphrase{vacant room（空房间）}
  \wordphrase{vacant position（空职位）}
  \wordphrase{vacant possession（空屋（或空地）的合法占有）}
  \wordphrase{vacant expression / stare（茫然的表情／茫然的凝视）}
  \wordexample{vacant space（未使用的空间）}
  \wordexample{vacant room（空房间）}
  \wordexample{vacant position（空职位）}
  \wordexample{vacant possession（空屋（或空地）的合法占有）}
  \wordexample{vacant expression / stare（茫然的表情／茫然的凝视）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{vacation}{və'keiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VAC[VAS]}
  \wordfamily{vacationist（休假者）；devastate（蹂躏）；vacate（腾出屋子）}
  \wordsense{n. （大学的）假期；（法庭的）休庭期；休假日，休假；（特指避暑、避寒等的）休假旅行；（房屋等的）空出；辞职}
  \wordphrase{on vacation（休假中）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "on vacation".（常见搭配：休假中。）}
  \wordsense{v. 度假}
  \wordphrase{vacation of a good}
  \wordexample{His vacation of a good position in the Civil Service was unwise.（他辞去不错的公务员工作非明智之举。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{vacuous}{'vækjuəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VAC[VAS]}
  \wordsense{a. 发呆的；愚蠢的；茫然的；没有意义的；漫无目的的}
  \wordfamily{vacuously（直愣愣地；愚蠢地）}
  \wordfamily{vacuity（心不在焉；空虚；愚蠢；空洞无聊的话（或行为））}
  \wordfamily{be vacuous（发呆的）}
  \wordphrase{be vacuous（发呆的）}
  \wordexample{be vacuous（发呆的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{vacuum}{'vækjuəm}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VAC[VAS]}
  \wordfamily{devastate（蹂躏）；vacate（腾出屋子）；vacillate（犹豫不决）}
  \wordsense{n. 真空}
  \wordphrase{vacuum cleaner（真空吸尘器）}
  \wordphrase{vacuum flask / bottle（保温瓶，热水瓶）}
  \wordphrase{vacuum pump（真空泵，真空抽气机）}
  \wordexample{vacuum cleaner（真空吸尘器）}
  \wordexample{vacuum flask / bottle（保温瓶，热水瓶）}
  \wordexample{vacuum pump（真空泵，真空抽气机）}
\end{word}



\chapter{SPOND, SPONS[SPOUS]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根 SPOND, SPONS[SPOUS] 来自拉丁语的 spond, spons 意为 to promise, to answer，它的变形为 spous。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{despondent}{di'spɔndənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPOND, SPONS[SPOUS]}
  \wordfamily{despondency（/ despondence 失望，沮丧，意志消沉）；despondently（意志消沉地）；responsive（回答的）}
  \wordsense{a. 失望的，沮丧的 *de (= from) + spond (= promise)}
  \wordphrase{Despondent after a long}
  \wordexample{Despondent after a long illness, he took his own life last Tuesday.（长期患病使他意志消沉，上星期二他自杀了。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{responsive}{ri'spɔnsiv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPOND, SPONS[SPOUS]}
  \wordfamily{respond（应答）；response（应答）；respondent（被告）；despondent（失望的）}
  \wordsense{a. 回答的，回应的；易于感动的 *re (= back) + spons (= answer, promise, pledge)}
  \wordphrase{responsive in class that}
  \wordexample{The student was especially responsive in class that day.（这个学生在那天的课堂上表现得特别活跃。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{correspond}{,kɔrə'spɔnd}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPOND, SPONS[SPOUS]}
  \wordsense{v. (\textasciitilde{} to) 与...对等（同等），（地位等）与...相当；与...一致，符合；通信 *cor<com (= together), re (= again)}
  \wordfamily{responsive}
  \wordfamily{corresponding}
  \wordfamily{despondent}
  \wordfamily{correspondence}
  \wordphrase{corresponds to the British}
  \wordexample{The U.S. Congress corresponds to the British Parliament.（美国的国会相当于英国的议会。）}
  \wordexample{His actions do not correspond with his words.（他的言行不一致。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{corresponding}{,kɔrə'spɔndiŋ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPOND, SPONS[SPOUS]}
  \wordfamily{despondent（失望的）；responsive（回答的）；correspond（(\textasciitilde{} to) 与）}
  \wordsense{a. 对应的，相当的；符合的，一致的；通信的 *cor<com (= together), re (= again)}
  \wordphrase{corresponding period of last year（去年这个时候）}
  \wordphrase{corresponding member（（学会等的）通信会员）}
  \wordexample{corresponding period of last year（去年这个时候）}
  \wordexample{corresponding member（（学会等的）通信会员）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{correspondence}{,kɔrə'spɔndəns}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPOND, SPONS[SPOUS]}
  \wordfamily{despondent（失望的）；responsive（回答的）；correspond（(\textasciitilde{} to) 与）}
  \wordsense{n. 一致，相似，对应；通信联系；信件 *cor<com (= together), re (= again)}
  \wordphrase{correspondence course / school（函授课程／函授学校）}
  \wordphrase{correspondence column（（报纸上的）读者来信专栏）}
  \wordphrase{get into correspondence with（开始与...通信联系）}
  \wordexample{correspondence course / school（函授课程／函授学校）}
  \wordexample{correspondence column（（报纸上的）读者来信专栏）}
  \wordexample{get into correspondence with（开始与...通信联系）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{correspondent}{,kɔrə'spɔndənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPOND, SPONS[SPOUS]}
  \wordfamily{good（/ bad (negligent)）；despondent（失望的）；responsive（回答的）}
  \wordsense{n. 通信者；（报社的）特派员，（特指远地区的）通讯记者（定期向报社投稿，提供地方新闻或特别报道）；客户 *cor<com (= together), re (= again)}
  \wordphrase{our Moscow correspondent（本社莫斯科通讯记者）}
  \wordphrase{foreign / war correspondent（海外特派员／战地记者）}
  \wordexample{our Moscow correspondent（本社莫斯科通讯记者）}
  \wordexample{foreign / war correspondent（海外特派员／战地记者）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{respond}{ri'spɔnd}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPOND, SPONS[SPOUS]}
  \wordfamily{correspondent；response；correspondence；respondent；despondent（失望的）}
  \wordsense{v. 应答，回答；反应；（礼拜时会众的）例行应答 *re (= again)}
  \wordphrase{to respond（应答，回答）}
  \wordexample{to respond（应答，回答）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{response}{ri'spɔns}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPOND, SPONS[SPOUS]}
  \wordfamily{respond；respondent；correspondent；responsible；despondent（失望的）}
  \wordsense{n. 应答，回答；反应；（礼拜时会众的）应答（或唱和）短诗 *re (= again)}
  \wordphrase{in response to（对...反应，响应...）}
  \wordexample{in response to（对...反应，响应...）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{respondent}{ri'spɔndənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPOND, SPONS[SPOUS]}
  \wordfamily{response；responsible；respond；responsibility；despondent（失望的）}
  \wordsense{n. 回答者；（离婚、上诉等案件中的）被告 *re (= again)}
  \wordphrase{a respondent（回答者）}
  \wordexample{a respondent（回答者）}

\begin{word}[***]{responsible}{ri'spɔnsəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPOND, SPONS[SPOUS]}
  \wordfamily{respondent；responsibility；response；irresponsible；despondent（失望的）}
  \wordsense{a. 有责任的，应负责任的；责任重大的；可信赖的；（be responsible for）作为...原因的，对...负责的 *re (= again)}
  \wordphrase{responsible government（负责任的政府）}
  \wordexample{responsible government（负责任的政府）}
  \wordexample{Bad workmanship was responsible for the collapse of the block of flats.（施工质量差导致了公寓楼的坍塌。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{responsibility}{ri'spɔnsə'biləti}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPOND, SPONS[SPOUS]}

  \wordsense{n. 责任，任务，义务 *re (= again)}
  \wordphrase{a responsibility（责任，任务，义务）}
  \wordexample{a responsibility（责任，任务，义务）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{irresponsible}{,iri'spɔnsəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SPOND, SPONS[SPOUS]}
  \wordfamily{responsibility；sponsor；responsible；spouse；despondent（失望的）}
  \wordsense{a. 不承担责任的；不负责任的，无责任感的，不可靠的；无责任能力的 *ir<in (= not), re (= again)}
  \wordphrase{irresponsible child（无责任能力的孩子）}
  \wordphrase{irresponsible teenagers（无责任感的十几岁的孩子）}
  \wordphrase{irresponsible behavior（不负责任的行为）}
  \wordexample{irresponsible child（无责任能力的孩子）}
  \wordexample{irresponsible teenagers（无责任感的十几岁的孩子）}
  \wordexample{irresponsible behavior（不负责任的行为）}

  \wordphrase{a sponsor（保证人）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "a sponsor".（常见搭配：保证人。）}
  \wordsense{v. 赞助，发起，资助；成为（商业节目的）赞助商}
  \wordphrase{a sponsor（保证人）}
  \wordexample{a sponsor（保证人）}

  \wordexample{an espousal（支持，信奉）}

\chapter{TEST}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根 TEST 来自拉丁语的 test 意为 witness。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{detest}{di'test}
  \wordbreakdown{在眼前看到脱离正常的事物 de (= from) + test (= witness)}
  \wordsense{vt. 厌恶，憎恶}
  \wordfamily{detestation 厌恶，憎恶}
  \wordfamily{detestable 令人厌恶的}
  \wordphrase{detest the taste}
  \wordexample{Many children detest the taste of Brussels sprouts.（许多小孩子都讨厌芽甘蓝的味道。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{attest}{ə'test}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TEST}
  \wordfamily{contest；intestate；pretest；protest；detest（厌恶）}
  \wordsense{v. 证明，证实，是…的证据；（使）发誓 *at<ad (= to)}
  \wordphrase{attest a signature（证明签名是真的）}
  \wordexample{feats which attest to his strength of will（可证明他坚强意志的功绩）}
  \wordexample{attest a signature（证明签名是真的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{contest}{'kɔntest}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TEST}
  \wordfamily{contestant（竞争对手，竞争者）；detest（厌恶）；attest（证明）}
  \wordsense{n. 竞争，竞赛；争论，论战，争辩}
  \wordphrase{the contest}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of contest.（此处展示 contest 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. 竞赛，争夺，竞争；争论，争辩 *con<com (= together)}
  \wordphrase{three-round featherweight contest（（拳击）三轮的次轻量级选手比赛）}
  \wordexample{beauty / musical / speech contest（选美比赛／音乐比赛／演讲比赛）}
  \wordexample{three-round featherweight contest（（拳击）三轮的次轻量级选手比赛）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{intestate}{in'testeit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TEST}
  \wordfamily{detest（厌恶）；attest（证明）；contest（竞争）}
  \wordsense{a. 未留遗嘱的 *in (= not)}
  \wordphrase{be intestate（未留遗嘱的）}
  \wordexample{be intestate（未留遗嘱的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{pretest}{'pri:test}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TEST}
  \wordfamily{intestate；protest；contest；protestation；detest（厌恶）}
  \wordsense{n. 预备考试 *pre (= before)}
  \wordphrase{a pretest（预备考试）}
  \wordexample{a pretest（预备考试）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{protest}{prə'test}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TEST}
  \wordfamily{pretest；protestation；intestate；protestant；detest（厌恶）}
  \wordsense{v. 抗议，提出异议；断言，主张}
  \wordphrase{protest the issue}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of protest.（此处展示 protest 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 抗议，异议；主张 *pro (= forth, forward)}
  \wordphrase{protest movement（抗议运动）}
  \wordphrase{protest march（抗议游行）}
  \wordexample{protest movement（抗议运动）}
  \wordexample{protest march（抗议游行）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{protestation}{ˌprɒtə'steiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TEST}
  \wordfamily{protest；protestant；pretest；testament；detest（厌恶）}
  \wordsense{n. 断言，声明，主张 *pro (= forth, forward)}
  \wordphrase{protestations of innocence / friendship（声明无罪／友好宣言）}
  \wordexample{protestations of innocence / friendship（声明无罪／友好宣言）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{protestant}{'prɒtistənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TEST}
  \wordfamily{Protestantism（新教，新教教义）；detest（厌恶）；attest（证明）}
  \wordsense{a. 新教的，新教徒的}
  \wordphrase{be protestant}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of protestant.（此处展示 protestant 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 新教徒 *pro (= forth, forward)}
  \wordphrase{Protestant Reformation（新教改革）}
  \wordexample{Protestant Reformation（新教改革）}
\end{word}

  \wordfamily{testamentary（遗嘱的，据遗嘱的）}
  \wordfamily{a testament（遗嘱）}
  \wordphrase{a testament（遗嘱）}
  \wordexample{a testament（遗嘱）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{testamur}{te'steimə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TEST}
  \wordsense{n.（英国大学）考试及格证书}

  \wordfamily{testate}

  \wordfamily{testicle}
  \wordfamily{a testamur（考试及格证书）}
  \wordphrase{a testamur（考试及格证书）}
  \wordexample{a testamur（考试及格证书）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{testate}{'testeɪt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TEST}
  \wordsense{a. / n. 留有遗嘱的（人）}
  \wordfamily{testator（立遗嘱者）}
  \wordfamily{testatrix（女遗嘱人）}
  \wordfamily{be testate（/ n. 留有遗嘱的（人））}
  \wordphrase{be testate（/ n. 留有遗嘱的（人））}
  \wordexample{be testate（/ n. 留有遗嘱的（人））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{testicle}{'testikl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TEST}
  \wordfamily{testate；testify；testamur；testimonial；detest（厌恶）}
  \wordsense{n. （pl.）睾丸 *cle (= small)}
  \wordphrase{a testicle（睾丸）}
  \wordexample{a testicle（睾丸）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{testify}{'testifai}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TEST}
  \wordfamily{testicle；testimonial；testate；testimony；detest（厌恶）}
  \wordsense{v. 作证，证实，是…的证据，证明 *ify (= make)}
  \wordphrase{to testify（作证，证实，是…的证据，证明）}
  \wordexample{to testify（作证，证实，是…的证据，证明）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{testimonial}{ˌtesti'məuniəl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TEST}
  \wordsense{n.（能力、资格等的）证明书，推荐信；感谢信，表扬信；奖状，纪念品}
  \wordfamily{testify}
  \wordfamily{testimony}
  \wordfamily{testicle}
  \wordfamily{testate}
  \wordfamily{a testimonial（证明书，推荐信）}
  \wordphrase{a testimonial（证明书，推荐信）}
  \wordexample{a testimonial（证明书，推荐信）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{testimony}{'testiməuni}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TEST}
  \wordfamily{ced（/ cess [ceed, ceas]，it，vad / vas 和来自希腊语的）；bat（[bet, bit]，以及来自盎格鲁-撒克逊语的）；detest（厌恶）}
  \wordsense{n. 【律】证词；（信仰等的）声明}
  \wordphrase{give false testimony（作伪证）}
  \wordexample{give false testimony（作伪证）}
  \wordexample{His poverty is a testimony to his innocence.（他的穷困证明他是清白的。）}
\end{word}



\chapter{GRAD, GRESS[GRED]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根 GRAD, GRESS[GRED] 来自拉丁语的 grad, gress 意为 to go，它们的变形为 gred。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 ced / cess [ceed, ceas]，it，vad / vas 和来自希腊语的 bat [bet, bit]，以及来自盎格鲁-撒克逊语的 fare。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{degrade}{di'greid}
  \wordbreakdown{使向下走 de (= down) + grad (= go)}
  \wordfamily{degradation（降级）；ingredient（（混合物的）成分）；digress（from + sth（话、文章等）离题）}
  \wordsense{vt. 使降级，降低…的地位；使堕落，降低…的品格}
  \wordphrase{The financial scandal degraded}
  \wordexample{The financial scandal degraded the bank officers involved.（卷入此次金融丑闻的银行官员们都被降了级。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{ingredient}{in'gri:diənt}
  \wordbreakdown{进入某物内部 in (= in, into) + gred<grad (= go, step)}
  \wordsense{n.（混合物的）成分，材料；要素，要因}
  \wordfamily{degrade}
  \wordfamily{digress}
  \wordfamily{transgress}
  \wordfamily{centigrade}
  \wordfamily{ingredients}
  \wordphrase{ingredients}
  \wordexample{Delicious homemade ice cream can be made with a few simple ingredients.（用几样简单的材料就能做出美味的家庭自制冰淇淋。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{digress}{dai'gres}
  \wordbreakdown{分开走 di<dis (= apart) + gress (= go, step)}
  \wordfamily{digression（离题，脱轨）；degrade（使降级）；ingredient（（混合物的）成分）}
  \wordsense{vi. from + sth（话、文章等）离题；走向岔道}
  \wordphrase{digress the way you}
  \wordexample{Tell me what happened, and don't digress the way you usually do.（告诉我发生什么事了，别像你平时那样说跑题了。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{transgress}{trænz'gres}
  \wordbreakdown{越过去 trans (= over, across) + gress (= go)}
  \wordsense{vt. 超过（限制、范围）；违反（法律、条约、合同）}
  \wordfamily{transgression 违反，违法，（宗教、道德上的）罪}
  \wordfamily{transgressor 违反者，罪人}
  \wordphrase{transgress the law but}
  \wordexample{It's not my habit to transgress the law, but I do smoke marijuana now and then.（我不是违法成性，不过我确实偶尔会吸大麻。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{centigrade}{'sentigreid}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 GRAD, GRESS[GRED]}
  \wordfamily{degrade（使降级）；ingredient（（混合物的）成分）；digress（from + sth（话、文章等）离题）}
  \wordsense{a. （温度计上）百分度的；摄氏的 *cent (= hundred)}
  \wordphrase{centigrade thermometer（摄氏温度计）}
  \wordphrase{thirty degrees centigrade（30 摄氏度）}
  \wordexample{centigrade thermometer（摄氏温度计）}
  \wordexample{thirty degrees centigrade（30 摄氏度）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{gradation}{grə'deiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 GRAD, GRESS[GRED]}
  \wordsense{n. 阶段性变化，逐渐的变化；（发展的）阶段；层次；程度}
  \wordfamily{centigrade}
  \wordfamily{grade}
  \wordfamily{gradient}
  \wordfamily{gradual}
  \wordphrase{gradation of color in the rainbow（彩虹色彩的逐渐变化）}
  \wordexample{gradation of color in the rainbow（彩虹色彩的逐渐变化）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{grade}{greid}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 GRAD, GRESS[GRED]}
  \wordfamily{grader（…年级学生）；degrade（使降级）；ingredient（（混合物的）成分）}
  \wordsense{n. 等级；程度；（中、小学的）年级；成绩，分数；倾斜（度）}
  \wordphrase{tenth grade（十年级）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "tenth grade".（常见搭配：十年级。）}
  \wordsense{v. 分等级；给（答案等）评分；把（路面等）筑平}
  \wordphrase{grade mark（货品质量等级标记）}
  \wordphrase{a seventh grader（七年级学生）}
  \wordphrase{Grade A（最高级的，一流的）}
  \wordexample{grade mark（货品质量等级标记）}
  \wordexample{a seventh grader（七年级学生）}
  \wordexample{Grade A（最高级的，一流的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{gradient}{'greidiənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 GRAD, GRESS[GRED]}
  \wordfamily{degrade（使降级）；ingredient（（混合物的）成分）；digress（from + sth（话、文章等）离题）}
  \wordsense{n. 倾斜度；坡度；斜坡}
  \wordphrase{a gradient（倾斜度）}
  \wordexample{a gradient（倾斜度）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{gradual}{'grædʒuəl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 GRAD, GRESS[GRED]}
  \wordsense{a. 逐渐的，阶段性的，循序渐进的；（坡度）平缓的}
  \wordfamily{gradually（渐渐地，逐渐地）}
  \wordfamily{gradualism（渐进主义）}
  \wordfamily{be gradual（逐渐的，阶段性的，循序渐进的）}
  \wordphrase{be gradual（逐渐的，阶段性的，循序渐进的）}
  \wordexample{be gradual（逐渐的，阶段性的，循序渐进的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{graduate}{'grædʒuət}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 GRAD, GRESS[GRED]}
  \wordfamily{degrade（使降级）；ingredient（（混合物的）成分）；digress（from + sth（话、文章等）离题）}
  \wordsense{n. 毕业生；学位获得者}
  \wordphrase{graduated ruler（标有刻度的尺子）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "graduated ruler".（常见搭配：标有刻度的尺子。）}
  \wordsense{a. 有学位的；毕业的；研究生的}
  \wordphrase{graduated ruler（标有刻度的尺子）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "graduated ruler".（常见搭配：标有刻度的尺子。）}
  \wordsense{v. 毕业；（在…上）标刻度；分等级；（蒸发）浓缩}
  \wordphrase{high school graduates（高中毕业生）}
  \wordphrase{graduate school（研究生院）}
  \wordphrase{Graduate Record Examination（（缩写：GRE）美国研究生入学资格考试）}
  \wordexample{high school graduates（高中毕业生）}
  \wordexample{graduate school（研究生院）}
  \wordexample{Graduate Record Examination（（缩写：GRE）美国研究生入学资格考试）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{postgraduate}{ˌpəust'grædʒuət}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 GRAD, GRESS[GRED]}

  \wordexample{postgraduate school（《英》研究生院）}
  \wordexample{postgraduate student（《英》研究生）}

  \wordphrase{undergraduate work / studies（大学生的学业）}

  \wordexample{undergraduate work / studies（大学生的学业）}
  \wordexample{in my undergraduate days（我上大学的时候）}

  \wordphrase{retrograde motion（逆行）}
  \wordphrase{retrograde policy（倒退的政策）}
  \wordphrase{retrograde cancer（退行性癌症）}
  \wordexample{retrograde motion（逆行）}
  \wordexample{retrograde policy（倒退的政策）}

  \wordexample{on the upgrade（改善的，上升的）}

  \wordexample{war of aggression（侵略战争）}

\begin{word}[***]{aggressive}{ə'gresiv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 GRAD, GRESS[GRED]}

\begin{word}[***]{congress}{'kɔŋgres}

  \wordfamily{Congressman（《美》国会议员（尤指众议员））}

  \wordfamily{AFL（the American Federation of Labor 美国劳工联合会）合并后改称为}
  \wordfamily{AFL-CIO，即美国劳联-产联））}

  \wordphrase{the Congress of Industrial Organizations（产业工会联合会（缩写：CIO，1955 年与}

  \wordexample{the Congress of Industrial Organizations（产业工会联合会（缩写：CIO，1955 年与}

  \wordphrase{means of ingress（入口）}
  \wordexample{means of ingress（入口）}

  \wordbreakdown{词根 GRAD, GRESS[GRED]}

  \wordphrase{The Pilgrim's Progress（《天路历程》（1678 年英国作家}
  \wordexample{The Pilgrim's Progress（《天路历程》（1678 年英国作家}
\end{word}

  \wordbreakdown{词根 GRAD, GRESS[GRED]}

  \wordsense{n. 进步，发展；前进，进行；【数】级数 *pro (= forward)}
  \wordphrase{in geometrical progression（以几何级数）}
  \wordexample{in geometrical progression（以几何级数）}

\begin{word}[***]{progressive}{prə'gresiv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 GRAD, GRESS[GRED]}

  \wordphrase{be progressive}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of progressive.（此处展示 progressive 的常见用法。）}

  \wordphrase{progressive taxation（累进税制）}
  \wordexample{make a progressive advance（进步）}

  \wordphrase{to regress（回归）}

\chapter{PLOR}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根 PLOR 来自拉丁语的 plor 意为 to weep。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{deplore}{di'plɔ:(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{非常伤心地哭 de (= intensive) + plor (= weep)}
  \wordsense{vt. 悲叹，哀叹；深感遗憾；谴责，强烈反对}
  \wordfamily{deplorable 可叹的，可怜的}
  \wordfamily{deplorably 可叹地}
  \wordphrase{deplore the recent crime}
  \wordexample{The neighborhood residents deplore the recent crime wave.（该地区的居民哀叹近来犯罪事件剧增。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{implore}{im'plɔ:(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{哗哗地流泪 im<in (= intensive) + plor (= weep)}
  \wordsense{vt. 恳求，哀求}
  \wordfamily{imploration 恳求，哀求}
  \wordfamily{imploring 哀求的}
  \wordfamily{imploringly 哀求地}
  \wordphrase{The woman implored the}
  \wordexample{The woman implored the doctors to locate a liver donor for her dying son.（那个女人哀求医生为她垂死的儿子寻找一个肝脏捐赠者。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{explore}{ik'splɔ:(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PLOR}
  \wordfamily{deplore（悲叹）；implore（恳求）；exploration（探险）}
  \wordsense{v. 探险；勘探，考察；仔细研究 *ex (= out)}
  \wordphrase{explore the Arctic regions（考察北极地区）}
  \wordphrase{explore possibilities / problems（研究可能性／问题）}
  \wordexample{explore the Arctic regions（考察北极地区）}
  \wordexample{explore possibilities / problems（研究可能性／问题）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{exploration}{ˌeksplə'reiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PLOR}
  \wordfamily{spect（/ spec [spic, spi, spy]，the，tu 和来自希腊语的）；eye（的）；deplore（悲叹）}
  \wordsense{n. 探险；勘查；探究 *ex (= out)}
  \wordphrase{exploration of the ocean depths（勘探海洋深度）}
  \wordexample{exploration of the ocean depths（勘探海洋深度）}
\end{word}



\chapter{VID, VIS[VIC, VIEW, VOY, VEY, VEI, VY, VI, UD]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根 VID, VIS[VIC, VIEW, VOY, VEY, VEI, VY, VI, UD] 来自拉丁语的 vid, vis，意为 to look, to see，它们的变形为 vic, view, voy, vey / vei, vy / vi, ud 等。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 spect / spec [spic, spi, spy]，the，tu 和来自希腊语的 orama，scop，以及来自盎格鲁-撒克逊语的 see 等。此外，还有来自拉丁语的意为 eye 的 ocul，来自希腊语的 op(t), ophthalm 等，含义相似。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{devise}{di'vaiz}
  \wordbreakdown{向下看 de (= down) + vis (= look)}
  \wordsense{vt. 设计；计划；遗赠（财产）}
  \wordfamily{device 设计；装置，设备，仪器}
  \wordphrase{devise a solution if}
  \wordexample{I'm sure we can devise a solution if we just put our minds to it.（我相信如果我们努力去想，就一定能想出解决办法。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{envisage}{in'vizidʒ}
  \wordbreakdown{在内部看 en (= in) + vis (= look)}
  \wordsense{vt. 设想，想像}
  \wordfamily{devise}
  \wordfamily{improvident}
  \wordfamily{improvise}
  \wordfamily{invidious}
  \wordphrase{I envisaged living in}
  \wordexample{I envisaged living in a beautiful home like this for many years.（很多年来我一直想像着住在这样漂亮的房子里。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{improvident}{im'prɔvidənt}
  \wordbreakdown{无法向前看的 im<in (= not) + pro (= before, forth) + vid (= look)}
  \wordfamily{improvidence（无远见）；improvidently（无远见地）；devise（设计）}
  \wordsense{a. 无远见的；浪费的}
  \wordphrase{improvident nations}
  \wordexample{Some people criticize the IMF for funding improvident nations.（一些人批评国际货币基金组织给那些浪费资金的国家提供资金。）}
  \wordexample{improvident nations}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{improvise}{'imprəvaiz}
  \wordbreakdown{事先不看就做出某事物 im<in (= not) + pro (= before) + vis (= look)}
  \wordsense{v. 即兴创作（诗歌、乐曲）；临时准备}
  \wordfamily{improvisation 即席之作}
  \wordfamily{improviser 即兴诗人，即兴演奏家}
  \wordphrase{improvises entire public performances}
  \wordexample{Keith Jarrett, the famous pianist, improvises entire public performances.（著名钢琴家基思·贾勒特的所有公演都是即兴表演。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{invidious}{in'vidiəs}
  \wordbreakdown{朝内看的 in (= in, on, against) + vid (= look)}
  \wordsense{a.（因不公平而）招致不满的，引起反感的}
  \wordfamily{improvise}
  \wordfamily{visionary}
  \wordfamily{improvident}
  \wordfamily{preview}
  \wordphrase{invidious comments destroyed any}
  \wordexample{Her invidious comments destroyed any chance for the negotiation to succeed.（她那令人不快的言论使谈判完全失去了成功的可能性。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{visionary}{'viʒənri}
  \wordbreakdown{和看到的事物相关联的 vis (= look)}
  \wordfamily{invidious；preview；improvise；purview；devise（设计）}
  \wordsense{a. 幻想的，空想的；不切实际的；富于想像力的}
  \wordphrase{be visionary}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of visionary.（此处展示 visionary 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 空想家，幻想家}
  \wordphrase{visionary author}
  \wordexample{Jules Verne has often been hailed as a remarkably visionary author.（朱尔斯·维恩常常被人们赞许为拥有惊人想像力的作家。）}
  \wordexample{visionary author}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{preview}{'pri:vju:}
  \wordbreakdown{事先看 pre (= before) + view (= look)}
  \wordfamily{visionary；purview；invidious；clairvoyant；devise（设计）}
  \wordsense{n. 电影等的试映，预演}
  \wordphrase{the preview}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of preview.（此处展示 preview 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 试映，预演}
  \wordphrase{preview and loved it}
  \wordexample{I first saw Raiders of the Lost Ark at a sneak preview and loved it!（我第一次看《夺宝奇兵》是在电影试映会上，那部电影真是太棒了！）}
  \wordexample{preview and loved it}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{purview}{'pə:vju:}
  \wordbreakdown{向前看 pur<pro (= forth) + view (= look)}
  \wordsense{n. 范围；视野，眼界；要项，条款}
  \wordfamily{preview}
  \wordfamily{clairvoyant}
  \wordfamily{visionary}
  \wordfamily{surveillance}
  \wordphrase{purview extends}
  \wordexample{The program's purview extends to all persons who reside in on-campus housing.（该项目条款涉及居住在校园内的所有人。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{clairvoyant}{kleə'vɔiənt}
  \wordbreakdown{看起来很清楚或很透明 clair<clar (= clear) + voy (= look)}
  \wordfamily{clairvoyance（超人的视力）；devise（设计）；envisage（设想）}
  \wordsense{a. 有超人视力的；有洞察力的}
  \wordphrase{be clairvoyant}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of clairvoyant.（此处展示 clairvoyant 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 千里眼；有洞察力的人}
  \wordphrase{clairvoyant counselor}
  \wordexample{She bills herself as a clairvoyant counselor.（她在海报上宣传自己是一个有洞察力的顾问。）}
  \wordexample{clairvoyant counselor}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{surveillance}{sə:'veiləns}
  \wordbreakdown{从上往下看 sur (= over) + vei (= look)}
  \wordsense{n.（对嫌疑者等的）监视；监督；检查}
  \wordfamily{surveying 测量}
  \wordfamily{surveyor 测量员；鉴定人}
  \wordfamily{survey 俯瞰；测量；调查；（n.）['sə:vei] 概观；俯瞰；测量；调查}
  \wordphrase{surveillance}
  \wordexample{A team of 6 agents kept the suspect under constant surveillance.（六名特工成立了一个小组，对嫌疑人进行连续的监视。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{prudent}{'pru:dnt}
  \wordbreakdown{事先看的 pr<pro (= before) + ud<vid (= look)}
  \wordfamily{prudence（谨慎）；prudential（谨慎的，小心的）；devise（设计）}
  \wordsense{a. 谨慎的，小心的；明智的，有判断力的}
  \wordphrase{prudent to wait until}
  \wordexample{It would be prudent to wait until you've saved more money to buy the car.（等你多攒一点钱再买车，那样比较明智。）}
  \wordexample{prudent to wait until}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{advise}{əd'vaiz}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VID, VIS[VIC, VIEW, VOY, VEY, VEI, VY, VI, UD]}
  \wordfamily{advised（仔细考虑过的，细想过的）；advisedly（深思熟虑地）；well-advised（谨慎的，稳妥的）；devise（设计）}
  \wordsense{v. 忠告，建议 *ad (= to)}
  \wordphrase{to advise（忠告，建议）}
  \wordexample{to advise（忠告，建议）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{advice}{əd'vais}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VID, VIS[VIC, VIEW, VOY, VEY, VEI, VY, VI, UD]}
  \wordfamily{advise；advisable；advisory；adviser；devise（设计）}
  \wordsense{n. 忠告，建议，劝告；【商】通知 *ad (= to), vic<vis (= look)}
