\chapter{VOID}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根 VOID 来自拉丁语的 void 意为 empty，同义词根有来自拉丁语的 vac，van 以及来自盎格鲁-撒克逊语的 wan 等。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{devoid}{di'vɔid}
  \wordbreakdown{完全空着的 de (= intensive) + void (= empty)}
  \wordsense{a. 没有…的；缺乏的}
  \wordfamily{avoid}
  \wordfamily{avoidance}
  \wordfamily{void}
  \wordphrase{devoid of color}
  \wordexample{The lunar landscape is devoid of color.（月球上的风景是没有颜色的。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{avoid}{ə'vɔid}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VOID}
  \wordfamily{avoidable（可以避免的）；devoid（没有…的）；avoidance（回避 *a (= off)）}
  \wordsense{v. 避免，逃避 *a (= off)}
  \wordphrase{to avoid（避免，逃避）}
  \wordexample{to avoid（避免，逃避）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{avoidance}{ə'vɔidəns}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VOID}
  \wordfamily{avoid；void；devoid（没有…的）}
  \wordsense{n. 回避 *a (= off)}
  \wordphrase{the avoidance of bad companions（回避不好的朋友）}
  \wordphrase{avoidance of taxation（避税）}
  \wordexample{the avoidance of bad companions（回避不好的朋友）}
  \wordexample{avoidance of taxation（避税）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{void}{vɔid}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VOID}
  \wordfamily{avoidance；avoid；devoid（没有…的）}
  \wordsense{a. 空的；空闲的；（\textasciitilde{}of）没有的，缺乏的；【律】无效的}
  \wordphrase{be void}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of void.（此处展示 void 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 空间；空虚感；空地}
  \wordphrase{the void}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of void.（此处展示 void 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. 使无效}
  \wordphrase{void hours（空闲时间）}
  \wordphrase{subject void of interest（乏味的主题）}
  \wordphrase{null and void（无效的）}
  \wordexample{void hours（空闲时间）}
  \wordexample{subject void of interest（乏味的主题）}
  \wordexample{null and void（无效的）}
\end{word}



\chapter{CREP}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 `crep` 意为 `to rattle`。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{decrepit}{di'krepit}
  \wordbreakdown{出声变小的 de (= down) + crep (= rattle)}
  \wordfamily{decrepitude（衰老，衰朽）；discrepancy（（陈述、言语的）不相符）；crepitate（发出爆裂声）}
  \wordsense{a. 老弱的；衰老的；衰朽的}
  \wordphrase{decrepit old woman across}
  \wordexample{Believe it or not, the decrepit old woman across the street baked this bread!（信不信由你，这个面包是街对面的那个老奶奶烤的！）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{discrepancy}{dis'krepənsi}
  \wordbreakdown{分别出声 dis (= apart) + crep (= rattle)}
  \wordsense{n.（陈述、言语的）不相符，不一致；矛盾}
  \wordfamily{discrepant 相差的，有差异的，矛盾的}
  \wordphrase{discrepancy between his kind}
  \wordexample{There is an obvious discrepancy between his kind words and cruel deeds.（他亲切的话语和他残忍的行为显然是自相矛盾的。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{crepitate}{'krepiteit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CREP}
  \wordfamily{crepitation（爆裂声）；decrepit（老弱的）；discrepancy（（陈述、言语的）不相符）}
  \wordsense{v. 发出爆裂声}
  \wordphrase{crepitate the issue}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of crepitate.（此处展示 crepitate 的常见用法。）}
\end{word}



\chapter{MOL, MOLL}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 `mol` 意为 `to grind, grain, burden, heap`，`moll` 意为 `soft`。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{demolition}{ˏdemə'liʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{完全弄成粉末 de (= down, intensive) + mol (= grind, heap, burden)}
  \wordsense{n.（对老建筑或旧制度等的）拆毁；破坏，爆破；推翻}
  \wordfamily{demolish 拆毁；破坏；推翻}
  \wordphrase{demolition of the hotel}
  \wordexample{The demolition of the hotel is scheduled for next Thursday.（那家宾馆的爆破工作定于下星期四进行。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{emolument}{i'mɔljumənt}
  \wordbreakdown{磨出来 e(ex (= out)) + mol (= grind, grain)}
  \wordsense{n. 薪金，报酬，工资}
  \wordfamily{demolition}
  \wordfamily{immolate}
  \wordfamily{molecule}
  \wordfamily{molecular}
  \wordfamily{emoluments}
  \wordphrase{emoluments}
  \wordexample{CEOs of many major companies enjoy extravagantly generous emoluments.（许多大企业的首席执行官们都享有极其丰厚的薪金。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{immolate}{'iməleit}
  \wordbreakdown{在某物上面使成为谷粒、谷物 im<in (= on) + mol (= grain, heap, grind)}
  \wordsense{vt. sth + to + sth / do 杀死……作为祭物，献祭}
  \wordfamily{immolation 供奉祭品；祭物；牺牲}
  \wordphrase{Ancient Mayas are known}
  \wordexample{Ancient Mayas are known to have immolated human victims to appease their gods.（据说古玛雅人以人为祭物来平息他们所信的神的愤怒。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{molecule}{'mɔlikju:l}
  \wordbreakdown{mol (= heap, grind, grain), cule (= small)}
  \wordsense{n. 分子}
  \wordfamily{immolate}
  \wordfamily{molecular}
  \wordfamily{emolument}
  \wordfamily{molest}
  \wordfamily{a molecule（分子）}
  \wordphrase{a molecule（分子）}
  \wordexample{a molecule（分子）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{molecular}{mə'lekjulə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{mol (= grind, grain), cul<cule (= small)}
  \wordsense{a. 分子的，由分子组成的}
  \wordfamily{molecule}
  \wordfamily{molest}
  \wordfamily{immolate}
  \wordfamily{molar}
  \wordphrase{molecular structure（分子结构）}
  \wordphrase{molecular attraction / model / weight（分子引力 / 分子模型 / 分子量）}
  \wordexample{molecular structure（分子结构）}
  \wordexample{molecular attraction / model / weight（分子引力 / 分子模型 / 分子量）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{molest}{mə'lest}
  \wordbreakdown{mol (= burden, grind, grain)}
  \wordsense{v. 骚扰；干扰；（对女性）调戏}
  \wordfamily{molestation（妨碍；干扰）}
  \wordfamily{to molest（骚扰）}
  \wordphrase{to molest（骚扰）}
  \wordexample{to molest（骚扰）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{molar}{'məulə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{mol (= grind, grain)}
  \wordfamily{molest；premolar；molecular；emollient；demolition（（对老建筑或旧制度等的）拆毁）}
  \wordsense{n. 臼齿}
  \wordphrase{a molar（臼齿）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "a molar".（常见搭配：臼齿。）}
  \wordsense{a. 臼齿的；磨碎的}
  \wordphrase{a molar（臼齿）}
  \wordexample{a molar（臼齿）}
  \wordexample{be molar（臼齿的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{premolar}{pri:'məulə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{pre (= before) + moll (= grind, grain)}
  \wordfamily{incisor（门牙）；canine（犬齿）；demolition（（对老建筑或旧制度等的）拆毁）}
  \wordsense{n. 前臼齿}
  \wordphrase{a premolar（前臼齿）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "a premolar".（常见搭配：前臼齿。）}
  \wordsense{a. 前臼齿的}
  \wordphrase{a premolar（前臼齿）}
  \wordexample{a premolar（前臼齿）}
  \wordexample{be premolar（前臼齿的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{emollient}{i'mɔliənt}
  \wordbreakdown{e(ex (= out)) + moll (= soft)}
  \wordfamily{premolar；mollify；molar；mollusk；demolition（（对老建筑或旧制度等的）拆毁）}
  \wordsense{a. 使柔软的}
  \wordphrase{be emollient（使柔软的）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "be emollient".（常见搭配：使柔软的。）}
  \wordsense{n. （皮肤）润肤剂，软化剂}
  \wordphrase{be emollient（使柔软的）}
  \wordexample{be emollient（使柔软的）}
  \wordexample{an emollient（润肤剂，软化剂）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{mollify}{'mɔlifai}
  \wordbreakdown{moll (= soft) + ify (= make)}
  \wordsense{v. 平息，缓和，减轻；使安静，使平静}
  \wordfamily{mollification（缓和，减轻）}
  \wordfamily{mollifying remarks（抚慰的话）}
  \wordphrase{mollify her anger（平息她的怒火）}
  \wordexample{mollify her anger（平息她的怒火）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{mollusk}{'mɔləsk}
  \wordbreakdown{moll (= soft)}
  \wordfamily{mollify；emollient；premolar；molar；demolition（（对老建筑或旧制度等的）拆毁）}
  \wordsense{n. 软体动物}
  \wordphrase{a mollusk（软体动物）}
  \wordexample{a mollusk（软体动物}
\end{word}



\chapter{RID, RIS}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 `rid, ris` 意为 `to laugh`。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{deride}{di'raid}
  \wordbreakdown{向下笑 de (= down) + rid (= laugh)}
  \wordfamily{derision（嘲笑，嘲弄）；derisive（/ derisory 嘲笑的）；ridicule（戏弄）}
  \wordsense{vt. 嘲笑，嘲弄}
  \wordphrase{derisive / derisory 嘲笑的}
  \wordexample{Her friends derided her for wearing such old-fashioned clothes.（她的朋友嘲笑她穿了一件如此过时的衣服。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{ridicule}{'ridikju:l}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 RID, RIS}
  \wordfamily{ridiculous；risible；deride（嘲笑）}
  \wordsense{n. 戏弄，取笑，嘲笑}
  \wordphrase{the ridicule}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of ridicule.（此处展示 ridicule 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. 戏弄，嘲笑}
  \wordphrase{pour ridicule on a scheme（嘲笑一个计划）}
  \wordphrase{object of ridicule（嘲弄的对象）}
  \wordexample{pour ridicule on a scheme（嘲笑一个计划）}
  \wordexample{object of ridicule（嘲弄的对象）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{ridiculous}{ri'dikjuləs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 RID, RIS}
  \wordfamily{ridicule；risible；deride（嘲笑）}
  \wordsense{a. 可笑的；荒谬的}
  \wordphrase{be ridiculous}
  \wordexample{What a ridiculous idea!（这想法真够荒唐的！）}
  \wordexample{You look ridiculous in those tight jeans.（你穿那样的紧身牛仔服的样子很可笑。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{risible}{'rizəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 RID, RIS}
  \wordfamily{risibility（笑）；deride（嘲笑）；ridicule（戏弄）}
  \wordsense{a. 笑的；爱大笑的；可笑的}
  \wordphrase{ST STAT STIT SIST}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of risible.（此处展示 risible 的常见用法。）}
\end{word}



\chapter{ST, STAT, STIT, SIST}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 `st, stat, stit, sist` 意为 `to stand`。反义词根有来自拉丁语的 `sed, sid, sess` 等。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{destitute}{'destitju:t}
  \wordbreakdown{站在、位于下面的 de (= down, away) + stit (= stand)}
  \wordfamily{destitution（贫困）；extant（（文件、记录等）现存的）；institute（研究所）}
  \wordsense{a. 贫困的，赤贫的；缺乏的}
  \wordphrase{destitute my father was}
  \wordexample{Though not destitute, my father was certainly very poor when growing up.（我父亲小时候尽管不是赤贫，但的确也是很穷的。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{extant}{ek'stænt}
  \wordbreakdown{站到最终、最后的 ex (= out) + t<st (= stand)}
  \wordsense{a.（文件、记录等）现存的；尚存的}
  \wordfamily{destitute}
  \wordfamily{institute}
  \wordfamily{restitution}
  \wordfamily{instantaneous}
  \wordphrase{extant in many parts}
  \wordexample{Matchmaking is extant in many parts of Asia.（亚洲许多地方仍存在做媒现象。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{institute}{'institju:t}
  \wordbreakdown{站在某物之上 in (= on, in) + stit (= stand)}
  \wordfamily{institutionalize（制度化）；institution（公共机构，公共机构的建筑物）；institutional（制度上的，惯例的，学会的，慈善团体的）；destitute（贫困的）}
  \wordsense{n. 研究所；训练班；成人业余学校；学院；协会，学会}
  \wordphrase{the institute}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of institute.（此处展示 institute 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 制定（规则等）；成立（政府等）}
  \wordphrase{institute}
  \wordexample{I learned how to repair computers at a technical institute.（我在技术学校学习了电脑维修。）}
  \wordexample{institute}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{restitution}{ˏresti'tju:ʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{使站成原来的状态，站回原位置 re (= back, again) + stit (= stand)}
  \wordsense{n. 归还；赔偿}
  \wordfamily{institute}
  \wordfamily{instantaneous}
  \wordfamily{extant}
  \wordfamily{substantial}
  \wordphrase{restitution to their victims}
  \wordexample{Some states require thieves to make restitution to their victims.（有些州规定小偷要对受害者进行赔偿。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{instantaneous}{ˏinstən'teiniəs}
  \wordbreakdown{站在某物上的 in (= on) + st (= stand)}
  \wordfamily{instantaneously（即刻地，瞬间地）；destitute（贫困的）；extant（（文件、记录等）现存的）}
  \wordsense{a. 瞬间的，即刻的}
  \wordphrase{instantaneous The money arrives}
  \wordexample{Wire transfers are instantaneous. The money arrives in milliseconds.（电汇瞬间就可完成，钱即刻到账。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{substantial}{səb'stænʃl}
  \wordbreakdown{站在下面的 sub (= under) + st (= stand)}
  \wordfamily{substantiate（证实）；substance（物质，实质，本质）；substantive（独立的）；substantially（实际上）；destitute（贫困的）}
  \wordsense{a. 真实的，实在的；结实的，健壮的；大量的；丰富的}
  \wordphrase{substantially 实际上；本质上；相当多地}
  \wordexample{He promised me a substantial raise around the first of the year.（他答应我在年初给我大幅度加薪。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{desist}{di'zist}
  \wordbreakdown{从某物分离出来站着 de (= from) + sist (= stand)}
  \wordsense{vi. from + \textasciitilde{}ing 停止，终止；打消念头}
  \wordfamily{substantial}
  \wordfamily{persist}
  \wordfamily{instantaneous}
  \wordfamily{subsist}
  \wordphrase{desist from ridiculing me}
  \wordexample{Please desist from ridiculing me. It hurts my feelings.（拜托不要再嘲笑我了，我会觉得受伤害。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{persist}{pə'sist}
  \wordbreakdown{从头到尾一直站着、持续 per (= through, throughout) + sist (= stand)}
  \wordsense{vi. 固执，坚持；持续，存续}
  \wordfamily{persistence 持久；固执，坚持}
  \wordfamily{persistent 固执的，坚持的；持续的}
  \wordphrase{persist with daily life}
  \wordexample{It's difficult to persist with daily life tasks when suffering severe depression.（在情绪严重抑郁时很难坚持日常工作。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{subsist}{səb'sist}
  \wordbreakdown{站在下面 sub (= under) + sist (= stand)}
  \wordsense{vi. on + sth 存在，存续；活下去，维持生活}
  \wordfamily{subsistence 存在；生存；生计}
  \wordfamily{subsisted largely}
  \wordphrase{subsisted largely}
  \wordexample{In the first winter of our marriage we subsisted largely on beans and Wonder bread.（我们婚后的第一个冬天主要靠豆子和奇迹牌面包维持生活。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{constitute}{'kɔnstitju:t}
  \wordbreakdown{con<com (= together)}
  \wordsense{v. 构成（整体），组成；制定（法令等）；设立（机构等）；指派，任命}
  \wordfamily{constitution}
  \wordfamily{constitutional}
  \wordfamily{constituent}
  \wordfamily{consist}
  \wordphrase{He is strongly constituted.（他的身体很健壮。）}
  \wordexample{Twelve months constitute a year.（一年有 12 个月。）}
  \wordexample{Murder constitutes a criminal offense.（谋杀构成刑事犯罪。）}
  \wordexample{He is strongly constituted.（他的身体很健壮。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{constitution}{ˏkɔnsti'tju:ʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{con<com (= together)}
  \wordfamily{destitute（贫困的）；extant（（文件、记录等）现存的）；institute（研究所）}
  \wordsense{n. 宪法；政体；章程；构成，构造；制定；任命；设立；体质；体格}
  \wordphrase{suit one's constitution（适合某人的体质）}
  \wordexample{the constitution of the solar spectrum（光谱的构成）}
  \wordexample{people with strong / weak constitutions（体格强壮的 / 虚弱的人）}
  \wordexample{Great Britain has an unwritten constitution; the United States has a written constitution.（英国的宪法是不成文宪法，而美国的宪法是成文宪法。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{constitutional}{ˏkɔnsti'tju:ʃənl}
  \wordbreakdown{con<com (= together)}
  \wordsense{a. 宪法（上）的，立宪的；构造上的；体质的；生来的}
  \wordfamily{constitutive（构成的；本质的）}
  \wordfamily{constitutionalism（立宪主义；立宪政体；拥护立宪）}
  \wordfamily{constitutionalist（立宪主义者）}
  \wordfamily{constitutionalize（使宪法化）}
  \wordphrase{constitutional law（宪法）}
  \wordexample{constitutional law（宪法）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{constituent}{kən'stitjuənt}
  \wordbreakdown{con<com (= together)}
  \wordfamily{destitute（贫困的）；extant（（文件、记录等）现存的）；institute（研究所）}
  \wordsense{n. 选举人，选民；（组成的）成分，要素}
  \wordphrase{potential constituent（潜在的选举人）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "potential constituent".（常见搭配：潜在的选举人。）}
  \wordsense{a. 有宪法制定（或修改）权限的；构成（全体）的}
  \wordphrase{constituents of happiness（幸福的要素）}
  \wordexample{constituents of happiness（幸福的要素）}
  \wordexample{a constituent（选举人，选民）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{prostitute}{'prɔstitju:t}
  \wordbreakdown{pro (= forth, forward)}
  \wordfamily{prostitution；destitute（贫困的）；extant（（文件、记录等）现存的）}
  \wordsense{n. 妓女；娼妓；男妓；出卖节操的人}
  \wordphrase{the prostitute}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of prostitute.（此处展示 prostitute 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. [\textasciitilde{} oneself] 使沦为娼妓；滥用（能力、才能等）；（为了利益而）出卖（名誉等）}
  \wordphrase{prostitute one's energies / abilities（滥用精力 / 才能）}
  \wordphrase{prostitute one's honor（出卖名誉）}
  \wordexample{prostitute one's energies / abilities（滥用精力 / 才能）}
  \wordexample{prostitute one's honor（出卖名誉）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{prostitution}{ˏprɔsti'tju:ʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{pro (= forth, forward)}
  \wordfamily{destitute（贫困的）；extant（（文件、记录等）现存的）；institute（研究所）}
  \wordsense{n. 卖淫，出卖节操；（才能等的）滥用}
  \wordphrase{licensed / public prostitution（公娼〔制度〕）}
  \wordexample{licensed / public prostitution（公娼〔制度〕）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{substitute}{'sʌbstitju:t}
  \wordbreakdown{sub (= in place of)}

  \wordsense{n. 代替人；替代物，替代品}
  \wordphrase{the substitute}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of substitute.（此处展示 substitute 的常见用法。）}

  \wordphrase{be substitute}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of substitute.（此处展示 substitute 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. 代用，代替}
  \wordphrase{substitutes for rubber（橡胶的替代品）}
  \wordphrase{substitute food / fuel（替代食品 / 替代燃料）}
  \wordexample{substitutes for rubber（橡胶的替代品）}
  \wordexample{substitute food / fuel（替代食品 / 替代燃料）}

  \wordexample{do away with a superstition（破除迷信）}

\begin{word}[***]{superstitious}{ˏsu:pə'stiʃəs}

\begin{word}[*]{circumstance}{'sə:kəmstəns}
  \wordbreakdown{circum (= around)}
  \wordfamily{circumstantial；destitute（贫困的）；extant（（文件、记录等）现存的）}
  \wordsense{n. （pl.）情况，环境，情形；境遇，经济状况；事实；详情，细节}
  \wordphrase{under no circumstances（决不）}
  \wordexample{Circumstances alter cases.（《谚》环境改变，具体情况也改变。）}
  \wordexample{under no circumstances（决不）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{circumstantial}{ˏsə:kəm'stænʃl}
  \wordbreakdown{circum (= around)}
  \wordfamily{circumstance；destitute（贫困的）；extant（（文件、记录等）现存的）}
  \wordsense{a. （描述）详尽的；（证据）依照情况的，与情况有关的}
  \wordphrase{circumstantial evidence（间接证据；情况证据）}
  \wordexample{circumstantial evidence（间接证据；情况证据）}

\begin{word}[**]{constable}{'kʌnstəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{con<com (= together, intensive)}
  \wordsense{n. 治安官；《英》警察；（中世纪皇家或贵族的）总管；（皇家城堡的）主管}
  \wordfamily{chief constable（《英》警察局局长，在美国被称为}
  \wordfamily{police chief）}
  \wordfamily{special constable（紧急情况时的临时〔特种〕巡警）}
  \wordfamily{a constable（治安官）}
  \wordphrase{a constable（治安官）}
  \wordexample{a constable（治安官）}

  \wordfamily{constant}

  \wordexample{a constabulary（［总称］警察）}

\begin{word}[**]{constant}{'kɔnstənt}
  \wordbreakdown{con<com (= together, intensive)}
  \wordfamily{constantly（经常地，不断地）；destitute（贫困的）；extant（（文件、记录等）现存的）}
  \wordsense{a. 不停的；坚固的；忠实的}
  \wordphrase{be constant}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of constant.（此处展示 constant 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 常数}
  \wordphrase{constant complaints（不停的抱怨）}
  \wordphrase{constant friend / wife（忠实的朋友 / 坚贞不渝的妻子）}
  \wordexample{constant complaints（不停的抱怨）}
  \wordexample{constant friend / wife（忠实的朋友 / 坚贞不渝的妻子）}

\begin{word}[***]{constancy}{'kɔnstənsi}
  \wordbreakdown{con<com (= together, intensive)}
  \wordsense{n. 始终如一，持久不变；坚定；坚决；忠诚}
  \wordfamily{constant}
  \wordfamily{constabulary}
  \wordfamily{constable}
  \wordphrase{constancy of purpose（意志的坚定）}
  \wordexample{constancy of purpose（意志的坚定）}

\begin{word}[*]{contrast}{'kɔntrɑ:st}
  \wordbreakdown{contra (= against, opposite)}
  \wordfamily{constancy；destination；constant；destine；destitute（贫困的）}
  \wordsense{n. 对照，对比，比较；（对照之下形成的）差异；大不相同的人（或物）}
  \wordphrase{the contrast}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of contrast.（此处展示 contrast 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. 对照，比较，形成对照}
  \wordphrase{in contrast to / with（和……形成对比、对照）}
  \wordexample{She is a great contrast to her sister.（她和她妹妹有很大不同。）}
  \wordexample{in contrast to / with（和……形成对比、对照）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{destination}{ˏdesti'neiʃn}

  \wordfamily{a destination（目的地）}

  \wordsense{v. ［常用被动语态］注定}
  \wordphrase{They were destined never}

\begin{word}[***]{destiny}{'destini}

\begin{word}[*]{distant}{'distənt}

  \wordphrase{assistant professor（助理教授）}
  \wordphrase{assistant clerk（书记员）}
  \wordphrase{assistant manager（副经理，协理）}
  \wordexample{Special Assistant to the National Security Affairs（《美》国家安全事务特别助理）}
  \wordexample{assistant professor（助理教授）}
  \wordexample{assistant clerk（书记员）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{consist}{kən'sist}
  \wordbreakdown{con<com (= together)}
  \wordsense{v.（\textasciitilde{}of）由……组成，构成；（\textasciitilde{}in）在于}
  \wordfamily{consistency}
  \wordfamily{consistent}
  \wordfamily{constituent}
  \wordfamily{constitutional}
  \wordfamily{to consist（由……组成，构成）}
  \wordphrase{to consist（由……组成，构成）}
  \wordexample{to consist（由……组成，构成）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{consistency}{kən'sistənsi}
  \wordbreakdown{con<com (= together)}
  \wordfamily{destitute（贫困的）；extant（（文件、记录等）现存的）；institute（研究所）}
  \wordsense{n. （思维、行动等的）一贯性；（液体等的）浓度，稠度；坚硬度}
  \wordphrase{mixtures of various consistencies（浓度不同的各种混合物）}
  \wordexample{His actions lack consistency.（他的行动缺乏一贯性。）}
  \wordexample{mixtures of various consistencies（浓度不同的各种混合物）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{consistent}{kən'sistənt}
  \wordbreakdown{con<com (= together)}
  \wordfamily{consistently（始终如一地）；destitute（贫困的）；extant（（文件、记录等）现存的）}
  \wordsense{a. 始终如一的，稳定的，一贯的；（\textasciitilde{}with）与……符合的，一致的}
  \wordphrase{consistent friend（忠实的朋友）}
  \wordexample{consistent friend（忠实的朋友）}
  \wordexample{What you say now is not consistent with what you said last week.（你现在说的和你上星期说的不一致。）}
\end{word}

  \wordbreakdown{ex (= out), ist<sist (= stand)}

  \wordsense{v. 存在；生存}
  \wordphrase{to exist（存在）}
  \wordexample{to exist（存在）}

  \wordexample{struggle for existence（生存斗争）}
  \wordexample{ruins in existence（现存的遗迹）}

  \wordphrase{an existentialism（存在主义）}
  \wordexample{an existentialism（存在主义）}

  \wordfamily{coexist（共存）}
  \wordfamily{peaceful coexistence（和平共处）}
  \wordfamily{a coexistence（共存，并立）}
  \wordphrase{a coexistence（共存，并立）}
  \wordexample{a coexistence（共存，并立）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{insist}{in'sist}
  \wordbreakdown{in (= into, in)}

  \wordphrase{to insist（/（\textasciitilde{}that）主张，坚持，执意）}
  \wordexample{to insist（/（\textasciitilde{}that）主张，坚持，执意）}

\begin{word}[**]{insistence}{in'sistəns}
  \wordbreakdown{in (= into, in)}
  \wordfamily{destitute（贫困的）；extant（（文件、记录等）现存的）；institute（研究所）}
  \wordsense{n. 主张，竭力主张；坚决要求}
  \wordphrase{officer's insistence on strict obedience（军官要求严格服从）}
  \wordexample{officer's insistence on strict obedience（军官要求严格服从）}

\begin{word}[*]{insistent}{in'sistənt}
  \wordbreakdown{in (= into, in)}

  \wordsense{a. 坚持的，执意的}
  \wordphrase{be insistent}
  \wordexample{the insistent demands of the Commander-in-Chief for more troops（总司令坚持要求增派部队）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{resist}{ri'zist}
  \wordbreakdown{re (= against)}
  \wordsense{v. 抵抗，反抗；抵御；按捺，忍住}
  \wordfamily{resister（抵抗者，反抗者）}
  \wordfamily{resistor（电路中的电阻器）}
  \wordfamily{resistless（无法抵抗的，无抵抗力的）}
  \wordphrase{resist authority（反抗威权）}
  \wordphrase{resist heat（耐热）}
  \wordphrase{resist temptation（抵挡住诱惑）}
  \wordphrase{She can't resist chocolates.（她抵挡不住巧克力的诱惑。）}
  \wordexample{resist authority（反抗威权）}
  \wordexample{resist heat（耐热）}
  \wordexample{resist temptation（抵挡住诱惑）}
  \wordexample{She can't resist chocolates.（她抵挡不住巧克力的诱惑。）}

\begin{word}[*]{resistance}{ri'zistəns}

  \wordsense{n. 抵抗，反抗；（the R\textasciitilde{}）（特指第二次世界大战时期法西斯占领区内的）抵抗运动}
  \wordphrase{resistance movement（敌军占领地的抵抗运动）}
  \wordphrase{sales / consumer resistance（销售 / 消费者抵抗）}
  \wordexample{resistance movement（敌军占领地的抵抗运动）}
  \wordexample{sales / consumer resistance（销售 / 消费者抵抗）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{resistant}{ri'zistənt}
  \wordbreakdown{re (= against)}
  \wordfamily{resistance；resist；destitute（贫困的）}
  \wordsense{a. 抵抗的，有抵抗能力的}
  \wordphrase{corrosion-resistant materials（防腐物质）}
  \wordphrase{resistant strains of mosquitoes（有抵抗力的蚊子种群）}
  \wordexample{corrosion-resistant materials（防腐物质）}
  \wordexample{resistant strains of mosquitoes（有抵抗力的蚊子种群）}
\end{word}

  \wordbreakdown{tran<trans (= across)}

  \wordexample{transistor set / radio（晶体管机 / 晶体管收音机）}
\end{word}



\chapter{FAC}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根FAC 来自拉丁语的fac意为face。注意不要将其与意为to do, to make的fac相混淆。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{deface}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC；使从原先的脸上分离出来de（=away）+fac（=face）；di'feis}
  \wordfamily{defacement（损坏）；efface（擦掉）；outface（无畏地面对）}
  \wordsense{vt. 损坏外观，弄脏表面；涂污（墓碑铭文等使难辨认）}
  \wordphrase{defacing school property}
  \wordexample{You can be expelled for defacing school property with graffiti.（在学校的财产上涂抹乱写是会被开除的。）}
  \wordexample{defacing school property}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{efface}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC；使脱离本来的面部长相ef(ex)（=out）+fac（=face）；i'feis}
  \wordfamily{effacement（擦掉）；deface（损坏外观）；outface（无畏地面对）}
  \wordsense{vt. 擦掉；消除，抹去（记忆等）；使（自己）不受注意}
  \wordphrase{The effects of time}
  \wordexample{The effects of time had effaced the inscription on the ancient coin.（随着时间的流逝，那枚古钱上刻的文字已经被擦掉了。）}
  \wordexample{The effects of time}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{outface}{aut'feis}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC}
  \wordsense{v.无畏地面对；吓退}
  \wordfamily{efface}
  \wordfamily{reface}
  \wordfamily{deface}
  \wordfamily{surface}
  \wordfamily{to outface（无畏地面对）}
  \wordphrase{to outface（无畏地面对）}
  \wordexample{to outface（无畏地面对）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{reface}{ri:'feis}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC}
  \wordsense{v.重修……的表面}
  \wordfamily{outface}
  \wordfamily{surface}
  \wordfamily{efface}
  \wordfamily{facade}
  \wordfamily{to reface（重修……的表面）}
  \wordphrase{to reface（重修……的表面）}
  \wordexample{to reface（重修……的表面）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{surface}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC}
  \wordfamily{reface；facade；outface；face；deface（损坏外观）}
  \wordsense{n. （物体等的）表面，外表；地面；（液体的）表层，水面}
  \wordphrase{the surface}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of surface.（此处展示 surface 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{a. 表面的}
  \wordphrase{be surface}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of surface.（此处展示 surface 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. 在……上加表面；升到地面；对……进行表面处理 *}
  \wordphrase{rise to the surface（浮出水面）}
  \wordphrase{surface-to-air missile（地对空导弹）}
  \wordphrase{surface-to-surface missile（地对地导弹）}
  \wordexample{rise to the surface（浮出水面）}
  \wordexample{surface-to-air missile（地对空导弹）}
  \wordexample{surface-to-surface missile（地对地导弹）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{facade}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC；fa'sa:d}
  \wordfamily{deface（损坏外观）；efface（擦掉）；outface（无畏地面对）}
  \wordsense{n. （建筑物的）正面；外表，外观}
  \wordphrase{facade of indifference（外表冷漠）}
  \wordexample{facade of indifference（外表冷漠）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{face}{feis}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC}
  \wordfamily{deface（损坏外观）；efface（擦掉）；outface（无畏地面对）}
  \wordsense{n. 脸，面容；表情；面，表面；铅字面}
  \wordphrase{lose face（丢脸）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "lose face".（常见搭配：丢脸。）}
  \wordsense{v. 面对（某个方向），朝，向；应付，正视（……的存在）}
  \wordphrase{face lifting（（消除皱纹的）脸部整形）}
  \wordphrase{keep a straight face（不苟言笑，正色）}
  \wordphrase{face the enemy（直面敌人）}
  \wordexample{face lifting（（消除皱纹的）脸部整形）}
  \wordexample{keep a straight face（不苟言笑，正色）}
  \wordexample{face the enemy（直面敌人）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{faceless}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC；'feislis}
  \wordfamily{deface（损坏外观）；efface（擦掉）；outface（无畏地面对）}
  \wordsense{a. 匿名的；没有个性的，无特性的 *}
  \wordphrase{faceless kidnapper（匿名绑架者）}
  \wordexample{faceless kidnapper（匿名绑架者）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{facetious}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC；fə'si:ʃəs}
  \wordfamily{deface（损坏外观）；efface（擦掉）；outface（无畏地面对）}
  \wordsense{a. 滑稽的；好开玩笑的}
  \wordphrase{facetious remark/young man（滑稽的话/幽默的年轻人）}
  \wordexample{facetious remark/young man（滑稽的话/幽默的年轻人）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{facial}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC；'feiʃəl}
  \wordfamily{deface（损坏外观）；efface（擦掉）；outface（无畏地面对）}
  \wordsense{a. 脸的，面部的；面部用的}
  \wordphrase{be facial}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of facial.（此处展示 facial 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 面部按摩；美容}
  \wordphrase{facial expression（面部表情）}
  \wordphrase{facial massage（面部按摩）}
  \wordphrase{facial tissue（面巾纸）}
  \wordexample{facial expression（面部表情）}
  \wordexample{facial massage（面部按摩）}
  \wordexample{facial tissue（面巾纸）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{facing}{'feisiŋ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FAC}
  \wordsense{n.（衣服的）镶边，贴边；（制服等衣物上领子及袖口的）异色镶边}
  \wordfamily{facial}
  \wordfamily{facetious}
  \wordfamily{faceless}
  \wordfamily{face}
  \wordfamily{a facing（镶边，贴边）}
  \wordphrase{a facing（镶边，贴边）}
  \wordexample{a facing（镶边，贴边）}
\end{word}



\chapter{MEN[MEAN]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根MEN[MEAN] 来自拉丁语的men意为to lead，它的变形为mean。同义词根有来自拉丁语的duct/duc[du]以及来自希腊语的agog。此外，意为to drive的来自拉丁语的ag/ig/act以及pel/puls[peal]等也具有近似的含义。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{demeanor}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MEN[MEAN]；很好地引导自己de（=intensive）+mean(men)（=lead）；di'mi:nə(r)}
  \wordfamily{demean（使表现）；misdemeanor（轻罪 *mis（=bad）, de（=down））；amenable（顺从的）}
  \wordsense{n. 举动，行为}
  \wordphrase{demeanor is reserved and}
  \wordexample{Her professional demeanor is reserved and cultured.（她工作时言语矜持，举止文雅。）}
  \wordexample{demeanor is reserved and}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{misdemeanor}{,misdi'mi:nə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MEN[MEAN]}
  \wordfamily{demeanor；amenable；commence；commencement}
  \wordsense{n. 轻罪 *mis（=bad）, de（=down）, mean(men)（=lead）}
  \wordphrase{a misdemeanor（轻罪）}
  \wordexample{a misdemeanor（轻罪）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{amenable}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MEN[MEAN]；ə'mi:nəbl}
  \wordfamily{demeanor（举动）；misdemeanor（轻罪 *mis（=bad）, de（=down））；commence（开始）}
  \wordsense{a. 顺从的；对……负有义务（或责任）的；经得起检验的 *a(ad)（=to）}
  \wordphrase{amenable to kindness/advice/reason/flattery（易受好意感动的/能听从劝告的/通情达理的/易为吹捧所动的）}
  \wordphrase{amenable to the law（服从法律的）}
  \wordexample{amenable to kindness/advice/reason/flattery（易受好意感动的/能听从劝告的/通情达理的/易为吹捧所动的）}
  \wordexample{amenable to the law（服从法律的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{commence}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MEN[MEAN]；kə'mens}
  \wordfamily{demeanor（举动）；misdemeanor（轻罪 *mis（=bad）, de（=down））；amenable（顺从的）}
  \wordsense{v. 开始，着手 *}
  \wordphrase{commence studying political science（开始学习政治学）}
  \wordexample{commence studying political science（开始学习政治学）}
  \wordexample{The performance will commence soon.（演出马上就要开始了。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{commencement}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MEN[MEAN]；kə'mensmənt}
  \wordfamily{demeanor（举动）；misdemeanor（轻罪 *mis（=bad）, de（=down））；amenable（顺从的）}
  \wordsense{n. 开始，开端；（大学等的）毕业典礼；学位授予仪式 *}
  \wordphrase{commencement ceremony was held}
  \wordexample{The commencement ceremony was held on May 20, 1990.（毕业典礼于1990年5月20日举行。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{promenade}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MEN[MEAN]}
  \wordfamily{demeanor（举动）；misdemeanor（轻罪 *mis（=bad）, de（=down））；amenable（顺从的）}
  \wordsense{n. 散步，漫步；骑马；散步场所，海滨人行道；（大学、高中等的）舞会（也作prom） [,prɔmə'neid]}
  \wordphrase{Senior Prom（毕业班舞会）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "Senior Prom".（常见搭配：毕业班舞会。）}
  \wordsense{v. 散步；带（某人）散步 *}
  \wordphrase{promenade concert（（听众站立或走动着听的）散步音乐会）}
  \wordphrase{promenade deck（散步甲板，供一等舱乘客使用）}
  \wordexample{promenade his wife along the sea-front（带着他的妻子在滨海区散步）}
  \wordexample{promenade concert（（听众站立或走动着听的）散步音乐会）}
  \wordexample{promenade deck（散步甲板，供一等舱乘客使用）}
\end{word}



\chapter{LEC[LET, LIC, LIG]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根LEC[LET, LIC, LIG] 来自拉丁语的lec意为to entice，它的变形为let，lic，lig等。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{delectable}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LEC[LET, LIC, LIG]；引诱、唆使使分离出来的de（=away, from）+lec（=entice）；di'lektəbl}
  \wordfamily{delectation（愉快）；dilettante（（艺术等的）一知半解者）；delicate（微妙的）}
  \wordsense{a. 令人愉快的，使人高兴的，宜人的；好吃的}
  \wordphrase{delectable}
  \wordexample{The smoked salmon was absolutely delectable.（熏鲑鱼真是美味可口。）}
  \wordexample{delectable}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{dilettante}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LEC[LET, LIC, LIG]}
  \wordfamily{delectable；delicate；delicacy；delicatessen}
  \wordsense{n. （艺术等的）一知半解者，业余艺术爱好者 [,dili'tænti] *di(dis)（=away, from）,}
  \wordphrase{a dilettante（一知半解者，业余艺术爱好者 [,dili'tænti]）}
  \wordexample{a dilettante（一知半解者，业余艺术爱好者 [,dili'tænti]）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{delicate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LEC[LET, LIC, LIG]}
  \wordfamily{dilettante；delicacy；delectable；delicatessen}
  \wordsense{a. 微妙的，需要小心处理的；柔软的；纤弱的；精致的，精巧的；易损坏的；（光线）柔和的；（感觉）敏锐的，敏感的；（仪器）精密的；（食物）清淡的；鲜美的 *de（=intensive）,}
  \wordphrase{delicate situation/question（微妙的事态/辣手的问题）}
  \wordphrase{delicate workmanship（精巧的工艺）}
  \wordphrase{delicate china（易碎的瓷器）}
  \wordexample{delicate skin of a young girl（小女孩柔嫩的肌肤）}
  \wordexample{delicate situation/question（微妙的事态/辣手的问题）}
  \wordexample{delicate workmanship（精巧的工艺）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{delicacy}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LEC[LET, LIC, LIG]}
  \wordfamily{delectable（令人愉快的）；dilettante（（艺术等的）一知半解者）；delicate（微妙的）}
  \wordsense{n. 精致，精美；优雅；敏感；微妙；棘手；柔软；（身体）纤弱；美味，佳肴 *de（=intensive）,}
  \wordphrase{the delicacy of her features（她容貌的优雅）}
  \wordphrase{of extreme delicacy（非常棘手的）}
  \wordexample{all the delicacies of the season（该季节的所有美味）}
  \wordexample{the delicacy of her features（她容貌的优雅）}
  \wordexample{The political situation is one of great delicacy.（政局是最微妙的情形之一。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{delicatessen}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LEC[LET, LIC, LIG]}
  \wordfamily{delicacy；delicious；delicate；delight；delectable（令人愉快的）}
  \wordsense{n. （出售已做好的肉、沙拉、熏鱼、香肠、罐头等的）熟食店（在美国多被称做deli） [,deliki'tesn] *de（=intensive）,}
  \wordphrase{a delicatessen（熟食店（在美国多被称做deli） [,deliki'tesn]）}
  \wordexample{a delicatessen（熟食店（在美国多被称做deli） [,deliki'tesn]）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{delicious}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LEC[LET, LIC, LIG]；di'liʃəs}
  \wordfamily{delectable（令人愉快的）；dilettante（（艺术等的）一知半解者）；delicate（微妙的）}
  \wordsense{a. 美味的，香喷喷的；妙的，有趣的 *de（=intensive）,}
  \wordphrase{delicious cake（美味的蛋糕）}
  \wordphrase{delicious moment（愉快的瞬间）}
  \wordexample{What a delicious joke!（多么有趣的笑话！）}
  \wordexample{delicious cake（美味的蛋糕）}
  \wordexample{delicious moment（愉快的瞬间）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{delight}{di'lait}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LEC[LET, LIC, LIG]}
  \wordfamily{delicious；delighted；delicatessen；delightful；delectable（令人愉快的）}
  \wordsense{n. 快乐，高兴；乐事，乐趣}
  \wordphrase{a delight（快乐，高兴）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "a delight".（常见搭配：快乐，高兴。）}
  \wordsense{v. 使人高兴 *de（=intensive）,}
  \wordphrase{a delight（快乐，高兴）}
  \wordexample{a delight（快乐，高兴）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{delighted}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LEC[LET, LIC, LIG]；di'laitid}
  \wordfamily{delectable（令人愉快的）；dilettante（（艺术等的）一知半解者）；delicate（微妙的）}
  \wordsense{a. 高兴的 *de（=intensive）,}
  \wordphrase{delighted to see you}
  \wordexample{I'm delighted to see you.（很高兴见到您。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{delightful}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LEC[LET, LIC, LIG]；di'laitful}
  \wordfamily{delectable（令人愉快的）；dilettante（（艺术等的）一知半解者）；delicate（微妙的）}
  \wordsense{a. 快乐的；高兴的 *de（=intensive）,}
  \wordphrase{delightful holiday（愉快的假期）}
  \wordexample{delightful holiday（愉快的假期）}
\end{word}



\chapter{LIN, LIGN}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根LIN, LIGN 来自拉丁语的lin，lign意为line。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{delineate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LIN, LIGN；好好地描写de（=intensive）+lin（=line）；di'lini:eit}
  \wordfamily{delineation（画轮廓）；line（线）；linage（行数）}
  \wordsense{vt. 画……的轮廓；描述}
  \wordphrase{The CEO delineated the}
  \wordexample{The CEO delineated the company's 5-year growth plan at the shareholders' meeting.（首席执行官在股东大会上描绘了公司的五年发展计划。）}
  \wordexample{The CEO delineated the}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{line}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LIN, LIGN；lain}
  \wordfamily{communication（lines（交通线路）；delineate（画……的轮廓）；linage（行数）}
  \wordsense{n. 线，绳，索；电话线；（人或物体的）行，列；（脸上的）皱纹；（手掌的）手纹；运输线；运输公司，航空公司；路线；方针；（行动、处理的）方式，方法；家系，家世；（书写或印刷的字符的）行；（用堡垒、战壕等连接的）战线，防线；营地；【军】（二列）横队；行业；（商品的）种类}
  \wordphrase{fishing lines（钓鱼线）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "fishing lines".（常见搭配：钓鱼线。）}
  \wordsense{v. 在……上画线；使起皱纹；使排成行，沿……排列；装衬里；中饱私囊}
  \wordphrase{draw a line（画线）}
  \wordphrase{a line of trees（一排树）}
  \wordphrase{form a line（排成一队）}
  \wordexample{Line busy.（（电话）通话中。）}
  \wordexample{descendant of King David in a direct line（大卫王的直系后裔）}
  \wordexample{draw a line（画线）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{linage}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LIN, LIGN}
  \wordsense{n.行数；按行数的稿费计算法；（报刊等处）按行数计算的广告费}
  \wordfamily{['lainidʒ]}
  \wordfamily{advertising linage（按行数计算的广告费）}
  \wordfamily{a linage（行数）}
  \wordphrase{a linage（行数）}
  \wordexample{a linage（行数）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{lineage}{'liniidʒ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LIN, LIGN}
  \wordsense{n.血统，家系}
  \wordfamily{linage}
  \wordfamily{lineal}
  \wordfamily{line}
  \wordfamily{delineate}
  \wordfamily{a lineage（血统，家系）}
  \wordphrase{a lineage（血统，家系）}
  \wordexample{a lineage（血统，家系）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{lineal}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LIN, LIGN；'liniəl}
  \wordfamily{delineate（画……的轮廓）；line（线）；linage（行数）}
  \wordsense{a. 直系的}
  \wordphrase{lineal descendant/heir（直系后裔/继承者）}
  \wordexample{lineal descendant/heir（直系后裔/继承者）}
\end{word}



\chapter{LINQU[LIC, LICT]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根LINQU[LIC, LICT] 来自拉丁语的linqu意为to leave，它的变形为lic，lict。同义词根有来自拉丁语的lip。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{delinquent}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LINQU[LIC, LICT]；从某物上分离出去de（=away）+linqu（=leave）；di'liŋkwənt}
  \wordfamily{delinquency（懈怠）；derelict（被抛弃的）；relinquish（放弃）}
  \wordsense{a. 失职的；怠慢的；违法的；有过失的，有罪的；拖欠的}
  \wordphrase{be delinquent}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of delinquent.（此处展示 delinquent 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 违法者；罪犯（尤指少年犯）}
  \wordphrase{delinquent in paying the}
  \wordexample{The power got shut off because I've been delinquent in paying the electric bill.（我拖欠了电费，所以断电了。）}
  \wordexample{delinquent in paying the}
  \wordexample{delinquent（a.失职的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{derelict}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LINQU[LIC, LICT]；完全置后de（=intensive）+re（=back）+lict<linqu（=leave）；'derilikt}
  \wordfamily{dereliction（遗弃）；delinquent（失职的）；relinquish（放弃）}
  \wordsense{a. 被抛弃的，被遗弃的；荒废的；破败的；玩忽职守的}
  \wordphrase{be derelict}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of derelict.（此处展示 derelict 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 被社会遗弃的人，流浪汉；玩忽职守者；遗弃物；在海上被遗弃的船只}
  \wordphrase{derelict ruin}
  \wordexample{The family farm where I grew up is now a derelict ruin.（我在家里的农场上长大，如今那里已成为一片被遗弃的废墟。）}
  \wordexample{derelict ruin}
  \wordexample{derelict（a.被抛弃的，被遗弃的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{relinquish}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LINQU[LIC, LICT]}
  \wordfamily{derelict；relic；delinquent；relict}
  \wordsense{v. 放弃，抛弃；让与 *re（=back） relinquish a hope/shares to a partner（放弃希望/把股份让给合作伙伴）}
  \wordphrase{relinquish a hope/shares to a partner（放弃希望/把股份让给合作伙伴）}
  \wordphrase{to relinquish（放弃，抛弃）}
  \wordexample{to relinquish（放弃，抛弃）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{relic}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LINQU[LIC, LICT]}
  \wordfamily{delinquent（失职的）；derelict（被抛弃的）；relinquish（放弃）}
  \wordsense{n. 圣骨，圣徒遗物；（风俗、信仰等的）残余；遗物；遗迹 *re（=back）,}
  \wordphrase{relics of antiquity（古代的遗风）}
  \wordphrase{relics of superstition（迷信的残余）}
  \wordexample{relics of antiquity（古代的遗风）}
  \wordexample{relics of superstition（迷信的残余）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{relict}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LINQU[LIC, LICT]；'relikt}
  \wordfamily{delinquent（失职的）；derelict（被抛弃的）；relinquish（放弃）}
  \wordsense{n. 遗孀，寡妇 *re（=back）,}
  \wordphrase{the relict}
  \wordexample{Alice, relict of Arthur Williams（阿瑟·威廉斯的遗孀艾丽斯）}
\end{word}



\chapter{PREC}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根PREC 来自拉丁语的prec意为to pray。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{deprecate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PREC；祈祷以使终止、停止de（=off, from）+prec（=pray）；'deprikeit}
  \wordfamily{deprecation（不赞成）；deprecatory（不赞成的）；imprecate（祈求（灾难、不幸降临在某人身上））}
  \wordsense{vt. 反对（战争等），声明不赞成}
  \wordphrase{deprecate your spouse publicly}
  \wordexample{Never deprecate your spouse publicly if you want to save your marriage.（如果你想维持婚姻，就不要当着众人指责你的配偶。）}
  \wordexample{deprecate your spouse publicly}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{imprecate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PREC；'imprikeit}
  \wordfamily{imprecation（诅咒）；deprecate（反对（战争等））；precarious（不确定的）}
  \wordsense{v. 祈求（灾难、不幸降临在某人身上），诅咒 *}
  \wordphrase{imprecate evil upon him（祈祷降祸于他）}
  \wordexample{imprecate evil upon him（祈祷降祸于他）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{precarious}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PREC；pri'keəriəs}
  \wordfamily{deprecate（反对（战争等））；imprecate（祈求（灾难、不幸降临在某人身上））；relict（遗孀）}
  \wordsense{a. 不确定的；不稳的；危险的；碰机会的}
  \wordphrase{be precarious}
  \wordexample{make a precarious living as an author（作为一名作家，过着不稳定的生活）}
\end{word}



\chapter{PRED}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根PRED 来自拉丁语的pred意为to plunder。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{depredation}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PRED；完全掠夺de（=intensive）+pred（=plunder）；,depri'deiʃən}
  \wordfamily{depredate（蹂躏）；predatory（掠夺的）；predator（掠夺者）}
  \wordsense{n. 破坏；掠夺}
  \wordphrase{depredation over hundreds}
  \wordexample{The hurricane left a path of depredation over hundreds of miles.（台风留下了数百英里的破坏痕迹。）}
  \wordexample{depredation over hundreds}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{predatory}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PRED；'predətəri}
  \wordfamily{depredation（破坏）；predator（掠夺者）；precarious（不确定的）}
  \wordsense{a. 掠夺的；掠夺成性的；（动物）以捕食其他动物为生的，食肉的}
  \wordphrase{predatory tribesmen（掠夺成性的部落成员）}
  \wordphrase{predatory incursions（掠夺性入侵）}
  \wordphrase{predatory birds（食肉鸟）}
  \wordexample{predatory tribesmen（掠夺成性的部落成员）}
  \wordexample{predatory incursions（掠夺性入侵）}
  \wordexample{predatory birds（食肉鸟）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{predator}{'predətə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PRED}
  \wordsense{n.掠夺者；食肉动物}
  \wordfamily{predatory}
  \wordfamily{depredation}
  \wordfamily{a predator（掠夺者）}
  \wordphrase{a predator（掠夺者）}
  \wordexample{a predator（掠夺者）}
\end{word}



\chapter{CRED[CREED, CRE]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根CRED[CREED, CRE] 来自拉丁语的cred意为to believe, to trust，它的变形为creed和cre。同义词根有来自拉丁语的fid[fi, fy, feder, feal]以及来自盎格鲁-撒克逊语的tru[tro]等。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{discredit}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CRED[CREED, CRE]；不相信dis（=not）+cred（=believe）；dis'kredit}
  \wordfamily{discreditable（有损信用的）；discreditably（丢脸地）；credibility（可信性）}
  \wordsense{vt. 使不足信；使丢脸}
  \wordphrase{discredit the issue}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of discredit.（此处展示 discredit 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 信用的丧失；名声的败坏}
  \wordphrase{discredit the senator appeared}
  \wordexample{Press leaks designed to discredit the senator appeared in morning newspapers.（为破坏那个参议员的声誉而由报界泄露的秘密被登在了早报上。）}
  \wordexample{discredit the senator appeared}
  \wordexample{discredit（vt.使不足信）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{credibility}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CRED[CREED, CRE]；可以信赖cred（=trust）；,kredi'biləti}
  \wordfamily{credible（可信的）；credibly（可信地）；discredit（使不足信）}
  \wordsense{n. 可信性}
  \wordphrase{credibility of a witness}
  \wordexample{The credibility of a witness who testifies at trial is key.（出庭作证的证人的可信度很重要。）}
  \wordexample{credibility of a witness}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{credulous}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CRED[CREED, CRE]；信赖的cred（=trust）；'kredjələs}
  \wordfamily{credulity（轻信，易受骗）；discredit（使不足信）；credibility（可信性）}
  \wordsense{a. 轻信的；易受骗的}
  \wordphrase{credulous that she believes}
  \wordexample{My mother's so credulous that she believes the car dealer is on her side.（我妈妈是如此轻信别人，以至于相信汽车销售员是站在她这一边的。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{accredit}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CRED[CREED, CRE]}
  \wordfamily{discredit（使不足信）；credibility（可信性）；credulous（轻信的）}
  \wordsense{v. 任命，派出（使节）；（政府等）认可，承认（资格等）；鉴定……为合格 *}
  \wordphrase{accredited law schools（经鉴定合格的法学院）}
  \wordexample{accredited law schools（经鉴定合格的法学院）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{credence}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CRED[CREED, CRE]；'kri:dns}
  \wordfamily{accredit；credential；credulous；incredible；discredit（使不足信）}
  \wordsense{n. 相信；凭证}
  \wordphrase{give / attach credence to（相信〔传闻或别人的话等〕）}
  \wordphrase{a letter of credence（证明信）}
  \wordexample{give / attach credence to（相信〔传闻或别人的话等〕）}
  \wordexample{a letter of credence（证明信）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{credential}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CRED[CREED, CRE]；kri'denʃl}
  \wordfamily{discredit（使不足信）；credibility（可信性）；credulous（轻信的）}
  \wordsense{n. （pl.）证明书，证件；文凭；（大使递交的）国书}
  \wordphrase{present one's credentials（（大使等）递交国书）}
  \wordexample{His credentials were so satisfactory that he was given the post of manager.（他的文凭很令人满意，这使他获得了经理的职位。）}
  \wordexample{present one's credentials（（大使等）递交国书）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{incredible}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CRED[CREED, CRE]}
  \wordfamily{incredibly（难以置信地）；discredit（使不足信）；credibility（可信性）}
  \wordsense{a. 难以置信的，惊人的，不可思议的 *}
  \wordphrase{incredible cost（惊人的费用）}
  \wordexample{incredible cost（惊人的费用）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{credit}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CRED[CREED, CRE]}
  \wordfamily{incredible；creditable；credential；creditor；discredit（使不足信）}
  \wordsense{n. （赊账或交易的）信用；名誉，信誉；（银行等的）贷款（金）；贷方（↔ debit 借方）；（某科目的）（所修）学分；荣誉；赞扬}
  \wordphrase{the credit}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of credit.（此处展示 credit 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. 信赖；把（金额）记入贷方；给（学生）记学分}
  \wordphrase{credit standing（债务偿付能力，信誉）}
  \wordphrase{credit rating（信誉评价，信用等级）}
  \wordphrase{letter of credit（信用证，缩写：L/C）}
  \wordexample{a man of the highest credit（名望最高的人）}
  \wordexample{credit standing（债务偿付能力，信誉）}
  \wordexample{credit rating（信誉评价，信用等级）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{creditable}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CRED[CREED, CRE]；'kreditəbl}
  \wordfamily{creditably（值得称赞地，有好名声地）；discredit（使不足信）；credibility（可信性）}
  \wordsense{a. 可信的；名誉好的；值得赞许的}
  \wordphrase{creditable attempt（值得赞许的尝试）}
  \wordexample{creditable attempt（值得赞许的尝试）}
  \wordexample{be creditable（可信的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{creditor}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CRED[CREED, CRE]；'kreditə(r)}
  \wordfamily{creditable；credo；credit；incredulous；discredit（使不足信）}
  \wordsense{n. 贷方，债权人（↔ debtor 借方，债务人）}
  \wordphrase{run away from one's creditors（为躲避债权人而逃跑）}
  \wordexample{run away from one's creditors（为躲避债权人而逃跑）}
  \wordexample{a creditor（贷方，债权人（↔ debtor 借方，债务人））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{credo}{'kri:dou}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CRED[CREED, CRE]}
  \wordsense{n.信条；（the Credo）信经}
  \wordfamily{creditor}
  \wordfamily{incredulous}
  \wordfamily{creditable}
  \wordfamily{creed}
  \wordfamily{a credo（信条）}
  \wordphrase{a credo（信条）}
  \wordexample{a credo（信条）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{incredulous}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CRED[CREED, CRE]}
  \wordfamily{credo；creed；creditor；miscreant；discredit（使不足信）}
  \wordsense{a. 不相信的；怀疑的 *}
  \wordphrase{incredulous smiles / looks（怀疑的微笑 / 怀疑的表情）}
  \wordexample{incredulous smiles / looks（怀疑的微笑 / 怀疑的表情）}
\end{word}

  \wordphrase{a creed（宗教信条）}
  \wordexample{a creed（宗教信条）}

  \wordbreakdown{词根 CRED[CREED, CRE]}

\chapter{CRET, CERN[CREET, CRE]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根CRET, CERN[CREET, CRE] 来自拉丁语的cret, cern意为to observe, to separate，变形为creet, cre。此外，意为to grow, to increase的cre, cru与这两词根的形态相似，注意不要混淆。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{discretion}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CRET, CERN[CREET, CRE]；分别观察dis（=apart）+cret（=observe, separate）；di'skreʃn}
  \wordfamily{discretionary（任意的，自由决定的）；discern（分辨）；discreet（谨慎的）}
  \wordsense{n. 慎重；判断力；决定或行动的自由}
  \wordphrase{discretion when disciplining an}
  \wordexample{It's important to use discretion when disciplining an underperforming employee.（在处罚工作表现不佳的职员时慎重考虑是非常重要的。）}
  \wordexample{discretion when disciplining an}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{discern}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CRET, CERN[CREET, CRE]；分别切开使分开dis（=apart）+cern（=separate）；di'sə:n}
  \wordfamily{discernment（洞察）；discerning（有辨别力的）；discernible（可辨别的）；discretion（慎重）}
  \wordsense{vt. 分辨，识别；察觉出}
  \wordphrase{discernible 可辨别的}
  \wordexample{You can barely discern the white fences in the snow banks.（在雪堆中很难看出哪里是白栅栏。）}
  \wordexample{discernible 可辨别的}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{discreet}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CRET, CERN[CREET, CRE]}
  \wordfamily{discern；discrete；discretion；decree}
  \wordsense{a. 谨慎的，慎重的，深思熟虑的 *dis（=apart）,}
  \wordphrase{maintain a discreet silence（谨慎地保持沉默）}
  \wordexample{maintain a discreet silence（谨慎地保持沉默）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{discrete}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CRET, CERN[CREET, CRE]}
  \wordfamily{discreet；decree；discern；excrete；discretion（慎重）}
  \wordsense{a. 分离的；不连续的 *}
  \wordphrase{be discrete（分离的）}
  \wordexample{be discrete（分离的）}

  \wordphrase{a decree（法令）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "a decree".（常见搭配：法令。）}
  \wordsense{v. 颁布；注定 *de（=apart, away）,}
  \wordphrase{issue a decree（颁布一项法令）}
  \wordexample{issue a decree（颁布一项法令）}
  \wordexample{a decree（法令）}

\begin{word}[***]{concern}{[]}

\chapter{EST, ESS, ENT}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根EST, ESS, ENT 来自拉丁语的est, ess, ent意为to be。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{disinterested}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 EST, ESS, ENT；两人之间不存在什么的dis（=not）+inter（=between）+est（=to be）；dis'intrəstid}
  \wordfamily{nonentity；uninterested；uninteresting；interest}
  \wordsense{a. 无私的；公正的；不关心的}
  \wordphrase{disinterested party-an advocate}
  \wordexample{A trial judge is, ideally, a disinterested party-an advocate of neither side.（理想状态下，审判官应当是无私的，不支持双方中任何一方。）}
  \wordexample{disinterested party-an advocate}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{nonentity}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 EST, ESS, ENT；不存在non（=not）+ent（=to be）；nɔn'entəti}
  \wordfamily{disinterested；uninterested；uninteresting；interest}
  \wordsense{n. 不存在的东西；无足轻重的人}
  \wordphrase{nonentity in the Democratic}
  \wordexample{He's a nonentity in the Democratic Party.（他在民主党内无足轻重。）}
  \wordexample{nonentity in the Democratic}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{uninterested}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 EST, ESS, ENT}
  \wordfamily{nonentity；uninteresting；disinterested；interest}
  \wordsense{a. 没有兴趣的；不关心的 *un（=not）,}
  \wordphrase{be uninterested（没有兴趣的）}
  \wordexample{be uninterested（没有兴趣的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{uninteresting}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 EST, ESS, ENT}
  \wordfamily{uninterested；interest；nonentity；interested；disinterested（无私的）}
  \wordsense{a. 无趣的；引不起兴趣的 *un（=not）,}
  \wordphrase{be uninteresting（无趣的）}
  \wordexample{be uninteresting（无趣的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{interest}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 EST, ESS, ENT}
  \wordfamily{disinterested（无私的）；nonentity（不存在的东西）；uninterested（没有兴趣的）}
  \wordsense{n. 关心；兴趣，趣味；权益；利息；（pl.）利益}
  \wordphrase{annual interest（年利息）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "annual interest".（常见搭配：年利息。）}
  \wordsense{v. 引起……的兴趣；引起……的注意 *}
  \wordphrase{French interests in Algeria（法国在阿尔及利亚的权益）}
  \wordphrase{interest rate（利率）}
  \wordphrase{simple / compound interest（单利 / 复利）}
  \wordexample{pay 6\% interest on a loan（付6\%的贷款利息）}
  \wordexample{French interests in Algeria（法国在阿尔及利亚的权益）}
  \wordexample{interest rate（利率）}
\end{word}

  \wordbreakdown{词根 EST, ESS, ENT}
  \wordfamily{interest；interesting；uninteresting；intestine；disinterested（无私的）}

  \wordexample{small / large intestine（小肠 / 大肠）}

  \wordexample{A common collocation is "be essential".（常见搭配：必要的，不可缺少的。）}
  \wordsense{n. （pl.）本质；要素}
  \wordphrase{be essential（必要的，不可缺少的）}

  \wordexample{be essential（必要的，不可缺少的）}

\chapter{SECT}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 `sect` 意为 `to cut`，同义词根有来自希腊语的 `tom / tem` 和来自拉丁语的 `cis`、`cop / coup`、`scind / scis`、`sever`、`tons`、`trench` 以及来自盎格鲁-撒克逊语的 `tail`。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{dissect}{di'sekt}
  \wordbreakdown{分开切 dis (= apart) + sect (= cut)}
  \wordfamily{dissection（解剖）；intersect（横断）；bisect（分为二等分）}
  \wordsense{vt. 解剖；分析；仔细研究}
  \wordphrase{dissect a frog}
  \wordexample{I still remember with horror having to dissect a frog in 9th grade biology.（我至今还记着我中学三年级的生物课上必须解剖青蛙时的恐惧。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{intersect}{ˏintə'sekt}
  \wordbreakdown{在其间切开 inter (= between) + sect (= cut)}
  \wordfamily{intersection（横断）；交叉（点）；dissect（解剖）}
  \wordsense{v. 横断；交叉}
  \wordphrase{intersects a dirt road}
  \wordexample{Take a left where this street intersects a dirt road in about a mile.（大约走一英里，在这条路和一条土路相交叉的地方左转。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{bisect}{bai'sekt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SECT}
  \wordsense{v. 分为二等分，平分}
  \wordfamily{bisection（一分为二）}
  \wordfamily{to bisect（分为二等分，平分）}
  \wordphrase{to bisect（分为二等分，平分）}
  \wordexample{to bisect（分为二等分，平分）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{cross section}{'krɔs sekʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SECT}
  \wordsense{n. 横截面；（代表全体的）典型，代表；横截面图}
  \wordfamily{bisect}
  \wordfamily{insect}
  \wordfamily{sect}
  \wordfamily{sectarian}
  \wordphrase{cross-section of the electors / the middle classes（选民代表 / 中产阶级的代表）}
  \wordexample{cross-section of the electors / the middle classes（选民代表 / 中产阶级的代表）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{insect}{'insekt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SECT}
  \wordsense{n. 昆虫}
  \wordfamily{insecticide（杀虫剂）}
  \wordfamily{insectivorous（食虫的）}
  \wordfamily{an insect（昆虫）}
  \wordphrase{an insect（昆虫）}
  \wordexample{an insect（昆虫）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{sect}{sekt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SECT}
  \wordsense{n.（持不同信仰、意见的）派系，宗派}
  \wordfamily{insect}
  \wordfamily{sectarian}
  \wordfamily{cross section}
  \wordfamily{section}
  \wordfamily{a sect（派系，宗派）}
  \wordphrase{a sect（派系，宗派）}
  \wordexample{a sect（派系，宗派）}
\end{word}

  \wordbreakdown{词根 SECT}

  \wordphrase{be sectarian}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of sectarian.（此处展示 sectarian 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 派系分子}

  \wordphrase{sectarian jealousies（派系〔宗派〕间的忌妒）}

  \wordexample{sectarian jealousies（派系〔宗派〕间的忌妒）}

  \wordfamily{sectarian}
  \wordfamily{sectional}

  \wordexample{sectional fishing-rod（分节钓鱼竿）}
  \wordexample{sectional furniture（组合家具）}

\begin{word}[**]{sector}{'sektə(r)}

  \wordsense{v. 活体解剖}

\chapter{LAPID}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 `lapid` 意为 `stone`，同义词根有来自希腊语的 `lith`、`petr`。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{dilapidated}{di'læpideitid}
  \wordbreakdown{石头分别破成碎片的 di<dis (= apart) + lapid (= stone)}
  \wordfamily{dilapidation（荒废）；lapidary（铭刻在石头上的）；vivisect（活体解剖）}
  \wordsense{a. （建筑物、家具、汽车等）破损的；荒废的}
  \wordphrase{dilapidated but he loved}
  \wordexample{My brother's first car was really dilapidated, but he loved it anyway.（我弟弟的第一辆车实在是破旧不堪，不过他还是很喜爱它。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{lapidary}{'læpidəri}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LAPID}
  \wordfamily{dilapidated；vivisect（活体解剖）；sector（部分）}
  \wordsense{a. 铭刻在石头上的；优雅简洁的}
  \wordphrase{be lapidary}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of lapidary.（此处展示 lapidary 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 宝石匠 *ary (= pertaining to, person who)}
  \wordphrase{lapidary inscription / speech（碑铭 / 简洁而精确的演讲）}
  \wordexample{lapidary inscription / speech（碑铭 / 简洁而精确的演讲）}
\end{word}



\chapter{MIN[MEN]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 `min` 意为 `to project, to hang`，它的变形为 `men`。此外，来自拉丁语 `minutus` 的 `min` 也有 `small` 之意，注意不要混淆。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{diminish}{di'miniʃ}
  \wordbreakdown{使分离后变小 di<dis (= apart) + min (= small)}
  \wordsense{v. 缩小；（使）减少}
  \wordfamily{diminution 减小，缩小}
  \wordfamily{diminutive 微小的；（n.）表示“小”的词尾}
  \wordphrase{diminishes in teen pop}
  \wordexample{After an initial burst of fame, public interest normally diminishes in teen pop idols.（十几岁的偶像歌手们在初期红极一时之后，大众对他们的关注一般都会减少。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{eminent}{'eminənt}
  \wordbreakdown{向外突出的 e<ex (= out) + min (= project, hang)}
  \wordsense{a. 著名的；出众的；明显的；高耸的}
  \wordfamily{eminence 著名，卓越}
  \wordfamily{eminently 出众地；明显地}
  \wordphrase{eminent physician}
  \wordexample{My grandfather was an eminent physician in this community.（我爷爷是这个社区有名的内科医生。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{preeminent}{ˏpri:'eminənt}
  \wordbreakdown{之前向外突出的 pre (= before) + e<ex (= out) + min (= project)}
  \wordsense{a. 优秀的，出类拔萃的，卓越的}
  \wordfamily{preeminence 杰出，卓越，超群}
  \wordphrase{preeminent attorney}
  \wordexample{Ms. Barns is the preeminent attorney in this city.（巴恩斯夫人是本市的优秀律师。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{imminent}{'iminənt}
  \wordbreakdown{悬挂在某物上的 im<in (= on) + min (= hang, project)}
  \wordsense{a. 紧急的；迫切的}
  \wordfamily{imminence 紧迫，迫近}
  \wordfamily{imminently 迫切地，迫近地}
  \wordphrase{imminent}
  \wordexample{I'll wait and buy it later because their clearance sale is imminent.（因为他们马上就要清仓甩卖，所以我决定晚些时候再买那个东西。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{prominent}{'prɔminənt}
  \wordbreakdown{向前突出的 pro (= forth) + min (= project)}
  \wordsense{a. 显眼的，显著的；卓越的，著名的；突出的，突起的}
  \wordfamily{prominence 突出；显著；卓越，著名}
  \wordphrase{prominent member}
  \wordexample{My uncle is a prominent member of the city council.（我的叔叔是著名的市议会议员。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{menace}{'menəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MIN[MEN]}
  \wordfamily{diminuendo；administer；administration；administrative；diminish（缩小）}
  \wordsense{n. 威胁，恐吓；极端讨厌的人或物}
  \wordphrase{the menace}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of menace.（此处展示 menace 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. 威胁 *men<min (= project)}
  \wordphrase{menace to world peace（对世界和平的威胁）}
  \wordphrase{countries menaced by war（受到战争威胁的国家）}
  \wordexample{menace to world peace（对世界和平的威胁）}
  \wordexample{That woman is a menace!（那个女人真讨厌！）}
  \wordexample{countries menaced by war（受到战争威胁的国家）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{diminuendo}{diˏminju'endou}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MIN[MEN]}
  \wordfamily{diminish（缩小）；eminent（著名的）；preeminent（优秀的）}
  \wordsense{n. 【音】渐弱 *di<dis (= apart), min (= small)}
  \wordphrase{a diminuendo（渐弱）}
  \wordexample{a diminuendo（渐弱）}
\end{word}

  \wordbreakdown{词根 MIN[MEN]}
  \wordfamily{diminuendo；administration；menace；administrative；diminish（缩小）}
  \wordsense{v. 管理；执行（法令、仪式）；提供；执行管理 / 处理（遗产）职责 *ad (= to), min (= small)}
  \wordphrase{administer the affairs of state（管理国家事务）}

  \wordphrase{administer medicine to him（给他服药）}
  \wordexample{administer the affairs of state（管理国家事务）}
  \wordexample{administer the law（执法）}
  \wordexample{administer medicine to him（给他服药）}

  \wordsense{n. 管理，经营；行政；行政机关，政府；管理部门，行政部门；（法律的）执行；用药；（多写作 A\textasciitilde{}）行政机关，政府 *ad (= to), min (= small)}
  \wordphrase{Master of Business Administration（工商管理硕士）}

  \wordexample{civil / military administration（民政 / 军政）}

  \wordbreakdown{词根 MIN[MEN]}

  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of minister.（此处展示 minister 的常见用法。）}

\begin{word}[***]{minute}{mai'nju:t}

  \wordsense{n. 【'minit】（时间的）分；（角度的）分；备忘录；（pl.）会议记录}
  \wordphrase{the minute}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of minute.（此处展示 minute 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. 【'minit】将……记入议事录；将……记入备忘录 *min (= small)}
  \wordphrase{minute particles of gold dust（金粉的微粒子）}
  \wordphrase{minute difference（微小的差异）}
  \wordphrase{minute description（详细的描述）}
  \wordexample{minute particles of gold dust（金粉的微粒子）}
  \wordexample{minute difference（微小的差异）}
  \wordexample{minute description（详细的描述）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{minutiae}{mai'nju:ʃii:}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MIN[MEN]}
  \wordfamily{minute；mince；minuscule；mincing；diminish（缩小）}
  \wordsense{n. 细节；琐事 *min (= small)}
  \wordphrase{a minutiae（细节）}
  \wordexample{a minutiae（细节）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{mince}{mins}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MIN[MEN]}
  \wordfamily{mincer（碎肉机）；diminish（缩小）；eminent（著名的）}
  \wordsense{v. 剁（肉等），切碎；矫揉造作地说}
  \wordphrase{minced meat（碎肉）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "minced meat".（常见搭配：碎肉。）}
  \wordsense{n. 碎肉，肉末 *min (= small)}
  \wordphrase{mince pie（肉馅饼）}
  \wordexample{mince pie（肉馅饼）}
  \wordexample{to mince（剁（肉等），切碎）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{mincing}{'minsiŋ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MIN[MEN]}
  \wordfamily{mince；minutiae；minute；minuscule；diminish（缩小）}
  \wordsense{a. 装腔作势的，矫揉造作的 *min (= small)}
  \wordphrase{take mincing steps（迈着做作的步子）}
  \wordexample{affected, mincing young girl（装腔作势的小女孩）}
  \wordexample{take mincing steps（迈着做作的步子）}
\end{word}



\chapter{PEL,PULS[PEAL]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 `pel, puls` 意为 `to drive`，它们的变形为 `peal`。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 `ig / ag / act` 和 `fig`。此外，来自拉丁语的意为 `to lead` 的 `duc / duct[du]`、`men[mean]` 以及来自希腊语的 `agog` 也有近似的含义。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{dispel}{di'spel}
  \wordbreakdown{远远地驱逐或者分别撑 dis (= away) + pel (= drive)}
  \wordsense{vt. 驱走；驱散}
  \wordfamily{impel}
  \wordfamily{expulsion}
  \wordfamily{repellent}
  \wordfamily{appeal}
  \wordfamily{dispels nightmares}
  \wordphrase{dispels nightmares}
  \wordexample{My father always told me that a glass of cold water dispels nightmares.（我爸爸经常跟我说一杯凉水可以赶走噩梦。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{impel}{im'pel}
  \wordbreakdown{在里面驱赶 im<in (= in) + pel (= drive)}
  \wordsense{vt. sb + to do 驱使；迫使；催促；推进}
  \wordfamily{impulsive 冲动的；由冲动造成的}
  \wordfamily{impulse 推动力；（内心的）冲动}
  \wordfamily{impulsion 冲动；推进}
  \wordfamily{impeller 叶轮}
  \wordfamily{impels him}
  \wordphrase{impels him}
  \wordexample{He has billions, but fear impels him to earn more money.（他虽已有数十亿的财产，但恐惧驱使他去赚取更多的钱。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{expulsion}{ik'spʌlʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{向外摔、驱赶 ex (= out) + puls (= drive)}
  \wordfamily{expel（赶出，驱逐）；dispel（驱走）；impel（sb + to do 驱使）}
  \wordsense{n. 驱逐，逐出；排除；除名，开除}
  \wordphrase{expulsion from school}
  \wordexample{Disobedience of the rules will result in immediate expulsion from school.（违反校规的结果就是立即被学校开除。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{repellent}{ri'pelənt}
  \wordbreakdown{向后驱赶 re (= back) + pel (= drive)}
  \wordfamily{repulsive（令人厌恶的，令人不愉快的）；repulsion（击退）；repel（驱赶）；repulse（反击，击退）；dispel（驱走）}
  \wordsense{a. 击退的；令人不愉快的，令人厌恶的}
  \wordphrase{be repellent}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of repellent.（此处展示 repellent 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 驱虫剂}
  \wordphrase{That woman has a}
  \wordexample{That woman has a repellant personality. I abhor her.（那个女人的性格令人反感，我很讨厌她。）}
  \wordexample{That woman has a}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{appeal}{ə'pi:l}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEL,PULS[PEAL]}
  \wordfamily{repel（驱赶）；repulse（反击，击退）；repulsive（令人厌恶的，令人不愉快的）；repulsion（击退）；dispel（驱走）}
  \wordsense{v. 上诉；恳求；有吸引力 *ap<ad (= toward, to), peal<pel (= drive)}
  \wordphrase{appeal the issue}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of appeal.（此处展示 appeal 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 驱虫剂}
  \wordphrase{That woman has a}
  \wordexample{That woman has a repellant personality. I abhor her.（那个女人的性格令人反感，我很讨厌她。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{appellate}{ə'pelət}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEL,PULS[PEAL]}
  \wordfamily{appeal；compel；compulsion；compulsive；dispel（驱走）}
  \wordsense{a. 上诉的；受理上诉的 *ap<ad (= to, toward)}
  \wordphrase{appellate court（受理上诉的法院）}
  \wordexample{appellate court（受理上诉的法院）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{compel}{kəm'pel}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEL,PULS[PEAL]}
  \wordfamily{appellate；compulsion；appeal；compulsive；dispel（驱走）}
  \wordsense{v. 迫使，强迫 *com (= with, together)}
  \wordphrase{to compel（迫使，强迫）}
  \wordexample{to compel（迫使，强迫）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{compulsion}{kəm'pʌlʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEL,PULS[PEAL]}
  \wordfamily{compel；compulsive；appellate；compulsory；dispel（驱走）}
  \wordsense{n. 强迫，强制 *com (= with, together)}
  \wordphrase{a compulsion（强迫，强制）}
  \wordexample{a compulsion（强迫，强制）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{compulsive}{kəm'pʌlsiv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEL,PULS[PEAL]}
  \wordfamily{compulsion；compulsory；compel；propel；dispel（驱走）}
  \wordsense{a. 强制性的，强迫的 *com (= with, together)}
  \wordphrase{compulsive eater（吃起来停不住的人）}
  \wordphrase{compulsive TV viewer（不由自主看电视的人）}
  \wordexample{compulsive eater（吃起来停不住的人）}
  \wordexample{compulsive TV viewer（不由自主看电视的人）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{compulsory}{kəm'pʌlsəri}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEL,PULS[PEAL]}
  \wordfamily{compulsive；propel；compulsion；propellant；dispel（驱走）}
  \wordsense{a. 义务性的；强制的；必修的 *com (= with, together)}
  \wordphrase{compulsory education（义务教育）}
  \wordphrase{compulsory service（义务兵役）}
  \wordphrase{compulsory execution（强制执行）}
  \wordexample{compulsory education（义务教育）}
  \wordexample{compulsory service（义务兵役）}
  \wordexample{compulsory execution（强制执行）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{propel}{prə'pel}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEL,PULS[PEAL]}
  \wordfamily{compulsory；propellant；compulsive；propeller；dispel（驱走）}
  \wordsense{v. 推进 *pro (= forward)}
  \wordphrase{to propel（推进）}
  \wordexample{to propel（推进）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{propellant}{prə'pelənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEL,PULS[PEAL]}
  \wordfamily{propel；propeller；compulsory；pulse；dispel（驱走）}
  \wordsense{a. 推进的}
  \wordphrase{be propellant（推进的）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "be propellant".（常见搭配：推进的。）}
  \wordsense{n. 推动者（物）；（枪炮的）发射火药；（火箭等的）推进燃料 *pro (= forward)}
  \wordphrase{be propellant（推进的）}
  \wordexample{be propellant（推进的）}
  \wordexample{a propellant（推动者（物））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{propeller}{prə'pelə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEL,PULS[PEAL]}
  \wordfamily{propellant；pulse；propel；pulsate；dispel（驱走）}
  \wordsense{n. 推进器；螺旋桨 *pro (= forward)}
  \wordphrase{a propeller（推进器）}
  \wordexample{a propeller（推进器）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{pulse}{pʌls}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEL,PULS[PEAL]}
  \wordfamily{propeller；pulsate；propellant；pulsation；dispel（驱走）}
  \wordsense{n. 脉搏；有节奏的跳动}
  \wordphrase{a pulse（脉搏）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "a pulse".（常见搭配：脉搏。）}
  \wordsense{v. 搏动；起伏}
  \wordphrase{a pulse（脉搏）}
  \wordexample{a pulse（脉搏）}

\end{word}

  \wordbreakdown{词根 PEL,PULS[PEAL]}

\chapter{SUAD, SUAS[SUAV]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 `suad, suas` 意为 `to persuade, to advise`，它的变形为 `suav`。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{dissuade}{di'sweid}
  \wordbreakdown{说服以使脱离 dis (= away, from) + suad (= persuade, advise)}
  \wordsense{vt. sb + from + sth / \textasciitilde{}ing 劝阻}
  \wordfamily{dissuasion 劝阻；规劝}
  \wordfamily{dissuasive 劝阻的}
  \wordphrase{dissuade my son}
  \wordexample{I tried to dissuade my son from learning to parachute, but in vain.（我曾试图劝说我儿子不要去学跳伞，但是没有用。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{persuade}{pə'sweid}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SUAD, SUAS[SUAV]}
  \wordfamily{persuadable（可以说服的）；dissuade（sb + from + sth / \texta）；persuasion（说服力）}
  \wordsense{v. 说服，使相信，劝服（某人做某事） *per (= through)}
  \wordphrase{to persuade（说服，使相信，劝服（某人做某事））}
  \wordexample{to persuade（说服，使相信，劝服（某人做某事））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{persuasion}{pə'sweiʒn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SUAD, SUAS[SUAV]}
  \wordfamily{dissuade（sb + from + sth / \texta）；persuade（说服）；persuasive（有说服力的 *per (= through)）}
  \wordsense{n. 说服力；说服，劝说；信念；教派 *per (= through)}
  \wordphrase{men of various persuasions（各种教派的人）}
  \wordexample{It is my persuasion that \textasciitilde{}（……是我的信念。）}
  \wordexample{men of various persuasions（各种教派的人）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{persuasive}{pə'sweisiv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SUAD, SUAS[SUAV]}
  \wordfamily{persuasion；suasion；persuade；suave；dissuade（sb + from + sth / \texta）}
  \wordsense{a. 有说服力的 *per (= through)}
  \wordphrase{persuasive manner}
  \wordexample{She has a persuasive manner.（她的态度很有说服力。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{suasion}{'sweiʒn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SUAD, SUAS[SUAV]}
  \wordfamily{dissuade（sb + from + sth / \texta）；persuade（说服）；persuasion（说服力）}
  \wordsense{n. 说服；劝告}
  \wordphrase{a suasion（说服）}
  \wordexample{a suasion（说服）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{suave}{swɑ:v}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SUAD, SUAS[SUAV]}
  \wordfamily{dissuade（sb + from + sth / \texta）；persuade（说服）；persuasion（说服力）}
  \wordsense{a. 精明练达的；圆滑的 *suav<suad (= persuade)}
  \wordphrase{be suave}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of suave.（此处展示 suave 的常见用法。）}
\end{word}



\chapter{ARM}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 `arm` 意为 `weapon`。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{disarming}{dis'ɑ:miŋ}
  \wordbreakdown{将武器扔远的 dis (= away) + arm (= weapon)}
  \wordsense{a. 使消除疑惑、害怕、敌意的；使人消气的}
  \wordfamily{disarm 解除武装；裁军}
  \wordfamily{disarmament 解除武装；裁军}
  \wordphrase{disarming smile}
  \wordexample{The little boy gave me a disarming smile.（那个小男孩冲我微笑了一下，解除了我的疑虑。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{alarm}{ə'lɑ:m}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ARM}
  \wordfamily{alarmist（危言耸听的人）；disarming（使消除疑惑、害怕、敌意的）；forearm（预先武装）}
  \wordsense{n. 警报；警报器（装置）；惊慌，恐慌；担忧}
  \wordphrase{the alarm}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of alarm.（此处展示 alarm 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. 向……发出警报；使惊慌}
  \wordphrase{fire / thief alarm（火灾报警（器）/ 防盗报警（器））}
  \wordphrase{alarm bell（警钟）}
  \wordphrase{alarm clock（闹钟）}
  \wordexample{set the alarm clock for six o'clock（把闹钟定在 6 点）}
  \wordexample{fire / thief alarm（火灾报警（器）/ 防盗报警（器））}
  \wordexample{alarm bell（警钟）}
\end{word}
