  \wordbreakdown{词根 ARM}
  \wordfamily{disarming（使消除疑惑、害怕、敌意的）；alarm（警报）；forearm（预先武装）}
  \wordsense{n. （包括军队、军舰、飞机等在内的）装备；（pl.）军事力量；军备}
  \wordphrase{planes with the newest armament（配有最新装备的飞机）}
  \wordphrase{limitation / reduction of armaments（军备的限制 / 裁减军备）}
  \wordexample{planes with the newest armament（配有最新装备的飞机）}
  \wordexample{limitation / reduction of armaments（军备的限制 / 裁减军备）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{armistice}{'ɑ:mistis}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ARM}
  \wordfamily{disarming（使消除疑惑、害怕、敌意的）；alarm（警报）；forearm（预先武装）}
  \wordsense{n. 休战，暂时停战 *stic (= stand, stop)}
  \wordphrase{an armistice（休战，暂时停战）}
  \wordexample{an armistice（休战，暂时停战）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{armor}{'ɑ:mə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ARM}
  \wordsense{n. 盔甲；（军舰、坦克、汽车等的）装甲；【总称】装甲兵；装甲部队；（保护电缆的）铠装，色层；动植物的防护层（如鱼的鳞片等）；防护服；纹章；保障}
  \wordfamily{armorer（兵器制造者；军械士；军械保管人）}
  \wordfamily{armorial（纹章的） / armorial bearings（纹章）}
  \wordphrase{clad in armor（身穿盔甲的）}
  \wordphrase{armor plate（装甲用钢板）}
  \wordexample{clad in armor（身穿盔甲的）}
  \wordexample{armor plate（装甲用钢板）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{armored}{'ɑ:məd}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ARM}
  \wordsense{a. 披甲的，装甲的；在混凝土中加入钢筋的；配备有装甲车辆的}
  \wordfamily{armor}
  \wordfamily{armory}
  \wordfamily{armistice}
  \wordfamily{army}
  \wordphrase{armored car / cruiser（装甲车 / 装甲巡洋舰）}
  \wordphrase{armored column / division / forces（装甲队伍 / 装甲师 / 装甲部队）}
  \wordphrase{armored cable / concrete（铠装电缆 / 钢筋混凝土）}
  \wordexample{armored car / cruiser（装甲车 / 装甲巡洋舰）}
  \wordexample{armored column / division / forces（装甲队伍 / 装甲师 / 装甲部队）}
  \wordexample{armored cable / concrete（铠装电缆 / 钢筋混凝土）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{armory}{'ɑ:məri}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ARM}
  \wordfamily{armored；army；armor；armistice；disarming（使消除疑惑、害怕、敌意的）}
  \wordsense{n. 军械库；兵工厂 *ory (= place where)}
  \wordphrase{an armory（军械库）}
  \wordexample{an armory（军械库）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{army}{'ɑ:mi}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ARM}
  \wordfamily{armory；armored；armor；armistice；disarming（使消除疑惑、害怕、敌意的）}
  \wordsense{n. 军队；（与海军、空军相对的）陆军；大群；（常作 A\textasciitilde{}）团体}
  \wordphrase{standing / reserve army（常备军 / 后备军）}
  \wordphrase{an army of occupation（占领军）}
  \wordphrase{join / leave the army（参军 / 退役）}
  \wordexample{standing / reserve army（常备军 / 后备军）}
  \wordexample{an army of occupation（占领军）}
  \wordexample{join / leave the army（参军 / 退役）}
\end{word}



\chapter{VERG}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 `verg` 意为 `to turn, to bend`。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 `vert / vers`、`rot` 和来自希腊语的 `stroph`，以及来自盎格鲁-撒克逊语的 `tour` 等。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{divergent}{dai'vɜ:dʒənt}
  \wordbreakdown{分别转换方向的 di<dis (= apart) + verg (= turn, bend)}
  \wordsense{a.（线、路等）分叉的；意见等有分歧的；不同的}
  \wordfamily{diverge 分叉；分歧}
  \wordfamily{divergence / divergency 分歧；偏离；相异，差异}
  \wordphrase{divergent opinions}
  \wordexample{My sister and I have divergent opinions on almost every topic imaginable.（我妹妹和我几乎在所有可以想像到的话题上意见都不一致。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{converge}{kən'vɜ:dʒ}
  \wordbreakdown{一起、同时朝向中心地点 con<com (= together) + verg (= turn, bend)}
  \wordfamily{convergence（会合）；convergent（会集于一点的）；divergent（（线、路等）分叉的）}
  \wordsense{vi. 集中于一点；人、车等聚集；（意见、行为等）趋于一致；收敛}
  \wordphrase{The coyotes converged on}
  \wordexample{The coyotes converged on the fallen caribou and devoured it.（郊狼聚集到倒下的驯鹿旁将它吞食掉了。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{verge}{vɜ:dʒ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VERG}
  \wordfamily{converge；divergent；army（军队）}
  \wordsense{n. 边缘，界限，边际}
  \wordphrase{a verge（边缘，界限，边际）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "a verge".（常见搭配：边缘，界限，边际。）}
  \wordsense{v. 接近，濒临}
  \wordphrase{verge}
  \wordexample{to verge（接近，濒临}
  \wordexample{verge}
\end{word}



\chapter{VULG[MULG]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 `vulg` 意为 `people`，它的变形为 `mulg`。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 `popul[publ]`、`pleb` 和来自希腊语的 `demo`，以及来自盎格鲁-撒克逊语的 `la` 等。此外，意为 `man`（人类、人）的 `anthrop / man`、`hom`，意为 `man`（男人）的 `andr / mascul / vir`，以及意为 `woman`（女人）的 `femin / gyn / virg` 等词根也与其意思相近。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{divulge}{daɪˈvʌldʒ}
  \wordbreakdown{从秘密状态中脱离告知人们 di<dis (= apart, away) + vulg (= people)}
  \wordfamily{divulgence（泄露）；promulgate（发布（命令、新法等））；vulgar（粗俗的）}
  \wordsense{vt. 泄露，透露}
  \wordphrase{The journalist divulged the}
  \wordexample{The journalist divulged the troops' location, infuriating the general.（记者泄露了军队的位置，使将军勃然大怒。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{promulgate}{ˈprɒmlɡeɪt}
  \wordbreakdown{使站到众人面前 pro (= forth) + mulg<vulg (= people)}
  \wordsense{vt. 发布（命令、新法等），公布；散布（信仰、知识、舆论、秘密等），传播}
  \wordfamily{promulgation 颁布，公布；普及，宣传}
  \wordphrase{The water commission promulgated}
  \wordexample{The water commission promulgated several new regulations to reduce pollution.（水资源委员会颁布了几项减少污染的新条例。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{vulgar}{ˈvʌlɡə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VULG[MULG]}
  \wordsense{a. 粗俗的，缺乏教养的，下流的；庸俗的；通俗的；大众的}
  \wordfamily{vulgarism（粗话，下流话；下流的行为；下流）}
  \wordfamily{vulgarity（粗俗行为；下流行为）}
  \wordfamily{vulgarize（使粗俗；使通俗化）}
  \wordphrase{vulgar language / behavior / ideas（下流话 / 下流的行为 / 庸俗的想法）}
  \wordphrase{vulgar mind（〔有〕粗俗想法〔的人〕）}
  \wordphrase{vulgar display of wealth（俗不可耐的对财富的炫耀）}
  \wordexample{vulgar language / behavior / ideas（下流话 / 下流的行为 / 庸俗的想法）}
  \wordexample{vulgar mind（〔有〕粗俗想法〔的人〕）}
  \wordexample{vulgar display of wealth（俗不可耐的对财富的炫耀）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{vulgarian}{vʌlˈɡeəriən}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VULG[MULG]；*arian (= person who)}
  \wordfamily{vulgar；divulge（泄露）；promulgate（发布（命令、新法等））}
  \wordsense{n. 俗物；俗人，俗不可耐的暴发户}
  \wordphrase{vulgarian（*arian (= person who)）}
  \wordexample{vulgarian（*arian (= person who)）}
  \wordexample{a vulgarian（俗物）}
\end{word}



\chapter{FID[FI, FY, FEDER, FEAL]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 `fid` 意为 `to trust`，它的变形为 `fi`、`fy`、`feder`、`feal` 等。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 `cred` 和来自盎格鲁-撒克逊语的 `tru[tro]`。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{diffident}{ˈdɪfɪdənt}
  \wordbreakdown{不相信自己能力的 dif<dis (= not) + fid (= trust)}
  \wordsense{a. 不相信自己能力的；缺乏自信的；羞怯的}
  \wordfamily{diffidence 缺乏自信；羞怯}
  \wordfamily{diffidently 缺乏自信地；羞怯地}
  \wordphrase{diffident manner made me}
  \wordexample{The young boy's diffident manner made me want to mother him.（看到那个男孩羞怯的样子，我很想像妈妈一样照顾他。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{fidelity}{fɪˈdeləti}
  \wordbreakdown{令人信赖 fid (= trust)}
  \wordsense{n. 忠实，忠诚；准确，精密}
  \wordfamily{diffident}
  \wordfamily{perfidious}
  \wordfamily{confide}
  \wordfamily{confidence}
  \wordphrase{fidelity than any human}
  \wordexample{I have seven dogs, and they show me more fidelity than any human ever did.（我养了七条狗，它们对我比任何人都忠诚。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{perfidious}{pəˈfɪdiəs}
  \wordbreakdown{利用信任来欺骗的或者假装信任的 per (= through, falsely) + fid (= trust)}
  \wordsense{a. 背信弃义的；不诚实的}
  \wordfamily{perfidy 不义，不忠，背叛}
  \wordphrase{perfidious fiend gained my}
  \wordexample{The perfidious fiend gained my confidence and then stole my wallet!（那个背信弃义的恶魔般的人在获取了我的信任后偷走了我的钱包！）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{confide}{kənˈfaɪd}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FID[FI, FY, FEDER, FEAL]；*con<com (= completely)}
  \wordfamily{confiding（易于信任别人的）；diffident（不相信自己能力的）；fidelity（忠实）}
  \wordsense{v. 吐露（秘密等）；托付，委托（事情、责任）；信赖}
  \wordphrase{confide（*con<com (= completely)）}
  \wordexample{confide（*con<com (= completely)）}
  \wordexample{to confide（吐露（秘密等））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{confidence}{ˈkɒnfɪdəns}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FID[FI, FY, FEDER, FEAL]；*con<com (= completely)}
  \wordfamily{diffident（不相信自己能力的）；fidelity（忠实）；perfidious（背信弃义的）}
  \wordsense{n. （对他人的）信任，信赖；自信，确信；私房话，秘密}
  \wordphrase{vote of (no)}
  \wordphrase{in strict confidence（绝对保密地，绝密地）}
  \wordphrase{confidence man（骗子）}
  \wordexample{vote of (no)}
  \wordexample{in strict confidence（绝对保密地，绝密地）}
  \wordexample{confidence man（骗子）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{confident}{ˈkɒnfɪdənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FID[FI, FY, FEDER, FEAL]；*con<com (= completely)}
  \wordfamily{confidently（自信地）；diffident（不相信自己能力的）；fidelity（忠实）}
  \wordsense{a. 确信的；有信心的；自负的}
  \wordphrase{confident smile（充满自信的微笑）}
  \wordexample{confident smile（充满自信的微笑）}
  \wordexample{confident（*con<com (= completely)）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{confidential}{ˌkɒnfɪˈdenʃəl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FID[FI, FY, FEDER, FEAL]；*con<com (= completely)}
  \wordfamily{diffident（不相信自己能力的）；fidelity（忠实）；perfidious（背信弃义的）}
  \wordsense{a. 机密的，秘密的；可信的；（人）表示信任的；易于信任他人的}
  \wordphrase{confidential documents（机密文件）}
  \wordphrase{confidential inquiry（秘密调查）}
  \wordexample{confidential documents（机密文件）}
  \wordexample{confidential inquiry（秘密调查）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{confidant}{ˈkɒnfɪdænt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FID[FI, FY, FEDER, FEAL]；*con<com (= completely)}
  \wordfamily{confidante（confidant 的阴性词）；diffident（不相信自己能力的）；fidelity（忠实）}
  \wordsense{n. （能够敞开胸襟吐露秘密，尤其是爱情问题的）知己}
  \wordphrase{confidant（*con<com (= completely)）}
  \wordexample{confidant（*con<com (= completely)）}
  \wordexample{a confidant（知己）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{infidel}{ˈɪnfɪdəl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FID[FI, FY, FEDER, FEAL]；*in (= not)}
  \wordfamily{confidant；infidelity；confidential；affidavit；diffident（不相信自己能力的）}
  \wordsense{n. 无宗教信仰的人；异教徒}
  \wordphrase{an infidel（无宗教信仰的人）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "an infidel".（常见搭配：无宗教信仰的人。）}
  \wordsense{a. 无宗教信仰的；异教徒的}
  \wordphrase{infidel contempt for sacred places（异教徒对圣域的蔑视）}
  \wordexample{infidel contempt for sacred places（异教徒对圣域的蔑视）}
  \wordexample{infidel（*in (= not)）}
  \wordexample{an infidel（无宗教信仰的人）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{infidelity}{ˌɪnfɪˈdeləti}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FID[FI, FY, FEDER, FEAL]；*in (= not)}
  \wordfamily{diffident（不相信自己能力的）；fidelity（忠实）；perfidious（背信弃义的）}
  \wordsense{n. 背信弃义；不忠；无宗教信仰}
  \wordphrase{infidelity（*in (= not)）}
  \wordexample{infidelity（*in (= not)）}
  \wordexample{an infidelity（背信弃义）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{affidavit}{ˌæfəˈdeɪvɪt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FID[FI, FY, FEDER, FEAL]；*af<ad (= to)}
  \wordfamily{infidelity；bona（fide）；infidel；defy；diffident（不相信自己能力的）}
  \wordsense{n. （可采作证据的）宣誓书}
  \wordphrase{affidavit of support（资助宣誓书）}
  \wordexample{swear / make / take an affidavit（立下宣誓书）}
  \wordexample{affidavit of support（资助宣誓书）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{bona fide}{ˌbəʊnə ˈfaɪdi}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FID[FI, FY, FEDER, FEAL]；*bon (= good)}
  \wordfamily{bona（fides（善意）；diffident（不相信自己能力的）；fidelity（忠实）}
  \wordsense{a. /}
  \wordphrase{be bona fide}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of bona fide.（此处展示 bona fide 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{ad. 真实的（地）；真诚的（地）；守信的（地）}
  \wordphrase{bona fide buyer（有诚意的买家）}
  \wordphrase{bona fide student（真正的学生）}
  \wordexample{bona fide buyer（有诚意的买家）}
  \wordexample{bona fide student（真正的学生）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{defy}{dɪˈfaɪ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FID[FI, FY, FEDER, FEAL]；*de (= away), fy<fid (= trust)}
  \wordfamily{bona（fide）；defiance；affidavit；defiant；diffident（不相信自己能力的）}
  \wordsense{v. 挑战，挑衅，公然反抗（权威等）；藐视（竞争、攻击等）；使不能}
  \wordphrase{defy competition（藐视竞争）}
  \wordexample{I defy you to do so.（我倒要看看你能不能这么做。）}
  \wordexample{defy competition（藐视竞争）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{defiance}{dɪˈfaɪəns}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FID[FI, FY, FEDER, FEAL]；*de (= away), fi<fid (= trust)}
  \wordfamily{defy；defiant；bona（fide）；affiance；diffident（不相信自己能力的）}
  \wordsense{n. 挑战；抵抗，反抗；蔑视}
  \wordphrase{act in defiance of orders（无视命令而行动）}
  \wordexample{set the public opinion at defiance（蔑视舆论）}
  \wordexample{act in defiance of orders（无视命令而行动）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{defiant}{dɪˈfaɪənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FID[FI, FY, FEDER, FEAL]；*de (= away), fi<fid (= trust)}
  \wordfamily{defiance；affiance；defy；diffident（不相信自己能力的）}
  \wordsense{a. 挑战的；反叛的}
  \wordphrase{defiant（*de (= away), fi<fid (= trust)）}
  \wordexample{defiant（*de (= away), fi<fid (= trust)）}
  \wordexample{be defiant（挑战的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{affiance}{əˈfaɪəns}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FID[FI, FY, FEDER, FEAL]；*af<ad (= to, near), fi<fid (= trust)}
  \wordfamily{defiant；defiance；confederate；diffident（不相信自己能力的）}
  \wordsense{v. 【常用被动语态】使订婚}
  \wordphrase{affiance（*af<ad (= to, near), fi<fid (= trust)）}
  \wordexample{affiance（*af<ad (= to, near), fi<fid (= trust)）}
  \wordexample{to affiance（使订婚）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{fiancé}{fiˈɒnseɪ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FID[FI, FY, FEDER, FEAL]；*fi<fid (= trust)}
  \wordfamily{fiancée（未婚妻）；diffident（不相信自己能力的）；fidelity（忠实）}
  \wordsense{n. 未婚夫}
  \wordphrase{fiancé（*fi<fid (= trust)）}
  \wordexample{fiancé（*fi<fid (= trust)）}
  \wordexample{a fiancé（未婚夫）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{confederate}{kənˈfedərət}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FID[FI, FY, FEDER, FEAL]；*con<com (= together)}
  \wordfamily{confederacy；affiance；confederation；diffident（不相信自己能力的）}
  \wordsense{a. 结为同盟的，联合的；（C\textasciitilde{}）（美国南北战争时）南部联邦的}
  \wordphrase{be confederate（结为同盟的，联合的）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "be confederate".（常见搭配：结为同盟的，联合的。）}
  \wordsense{n. 同盟者，同盟国；同谋}
  \wordphrase{be confederate（结为同盟的，联合的）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "be confederate".（常见搭配：结为同盟的，联合的。）}
  \wordsense{v. 和……结成同盟；使联合}
  \wordphrase{Confederate States of America（美国南部邦联〔美国南北战争时期从合众国中分离出来的南部 11 个州〕）}
  \wordexample{Confederate States of America（美国南部邦联〔美国南北战争时期从合众国中分离出来的南部 11 个州〕）}
  \wordexample{confederate（*con<com (= together)）}
  \wordexample{be confederate（结为同盟的，联合的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{confederacy}{kənˈfedərəsi}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FID[FI, FY, FEDER, FEAL]；*con<com (= together)}
  \wordfamily{diffident（不相信自己能力的）；fidelity（忠实）；perfidious（背信弃义的）}
  \wordsense{n. （国家、政党、人的）联合，同盟；同盟国，联邦}
  \wordphrase{confederacy（*con<com (= together)）}
  \wordexample{confederacy（*con<com (= together)）}
  \wordexample{a confederacy（联合，同盟）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{confederation}{kənˌfedəˈreɪʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FID[FI, FY, FEDER, FEAL]；*con<com (= together)}
  \wordfamily{confederacy；federal；confederate；federation；diffident（不相信自己能力的）}
  \wordsense{n. 同盟，联合；同盟国，联盟国；联邦；美利坚殖民地同盟（1781-1789 年的美国 13 州邦盟）}
  \wordphrase{confederation（*con<com (= together)）}
  \wordexample{confederation（*con<com (= together)）}
  \wordexample{a confederation（同盟，联合）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{federal}{ˈfedərəl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FID[FI, FY, FEDER, FEAL]}
  \wordsense{a. 联邦（制）的；联邦政府的；支持联邦政府的}
  \wordfamily{federalism（联邦主义〔制度〕；（F\textasciitilde{}）联邦党的主义〔主张〕）}
  \wordfamily{federalist（联邦主义者；（F\textasciitilde{}）联邦党党员）}
  \wordphrase{Federal Bureau of Investigation（联邦调查局〔缩写：FBI〕）}
  \wordphrase{Federal Government（联邦〔中央〕政府）}
  \wordphrase{Federal Reserve Bank（〔美国〕联邦储备银行）}
  \wordexample{Federal Bureau of Investigation（联邦调查局〔缩写：FBI〕）}
  \wordexample{Federal Government（联邦〔中央〕政府）}
  \wordexample{Federal Reserve Bank（〔美国〕联邦储备银行）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{federation}{ˌfedəˈreɪʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FID[FI, FY, FEDER, FEAL]}
  \wordsense{n. （协会、工会等的）联合，联盟，同盟；联邦制；联邦政府}
  \wordfamily{federal}
  \wordfamily{federate}
  \wordfamily{confederation}
  \wordfamily{fealty}
  \wordfamily{a federation（联合，联盟，同盟）}
  \wordphrase{a federation（联合，联盟，同盟）}
  \wordexample{a federation（联合，联盟，同盟）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{federate}{ˈfedəreɪt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FID[FI, FY, FEDER, FEAL]}
  \wordsense{v. （国家、社会、团体）结成联邦，结成同盟}
  \wordfamily{federation}
  \wordfamily{fealty}
  \wordfamily{federal}
  \wordfamily{confederation}
  \wordfamily{to federate（结成联邦，结成同盟）}
  \wordphrase{to federate（结成联邦，结成同盟）}
  \wordexample{to federate（结成联邦，结成同盟）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{fealty}{ˈfiːəlti}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FID[FI, FY, FEDER, FEAL]}
  \wordfamily{federate；federation；federal；confederation；diffident（不相信自己能力的）}
  \wordsense{n. （对领主的）忠诚}
  \wordphrase{a fealty（忠诚）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "a fealty".（常见搭配：忠诚。）}
\end{word}



\chapter{CLIN[CL]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 `clin` 意为 `to bend`，它的变形为 `cli`。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 `cliv`、`flect / flex`、`cumb / cub` 和来自盎格鲁-撒克逊语的 `croach[croch, crouch, crotch, crutch, crook]`。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{disinclined}{ˌdisin'klaind}
  \wordbreakdown{对某事不屈身的 dis (= not) + in (= on) + clin (= bend)}
  \wordfamily{disinclination（不情愿）；disincline（不想做...）；inclined（想要做...的）}
  \wordsense{a. 不愿意做...的}
  \wordphrase{disinclined to practice the}
  \wordexample{Despite natural musical gifts, our son is disinclined to practice the piano.（我儿子虽然有音乐天赋，但是却很不愿意练钢琴。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{inclined}{in'klaind}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CLIN[CL]}
  \wordfamily{incline；inclination；decline；declining；disinclined（不愿意做...的）}
  \wordsense{a. 想要做...的；有...倾向的；倾斜的 *in (= on)}
  \wordphrase{be inclined（想要做...的）}
  \wordexample{be inclined（想要做...的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{incline}{in'klain}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CLIN[CL]}
  \wordfamily{inclined；inclination；decline；declining；disinclined（不愿意做...的）}
  \wordsense{v. （使）倾向于，（使）倾斜；俯首，低头，屈身；使愿意，使想要；有...倾向；（\textasciitilde{}to, towards）易于}
  \wordphrase{incline the issue}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of incline.（此处展示 incline 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 倾斜；斜面 *in (= in)}
  \wordphrase{incline of 1 in 5（1/5 的倾斜（度））}
  \wordexample{incline of 1 in 5（1/5 的倾斜（度））}
  \wordexample{to incline（倾向于，（使）倾斜）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{inclination}{ˌinkli'neiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CLIN[CL]}
  \wordfamily{incline；decline；inclined；declining；disinclined（不愿意做...的）}
  \wordsense{n. 倾斜；弯曲；点头；趋向，意向；倾向；爱好 *in (= on)}
  \wordphrase{inclination of the head（点头（打招呼））}
  \wordexample{inclination of the head（点头（打招呼））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{decline}{di'klain}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CLIN[CL]}
  \wordfamily{inclination；declining；incline；declination；disinclined（不愿意做...的）}
  \wordsense{v. 婉拒，谢绝；减少，衰退，下跌；倾斜；（太阳）下沉}
  \wordphrase{decline the issue}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of decline.（此处展示 decline 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 衰退，衰弱；晚期；倾斜 *de (= down)}
  \wordphrase{the decline}
  \wordexample{decline an offer / an invitation to dinner（婉拒提议 / 谢绝晚餐邀请）}
  \wordexample{the decline of the Roman Empire（罗马帝国的没落）}
  \wordexample{The sun declines towards the west.（太阳西沉。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{declining}{di'klainiŋ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CLIN[CL]}
  \wordfamily{decline；declination；inclination；recline；disinclined（不愿意做...的）}
  \wordsense{a. 倾斜的；衰退中的 *de (= down)}
  \wordphrase{be declining}
  \wordexample{declining birthrate / fortune / sales（下降的出生率 / 衰运 / 减少的销售额）}
  \wordexample{spend his declining years in Geoje island（在巨济岛上度过他的晚年）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{declination}{ˌdekli'neiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CLIN[CL]}
  \wordfamily{declining；recline；decline；clinch；disinclined（不愿意做...的）}
  \wordsense{n. （指南针的）偏差；下倾 *de (= down)}
  \wordphrase{a declination（偏差）}
  \wordexample{a declination（偏差）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{recline}{ri'klain}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CLIN[CL]}
  \wordfamily{disinclined（不愿意做...的）；inclined（想要做...的）；incline（（使）倾向于）}
  \wordsense{v. （使）斜倚；靠着躺；（\textasciitilde{}on）倚靠 *re (= back)}
  \wordphrase{recline on a couch（斜靠在长椅上）}
  \wordexample{recline his head on a pillow（将头斜靠在枕头上）}
  \wordexample{recline on a couch（斜靠在长椅上）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{clinch}{klintʃ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CLIN[CL]}
  \wordfamily{disinclined（不愿意做...的）；inclined（想要做...的）；incline（（使）倾向于）}
  \wordsense{v. 敲弯（钉尖）；结束（合约、讨论），了结；（拳击中）扭住对手；用臂互抱，（热烈地）拥抱}
  \wordphrase{a clinch（敲弯）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "a clinch".（常见搭配：敲弯。）}
  \wordsense{n. 敲弯；用臂互抱，热烈拥抱}
  \wordphrase{to clinch（敲弯（钉尖））}
  \wordexample{to clinch（敲弯（钉尖））}
  \wordexample{a clinch（敲弯）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{clinic}{'klinik}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CLIN[CL]}
  \wordsense{n.（上临床课的）医院，（上临床课的）讲习所（培训班）；诊所，诊室；临床课；会诊}
  \wordfamily{clinch}
  \wordfamily{clinical}
  \wordfamily{recline}
  \wordfamily{client}
  \wordphrase{Dr. King's eye clinic（金大夫眼科门诊）}
  \wordphrase{free / birth-control / ante-natal / abortion clinic（免费诊所 / 节育诊所 / 产前诊所 / 堕胎诊所）}
  \wordexample{Dr. King's eye clinic（金大夫眼科门诊）}
  \wordexample{free / birth-control / ante-natal / abortion clinic（免费诊所 / 节育诊所 / 产前诊所 / 堕胎诊所）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{clinical}{'klinikl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CLIN[CL]}
  \wordsense{a. 临床的；临床课的；病房的；诊所的；客观的}
  \wordfamily{clinic}
  \wordfamily{client}
  \wordfamily{clinch}
  \wordfamily{clientele}
  \wordphrase{clinical pathology / psychology / diary（临床病理学 / 临床心理学 / 病房日志）}
  \wordphrase{clinical thermometer（体温计）}
  \wordphrase{clinical judgment（客观的判断）}
  \wordexample{clinical pathology / psychology / diary（临床病理学 / 临床心理学 / 病房日志）}
  \wordexample{clinical thermometer（体温计）}
  \wordexample{clinical judgment（客观的判断）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{client}{'klaiənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CLIN[CL]}
  \wordfamily{clinical；clientele；clinic；clinch；disinclined（不愿意做...的）}
  \wordsense{n. 诉讼（辩护）委托人；（商店的）顾客，常客；受保护者 *cli<clin (= bend), ent (= person who)}
  \wordphrase{client state（附属国）}
  \wordexample{a successful lawyer with hundreds of clients（拥有数百名委托人的成功律师）}
  \wordexample{client state（附属国）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{clientele}{ˌkli:ən'tel}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CLIN[CL]}
  \wordfamily{client；clinical；clinic；clinch；disinclined（不愿意做...的）}
  \wordsense{n. 【总称】委托人；（饭店、酒吧、剧院等的）主顾，常客；受保护者；扈从 *cli<clin (= bend)}
  \wordphrase{a clientele（委托人）}
  \wordexample{a clientele（委托人）}
\end{word}



\chapter{PUT}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 `put` 意为 `to think`。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{disreputable}{dis'repjətəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{不再想的 dis (= not) + re (= again) + put (= think)}
  \wordsense{a. 声名狼藉的，丢脸的，不名誉的}
  \wordfamily{disrepute 坏名声，不名誉}
  \wordfamily{disreputably 声誉不好地}
  \wordphrase{disreputable behavior}
  \wordexample{The priest was reprimanded for his disreputable behavior.（那个牧师因不名誉的行为而受到指责。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{indisputable}{ˌindi'spju:təbl}
  \wordbreakdown{不必分开考虑的 in (= not) + dis (= apart) + put (= think)}
  \wordsense{a. 无争论之余地的；明白的}
  \wordfamily{disreputable}
  \wordfamily{reputable}
  \wordfamily{compute}
  \wordfamily{computation}
  \wordphrase{indisputable fact that I}
  \wordexample{It's an indisputable fact that I earn more in sales commissions than she does.（我的销售佣金要比她的多，这是无可争辩的事实。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{reputable}{'repjətəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{再一次思考的 re (= again) + put (= think)}
  \wordsense{a. 声誉好的；值得尊敬的}
  \wordfamily{repute 把...称为，认为；（n.）名誉，名声}
  \wordfamily{reputation 名誉，名声}
  \wordfamily{reputed 名誉好的，有名的}
  \wordphrase{reputable law firm upon}
  \wordexample{I hope to join a reputable law firm upon graduating from law school.（我希望从法学院毕业后能够进入一家有名望的律师事务所工作。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{compute}{kəm'pju:t}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PUT}
  \wordfamily{computation；computer；depute；deputation；disreputable（声名狼藉的）}
  \wordsense{v. 计算；估算，估计 *com (= together)}
  \wordphrase{to compute（计算）}
  \wordexample{to compute（计算）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{computation}{ˌkɒmpju'teiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PUT}
  \wordfamily{compute；computer；depute；deputation；disreputable（声名狼藉的）}
  \wordsense{n. 计算；计算结果；数值 *com (= together)}
  \wordphrase{computation}
  \wordexample{Addition and division are forms of computation.（加法和除法都是计算方法。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{computer}{kəm'pju:tə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PUT}
  \wordfamily{computerman（电脑专家）；disreputable（声名狼藉的）；indisputable（无争论之余地的）}
  \wordsense{n. 电子计算机，电脑 *com (= together)}
  \wordphrase{computer dating（计算机约会安排（婚介），电脑择友）}
  \wordphrase{computer language（计算机语言）}
  \wordphrase{computer science（计算机科学）}
  \wordexample{computer dating（计算机约会安排（婚介），电脑择友）}
  \wordexample{computer language（计算机语言）}
  \wordexample{computer science（计算机科学）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{depute}{di'pju:t}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PUT}
  \wordfamily{computer；deputation；computation；deputy；disreputable（声名狼藉的）}
  \wordsense{v. 委任（事情、权限）；指定某人为代理（人） *de (= down, away)}
  \wordphrase{I deputed him to}
  \wordexample{I deputed him to look after Keoro Publishing Company during my absence.（在我不在期间，我委托他负责管理巨路出版社的业务。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{deputation}{ˌdepju'teiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PUT}
  \wordfamily{depute；deputy；computer；dispute；disreputable（声名狼藉的）}
  \wordsense{n. 代表；代理任命 *de (= down, away)}
  \wordphrase{a deputation（代表）}
  \wordexample{a deputation（代表）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{deputy}{'depjuti}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PUT}
  \wordfamily{deputize（代理）；disreputable（声名狼藉的）；indisputable（无争论之余地的）}
  \wordsense{n. 代理人，代表；副手；（法国、意大利等的）议员}
  \wordphrase{by deputy（请人代表做，通过代理人做）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "by deputy".（常见搭配：请人代表做，通过代理人做。）}
  \wordsense{a. 代理的；副的 *de (= down, away)}
  \wordphrase{deputy chairman / mayor（副议长 / 副市长）}
  \wordphrase{Deputy Assistant Secretary（助理部长帮办）}
  \wordexample{deputy chairman / mayor（副议长 / 副市长）}
  \wordexample{Deputy Assistant Secretary（助理部长帮办）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{dispute}{di'spju:t}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PUT}
  \wordfamily{beyond（(out of, past, without)）；disreputable（声名狼藉的）；indisputable（无争论之余地的）}
  \wordsense{n. 讨论；争论，争执；争吵}
  \wordphrase{a dispute（讨论）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "a dispute".（常见搭配：讨论。）}
  \wordsense{v. 讨论；争吵 *dis (= apart, away)}
  \wordphrase{a dispute（讨论）}
  \wordexample{a dispute（讨论）}
  \wordexample{to dispute（讨论）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{disputable}{di'spju:təbl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PUT}
  \wordfamily{dispute；disputatious；deputy；disputation；disreputable（声名狼藉的）}
  \wordsense{a. 有讨论余地的；真假可疑的 *dis (= apart, away)}
  \wordphrase{be disputable（有讨论余地的）}
  \wordexample{be disputable（有讨论余地的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{disputatious}{ˌdispju'teiʃəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PUT}
  \wordfamily{disputable；disputation；dispute；disputant；disreputable（声名狼藉的）}
  \wordsense{a. 引起争论的；好争辩的 *dis (= apart, away)}
  \wordphrase{be disputatious（引起争论的）}
  \wordexample{be disputatious（引起争论的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{disputation}{ˌdispju'teiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PUT}
  \wordfamily{disputatious；disputant；disputable；impute；disreputable（声名狼藉的）}
  \wordsense{n. 争论；讨论 *dis (= apart, away)}
  \wordphrase{a disputation（争论）}
  \wordexample{a disputation（争论）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{disputant}{di'spju:tənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PUT}
  \wordfamily{disputation；impute；disputatious；imputation；disreputable（声名狼藉的）}
  \wordsense{n. 争论者，辩论者 *dis (= apart)}
  \wordphrase{a disputant（争论者，辩论者）}
  \wordexample{a disputant（争论者，辩论者）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{impute}{im'pju:t}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PUT}
  \wordfamily{disputant；imputation；disputation；putative；disreputable（声名狼藉的）}
  \wordsense{v. 归咎于；归因于 *im<in (= to)}
  \wordphrase{to impute（归咎于）}
  \wordexample{to impute（归咎于）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{imputation}{ˌimpju'teiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PUT}
  \wordfamily{impute；putative；disputant；disputation；disreputable（声名狼藉的）}
  \wordsense{n. 归罪，（将责任等）归咎；非难 *im<in (= into)}
  \wordphrase{imputations on a person's character（诬蔑某人的人格）}
  \wordexample{imputations on a person's character（诬蔑某人的人格）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{putative}{'pju:tətiv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PUT}
  \wordfamily{imputation；impute；disputant；disputation；disreputable（声名狼藉的）}
  \wordsense{a. 推定的，猜测的；传说的}
  \wordphrase{his putative father（推定是他父亲的那个人）}
  \wordphrase{putative marriage（公认婚姻，善意婚姻（指善意缔结的婚姻，但一方或双方却未意识到其为非法婚姻））}
  \wordexample{his putative father（推定是他父亲的那个人）}
  \wordexample{putative marriage（公认婚姻，善意婚姻（指善意缔结的婚姻，但一方或双方却未意识到其为非法婚姻））}
\end{word}



\chapter{UT, US}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 `ut, us` 意为 `to use`。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{disabuse}{ˌdisə'bju:z}
  \wordbreakdown{使不错用 dis (= not) + ab (= away) + us (= use)}
  \wordsense{vt. sb + of + sth 使（对错误的想法或犯的错）省悟}
  \wordfamily{peruse}
  \wordfamily{abuse}
  \wordfamily{abusive}
  \wordfamily{usage}
  \wordphrase{Professor King disabused the}
  \wordexample{Professor King disabused the student of the idea that he can pass this course without hard regular work.（金教授使那个学生认识到，不努力地、长期地学习是不可能通过这门课的。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{peruse}{pə'ru:z}
  \wordbreakdown{很好地使用书 per (= intensive) + us (= use)}
  \wordfamily{perusal（细读，精读）；disabuse（sb + of + sth 使（对错误的想法或犯）；abuse（滥用）}
  \wordsense{vt. 细读，精读}
  \wordphrase{I perused the manual}
  \wordexample{I perused the manual for hours, but couldn't figure out how the program works.（我花几个小时仔细阅读了使用手册，但还是没弄清楚程序是怎么运行的。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{abuse}{ə'bju:z}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 UT, US}
  \wordfamily{disabuse（sb + of + sth 使（对错误的想法或犯）；peruse（细读）；abusive（辱骂的）}
  \wordsense{n. 滥用，乱用，误用；虐待；辱骂，谩骂；（pl.）弊端，恶习}
  \wordphrase{child abuse（虐待儿童）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "child abuse".（常见搭配：虐待儿童。）}
  \wordsense{v. 滥用，误用，乱用；虐待；辱骂 *ab (= away)}
  \wordphrase{abuse of power（滥用权力）}
  \wordexample{abuse of power（滥用权力）}
  \wordexample{an abuse（滥用，乱用，误用）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{abusive}{ə'bju:siv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 UT, US}
  \wordfamily{abuse；usage；useful；usual；disabuse（sb + of + sth 使（对错误的想法或犯）}
  \wordsense{a. 辱骂的，诽谤的，谩骂的；滥用的 *ab (= away)}
  \wordphrase{use abusive language（破口大骂）}
  \wordexample{use abusive language（破口大骂）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{usage}{'ju:sidʒ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 UT, US}
  \wordfamily{disabuse（sb + of + sth 使（对错误的想法或犯）；peruse（细读）；abuse（滥用）}
  \wordsense{n. 使用（法）；对待；（语言的）惯用法；语法；习惯，惯例}
  \wordphrase{annual usage（年使用量）}
  \wordexample{Fowler's Dictionary of Modern English Usage（《福勒现代英语用法词典》）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{useful}{'ju:sfl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 UT, US}
  \wordsense{a. 有用的，有帮助的}
  \wordfamily{usefulness（有用；有效性）}
  \wordfamily{useless（无用的；无益的；无效的）}
  \wordfamily{be useful（有用的，有帮助的）}
  \wordphrase{be useful（有用的，有帮助的）}
  \wordexample{be useful（有用的，有帮助的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{usual}{'ju:ʒuəl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 UT, US}
  \wordsense{a. 平常的；通常的，惯例的}
  \wordfamily{usually（通常地，惯常地）}
  \wordfamily{as usual（像往常一样，照常）}
  \wordphrase{as is usual with picnickers（对郊游者而言很平常地）}
  \wordexample{as is usual with picnickers（对郊游者而言很平常地）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{usurp}{ju:'zɜ:p}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 UT, US}
  \wordsense{v. 篡夺（权力、地位等），篡位；侵占}
  \wordfamily{usurper（篡夺者，篡位者；侵占者）}
  \wordfamily{usurpation（篡权，篡位；侵占）}
  \wordphrase{usurp the throne（篡夺王位）}
  \wordexample{usurp the throne（篡夺王位）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{usury}{'ju:ʒəri}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 UT, US}
  \wordfamily{disabuse（sb + of + sth 使（对错误的想法或犯）；peruse（细读）；abuse（滥用）}
  \wordsense{n. 高利贷；高利}
  \wordphrase{an usury（高利贷）}
  \wordexample{an usury（高利贷）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{usurious}{ju:'zjuəriəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 UT, US}
  \wordsense{a. 高利贷的；放高利贷的}
  \wordfamily{usury}
  \wordfamily{usurer}
  \wordfamily{usurp}
  \wordfamily{utensil}
  \wordphrase{usurious transaction（高利贷交易）}
  \wordphrase{usurious rate of interest（高利贷利率）}
  \wordexample{usurious transaction（高利贷交易）}
  \wordexample{usurious rate of interest（高利贷利率）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{usurer}{'ju:ʒərə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 UT, US}
  \wordfamily{usurious；utensil；usury；utilitarian；disabuse（sb + of + sth 使（对错误的想法或犯）}
  \wordsense{n. 高利贷者 *er (= person who)}
  \wordphrase{an usurer（高利贷者）}
  \wordexample{an usurer（高利贷者）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{utensil}{ju:'tensl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 UT, US}
  \wordfamily{disabuse（sb + of + sth 使（对错误的想法或犯）；peruse（细读）；abuse（滥用）}
  \wordsense{n. （尤指家用的）器皿，用具}
  \wordphrase{utensils of war（武器）}
  \wordexample{household / kitchen / farming utensils（家用物品 / 厨房用具 / 农具）}
  \wordexample{utensils of war（武器）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{utilitarian}{ju:ˌtili'teəriən}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 UT, US}
  \wordfamily{utensil；utilitarianism；usurer；utility；disabuse（sb + of + sth 使（对错误的想法或犯）}
  \wordsense{a. 功利的，实利的；（U\textasciitilde{}）功利主义（者）的}
  \wordphrase{be utilitarian（功利的，实利的）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "be utilitarian".（常见搭配：功利的，实利的。）}
  \wordsense{n. （U\textasciitilde{}）功利主义者}
  \wordphrase{be utilitarian（功利的，实利的）}
  \wordexample{be utilitarian（功利的，实利的）}
  \wordexample{an utilitarian（功利主义者）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{utilitarianism}{ju:ˌtili'teəriənizəm}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 UT, US}
  \wordsense{n.（信奉最大多数人的最大幸福的）功利主义；实利主义}
  \wordfamily{utilitarian}
  \wordfamily{utility}
  \wordfamily{utensil}
  \wordfamily{utilize}
  \wordfamily{an utilitarianism（功利主义）}
  \wordphrase{an utilitarianism（功利主义）}
  \wordexample{an utilitarianism（功利主义）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{utility}{ju:'tiləti}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 UT, US}
  \wordfamily{disabuse（sb + of + sth 使（对错误的想法或犯）；peruse（细读）；abuse（滥用）}
  \wordsense{n. 有用，有益；效用；（pl.）（水、电、气等的）公用事业}
  \wordphrase{public utility（公共事业）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "public utility".（常见搭配：公共事业。）}
  \wordsense{a. 实用的；（家具、衣服等）多用途的；（肉等）下等的}
  \wordphrase{utility van / truck（多用途货车 / 卡车）}
  \wordexample{utility van / truck（多用途货车 / 卡车）}
  \wordexample{an utility（有用，有益）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{utilize}{'ju:tilaiz}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 UT, US}
  \wordfamily{utilizable（可利用的）；utilization（利用）；CUR（CURS[COR COUR COURS）；disabuse（sb + of + sth 使（对错误的想法或犯）}
  \wordsense{v. 利用 *ize (= make)}
  \wordphrase{CUR CURS[COR COUR COURS}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of utilize.（此处展示 utilize 的常见用法。）}
\end{word}



\chapter{CUR, CURS[COR, COUR, COURS, COARS]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 `cur, curs` 意为 `to run`，它们的变形为 `cor`、`cour`、`cours`、`coars`。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 `lop`，来自希腊语的 `drom`，以及来自盎格鲁-撒克逊语的 `run`。此外，还有意为 `vehicle` 的 `car / char` 等。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{discursive}{dis'kɜ:siv}
  \wordbreakdown{分开跑的 dis (= apart) + curs (= run)}
  \wordsense{a.（人）言行散漫的，不得要领的；离题的；推论的}
  \wordfamily{discursiveness 散漫，不得要领}
  \wordfamily{discursively 散漫地}
  \wordphrase{discursive speech difficult}
  \wordexample{He gave a discursive speech, difficult to follow.（他说的话东拉西扯，让人难以理解。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{concur}{kən'kɜ:(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{一起跑 con<com (= together) + cur (= run)}
  \wordsense{vi. with + sb; in + sth 同意，一致；同时发生；合作}
  \wordfamily{concurrence 同意，一致；合作；同时发生}
  \wordfamily{concurrent 同时发生的；合作的；一致的}
  \wordfamily{concurrently 同时地}
  \wordphrase{concur with my parents}
  \wordexample{I concur with my parents that illegal drugs carry many risks.（我同意我父母关于非法药品带有很大风险的意见。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{precursor}{pri:'kɜ:sə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{事先、首先跑 pre (= before) + curs (= run)}
  \wordsense{n. 预告，预兆；先驱者，先觉者；前任}
  \wordfamily{precursory 先驱的；先兆的；预备的}
  \wordphrase{precursors to the modern}
  \wordexample{It has been said the Poe's novels were precursors to the modern detective novel.（坡的小说被认为是现代侦探小说的先驱。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{cursory}{'kɜ:səri}
  \wordbreakdown{仿佛跑似的 curs (= run)}
  \wordsense{a.（做事、阅读等）草率的，匆匆忙忙的，粗略的}
  \wordfamily{precursor}
  \wordfamily{recourse}
  \wordfamily{concur}
  \wordfamily{extracurricular}
  \wordphrase{cursory glance}
  \wordexample{The customs agent gave only a cursory glance at our luggage.（海关人员只是草草地看了一眼我们的行李。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{recourse}{ri'kɔ:s}
  \wordbreakdown{向后跑 re (= back) + cours<curs (= run)}
  \wordfamily{cursory；extracurricular；precursor；incur；discursive（（人）言行散漫的）}
  \wordsense{n. 依靠，依赖；求助对象}
  \wordphrase{recourse left}
  \wordexample{The condemned man had no legal recourse left to him and was executed.（那个死刑囚犯没有任何法律求助途径，最终被处死。）}
  \wordexample{recourse left}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{extracurricular}{ˌekstrəkə'rikjulə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CUR, CURS[COR, COUR, COURS, COARS]}
  \wordfamily{incur；occur；occurrence；recur；discursive（（人）言行散漫的）}
  \wordsense{a. 课外的 *extra (= outside)}
  \wordphrase{extracurricular activities（课外活动）}
  \wordexample{extracurricular activities（课外活动）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{incur}{in'kɜ:(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CUR, CURS[COR, COUR, COURS, COARS]}
  \wordfamily{extracurricular；occur；occurrence；recur；discursive（（人）言行散漫的）}
  \wordsense{v. 招致，带来；背负（债务），蒙受（损失），遭受 *in (= into)}
  \wordphrase{incur the issue}
  \wordexample{incur debts / hatred / great expense / danger / displeasure（负债 / 招致仇恨 / 花费大量经费 / 招致危险 / 触犯，得罪）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{occur}{ə'kɜ:(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CUR, CURS[COR, COUR, COURS, COARS]}
  \wordfamily{incur；occurrence；extracurricular；recur；discursive（（人）言行散漫的）}
  \wordsense{v. 出现，发生；（在心中）浮现；存在；被发现 *oc<ob (= to)}
  \wordphrase{An idea has occurred}
  \wordexample{An idea has occurred to me.（我有了一个主意。）}
  \wordexample{It occurred to me that\textasciitilde{}（我想到...）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{occurrence}{ə'kʌrəns}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CUR, CURS[COR, COUR, COURS, COARS]}
  \wordfamily{discursive（（人）言行散漫的）；concur（with + sb; in + sth 同意）；precursor（预告）}
  \wordsense{n. 发生的事情、事件；出现，发生 *oc<ob (= to)}
  \wordphrase{an occurrence（发生的事情、事件）}
  \wordexample{an occurrence（发生的事情、事件）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{recur}{ri'kɜ:(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CUR, CURS[COR, COUR, COURS, COARS]}
  \wordfamily{occurrence；recurrence；occur；recurrent；discursive（（人）言行散漫的）}
  \wordsense{v. 再发生，复发；回归，重新浮上心头，重新忆起 *re (= again)}
  \wordphrase{problem which recurs periodically（定期反复出现的问题）}
  \wordexample{problem which recurs periodically（定期反复出现的问题）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{recurrence}{ri'kʌrəns}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CUR, CURS[COR, COUR, COURS, COARS]}
  \wordfamily{recur；recurrent；occurrence；undercurrent；discursive（（人）言行散漫的）}
  \wordsense{n. 重新想起；复发，反复 *re (= again)}
  \wordphrase{frequent recurrence of these headaches（这样的头疼的频繁复发）}
  \wordexample{frequent recurrence of these headaches（这样的头疼的频繁复发）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{recurrent}{ri'kʌrənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CUR, CURS[COR, COUR, COURS, COARS]}
  \wordfamily{recurrence；undercurrent；recur；curriculum；discursive（（人）言行散漫的）}
  \wordsense{a. 定期（频繁）重复的；复发的，周期性的 *re (= again)}
  \wordphrase{recurrent expenses（定期开支）}
  \wordphrase{recurrent fever（回归热）}
  \wordexample{recurrent expenses（定期开支）}
  \wordexample{recurrent fever（回归热）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{undercurrent}{'ʌndəkʌrənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CUR, CURS[COR, COUR, COURS, COARS]}
  \wordfamily{recurrent；curriculum；recurrence；currish；discursive（（人）言行散漫的）}
  \wordsense{n. 底流，潜流；（不浮于表面的思想、感情等的）暗流 *under (= under)}
  \wordphrase{undercurrent of opposition / melancholy（潜在的敌对 / 潜在的忧郁）}
  \wordexample{undercurrent of opposition / melancholy（潜在的敌对 / 潜在的忧郁）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{curriculum}{kə'rikjuləm}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CUR, CURS[COR, COUR, COURS, COARS]}
  \wordsense{n.（学校、大学等的）课程}
  \wordfamily{undercurrent}
  \wordfamily{currish}
  \wordfamily{recurrent}
  \wordfamily{current}
  \wordphrase{curriculum vitae（履历）}
  \wordexample{curriculum vitae（履历）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{currish}{'kɜ:riʃ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CUR, CURS[COR, COUR, COURS, COARS]}
  \wordsense{a. 下贱的}
  \wordfamily{curriculum}
  \wordfamily{current}
  \wordfamily{undercurrent}
  \wordfamily{currency}
  \wordfamily{be currish（下贱的）}
  \wordphrase{be currish（下贱的）}
  \wordexample{be currish（下贱的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{current}{'kʌrənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CUR, CURS[COR, COUR, COURS, COARS]}
  \wordfamily{go（(swim)）；with（(against)）；discursive（（人）言行散漫的）}
  \wordsense{a. 通用的，现行的；现在的，正在进行的；流通的}
  \wordphrase{air currents（气流）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "air currents".（常见搭配：气流。）}
  \wordsense{n. （电、水、气等的）流；（时间、舆论、思想等的）潮流，倾向，趋势}
  \wordphrase{current money（通用货币）}
  \wordphrase{current news（时事新闻）}
  \wordexample{the current of time (the times)（潮流，时代潮流）}
  \wordexample{current money（通用货币）}
  \wordexample{current news（时事新闻）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{currency}{'kʌrənsi}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CUR, CURS[COR, COUR, COURS, COARS]}
  \wordfamily{discursive（（人）言行散漫的）；concur（with + sb; in + sth 同意）；precursor（预告）}
  \wordsense{n. 通货，货币；流通，通用；流行，普及}
  \wordphrase{gold / paper currency（金币 / 纸币）}
  \wordphrase{currency circulation（货币流通）}
  \wordexample{gold / paper currency（金币 / 纸币）}
  \wordexample{currency circulation（货币流通）}
  \wordexample{Many slang words have short currency.（很多俚语的流行时间都非常短。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{courier}{'kuriə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CUR, CURS[COR, COUR, COURS, COARS]}
  \wordfamily{currency；succour；current；corridor；discursive（（人）言行散漫的）}
  \wordsense{n. 送快信、外交信件的信使；（旅行社的）导游，向导 *cour<cur (= run), ier (= person who)}
  \wordphrase{the courier}
  \wordexample{act as a diplomatic courier bringing an important message from the president for the new premier（以外交信使的身份向新任首相传达总统的重要信息）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{succour}{'sʌkə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CUR, CURS[COR, COUR, COURS, COARS]}
  \wordfamily{courier；corridor；currency；excursion；discursive（（人）言行散漫的）}
  \wordsense{n. （困难时的）援助，救助}
  \wordphrase{a succour（援助，救助）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "a succour".（常见搭配：援助，救助。）}
  \wordsense{v. 帮助（困难的人），援助 *suc<sub (= under), cor<cur (= run)}
  \wordphrase{a succour（援助，救助）}
  \wordexample{a succour（援助，救助）}
  \wordexample{to succour（帮助（困难的人），援助）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{corridor}{'kɒridɔ:(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CUR, CURS[COR, COUR, COURS, COARS]}
  \wordfamily{discursive（（人）言行散漫的）；concur（with + sb; in + sth 同意）；precursor（预告）}
  \wordsense{n. 走廊，回廊 *cor<cur (= run)}
  \wordphrase{corridors of power（权力走廊：暗中左右决策的权力中心）}
  \wordexample{corridors of power（权力走廊：暗中左右决策的权力中心）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{excursion}{ik'skɜ:ʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CUR, CURS[COR, COUR, COURS, COARS]}
  \wordfamily{discursive（（人）言行散漫的）；concur（with + sb; in + sth 同意）；precursor（预告）}
  \wordsense{n. 远足，短程旅行 *ex (= out)}
  \wordphrase{the excursion}
  \wordexample{make an excursion to the mountain（去大山中远足）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{incursion}{in'kɜ:ʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CUR, CURS[COR, COUR, COURS, COARS]}
  \wordfamily{excursion；cursive；corridor；curse；discursive（（人）言行散漫的）}
  \wordsense{n. （多指非永久占领的）袭击，侵犯，入侵 *in (= into)}
  \wordphrase{the incursion}
  \wordexample{the Danish incursions on our coasts in early times（早期丹麦人对我们（英国）海岸的入侵）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{cursive}{'kɜ:siv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CUR, CURS[COR, COUR, COURS, COARS]}
  \wordsense{a.（笔迹）草书的，草书体的}
  \wordfamily{incursion}
  \wordfamily{curse}
  \wordfamily{excursion}
  \wordfamily{course}
  \wordfamily{be cursive（草书的，草书体的）}
  \wordphrase{be cursive（草书的，草书体的）}
  \wordexample{be cursive（草书的，草书体的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{curse}{kɜ:s}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CUR, CURS[COR, COUR, COURS, COARS]}
  \wordfamily{discursive（（人）言行散漫的）；concur（with + sb; in + sth 同意）；precursor（预告）}
  \wordsense{v. 诅咒，咒骂；（be cursed with）使因...而遭难}
  \wordphrase{to curse（诅咒，咒骂）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "to curse".（常见搭配：诅咒，咒骂。）}
  \wordsense{n. 诅咒，咒骂；祸因，祸根；被诅咒的东西；（the\textasciitilde{}）月经}
  \wordphrase{curse at a fate（诅咒命运）}
  \wordexample{curse at a fate（诅咒命运）}
  \wordexample{to curse（诅咒，咒骂）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{course}{kɔ:s}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CUR, CURS[COR, COUR, COURS, COARS]}
  \wordfamily{coursing（带着猎犬打猎（野兔））；discursive（（人）言行散漫的）；concur（with + sb; in + sth 同意）}
  \wordsense{n. 进程；道路；（赛跑、比赛的）跑道；进展，趋势；（行动的）方针，（行动的）方向；（连续的）课，（学校的）课程，（大学的）科目；单位；讲座；（吃饭时按顺序端上来的一道道的）菜}
  \wordphrase{graduate course（研究生课程）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "graduate course".（常见搭配：研究生课程。）}
  \wordsense{v. 以犬（灰狗）狩猎（特别是野兔）；（快速移动地）穿过；（液体的）流动 *cours<curs (= run)}
  \wordphrase{in due course（在今后适当的时候）}
  \wordphrase{a matter of course（理所当然之事，必然的结果）}
  \wordphrase{stay the course（坚持到底，不放弃）}
  \wordexample{in due course（在今后适当的时候）}
  \wordexample{a matter of course（理所当然之事，必然的结果）}
  \wordexample{stay the course（坚持到底，不放弃）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{concourse}{'kɒŋkɔ:s}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CUR, CURS[COR, COUR, COURS, COARS]}
  \wordfamily{course；intercourse；curse；coarse；discursive（（人）言行散漫的）}
  \wordsense{n. （物品或人等的）集合；合流；集合场所；（站、机场、会馆等的）中央大厅，中央广场 *con<com (= together), cours<curs (= run)}
  \wordphrase{unforeseen concourse of circumstances（未能预料到的各种情况的同时发生）}
  \wordexample{unforeseen concourse of circumstances（未能预料到的各种情况的同时发生）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{intercourse}{'intəkɔ:s}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CUR, CURS[COR, COUR, COURS, COARS]}
  \wordfamily{concourse；coarse；course；curse；discursive（（人）言行散漫的）}
  \wordsense{n. （个人间的）交往；（人、国家间的）往来；（意见等的）交换；性交 *inter (= between), cours<curs (= run)}
  \wordphrase{social / sexual intercourse（社交 / 性交）}
  \wordexample{our commercial intercourse with the Soviet Union（我国与前苏联的贸易往来）}
  \wordexample{social / sexual intercourse（社交 / 性交）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{coarse}{kɔ:s}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CUR, CURS[COR, COUR, COURS, COARS]}
  \wordfamily{intercourse；concourse；course；curse；discursive（（人）言行散漫的）}
  \wordsense{a. 粗劣的；（布、网等）质地粗糙的；粗俗的，下流的 *coars<curs (= run)}
  \wordphrase{coarse food（粗粮）}
  \wordphrase{coarse joke（低俗的笑话）}
  \wordexample{coarse food（粗粮）}
  \wordexample{coarse joke（低俗的笑话）}
\end{word}



\chapter{SEMIN[SEMEN]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 `semin` 意为 `seed`，它的变形为 `semen`。同义词根有来自希腊语的 `spor`。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{disseminate}{di'semineit}
  \wordbreakdown{使分离从而分开播种 dis (= apart) + semin (= seed)}
  \wordfamily{dissemination（散布）；inseminate（播种）；insemination（播种）}
  \wordsense{vt. 散布（思想、主义等）；普及，传播}
  \wordphrase{disseminating information globally}
  \wordexample{The Internet is an amazing medium for disseminating information globally.（网络是一个将信息传播到全世界的惊人媒体。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{inseminate}{in'semineit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SEMIN[SEMEN]}
  \wordfamily{insemination；seminary；seminar；semen；disseminate（散布（思想、主义等））}
  \wordsense{v. 播种；灌输；使...受精 *in (= into)}
  \wordphrase{to inseminate（播种）}
  \wordexample{to inseminate（播种）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{insemination}{inˌsemi'neiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SEMIN[SEMEN]}
  \wordfamily{disseminate（散布（思想、主义等））；inseminate（播种）；seminary（神学院）}
  \wordsense{n. 播种；受孕；授精 *in (= into)}
  \wordphrase{an insemination（播种）}
  \wordexample{an insemination（播种）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{seminary}{'seminəri}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SEMIN[SEMEN]}
  \wordfamily{seminarist（神学院学生）；disseminate（散布（思想、主义等））；inseminate（播种）}
  \wordsense{n. 神学院；（罪恶等的）温床 *ary (= place where)}
  \wordphrase{seminary of vice（罪恶的温床）}
  \wordexample{seminary of vice（罪恶的温床）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{seminar}{'semina:(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SEMIN[SEMEN]}
  \wordfamily{seminary；semen；insemination；inseminate；disseminate（散布（思想、主义等））}
  \wordsense{n. （大学的）研讨会（学生和讲师或教授为对某些问题进行研究而开设的研讨班或讨论会）；研究会；专家讨论会}
  \wordphrase{a seminar（研讨会（学生和讲师或教授为对某些问题进行研究而开设的研讨班或讨论会））}
  \wordexample{a seminar（研讨会（学生和讲师或教授为对某些问题进行研究而开设的研讨班或讨论会））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{semen}{'si:men}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SEMIN[SEMEN]}
  \wordfamily{seminar；seminary；insemination；inseminate；disseminate（散布（思想、主义等））}
  \wordsense{n. 精液 *semen<semin (= seed)}
  \wordphrase{a semen（精液）}
  \wordexample{a semen（精液）}
\end{word}



\chapter{TORT[TOR]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 `tort` 意为 `to twist`，它的变形为 `tor`。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{distortion}{di'stɔ:ʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{用力拧 dis (= intensive) + tort (= twist)}
  \wordsense{n. 曲解，歪曲；变形，扭曲}
  \wordfamily{distort 歪曲，使变形}
  \wordfamily{distorted 歪曲的}
  \wordphrase{distortion of the truth}
  \wordexample{What he says is a distortion of the truth.（他的话是对事实的歪曲。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{extort}{ik'stɔ:t}
  \wordbreakdown{扭曲使出来 ex (= out) + tort (= twist)}
  \wordsense{vt. sth + from + sb 敲诈，强求，逼取；强夺，强取}
  \wordfamily{extortion 强求，强夺；勒索钱财}
  \wordfamily{extortionate 勒索的；强求的}
  \wordfamily{extorted money}
  \wordphrase{extorted money}
  \wordexample{The Mafioso extorted money from an old widow to pay her husband's debt.（黑手党成员向一个老寡妇勒索钱财以偿还她丈夫的债务。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{tortuous}{'tɔ:tʃuəs}
  \wordbreakdown{扭曲的 tort (= twist)}
  \wordsense{a. 弯曲的，曲折的；不正直的，不坦率的；绕圈子的}
  \wordfamily{extort}
  \wordfamily{contort}
  \wordfamily{distortion}
  \wordfamily{contortion}
  \wordphrase{tortuous mountain road}
  \wordexample{My arms are tired from steering along this tortuous mountain road.（沿着这条曲折的山路开车，我的胳膊很累。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{contort}{kən'tɔ:t}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TORT[TOR]}
  \wordfamily{contortion；retort；tort；tortoise；distortion（曲解）}
  \wordsense{v. 扭曲，歪曲（意义） *con<com (= together)}
  \wordphrase{face contorted with pain（因疼痛而扭曲的脸）}
  \wordexample{contort a word out of its ordinary meaning（曲解词义）}
  \wordexample{face contorted with pain（因疼痛而扭曲的脸）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{contortion}{kən'tɔ:ʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TORT[TOR]}
  \wordfamily{contortionist（（身体可以自由弯曲的）柔体杂技演员）；distortion（曲解）；extort（sth + from + sb 敲诈）}
  \wordsense{n. （特指脸、身体的）扭曲，歪曲；扭曲状态 *con<com (= together)}
  \wordphrase{contortions of an acrobat（杂技演员的软功）}
  \wordexample{contortions of an acrobat（杂技演员的软功）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{retort}{ri'tɔ:t}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TORT[TOR]}
  \wordfamily{contortion；tort；contort；tortoise；distortion（曲解）}
  \wordsense{v. （特指对指责、挑战等）回嘴；反击，报复}
  \wordphrase{retort the issue}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of retort.（此处展示 retort 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 回嘴，反驳；蒸馏器；曲颈瓶；（提纯水银等使用的）干馏釜 *re (= back)}
  \wordphrase{retort insult for insult（以牙还牙）}
  \wordphrase{retort an argument（反驳论点）}
  \wordphrase{make an insolent retort（傲慢地回嘴）}
  \wordexample{retort insult for insult（以牙还牙）}
  \wordexample{retort an argument（反驳论点）}
  \wordexample{make an insolent retort（傲慢地回嘴）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{tort}{tɔ:t}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TORT[TOR]}
  \wordsense{n. 侵权行为}
  \wordfamily{retort}
  \wordfamily{tortoise}
  \wordfamily{contortion}
  \wordfamily{torture}
  \wordfamily{a tort（侵权行为）}
  \wordphrase{a tort（侵权行为）}
  \wordexample{a tort（侵权行为）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{tortoise}{'tɔ:təs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TORT[TOR]}
  \wordfamily{tortoiseshell（龟甲，玳甲）；distortion（曲解）；extort（sth + from + sb 敲诈）}
  \wordsense{n. 陆龟，乌龟}
  \wordphrase{Hare and Tortoise（龟兔赛跑）}
  \wordexample{Hare and Tortoise（龟兔赛跑）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{torture}{'tɔ:tʃə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TORT[TOR]}
  \wordfamily{torturer（拷打者）；distortion（曲解）；extort（sth + from + sb 敲诈）}
  \wordsense{n. 拷问；折磨，痛苦}
  \wordphrase{the torture}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of torture.（此处展示 torture 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. 拷问；折磨；歪曲}
  \wordphrase{torture politics（酷刑政治）}
  \wordphrase{instruments of torture（刑具，刑架，拷问工具）}
  \wordphrase{tortured with anxiety（因焦虑而备受折磨）}
  \wordexample{torture politics（酷刑政治）}
  \wordexample{instruments of torture（刑具，刑架，拷问工具）}
  \wordexample{tortured with anxiety（因焦虑而备受折磨）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{torch}{tɔ:tʃ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TORT[TOR]}
  \wordfamily{torchlight（火炬之光）；distortion（曲解）；extort（sth + from + sb 敲诈）}
  \wordsense{n. 火把，火炬；照明物；（焊接用）喷灯，喷枪}
  \wordphrase{torch race（（古希腊的）火炬接力赛跑）}
  \wordphrase{torch of Hymen（爱情）}
  \wordexample{torch race（（古希腊的）火炬接力赛跑）}
  \wordexample{torch of Hymen（爱情）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{torment}{'tɔ:ment}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TORT[TOR]}
  \wordfamily{torch；tornado；torture；torpedo；distortion（曲解）}
  \wordsense{n. （身体、精神上的）剧痛；苦恼}
  \wordphrase{the torment}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of torment.（此处展示 torment 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. 使痛苦，折磨；烦扰}
  \wordphrase{torments of jealousy（因嫉妒而痛苦）}
  \wordexample{tormented with neuralgia / hunger / mosquitoes（受神经痛的折磨 / 受饥饿的折磨 / 受蚊子的烦扰）}
  \wordexample{torments of jealousy（因嫉妒而痛苦）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{tornado}{tɔ:'neidəu}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TORT[TOR]}
  \wordfamily{torment；torpedo；torch；torture；distortion（曲解）}
  \wordsense{n. 龙卷风；旋风；飓风}
  \wordphrase{a tornado（龙卷风）}
  \wordexample{a tornado（龙卷风）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{torpedo}{tɔ:'pi:dəu}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TORT[TOR]}
  \wordfamily{tornado；torment；torch；torture；distortion（曲解）}
  \wordsense{n. 鱼雷，水雷}
  \wordphrase{the torpedo}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of torpedo.（此处展示 torpedo 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. 用鱼雷攻击（破坏）；使彻底完蛋，破坏（政策、制度等）}
  \wordphrase{torpedo boat（鱼雷艇）}
  \wordphrase{torpedo tube（鱼雷发射管）}
  \wordexample{torpedo boat（鱼雷艇）}
  \wordexample{torpedo tube（鱼雷发射管）}
  \wordexample{Who torpedoed the Disarmament Conference?（谁破坏了裁军会议？）}
\end{word}



\chapter{SEMBL}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 `sembl` 意为 `same, equal`。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 `equ`、`simil / simul`、`par`、`ident` 以及来自希腊语的 `tauto`。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{dissemble}{di'sembl}
  \wordbreakdown{使不一样 dis (= not) + sembl (= same)}
  \wordfamily{dissembler（伪善者，伪君子）；assemble（聚集）；assemblage（集合）}
  \wordsense{vt. 隐藏，伪装}
  \wordphrase{He dissembled when he}
  \wordexample{He dissembled when he claimed to be sick.（他说身体不适，其实是装的。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{assemble}{ə'sembl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SEMBL}
  \wordfamily{assemblage；assembly；ensemble；resemble；dissemble（隐藏）}
  \wordsense{v. 聚集，召集；装配（机器） *as<ad (= near)}
  \wordphrase{to assemble（聚集，召集）}
  \wordexample{to assemble（聚集，召集）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{assemblage}{ə'semblidʒ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SEMBL}
  \wordfamily{assemble；assembly；ensemble；resemble；dissemble（隐藏）}
  \wordsense{n. 集合，集会；组装；集合物；群众，会众 *as<ad (= near)}
  \wordphrase{an assemblage（集合，集会）}
  \wordexample{an assemblage（集合，集会）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{assembly}{ə'sembli}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SEMBL}
  \wordfamily{dissemble（隐藏）；assemble（聚集）；assemblage（集合）}
  \wordsense{n. 集会，会合；会议；（韩国、法国等的）国会；（汽车等零件的）组装；（军队的）集合号 *as<ad (= near)}
  \wordphrase{assembly hall（会场；装配厂）}
  \wordphrase{assembly line（（工厂产品的）流水线）}
  \wordphrase{assembly plant（装配厂）}
  \wordexample{assembly hall（会场；装配厂）}
  \wordexample{assembly line（（工厂产品的）流水线）}
  \wordexample{assembly plant（装配厂）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{ensemble}{ɒn'sɔmbl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SEMBL}
  \wordfamily{assembly；resemble；assemblage；resemblance；dissemble（隐藏）}
  \wordsense{n. 整体；总效果；合唱曲；合奏团；（女性）套装 *en (= into)}
  \wordphrase{an ensemble（整体）}
  \wordexample{an ensemble（整体）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{resemble}{ri'zembl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SEMBL}
  \wordfamily{ensemble；resemblance；assembly；semblance；dissemble（隐藏）}
  \wordsense{v. 和...相似 *re (= again)}
  \wordphrase{to resemble（和...相似）}
  \wordexample{to resemble（和...相似）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{resemblance}{ri'zembləns}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SEMBL}
  \wordfamily{resemble；semblance；ensemble；assembly；dissemble（隐藏）}
  \wordsense{n. 相似，相像，相似点 *re (= again)}
  \wordphrase{a resemblance（相似，相像，相似点）}
  \wordexample{a resemblance（相似，相像，相似点）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{semblance}{'sembləns}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SEMBL}
  \wordfamily{resemblance；resemble；ensemble；assembly；dissemble（隐藏）}
  \wordsense{n. 相似}
  \wordphrase{semblance}
  \wordexample{a semblance（相似}
\end{word}



\chapter{QUISIT, QUEST, QUIR, QUER[QUIST]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 `quisit`、`quest`、`quir`、`quer` 意为 `to ask, to seek`，变形为 `quist`。同义词根为来自拉丁语的 `rog`。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{disquisition}{ˌdiskwi'ziʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{分别地、个别地询问 dis (= apart) + quisit (= ask)}
  \wordsense{n. 论文；研究}
  \wordfamily{acquisitive}
  \wordfamily{inquiry}
  \wordfamily{inquisitive}
  \wordfamily{requisite}
  \wordphrase{disquisition on the evolution}
  \wordexample{I authored a disquisition on the evolution of the common law.（我写了一篇关于习惯法发展的论文。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{acquisitive}{ə'kwizətiv}
  \wordbreakdown{补充要求的 ac<ad (= to, add) + quisit (= ask)}
  \wordfamily{acquisitiveness（贪婪）；acquisition（取得，获得）；acquire（获得）；disquisition（论文）}
  \wordsense{a. 想获得（知识、财富、势力等）的，贪婪的，欲望多的；可获得的}
  \wordphrase{acquisitive mind}
  \wordexample{She's a great student, with a wonderfully acquisitive mind.（她是一名优秀的学生，有着强烈的求知欲。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{inquiry}{in'kwaiəri}
  \wordbreakdown{in (= into) + quir (= ask) 追问到里面}
  \wordsense{n. 询问，质询；研究，探求；调查，审查}
  \wordfamily{inquire 询问，打听}
  \wordfamily{inquirer 审查者，调查者}
  \wordfamily{inquiring 咨询的；爱追根究底的}
  \wordphrase{inquiry into the financial}
  \wordexample{Some Democrats joined their call for the inquiry into the financial dealings of the Clintons.（一些民主党议员联合要求对克林顿夫妇的金融交易进行调查。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{inquisitive}{in'kwizətiv}
  \wordbreakdown{追问到里面的 in (= in, into) + quisit (= ask, seek)}
  \wordsense{a. 好奇的；爱打听别人的事的}
  \wordfamily{inquisition 调查；审讯；对异端者的镇压；（the Inquisition）宗教法庭}
  \wordfamily{inquisitor 调查者；宗教法庭审判官}
  \wordfamily{inquisitorial 审讯者的；宗教法庭审判官的}
  \wordphrase{inquisitive man claiming}
  \wordexample{An inquisitive man claiming to be an insurance claims adjuster came to the door today.（今天来了一个特别爱追根问底的人，他自称是保险索赔调停员。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{requisite}{'rekwizit}
  \wordbreakdown{一定要求 re (= intensive, again) + quisit (= ask)}
  \wordfamily{requirement（要求）；disquisition（论文）；acquisitive（想获得（知识、财富、势力等）的）}
  \wordsense{n. 必要条件；必需品}
  \wordphrase{the requisite}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of requisite.（此处展示 requisite 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{a. 必要的，必需的}
  \wordphrase{be requisite}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of requisite.（此处展示 requisite 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. ）要求；征用}
  \wordphrase{requisite number}
  \wordexample{If you have accumulated the requisite number of credits, you'll graduate.（如果你修满了所必需的学分，就可以毕业了。）}
  \wordexample{requisite number}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{conquistador}{kɒn'kwistədɔ:(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 QUISIT, QUEST, QUIR, QUER[QUIST]}
  \wordfamily{exquisite；prerequisite；bequest；inquest；disquisition（论文）}
  \wordsense{n. （新大陆）征服者（16 世纪征服墨西哥、秘鲁的西班牙人） *con<com (= completely, together), quist<quisit (= ask, seek)}
  \wordphrase{a conquistador（征服者（16 世纪征服墨西哥、秘鲁的西班牙人））}
  \wordexample{a conquistador（征服者（16 世纪征服墨西哥、秘鲁的西班牙人））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{exquisite}{ik'skwizit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 QUISIT, QUEST, QUIR, QUER[QUIST]}
  \wordfamily{conquistador；prerequisite；bequest；inquest；disquisition（论文）}
  \wordsense{a. 美妙的，精致的；（痛苦、快乐等）剧烈的；（感知能力）敏锐的 *ex (= out)}
  \wordphrase{exquisite workmanship（精致的工艺）}
  \wordphrase{exquisite lace（精美的花边）}
  \wordexample{exquisite workmanship（精致的工艺）}
  \wordexample{exquisite lace（精美的花边）}
\end{word}

\chapter{POT[POW,POSS]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 `pot` 意为 `powerful, capable of`，它的变形有 `pow`、`poss` 等。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{empower}{im'pauə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{使强有力 em<en (= make) + pow<pot (= powerful)}
  \wordfamily{empowerment（授权）；empowered（millions）；omnipotent（全能的）}
  \wordsense{vt. 授权}
  \wordphrase{empowered millions}
  \wordexample{The feminist movement empowered millions of women in America.（美国的女权运动赋予数百万女性以权力。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{omnipotent}{ɒm'nipətənt}
  \wordbreakdown{能够做所有的事的 omni (= all) + pot (= capable of, powerful)}
  \wordfamily{omnipotence（全能）；empower（授权）；potent（有力的）}
  \wordsense{a. 全能的}
  \wordphrase{omnipotent}
  \wordexample{In the face of evil, many wonder if God is omnipotent.（在面对罪恶时，很多人怀疑上帝是否是无所不能的。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{potent}{'pəutnt}
  \wordbreakdown{拥有力量的 pot (= powerful)}
  \wordsense{a. 有力的；有权势的；有效力的，药物有效能的；有性交能力的}
  \wordfamily{potency 力量；势力，权势；效能，效力；性交能力}
  \wordfamily{potentate 当权者，有权势的人；君主}
  \wordphrase{potent poison that causes}
  \wordexample{A manta ray's tail contains a potent poison that causes a painful sting.（蝠鲼尾部含有一种能够引起剧烈疼痛的剧毒。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{despot}{'despət}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POT[POW,POSS]}
  \wordfamily{专制（政治）；empower（授权）；omnipotent（全能的）}
  \wordsense{n. 专制君主；独裁者；暴君 *des (= ligament)}
  \wordphrase{a despot（专制君主）}
  \wordexample{a despot（专制君主）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{impotent}{'impətənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POT[POW,POSS]}
  \wordfamily{despot；impotence；potentate；potential；empower（授权）}
  \wordsense{a. 无能为力的；无力的，无力量的；（男性）阳痿的 *im<in (= not)}
  \wordphrase{be impotent（无能为力的）}
  \wordexample{be impotent（无能为力的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{impotence}{'impətəns}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POT[POW,POSS]}
  \wordfamily{impotent；potentate；despot；potential；empower（授权）}
  \wordsense{n. 无能为力；无力，虚弱；阳痿 *im<in (= not)}
  \wordphrase{reduce the enemy to impotence（大大削弱敌人的力量）}
  \wordexample{reduce the enemy to impotence（大大削弱敌人的力量）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{potentate}{'pəutnteit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POT[POW,POSS]}
  \wordfamily{impotence；potential；impotent；potentiality；empower（授权）}
  \wordsense{n. 有权势者，当权者；君主，统治者 *ate (= person who)}
  \wordphrase{a potentate（有权势者，当权者）}
  \wordexample{a potentate（有权势者，当权者）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{potential}{pə'tenʃl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POT[POW,POSS]}
  \wordfamily{potentially（潜在地）；empower（授权）；omnipotent（全能的）}
  \wordsense{a. 潜在的，可能的}
  \wordphrase{be potential}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of potential.（此处展示 potential 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 潜在事物；潜力；可能性}
  \wordphrase{potentially rich country（有潜力的富庶国）}
  \wordphrase{potential wealth / resources（潜在财富 / 潜在资源）}
  \wordphrase{potential customer（有可能成为主顾的人，潜在顾客）}
  \wordexample{potentially rich country（有潜力的富庶国）}
  \wordexample{potential wealth / resources（潜在财富 / 潜在资源）}
  \wordexample{potential customer（有可能成为主顾的人，潜在顾客）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{potentiality}{pətenʃi'æləti}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POT[POW,POSS]}
  \wordsense{n. 潜力；可能性}
  \wordfamily{potential}
  \wordfamily{possibility}
  \wordfamily{potentate}
  \wordfamily{impotence}
  \wordphrase{country with great potentialities（具有巨大发展潜力的国家）}
  \wordexample{country with great potentialities（具有巨大发展潜力的国家）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{possibility}{ˌpɒsə'biləti}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POT[POW,POSS]}
  \wordfamily{potentiality；potential；potentate；impotence；empower（授权）}
  \wordsense{n. 可能（性）；可能发生的事 *poss<pot (= capable of)}
  \wordphrase{a possibility（可能（性））}
  \wordexample{a possibility（可能（性））}
\end{word}



\chapter{PASS}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 `pass` 意为 `to stride, to step`。此外，`pass` 也有 `to feel` 的意思，注意区分。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{encompass}{in'kʌmpəs}
  \wordbreakdown{使一起大步走 en (= make) + com (= together) + pass (= stride, step)}
  \wordsense{vt. 包含；围绕，包围}
  \wordfamily{encompassment 围绕，包围；包括}
  \wordfamily{CD 里音乐的类型很广泛。）}
  \wordphrase{encompasses a wide range}
  \wordexample{My CD collection encompasses a wide range of musical genres.（我收集的}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{impassable}{im'pɑ:səbl}
  \wordbreakdown{不能大步走过或跨过的 im<in (= not) + pass (= stride, step)}
  \wordfamily{impassability（不能通行）；encompass（包含）；impasse（死胡同）}
  \wordsense{a. 不能通行（通过）的；不可逾越的}
  \wordphrase{impassable in winter}
  \wordexample{Many of the highest mountain passes are impassable in winter.（在冬天高山上的很多通路无法通行。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{impasse}{'æmpɑ:s}
  \wordbreakdown{不能跨过或越过的状态 im<in (= not) + pass (= stride, step)}
  \wordsense{n. 死胡同；绝境；僵局，困境}
  \wordfamily{impassable}
  \wordfamily{trespass}
  \wordfamily{encompass}
  \wordfamily{compass}
  \wordphrase{impasse}
  \wordexample{Professional mediators can help negotiators resolve an impasse.（专业仲裁人员能够帮助谈判者打破僵局。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{trespass}{'trespæs}
  \wordbreakdown{越过去大步走 tres<trans (= over, across) + pass (= stride, step)}
  \wordfamily{trespasser（侵害者）；encompass（包含）；impassable（不能通行（通过）的）}
  \wordsense{vi. 侵入（他人的土地等）；侵害（他人的权利等）；冒犯；侵犯}
  \wordphrase{trespass in practice}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of trespass.（此处展示 trespass 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 侵入，侵害；打扰，妨碍；宗教、道德上的罪过}
  \wordphrase{trespassing onto my land}
  \wordexample{Your cattle keep trespassing onto my land, and I'm tired of it.（你的牛总是不断地侵入我的土地，我真是烦透了。）}
  \wordexample{trespassing onto my land}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{compass}{'kʌmpəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PASS}
  \wordfamily{surpass；passable；passage；passenger；encompass（包含）}
  \wordsense{n. 指南针；界限，范围；（pl.）（一般制图用的）圆规 *com (= together)}
  \wordphrase{the compass}
  \wordexample{beyond the compass of the human mind（超出人类的智力范围）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{surpass}{sə'pɑ:s}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PASS}
  \wordfamily{surpassing（无与伦比的，超群的）；surpassingly（超群地）；encompass（包含）}
  \wordsense{v. 优于，胜过 *sur (= over)}
  \wordphrase{of surpassing beauty（出众地美丽的）}
  \wordexample{of surpassing beauty（出众地美丽的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{passable}{'pɑ:səbl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PASS}
  \wordsense{a. 可通行的；尚可的}
  \wordfamily{surpass}
  \wordfamily{passage}
  \wordfamily{compass}
  \wordfamily{passenger}
  \wordphrase{passable knowledge of German（尚可的德语知识）}
  \wordexample{passable knowledge of German（尚可的德语知识）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{passage}{'pæsidʒ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PASS}
  \wordfamily{passageway（通道）；encompass（包含）；impassable（不能通行（通过）的）}
  \wordsense{n. 通过，通行；通道，走廊，过道；航海；旅行；（议案的）通过；（从演讲或文章中节选的很短的）一段；（pl.）（双方的）交换的言辞；争论；打斗}
  \wordphrase{passage of time（时间的流逝）}
  \wordphrase{passage of a bill（议案的通过）}
  \wordphrase{some passages from Shakespeare（莎士比亚作品某些选段）}
  \wordexample{have angry passages with an opponent during a debate（在辩论中与对手恶言相向）}
  \wordexample{passage of time（时间的流逝）}
  \wordexample{passage of a bill（议案的通过）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{passenger}{'pæsindʒə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PASS}
  \wordsense{n. 乘客；旅客；（球队中或乘务员中）不积极的人}
  \wordfamily{passage}
  \wordfamily{passing}
  \wordfamily{passable}
  \wordfamily{passé}
  \wordfamily{a passenger（乘客）}
  \wordphrase{a passenger（乘客）}
  \wordexample{a passenger（乘客）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{passing}{'pɑ:siŋ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PASS}
  \wordfamily{passenger；passage；passer-by；encompass（包含）}
  \wordsense{a. 经过的，路过的；短暂的，一时的}
  \wordphrase{be passing}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of passing.（此处展示 passing 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 通过；经过}
  \wordphrase{passing years（流年岁月）}
  \wordphrase{passing of the old year（陈年流逝）}
  \wordexample{passing years（流年岁月）}
  \wordexample{passing of the old year（陈年流逝）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{passé}{'pæsei}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PASS}
  \wordfamily{passing；passer-by；passenger；pastime；encompass（包含）}
  \wordsense{a. 已过全盛期的；过时的，陈旧的}
  \wordphrase{be passé（已过全盛期的）}
  \wordexample{be passé（已过全盛期的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{passer-by}{'pɑ:sə'bai}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PASS}
  \wordsense{n. 过路人，路人}
  \wordfamily{passé}
  \wordfamily{pastime}
  \wordfamily{passing}
  \wordfamily{passenger}
  \wordphrase{a passer-by（过路人，路人）}
  \wordexample{a passer-by（过路人，路人）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{pastime}{'pɑ:staim}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PASS}
  \wordfamily{passer-by；passing；passenger；encompass（包含）}
  \wordsense{n. 娱乐，游戏，消遣}
  \wordphrase{her favorite pastime（她最喜欢的游戏）}
  \wordexample{her favorite pastime（她最喜欢的游戏）}
\end{word}



\chapter{BOL,BL,BAL}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 `bol`、`bl`、`bal` 意为 `to throw`，同义词根有来自拉丁语的 `ject[jec, jac]`、`lanc[launc]`、`sip` 等。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{emblem}{'embləm}
  \wordbreakdown{向内扔 em<en (= in) + bl<bol (= throw)}
  \wordfamily{emblematic（象征性的）；parable（寓言）；problematic（成问题的）}
  \wordsense{n. 象征，标志；徽章}
  \wordphrase{emblem of peace}
  \wordexample{In most cultures, the dove is seen as an emblem of peace.（在大部分文化中鸽子都被看做是和平的象征。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{parable}{'pærəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{向旁边扔 para (= beside) + bl<bol (= throw)}
  \wordfamily{parabolical（用寓言形式来表达的）；parables；emblem（象征）}
  \wordsense{n. 寓言；隐喻的比较}
  \wordphrase{parables}
  \wordexample{According to the Bible, Jesus taught his followers with parables.（据《圣经》记载，耶稣曾用寓言来教导他的信徒。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{problematic}{ˌprɒblə'mætik}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 BOL,BL,BAL}
  \wordfamily{problem（问题）；emblem（象征）；parable（寓言）}
  \wordsense{a. 成问题的；（结果）有疑问的；未决的，不确定的 *pro (= forth, forward)}
  \wordphrase{be problematic（成问题的）}
  \wordexample{be problematic（成问题的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{anabolism}{ə'næbəlizəm}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 BOL,BL,BAL}
  \wordfamily{problematic；catabolism；embolism；hyperbola；emblem（象征）}
  \wordsense{n. 合成代谢 *ana (= up, again)}
  \wordphrase{an anabolism（合成代谢）}
  \wordexample{an anabolism（合成代谢）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{catabolism}{kə'tæbəlizəm}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 BOL,BL,BAL}
  \wordfamily{anabolism；embolism；problematic；hyperbola；emblem（象征）}
  \wordsense{n. 异化作用 *cata (= down)}
  \wordphrase{a catabolism（异化作用）}
  \wordexample{a catabolism（异化作用）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{embolism}{'embəlizəm}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 BOL,BL,BAL}
  \wordfamily{catabolism；hyperbola；anabolism；hyperbole；emblem（象征）}
  \wordsense{n. 栓塞 *em<en (= into)}
  \wordphrase{an embolism（栓塞）}
  \wordexample{an embolism（栓塞）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{hyperbola}{hai'pə:bələ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 BOL,BL,BAL}
  \wordfamily{embolism；hyperbole；catabolism；metabolism；emblem（象征）}
  \wordsense{n. 双曲线 *hyper (= over)}
  \wordphrase{a hyperbola（双曲线）}
  \wordexample{a hyperbola（双曲线）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{hyperbole}{hai'pə:bəli}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 BOL,BL,BAL}
  \wordfamily{hyperbola；metabolism；embolism；balance；emblem（象征）}
  \wordsense{n. 夸张法；夸张的语句 *hyper (= over)}
  \wordphrase{a hyperbole（夸张法）}
  \wordexample{a hyperbole（夸张法）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{metabolism}{mə'tæbəlizəm}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 BOL,BL,BAL}
  \wordfamily{metabolic（新陈代谢的）；emblem（象征）；parable（寓言）}
  \wordsense{n. 新陈代谢 *meta (= change)}
  \wordphrase{a metabolism（新陈代谢）}
  \wordexample{a metabolism（新陈代谢）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{balance}{'bæləns}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 BOL,BL,BAL}
  \wordfamily{metabolism；balanced；hyperbole；imbalance；emblem（象征）}
  \wordsense{n. 均衡，平衡；天平，秤；收支差额；结余，余额；（收支等的）平衡；（the\textasciitilde{}）剩余部分，剩余物}
  \wordphrase{the balance}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of balance.（此处展示 balance 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. 使平衡，权衡（问题等）；犹豫不决；结算}
  \wordphrase{balance of power（国家间的均势）}
  \wordphrase{checks and balances（政府机关彼此之间的相互制衡）}
  \wordphrase{balance at a bank（银行结余）}
  \wordexample{balance of power（国家间的均势）}
  \wordexample{checks and balances（政府机关彼此之间的相互制衡）}
  \wordexample{balance at a bank（银行结余）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{balanced}{'bælənst}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 BOL,BL,BAL}
  \wordsense{a. 平衡的}
  \wordfamily{balance}
  \wordfamily{imbalance}
  \wordfamily{metabolism}
  \wordfamily{hyperbole}
  \wordphrase{balanced diet（均衡饮食）}
  \wordphrase{balanced budget（平衡预算）}
  \wordexample{balanced diet（均衡饮食）}
  \wordexample{balanced budget（平衡预算）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{imbalance}{im'bæləns}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 BOL,BL,BAL}
  \wordfamily{balanced；balance；metabolism；hyperbole；emblem（象征）}
  \wordsense{n. 不均衡 *im<in (= not)}
  \wordphrase{the imbalance}
  \wordexample{South Korea's imbalance in world payments（韩国在国际收支中的不平衡）}
\end{word}



\chapter{BAR}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自拉丁语的 `bar` 意为 `bar`。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{embargo}{im'bɑ:gəu}
  \wordbreakdown{在某物内放入障碍物、棒子 em<en (= in) + bar (= bar)}
  \wordfamily{barrage；debar；embarrass；embarrassment}
  \wordsense{n. 禁止通商；禁港令；禁止}
  \wordphrase{the embargo}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of embargo.（此处展示 embargo 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 禁止船只出入港口；禁止通商}
  \wordphrase{embargo against Cuba}
  \wordexample{The U. S. has long enforced a trade embargo against Cuba.（美国对古巴实施了很长时间的贸易禁运政策。）}
  \wordexample{embargo against Cuba}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{barrage}{'bærɑ:ʒ}
  \wordbreakdown{像棒子一样长或连续 bar(r) (= bar)}
  \wordfamily{embargo；debar；embarrass；embarrassment}
  \wordsense{n. 弹幕；一连串；['bɑ:ridʒ] 渠，堰}
  \wordphrase{barrage of gunfire miraculously}
  \wordexample{The soldiers sprinted through a barrage of gunfire, miraculously unharmed.（战士们穿过密集的火力进攻，竟然奇迹般地毫发未伤。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{debar}{di'bɑ:(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 BAR}
  \wordfamily{embarrass；embarrassment；barbecue；barrack；embargo（禁止通商）}
  \wordsense{v. 排除，阻止 *de (= down)}
  \wordphrase{to debar（排除，阻止）}
  \wordexample{to debar（排除，阻止）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{embarrass}{im'bærəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 BAR}
  \wordfamily{embargo（禁止通商）；barrage（弹幕）；debar（排除）}
  \wordsense{v. 使窘迫，使局促不安 *em(en (= in)}
  \wordphrase{embarrassed by lack of money（因缺钱而窘迫）}
  \wordexample{embarrassed by lack of money（因缺钱而窘迫）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{embarrassment}{im'bærəsmənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 BAR}
  \wordfamily{embargo（禁止通商）；barrage（弹幕）；debar（排除）}
  \wordsense{n. 为难，困窘；使人为难的事 *em(en (= in)}
  \wordphrase{embarrassment of riches（钱多得不知如何花）}
  \wordexample{embarrassment of riches（钱多得不知如何花）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{barbecue}{'bɑ:bikju:}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 BAR}
  \wordfamily{embarrassment；barrack；embarrass；barrel；embargo（禁止通商）}
  \wordsense{n. 烧烤用架；（牛、猪等的）烤全牲；吃烧烤食品的野餐会}
  \wordphrase{a barbecue（烧烤用架）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "a barbecue".（常见搭配：烧烤用架。）}
  \wordsense{v. 烤全牲}
  \wordphrase{barbecue sauce（烤肉汁，由醋和调味料制成）}
  \wordexample{barbecue sauce（烤肉汁，由醋和调味料制成）}
  \wordexample{a barbecue（烧烤用架）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{barrack}{'bærək}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 BAR}
  \wordsense{n.（pl.）兵营，军营；（临时性的）木板房；简陋的房屋}
  \wordfamily{break barracks（逃离军营）}
  \wordfamily{a barrack（兵营，军营）}
  \wordphrase{a barrack（兵营，军营）}
  \wordexample{a barrack（兵营，军营）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{barrel}{'bærəl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 BAR}
  \wordfamily{barrack；barricade；barbecue；barrier；embargo（禁止通商）}
  \wordsense{n. （木头、塑料制的）桶；一桶的量；枪管；钢笔的墨水管}
  \wordphrase{the barrel}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of barrel.（此处展示 barrel 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. 装入（装满）桶内}
  \wordphrase{barrel-roofed vault（筒形穹顶）}
  \wordphrase{barrel organ（手摇风琴）}
  \wordexample{barrel-roofed vault（筒形穹顶）}
  \wordexample{barrel organ（手摇风琴）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{barricade}{ˌbæri'keid}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 BAR}
  \wordfamily{barrel；barrier；barrack；barrister；embargo（禁止通商）}
  \wordsense{n. 栅栏；路障}
  \wordphrase{the barricade}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of barricade.（此处展示 barricade 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. 设栅栏、路障}
  \wordphrase{The radicals barricaded the}
  \wordexample{The radicals barricaded the road with desks and chairs.（激进分子用桌椅封锁了道路。）}
  \wordexample{The radicals barricaded the}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{barrier}{'bæriə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 BAR}
  \wordfamily{embargo（禁止通商）；barrage（弹幕）；debar（排除）}
  \wordsense{n. 栅栏；屏障；阻碍，障碍（物）}
  \wordphrase{barrier to progress（进步的障碍）}
  \wordexample{barrier to progress（进步的障碍）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{barrister}{'bæristə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 BAR}
  \wordfamily{barrier；barricade；barrel；barrack；embargo（禁止通商）}
  \wordsense{n. 出庭律师 cf. solicitor（《英》初级律师） attorney-at-law（《美》律师）}
  \wordphrase{attorney-at-law（《美》律师）}
  \wordexample{attorney-at-law（《美》律师））}
\end{word}



\chapter{BODY}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自盎格鲁-撒克逊语的 `body` 意为 `body`。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 `corp` 和来自希腊语的 `som, somato`。词根 `buddy` 是它的变形。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{embody}{im'bɔdi}
  \wordbreakdown{给身体 em<en (= give) + body (= body)}
  \wordfamily{embodiment（具体化）；antibody（抗体 *anti (= against)）；bodily（身体的）}
  \wordsense{vt. 使（思想、情感等具体）体现；包含；赋予…以形体}
  \wordphrase{embody a kind}
  \wordexample{Children embody a kind of innocent joy that adults can rarely experience.（孩子们会表现出一种成年人很难体会到的纯真的快乐。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{antibody}{'æntibɒdi}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 BODY}
  \wordfamily{bodily；buddy；embody（使（思想、情感等具体）体现）}
  \wordsense{n. 抗体 *anti (= against)}
  \wordphrase{an antibody（抗体）}
  \wordexample{an antibody（抗体）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{bodily}{'bɔdili}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 BODY}
  \wordfamily{antibody；buddy；embody（使（思想、情感等具体）体现）}
  \wordsense{a. 身体的，肉体的}
  \wordphrase{be bodily（身体的，肉体的）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "be bodily".（常见搭配：身体的，肉体的。）}
  \wordsense{ad. 整个地，全体地}
  \wordphrase{be bodily（身体的，肉体的）}
  \wordexample{be bodily（身体的，肉体的）}
  \wordexample{bodily（整个地，全体地）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{buddy}{'bʌdi}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 BODY}
  \wordfamily{bodily；antibody；embody（使（思想、情感等具体）体现）}
  \wordsense{n. （用做亲密的称呼）老兄，老弟；伙伴，朋友 *buddy<body (= body)}
  \wordphrase{a buddy（老兄，老弟）}
  \wordexample{a buddy（老兄，老弟）}
\end{word}



\chapter{CROCH[CROACH, CROUCH, CROTCH, CRUTCH, CROOK]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 来自盎格鲁-撒克逊语的 `croch` 意为 `hook, to bend`。其变形为 `croach, crouch, crotch, crutch, crook`。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 `clin/cli, cliv, flect/flex, cumb/cub` 等。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{encroach}{in'krautʃ}
  \wordbreakdown{像钩子一样蜷缩在里边 en (= in) + croach<croch (= hook, bend)}
  \wordsense{vi. on + sth 侵入；侵蚀；侵害（权利等）}
  \wordfamily{encroachment 侵入；侵害；侵蚀}
  \wordphrase{encroaching on the family}
  \wordexample{Suburban creep is encroaching on the family farm where I grew up.（我在我家的农场长大，现在它正受到郊区那些小偷的侵害。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{crochet}{'krəuʃei}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CROCH[CROACH, CROUCH, CROTCH, CRUTCH, CROOK]}
  \wordfamily{crouch；crotchety；crotch；crutch；encroach（on + sth 侵入）}
  \wordsense{n. 钩针编织（品）}
  \wordphrase{the crochet}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of crochet.（此处展示 crochet 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. 用钩针编织}
  \wordphrase{crochet hook（钩针）}
  \wordexample{crochet hook（钩针）}
  \wordexample{a crochet（钩针编织（品））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{crouch}{'krautʃ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CROCH[CROACH, CROUCH, CROTCH, CRUTCH, CROOK]}
  \wordfamily{crochet；crotchety；crotch；crutch；encroach（on + sth 侵入）}
  \wordsense{v. （因害怕或要躲藏而）蹲伏；（动物准备跳跃前）蜷缩（身体）}
  \wordphrase{to crouch（蹲伏）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "to crouch".（常见搭配：蹲伏。）}
  \wordsense{n. 下蹲（姿势），蜷缩（姿势） *crouch<croch (= bend)}
  \wordphrase{to crouch（蹲伏）}
  \wordexample{to crouch（蹲伏）}
  \wordexample{a crouch（下蹲（姿势），蜷缩（姿势））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{crotchety}{'krɒtʃəti}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CROCH[CROACH, CROUCH, CROTCH, CRUTCH, CROOK]}
  \wordfamily{encroach（on + sth 侵入）；crochet（钩针编织（品））；crouch（（因害怕或要躲藏而）蹲伏）}
  \wordsense{a. 反复无常的；有奇想的 *crotch<croch (= bend)}
  \wordphrase{be crotchety（反复无常的）}
  \wordexample{be crotchety（反复无常的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{crotch}{krɒtʃ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CROCH[CROACH, CROUCH, CROTCH, CRUTCH, CROOK]}
  \wordfamily{crotchety；crutch；crouch；crook；encroach（on + sth 侵入）}
  \wordsense{n. （树木的）枝丫；胯部，裤裆 *crotch<croch (= bend)}
  \wordphrase{the crotch}
  \wordexample{sit in a crotch of a tree（坐在树枝上）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{crutch}{krʌtʃ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CROCH[CROACH, CROUCH, CROTCH, CRUTCH, CROOK]}
  \wordfamily{crotch；crook；crotchety；crooked；encroach（on + sth 侵入）}
  \wordsense{n. 拐杖；（样子、用途像拐杖的）支撑物；精神支柱 *crotch<croch (= bend)}
  \wordphrase{go about on crutches（拄着拐杖走）}
  \wordexample{go about on crutches（拄着拐杖走）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{crook}{kruk}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CROCH[CROACH, CROUCH, CROTCH, CRUTCH, CROOK]}
  \wordfamily{crookback（驼背）；encroach（on + sth 侵入）；crochet（钩针编织（品））}
  \wordsense{n. 钩状物；（牧童的）曲柄杖；（道路或河流的）拐弯处，弯曲处；骗子，小偷}
  \wordphrase{the crook}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of crook.（此处展示 crook 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. （使）弯曲；诈骗，欺骗 *crook<croch (= bend)}
  \wordphrase{on the crook（诈骗地，以不正当手段地）}
  \wordphrase{crook one's finger / arm（弯曲手指 / 胳膊）}
  \wordphrase{crook a friend（欺骗朋友）}
  \wordexample{on the crook（诈骗地，以不正当手段地）}
  \wordexample{crook one's finger / arm（弯曲手指 / 胳膊）}
  \wordexample{crook a friend（欺骗朋友）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{crooked}{'krukid}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CROCH[CROACH, CROUCH, CROTCH, CRUTCH, CROOK]}
  \wordfamily{encroach（on + sth 侵入）；crochet（钩针编织（品））；crouch（（因害怕或要躲藏而）蹲伏）}
  \wordsense{a. 弯曲的；歪的；（人、行动等）不正当的，不老实的，不正派的 *crook<croch (= bend)}
  \wordphrase{crooked lane（弯曲的小路）}
  \wordphrase{Crooked Stick（懒汉；不诚实的人）}
  \wordexample{crooked lane（弯曲的小路）}
  \wordexample{Crooked Stick（懒汉；不诚实的人）}
\end{word}



\chapter{GROSS[GROC]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根 GROSS[GROC] 盎格鲁一撒克逊语的gross意为large，thick，它的变形为groc。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{engross}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{使变厚en（=make）+gross（=thick,large)；'graus}
  \wordfamily{Detectivefictioninvariablyengrossesme（侦探小说总是很 吸引我）；gross（总的）；grocer（食品商）}
  \wordsense{v. sb+in+sth占用（时间），吸引（注意力），使全神贯注；用大字体清晰或端正地 书写或誉写)}
  \wordphrase{.}
  \wordexample{.}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{gross}{graus}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 GROSS[GROC]}
  \wordfamily{grossly（非常，很）；engross（sb+in+sth占用（时间））；grocer（食品商）}
  \wordsense{a. 总的，总体的，全部的；臃肿的（以至看起来令人生厌的）；（错误、不正当行为等）严重的；不雅的，粗俗的；（食物）粗劣的；（植物）茂盛的，茂密的}
  \wordphrase{be gross}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of gross.（此处展示 gross 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. 总共收入}
  \wordphrase{gross national product（国民生产总值（缩写：GNP））}
  \wordphrase{gross domestic product（国内生产总值（缩写：GDP））}
  \wordphrase{his gross income（他的总收入）}
  \wordexample{the gross vegetation of the tropical rain-forest（热带雨林的茂密植被）}
  \wordexample{gross national product（国民生产总值（缩写：GNP））}
  \wordexample{gross domestic product（国内生产总值（缩写：GDP））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{grocer}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 GROSS[GROC]}
  \wordfamily{gross；grocery；engross（sb+in+sth占用（时间））}
  \wordsense{n. 食品商，杂货商 ['grausa(r)]}
  \wordphrase{a grocer（食品商，杂货商 ['grausa(r)]）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "a grocer".（常见搭配：食品商，杂货商 ['grausa(r)]。）}
  \wordsense{n. 食品商，杂货商}
  \wordphrase{a grocer（食品商，杂货商 ['grausa(r)]）}
  \wordexample{a grocer（食品商，杂货商 ['grausa(r)]）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{grocery}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 GROSS[GROC]}
  \wordfamily{grocer；gross；engross（sb+in+sth占用（时间））}
  \wordsense{n. 食品店；（pl.）食品，杂货；食品杂货业}
  \wordphrase{a grocery（食品店）}
  \wordexample{a grocery（食品店）}
