\chapter{AM[EM, IM, M]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根 AM[EM, IM, M] 来自拉丁语的 am 意为 to love，friendly，它的变形为 em，im，m 等。
  \item 同义词根有来自希腊语的 phil 和 ero。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{enmity}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{不爱 en (= not) + m(em<am (= love)；'enməti}
  \wordfamily{inimical；amenity；amiable；amicable}
  \wordsense{n. 不和；敌意，仇恨，憎恨}
  \wordphrase{the enmity}
  \wordexample{Longstanding enmity between Muslims and Jews has caused much loss of life.（穆斯林和犹太人之间长久的不和导致很多人丧生。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{inimical}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 AM[EM, IM, M]；没有爱的in（=not）+im(am（=love）］ [i'nimikl}
  \wordfamily{amenity；amiable；enamor；paramour；enmity（不和）}
  \wordsense{a. 敌意的；有害的}
  \wordphrase{inimical to good health}
  \wordexample{Obesity is inimical to good health and longevity.（肥胖不利于健康和长寿。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{amenity}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 AM[EM, IM, M]；【使喜欢上am（=love）] [ə'menəti]}
  \wordfamily{inimical；amiable；enamor；paramour；amenities；enmity（不和）}
  \wordsense{n. 使生活便利舒适的设施；舒适，愉快，宜人；（交际上的)礼仪}
  \wordphrase{amenities}
  \wordexample{I always stay in Marriott hotels because of all the amenities.（万豪国际酒店 的设施便利舒适，所以我总是住在那里。）}
  \wordexample{amenities}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{amiable}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 AM[EM, IM, M]；很容易喜欢上的am（=love,friendly）+i+able（=capableof）］ ['eimiəbl}
  \wordfamily{amiability（可爱）；amiably（和蔼可亲地）；Travel（Magazine rates the people there as the most amiable anywhere in South East Asia. (《旅游杂 志》将那里的人评选为东南亚地区最友善的人）；enmity（不和）}
  \wordsense{a. 友善的；和蔼可亲的}
  \wordphrase{amiable anywhere}
  \wordexample{Travel Magazine rates the people there as the most amiable anywhere in South East Asia. (《旅游杂 志》将那里的人评选为东南亚地区最友善的人。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{amicable}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{能够喜欢的am（=love）+ic（=natureof）+able（=capableof)；'æmikəbl}
  \wordfamily{amicability（友好，友善）；amicably（友好地）；enmity（不和）}
  \wordsense{a. 友好的，友善的}
  \wordphrase{be amicable}
  \wordexample{While they aren't close friends，bothmenwereamicablethroughout.（尽管两人不是亲密的朋友，但彼 此一直很友好。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{enamor}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 AM[EM, IM, M]；i'nema(r)}
  \wordfamily{paramour；amiable；amity；amenity；enmity（不和）}
  \wordsense{v. 【常用被动语态】使倾心；使迷恋 *}
  \wordphrase{enamored of her}
  \wordexample{I was enamored of her.（我被她迷住了。）}
  \wordexample{enamored of her}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{paramour}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 AM[EM, IM, M]}
  \wordfamily{enamor；amity；amorous；amiable；enmity（不和）}
  \wordsense{n. 情妇（夫）；《古》情人 *par<per（=by） ['paramua(r)]}
  \wordphrase{a paramour（情妇（夫））}
  \wordexample{a paramour（情妇（夫））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{amity}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 AM[EM, IM, M]；'æməti}
  \wordfamily{paramour；amorous；enamor；amiable；enmity（不和）}
  \wordsense{n. （个人、国家间的）友好关系}
  \wordphrase{treaty of amity（友好条约）}
  \wordexample{treaty of amity（友好条约）}
  \wordexample{an amity（友好关系）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{amorous}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 AM[EM, IM, M]；'æmərəs}
  \wordfamily{enmity（不和）；inimical（敌意的）；amenity（使生活便利舒适的设施）}
  \wordsense{a. 多情的；爱情的；（特指）色情的}
  \wordphrase{amorous looks（好色的表情）}
  \wordphrase{amorous poetry（情诗）}
  \wordexample{amorous looks（好色的表情）}
  \wordexample{amorous poetry（情诗）}
\end{word}



\chapter{TAIL}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根TAIL 来自盎格鲁-撒克逊语的tail意为to cut。同义词根有来自希腊语的tom[tem]
  \item 和来自拉丁语的cis，cop/coup，scind/scis，sect，sever，tons，trench等。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{entail}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{'enˌteil] [用力切 en（=intensive）+tail（=cut）}
  \wordfamily{entailment（继承人之限定）；curtail（缩减）；detail（细节）}
  \wordsense{v. 使必需；使承担；限定不动产的继承人}
  \wordphrase{to entail（使必需）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "to entail".（常见搭配：使必需。）}
  \wordsense{n. 限定继承}
  \wordphrase{to entail（使必需）}
  \wordexample{Applying for college entails a great deal of paperwork.（申请入大学需要做大量的书面工作。）}
  \wordexample{to entail（使必需）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{curtail}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TAIL；'kə:'teil}
  \wordfamily{curtailment（缩减，缩短）；entail（使必需）；detail（细节）}
  \wordsense{v. 缩减，缩短 *}
  \wordphrase{curtail a speech/one's holiday（缩短讲话/假期）}
  \wordexample{curtail a speech/one's holiday（缩短讲话/假期）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{detail}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TAIL}
  \wordfamily{entail（使必需）；curtail（缩减）；retail（零售）}
  \wordsense{n. 细节，细目；详情；详述；（美术中）细部（描绘）}
  \wordphrase{detailed report（详细报道）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "detailed report".（常见搭配：详细报道。）}
  \wordsense{v. 详述；（为特殊任务）派遣}
  \wordphrase{go/enter into details（详述，逐一细说）}
  \wordexample{go/enter into details（详述，逐一细说）}
  \wordexample{Five soldiers were detailed to guard the bridge.（五名士兵被派去守卫 大桥。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{retail}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TAIL}
  \wordfamily{detail；tailor；curtail；entail（使必需）}
  \wordsense{v. 零售；详述；传播（消息）}
  \wordphrase{retail dealer（零售商）}
  \wordphrase{retail price（零售价格）}
  \wordphrase{retail shop（零售 店）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "retail dealer".（常见搭配：零售商。）}
  \wordsense{n. 零售}
  \wordphrase{to retail（零售）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "to retail".（常见搭配：零售。）}
  \wordsense{a. /}
  \wordphrase{to retail（零售）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "to retail".（常见搭配：零售。）}
  \wordsense{ad. 零售的，以零售方式 *re（=again,back） retailer（零售商）retail dealer（零售商）retail price（零售价格）retail shop（零售 店）retail sales（零售）at retail（以零售方式）sell retail（零售）retail a slander（传播诽 谤之言）}
  \wordphrase{to retail（零售）}
  \wordexample{to retail（零售）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{tailor}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TAIL}
  \wordfamily{retail；detail；curtail；v.缝制（衣服））；entail（使必需）}
  \wordsense{n. （尤指做男装的）裁缝}
  \wordphrase{tailor-made（裁缝制的；量身做的，合适的）}
  \wordphrase{well-tailored suit（做工精良的西服）}
  \wordphrase{tailored for a special purpose/to a particular audience（适合特殊目的/适合特殊的观 众要求的）}
  \wordexample{tailored for a special purpose/to a particular audience（适合特殊目的/适合特殊的观 众要求的）}
  \wordsense{v. 缝制（衣服）；使适合 ['teilə(r)] tailor-made（裁缝制的；量身做的，合适的）well-tailored suit（做工精良的西服） tailored for a special purpose/to a particular audience（适合特殊目的/适合特殊的观 众要求的）}
  \wordphrase{tailor the issue}
  \wordexample{a tailor（裁缝 v.缝制（衣服））}
\end{word}



\chapter{LIGHT}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根LIGHT 来自盎格鲁-撒克逊语的light意为light。同义词根有来自拉丁语的lumin
  \item [lun], luc/lux/lust和来自希腊语的photo。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{enlightened}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LIGHT；【给光的 en（=give）+light（=light)］ [in'laitnd]}
  \wordfamily{enlightenment（启蒙，教化）；searchlight（探照灯）；spotlight（聚光灯）}
  \wordsense{a. 开通的；有知识的；摆脱偏见(或迷信)的；文明的}
  \wordphrase{enlightened for country people}
  \wordexample{parents are fairly enlightened for country people.（作为乡下人，我父母算是 相当开通了。）}
  \wordexample{My parents are fairly enlightened for country people.（作为乡下人，我父母算是 相当开通了。）}
\end{word}

  \wordbreakdown{词根 LIGHT}

  \wordexample{a searchlight（探照灯）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{spotlight}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LIGHT}
  \wordfamily{searchlight；light；enlightened；lighten}
  \wordsense{n. 聚光灯；（the～）公众的关注v聚光照明；使突出醒目 come into the spotlight（吸引众人的关注）}
  \wordphrase{come into the spotlight（吸引众人的关注）}
  \wordphrase{a spotlight（聚光灯）}
  \wordexample{a spotlight（聚光灯）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{light}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LIGHT}
  \wordfamily{spotlight；lighten；searchlight；lightning；enlightened（开通的）}
  \wordsense{n. 光；发光体（如蜡烛、灯等）；交通信号；照明；火柴、香烟等点火物；脸上的光彩， 眼神；名人，权威的人}
  \wordphrase{light bulb（电灯泡）}
  \wordphrase{All the lights went out-there was a power failure（所有的灯都灭 了——停电了。）}
  \wordphrase{strike a light（开 灯）}
  \wordexample{All the lights went out-there was a power failure.（所有的灯都灭 了——停电了。）}
  \wordexample{Can you give me a light, please?（能借个火吗？）}
  \wordsense{a. （地点）有光的，明亮的；（颜色）淡的，浅的；轻微的；轻快 的；行李（或负担）轻的；（吃饭）量少的；（食物）易消化的；（酒类）低度的；易醒 的，睡得不沉的；轻率的，轻浮的，行为轻桃的；（女子）水性杨花的}
  \wordphrase{a light（光）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "a light".（常见搭配：光。）}
  \wordsense{ad. 轻地；轻装 地}
  \wordphrase{a light（光）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "a light".（常见搭配：光。）}
  \wordsense{v. 点火，点燃；使光明，照亮；（\textasciitilde{}up）点（电灯或煤气灯）；（\textasciitilde{}upon）偶然发现； 下（马等)；（鸟）停落 light bulb（电灯泡）All the lights went out-there was a power failure.（所有的灯都灭 了——停电了。）Can you give me a light, please?（能借个火吗？）strike a light（开 灯）Light come, light go.（来得容易去得快。）}
  \wordphrase{a light（光）}
  \wordexample{a light（光）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{lighten}{[]}

  \wordphrase{lighten the darkness of the cellar（照亮黑暗的地下室）}
  \wordphrase{lighten a ship's cargo / taxes（减轻船上的货物 / 降低税收）}
  \wordphrase{to lighten（发亮，照亮，变亮）}

\chapter{CUMB, CUB}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根 CUMB, CUB 格鲁-撒克逊语的croch[croach，crouch，crotch，crutch，crook]
  \item 和来自拉丁语的clin/cli，cliv，flect/flex等。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{encumber}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CUMB, CUB；【靠在里面en（=in）+cumb（=lean,lie)] [in'kamba(r)] ut.sth/sb+with+sth妨碍；使负担（债务、义务等）；因.而负重担；阻塞（某个 场所）}
  \wordfamily{incumbent（在职者）；recumbent（（特指人）躺着的）；succumb（to+sth屈服）}
  \wordsense{n. 常用义项待统一核对}
  \wordphrase{the encumber}
  \wordexample{encumbrance妨碍，障碍；令人头疼的事?His heirs discovered his estate was encumbered with substantial debt.（他的继承人发现他的遗产负有 相当多的债务。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{incumbent}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{躺在某物上面in(=on）+cumb（=lie)；n'kAmbant}
  \wordfamily{encumber（常用义项待统一核对）；recumbent（（特指人）躺着的）；succumb（to+sth屈服）}
  \wordsense{n. 在职者，现任者}
  \wordphrase{the incumbent}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of incumbent.（此处展示 incumbent 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{a. 有义务的；倚靠的}
  \wordphrase{incumbent usually has an}
  \wordexample{The incumbent usually has an advantage inanyelectioncampaign.（在任何选举活动中，在职者通常都占有优势。）}
  \wordexample{incumbent usually has an}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{recumbent}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CUMB, CUB；【躺在后面的re（=back）+cumb（=lie） [ri'kambant]；recumbent（【躺在后面的re（=back）+cumb（=lie） [ri'kambant]}
  \wordfamily{Iwasrecumbentinahammockwhentheearthquakestruck（地震发生的时 候，我正躺在吊床上）；encumber（常用义项待统一核对）；incumbent（在职者）}
  \wordsense{a. （特指人）躺着的，靠着的；休息的}
  \wordphrase{be recumbent（躺着的，靠着的）}
  \wordexample{be recumbent（躺着的，靠着的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{succumb}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CUMB, CUB；【躺在下方suc(sub（=under）+cumb（=lean，lie）］ [sa'am]}
  \wordfamily{encumber（常用义项待统一核对）；incumbent（在职者）；recumbent（（特指人）躺着的）}
  \wordsense{vi. to+sth屈服，屈从；死}
  \wordphrase{succumbed to malaria after}
  \wordexample{He succumbed to malaria after several long nights of chills and fevers. (经 受 了几夜的风寒和高烧，他最终死于症疾。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{incubus}{[]}

  \wordfamily{succumb；cumber；recumbent；cumbersome；encumber（常用义项待统一核对）}

  \wordphrase{incubus but was probably}
  \wordexample{The woman claimed to have been violated by an incubus, but was probably insane.（那个女人说自己一直受垂梦的折磨，但很可能她有些精神错乱。）}
  \wordexample{incubus but was probably}
\end{word}

\chapter{BELL}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根BELL 来自拉丁语的bell 意为beautiful，war，在盎格鲁-撒克逊语里意为bag，to
  \item swell。当bell用做beautiful这一含义时，其同义词根有来自拉丁语的pulchr
  \item 和来自希腊语的calli，cosmeto
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{embellish}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{使变美丽 em<en (=make)+bell （=beautiful)；im'beliʃ}
  \wordfamily{Sheembellishedthecakewithreal（cut）；embellishment装饰（物）；flowers（她用折来的鲜花装饰蛋糕）}
  \wordsense{vt. sth + with + sth 美化；装饰；使（故事等）更有趣；润色}
  \wordphrase{embellish the issue}
  \wordexample{Sheembellishedthecakewithreal cut flowers.（她用折来的鲜花装饰蛋糕。）}
\end{word}

\chapter{COM}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根COM 来自拉丁语的com意为revel, hair, sleep。

\end{word}

\chapter{PER[PIR, PAR]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根 PER[PIR, PAR] 来自拉丁语的per意为to try out，to risk，它的变形为pir和par。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{empirical}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PER[PIR, PAR]；在内部严密地实验的 em<en（=in）+ pir<per（=try out）；im'pirikl}
  \wordfamily{empirically（凭经验地）；peril（危险）；imperil（危及（生命、财产等））}
  \wordsense{a. 依赖观察和经验的；经验的，经验上的}
  \wordphrase{be empirical}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of empirical.（此处展示 empirical 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. empiricism经验论；经验主义empiricist经验主义者}
  \wordphrase{the empirical}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of empirical.（此处展示 empirical 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. ）经验主义者}
  \wordphrase{Science requires empirical proof}
  \wordexample{to support hypotheses.（科学需要实验的证据去支持假设。）}
  \wordexample{Science requires empirical proof to support hypotheses.（科学需要实验的证据去支持假设。）}
  \wordexample{Science requires empirical proof}
\end{word}

\chapter{CLAV}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根CLAV 来自拉丁语的clav意为key。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{enclave}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{在钥匙内en（=in）+clav（=key）；‘enkleiv}
  \wordfamily{conclave；clavichord；clavicle；enclaves}
  \wordsense{n. （被他国属地包围的)地域、领土，飞地}
  \wordphrase{enclaves}
  \wordexample{Large cities like New York contain numerous ethnic enclaves.（像纽约这样 的大城市里有许多不同民族的圈子。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{conclave}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CLAV；'kɔŋkleiv}
  \wordfamily{enclave（（被他国属地包围的)地域、领土）；clavichord（古钢琴（早期的键盘乐器）；clavicle（锁骨）}
  \wordsense{n. （红衣主教们聚在一起选举教皇的）秘密会议}
  \wordphrase{sit in conclave（召开秘密会议）}
  \wordexample{sit in conclave（召开秘密会议）}
\end{word}

  \wordbreakdown{词根 CLAV}
  \wordfamily{conclave；clavicle；enclave（（被他国属地包围的)地域、领土）}
  \wordsense{n. 古钢琴（早期的键盘乐器，钢琴的前身）*}
  \wordphrase{a clavichord（古钢琴（早期的键盘乐器，钢琴的前身）*chord（=a string））}
  \wordexample{a clavichord（古钢琴（早期的键盘乐器，钢琴的前身）*chord（=a string））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{clavicle}{ˈklævikl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CLAV}
  \wordfamily{clavichord；conclave；enclave（（被他国属地包围的)地域、领土）}

\chapter{NUMER}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根 NUMER 来自拉丁语的 numer 意为 number，to count。同义词根有 arithm。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{enumerate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{彻底地数、列举 e(ex(= out) + numer(= number, count))；'njumareit}
  \wordfamily{enumeration（逐一清点，列举）；innumerable（无数的）；supernumerary（定额以外的）}
  \wordsense{vt. 逐一清点，列举}
  \wordphrase{The teacher enumerated the}
  \wordexample{The teacher enumerated the pupil's mistakes before punishing him.（老师在惩罚那个学生之前先逐一列举了他所 犯的错。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{innumerable}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{不可以数的 in（= not）+numer（= number, count)；I'njuimarabl}
  \wordfamily{enumerate；supernumerary；numerable；numeral}
  \wordsense{a. 无数的，数不清的，不计其数的}
  \wordphrase{innumerable former students around}
  \wordexample{I've run into innumerable former students around town over the years.（这几 年我在小镇周围碰到无数我以前教过的学生。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{supernumerary}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 NUMER}
  \wordfamily{numerable；numeral；numerate；numeration；enumerate（逐一清点）}
  \wordsense{a. 定额以外的；额外的，多余的}
  \wordphrase{be supernumerary（定额以外的）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "be supernumerary".（常见搭配：定额以外的。）}
  \wordsense{n. 额外人员；临时雇员；多余的人（或物）；小配角； 临时演员 *}
  \wordphrase{be supernumerary（定额以外的）}
  \wordexample{be supernumerary（定额以外的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{numerable}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 NUMER}
  \wordfamily{supernumerary；numeral；numerate；numeration；enumerate（逐一清点）}
  \wordsense{a. 可数的；可以计算的 *able（=capable of） 【'njumaral]}
  \wordphrase{be numerable（可数的）}
  \wordexample{be numerable（可数的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{numeral}{[]}

  \wordexample{A common collocation is "be numeral".（常见搭配：数字的。）}
  \wordsense{n. 数字}
  \wordphrase{Arabic / Roman numerals（阿拉伯数字 / 罗马数字）}
  \wordexample{Arabic / Roman numerals（阿拉伯数字 / 罗马数字）}
  \wordexample{be numeral（数字的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{numerate}{'njumareit}

  \wordsense{v. 数，计算；读数}
  \wordphrase{to numerate（数，计算）}

  \wordphrase{to numerate（数，计算）}
  \wordexample{to numerate（数，计算）}

\chapter{ALT[HAUT]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根 ALT[HAUT] 来自拉丁语的 alt 意为 high，它的变形有来自法语的 haut。同义词根有来自希腊语的 acro。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{exalt}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{使上得非常高 ex（= out, intensive）+alt（= high）；igzo:lt}
  \wordfamily{exaltation（提高，晋升）；exalted（高贵的）；altar（祭坛）}
  \wordsense{vt. 提高或提升（地位、权力、品位、名誉、财富等）；赞扬；使喜悦，使得意}
  \wordphrase{exalt the issue}
  \wordexample{Vice Presidents are sometimes suddenly exalted to the Presidency by assassinations.（有时副 总统会因暗杀事件而突然晋升为总统。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{altar}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ALT[HAUT]；'ɔ:ltə(r)}
  \wordfamily{altarpiece（祭坛后面、上面的装饰（如绘画、雕刻、屏风等））；exalt（提高或提升（地位、权力、品位、名誉、财富等））；altimeter（飞机等的高度计）}
  \wordsense{n. 祭坛，（基督教教堂内的）圣坛}
  \wordphrase{altar bread（圣餐面包）}
  \wordphrase{altar cloth（祭 坛罩）}
  \wordexample{lead a woman to the altar（和一个女子结婚）}
  \wordexample{altar bread（圣餐面包）}
  \wordexample{altar cloth（祭 坛罩）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{altimeter}{'æltimitə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ALT[HAUT]}
  \wordfamily{altar；altitude；alto；hauteur；exalt（提高或提升（地位、权力、品位、名誉、财富等））}
  \wordsense{n. 飞机等的高度计，测高仪 *}
  \wordphrase{an altimeter（飞机等的高度计，测高仪）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "an altimeter".（常见搭配：飞机等的高度计，测高仪。）}
  \wordsense{n. 高度，（特指）海拔；（pl.）高处（海拔甚高的地方）；地平纬度（地平线以上天体的}
  \wordphrase{an altimeter（飞机等的高度计，测高仪）}
  \wordexample{an altimeter（飞机等的高度计，测高仪）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{altitude}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ALT[HAUT]；'æltitju:d}
  \wordfamily{altimeter；alto；altar；hauteur；exalt（提高或提升（地位、权力、品位、名誉、财富等））}
  \wordsense{n. 角度距离） altitude flight / record（高空飞行 / 登高记录） mountain altitudes（高山地带） altitude sickness（高空病） grabbing for altitude（抢占高度）}
  \wordphrase{altitude flight / record（高空飞行 / 登高记录）}
  \wordphrase{mountain altitudes（高山地带）}
  \wordphrase{altitude sickness（高空病）}
  \wordexample{altitude flight / record（高空飞行 / 登高记录）}
  \wordexample{altitude sickness（高空病）}
  \wordexample{grabbing for altitude（抢占高度））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{alto}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ALT[HAUT]；'æltəu}
  \wordfamily{altocumulus（高积云）；exalt（提高或提升（地位、权力、品位、名誉、财富等））；altar（祭坛）}
  \wordsense{n. 女低音，男声最高音；女低音歌手；中音乐器}
  \wordphrase{alto saxophone（中音萨克斯风）}
  \wordexample{alto saxophone（中音萨克斯风）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{hauteur}{ou'tə:(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ALT[HAUT]}
  \wordfamily{alto；haughty；altitude；altimeter；exalt（提高或提升（地位、权力、品位、名誉、财富等））}
  \wordsense{n. 傲慢，自大 *}
  \wordphrase{a hauteur（傲慢，自大）}
  \wordexample{a hauteur（傲慢，自大）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{haughty}{['hɔ:ti]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ALT[HAUT]}
  \wordfamily{haughtily（傲慢地，高傲地）；pung（/ punct）；exalt（提高或提升（地位、权力、品位、名誉、财富等））}
  \wordsense{a. 傲慢的，高傲的，自大的 *}
  \wordphrase{be haughty}
  \wordexample{treat the common people with haughty contempt（以不屑一顾的傲慢态度对待老百姓）}
\end{word}



\chapter{STINCT, STING, STIG, STI}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根 STINCT, STING, STIG, STI 来自拉丁语的 stinct, sting, stig, sti 意为 to prick, to bind。同义
  \item 词根有来自拉丁语的 pung / punct。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{extinct}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{用针刺使没有的 ex（= out）+tinct<stinct（= prick）；ik'stinkt}
  \wordfamily{extinguisher（灭火器）；extinction（消灭，绝种）；使消灭（每年都有数百种昆虫灭绝）}
  \wordsense{a. 不再存在的；消亡了的，绝种的，灭绝的；（火）熄灭的；废除了的}
  \wordphrase{Hundreds of insect species become extinct each year}
  \wordexample{Hundreds of insect species become extinct each year （每年都有数百种昆虫灭绝。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{instigate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 STINCT, STING, STIG, STI；［在里面刺 in（= in）+stig（= prick）] ['instigeit]}
  \wordfamily{instigation（煽动）；instigator（煽动者）；extinct（不再存在的）}
  \wordsense{vt. 煽动；怂恿}
  \wordphrase{The union head instigated}
  \wordexample{The union head instigated the unrest among the workers.（工会的领导者煽动工人动乱。）}
  \wordexample{The union head instigated}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{prestigious}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{事先刺、捆的 pre（=before）+stig（=prick, bind）；pre'stidʒəs}
  \wordfamily{prestige（声望，威信）；extinct（不再存在的）；instigate（煽动）}
  \wordsense{a. 有名望的}
  \wordphrase{prestigious law firm}
  \wordexample{He was hired directly out of law school by a very prestigious law firm.（他一从法学院毕业就被一家知名律师事务所聘用了。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{extinguish}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 STINCT, STING, STIG, STI}
  \wordfamily{extinguisher；instigate；distinct；distinctive；extinct（不再存在的）}
  \wordsense{v. （光、火等）熄灭；使（希望、爱情、热情等）破灭；偿清（债务） *ex（= out）, ting（sting（= prick））}
  \wordphrase{to extinguish（熄灭）}
  \wordexample{to extinguish（熄灭）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{extinguisher}{ik'stiŋgwiʃə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 STINCT, STING, STIG, STI}
  \wordfamily{extinguish；distinct；distinctive；instigate；extinct（不再存在的）}
  \wordsense{n. 灭火器 *ex（= out）, ting（sting（= prick））}
  \wordphrase{an extinguisher（灭火器）}
  \wordexample{an extinguisher（灭火器）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{distinct}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 STINCT, STING, STIG, STI}

  \wordfamily{[di'stiŋkt]}
  \wordfamily{distinctly（清楚地，清晰地）}
  \wordfamily{speak distinctly（清楚地说） （清晰的发音）}
  \wordfamily{speak distinctly（清楚地说）}
  \wordphrase{distinct pronunciation}
  \wordphrase{distinct pronunciation （清晰的发音）}
  \wordexample{Keep the two ideas distinct.（将这两个概念区分开来。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{distinctive}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 STINCT, STING, STIG, STI；di'stiŋktiv}

  \wordphrase{distinctive uniform（独特的制服）}
  \wordexample{distinctive uniform（独特的制服）}
\end{word}

  \wordphrase{distinction between poetry and prose（诗和散文的不同）}
  \wordphrase{writer of distinction（知名作 家）}
  \wordphrase{academic distinction（学术上的荣誉）}
  \wordexample{a distinction（差别）}
\end{word}

  \wordbreakdown{词根 STINCT, STING, STIG, STI}

  \wordphrase{distinguish between good and evil（区分善与恶）}
  \wordexample{distinguish between good and evil（区分善与恶）}

\begin{word}[***]{sting}{'stiŋ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 STINCT, STING, STIG, STI}
  \wordfamily{stingless（没有毒针的，无刺的）；stingray（刺鳐）；刺人的动物（或植物）；extinct（不再存在的）}

  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of sting.（此处展示 sting 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. （用针 等）刺；螫；刺痛，使疼痛；（身体局部）刺痛；激怒；漫天要价，敲诈}
  \wordphrase{sting the issue}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of sting.（此处展示 sting 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. stinger（痛击；刺人的动物（或植物）；爱挖苦的人，讽刺者；螯针，刺）}
  \wordphrase{sting of a scorpion（蝎子的毒刺）}
  \wordphrase{sting operation（美国联邦调查局等的“针刺行动”）}
  \wordexample{sting of a scorpion（蝎子的毒刺）}
  \wordexample{sting operation（美国联邦调查局等的“针刺行动”）}
\end{word}

  \wordexample{stingy person（吝 啬鬼）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{stigma}{[]}

  \wordphrase{a stimulant（兴奋剂）}
  \wordexample{a stimulant（兴奋剂）}

\chapter{LONG}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根 LONG 来自拉丁语的 long 意为 long。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{elongate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{使伸展得长长地 e<ex（= out）+long（= long）；i'lɒŋgeit}
  \wordfamily{elongation（伸长，延长）；prolong（延长）；oblong（/）}
  \wordsense{v. 使伸长，拉长；延长}
  \wordphrase{elongate it}
  \wordexample{Pull on salt water taffy to elongate it as it cools. （当盐水太妃糖冷却的时候用力拉使它变长。）}
  \wordexample{elongate it}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{prolong}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{使向前拉长 pro（= forth）+long（= long）；prə'lɒŋ}
  \wordfamily{prolongation（延长）；prolonged（长期持续的）；elongate（使伸长）}
  \wordsense{v. 延长，拉长；拖长（元音等的）发音}
  \wordphrase{prolong our dog's suffering}
  \wordexample{We didn't want to prolong our dog's suffering, so we put him to sleep.（我们不想延长我们狗的痛苦，所以让它安乐死了。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{oblong}{'ɒblɒŋ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LONG}
  \wordfamily{longitude；longitudinal；longeron；long-bill；elongate（使伸长）}
  \wordsense{n. /}
  \wordphrase{the oblong}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of oblong.（此处展示 oblong 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{a. 椭圆形（的），长方形（的） *}
  \wordphrase{be oblong}
  \wordexample{an oblong（/a.椭圆形（的），长方形（的））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{longitude}{[]}

  \wordexample{ten degrees fifteen minutes of east longitude（东经10度15分）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{longitudinal}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LONG；ˌlɒndʒi'tju:dinl}

  \wordphrase{longitudinal stripes（纵向的条纹）}
  \wordexample{longitudinal survey（纵向调查）}
  \wordexample{longitudinal stripes（纵向的条纹）}
\end{word}

\chapter{RADIC[RADIS]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根 RADIC[RADIS] 来自拉丁语的 radic 意为 root，它的变形为 radis。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{eradicate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{使连根拔出 e(ex（= out）+radic（= root）；i'rædikeit}
  \wordfamily{eradication（根除）；eradicator（除草机）；radical（根本的）}
  \wordsense{vt. 根除，根绝；清除；消灭}
  \wordphrase{Polio has been eradicated}
  \wordexample{Polio has been eradicated in North America, but not in Africa.（小儿麻痹在北美已被消灭，但在非洲还没有。）}
  \wordexample{Polio has been eradicated in North America，but not in Africa.（小儿麻痹在北美已被消 灭，但在非洲还没有。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{radical}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 RADIC[RADIS]}
  \wordfamily{eradicate（根除）；radicalize（使激进）；radicle（胚根 *）}
  \wordsense{a. 根本的，基本的；（意见、政策等）激进的，过激的；【数】根的}
  \wordphrase{be radical}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of radical.（此处展示 radical 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 激进分子，激进党派成员；根号；根数}
  \wordphrase{radical change / reform（彻底的变化 / 彻底的 改革）}
  \wordphrase{radical program（激进的方案）}
  \wordphrase{the radical sign（根号√）}
  \wordexample{radical change / reform（彻底的变化 / 彻底的 改革）}
  \wordexample{radical program（激进的方案）}
  \wordexample{the radical sign（根号√）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{radicalize}{[]}

  \wordexample{to radicalize（使激进，使过激 ['rædikəlaiz] radicalism（激进主义））}
\end{word}

  \wordexample{a radicle（胚根）}

\chapter{LIP}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根 LIP 来自拉丁语的 lip 意为 to leave。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 linqu / lic / lict。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{eclipse}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{因离开而没有了、火熄灭了 ec(ex（= out）+lip（= leave）；i'klips}
  \wordfamily{ecliptic（黄道 *ec(ex(= out)）；radish（（做沙拉时使用的白色或红色的）萝卜 *）；radicle（胚根 *）}
  \wordsense{n. （太阳、月亮的)食；（势力、名誉等)黯然失色}
  \wordphrase{the eclipse}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of eclipse.（此处展示 eclipse 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 遮光；使失色}
  \wordphrase{eclipse the issue}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of eclipse.（此处展示 eclipse 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. ）黄道}
  \wordphrase{His accomplishments were eclipsed}
  \wordexample{His accomplishments were eclipsed by those of his famous father.（他的成就与他出名的父亲的业绩相比黯然失色。）}
  \wordexample{His accomplishments were eclipsed}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{ecliptic}{i'kliptik}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LIP}
  \wordfamily{eclipse；radish（（做沙拉时使用的白色或红色的）萝卜 *）；radicle（胚根 *）}
  \wordsense{n. 黄道 *ec(ex(= out)}
  \wordphrase{an ecliptic（黄道）}
  \wordexample{an ecliptic（黄道）}
\end{word}



\chapter{MANU[MAN]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根 MANU[MAN] 来自拉丁语的 manu 意为 hand，它的变形为 man。同义词根有来自
  \item 希腊语的 chiro。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{emancipate}{i'mænsipeit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MANU[MAN]；［将抓着的手递到外边 e(ex（= out）+man<manu（= hand）+cip(cap（= take）］}
  \wordfamily{manipulate（（熟练地）操作）；manacle（（pl.）手铐）；management（经营）}
  \wordsense{vt. sb/sth + from + sth 解放（奴隶等）；给予自由}
  \wordphrase{The army arrived and}
  \wordexample{The army arrived and emancipated the prisoners of war.（军队到了之后释放了战俘。）}
  \wordexample{The army arrived and}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{manipulate}{mə'nipjuleit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MANU[MAN]；［用手拉走 man<manu（= hand）+pul（= drive）］}
  \wordfamily{manipulation（巧妙处理）；manipulator（操作者）；emancipate（sb/sth + from + sth 解放（奴）}
  \wordsense{vt. （熟练地）操作，使用；巧妙地处理}
  \wordphrase{manipulates the vocal cords}
  \wordexample{The larynx is the structure that manipulates the vocal cords to produce speech.（喉是控制声带使之发声的器官。）}
  \wordexample{manipulates the vocal cords}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{manacle}{'mænəkl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MANU[MAN]}
  \wordfamily{manipulate；management；emancipate；manoeuver}
  \wordsense{n. （pl.）手铐；脚镣}
  \wordphrase{a manacle（手铐）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "a manacle".（常见搭配：手铐。）}
  \wordsense{v. 戴上手铐；束缚 *man(manu（= hand）, cle（= small）)}
  \wordphrase{a manacle（手铐）}
  \wordexample{a manacle（手铐）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{management}{'mænidʒmənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MANU[MAN]}
  \wordfamily{emancipate（sb/sth + from + sth 解放（奴）；manipulate（（熟练地）操作）；manacle（（pl.）手铐）}
  \wordsense{n. 经营，运营；管理；处理；（the\textasciitilde{}）［总称］经营层；管理层 *}
  \wordphrase{the management}
  \wordexample{manageability（易管理；易处理） ?joint consultation between workers and management（劳资双方的联席会议）}
  \wordexample{joint consultation between workers}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{manoeuver}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MANU[MAN]}
  \wordfamily{maneuverable（有机动性的，可调动的）；maneuverability（机动性）；emancipate（sb/sth + from + sth 解放（奴）}
  \wordsense{n. （军队等的）机动；（pl.）机动演习，军事演习；花招，伎俩；策略}
  \wordphrase{the manoeuver}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of manoeuver.（此处展示 manoeuver 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. 机动；机动演习 [mə'nu:və(r)] *}
  \wordphrase{troops on maneuvers（机动训练中的部队）}
  \wordphrase{despicable maneuvers of some politicians（一些政治家的卑劣伎俩）}
  \wordphrase{army/fleet maneuvers（陆军/舰队的机动演习）}
  \wordexample{troops on maneuvers（机动训练中的部队）}
  \wordexample{despicable maneuvers of some politicians（一些政治家的卑劣伎俩）}
  \wordexample{The fleet is maneuvering off the east coast.（舰队正在东海岸进行机动演习。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{manicure}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MANU[MAN]；'mænikjua(r)}
  \wordfamily{manicurist（指甲修饰师，美甲师）；emancipate（sb/sth + from + sth 解放（奴）；manipulate（（熟练地）操作）}
  \wordsense{n. 修指甲}
  \wordphrase{the manicure}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of manicure.（此处展示 manicure 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. 修指甲；修剪 *man<manu（= hand）,}
  \wordphrase{manicure the issue}
  \wordexample{have a manicure once a week（一星期修剪一次 指甲）}
  \wordexample{manicurist（指甲修饰师，美甲师）?have a manicure once a week（一星期修剪一次 指甲）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{manifest}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MANU[MAN]}
  \wordfamily{manicure；manifestation；manoeuver；manifesto；emancipate（sb/sth + from + sth 解放（奴）}
  \wordsense{a. 明白的，显然的，清楚的}
  \wordphrase{be manifest}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of manifest.（此处展示 manifest 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. 表明，显示，表露；（幽灵等）出现；（在集会等）发表意见}
  \wordphrase{manifest the issue}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of manifest.（此处展示 manifest 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. （向海关提交的船运的）载货清单，舱单 *man<manu(= hand), fest(= strike)}
  \wordphrase{manifest truth（显然的事实）}
  \wordexample{manifest truth（显然的事实）}
  \wordexample{She doesn't manifest much desire to marry him.（她没表示很想和他结婚。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{manifestation}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MANU[MAN]}
  \wordfamily{manifest；manifesto；manicure；manual；emancipate（sb/sth + from + sth 解放（奴）}

\chapter{HOD[OD]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根 HOD[OD] 来自拉丁语的 hod 意为 way, road，其变形为 od。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{exodus}{'eksədəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 HOD[OD]}
  \wordfamily{methodical（有秩序的）；anode（（电子管的、电解槽等的）阳极）；cathode（（电池的）阴极）}
  \wordsense{n. （大批）的出走，迁出；（the E\textasciitilde{}）古代以色列人出埃及；（E\textasciitilde{}）基督教《圣经》中的《出埃及记》}
  \wordphrase{exodus of peasants}
  \wordexample{After the earthquake, an exodus of peasants from the decimated city clogged the highways.（地震后，大批从遭到破坏的城市中逃离的农民堵塞了公路。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{methodical}{mə'θodikl}
  \wordbreakdown{端正地走路的 met<meta（= intensive, change, beyond）+ hod（= way）}
  \wordsense{a.有秩序的，井然的；（指人）做事有条理的}
  \wordfamily{method 条理，秩序；方法}
  \wordfamily{methodology 方法论，方法学}
  \wordphrase{methodical approach}
  \wordexample{Science can be defined as a methodical approach to knowledge acquisition.（科学可以被定义为一种系统获取知识的方法。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{anode}{'ænəud}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 HOD[OD]}
  \wordfamily{cathode；exodus；odometer；period}
  \wordsense{n. （电子管的、电解槽等的）阳极，（干电池、蓄电池等的）正极 *an(ana(= up), od(hod(= way, road)}
  \wordphrase{an anode（阳极，（干电池、蓄电池等的）正极）}
  \wordexample{an anode（阳极，（干电池、蓄电池等的）正极）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{cathode}{'kæθəud}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 HOD[OD]}
  \wordfamily{anode；odometer；period；exodus}
  \wordsense{n. （电池的）阴极，负极 *cat(cata(= down)}
  \wordphrase{cathode ray（（雷达、电视等使用的）阴极射线）}
  \wordexample{cathode ray（（雷达、电视等使用的）阴极射线）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{odometer}{au'dɒmitə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 HOD[OD]}
  \wordfamily{cathode；period；anode；periodic；exodus（（大批）的出走）}
  \wordsense{n. （汽车等的）里程表 *od(hod(= way, road), meter(= measure)}
  \wordphrase{an odometer（里程表）}
  \wordexample{an odometer（里程表）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{period}{'pɪəriəd}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 HOD[OD]}
  \wordfamily{exodus（（大批）的出走）；methodical（有秩序的）；anode（（电子管的、电解槽等的）阳极）}
  \wordsense{n. 期间；周期；时代，时期；（学校的）学时，一节（课）；句号；疾病的期间（周期）；经期 *peri(= around), od(hod(= way, road)}
  \wordphrase{the second period（第二节课）}
  \wordphrase{period of incubation（潜伏期）}
  \wordexample{the second period（第二节课）}
  \wordexample{period of incubation（潜伏期）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{periodic}{pɪəri'ɒdik}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 HOD[OD]}
  \wordfamily{period；periodical；odometer；synod；exodus（（大批）的出走）}
  \wordsense{a. 周期的；定期的 *peri(= around), od(hod(= way, road)}
  \wordphrase{periodic attacks of malaria（疟疾的周期性发作）}
  \wordphrase{periodic table（（元素）周期表）}
  \wordexample{periodic revolution of a heavenly body（天体的周期运转）}
  \wordexample{periodic attacks of malaria（疟疾的周期性发作）}
  \wordexample{periodic table（（元素）周期表）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{periodical}{pɪəri'ɒdikl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 HOD[OD]}
  \wordfamily{periodic；synod；period；odometer；exodus（（大批）的出走）}
  \wordsense{n. 期刊（日刊除外）}
  \wordphrase{the periodical}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of periodical.（此处展示 periodical 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{a. 定期发行的；期刊的；周期的；定期的 *peri(= around), od(hod(= way, road)}
  \wordphrase{be periodical}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of periodical.（此处展示 periodical 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{a. 定期发行的）}
  \wordphrase{be periodical}
  \wordexample{a periodical（期刊（日刊除外）a.定期发行的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{synod}{'sɪnəd}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 HOD[OD]}
  \wordfamily{periodical；periodic；period；odometer；exodus（（大批）的出走）}
  \wordsense{n. 宗教会议 *syn(= together), od(hod(= way, road)}
  \wordphrase{a synod（宗教会议）}
  \wordexample{a synod（宗教会议）}
\end{word}



\chapter{ORB}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根ORB 来自拉丁语的orb意为circle，track。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{exorbitant}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ORB；脱离常规、轨道的 ex（=out）+orb（=track,circle）］ [ig'zɔbitənt}
  \wordfamily{exorbitantly（过高地，昂贵地）；suborbital（未进入（地球）轨道的）；orbit（（天体、宇宙飞船等的）轨道）}
  \wordsense{a. （价格、要求等）过高的，过分的}
  \wordphrase{exorbitant during WW II}
  \wordexample{Prices for butter and eggs were exorbitant during WW II.（第二次世界大战期间黄油和鸡蛋的价格贵得离谱。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{suborbital}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ORB；sʌb'ɔbitl}
  \wordfamily{exorbitant（（价格、要求等）过高的）；orbit（（天体、宇宙飞船等的）轨道）；orbital（轨道的）}
  \wordsense{a. 未进入（地球）轨道的，亚轨道的；在眼眶之下的 *}
  \wordphrase{suborbital flight（（宇宙飞船等的）亚轨迹飞行）}
  \wordexample{suborbital flight（（宇宙飞船等的）亚轨迹飞行）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{orbit}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ORB}
  \wordfamily{suborbital；orbital；exorbitant}
  \wordsense{n. （天体、宇宙飞船等的）轨道}
  \wordphrase{the orbit}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of orbit.（此处展示 orbit 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. 进入轨道；沿轨道飞行、盘旋}
  \wordphrase{orbit the issue}
  \wordexample{the earth's orbit round the sun（地球围绕太阳的轨道）}
  \wordexample{How many spacecraft have orbited the moon?（有多少宇宙飞船绕月球运行过？）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{orbital}{'ɔbitl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ORB}
  \wordfamily{orbit；suborbital；exorbitant}
  \wordsense{a. 轨道的}
  \wordphrase{orbital velocity（轨道速度）}
  \wordexample{spacecraft's orbital distance from the earth（宇宙飞船距离地球的轨道距离）}
  \wordexample{orbital velocity（轨道速度）}
\end{word}



\chapter{TERMIN}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根TERMIN 来自拉丁语的termin意为end，limit，boundary。同义词根有来自
  \item 拉丁语的fin，ultim和来自希腊语的bys等。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{exterminate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{完全结束 ex（=out）+termin（=end）；ik'st3imineit}
  \wordfamily{extermination（根除，灭绝）；interminable（无限的）；terminate（使停止）}
  \wordsense{vt. 根除（疾病、想法等）；消灭，歼灭}
  \wordphrase{exterminate mankind}
  \wordexample{Nuclear weapons may be used to exterminate mankind.（核武器很可能会被用来消灭人类。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{interminable}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{没有终止的 in（=not）+termin（=end）；in't3iminabl}
  \wordfamily{interminably（无限地）；exterminate（根除（疾病、想法等））；terminate（使停止）}
  \wordsense{a. 无限的，无休止的；冗长的}
  \wordphrase{interminable I was so}
  \wordexample{The movie was interminable. I was so bored I actually slept!（电影太长了，我无聊得都睡着了！）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{terminate}{'t3:mineit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TERMIN}
  \wordsense{v. 使停止；使结束；解雇}
  \wordfamily{termination 终止，终结，结局}
  \wordfamily{terminus 终点站；终点}
  \wordfamily{terminable 有期限的；可终结的}
  \wordphrase{terminating your employment effective}
  \wordexample{We are terminating your employment effective February 24th.（我们将于2月24日终止对你的聘用。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{conterminous}{kpn'timinəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TERMIN}
  \wordfamily{exterminate（根除（疾病、想法等））；interminable（无限的）；terminate（使停止）}
  \wordsense{a. 有共同边界的；邻接的；（空间、时间、范围上）同样广大的 *}
  \wordphrase{be conterminous（有共同边界的）}
  \wordexample{be conterminous（有共同边界的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{determine}{di't3:min}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TERMIN；语}
  \wordfamily{determinable（可决定的）；exterminate（根除（疾病、想法等））；interminable（无限的）}
  \wordsense{v. 下决心；决定；判定；确定 *}
  \wordphrase{to determine（下决心）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "to determine".（常见搭配：下决心。）}
  \wordsense{n. determiner（限定词）}
  \wordphrase{to determine（下决心）}
  \wordexample{to determine（下决心）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{determined}{di't3:mind}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TERMIN}
  \wordfamily{determine；determination；conterminous；determinant；exterminate（根除（疾病、想法等））}
  \wordsense{a. 坚决的，坚定的，决然的 *de（= away） determined look（坚决的表情）}
  \wordphrase{determined look（坚决的表情）}
  \wordphrase{be determined（坚决的，坚定的，决然的）}
  \wordexample{be determined（坚决的，坚定的，决然的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{determination}{di,t3:mi'neifn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TERMIN}
  \wordsense{n. 决定；（\textasciitilde{}to do）决心；决意，果断；（\textasciitilde{}of）（数量等的）计算，测定 *de（= away） his determination to learn English（他学英语的决心） with an air of determination（神情坚决） the determination of the amount of metal in a specimen of ore（矿石标本中金属含量的测定）}
  \wordfamily{determined}
  \wordfamily{determinant}
  \wordfamily{determine}
  \wordfamily{predetermine}
  \wordfamily{a determination（决定）}
  \wordphrase{a determination（决定）}
  \wordexample{a determination（决定）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{determinant}{di't3:minənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TERMIN}
  \wordfamily{determination；predetermine；determined；terminal；exterminate（根除（疾病、想法等））}
  \wordsense{n. 决定物；决定因素}
  \wordphrase{a determinant（决定物）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "a determinant".（常见搭配：决定物。）}
  \wordsense{a. 决定的 *}
  \wordphrase{a determinant（决定物）}
  \wordexample{a determinant（决定物）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{predetermine}{,pri:di't3:min}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TERMIN}
  \wordfamily{determinant；terminal；determination；terminus；exterminate（根除（疾病、想法等））}
  \wordsense{v. 预先决定；预先查明 *pre（= before）, de（= away） predetermination（预先决定）}
  \wordphrase{to predetermine（预先决定）}
  \wordexample{to predetermine（预先决定）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{terminal}{'t3:minl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TERMIN}
  \wordsense{a. 末端的；终点的；学期的，期末的；（致命疾病的）末期的 n. 终端，末端；终点；终点站；【电】端子 terminal accounts（终结账户） terminal cancer（晚期癌症） the West London Air Terminal（西伦敦航空终点站） the terminal of a battery（电池的端子）}
  \wordfamily{predetermine}
  \wordfamily{terminus}
  \wordfamily{determinant}
  \wordfamily{terminology}
  \wordfamily{be terminal（末端的）}
  \wordphrase{be terminal（末端的）}

  \wordphrase{a terminus（终点站）}

\begin{word}[**]{terminology}{.t3:mi'nɒlədʒi}

\chapter{LUC[LUX,LUS]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根LUC[LUX,LUS] 拉丁语的lumin[lun】和来自希腊语的photo以及来自盎格鲁-撒
  \item 克逊语的light。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{elucidate}{iluisideit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LUC[LUX,LUS]；［拉出外边使清楚 e(ex（=out）+luc（=light）］}
  \wordfamily{elucidation（说明，解释）；elucidator（解释者）；lucubration（（夜间挑灯）刻苦学习）}
  \wordsense{vt. 说明，阐明（问题）}
  \wordphrase{elucidator 解释者 ?The rules}
  \wordexample{elucidator 解释者 ?The rules of rhetoric are elucidated in several works by Aristotle.（亚里士多德的几部作品都对修辞规则作了阐述。）}
  \wordexample{The rules of rhetoric are elucidated in several works by Aristotle.（亚里士多德的几部作品都对修辞规则作了阐述。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{lucubration}{ljukju:"breifn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LUC[LUX,LUS]；［在火光、灯光下努力后出现的结果 luc（=light）］}
  \wordfamily{elucidate（说明）；pellucid（透明的）；translucent（半透明的 *trans（=through） tr）}
  \wordsense{n. （夜间挑灯）刻苦学习；苦心的著作}
  \wordphrase{the lucubration}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of lucubration.（此处展示 lucubration 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. lucubrate 刻苦研究，用功学习；著书立说}
  \wordphrase{lucubration have furrowed your}
  \wordexample{All those hours of lucubration have furrowed your forehead.（长期的刻苦在你的额头上留下了深深的皱纹。）}
  \wordexample{lucubration have furrowed your}
\end{word}

\chapter{EMPT, EM[EEM, MPT, M, ANSOM]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根 EMPT, EM[EEM, MPT, M, ANSOM] 来自拉丁语，意为 to take。
  \item 同义词根有来自拉丁语的 prehend/prehens、pris、cap/capt/cept/ceiv[cip,cup,ceit]、rap/rav、sum/sumpt、lab/lep 等。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{exemplary}{ig'zemplari}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 EMPT, EM[EEM, MPT, M, ANSOM]；［抓向外边的、举例的 ex（=out）+em（=take）］}
  \wordfamily{exemplify（例证）；exemplification（例证）；impromptu（/）}
  \wordsense{a. 模范的，典范的；惩戒性的，警告性的}
  \wordphrase{exemplary academic performance}
  \wordexample{He was named valedictorian for his exemplary academic performance.（他因优异的学习成绩而被选为毕业生代表。）}
  \wordexample{exemplary academic performance}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{impromptu}{im'prpmptju:}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 EMPT, EM[EEM, MPT, M, ANSOM]；［不提前抓 im<in（=not）+pro（=before）+mpt<empt（=take）］}
  \wordfamily{exemplary；peremptory；preempt；exempt}
  \wordsense{a. /}
  \wordphrase{be impromptu}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of impromptu.（此处展示 impromptu 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{ad. 无准备的（地），即兴的（地）}
  \wordphrase{be impromptu}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of impromptu.（此处展示 impromptu 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 即席之作（如演奏、作诗等）}
  \wordphrase{impromptu party}
  \wordphrase{im<in（=not）+pro（=before）+mpt<empt（=take）］）}
  \wordexample{We had an impromptu party in the dorm the day after finals.（期末考试结束后的第二天，我们在宿舍临时开了一个晚会。）}
  \wordexample{impromptu party}
  \wordexample{im<in（=not）+pro（=before）+mpt<empt（=take）］）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{peremptory}{pa'remptari}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 EMPT, EM[EEM, MPT, M, ANSOM]；［采取强硬态度的 per（=intensive）+empt（=take）］}
  \wordfamily{impromptu；preempt；exemplary；exempt}
  \wordsense{a. （命令）断然的；独断的；强制的}
  \wordphrase{peremptory order}
  \wordexample{The constable issued a peremptory order for a 10 p.m. curfew.（警官发布了晚间十点宵禁的强制禁令。）}
  \wordexample{peremptory order}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{preempt}{pri'empt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 EMPT, EM[EEM, MPT, M, ANSOM]；［事先抓住、采取 pre（=before）+empt（=take）］}
  \wordfamily{preemptive（先取得的）；exemplary（模范的）；impromptu（/）}
  \wordsense{vt. 通过优先购买权获得；为得到优先购买权而占有（公地）；先发制人}
  \wordphrase{preempted regular programming}
  \wordexample{preemptive 先取得的；优先购买权的；先发制人的 ?The special news bulletin preempted regular programming.（特别新闻快报取代了正常播放的节目。）}
  \wordexample{The special news bulletin preempted regular programming.（特别新闻快报取代了正常播放的节目。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{exempt}{[]}

  \wordphrase{exempt the issue}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of exempt.（此处展示 exempt 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{a. 被免除的；被豁免的 *}
  \wordphrase{exempt from tax（免税的）}
  \wordexample{exempt from tax（免税的）}
\end{word}

\chapter{TOL}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根TOL 来自拉丁语的tol意为to raise，to support。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{extol}{ik'staul}
  \wordbreakdown{向外、向上举 ex（=out,up）+tol（=raise,support）}
  \wordsense{vt. 高度赞扬，极力称赞}
  \wordfamily{intolerable}
  \wordfamily{tolerate}
  \wordfamily{tolerance}
  \wordfamily{tolerable}
  \wordphrase{extol the virtues}
  \wordexample{Newlyweds tend to extol the virtues of their mates to all who will listen.（新婚夫妇往往喜欢向所有愿意听的人极力称赞自己爱人的美德。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{intolerable}{in'tplarabl}
  \wordbreakdown{无法支撑自我的 in（=not）+tol（=support,raise）}
  \wordsense{a. 无法忍受的，难耐的}
  \wordfamily{intolerance 不宽容；无法忍受}
  \wordfamily{intolerant 无法忍受的；偏执的}
  \wordphrase{intolerable}
  \wordexample{I find the heat and humidity in Panama to be intolerable.（我觉得巴拿马的高温和潮湿实在难以忍受。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{tolerate}{'tplareit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TOL}
  \wordfamily{extol（高度赞扬）；intolerable（无法忍受的）；tolerance（（对与自己不同的意见、信仰、习惯、行为等的）宽容）}
  \wordsense{v. 容忍，忍受；宽容}
  \wordphrase{tolerate that pompous idiot}
  \wordexample{How can you tolerate that pompous idiot?（你怎么能忍受那个狂妄自大的白痴？）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{tolerance}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TOL}
  \wordfamily{tolerate；tolerable；tolerant；toll；extol（高度赞扬）}
  \wordsense{n. （对与自己不同的意见、信仰、习惯、行为等的）宽容；容忍 religious / racial tolerance（宗教 / 种族宽容）}
  \wordphrase{religious / racial tolerance（宗教 / 种族宽容）}
  \wordphrase{a tolerance（宽容）}
  \wordexample{a tolerance（宽容）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{tolerable}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TOL}
  \wordsense{a. 可忍受的，可容忍的；尚好的；还可以的，过得去的；《口》（身体）还算健康的}
  \wordfamily{['tplarab]]}
  \wordfamily{tolerably（过得去地；可容忍地）}
  \wordphrase{tolerable food（还可以的食物）}
  \wordphrase{in tolerable health（身体还算健康）}
  \wordexample{tolerable food（还可以的食物）}
  \wordexample{in tolerable health（身体还算健康）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{tolerant}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TOL；'tplarant}
  \wordfamily{extol（高度赞扬）；intolerable（无法忍受的）；tolerate（容忍）}
  \wordsense{a. 宽容的；容忍的}
  \wordphrase{tolerant of criticism / contradiction（容忍批评 / 容忍反驳）}
  \wordexample{tolerant of criticism / contradiction（容忍批评 / 容忍反驳）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{toll}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TOL}
  \wordfamily{tolerant；tolerable；tolerance；tolerate；extol（高度赞扬）}
  \wordsense{n. （桥梁、道路等的）通行费；代价；（事故等的）伤亡人数；长途电话费；（缓慢而有规律地敲响的）钟（声）}
  \wordphrase{toll gate（收费门）}
  \wordphrase{toll collector（收费员；收费机）}
  \wordphrase{toll call（长途电话）}
  \wordexample{take a heavy toll of South Korea's manhood（韩国男子大量死伤）}
  \wordexample{The funeral bell tolled solemnly.（丧钟庄严地鸣响。）}
  \wordsense{v. 征收捐税，收费；（有规律地、缓慢地）敲钟 toll gate（收费门）toll collector（收费员；收费机）toll call（长途电话）toll-free（不用付电话费的）road toll（交通事故死伤者）take a heavy toll of South Korea's manhood（韩国男子大量死伤）The funeral bell tolled solemnly.（丧钟庄严地鸣响。）}
  \wordphrase{a toll（通行费）}
  \wordexample{a toll（通行费）}
\end{word}



\chapter{GREG}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根GREG 来自拉丁语的greg意为flock，mob。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{egregious}{i'gri:dʒəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 GREG；［脱离人群的 e<ex（=out）+greg（=flock,mob）］}
  \wordfamily{egregiously（极坏地）；gregarious（群居的）；segregate（分离）}
  \wordsense{a. 极坏的；异乎寻常的}
  \wordphrase{egregious mistakes}
  \wordexample{The bassoon soloist made several egregious mistakes at the recital.（那个低音管独奏者在演奏会上出现了几次极大的失误。）}
  \wordexample{egregious mistakes}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{gregarious}{grɪ'geəriəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 GREG}
  \wordfamily{egregious；segregate；aggregate；congregate}
  \wordsense{a. 群居的，集体的；合群的；爱社交的}
  \wordphrase{gregarious friend}
  \wordexample{I tend to be lost in the shadow of my gregarious friend.（同我善于社交的朋友在一起，我会不知所措。）}
  \wordexample{gregarious friend}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{segregate}{'segrigeit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 GREG；［使脱离于群 se（=away）+greg（=flock,mob）］}
  \wordfamily{segregation（分离，隔离）；segregationist（种族隔离主义者）；egregious（极坏的）}
  \wordsense{v. 分离，隔离；实行种族隔离}
  \wordphrase{segregate the bitch}
  \wordexample{segregationist 种族隔离主义者 ?You need to segregate the bitch from the other dogs when she's in heat.（当母狗发情时，你需要把它和其他狗隔离开来。）}
  \wordexample{You need to segregate the bitch from the other dogs when she's in heat.（当母狗发情时，你需要把它和其他狗隔离开来。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{aggregate}{‘ægrigeit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 GREG}
  \wordfamily{aggregation（集合体，集成体）；egregious（极坏的）；gregarious（群居的）}
  \wordsense{v. 聚集，集合；总计达}
  \wordphrase{aggregate the issue}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of aggregate.（此处展示 aggregate 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. ［'ægrigat］合计，总数，总额；（混凝土的）骨料}
  \wordphrase{the aggregate}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of aggregate.（此处展示 aggregate 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{a. 总计的，合计的 *ag(}
  \wordphrase{in the aggregate（总计）}
  \wordphrase{aggregate demand（总需求）}
  \wordexample{The money collected aggregated \$1,000.（筹集的资金总计1000美元。）}
  \wordexample{in the aggregate（总计）}
  \wordexample{aggregate demand（总需求）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{congregate}{kɒŋgrigeit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 GREG}
  \wordfamily{aggregate；congregation；segregate；congregational；egregious（极坏的）}
  \wordsense{v. （使）集合 *}
  \wordphrase{People quickly congregated around}
  \wordexample{People quickly congregated around the speaker.（人们很快就聚集到了演说者周围。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{congregation}{ikDpgri'gein}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 GREG}
  \wordsense{n. 会集，集合；（教堂的）会众（通常牧师、圣歌队除外）；（宗教的）集会 'con<com（=together）}
  \wordfamily{congregate}
  \wordfamily{congregational}
  \wordfamily{aggregate}
  \wordfamily{desegregate}
  \wordfamily{a congregation（会集，集合）}
  \wordphrase{a congregation（会集，集合）}
  \wordexample{a congregation（会集，集合）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{congregational}{ikpngrr'geinl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 GREG}
  \wordsense{a.（教堂）会众的；集合的；（C～）（基督教）公理会的 'con<com（=together）}
  \wordfamily{congregation}
  \wordfamily{desegregate}
  \wordfamily{congregate}
  \wordfamily{aggregate}
  \wordphrase{Congregational church（（基督教的）公理教堂）}
  \wordexample{Congregational church（（基督教的）公理教堂）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{desegregate}{,di:'segrigeit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 GREG}
  \wordfamily{desegregation（废除种族隔离）；egregious（极坏的）；gregarious（群居的）}
  \wordsense{v. 废除种族隔离 *de（=reverse the action of）,}
  \wordphrase{desegregate schools in Alabama（亚拉巴马州取消种族隔离的学校）}
  \wordphrase{to entice，to permit。}
  \wordexample{desegregate schools in Alabama（亚拉巴马州取消种族隔离的学校）}
\end{word}



\chapter{LIC}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根LIC 来自拉丁语的 lic 意为 to entice，to permit。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{elicit}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{诱骗使出到外边e(ex（=out）+lic（=entice,permit)；i'lisit}
  \wordfamily{elicitation（引出）；illicit（非法的）；licit（合法的）}
  \wordsense{vt. sth + from + sb 引出；诱出}
  \wordphrase{elicited only laughter}
  \wordexample{Her remarks, intended seriously, elicited only laughter from the group.（她的发言本是很严肃的，但却只让大家大笑起来。）}
  \wordexample{elicited only laughter}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{illicit}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{法律不允许的 i<in（=not）+lic（=permit）；i'lisit}
  \wordfamily{illicitly（非法地）；elicit（sth + from + sb 引出）；licit（合法的）}
  \wordsense{a. 非法的，法律禁止的}
  \wordphrase{illicit affair}
  \wordexample{The minister had conducted an illicit affair with a parishioner for years.（那个牧师多年以来一直和一个教区居民保持着非法关系。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{licit}{lisit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LIC}
  \wordsense{a.合法的，正当的}
  \wordfamily{license}
  \wordfamily{licensed}
  \wordfamily{licentious}
  \wordfamily{leisure}
  \wordfamily{be licit（合法的，正当的）}
  \wordphrase{be licit（合法的，正当的）}
  \wordexample{be licit（合法的，正当的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{license}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LIC；laisns}
  \wordfamily{licentiate（有开业执照者）；elicit（sth + from + sb 引出）；illicit（非法的）}
  \wordsense{n. 执照；许可证；放纵，放肆}
  \wordphrase{driver's license（驾驶执照）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "driver's license".（常见搭配：驾驶执照。）}
  \wordsense{v. 许可，准许；发放执照}
  \wordphrase{license to practice medicine（医生的行医执照）}
  \wordexample{license to practice medicine（医生的行医执照）}
  \wordexample{a license（执照）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{licensed}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LIC；'larsnst}
  \wordfamily{elicit（sth + from + sb 引出）；illicit（非法的）；licit（合法的）}
  \wordsense{a. 获准售酒的}
  \wordphrase{licensed house（获准售酒处）}
  \wordexample{licensed house（获准售酒处）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{licentious}{lar'senJes}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LIC}

  \wordfamily{licensed}
  \wordfamily{leisure}
  \wordfamily{license}
  \wordfamily{leisurely}
  \wordfamily{be licentious（放荡的）}
  \wordphrase{be licentious（放荡的）}
  \wordexample{be licentious（放荡的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{leisure}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LIC；le3a(r)}
  \wordfamily{elicit（sth + from + sb 引出）；illicit（非法的）；licit（合法的）}
  \wordsense{n. 空闲，闲暇；悠闲自在}

  \wordsense{a. 空闲的；悠闲的 *}
  \wordphrase{life of leisure（悠闲的生 活）}
  \wordphrase{leisure wear（休闲服）}
  \wordphrase{leisure hours / industries（闲暇时间 / 娱乐业）}
  \wordexample{life of leisure（悠闲的生 活）}
  \wordexample{leisure wear（休闲服）}
  \wordexample{leisure hours / industries（闲暇时间 / 娱乐业）}

\begin{word}[***]{leisurely}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LIC}
  \wordfamily{elicit（sth + from + sb 引出）；illicit（非法的）；licit（合法的）}

  \wordphrase{work leisurely（不慌不忙地工作）}

\chapter{RUD}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根RUD 来自拉丁语的rud意为crude。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{erudite}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{摆脱未成熟的、粗野的状态的e(ex（=out）+rud（=crude)；rerudait}
  \wordfamily{erudition（博学）；eruditely（博学地）；rude（（人或言行）无礼的）}
  \wordsense{a. 博学的；有学识的}
  \wordphrase{be erudite}
  \wordexample{Though far from erudite, the instructor at least has a pleasant personality.（虽然那位讲师远谈不上博学，但 至少性格很好。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{rude}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 RUD}
  \wordfamily{erudite（博学的）；rudiment（（pl.）基础）；rudimentary（基本的）}
  \wordsense{a. （人或言行）无礼的；粗鲁的；粗暴的；猛烈的；未开化的，野蛮的；粗糙的，不精 致的；天然的，未加工的}
  \wordphrase{rudely fashioned craft（样式极其简陋的 船）}
  \wordphrase{rude reply（无礼的回答）}
  \wordphrase{rude ore / produce（原矿/天然产品）}
  \wordexample{rudely fashioned craft（样式极其简陋的 船）}
  \wordexample{rude reply（无礼的回答）}
  \wordexample{rude ore / produce（原矿/天然产品）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{rudiment}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 RUD；'ru:dimant}
  \wordfamily{erudite（博学的）；rude（（人或言行）无礼的）；rudimentary（基本的）}
  \wordsense{n. （pl.）基础，基本原理；初步；入门；萌芽}
  \wordphrase{rudiments of grammar（语法入门）}
  \wordexample{rudiments of grammar（语法入门）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{rudimentary}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 RUD；.ru:dt'mentri}
  \wordfamily{erudite（博学的）；rude（（人或言行）无礼的）；rudiment（（pl.）基础）}
  \wordsense{a. 基本的，初步的；未发展完全的}
  \wordphrase{rudimentary knowledge of mechanics（力学的基本知识）}
  \wordphrase{vac，void和来自盎格鲁-撒克逊语的wan。}
  \wordexample{rudimentary knowledge of mechanics（力学的基本知识）}
\end{word}



\chapter{VAN[VAIN]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根 VAN[VAIN] 来自拉丁语的van意为empty，它的变形为vain。同义词根有来自拉丁语的
  \item vac，void和来自盎格鲁-撒克逊语的wan。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{evanescent}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{完全空着的e(ex（=out,intensive）+van（=empty）+esc（=becoming)；riva'nesnt}
  \wordfamily{evanescence（逐渐消失）；vanish（消失）；vanity（自负）}
  \wordsense{a. 逐渐消失的；短暂的}
  \wordphrase{be evanescent}
  \wordexample{She's a shy girl with an evanescent presence. You'll hardly notice her.（她是个不惹人注意的羞涩的女孩。你几乎都 察觉不到她的存在。）}
  \wordexample{She's a shy girl with an evanescent}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{vanish}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VAN[VAIN]；'væniʃ}
  \wordfamily{evanescent（逐渐消失的）；vanity（自负）；vain（无益的）}
  \wordsense{v. 消失；绝迹；突然不见}
  \wordphrase{vanish into thin air（不知去向，消失得无影无踪）}
  \wordexample{vanish into thin air（不知去向，消失得无影无踪）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{vanity}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VAN[VAIN]；'vænəti}
  \wordfamily{evanescent（逐渐消失的）；vanish（消失）；vain（无益的）}
  \wordsense{n. 自负，自满；虚荣（心）；无价值；空虚，无意义}
  \wordphrase{tickle her vanity（满足她的虚荣心）}
  \wordexample{tickle her vanity（满足她的虚荣心）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{vain}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VAN[VAIN]}
  \wordfamily{in（vain（无益地，徒劳地）；evanescent（逐渐消失的）；vanish（消失）}
  \wordsense{a. 无益的，无用的，徒劳的；无价值的；自负的，自大的 *vain<van (= empty)}
  \wordphrase{be vain（无益的，无用的，徒劳的）}
  \wordexample{be vain（无益的，无用的，徒劳的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{vainglory}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VAN[VAIN]；ven'glo:ri}
  \wordfamily{vainglorious；evanescent（逐渐消失的）；vanish（消失）}
  \wordsense{n. 自满，自负；虚荣 *vain<van (= empty)}
  \wordphrase{vainglorious}
  \wordexample{vainglorious（自大的，自满的；虚荣的）}
\end{word}



\chapter{HILAR}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根HILAR 来自希腊语的 hilar 意为 merry。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{exhilarate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 HILAR；使非常明朗 ex（= out, intensive）+ hilar（= merry）；ig'zilareit}
  \wordfamily{exhilaration（振奋，愉快）；hilarious（欢闹的）；hilarity（欢闹）}
  \wordsense{vt. 使振奋，使兴奋；使愉快}
  \wordphrase{exhilarate the issue}
  \wordexample{Carnival rides exhilarate the children, but they just make me sick!（嘉年华的游艺木马让孩子们兴奋不已，但却让我感到难受！）}
  \wordexample{exhilarate（vt.使振奋，使兴奋）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{hilarious}{hi'learias}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 HILAR}
  \wordsense{a.欢闹的，热闹的}
  \wordfamily{exhilarate}
  \wordfamily{hilarity}
  \wordfamily{be hilarious（欢闹的，热闹的）}
  \wordphrase{be hilarious（欢闹的，热闹的）}
  \wordexample{be hilarious（欢闹的，热闹的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{hilarity}{hi'lærati}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 HILAR}
  \wordsense{n.欢闹，兴高采烈}
  \wordfamily{hilarious}
  \wordfamily{exhilarate}
  \wordfamily{a hilarity（欢闹，兴高采烈）}
  \wordphrase{a hilarity（欢闹，兴高采烈）}
  \wordexample{a hilarity（欢闹，兴高采烈）}
\end{word}



\chapter{HORT}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根HORT 来自拉丁语的 hort 意为 to urge。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{exhort}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 HORT；强烈地主张、催促 ex（= out, intensive）+ hort（= urge）；ig'zɔ:t}
  \wordfamily{exhortation（劝说，劝告）；hortative（劝告的）；hilarity（欢闹）}
  \wordsense{vt. sb + to do 力劝，劝说；告诫}
  \wordphrase{exhort the issue}
  \wordexample{My father exhorted me to go to college, but unfortunately I ignored him.（我爸爸力劝我去念大学，但可惜的是 我没有听他的话。）}
  \wordexample{exhort（vt. sb + to do 力劝，劝说）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{hortative}{'hɔ:tətiv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 HORT}
  \wordsense{a.劝告的，忠告的；鼓舞的，激励的}
  \wordfamily{exhort}
  \wordfamily{be hortative（劝告的，忠告的）}
  \wordphrase{be hortative（劝告的，忠告的）}
  \wordexample{be hortative（劝告的，忠告的）}
\end{word}



\chapter{PI}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根PI 来自拉丁语的 pi 意为 holy。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 secr，sanct 和来自希腊语的 hagi，hier。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{expiate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PI；使变得非常神圣 ex（= out, intensive）+ pi（= holy）；'ekspieit}
  \wordfamily{expiation（赎罪）；expiatory（赎罪的）；pious（虔诚的）}
  \wordsense{vt. 赎罪；补偿过失}
  \wordphrase{expiate the issue}
  \wordexample{expiatory 赎罪的；补偿的 ?His guilt is so strong he feels he can never expiate his evil deeds.（他犯下的罪行太重了，以至于他 自己都觉得无法为自己的罪行赎罪。）}
  \wordexample{His guilt is so strong}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{pious}{'paiəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PI}
  \wordsense{a.虔诚的，笃信的；可嘉的}
  \wordfamily{expiate}
  \wordfamily{piety}
  \wordfamily{impious}
  \wordfamily{impiety}
  \wordfamily{be pious（虔诚的，笃信的）}
  \wordphrase{be pious（虔诚的，笃信的）}
  \wordexample{be pious（虔诚的，笃信的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{piety}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PI}
  \wordsense{n.（宗教上的）虔诚；（对国家、君主、父母等的）忠诚，孝顺}
  \wordfamily{['paiəti]}
  \wordfamily{filial piety（孝心）}
  \wordfamily{a piety（虔诚）}
  \wordphrase{a piety（虔诚）}
  \wordexample{a piety（虔诚）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{impious}{'impiəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PI}
  \wordfamily{piety；impiety；pious；expiate}
  \wordsense{a. 不敬的；不虔诚的 *}
  \wordphrase{be impious（不敬的）}
  \wordexample{be impious（不敬的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{impiety}{im'paiəti}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PI}
  \wordfamily{impious；piety；pious；expiate}
  \wordsense{n. 不虔诚；对神不敬（的言行） *}
  \wordphrase{an impiety（不虔诚）}
  \wordexample{an impiety（不虔诚）}
\end{word}



\chapter{PURG}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根PURG 来自拉丁语的 purg 意为 clean。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 pur。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{expurgate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PURG；去除不纯部分使变成干净、纯洁的状态 ex（= out）+ purg（= clean, clear）；'ekspəgeit}
  \wordfamily{expurgation（删除）；purge（肃清（反对势力等））；purgation（净化）}
  \wordsense{vt. sth + from + sth 删除（书中的不当之处）}
  \wordphrase{expurgate the issue}
  \wordexample{Ultraconservatives often want to expurgate passages from great works of literature.（极端保守主义分子常常想删除伟大的文学作品 中的一些段落。）}
  \wordexample{expurgate（vt. sth + from + sth 删除（书中的不当之处））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{purge}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PURG}
  \wordfamily{expurgate；purgation；purgative；purgatory}
  \wordsense{v. 肃清（反对势力等），清除；净化（水、心灵等）；（用泻药）使通便}
  \wordphrase{purge the issue}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of purge.（此处展示 purge 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 肃清；净化；泻药}
  \wordphrase{the purge}
  \wordexample{political purges that followed the overthrow of the government（颠覆政权之后伴随的 政治肃清）}
  \wordexample{to purge（肃清（反对势力等），清除）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{purgation}{p3:'geiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PURG}
  \wordsense{n.净化；洗涤；洗罪}
  \wordfamily{purge}
  \wordfamily{purgative}
  \wordfamily{expurgate}
  \wordfamily{purgatory}
  \wordfamily{a purgation（净化）}
  \wordphrase{a purgation（净化）}
  \wordexample{a purgation（净化）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{purgative}{'p3:gətiv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PURG}
  \wordsense{a.通便的，催泻的 n.泻药}
  \wordfamily{purgation}
  \wordfamily{purgatory}
  \wordfamily{purge}
  \wordfamily{expurgate}
  \wordfamily{be purgative（通便的，催泻的}
  \wordfamily{n.泻药）}
  \wordphrase{be purgative（通便的，催泻的 n.泻药）}
  \wordexample{be purgative（通便的，催泻的 n.泻药）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{purgatory}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PURG；'p3:gətri}
  \wordfamily{purgatorial；expurgate（sth + from + sth 删除（书中的不）；purge（肃清（反对势力等））}
  \wordsense{n. 涤罪；炼狱；暂时的苦难}
  \wordphrase{purgatorial}
  \wordexample{purgatorial（涤罪的；炼狱的）}
\end{word}



\chapter{TRIC[TRIG]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根TRIC[TRIG] 来自拉丁语的 tric 意为 petty obstacle，它的变形为 trig。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{extricate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TRIC[TRIG]；使摆脱小障碍物 ex（= out）+ tric（= petty obstacle）；'ekstrikeit}
  \wordfamily{extrication（解救）；extricable（可救出的）；intricate（错综复杂的）}
  \wordsense{vt. sb / sth + from + sth（从危险、困境中）救出，解救}
  \wordphrase{We extricated our poor}
  \wordexample{We extricated our poor collie from the large fishing net on the beach.（我们在海边救出了我们那只可怜的被 困在大渔网里的柯利狗。）}
  \wordexample{We extricated our poor}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{intricate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TRIC[TRIG]；里面存在小障碍物的 in（= in）+ tric（= petty obstacle）；'intrikət}
  \wordfamily{intricacy（错综复杂）；intricately（复杂地）；extricate（sb / sth + from + sth（从危）}
  \wordsense{a. 错综复杂的；难以理解的}
  \wordphrase{be intricate}
  \wordexample{Circuit diagrams are quite intricate and not easy to understand.（电路图相当复杂，让人难以理解。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{intrigue}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TRIC[TRIG]；在里面放入小的障碍物 in（= in）+ trig<tric（= petty obstacle）；in'tri:g}
  \wordfamily{intrigant（阴谋者）；intriguing（激起兴趣的，引起好奇心的）；intriguingly（激起兴趣地，引起好奇心地）；extricate（sb / sth + from + sth（从危）}
  \wordsense{vt. 策划阴谋；激起…的兴趣}
  \wordphrase{intrigue the issue}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of intrigue.（此处展示 intrigue 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 阴谋；密谋；私通}
  \wordphrase{the intrigue}
  \wordexample{I'm intrigued by the possibility of opening a small coffee shop.（我对能否开一家小咖啡店产生了兴趣。）}
\end{word}



\chapter{BULL}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根BULL 来自拉丁语的 bull 意为 to boil, seal。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 ferv。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{ebullient}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 BULL；向外、向上沸腾的 e（ex = out, up）+ bull（= boil）；i'bʌliənt}
  \wordfamily{ebullience（/ ebullition 沸腾）；bulletin（公告）；bullet（子弹）}
  \wordsense{a. 沸腾的；热情洋溢的，奔放的}
  \wordphrase{be ebullient}
  \wordexample{ebullience / ebullition 沸腾；感情的奔放 ?She's very lively, with an ebullient personality.（她热情洋溢又很漂亮。）}
  \wordexample{She's very lively, with an ebullient personality.（她热情洋溢又很漂亮。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{bulletin}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 BULL；'bulətin}
  \wordfamily{ebullient；bullet；bully}
  \wordsense{n. 公告，告示，公报；（电视广播的）新闻快报；（学会等的）会报，学报}
  \wordphrase{bulletin board（布告栏，布告牌）}
  \wordexample{bulletin board（布告栏，布告牌）}
  \wordexample{a bulletin（公告，告示，公报）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{bullet}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 BULL；'bulit}
  \wordfamily{bulletproof（防弹的）；ebullient（沸腾的）；bulletin（公告）}
  \wordsense{n. 子弹}
  \wordphrase{bulletproof jacket / vest（防弹衣 / 背心）}
  \wordexample{bulletproof jacket / vest（防弹衣 / 背心）}
  \wordexample{a bullet（子弹）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{bully}{'buli}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 BULL}
  \wordsense{n.欺凌弱小者，恶霸 v.威吓，胁迫 a.《口》极好的}
  \wordfamily{bullet}
  \wordfamily{bulletin}
  \wordfamily{ebullient}
  \wordfamily{a bully（欺凌弱小者，恶霸}
  \wordfamily{v.威吓，胁迫}
  \wordfamily{a.《口》极好的）}
  \wordphrase{a bully（欺凌弱小者，恶霸 v.威吓，胁迫 a.《口》极好的）}
  \wordexample{a bully（欺凌弱小者，恶霸 v.威吓，胁迫 a.《口》极好的）}
\end{word}



\chapter{FIG}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根FIG 来自拉丁语的 fig 意为 to form。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{effigy}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FIG；拔出来做成某种样子 ef（ex = out）+ fig（= form）；'efidʒi}
  \wordfamily{transfigure；figment；figure；figured}
  \wordsense{n. （用木、石等雕刻的）像，模拟像}
  \wordphrase{effigy by protesters}
  \wordexample{The U. S. President is frequently burned in effigy by protesters.（示威者们经 常烧掉美国总统像。）}
  \wordexample{effigy by protesters}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{transfigure}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FIG；træns'figə(r)}
  \wordfamily{effigy（（用木、石等雕刻的）像）；figment（虚构的事）；figure（数字）}
  \wordsense{v. 使…变形；改变…的外表 *}
  \wordphrase{transfigure the issue}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of transfigure.（此处展示 transfigure 的常见用法。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{figment}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FIG}
  \wordfamily{transfigure；figure；effigy；figured}
  \wordsense{n. 虚构的事，臆造的事 [‘figmənt] figments of the imagination（凭空想出来的事）}
  \wordphrase{figments of the imagination（凭空想出来的事）}
  \wordphrase{a figment（虚构的事，臆造的事 [‘figmənt] figments of the imagination（凭空想出来的事））}
  \wordexample{a figment（虚构的事，臆造的事 [‘figmənt] figments of the imagination（凭空想出来的事））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{figure}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FIG}
  \wordfamily{figurehead（傀儡）；effigy（（用木、石等雕刻的）像）；transfigure（使…变形）}
  \wordsense{n. 数字；图形；图解；图表；雕像，塑像；身材，外形；（飞行或滑冰）花样；舞步；人物 ['figə(r)]}
  \wordphrase{double figures（两位数）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "double figures".（常见搭配：两位数。）}
  \wordsense{v. 想像；描绘；计算；估计；（\textasciitilde{} out）理解}
  \wordphrase{a woman of graceful figure（外形优雅的女人）}
  \wordphrase{great figures of the age（那个时代的伟大人物）}
  \wordphrase{cut a brilliant figure（崭露头角）}
  \wordexample{a woman of graceful figure（外形优雅的女人）}
  \wordexample{great figures of the age（那个时代的伟大人物）}
  \wordexample{cut a brilliant figure（崭露头角）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{figured}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FIG；figəd}
  \wordfamily{effigy（（用木、石等雕刻的）像）；transfigure（使…变形）；figment（虚构的事）}
  \wordsense{a. 有花纹（或图案）的；用图形表示的}
  \wordphrase{figured glass window（压花玻璃窗）}
  \wordphrase{figured silk（有花纹的丝织品）}
  \wordexample{figured glass window（压花玻璃窗）}
  \wordexample{figured silk（有花纹的丝织品）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{figurative}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FIG；‘figərətiv}
  \wordfamily{effigy（（用木、石等雕刻的）像）；transfigure（使…变形）；figment（虚构的事）}
  \wordsense{a. 比喻的，象征的；用修辞手法的；【画】形象的}
  \wordphrase{in the figurative sense（就比喻意义而言）}
  \wordphrase{the figurative arts（形象艺术）}
  \wordexample{in the figurative sense（就比喻意义而言）}
  \wordexample{the figurative arts（形象艺术）}
\end{word}



\chapter{MAN[MN, MAIN]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根 MAN[MN, MAIN] 来自拉丁语的 man 意为 to flow, to stay，它的变形为 mn，main。
  \item 当意为 to flow 时，同义词根有来自拉丁语的 flu，lix 和来自希腊语的 rrh。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{emanate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MAN[MN, MAIN]；向外流 e（ex = out）+ man（= flow, stay）；'eməneit}
  \wordfamily{remnant（剩余）；immanent（（性质）内在的）；Immanuel（以马内利（基督教《圣经》中先知 Isaiah 及）}
  \wordsense{vi. from + sth 散发（气味、光、声音、蒸汽、热等）；发源}
  \wordphrase{emanate from the general}
  \wordexample{The pain seems to emanate from the general location of my liver.（疼痛好像来自肝那个部位。）}
  \wordexample{emanate from the general}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{remnant}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MAN[MN, MAIN]；再一次、继续逗留 re（= again）+ mn<man（= stay）；'remnənt}
  \wordfamily{emanate；immanent；Immanuel；permanent}
  \wordsense{n. 剩余，残余；（削价出售的）零料，零碎布头；（pl.）遗迹}
  \wordphrase{remnants for just fifty}
  \wordexample{I bought this big bag of fabric remnants for just fifty cents.（我只用 50 美分就 买下了这一大包零碎布头。）}
  \wordexample{remnants for just fifty}
\end{word}

  \wordsense{a. （性质）内在的；固有的；（上帝）无所不在的 *}
  \wordphrase{be immanent（内在的）}

  \wordexample{permanent peace / tooth / address（永久的和平 / 恒齿 / 固定地址）}

\chapter{MEND}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根MEND 来自拉丁语的 mend 意为 fault。此外，还有意为 to order 的 mand，mend，请勿混淆。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{emend}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MEND；指出错误、缺点 e（ex = out）+ mend（= fault）；i'mend}
  \wordfamily{emendation（校正）；emendate（校正）；amend（修改）}
  \wordsense{vt. 校正（文件等）；修改}
  \wordphrase{emend the issue}
  \wordexample{emendate 校正 ?A good editor emends a text without substantially altering it.（优秀的编辑可以在不改动文本本质内容的基础 上对之进行校对。）}
  \wordexample{A good editor emends a text without substantially altering it.（优秀的编辑可以在不改动文本本质内容的基础}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{amend}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MEND}
  \wordfamily{emend；amendment；amends；mendacious}
  \wordsense{v. 修改，修订（规则、法案等）；改善，改进 *a<ab（= away） amendable（可修正的；能改善的）}
  \wordphrase{to amend（修改，修订（规则、法案等））}
  \wordexample{to amend（修改，修订（规则、法案等））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{amendment}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MEND}
  \wordfamily{amend；amends；emend；mendacious}
  \wordsense{n. （法案等的）修改，修订；修正案；改善 *a<ab（= away） the Eighteenth Amendment（美国宪法第十八条修正案（禁酒令））}
  \wordphrase{an amendment（修改，修订）}
  \wordexample{an amendment（修改，修订）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{amends}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MEND；ə'mendz}
  \wordfamily{emend（校正（文件等））；amend（修改）；amendment（（法案等的）修改）}
  \wordsense{n. 赔偿 *}
  \wordphrase{make amends for（为…赔偿）}
  \wordexample{make amends for（为…赔偿）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{mendacious}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MEND；men'deiʃəs}
  \wordfamily{emend（校正（文件等））；amend（修改）；amendment（（法案等的）修改）}
  \wordsense{a. 虚假的；说谎的}
  \wordphrase{be mendacious（虚假的）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "be mendacious".（常见搭配：虚假的。）}
  \wordsense{n. mendacity（虚假；谎言）}
  \wordphrase{mendacious newspaper reports（虚假的报纸报道）}
  \wordexample{mendacious newspaper reports（虚假的报纸报道）}
  \wordexample{be mendacious（虚假的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{mending}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MEND}
  \wordfamily{mendacious；amends；amendment；amend；emend（校正（文件等））}
  \wordsense{n. （衣物等的）修补；需修补的东西 a basketful of mending（一篮子待补的衣物） invisible mending（织补）}
  \wordphrase{a basketful of mending（一篮子待补的衣物）}
  \wordphrase{invisible mending（织补）}
  \wordphrase{a mending（修补）}
  \wordexample{a mending（修补）}
\end{word}



\chapter{NERV}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根NERV 来自拉丁语的 nerv 意为 nerve。同义词根有来自希腊语的 neur。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{enervate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 NERV；使去除体力、神经、勇气 e（ex = out）+ nerv（= nerve）；'enəveit}
  \wordfamily{enervation（失去活力）；enervated（无活力的）；unnerve（使失去信心）}
  \wordsense{vt. 使失去活力；使衰弱}
  \wordphrase{be enervate（无活力的）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "be enervate".（常见搭配：无活力的。）}
  \wordsense{a. 无活力的}
  \wordphrase{be enervate（无活力的）}
  \wordexample{The president's repeated scandals have enervated the Republican Party.（总统丑 闻不断，弄得共和党疲惫无力。）}
  \wordexample{enervate（vt.使失去活力）}
  \wordexample{be enervate（无活力的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{unnerve}{ʌn'n3:v}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 NERV}
  \wordfamily{enervate；nerve；nervous}
  \wordsense{v. 使失去信心；使恐惧；使焦躁 *}
  \wordphrase{to unnerve（使失去信心）}
  \wordexample{to unnerve（使失去信心）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{nerve}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 NERV；n3:v}
  \wordfamily{enervate（使失去活力）；unnerve（使失去信心）；nervous（神经的）}
  \wordsense{n. 神经；勇气；胆量；厚颜；（pl.）神经过敏}
  \wordphrase{the nerve}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of nerve.（此处展示 nerve 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. 鼓励；激励}
  \wordphrase{nerve cell / center（神经细胞 / 神经中枢；核心）}
  \wordphrase{get on one's nerves（使某人心烦，使某人不安）}
  \wordphrase{war of nerves（神经战）}
  \wordexample{What a nerve!（真是个厚颜无耻的家伙！）}
  \wordexample{nerve cell / center（神经细胞 / 神经中枢；核心）}
  \wordexample{get on one's nerves（使某人心烦，使某人不安）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{nervous}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 NERV；'n3:vəs}
  \wordfamily{enervate（使失去活力）；unnerve（使失去信心）；nerve（神经）}
  \wordsense{a. 神经的；神经兴奋的；紧张的}
  \wordphrase{nervous breakdown（精神崩溃）}
  \wordphrase{nervous system（神经系统）}
  \wordphrase{nervous disorder（神经错乱）}
  \wordexample{Don't be nervous.（别紧张。）}
  \wordexample{nervous breakdown（精神崩溃）}
  \wordexample{nervous system（神经系统）}
\end{word}



\chapter{FET}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根FET 来自拉丁语的 fet 意为 productive。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{effete}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FET；生产能力竭尽的、没有的 ef<ex（= out）+ fet（= productive）；i'fi:t}
  \wordfamily{fetus；fetal；nervous（神经的）}
  \wordsense{a. 缺乏活力的；衰弱的；无能力的}
  \wordphrase{effete lifestyles}
  \wordexample{The very rich are sometimes accused of having effete lifestyles.（巨富们有时 会被指责生活方式过于颓废。）}
  \wordexample{effete lifestyles}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{fetus}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FET}
  \wordsense{n.（妊娠超过 3 个月的）胎儿（妊娠不足 3 个月的胎儿称作 embryo）}
  \wordfamily{['fi:təs]}
