  \wordfamily{a fetus（胎儿（妊娠不足 3 个月的胎儿称作}
  \wordfamily{embryo））}
  \wordphrase{a fetus（胎儿（妊娠不足 3 个月的胎儿称作 embryo））}
  \wordexample{a fetus（胎儿（妊娠不足 3 个月的胎儿称作 embryo））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{fetal}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FET；'fi:təl}
  \wordfamily{effete（缺乏活力的）；fetus（（妊娠超过 3 个月的）胎儿（妊娠不足 3 个月）；nervous（神经的）}
  \wordsense{a. 胎儿的；胎儿般的}
  \wordphrase{fetal position（胎位）}
  \wordphrase{to dip, to plunge。}
  \wordexample{fetal position（胎位）}
\end{word}



\chapter{MERS, MERG}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根MERS, MERG 来自拉丁语的 merg, mers 意为 to dip, to plunge。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{immerse}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{向内浸、浸湿 im<in(= into) + mers(= dip, plunge)；'m3:s}
  \wordfamily{immersion（沉浸）；emerge（出现）；emergency（紧急情况）}
  \wordsense{vt. sb/sth + in + sth 浸入；使深陷于；使埋首于；给…施浸礼}
  \wordphrase{He immersed himself in}
  \wordexample{He immersed himself in his studies to the detraction of his social life.（他因埋头于学业而 忽视了社交生活。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{emerge}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MERS, MERG；i'm3d3}
  \wordfamily{emergence（显现，出现）；emergent；immerse（sb/sth + in + sth 浸入）}
  \wordsense{v. 出现；浮现；（事实、想法等）显现出来 *}
  \wordphrase{countries of Africa（非洲的新兴国家）}
  \wordphrase{emergent countries of Africa（非洲的新兴国家）}
  \wordexample{countries of Africa（非洲的新兴国家）}
  \wordexample{The moon emerges from behind the clouds. （月亮出现在云层后面。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{emergency}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MERS, MERG；im3:d3ansi}
  \wordfamily{immerse（sb/sth + in + sth 浸入）；emerge（出现）；submerge（把……浸于水中）}
  \wordsense{n. 紧急情况，突然事件 *}
  \wordphrase{emergency power（紧急备用动力）}
  \wordphrase{emergency exit / fund（紧急出口 / 应急资金）}
  \wordphrase{emergency call（紧急呼叫）}
  \wordexample{emergency power（紧急备用动力）}
  \wordexample{emergency exit / fund（紧急出口 / 应急资金）}
  \wordexample{emergency call（紧急呼叫）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{submerge}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MERS, MERG；sab'm3:d3}
  \wordfamily{submersible（能潜入水的）；immerse（sb/sth + in + sth 浸入）；emerge（出现）}
  \wordsense{v. 把……浸于水中；淹没；潜入水中 *}
  \wordphrase{to submerge（把……浸于水中）}
  \wordexample{to submerge（把……浸于水中）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{submerged}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MERS, MERG}
  \wordfamily{submerge；merge；emergency；emerge；immerse（sb/sth + in + sth 浸入）}
  \wordsense{a. 浸没的；浸在水中的；陷于贫困的；【海】水下的；水下运行的 *sub（= under） submerged rocks（暗礁） submerged speed（水下行驶速度） wreck that is submerged at high tide（涨潮时被淹没的遇难船只） submerged tenth（社会的最底层）}
  \wordphrase{submerged rocks（暗礁）}
  \wordphrase{submerged speed（水下行驶速度）}
  \wordphrase{wreck that is submerged at high tide（涨潮时被淹没的遇难船只）}
  \wordexample{wreck that is submerged at high tide（涨潮时被淹没的遇难船只）}
  \wordexample{be submerged（浸没的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{merge}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MERS, MERG；m3id3}
  \wordfamily{merger（（公司等的）合并）；immerse（sb/sth + in + sth 浸入）；emerge（出现）}
  \wordsense{v. （使）合并；（使）渐渐消失}
  \wordphrase{The small banks were}
  \wordexample{The small banks were merged into one large organization.（那家小银行被并入一家大机构。）}
  \wordexample{His fear was gradually merged into curiosity.（他的恐惧逐渐被好奇心所代替。）}
\end{word}



\chapter{CALCUL}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根CALCUL 来自拉丁语的 calcul 意为 to reckon。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 rat。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{incalculable}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{无法计算的 in(= not) + calcul(= reckon)；in'kælkjuləbl}
  \wordfamily{calculable；calculate；calculation；calculus}
  \wordsense{a. 无法计算的；（数目）巨大的；不可预测的}
  \wordphrase{be incalculable}
  \wordexample{Prime numbers of more than 100 digits are incalculable by today's supercomputers.（今天的超级计算机也无法计算出超过100位的素数。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{calculable}{'kælkjuləbl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CALCUL}
  \wordsense{a. 可计算的；可信赖的}
  \wordfamily{calculate}
  \wordfamily{calculation}
  \wordfamily{calculus}
  \wordfamily{be calculable（可计算的）}
  \wordphrase{be calculable（可计算的）}
  \wordexample{be calculable（可计算的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{calculate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CALCUL；'kaelkjuleit}
  \wordfamily{incalculable（无法计算的）；calculable（可计算的）；calculation（计算）}
  \wordsense{v. 计算；推算；打算}
  \wordphrase{to calculate（计算）}
  \wordexample{to calculate（计算）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{calculation}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CALCUL；'kælkju'leiʃn}
  \wordfamily{incalculable（无法计算的）；calculable（可计算的）；calculate（计算）}
  \wordsense{n. 计算；仔细的分析（或思考等）}
  \wordphrase{after much calculation（深思熟虑后）}
  \wordexample{after much calculation（深思熟虑后）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{calculus}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CALCUL；'kælkjuləs}
  \wordfamily{incalculable（无法计算的）；calculable（可计算的）；calculate（计算）}
  \wordsense{n. 计算法；微积分；结石}
  \wordphrase{differential / integral calculus（微分学 / 积分学）}
  \wordexample{differential / integral calculus（微分学 / 积分学）}
\end{word}



\chapter{GRAN[GRAIN]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根GRAN[GRAIN] 来自拉丁语的 gran 意为 seed，它的变形为 grain。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{ingrained}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{把种子播在里面的 in（= in）+ grain<gran（= seed）；in'greind}
  \wordfamily{ingrain（生染）；granary（谷仓）；granite（花岗岩）}
  \wordsense{a. （习惯等)根深蒂固的；（谎言等)彻头彻尾的}
  \wordphrase{纤维}
  \wordexample{Adults' racial prejudice has typically been ingrained since childhood. （成年人的种族偏见从孩童时期起就已经根深蒂固了。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{granary}{'graenari}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 GRAN[GRAIN]}
  \wordfamily{granite；granulate；ingrained（（习惯等)根深蒂固的）}
  \wordsense{n. 谷仓，粮仓；产粮区 *}
  \wordphrase{a granary（谷仓，粮仓）}
  \wordexample{a granary（谷仓，粮仓）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{granite}{'granit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 GRAN[GRAIN]}
  \wordsense{n.花岗岩，花岗石}
  \wordfamily{granary}
  \wordfamily{granulate}
  \wordfamily{a granite（花岗岩，花岗石）}
  \wordphrase{a granite（花岗岩，花岗石）}
  \wordexample{a granite（花岗岩，花岗石）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{granulate}{'graenjuleit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 GRAN[GRAIN]}
  \wordsense{v.使成粒状}
  \wordfamily{granite}
  \wordfamily{granary}
  \wordfamily{to granulate（使成粒状）}
  \wordphrase{to granulate（使成粒状）}
  \wordexample{to granulate（使成粒状）}
\end{word}



\chapter{IT[I]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根IT[I] 来自拉丁语的 it 意为 to go，它的变形为 i 等。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 ced/cess[ceed，ceas]、grad/gress[gred]、vad/vas 和来自希腊语的 bat[bet，bit]，以及来自盎格鲁-撒克逊语的 fare 等。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{initiative}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{向里去 in（= in, into）+ it（= go）；i'niʃətiv}
  \wordfamily{initial（初始的，开始）；initially（初始地）；initiate（开始）；接纳（新成员）；initiation（开始，着手）}
  \wordsense{n. 主动的行动；倡议；进取心，积极性；公民的立法提案权}
  \wordphrase{the initiative}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of initiative.（此处展示 initiative 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. ）用姓名的首字母签名}
  \wordphrase{initiative are likely}
  \wordexample{Employees with initiative are likely to be promoted after their first year.（积极 进取的职员在第一年之后可能得到提升。）}
  \wordexample{initiative are likely}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{ambient}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 IT[I]；走向四方的 amb<ambi（= around, both）+ i<it（= go）；'æmbiənt}
  \wordfamily{ambience（环境）；initiative（主动的行动）；circuitous（迂回（线路）的）}
  \wordsense{a. 环绕四周的；周围的}
  \wordphrase{ambient air has a}
  \wordexample{The ambient air has a scent of blooming jasmine. （周围的空气中有一股茉莉花香。）}
  \wordexample{ambient air has a}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{circuitous}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{绕着圈走的 circ（= around）+ it（= go）；sə'kju:itəs}
  \wordfamily{ambient；transient；initiative；transition}
  \wordsense{a. 迂回（线路）的，曲折的；绕行的 We took a circuitous route to the concert to avoid heavy traffic. （为避开交通 堵塞，我们绕道去听音乐会。）}
  \wordphrase{We took a circuitous route to the concert to avoid heavy traffic（为避开交通 堵塞，我们绕道去听音乐会。）}
  \wordphrase{be circuitous（迂回（线路）的，曲折的）}
  \wordexample{We took a circuitous route to the concert to avoid heavy traffic.（为避开交通 堵塞，我们绕道去听音乐会。）}
  \wordexample{be circuitous（迂回（线路）的，曲折的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{transient}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{越过去 trans（= over）+ it（= go）；'trænziənt}
  \wordfamily{circuitous；transition；ambient；reiterate；initiative（主动的行动）}
  \wordsense{a. 一时的，暂时的，短暂的}
  \wordphrase{be transient}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of transient.（此处展示 transient 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 临时旅客，过往旅客}
  \wordphrase{transient phenomena lasting just}
  \wordexample{So-called falling stars are transient phenomena, lasting just a few seconds. （所谓的流星只是瞬时现象，仅仅持续几秒钟。）}
  \wordexample{transient phenomena lasting just}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{transition}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{超过去 trans（= over）+ it（= go）；træn'ziʃn}
  \wordfamily{transient；reiterate；circuitous；perishable；initiative（主动的行动）}
  \wordsense{n. 过渡（期）；变化，变迁}
  \wordphrase{transition period}
  \wordexample{The old currency will be used concurrently with the new during a transition period.（过渡期间新旧货币将同时使用。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{reiterate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{再一次、重复地去 re（= again）+ it（= go）；ri:'itəreit}
  \wordfamily{reiteration（反复）；initiative（主动的行动）；ambient（环绕四周的）}
  \wordsense{v. 重复，反复；重申}
  \wordphrase{The company reiterated last}
  \wordexample{The company reiterated last week that it expects losses in the next quarter.（那家公司上周重申预计下个季度可能会亏损。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{perishable}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 IT[I]；新鲜味易于消失去 per（= away）+ ish<it（= go）；'periʃəbl}
  \wordfamily{perish（灭亡，死亡）；initiative（主动的行动）；ambient（环绕四周的）}
  \wordsense{a. （食物等）易坏的，易腐烂的；易毁灭的}
  \wordphrase{a perishable（易腐烂的东西）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "a perishable".（常见搭配：易腐烂的东西。）}
  \wordsense{n. （pl.）易腐烂的东西}
  \wordphrase{a perishable（易腐烂的东西）}
  \wordexample{Perishable items will be shipped in dry ice via overnight express.（易坏的物品将用干冰包 好用隔夜快递发送。）}
  \wordexample{perishable（a.（食物等）易坏的，易腐烂的）}
  \wordexample{a perishable（易腐烂的东西）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{seditious}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{分离去的 sed<se（= away）+ it（= go）；si'diʃəs}
  \wordfamily{sedition（煽动）；initiative（主动的行动）；ambient（环绕四周的）}
  \wordsense{a. 煽动性的；参与煽动的}
  \wordphrase{seditious aliens}
  \wordexample{The senator wants to deport the seditious aliens.（那位参议员想把煽动叛乱的外国人驱逐出境。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{ambit}{'aembit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 IT[I]}
  \wordfamily{ambition；perishable；ambitious；circuit；initiative（主动的行动）}
  \wordsense{n. （pl.）界限；范围 *}
  \wordphrase{an ambit（界限）}
  \wordexample{an ambit（界限）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{ambition}{æm'biʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 IT[I]}
  \wordfamily{ambit；ambitious；circuit；perishable；initiative（主动的行动）}
  \wordsense{n. 野心；雄心，志向，抱负 *}
  \wordphrase{an ambition（野心）}
  \wordexample{an ambition（野心）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{ambitious}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 IT[I]；æm'biʃəs}
  \wordfamily{ambition；circuit；ambit；coition（/ coitus）；initiative（主动的行动）}
  \wordsense{a. 野心勃勃的；有雄心壮志的 *}
  \wordphrase{ambitious plans（野心勃勃的计划）}
  \wordphrase{ambitious attempt（雄心勃勃的尝试）}
  \wordexample{ambitious plans（野心勃勃的计划）}
  \wordexample{ambitious attempt（雄心勃勃的尝试）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{circuit}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 IT[I]；'s3:kit}
  \wordfamily{initiative（主动的行动）；ambient（环绕四周的）；circuitous（迂回（线路）的）}
  \wordsense{n. 巡回，周游；巡回审判；电路，线路 *}
  \wordphrase{circuit court（巡回法庭）}
  \wordphrase{circuit diagram（电路图，线路图）}
  \wordexample{circuit court（巡回法庭）}
  \wordexample{circuit diagram（电路图，线路图）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{coition / coitus}{kəu'iʃn / 'kəuitəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 IT[I]}
  \wordfamily{circuit；exit；ambitious；iterate；initiative（主动的行动）}
  \wordsense{n. 交媾，性交 *}
  \wordphrase{a coition / coitus（交媾，性交）}
  \wordexample{a coition / coitus（交媾，性交）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{exit}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 IT[I]；'eksit}
  \wordfamily{coition（/ coitus）；iterate；circuit；itinerant；initiative（主动的行动）}
  \wordsense{n. （高速公路、剧场等的）出口；（演员的）退场 *}
  \wordphrase{exit permit（出国许可（证））}
  \wordexample{exit permit（出国许可（证））}
  \wordexample{an exit（出口）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{iterate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 IT[I]}
  \wordsense{v.反复；重述}
  \wordfamily{['itəreit]}
  \wordfamily{iteration（反复；重述）}
  \wordfamily{to iterate（反复）}
  \wordphrase{to iterate（反复）}
  \wordexample{to iterate（反复）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{itinerant}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 IT[I]；ai'tinərənt}
  \wordfamily{initiative（主动的行动）；ambient（环绕四周的）；circuitous（迂回（线路）的）}
  \wordsense{a. 巡回的，巡游的}
  \wordphrase{itinerant library（流动图书馆）}
  \wordphrase{itinerant musicians（巡回表演的音乐家）}
  \wordexample{itinerant library（流动图书馆）}
  \wordexample{itinerant musicians（巡回表演的音乐家）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{itinerary}{ai'tinərəri}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 IT[I]}
  \wordsense{n.旅程；路线；旅行计划}
  \wordfamily{itinerant}
  \wordfamily{transit}
  \wordfamily{iterate}
  \wordfamily{transitive}
  \wordfamily{an itinerary（旅程）}
  \wordphrase{an itinerary（旅程）}
  \wordexample{an itinerary（旅程）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{transit}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 IT[I]；'trænzit}
  \wordfamily{itinerary；transitive；itinerant；transitory；initiative（主动的行动）}
  \wordsense{n. 运送；经过；通行；通路；机场的中途转机候机处 *}
  \wordphrase{goods delayed in transit（延迟运送的货物）}
  \wordphrase{transit camp（临时难民营；（部队的）过 境宿营地）}
  \wordphrase{transit passenger（中转旅客（在中途靠港停留但不能出机场的旅客））}
  \wordexample{goods delayed in transit（延迟运送的货物）}
  \wordexample{transit camp（临时难民营；（部队的）过 境宿营地）}
  \wordexample{transit passenger（中转旅客（在中途靠港停留但不能出机场的旅客））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{transitive}{'trænsitiv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 IT[I]}
  \wordfamily{transit；transitory；itinerary；itinerant；initiative（主动的行动）}
  \wordsense{a. 【语】及物的（++ intransitive 不及物的） *}
  \wordphrase{be transitive（及物的（++ intransitive 不及物的））}
  \wordexample{be transitive（及物的（++ intransitive 不及物的））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{transitory}{'trænsitəri}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 IT[I]}
  \wordfamily{transitive；transit；itinerary；itinerant；initiative（主动的行动）}
  \wordsense{a. 短暂的，片刻的；无常的 *}
  \wordphrase{be transitory（短暂的，片刻的）}
  \wordexample{be transitory（短暂的，片刻的）}
\end{word}



\chapter{NOV}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根NOV 来自拉丁语的 nov 意为 new。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{innovation}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 NOV；翻新里边 in（= in）+ nov（= new）；ˌinə'veiʃn}
  \wordfamily{innovate（革新，创新）；innovator（改革者，创新者）；innovative（革新性的）}
  \wordsense{n. 革新，创新；新方法，新事物}
  \wordphrase{innovation contributed}
  \wordexample{Her innovation contributed to several new and useful teaching methods.（她 的革新方案有助于开发一些新颖的、有效的教学方法。）}
  \wordexample{innovation contributed}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{renovate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 NOV；再一次使变新 re（= again）+ nov（= new）；'renəuveit}
  \wordfamily{renovation（修理，修复）；renovator（更新者，革新者）；innovation（革新）}
  \wordsense{vt. 修整（旧的建筑物），修复；更新}
  \wordphrase{renovate the issue}
  \wordexample{The Johnsons are renovating their rec room. That explains the mess.（约翰逊一家 正在修整他们的娱乐室，所以才会这么乱。）}
  \wordexample{renovate（vt.修整（旧的建筑物），修复）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{novel}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 NOV}
  \wordsense{n.小说 a.新奇的，新颖的}
  \wordfamily{['nɒvl]}
  \wordfamily{novelist（小说家）}
  \wordfamily{novelette（短篇、中篇小说）}
  \wordfamily{popular novel（通俗小说）}
  \wordphrase{novel idea（新奇的点子）}
  \wordexample{novel idea（新奇的点子）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{novelty}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 NOV}
  \wordfamily{novel；novice；renovate；nouveau（riche）；innovation（革新）}
  \wordsense{n. 新鲜，新奇，新颖；新颖的事物，新奇的事物；（pl.）新产品；（新奇而廉价的）小商 品（如玩具、小装饰品等）}
  \wordphrase{a novelty（新鲜，新奇，新颖）}
  \wordexample{a novelty（新鲜，新奇，新颖）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{novice}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 NOV}
  \wordfamily{novelty；nouveau（riche）；novel；nova；innovation（革新）}
  \wordsense{n. 新手，生手，初学者；见习修道士（或修女） noviciate / novitiate（修道士（或修女）的见习期；见习修道士（或修女））}
  \wordphrase{a novice（新手，生手，初学者）}
  \wordexample{a novice（新手，生手，初学者）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{nouveau riche}{ˌnu:vəu'ri:ʃ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 NOV}
  \wordfamily{novice；nova；novelty；novel；innovation（革新）}
  \wordsense{n. 暴发户 *}
  \wordphrase{a nouveau riche（暴发户）}
  \wordexample{a nouveau riche（暴发户）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{nova}{'nəuvə}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 NOV}
  \wordsense{n.新星}
  \wordfamily{nouveau riche}
  \wordfamily{novice}
  \wordfamily{novelty}
  \wordfamily{novel}
  \wordfamily{a nova（新星）}
  \wordphrase{a nova（新星）}
  \wordexample{a nova（新星）}
\end{word}



\chapter{TIM[TEM]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根TIM[TEM] 来自拉丁语的 tim 意为 to fear，元音弱化后变成 tem。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 trem/trep 和来自希腊语的 phob。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{intimidate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TIM[TEM]；使陷入恐惧的状态 in（= in）+ tim（= fear）；in'timideit}
  \wordfamily{intimidation（威吓）；timid（胆怯的）；timorous（极胆小的）}
  \wordsense{vt. 使害怕；胁迫，威胁}
  \wordphrase{intimidate the issue}
  \wordexample{Some adults compel obedience from young children by intimidating them.（一些大人通过威胁孩子来迫使他们服从。）}
  \wordexample{Some adults compel obedience from young}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{timid}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TIM[TEM]；'timid}
  \wordfamily{intimidate（使害怕）；timorous（极胆小的）；temerity（鲁莽）}
  \wordsense{a. 胆怯的，胆小的；羞怯的}
  \wordphrase{as timid as a rabbit（胆小如鼠）}
  \wordexample{as timid as a rabbit（胆小如鼠）}
  \wordexample{be timid（胆怯的，胆小的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{timorous}{'timərəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TIM[TEM]}
  \wordsense{a.极胆小的，易受惊的，提心吊胆的}
  \wordfamily{timid}
  \wordfamily{temerity}
  \wordfamily{intimidate}
  \wordfamily{be timorous（极胆小的，易受惊的，提心吊胆的）}
  \wordphrase{be timorous（极胆小的，易受惊的，提心吊胆的）}
  \wordexample{be timorous（极胆小的，易受惊的，提心吊胆的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{temerity}{tə'merəti}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TIM[TEM]}
  \wordsense{n.鲁莽，冒失}
  \wordfamily{timorous}
  \wordfamily{timid}
  \wordfamily{intimidate}
  \wordfamily{a temerity（鲁莽，冒失）}
  \wordphrase{a temerity（鲁莽，冒失）}
  \wordexample{a temerity（鲁莽，冒失）}
\end{word}



\chapter{PART}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根PART 来自拉丁语的 part 意为 part。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{impartial}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PART；不分割某物的 im<in（= not）+ part（= part）；im'pɑ:ʃl}
  \wordfamily{impartiality（公正，不偏不倚）；impartially（公平地）；impart（sth + to + sb（把拥有的东西等）分给）}
  \wordsense{a. 公平的，不偏不倚的}
  \wordphrase{be impartial}
  \wordexample{Ideally a judge will be impartial in his administration of justice.（理想地说，法官应该公正执法。）}
  \wordexample{impartial（a.公平的，不偏不倚的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{impart}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{把某物分给、分配给某人 im<in（= to）+ part（= part）；im'pɑ:t}
  \wordfamily{impartial；repartee；partition；partial}
  \wordsense{vt. sth + to + sb（把拥有的东西等）分给；给予；传授；告知（秘密、消息等）}
  \wordphrase{impart wisdom}
  \wordexample{The best teachers impart wisdom as well as knowledge to their students.（最 优秀的老师不仅向学生传授知识，还传授智慧。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{repartee}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PART；作为酬答拿出来 re（= back）+ part（= part）；ˌrepɑ:'ti:}
  \wordfamily{impartial；partial；apartheid；apartment}
  \wordsense{n. 巧妙的应答；机智的反驳 After a few minutes of repartee, she suddenly began to cry.（在几分钟的巧 妙应答后，她突然哭了起来。）}
  \wordphrase{After a few minutes of repartee, she suddenly began to cry（在几分钟的巧 妙应答后，她突然哭了起来。）}
  \wordexample{After a few minutes of repartee, she suddenly began to cry.（在几分钟的巧 妙应答后，她突然哭了起来。）}
  \wordsense{n. 巧妙的应答）}
  \wordphrase{a repartee（巧妙的应答）}
  \wordexample{repartee（n.巧妙的应答）}
  \wordexample{a repartee（巧妙的应答）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{partition}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{分割 part（= part）；pa:'tifn}
  \wordfamily{partitive（区分的）；impartial（公平的）；impart（sth + to + sb（把拥有的东西等）分给）}
  \wordsense{n. 分割，划分，分开；隔离物；隔墙；隔板}
  \wordphrase{the partition}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of partition.（此处展示 partition 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 分割；隔开}
  \wordphrase{partition between our cubicles}
  \wordexample{The partition between our cubicles fell over and broke a vase.（我们寝室间的隔板倒了，碰碎了花瓶。）}
  \wordexample{partition between our cubicles}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{partial}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PART；'pɑ:ʃl}
  \wordfamily{partiality（偏袒）；partially（部分地）；impartial（公平的）}

  \wordphrase{partial list}

  \wordexample{partial list}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{apartheid}{ə'pɑ:theit}

\chapter{FLAT}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根FLAT 来自拉丁语的 flat 意为 to blow。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{inflate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{在里面吹 in（= in）+ flat（= blow）；in'fleit}
  \wordfamily{deflate；deflationary；deflation；stagflation}
  \wordsense{vt. 使（通货）膨胀，提高物价；使充气；使得意 inflation 膨胀；通货膨胀 inflatable 可充气的；可膨胀的 inflationary 通货}
  \wordphrase{膨胀的}
  \wordexample{The street vendors often inflate their prices for tourists.（路边小贩常 常向游人提高价格。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{deflate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FLAT；di:'fleit}
  \wordfamily{deflationary；deflation；stagflation；afflatus；inflate（使（通货）膨胀）}
  \wordsense{v. 紧缩（通货）；放出（车胎等的）气体，抽出空气；使某人泄气 *}
  \wordphrase{deflate a pompous politician（使傲慢的政治家骄气大挫）}
  \wordexample{deflate a pompous politician（使傲慢的政治家骄气大挫）}
  \wordexample{to deflate（紧缩（通货））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{deflationary}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FLAT}
  \wordfamily{deflate；deflation；stagflation；afflatus；inflate（使（通货）膨胀）}
  \wordsense{a. 通货紧缩的 *de（= down） deflationary measures applied by the Secretary of Treasury（由财政部长实施的通货 紧缩措施）}
  \wordphrase{deflationary measures applied by the Secretary of Treasury（由财政部长实施的通货 紧缩措施）}
  \wordphrase{be deflationary（通货紧缩的）}
  \wordexample{deflationary measures applied by the Secretary of Treasury（由财政部长实施的通货 紧缩措施）}
  \wordexample{be deflationary（通货紧缩的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{deflation}{di:'fleiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FLAT}
  \wordfamily{deflationary；stagflation；deflate；afflatus；inflate（使（通货）膨胀）}
  \wordsense{n. 通货紧缩；抽出空气 *}
  \wordphrase{a deflation（通货紧缩）}
  \wordexample{a deflation（通货紧缩）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{stagflation}{stæg'fleiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FLAT}
  \wordfamily{deflation；afflatus；deflationary；deflate；inflate（使（通货）膨胀）}
  \wordsense{n. （不景气状况下的）滞胀 *}
  \wordphrase{a stagflation（滞胀）}
  \wordexample{a stagflation（滞胀）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{afflatus}{ə'fleitəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FLAT}
  \wordfamily{stagflation；deflation；deflationary；deflate；inflate（使（通货）膨胀）}
  \wordsense{n. 灵感 *}
  \wordphrase{an afflatus（灵感）}
  \wordexample{an afflatus（灵感）}
\end{word}



\chapter{ORD, ORDIN[ORDAIN]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根ORD, ORDIN[ORDAIN] 来自拉丁语的 ord, ordin 意为 to order，变形为 ordain。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{insubordinate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ORD, ORDIN[ORDAIN]；下边不接受命令的 in（= not）+ sub（= under）+ ordin（= order）；ˌinsə'bɔ:dinət}
  \wordfamily{insubordination（不服从）；inordinate（过度的）；subordinate（（地位、阶级等）低的）}
  \wordsense{a. 不服从的；反抗的}
  \wordphrase{be insubordinate}
  \wordexample{There's no room for insubordinate behavior in the army.（军队绝不允许任何不服从的行为。）}
  \wordexample{insubordinate（a.不服从的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{inordinate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{不合乎秩序、道理、情理的 in（= not）+ ordin（= order）；in'ɔ:dinət}
  \wordfamily{inordinately（过度地，过分地）；insubordinate（不服从的）；subordinate（（地位、阶级等）低的）}
  \wordsense{a. 过度的，过分的；无节制的；非常的}
  \wordphrase{inordinate amount}
  \wordexample{Our three teenage boys consume an inordinate amount of food.（我那三个十来岁的儿子饭量大得离谱。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{subordinate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ORD, ORDIN[ORDAIN]；命令在下层被接受 sub（= under）+ ordin（= order）；sə'bɔ:dinət}
  \wordfamily{subordination（下级，从属）；subordinative（从属的）；insubordinate（不服从的）}
  \wordsense{a. （地位、阶级等）低的，下级的；从属的}
  \wordphrase{be subordinate（低的，下级的）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "be subordinate".（常见搭配：低的，下级的。）}
  \wordsense{n. 下级，下属，部下}
  \wordphrase{be subordinate（低的，下级的）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "be subordinate".（常见搭配：低的，下级的。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 把…列入下级；使从属}
  \wordphrase{be subordinate（低的，下级的）}
  \wordexample{If you speak to my subordinate, he can get the answer for you.（如果你跟我的下属讲，他会给你 一个答复。）}
  \wordexample{subordinate（a.（地位、阶级等）低的，下级的）}
  \wordexample{be subordinate（低的，下级的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{coordinate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ORD, ORDIN[ORDAIN]}
  \wordfamily{subordinate；coordination；coordinator；insubordinate}
  \wordsense{a. 同等重要的；同样质量的，同一级别（或类别）的；【语】并列的}
  \wordphrase{be coordinate}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of coordinate.（此处展示 coordinate 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 同等物，同等 者；（pl.）颜色、质料、样式协调的配套服装（或家具）}
  \wordphrase{the coordinate}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of coordinate.（此处展示 coordinate 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. 调整，协调；使相配，使同等 *}
  \wordphrase{coordinate clause / conjunction（并列分句 / 并列连词）}
  \wordphrase{coordinate ideas（调整意见）}
  \wordexample{coordinate the movements of the arms and legs（协调胳膊和腿的动作）}
  \wordexample{coordinate clause / conjunction（并列分句 / 并列连词）}
  \wordexample{coordinate ideas（调整意见）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{coordination}{kəuˌɔ:di'neiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ORD, ORDIN[ORDAIN]}
  \wordfamily{coordinate；coordinator；subordinate；extraordinary；insubordinate（不服从的）}
  \wordsense{n. 协同，协调 *}
  \wordphrase{a coordination（协同，协调）}
  \wordexample{a coordination（协同，协调）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{coordinator}{kəu'ɔ:dineitə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ORD, ORDIN[ORDAIN]}

  \wordphrase{a man of extraordinary talents（拥有非凡才能的 人）}
  \wordphrase{extraordinary weather（反常的天气）}
  \wordexample{a man of extraordinary talents（拥有非凡才能的 人）}
  \wordexample{extraordinary weather（反常的天气）}

\begin{word}[***]{ordinary}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ORD, ORDIN[ORDAIN]}

  \wordfamily{['ɔ:dənri]}

  \wordphrase{nothing out of the ordinary（没有什么特别的东西）}

\begin{word}[***]{ordain}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ORD, ORDIN[ORDAIN]；ɔ:'dein}
  \wordfamily{preordain；insubordinate（不服从的）；inordinate（过度的）}
  \wordsense{v. 授予圣职；（用法律、法令等）规定，决定；命令；（命运）注定 *}
  \wordphrase{preordain}
  \wordexample{preordain（预先注定；预先规定） ?God has ordained that all men shall die.（上帝注 定了所有人都必然死亡。）}
  \wordexample{God has ordained that all men shall die.（上帝注 定了所有人都必然死亡。）}

\begin{word}[***]{disorder}{dis'ɔ:də(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ORD, ORDIN[ORDAIN]}
  \wordfamily{ordain；disorderly；ordinary；orderly；insubordinate（不服从的）}

  \wordphrase{the disorder}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of disorder.（此处展示 disorder 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. 扰乱，使混乱 *}
  \wordphrase{disorder the issue}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of disorder.（此处展示 disorder 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. 扰乱，使混乱）}
  \wordphrase{disorder the issue}

\begin{word}[**]{disorderly}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ORD, ORDIN[ORDAIN]；dis'ɔ:dəli}

\begin{word}[***]{orderly}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ORD, ORDIN[ORDAIN]}

  \wordphrase{orderly crowd（有秩序的人群）}

\chapter{PED[POD, PUS, PEACH, PATCH, FET]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根PED[POD, PUS, PEACH, PATCH, FET] 来自拉丁语的 ped 意为 foot，来自希腊语的 ped 意为 child。当
  \item ped 意为 foot 时，其变形为 pod，pus，peach，patch，fet 等。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{impediment}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PED[POD, PUS, PEACH, PATCH, FET]；把脚放在里面 im<in（= in）+ ped（= foot）；im'pedimənt}
  \wordfamily{impede（妨碍，阻碍）；expedite（促进）；expedient（方便的）}
  \wordsense{n. 妨碍，障碍；（口吃等）语言障碍}
  \wordphrase{impediment to learning}
  \wordexample{Overcrowded, uncomfortable classrooms are an impediment to learning.（过于 拥挤的、不舒适的教室会妨碍学习。）}
  \wordexample{impediment to learning}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{expedite}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PED[POD, PUS, PEACH, PATCH, FET]；将脚向外迈 ex（= out）+ ped（= foot）；'ekspədait}
  \wordfamily{expeditionary（探险的，）；expeditiously（迅速地）；Please；expeditious（敏捷的，迅速的）；impediment（妨碍）}
  \wordsense{vt. 促进，加速（行动、进程等）；快速执行，迅速处理}
  \wordphrase{expedite the issue}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of expedite.（此处展示 expedite 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 远征的 expeditious 敏捷的，迅速的}
  \wordphrase{expedite the shipment}
  \wordexample{expedite the shipment of fruit, as it is perishable.（水果易于腐烂，所以请迅速 装运。）}
  \wordexample{Please expedite the shipment of fruit, as it is perishable.（水果易于腐烂，所以请迅速 装运。）}
  \wordexample{expedite the shipment}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{expedient}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PED[POD, PUS, PEACH, PATCH, FET]；将脚向外迈 ex（= out）+ ped（= foot）；ik'spi:diənt}
  \wordfamily{expedience（/ expediency 便利）；expediently（便利地）；impediment（妨碍）}
  \wordsense{a. 方便的，便利的；权宜之计的；得当的；可取的}
  \wordphrase{an expedient（临时的措施，权宜之计）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "an expedient".（常见搭配：临时的措施，权宜之计。）}
  \wordsense{n. 临时的措施，权宜之计；简便的方法}
  \wordphrase{expedient to apply}
  \wordexample{It is expedient to apply to numerous universities rather than just your favorite.（申请多个学校要比只申请你最喜欢的学校有利一些。）}
  \wordexample{expedient to apply}
  \wordexample{expedient（a.方便的，便利的）}
\end{word}

  \wordphrase{dispatch the issue}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of dispatch.（此处展示 dispatch 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 派遣；快递；急件，快信；迅速，急速；杀死}
  \wordphrase{dispatched a letter}
  \wordexample{The executive dispatched a letter to the Chicago field office by overnight express.（那位高级官员用隔夜快递向位于芝加哥的驻地机构发送了一封 信件。）}
  \wordexample{dispatched a letter}
  \wordexample{dispatch（vt.快递）}
\end{word}

\chapter{PUN, PEN[POEN]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根PUN, PEN[POEN] 来自拉丁语的 pun，pen 意为 to punish，pen 变形为 poen。同义词根为来
  \item 自拉丁语的 veng。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{impunity}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PUN, PEN[POEN]；不受惩罚 im<in（= not）+ pun（= punish）；im'pju:nəti}
  \wordfamily{penal；impenitent；penance；penitence}
  \wordsense{n. 免除（惩罚、损害等）}
  \wordphrase{impunity in many third}
  \wordexample{Citizens bribe public officials with impunity in many third world nations.（在许 多第三世界国家，公民向公务员行贿可以不受惩罚。）}
  \wordexample{impunity in many third}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{penal}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PUN, PEN[POEN]；是惩罚、刑罚的 pen（= punishment）；'pi:nl}
  \wordfamily{penalize（对…处罚）；penalty（处罚，刑罚）；penally（用刑罚）；impunity（免除（惩罚、损害等））}
  \wordsense{a. 刑罚的，刑事的}
  \wordphrase{penal reform}
  \wordexample{The warden has been advocating penal reform for many years.（那个监狱长很多年以来一直在提倡刑罚改革。）}
  \wordexample{penal reform}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{impenitent}{im'penitənt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PUN, PEN[POEN]}
  \wordfamily{penal；penance；impunity；penitence}
  \wordsense{a. 不知悔改的 *}
  \wordphrase{be impenitent（不知悔改的）}
  \wordexample{be impenitent（不知悔改的）}
\end{word}

  \wordfamily{impenitent}
  \wordfamily{penitence}
  \wordfamily{penal}
  \wordfamily{penitent}
  \wordfamily{a penance（赎罪，苦修）}
  \wordphrase{a penance（赎罪，苦修）}
  \wordexample{a penance（赎罪，苦修）}
\end{word}

  \wordfamily{penance}
  \wordfamily{penitent}
  \wordfamily{impenitent}
  \wordfamily{penitential}
  \wordfamily{a penitence（后悔，悔过）}
  \wordphrase{a penitence（后悔，悔过）}
  \wordexample{a penitence（后悔，悔过）}
\end{word}

  \wordbreakdown{词根 PUN, PEN[POEN]}
  \wordsense{a.悔罪的；后悔的}
  \wordfamily{penitence}
  \wordfamily{penitential}
  \wordfamily{penance}
  \wordfamily{penitentiary}
  \wordfamily{be penitent（悔罪的）}
  \wordphrase{be penitent（悔罪的）}
  \wordexample{be penitent（悔罪的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{penitential}{peni'tenʃl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PUN, PEN[POEN]}

  \wordfamily{penology}

\end{word}

  \wordphrase{punitive justice（因果报应）}
  \wordexample{punitive expedition（讨伐）}
  \wordexample{punitive justice（因果报应）}

  \wordbreakdown{词根 PUN, PEN[POEN]}

  \wordsense{v. 后悔，悔改 *}

  \wordbreakdown{词根 PUN, PEN[POEN]}

\begin{word}[**]{repentant}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PUN, PEN[POEN]；ri'pentənt}

  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of subpoena.（此处展示 subpoena 的常见用法。）}

\chapter{CRIMIN}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根CRIMIN 来自拉丁语的 crimin 意为 crime，guilty，to separate。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{indiscriminate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CRIMIN；不各自分开的 in（= not）+ dis（= apart）+ crimin（= separate, guilty, crime）；,indi'skriminət}
  \wordfamily{indiscrimination（无差别，无分别）；indiscriminately（不加区别地）；incriminate（暗示（或显示）…有罪）}
  \wordsense{a. 无差别的；杂乱的；不分青红皂白的}
  \wordphrase{be indiscriminate}
  \wordexample{The indiscriminate use of corporal punishment creates angry, resentful students. （不分青红皂白地使用体罚只会让学生充满愤怒和怨恨。）}
  \wordexample{indiscriminate（a.无差别的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{incriminate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CRIMIN；使处于有罪状态 in（= in）+ crimin（= guilty, fault, separate）；in'krimineit}
  \wordfamily{DNA（证明他有罪）；indiscriminate（无差别的）；recrimination（反责）}
  \wordsense{vt. 暗示（或显示）…有罪；归罪于；控告（某人有罪）}
  \wordphrase{The presence of his}
  \wordexample{The presence of his DNA at the crime scene incriminated him.（在犯罪现场 发现的他的}
  \wordexample{The presence of his}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{recrimination}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CRIMIN；再一次作为酬答说有罪 re（= again, back）+ crimin（= crime）；ri,krimi'neiʃn}
  \wordfamily{recriminate（反责）；recriminatory（反责的）；indiscriminate（无差别的）}
  \wordsense{n. 反责；反诉}
  \wordphrase{recrimination}
  \wordexample{The two sides engaged in endless rounds of mutual recrimination.（双方陷入无止境的相互反 诉中。）}
  \wordexample{recrimination}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{discriminate}{di'skrimineit}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CRIMIN}
  \wordfamily{recrimination；discriminating；incriminate；discrimination；indiscriminate（无差别的）}
  \wordsense{v. 区别，辨别；有差别地对待；歧视 *}
  \wordphrase{to discriminate（区别，辨别）}
  \wordexample{to discriminate（区别，辨别）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{discriminating}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CRIMIN；di'skrimineitiŋ}
  \wordfamily{indiscriminate（无差别的）；incriminate（暗示（或显示）…有罪）；recrimination（反责）}
  \wordsense{a. 有识别力的；形成差别的；区别对待的 *}
  \wordphrase{discriminating taste in literature（文学鉴赏力）}
  \wordphrase{discriminating tariffs（差别关税）}
  \wordexample{discriminating taste in literature（文学鉴赏力）}
  \wordexample{discriminating tariffs（差别关税）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{discrimination}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CRIMIN；di,skrimi'neiʃn}
  \wordfamily{indiscriminate（无差别的）；incriminate（暗示（或显示）…有罪）；recrimination（反责）}
  \wordsense{n. 区别；识别（力），辨别（力）；歧视；差别待遇 *}
  \wordphrase{a discrimination（区别）}
  \wordexample{a discrimination（区别）}
  \wordexample{discrimination（[di,skrimi'neiʃn]）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{criminal}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CRIMIN；'kriminl}
  \wordfamily{criminology（犯罪学）；indiscriminate（无差别的）；incriminate（暗示（或显示）…有罪）}
  \wordsense{n. 罪犯}
  \wordphrase{habitual criminal（惯犯）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "habitual criminal".（常见搭配：惯犯。）}
  \wordsense{a. 犯罪的，犯法的；刑事的}
  \wordphrase{criminal act（犯罪行为）}
  \wordphrase{criminal psychology（犯罪心理学）}
  \wordphrase{criminal charge（刑事控告）}
  \wordexample{criminal act（犯罪行为）}
  \wordexample{criminal psychology（犯罪心理学）}
  \wordexample{criminal charge（刑事控告）}
\end{word}



\chapter{STILL}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根STILL 来自拉丁语的 still 意为 to drop。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{instill}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 STILL；滴答滴答掉到里边 in（= in, into）+ still（= drop）；in'stil}
  \wordfamily{distill（蒸馏）；distillation（蒸馏）；distiller（蒸馏者）}
  \wordsense{vt. sth + in / into + sb 慢慢地灌输（思想等）；滴注}
  \wordphrase{Honest, hardworking parents tend to instill}
  \wordexample{similar values in their children.（正直、苦干的父母往往倾向于子女灌输相似的价 值观。）}
  \wordexample{Honest, hardworking parents tend to instill similar values in their children.（正直、苦干的父母往往倾向于子女灌输相似的价 值观。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{distill}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 STILL；di'stil}
  \wordfamily{instill（sth + in / into + sb 慢慢地）；distillation（蒸馏）；distiller（蒸馏者）}
  \wordsense{v. 蒸馏；用蒸馏法制作（威士忌等），用蒸馏法净化；（使）滴下 *}
  \wordphrase{distilled liquor / water（蒸馏酒 / 蒸馏水）}
  \wordexample{distilled liquor / water（蒸馏酒 / 蒸馏水）}
  \wordexample{to distill（蒸馏）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{distillation}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 STILL；,disti'leiʃn}
  \wordfamily{distill；distiller；instill；distillery}
  \wordsense{n. 蒸馏；蒸馏法；蒸馏物 *}
  \wordphrase{the distillation of malted barley（（制作威士忌时的）麦芽蒸馏）}
  \wordexample{the distillation of malted barley（（制作威士忌时的）麦芽蒸馏）}
  \wordexample{a distillation（蒸馏）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{distiller}{di'stilə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 STILL}
  \wordfamily{distillation；distillery；distill；instill}
  \wordsense{n. 蒸馏者；蒸馏器 *}
  \wordphrase{a distiller（蒸馏者）}
  \wordexample{a distiller（蒸馏者）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{distillery}{di'stiləri}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 STILL}
  \wordfamily{distiller；distillation；distill；instill}
  \wordsense{n. 蒸馏间；酿酒厂 *dis（= away）,}
  \wordphrase{a distillery（蒸馏间）}
  \wordexample{a distillery（蒸馏间）}
\end{word}



\chapter{MORT,MORS,MOR}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根MORT,MORS,MOR 来自拉丁语的 mort，mors，mor 意为 death。同义词根有来自希腊
  \item 语的 leth，necr，thanat / than 等。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{immortal}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MORT,MORS,MOR；不死 im<in（= not）+ mort（= death）；i'mɔ:tl}
  \wordfamily{mortify；mortal；mortality；mortgage}
  \wordsense{a. 不死的；永生的；不朽的}
  \wordphrase{be immortal}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of immortal.（此处展示 immortal 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 不朽的人物}
  \wordphrase{immortal life}
  \wordexample{The Christian religion claims that the soul enjoys immortal life.（基督教主张 灵魂不死。）}
  \wordexample{immortal life}
  \wordexample{immortal（a.不死的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{mortify}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MORT,MORS,MOR；使达到死亡 mort（= death）；'mɔ:tifai}
  \wordfamily{mortification（屈辱感）；mortified（me）；immortal（不死的）}
  \wordsense{vt. 使蒙受屈辱，使失面子；抑制（情欲等）}
  \wordphrase{mortified me}
  \wordexample{It mortified me to be dressed down by the boss in front of my colleagues.（在同事面前被老板训斥让我感到羞辱难当。）}
  \wordexample{mortified me}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{mortal}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MORT,MORS,MOR；应该死 mort（= death）；'mɔ:tl}
  \wordfamily{mortally（致命地）；immortal（不死的）；mortify（使蒙受屈辱）}
  \wordsense{a. 必死的；（伤害、疾病等）致命的；凡人的；不共戴天的；临终的；极度的}
  \wordphrase{be mortal}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of mortal.（此处展示 mortal 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 人；凡人}
  \wordphrase{the mortal}
  \wordexample{Most Christians believe death is a release from mortal cares.（大多数基督教徒相信，死亡是从凡人尘世的烦恼中的解脱。）}
  \wordexample{mortal（a.必死的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{mortality}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MORT,MORS,MOR；mɔ:'tæləti}
  \wordfamily{mortal；mortgage；mortify；mortician；immortal（不死的）}
  \wordsense{n. 必死性；死亡人数；死亡率}
  \wordphrase{epidemic with a heavy mortality（死亡率很高的传染病）}
  \wordphrase{mortality tables（（保险的）死 亡率统计表）}
  \wordexample{epidemic with a heavy mortality（死亡率很高的传染病）}
  \wordexample{mortality tables（（保险的）死 亡率统计表）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{mortgage}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MORT,MORS,MOR；'mɔ:gidʒ}
  \wordfamily{mortgagee（受抵人）；immortal（不死的）；mortify（使蒙受屈辱）}
  \wordsense{n. 抵押（行为）；抵押权；抵押契据}
  \wordphrase{mortgager / mortgagor（抵押人，押出人）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "mortgager / mortgagor".（常见搭配：抵押人，押出人。）}
  \wordsense{v. 抵押 *}
  \wordphrase{mortgage bond}
  \wordphrase{pay off the mortgage（还清抵押）}
  \wordphrase{land that may be mortgaged（可 以抵押的土地）}
  \wordexample{mortgage my house to Citi Bank of \$100,000（以 10 万美元将我的房子抵押给花旗银行）}
  \wordexample{pay off the mortgage（还清抵押）}
  \wordexample{land that may be mortgaged（可 以抵押的土地）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{mortician}{mɔ:'tiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MORT,MORS,MOR}
  \wordfamily{mortgage；mortuary；mortality；post-mortem；immortal（不死的）}
  \wordsense{n. 殡仪业者 *}
  \wordphrase{a mortician（殡仪业者）}
  \wordexample{a mortician（殡仪业者）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{mortuary}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MORT,MORS,MOR；'mɔ:tʃəri}
  \wordfamily{mortician；post-mortem；mortgage；amortize；immortal（不死的）}
  \wordsense{n. 停尸房，太平间}
  \wordphrase{the mortuary}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of mortuary.（此处展示 mortuary 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{a. 死亡的；丧葬的 *}
  \wordphrase{mortuary rites（葬礼）}
  \wordphrase{mortuary monument（墓碑）}
  \wordexample{mortuary rites（葬礼）}
  \wordexample{mortuary monument（墓碑）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{post-mortem}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MORT,MORS,MOR；,pəust'mɔ:təm}
  \wordfamily{mortuary；amortize；mortician；morgue；immortal（不死的）}
  \wordsense{a. 死后的}
  \wordphrase{be post-mortem}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of post-mortem.（此处展示 post-mortem 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 尸体解剖，验尸；事后剖析 *}
  \wordphrase{post-mortem examination（尸体解剖）}
  \wordexample{post-mortem examination（尸体解剖）}
  \wordexample{A post-mortem showed that King Ivan had been poisoned.（验尸结果表明，伊万国王是被毒死的。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{amortize}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MORT,MORS,MOR；ə'mɔ:taiz}
  \wordfamily{immortal（不死的）；mortify（使蒙受屈辱）；mortal（必死的）}
  \wordsense{v. 用偿债基金（sinking fund）分期偿还（债务） *}
  \wordphrase{to amortize（用偿债基金（sinking fund）分期偿还（债务））}
  \wordexample{to amortize（用偿债基金（sinking fund）分期偿还（债务））}
  \wordexample{amortize（[ə'mɔ:taiz]）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{morgue}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MORT,MORS,MOR}
  \wordfamily{amortize；moribund；post-mortem；mortuary；immortal（不死的）}
  \wordsense{n. 停尸房（保存人的尸体的场所，直到身份被确认并被认领）；（报纸、电影制片厂等 的）资料库}
  \wordphrase{a morgue（停尸房（保存人的尸体的场所，直到身份被确认并被认领））}
  \wordexample{a morgue（停尸房（保存人的尸体的场所，直到身份被确认并被认领））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{moribund}{'mɔribʌnd}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MORT,MORS,MOR}
  \wordfamily{morgue；amortize；post-mortem；mortuary；immortal（不死的）}
  \wordsense{a. 垂死的，濒临死亡的，即将灭亡的 *}
  \wordphrase{be moribund（垂死的，濒临死亡的，即将灭亡的）}
  \wordexample{be moribund（垂死的，濒临死亡的，即将灭亡的）}
\end{word}



\chapter{POVER[PAUPER]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根POVER[PAUPER] 来自拉丁语的 pover 意为 poor，它的变形为 pauper。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{impoverish}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POVER[PAUPER]；造成贫穷的状态 im<in（= in）+ pover（= poor）；im'pɔvəriʃ}
  \wordfamily{impoverishment（贫穷）；impoverished（贫乏的）；poverty（贫穷）}
  \wordsense{vt. 使贫穷；使（资源、力量等）枯竭；使（土地）贫瘠}
  \wordphrase{impoverish the issue}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of impoverish.（此处展示 impoverish 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 的；无创造性的}
  \wordphrase{impoverished the population}
  \wordexample{The decade of drought impoverished the population of the Great Plains.（十年的干旱使大平原的人们陷入了贫困。）}
  \wordexample{impoverished the population}
  \wordexample{impoverish（vt.使贫穷）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{poverty}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POVER[PAUPER]；'pɔvəti}
  \wordfamily{poverty-stricken（为贫穷所困的，非常贫穷的）；impoverish（使贫穷）；pauper（赤贫者）}
  \wordsense{n. 贫穷，贫困；缺乏}
  \wordphrase{poverty-stricken homes（极度贫困的 家庭）}
  \wordphrase{poverty line（贫困线，最低生活保障标准）}
  \wordexample{poverty-stricken homes（极度贫困的 家庭）}
  \wordexample{poverty line（贫困线，最低生活保障标准）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{pauper}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 POVER[PAUPER]；'pɔ:pə(r)}
  \wordfamily{pauperism（贫穷，贫困）；impoverish（使贫穷）；poverty（贫穷）}
  \wordsense{n. 赤贫者，贫民；靠救济度日者；乞丐 *}
  \wordphrase{a pauper（赤贫者，贫民）}
  \wordexample{a pauper（赤贫者，贫民）}
  \wordexample{pauper（['pɔ:pə(r)]）}
\end{word}



\chapter{CINER}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根CINER 来自拉丁语的 ciner 意为 ashes。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{incinerate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CINER；使成灰的状态 in（= in, to）+ ciner（= ashes）；in'sinəreit}
  \wordfamily{incineration（焚化）；incinerator（焚化炉）；cinerarium（骨灰存放所 *）}
  \wordsense{vt. 把…烧成灰；焚化}
  \wordphrase{Certain kinds of plastics}
  \wordexample{Certain kinds of plastics should never be incinerated.（有些塑料不应该被焚化。）}
  \wordexample{Certain kinds of plastics should}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{cinerarium}{sinə'reəriəm}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CINER}
  \wordfamily{incinerate；pauper（赤贫者）；poverty（贫穷）}
  \wordsense{n. 骨灰存放所 *}
  \wordphrase{a cinerarium（骨灰存放所）}
  \wordexample{a cinerarium（骨灰存放所）}
\end{word}



\chapter{CIT}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根CIT 来自拉丁语的 cit 意为 to call，to arouse。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{incite}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CIT；在里面叫 in（= in, on）+ cit（= arouse, call）；in'sait}
  \wordfamily{excite；excitement；recite；recital}
  \wordsense{vt. sb + to do 刺激；煽动，鼓动}
  \wordphrase{The class president incited}
  \wordexample{The class president incited the students to protest the lunch price increase. （班长鼓动学生们抗议午餐涨价。）}
  \wordexample{The class president incited}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{excite}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CIT}
  \wordfamily{excitability（易激动性）；excited（兴奋的）；exciting（令人兴奋的）；excitable（易兴奋的）；excitedly（兴奋地）；incite（sb + to do 刺激）}
  \wordsense{v. 刺激，使兴奋，使激动；激起（感情等）；（通过刺激）促使（身体器官）活动 *}
  \wordphrase{to excite（刺激，使兴奋，使激动）}
  \wordexample{to excite（刺激，使兴奋，使激动）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{excitement}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CIT；ik'saitmənt}
  \wordfamily{incite（sb + to do 刺激）；excite（刺激）；recite（吟诵）}
  \wordsense{n. 兴奋；刺激物 *}
  \wordphrase{an excitement（兴奋）}
  \wordexample{an excitement（兴奋）}
  \wordexample{excitement（[ik'saitmənt]）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{recite}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CIT；ri'sait}
  \wordfamily{excitement；recital；excite；recitation；incite（sb + to do 刺激）}
  \wordsense{v. 吟诵，朗诵；详述；列举 *}
  \wordphrase{to recite（吟诵，朗诵）}
  \wordexample{recite to the president a long and tedious speech of welcome（为总统朗读一篇乏味 冗长的欢迎辞）}
  \wordexample{to recite（吟诵，朗诵）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{recital}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CIT；ri'saitl}
  \wordfamily{incite（sb + to do 刺激）；excite（刺激）；excitement（兴奋）}
  \wordsense{n. 背诵；朗诵；详述；独奏会，独唱会 *}
  \wordphrase{recital of songs（独唱会）}
  \wordexample{the long recital of his adventures（他讲述的冗长的探险故事）}
  \wordexample{recital of songs（独唱会）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{recitation}{,resi'teiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CIT}
  \wordsense{n.详述；列举；背诵，朗读；背诵的诗文；背书答问课}
  \wordfamily{recital}
  \wordfamily{recitative}
  \wordfamily{recite}
  \wordfamily{cite}
  \wordfamily{a recitation（详述）}
  \wordphrase{a recitation（详述）}
  \wordexample{a recitation（详述）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{recitative}{'resitətiv}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CIT}
  \wordfamily{recitation；cite；recital；recite；incite（sb + to do 刺激）}
  \wordsense{n. （歌剧等的）宣叙部；宣叙调 *}
  \wordphrase{a recitative（宣叙部）}
  \wordexample{a recitative（宣叙部）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{cite}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CIT；sait}
  \wordfamily{incite（sb + to do 刺激）；excite（刺激）；excitement（兴奋）}
  \wordsense{v. 引用，引证；表彰（战功等）；传唤}
  \wordphrase{be cited in dispatches（在派遣任务中受到表扬）}
  \wordphrase{be cited in divorce proceedings（在离婚诉讼过程中被传唤）}
  \wordphrase{cited in dispatches（在派遣任务中受 到表扬）}
  \wordexample{be cited in divorce proceedings（在离婚诉讼过程中被传唤）}
  \wordexample{be cited in dispatches（在派遣任务中受到表扬）}
\end{word}



\chapter{SINU}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根SINU 来自拉丁语 sinu 意为 curve，hollow。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{insinuate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SINU；在里边画曲线、弯曲 in（= in）+ sinu（= curve）；in'sinjueit}
  \wordfamily{insinuation（旁敲侧击的话）；sinuous（蜿蜒的）；sinus（窦（如鼻窦、静脉窦等））}
  \wordsense{vt. 暗示；含沙射影地说；使缓缓进入}
  \wordphrase{insinuate the issue}
  \wordexample{Some people are continuing to insinuate that I was responsible for the accident.（有些人一直含沙射影地说我应该对这 一事故负责。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{sinuous}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SINU；'sinjuəs}
  \wordfamily{sinuosity（蜿蜒，曲折）；insinuate（暗示）；sinus（窦（如鼻窦、静脉窦等））}
  \wordsense{a. 蜿蜒的，曲折的}
  \wordphrase{be sinuous（蜿蜒的，曲折的）}
  \wordexample{be sinuous（蜿蜒的，曲折的）}
  \wordexample{sinuous（['sinjuəs]）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{sinus}{'sainəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SINU}
  \wordsense{n.窦（如鼻窦、静脉窦等）}
  \wordfamily{sinuous}
  \wordfamily{insinuate}
  \wordfamily{a sinus（窦（如鼻窦、静脉窦等））}
  \wordphrase{a sinus（窦（如鼻窦、静脉窦等））}
  \wordexample{a sinus（窦（如鼻窦、静脉窦等））}
\end{word}



\chapter{TREP,TREM}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根TREP,TREM 来自拉丁语的 trep，trem 意为 to fear。同义词根有来自拉丁语的
  \item tim 和来自希腊语的 phob。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{intrepid}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TREP,TREM；不知道恐惧的 in（= not）+ trep（= fear）；in'trepid}
  \wordfamily{intrepidity（无畏，大胆）；intrepidly（无畏地，勇敢地）；trepidation（惊恐）}
  \wordsense{a. 无畏的，勇敢的}
  \wordphrase{intrepid so unlike most}
  \wordexample{Our 4-year-old child is intrepid, so unlike most other toddlers.（我们那四岁的孩子和大多数其他的小 孩不一样，他不知道害怕。）}
  \wordexample{intrepid so unlike most}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{trepidation}{,trepi'deiʃn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TREP,TREM}
  \wordsense{n.惊恐，不安}
  \wordfamily{intrepid}
  \wordfamily{tremble}
  \wordfamily{tremendous}
  \wordfamily{tremor}
  \wordfamily{a trepidation（惊恐，不安）}
  \wordphrase{a trepidation（惊恐，不安）}
  \wordexample{a trepidation（惊恐，不安）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{tremble}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TREP,TREM}
  \wordfamily{trembling（发抖的，战栗的）；intrepid（无畏的）；trepidation（惊恐）}
  \wordsense{v. （因恐惧、愤怒、寒冷、衰弱等而）发抖，战栗；（树叶、光线等）晃动，摇晃；焦虑， 担忧}
  \wordphrase{trembling poplar（欧洲山杨）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "trembling poplar".（常见搭配：欧洲山杨。）}
  \wordsense{n. 发抖，战栗；摇晃；震动}
  \wordphrase{in fear and trembling（恐 惧而焦虑）}
  \wordexample{in fear and trembling（恐 惧而焦虑）}
  \wordexample{His voice trembled with anger.（他因愤怒而声音颤抖。）}
  \wordexample{He was all of a tremble.（他浑身都在颤抖。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{tremendous}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TREP,TREM；trə'mendəs}
  \wordfamily{tremble；tremor；trepidation；tremulous；intrepid（无畏的）}
  \wordsense{a. 巨大的；极大的；极度的；《口》惊人的；极好的}
  \wordphrase{tremendous explosion（剧烈的爆炸）}
  \wordphrase{at a tremendous speed（以惊人的速度）}
  \wordphrase{tremendous eater / talker（食量惊人的人 / 非常健谈的人）}
  \wordexample{tremendous explosion（剧烈的爆炸）}
  \wordexample{at a tremendous speed（以惊人的速度）}
  \wordexample{tremendous eater / talker（食量惊人的人 / 非常健谈的人）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{tremor}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TREP,TREM；'tremə(r)}
  \wordfamily{intrepid（无畏的）；trepidation（惊恐）；tremble（（因恐惧、愤怒、寒冷、衰弱等而）发抖）}
  \wordsense{n. 发抖，战栗；震颤}
  \wordphrase{the tremor of a leaf（树叶的颤动）}
  \wordphrase{tremor of fear（因 恐惧而战栗）}
  \wordexample{the tremor of a leaf（树叶的颤动）}
  \wordexample{tremor of fear（因 恐惧而战栗）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{tremulous}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TREP,TREM；'tremjuləs}
  \wordfamily{intrepid（无畏的）；trepidation（惊恐）；tremble（（因恐惧、愤怒、寒冷、衰弱等而）发抖）}
  \wordsense{a. 发抖的，颤抖的；胆怯的}
  \wordphrase{in a tremulous voice（用颤抖的声音）}
  \wordphrase{with a tremulous hand（用颤抖的手）}
  \wordexample{in a tremulous voice（用颤抖的声音）}
  \wordexample{with a tremulous hand（用颤抖的手）}
\end{word}



\chapter{VAR}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根VAR 来自拉丁语的 var 意为 change。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{invariable}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VAR；不变的 in（= not）+ var（= change）；in'veəriəbl}
  \wordfamily{invariably（不变地）；variable（易变的）；variegated（杂色的）}
  \wordsense{a. 不变的；恒定的}
  \wordphrase{invariable truth}
  \wordexample{An invariable truth of life is that all that lives must die. （生命不变的一个真理就是所有活着的都会死去。）}
  \wordexample{invariable truth}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{variable}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VAR；'veəriəbl}
  \wordfamily{variance（不和）；varied（各种各样的）；variety（变化，多样）；杂耍表演（把歌舞等节目编排在一起的 演出）；variability（变化性）；variant（不同的，相异的）；invariable（不变的）}
  \wordsense{a. 易变的，不定的}
  \wordphrase{a variable（易变的事物）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "a variable".（常见搭配：易变的事物。）}
  \wordsense{n. 易变的事物；变量}
  \wordphrase{a variable（易变的事物）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "a variable".（常见搭配：易变的事物。）}
  \wordsense{n. 演出）variability 变化性；易变性}
  \wordphrase{a variable（易变的事物）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "a variable".（常见搭配：易变的事物。）}
  \wordsense{n. various 各种各样的 variant 不同的，相异的}
  \wordphrase{varied 各种各样的；有变化的；多彩的}
  \wordexample{His scholastic performance has been variable all year.（他的学习成绩在一年里一直不稳定。）}
  \wordexample{varied 各种各样的；有变化的；多彩的}
  \wordexample{variable（a.易变的，不定的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{variegated}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VAR；vəəri'geitid}
  \wordfamily{variegation（杂色，色彩斑驳）；invariable（不变的）；variable（易变的）}
  \wordsense{a. 杂色的，色彩斑驳的}
  \wordphrase{variegated}
  \wordexample{The flowers of pansies are often variegated.（三色 紫罗兰的花朵总是杂色的。）}
  \wordexample{variegated}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{variform}{'veərifɔ:m}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VAR}
  \wordsense{a.形状不同的；有各种形态的}
  \wordfamily{variegated}
  \wordfamily{variable}
  \wordfamily{invariable}
  \wordfamily{be variform（形状不同的）}
  \wordphrase{be variform（形状不同的）}
  \wordexample{be variform（形状不同的）}
\end{word}



\chapter{VER}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根VER 来自拉丁语的 ver 意为 to feel awe，to fear。此外，ver 还有 true 的意思，注
  \item 意不要混淆。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{irreverent}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VER；不再感到敬畏的 ir<in（= not）+ re（= again）+ ver（= feel awe）；i'revərənt}
  \wordfamily{irreverence（不敬）；不敬的行为（或言辞）；aver（断言）}
  \wordsense{a. 不尊敬的，不恭敬的}
  \wordphrase{So many teenagers today are}
  \wordexample{irreverent towards authority figures.（如今，许多十几岁的青少年对权威人士并 不尊敬。）}
  \wordexample{So many teenagers today are irreverent towards authority figures.（如今，许多十几岁的青少年对权威人士并 不尊敬。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{revere}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VER；ri'viə(r)}
  \wordfamily{可敬的）（我受人尊敬的祖父）；aver（断言）；verify（核实（真实性或正确性））}
  \wordsense{v. 尊敬，崇敬 *}
  \wordphrase{my revered grandfather}
  \wordexample{my revered grandfather}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{reverence}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VER；'revərəns}
  \wordfamily{revere；reverend；irreverent；verity；aver（断言）}
  \wordsense{n. 崇敬，尊敬，敬意 *}
  \wordphrase{saving your reverence（《古》恕我冒昧（地讲））}
  \wordphrase{You / His Reverence（（对神父、牧师 等的尊称）尊敬的阁下）}
  \wordexample{saving your reverence（《古》恕我冒昧（地讲））}
  \wordexample{You / His Reverence（（对神父、牧师 等的尊称）尊敬的阁下）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{reverend}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VER}
  \wordfamily{aver（断言）；verify（核实（真实性或正确性））；veritable（真正的）}
  \wordsense{a. 值得尊敬的；（the R\textasciitilde{}）对神职人员的尊称（书面语常写为 the Rev.，也略写为 Revd.）}
  \wordphrase{the Reverend（Rev.）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "the Reverend".（常见搭配：Rev.。）}
  \wordsense{n. 牧师，教士 *}
  \wordphrase{Martin Luther King（马丁·路德·金牧师）}
  \wordphrase{the Most Reverend（对 大主教的尊称）}
  \wordphrase{the Right Reverend（对主教的尊称）}
  \wordexample{Martin Luther King（马丁·路德·金牧师）}
  \wordexample{the Most Reverend（对 大主教的尊称）}
  \wordexample{the Right Reverend（对主教的尊称）}
\end{word}



\chapter{BIB[BEV]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根BIB[BEV] 来自拉丁语的 bib 意为 to drink，它的变形为 bev。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{imbibe}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 BIB[BEV]；喝进去 im<in（= in, into）+ bib（= drink）；im'baib}
  \wordfamily{imbibition（吸收，吸入）；bib（过多地喝酒）；beverage（饮料 *）}
  \wordsense{vt. 喝；吸入；接受（思想等）}
  \wordphrase{imbibe the issue}
  \wordexample{At a dude ranch the guests can imbibe the rural atmosphere for a few days.（客人们可以在观光牧场享受几天乡村气息。）}
  \wordexample{imbibe（vt.喝）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{bib}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 BIB[BEV]；bib}
  \wordfamily{wine-bibbing（贪杯的）；wine-bibber（酒鬼）；imbibe（喝）}
  \wordsense{v. 过多地喝酒，经常喝酒}
  \wordphrase{bib the issue}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of bib.（此处展示 bib 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 围兜；围裙（或工作服）的上部}
  \wordphrase{one's best bib and tucker（最好的衣服）}
  \wordexample{one's best bib and tucker（最好的衣服）}
  \wordexample{to bib（过多地喝酒，经常喝酒 n.围兜）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{beverage}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 BIB[BEV]；'bevəridʒ}
  \wordfamily{imbibe（喝）；bib（过多地喝酒）；reverend（值得尊敬的）}
  \wordsense{n. 饮料 *}
  \wordphrase{alcoholic / cooling beverage（酒精饮料 / 清凉饮料）}
  \wordexample{alcoholic / cooling beverage（酒精饮料 / 清凉饮料）}
\end{word}



\chapter{PET[PIT, PEAT]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根PET[PIT, PEAT] 来自拉丁语的 pet 意为 to rush, to seek, to strive，它的变形为 pit，peat。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{impetus}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PET[PIT, PEAT]；在内部猛冲 im<in（= in）+ pet（= rush）；'impitəs}
  \wordfamily{perpetual；propitious；propitiate；impetuous}
  \wordsense{n. 推动力，促进；刺激}
  \wordphrase{impetus to complete}
  \wordexample{I don't think he has the impetus to complete in the 3-mile race.（我认为他没 有完成 3 公里赛跑的动力。）}
  \wordexample{impetus to complete}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{perpetual}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PET[PIT, PEAT]；一直寻找的 per（= through, throughout）+ pet（= seek, strive）；pə'petʃuəl}
  \wordfamily{perpetuate（使永远存在）；perpetuity（永远，不朽）；perpetuation；perpetually（永久地）；impetus（推动力）}
  \wordsense{a. 永久的，永恒的；连续不断的}
  \wordphrase{be perpetual}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of perpetual.（此处展示 perpetual 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 永存，不朽}
  \wordphrase{the perpetual}
  \wordexample{Maintaining a state of perpetual inner peace is a truth seeker's goal.（永远保持内心的平静是真理追寻者的目标。）}
  \wordexample{perpetual（a.永久的，永恒的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{propitious}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PET[PIT, PEAT]；为事先求得、获得而费心思的 pro（= before, forward）+ pit<pet（= seek）；prə'piʃəs}
  \wordfamily{perpetual；propitiate；impetus；impetuous}
  \wordsense{a. 顺利的，形势有利的；慈悲的}
  \wordphrase{propitious for starting a}
  \wordexample{Economic conditions are propitious for starting a new business now.（现在的 经济状况利于开展新的生意。）}
  \wordexample{propitious for starting a}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{propitiate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PET[PIT, PEAT]；事先求得 pro（= before）+ pit<pet（= seek）；prə'piʃieit}
  \wordfamily{propitiation（劝解）；propitiatory（劝解的）；impetus（推动力）}
  \wordsense{vt. 劝解；安慰，抚慰；取悦}
  \wordphrase{I propitiated my offended}
  \wordexample{I propitiated my offended wife with gift of roses.（我用玫瑰作礼物取悦我生气的妻子。）}
  \wordexample{I propitiated my offended}
\end{word}

  \wordphrase{impetuous remarks（冲动的话）}
  \wordphrase{impetuous charge / gale（猛袭 / 狂风）}
  \wordexample{impetuous remarks（冲动的话）}
  \wordexample{impetuous charge / gale（猛袭 / 狂风）}

  \wordexample{be petulant（性急的）}

\chapter{DAMN[DEMN, DAM]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根DAMN[DEMN, DAM] 来自拉丁语的 damn 意为 curse，harm，其元音弱化后变成
  \item demn，它的变形为 dam。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{indemnity}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 DAMN[DEMN, DAM]；使不受损害 in（= not）+ demn<damn（= harm, curse）；in'demnəti}
  \wordfamily{indemnify（保障）；indemnification（赔偿）；condemn（谴责）}
  \wordsense{n. 保障；损害赔偿；保障物；赔偿物，战败国缴纳的赔偿金（或物资）}
  \wordphrase{the indemnity}
  \wordexample{Many people invest in the indemnity provided by a life insurance policy.（很多人投资购买人寿保险 以获得赔偿金的保障。）}
  \wordexample{indemnity（n.保障）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{condemn}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 DAMN[DEMN, DAM]}
  \wordfamily{imprisonment（判处杀人犯以终身监禁）；indemnity（保障）；damn（谴责）}
  \wordsense{v. 谴责，指责；宣判…有罪；宣告…不适用；宣告没收（走私物品、财产等）；充公； 宣告…患有不治之症 *con<com（= together）,}
  \wordphrase{condemn a murderer to life}
  \wordexample{condemn a murderer to life imprisonment（判处杀人犯以终身监禁）}
  \wordexample{condemn a murderer to life}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{damn}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 DAMN[DEMN, DAM]}
  \wordfamily{not（be）；indemnity（保障）；condemn（谴责）}
  \wordsense{v. 谴责，指责；罚…下地狱；诅咒}
  \wordphrase{damn the issue}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of damn.（此处展示 damn 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 诅咒；指责；【和否定词一起使用】一点点，丝毫}
  \wordphrase{the damn}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of damn.（此处展示 damn 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{a. 糟透的，可恶的；十足的}
  \wordphrase{be damn}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of damn.（此处展示 damn 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{ad. 极其，非常 int.他妈的，该死的}
  \wordphrase{don't give a damn（一点也不在乎）}
  \wordphrase{worth a damn（一文不值）}
  \wordexample{Damn it!（该 死的！）}
  \wordexample{The book was damned by the critics.（这本书受到了批评家们的谴责。）}
  \wordexample{don't give a damn（一点也不在乎）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{damned}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 DAMN[DEMN, DAM]；dæmd}
  \wordfamily{damn；damnable；condemn；damnation；indemnity（保障）}
  \wordsense{a. 被诅咒的；该死的；糟透的}
  \wordphrase{be damned}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of damned.（此处展示 damned 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{ad. 极其，非常}
  \wordphrase{damned hot / funny（极其热 / 非常好笑）}
  \wordexample{You damned fool!（你这个十足的笨蛋！）}
  \wordexample{damned hot / funny（极其热 / 非常好笑）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{damnable}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 DAMN[DEMN, DAM]；'dæmnəbl}
  \wordfamily{damned；damnation；damn；damage；indemnity（保障）}
  \wordsense{a. 讨厌的，可憎的；该遭天罚的；糟透的}
  \wordphrase{damnable weather（糟糕的天气）}
  \wordexample{damnable weather（糟糕的天气）}
  \wordexample{be damnable（讨厌的，可憎的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{damnation}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 DAMN[DEMN, DAM]；dæm'neiʃn}
  \wordfamily{damnable；damage；damned；damn；indemnity（保障）}
  \wordsense{n. 遭天罚，毁灭；谴责，指责}
  \wordphrase{suffer eternal damnation（永世受罚）}
  \wordexample{suffer eternal damnation（永世受罚）}
  \wordexample{a damnation（遭天罚，毁灭）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{damage}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 DAMN[DEMN, DAM]；'dæmidʒ}
  \wordfamily{indemnity（保障）；condemn（谴责）；damn（谴责）}
  \wordsense{n. 损害，损伤；（pl.）损害赔偿金；《口》费用}
  \wordphrase{the damage}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of damage.（此处展示 damage 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. 损害，毁坏 *}
  \wordphrase{furniture damaged by fire （因火灾而受损的家具）}
  \wordexample{do considerable damage to the crops（严重破坏农作物）}
  \wordexample{furniture damaged by fire （因火灾而受损的家具）}
\end{word}



\chapter{FALL}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根FALL 来自拉丁语的 fall 意为 to err。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{infallible}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FALL；没有做错的 in（= not）+ fall（= err）；in'fæləbl}
  \wordfamily{infallibility（无错误，无过失性）；fallible（易犯错误的）；fallacy（错误）}
  \wordsense{a. 绝对正确的；不会失效的；确实可靠的}
  \wordphrase{be infallible}
  \wordexample{Despite early hopes, computers have not proven themselves to be infallible.（计算机并没有像早期所期望的那样绝对正确 无误。）}
  \wordexample{infallible（a.绝对正确的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{fallible}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FALL；'fæləbl}
  \wordfamily{fallibility（容易犯错）；infallible（绝对正确的）；fallacy（错误）}
  \wordsense{a. 易犯错误的}
  \wordphrase{fallible}
  \wordexample{It behooves us to forgive others since we're all equally fallible.（我们应当宽以待人，因为我们自己也同样容易犯错。）}
  \wordexample{fallible}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{fallacy}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FALL；'fæləsi}
  \wordfamily{fallacious（谬误的）；infallible（绝对正确的）；fallible（易犯错误的）}
  \wordsense{n. 错误，谬误；谬论；谬误的推理}
  \wordphrase{statement based on fallacy（基于谬论的陈述）}
  \wordphrase{nox，nic，nec 等。}
  \wordexample{statement based on fallacy（基于谬论的陈述）}
\end{word}



\chapter{NOC[NOX,NIC,NEC]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根NOC[NOX,NIC,NEC] 来自拉丁语的 noc 意为 to harm，它的变形有 nox，nic，nec 等。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{innocuous}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 NOC[NOX,NIC,NEC]；不伤害的 in（= not）+ noc（= harm）；i'nɔkjuəs}
  \wordfamily{obnoxious；pernicious；innocent；noxious}
  \wordsense{a. 无害的，（特指蛇等）无毒的；没有恶意的，无意冒犯的}
  \wordphrase{be innocuous}
  \wordexample{Uncle Henry is an innocuous enough figure, though he tends to be a bit gruff.（亨利叔叔脾气是有点坏，不过他却是一个毫无恶意的人。）}
  \wordexample{innocuous（a.无害的，（特指蛇等）无毒的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{obnoxious}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 NOC[NOX,NIC,NEC]；给某人伤痛的 ob（= to）+ nox<noc（= hurt）；əb'nɔkʃəs}
  \wordfamily{innocuous；pernicious；innocent；noxious}
  \wordsense{a. 非常讨厌的；令人极为不愉快的；可憎的}
  \wordphrase{obnoxious little boy just}
  \wordexample{That obnoxious little boy just kicked me in the shin!（那个可恶的小男孩儿刚 好踢了我的胫骨！）}
  \wordexample{obnoxious little boy just}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{pernicious}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 NOC[NOX,NIC,NEC]；完全损害、损伤的 per（= through, thoroughly）+ nic<noc（= harm, kill）；pə'niʃəs}
  \wordfamily{obnoxious；innocent；innocuous；noxious}
  \wordsense{a. 有害的；恶性的}
  \wordphrase{pernicious computer virus is}
  \wordexample{A pernicious computer virus is invading home computers and erasing their data.（一种有害的计算机病毒正在侵入家用电脑，并删除电脑中的资料。）}
  \wordexample{pernicious computer virus is}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{innocent}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 NOC[NOX,NIC,NEC]}
  \wordfamily{innocently（天真地）；innocuous（无害的）；obnoxious（非常讨厌的）}
  \wordsense{a. 无罪的；无害的；单纯的，天真的；愚钝的}
  \wordphrase{be innocent（无罪的）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "be innocent".（常见搭配：无罪的。）}
  \wordsense{n. 清白的人；单纯的人；天真无邪的人 （尤指孩子） *}
  \wordphrase{be innocent（无罪的）}
  \wordexample{be innocent（无罪的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{noxious}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 NOC[NOX,NIC,NEC]；'nɔkʃəs}
  \wordfamily{innocent；internecine；pernicious；obnoxious；innocuous（无害的）}
  \wordsense{a. 有害的；有毒的 *}
  \wordphrase{noxious gases（有毒气体）}
  \wordexample{noxious gases（有毒气体）}
  \wordexample{be noxious（有害的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{internecine}{,intə'ni:sain}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 NOC[NOX,NIC,NEC]}
  \wordfamily{noxious；innocent；pernicious；obnoxious；innocuous（无害的）}
  \wordsense{a. 自相残杀的，两败俱伤的 *inter（= between）,}
  \wordphrase{be internecine（自相残杀的，两败俱伤的）}
  \wordexample{be internecine（自相残杀的，两败俱伤的）}
\end{word}



\chapter{PORT}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根PORT 来自拉丁语的 port 意为 to carry。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 fer，lat，ger，
  \item gest，veh / vect 和来自希腊语的 phor / pher。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{inopportune}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PORT；不是搬运某物的时候的 in（= not）+ op<ob（= toward）+ port（= carry）；in'ɔpətju:n}
  \wordfamily{importune；rapport；portable；opportune}
  \wordsense{a. 不合时宜的；不适当的}
  \wordphrase{be inopportune}
  \wordexample{Why do telemarketers always seem to call at the most inopportune moments?（为什么电话销售员似乎总是在最不合适的时间打电话呢？）}
  \wordexample{inopportune（a.不合时宜的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{importune}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PORT；向内搬 im<in（= in, into）+ port（= carry）；,impɔ:'tju:n}
  \wordfamily{importunate（胡搅蛮缠的）；importunity（执拗）；inopportune（不合时宜的）}
  \wordsense{vt. sb + for + sth 向…强求；纠缠}
  \wordphrase{I importuned the 911}
  \wordexample{I importuned the 911 operator to send an ambulance immediately.（我缠着 911 接线员要求立即 派一辆救护车来。）}
  \wordexample{I importuned the 911}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{rapport}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PORT；再次、继续搬得很近 r<re（= again）+ ap<ad（= near）+ port（= carry）；ræ'pɔ:(r)}
  \wordfamily{importune；portable；inopportune；opportune}
  \wordsense{n. 亲密关系；融洽，和谐}
  \wordphrase{rapport with my new}
  \wordexample{I'm afraid I don't have much of a rapport with my new stepchildren.（我和我 的继子女之间恐怕没有多少亲密感。）}
  \wordexample{rapport with my new}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{portable}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PORT；'pɔ:təbl}
  \wordfamily{portability（可携带）；porter（搬运工）；inopportune（不合时宜的）}
  \wordsense{a. 可携带的；可移动的}
  \wordphrase{be portable}
  \wordexample{This portable cook stove uses small, lightweight tanks of propane.（这种便携式烹饪炉使用的是轻小的 罐装丙烷。）}
  \wordexample{portable（a.可携带的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{opportune}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PORT；向着某物搬的 op<ob（= toward）+ port（= carry）；'ɔpətju:n}
  \wordfamily{portable；purport；rapport；comport；inopportune（不合时宜的）}
  \wordsense{a. 及时的；时机好的；适宜的}
  \wordphrase{opportune time}
  \wordexample{This is an opportune time for me to retire, at the end of the project.（这项工 程完成之时也正是我退休的好时机。）}
  \wordexample{opportune time}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{purport}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PORT；向前搬 pur<pro（= forth）+ port（= carry）；'pə:pɔ:t}
  \wordfamily{opportune；comport；portable；deport；inopportune（不合时宜的）}
  \wordsense{n. 意义，主旨，要旨}
  \wordphrase{the purport}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of purport.（此处展示 purport 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 意味着；以…为主旨，大意是；声称}
  \wordphrase{purports to cure snoring}
  \wordexample{A recently announced dietary supplement purports to cure snoring.（据称， 最近公布的膳食补充品可以治疗打鼾。）}
  \wordexample{purports to cure snoring}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{comport}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PORT；kəm'pɔ:t}
  \wordfamily{comportment（举止，行为）；inopportune（不合时宜的）；importune（sb + for + sth 向…强求）}
  \wordsense{v. 举止，举动；（\textasciitilde{} with）合适，相称 *}
  \wordphrase{comport oneself with dignity（举止庄重）}
  \wordexample{comport oneself with dignity（举止庄重）}
  \wordexample{His conduct did not comport with his position.（他的行为和他的地位不相称。）}
\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{transportation}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PORT}
