\chapter{SAG}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根SAG 来自拉丁语的 sag 意为 to perceive。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{presage}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SAG；事先感觉、感知 pre（= before）+ sag（= perceive）；'presidʒ}
  \wordfamily{sage；sagacious；sagacity}
  \wordsense{vt. 预示；预言；预知，预感}
  \wordphrase{presage the issue}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of presage.（此处展示 presage 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 前兆，预感；预知}
  \wordphrase{presage a heart attack}
  \wordexample{Chest and arm pains, nausea, and shortness of breath can presage a heart attack.（胸部和手臂疼痛、恶心以及气短都可能是心脏病发作的前兆。）}
  \wordexample{presage a heart attack}
  \wordexample{presage（vt.预示）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{sage}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SAG}
  \wordfamily{presage；sagacious；sagacity}
  \wordsense{n. 贤人，哲人}
  \wordphrase{a sage（贤人，哲人）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "a sage".（常见搭配：贤人，哲人。）}
  \wordsense{a. 智慧的；贤明的；（贬）道貌岸然的；一本正经的}
  \wordphrase{a sage（贤人，哲人）}
  \wordexample{a sage（贤人，哲人）}
  \wordexample{be sage（智慧的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{sagacious}{sə'geiʃəs}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SAG}
  \wordsense{a.精明的；有洞察力的；（动物）伶俐的}
  \wordfamily{sage}
  \wordfamily{sagacity}
  \wordfamily{presage}
  \wordfamily{be sagacious（精明的）}
  \wordphrase{be sagacious（精明的）}
  \wordexample{be sagacious（精明的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{sagacity}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SAG；sə'gæsəti}
  \wordfamily{presage（预示）；sage（贤人）；sagacious（精明的）}
  \wordsense{n. 贤明；睿智}
  \wordphrase{Sagacity unlike cleverness may}
  \wordexample{Sagacity, unlike cleverness, may increase with age.（与伶俐不同，睿智可能会随着年龄的增长而增长。）}
\end{word}



\chapter{SCI}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根SCI 来自拉丁语的 sci 意为 to know。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 gno，not。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{prescience}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCI；事先知道 pre（= before）+ sci（= know）；'preʃəns}
  \wordfamily{prescient（预知的）；omniscient（无所不知的）；conscience（良心）}
  \wordsense{n. 预知；先见}
  \wordphrase{prescience he got out}
  \wordexample{Thanks to his prescience, he got out of the stock market before it fell.（幸亏他有先见之明，能在股市下跌之前脱身。）}
  \wordexample{prescience he got out}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{omniscient}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCI；无论什么都知道的 omni（= all）+ sci（= know）；ɔm'niʃənt}
  \wordfamily{omniscience（无所不知的）；prescience（预知）；conscience（良心）}
  \wordsense{a. 无所不知的}
  \wordphrase{omniscient}
  \wordexample{Life would be boring if one were truly omniscient.（如果一个人真的通晓一切，那么他的生活将会很无聊。）}
  \wordexample{omniscient}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{conscience}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCI；'kɔnʃəns}
  \wordfamily{omniscient；conscientious；prescience；conscious}
  \wordsense{n. 良心；道义，善恶观念 *}
  \wordphrase{have no conscience（没有良心）}
  \wordexample{have no conscience（没有良心）}
  \wordexample{a conscience（良心）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{conscientious}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCI；ˌkɔnʃi'enʃəs}
  \wordfamily{conscience；conscious；omniscient；consciousness；prescience（预知）}
  \wordsense{a. 凭良心的；诚实的；认真的，勤勤恳恳的 *}
  \wordphrase{conscientious student / worker（勤奋的学生 / 勤勤恳恳的工人）}
  \wordphrase{conscientious objector（（根据宗教、道义上的信念）拒服兵役的人（缩写：C.O.））}
  \wordexample{conscientious student / worker（勤奋的学生 / 勤勤恳恳的工人）}
  \wordexample{conscientious objector（（根据宗教、道义上的信念）拒服兵役的人（缩写：C.O.））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{conscious}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCI；'kɔnʃəs}
  \wordfamily{class-conscious（阶级意识的）；prescience（预知）；omniscient（无所不知的）}
  \wordsense{a. 意识到的；神志清醒的；（行为等）有意的 *}
  \wordphrase{be conscious of（意识到……）}
  \wordexample{be conscious of（意识到……）}
  \wordexample{be conscious（意识到的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{consciousness}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCI；'kɔnʃəsnis}
  \wordfamily{lose（/ regain (recover)）；prescience（预知）；omniscient（无所不知的）}
  \wordsense{n. 知觉；意识 *}
  \wordphrase{stream of consciousness（意识流）}
  \wordphrase{consciousness of kind（同类意识）}
  \wordexample{moral consciousness of a political party（政党的道德意识）}
  \wordexample{stream of consciousness（意识流）}
  \wordexample{consciousness of kind（同类意识）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{unconscionable}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCI；ʌn'kɔnʃənəbl}
  \wordfamily{consciousness；unconscious；conscious；nescience；prescience（预知）}
  \wordsense{a. 不合理的；没良心的 *un（= not）,}
  \wordphrase{unconscionable bargain（不合理的交易）}
  \wordexample{unconscionable bargain（不合理的交易）}
  \wordexample{be unconscionable（不合理的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{unconscious}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCI；ʌn'kɔnʃəs}
  \wordfamily{unconscionable；nescience；consciousness；plebiscite；prescience（预知）}
  \wordsense{a. 无意识的，不知不觉的；没有知觉的，不省人事的}
  \wordphrase{be unconscious}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of unconscious.（此处展示 unconscious 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 无意识 *un（= not）,}
  \wordphrase{unconscious humor（无意识间产生的幽默）}
  \wordphrase{unconscious neglect（无意的疏忽）}
  \wordphrase{be unconscious of danger（没有意识到危险）}
  \wordexample{unconscious humor（无意识间产生的幽默）}
  \wordexample{unconscious neglect（无意的疏忽）}
  \wordexample{be unconscious of danger（没有意识到危险）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{nescience}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCI；'neʃəns}
  \wordfamily{nescient（无知的）；prescience（预知）；omniscient（无所不知的）}
  \wordsense{n. 无知 *}
  \wordphrase{be unconscious of danger（没有意识到危险）}
  \wordexample{be unconscious of danger（没有意识到危险）}
  \wordexample{nescience* n.无知 *ne（= not）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{plebiscite}{'plebisait}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCI}
  \wordfamily{nescience；sciolistic；unconscious；science；prescience（预知）}
  \wordsense{n. （对国家重要问题的）公民投票 *}
  \wordphrase{a plebiscite（公民投票）}
  \wordexample{a plebiscite（公民投票）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{sciolistic}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCI；ˌsaiəu'listik}
  \wordfamily{sciolist（一知半解的人）；prescience（预知）；omniscient（无所不知的）}
  \wordsense{a. 一知半解的}
  \wordphrase{be sciolistic（一知半解的）}
  \wordexample{be sciolistic（一知半解的）}
  \wordexample{sciolistic（[ˌsaiəu'listik]）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{science}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCI；'saiəns}
  \wordfamily{scientist（自然科学家）；prescience（预知）；omniscient（无所不知的）}
  \wordsense{n. 科学；（特指）自然科学；学科；技术}
  \wordphrase{science fiction（科幻小说）}
  \wordexample{science fiction（科幻小说）}
  \wordexample{a science（科学）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{scientific}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCI；ˌsaiən'tifik}
  \wordfamily{prescience（预知）；omniscient（无所不知的）；conscience（良心）}
  \wordsense{a. 科学的；系统的；精确的；有技术的 *}
  \wordphrase{scientific methods / farming（科学方法 / 科学种田）}
  \wordphrase{scientific name（学名）}
  \wordphrase{scientific argument（科学的论证）}
  \wordexample{scientific methods / farming（科学方法 / 科学种田）}
  \wordexample{scientific name（学名）}
  \wordexample{scientific argument（科学的论证）}
\end{word}



\chapter{CUR}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根CUR 来自拉丁语的 cur 意为 to take care。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{procure}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CUR；事先注意、小心 pro（= before）+ cur（= take care）；prə'kjuə(r)}
  \wordfamily{procurement（获得）；procurer（拉皮条者）；procuress（老鸨）；procurator（代诉人）；procurable（可获得的）}
  \wordsense{v. 取得，获得；筹措；引起，导致；介绍（娼妓）}
  \wordphrase{My fiancé procured a}
  \wordexample{My fiancé procured a string quartet to play when he proposed.（我未婚夫在向我求婚时，弄来了一个弦乐四重奏乐团。）}
  \wordexample{My fiancé procured a}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{accurate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CUR；'ækjurət}
  \wordfamily{accurately（精确地）；procure（取得）；accuracy（准确）}
  \wordsense{a. 准确的；精密的，精确的 *}
  \wordphrase{accurate machines / statements（精密的机器 / 精确的陈述）}
  \wordphrase{accurate typist（错误极少的打字员）}
  \wordexample{accurate machines / statements（精密的机器 / 精确的陈述）}
  \wordexample{accurate typist（错误极少的打字员）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{accuracy}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CUR；'ækjurəsi}
  \wordfamily{procure（取得）；accurate（准确的）；secure（安全的）}
  \wordsense{n. 准确；精密 *}
  \wordphrase{an accuracy（准确）}
  \wordexample{an accuracy（准确）}
  \wordexample{accuracy（['ækjurəsi]）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{secure}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CUR；si'kjuə(r)}
  \wordfamily{procure（取得）；accurate（准确的）；accuracy（准确）}
  \wordsense{a. 安全的；安心的；确定无疑的，确信的；稳固的；稳当的}
  \wordphrase{be secure}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of secure.（此处展示 secure 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. 使安全，确保；得到（不易获得的东西）；缚牢；关紧 *}
  \wordphrase{secure investments / position（稳当的投资 / 稳固的地位）}
  \wordexample{secure investments / position（稳当的投资 / 稳固的地位）}
  \wordexample{The success secured his reputation.（成功确保了他的名声。）}
  \wordexample{Please secure a seat for me.（请给我占一个位子。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{security}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CUR；si'kjuərəti}
  \wordfamily{procure（取得）；accurate（准确的）；accuracy（准确）}
  \wordsense{n. 安全（感）；保证；保安，防卫；担保（物）；（pl.）债券，证券 *}
  \wordphrase{the Security Council（（联合国）安全理事会）}
  \wordphrase{security police / forces（秘密警察 / 保安部队）}
  \wordphrase{security for a loan（贷款的担保）}
  \wordexample{the Security Council（（联合国）安全理事会）}
  \wordexample{security police / forces（秘密警察 / 保安部队）}
  \wordexample{Security is the greatest enemy.（安全是最大的敌人。不能掉以轻心。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{cure}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CUR；kjuə(r)}
  \wordfamily{cure-all（万能药）；procure（取得）；accurate（准确的）}
  \wordsense{n. 治疗；治愈；（处理社会问题等的）对策}
  \wordphrase{a cure（治疗）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "a cure".（常见搭配：治疗。）}
  \wordsense{v. 治疗；纠正；消除（弊病等）；加工处理（通常指为了保存肉、鱼等而腌制、熏制、风干等）}
  \wordphrase{cure for unemployment（失业的对策）}
  \wordphrase{well-cured bacon（加工得好的熏肉）}
  \wordexample{cure for unemployment（失业的对策）}
  \wordexample{well-cured bacon（加工得好的熏肉）}
  \wordexample{a cure（治疗）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{curable}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CUR；'kjuərəbl}
  \wordfamily{curability（治愈可能性）；procure（取得）；accurate（准确的）}
  \wordsense{a. 可医治的；可以纠正的}
  \wordphrase{be curable（可医治的）}
  \wordexample{be curable（可医治的）}
  \wordexample{curable（['kjuərəbl]）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{curative}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CUR；'kjuərətiv}
  \wordfamily{curable；curator；cure；curio；procure（取得）}
  \wordsense{a. 有疗效的；治疗的}
  \wordphrase{be curative（有疗效的）}
  \wordexample{curative value of sunshine and sea air（日光和海边空气的治疗价值）}
  \wordexample{be curative（有疗效的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{curator}{kjuə'reitə(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CUR}
  \wordsense{n.（博物馆、美术馆的）馆长；管理者}
  \wordfamily{curative}
  \wordfamily{curio}
  \wordfamily{curable}
  \wordfamily{curiosity}
  \wordfamily{a curator（馆长）}
  \wordphrase{a curator（馆长）}
  \wordexample{a curator（馆长）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{curio}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CUR；'kjuəriəu}
  \wordfamily{curator；curiosity；curative；curious；procure（取得）}
  \wordsense{n. 古董，真品}
  \wordphrase{curio dealer（古董商）}
  \wordexample{curio dealer（古董商）}
  \wordexample{a curio（古董，真品）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{curiosity}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CUR；ˌkjuəri'ɔsəti}
  \wordfamily{procure（取得）；accurate（准确的）；accuracy（准确）}
  \wordsense{n. 好奇心；珍品；古玩}
  \wordphrase{curiosity shop（古玩店）}
  \wordexample{curiosity shop（古玩店）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{curious}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CUR；'kjuəriəs}
  \wordfamily{procure（取得）；accurate（准确的）；accuracy（准确）}
  \wordsense{a. 有好奇心的；爱打听别人的事的；奇妙的}
  \wordphrase{hill, to rise。}
  \wordexample{hill, to rise。}
\end{word}



\chapter{MONT,MOUNT}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根MONT, MOUNT 来自拉丁语的 mont, mount 意为 hill, to rise。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{promontory}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MONT,MOUNT；向前突出的山坡 pro（= forth）+ mont（= hill, rise）；'prɔməntəri}
  \wordfamily{paramount；surmount；insurmountable；tantamount}
  \wordsense{n. 岬，海角}
  \wordphrase{promontory of land jutting}
  \wordexample{This is a small promontory of land jutting into the Pacific.（这是伸向太平洋的一个小海角。）}
  \wordexample{promontory of land jutting}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{paramount}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MONT,MOUNT；在山坡旁边的 para（= beside）+ mount（= hill）；'pærəmaunt}
  \wordfamily{paramountcy（最高权威）；promontory（岬）；surmount（克服（困难、障碍））}
  \wordsense{a. 至高无上的；卓越的；主要的，最重要的}
  \wordphrase{paramount importance}
  \wordexample{Childhood vaccinations are of paramount importance to prevent terrible diseases.（从小接种疫苗对预防重大疾病至关重要。）}
  \wordexample{paramount importance}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{surmount}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MONT,MOUNT；越过山坡走 sur（= over）+ mount（= hill, rise）；sə'maunt}
  \wordfamily{surmountable（可以克服的）；promontory（岬）；paramount（至高无上的）}
  \wordsense{vt. 克服（困难、障碍）；越过；在……顶上，高于}
  \wordphrase{surmounts difficulty and triumphs}
  \wordexample{The typical hero of a romance novel surmounts difficulty and triumphs.（典型的爱情小说中的男主人公总是能够克服困难取得胜利。）}
  \wordexample{surmounts difficulty and triumphs}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{insurmountable}{ˌinsə'mauntəbl}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MONT,MOUNT}
  \wordfamily{surmount；tantamount；paramount；dismount；promontory（岬）}
  \wordsense{a. 无法克服的；无法逾越的 *in（= not）,}
  \wordphrase{be insurmountable（无法克服的）}
  \wordexample{be insurmountable（无法克服的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{tantamount}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MONT,MOUNT；'tæntəmaunt}
  \wordfamily{insurmountable；dismount；surmount；remount；promontory（岬）}
  \wordsense{a. 相当于……的 *}
  \wordphrase{tantamount to a command}
  \wordexample{The Queen's request was tantamount to a command.（女王的要求等同于命令。）}
  \wordexample{tantamount to a command}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{dismount}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MONT,MOUNT；dis'maunt}
  \wordfamily{dismounted（（骑兵）像步兵一样战斗的）；promontory（岬）；paramount（至高无上的）}
  \wordsense{v. 使下马；使掉下马；卸下；拆开（机器等） *}
  \wordphrase{dismount a gun（（从炮架上）放下大炮）}
  \wordexample{dismount from one's horse / bicycle（从马 / 自行车上下来）}
  \wordexample{dismount a gun（（从炮架上）放下大炮）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{remount}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MONT,MOUNT}
  \wordfamily{dismount；mountain；tantamount；mountainous；promontory（岬）}
  \wordsense{v. 再骑上（马等）；重登上（梯子等）；补充新马}
  \wordphrase{a remount（补充的新马）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "a remount".（常见搭配：补充的新马。）}
  \wordsense{n. 补充的新马 *}
  \wordphrase{to remount（再骑上（马等））}
  \wordexample{to remount（再骑上（马等））}
  \wordexample{a remount（补充的新马）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{mountain}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MONT,MOUNT；'mauntin}
  \wordfamily{mountaineering（登山）；promontory（岬）；paramount（至高无上的）}
  \wordsense{n. 山；（pl.）山脉；〈喻〉堆积成山的东西；大量}
  \wordphrase{mountain sickness（高山病）}
  \wordexample{a mountain of debts / difficulties（堆积成山的债务 / 困难）}
  \wordexample{mountain sickness（高山病）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{mountainous}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MONT,MOUNT；'mauntənəs}
  \wordfamily{mountain；mount；remount；mountebank；promontory（岬）}
  \wordsense{a. 多山的；有山的；像山一般的；巨大的}
  \wordphrase{mountainous country（山岳地带）}
  \wordphrase{mountainous waves（如山的波浪）}
  \wordexample{mountainous country（山岳地带）}
  \wordexample{mountainous waves（如山的波浪）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{mount}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MONT,MOUNT；maunt}
  \wordfamily{Everest（珠穆朗玛峰）；promontory（岬）；paramount（至高无上的）}
  \wordsense{v. 爬上（山、梯子、楼梯等）；骑（马等）；（物价等）上涨；发动（攻击等）}
  \wordphrase{mount the issue}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of mount.（此处展示 mount 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 骑马；可乘骑的东西（如马、车等）；底座；炮架；山，峰（通常简称 Mt，附加在山的名字上）}
  \wordphrase{the Sermon on the Mount（《山顶布道》，出自《马太福音第五章》）}
  \wordphrase{Mount Rushmore（总统山）}
  \wordexample{the Sermon on the Mount（《山顶布道》，出自《马太福音第五章》）}
  \wordexample{Mount Rushmore（总统山）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{mountebank}{'mauntibæŋk}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MONT,MOUNT}
  \wordfamily{mount；montage；mountainous；Mont（Blanc）；promontory（岬）}
  \wordsense{n. 骗子；江湖郎中 *}
  \wordphrase{a mountebank（骗子）}
  \wordexample{a mountebank（骗子）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{montage}{mɔn'tɑ:ʒ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MONT,MOUNT}
  \wordsense{n.蒙太奇}
  \wordfamily{mountebank}
  \wordfamily{Mont Blanc}
  \wordfamily{mount}
  \wordfamily{piedmont}
  \wordfamily{a montage（蒙太奇）}
  \wordphrase{a montage（蒙太奇）}
  \wordexample{a montage（蒙太奇）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{Mont Blanc}{'mʌn'blɔŋ}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MONT,MOUNT}
  \wordfamily{montage；piedmont；mountebank；mount；promontory（岬）}
  \wordsense{n. 勃朗峰（位于法国、意大利国境线上的阿尔卑斯山的最高峰，4807 米） *}
  \wordphrase{a Mont Blanc（勃朗峰（位于法国、意大利国境线上的阿尔卑斯山的最高峰，4807 米））}
  \wordexample{a Mont Blanc（勃朗峰（位于法国、意大利国境线上的阿尔卑斯山的最高峰，4807 米））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{piedmont}{'pi:dmɔnt}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MONT,MOUNT}
  \wordsense{n.山麓地带；（P\textasciitilde{}）北美洲 Appalachian（阿巴拉契亚）山脉和大西洋沿岸间的高原 *pied（= foot）}
  \wordfamily{Mont Blanc}
  \wordfamily{montage}
  \wordfamily{mountebank}
  \wordfamily{mount}
  \wordfamily{a piedmont（山麓地带）}
  \wordphrase{a piedmont（山麓地带）}
  \wordexample{a piedmont（山麓地带）}
\end{word}



\chapter{FUS, FUT, FUND, FOUND}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根FUS, FUT, FUND, FOUND 来自拉丁语的 fus, fut, fund, found 意为 to pour。同义词根为 chemo。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{profuse}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FUS, FUT, FUND, FOUND；向前倾泻、倾倒的 pro（= forth）+ fus（= pour）；prə'fju:s}
  \wordfamily{profusion（丰富）；profusely（毫不吝惜地）；effusive（过分热情的）}
  \wordsense{a. 丰富的；浪费的，奢侈的；毫不吝惜的，慷慨的}
  \wordphrase{profuse stand}
  \wordexample{The profuse stand of Douglas fir trees perfumes the air with pine scent.（茂密的花旗松的松香弥漫在周围的空气中。）}
  \wordexample{profuse stand}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{effusive}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FUS, FUT, FUND, FOUND；向外倾泻的 ef<ex（= out）+ fus（= pour）；i'fju:siv}
  \wordfamily{effuse（使流出）；effusion（流出）；effusively（过分热情地）；profuse（丰富的）}
  \wordsense{a. 过分热情的；喷出的，溢出的}
  \wordphrase{effusive praise embarrassing but}
  \wordexample{I find effusive praise embarrassing, but I do appreciate it all the same.（虽然过分热情的称赞会让我有些不知所措，但我仍然对此表示真心感谢。）}
  \wordexample{effusive praise embarrassing but}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{irrefutable}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FUS, FUT, FUND, FOUND；不能往回倒的 ir<in（= not）+ re（= back）+ fut（= pour）；i'refju:təbl}
  \wordfamily{effusive；confuse；profuse；confusion}
  \wordsense{a. 无法反驳的}
  \wordphrase{"Irrefutable Laws}
  \wordexample{I attended a seminar on the "Irrefutable Laws of Leadership".（我参加了一个有关“无可辩驳的领导法则”的研讨会。）}
  \wordexample{"Irrefutable Laws}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{confuse}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FUS, FUT, FUND, FOUND；kən'fju:z}
  \wordfamily{confusing（令人困惑的）；profuse（丰富的）；effusive（过分热情的）}
  \wordsense{v. 搞乱，使混乱；使糊涂 *}
  \wordphrase{to confuse（搞乱，使混乱）}
  \wordexample{I got confused.（我困惑了。）}
  \wordexample{to confuse（搞乱，使混乱）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{confusion}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FUS, FUT, FUND, FOUND；kən'fju:ʒn}
  \wordfamily{Confusion!（混乱！）；profuse（丰富的）；effusive（过分热情的）}
  \wordsense{n. 混乱；糊涂 *}
  \wordphrase{confusion worse confounded（乱上加乱）}
  \wordexample{in the confusion of the moment（在片刻的混乱中）}
  \wordexample{confusion worse confounded（乱上加乱）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{defuse}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FUS, FUT, FUND, FOUND；di:'fju:z}
  \wordfamily{confusion；diffuse；confuse；diffusion；profuse（丰富的）}
  \wordsense{v. 拆去（炮弹等的）引信，使失去爆炸性；使镇定，缓和 *}
  \wordphrase{defuse a situation / crisis（使情况缓和 / 平息危机）}
  \wordexample{defuse a situation / crisis（使情况缓和 / 平息危机）}
  \wordexample{to defuse（拆去（炮弹等的）引信，使失去爆炸性）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{diffuse}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FUS, FUT, FUND, FOUND；di'fju:z}
  \wordfamily{defuse；diffusion；confusion；infuse；profuse（丰富的）}
  \wordsense{v. 使（热气体等）散开；传播（知识、消息等），散布}
  \wordphrase{diffuse the issue}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of diffuse.（此处展示 diffuse 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{a. 冗长的；扩散的 *}
  \wordphrase{diffuse learning / knowledge（普及知识）}
  \wordphrase{diffuse writer（废话多的作家）}
  \wordexample{diffuse learning / knowledge（普及知识）}
  \wordexample{diffuse writer（废话多的作家）}
  \wordexample{to diffuse（使（热气体等）散开）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{diffusion}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FUS, FUT, FUND, FOUND；di'fju:ʒn}
  \wordfamily{profuse（丰富的）；effusive（过分热情的）；irrefutable（无法反驳的）}
  \wordsense{n. 散布；普及，传播；扩散}
  \wordphrase{diffusion of gases and liquids（气体和液体的扩散）}
  \wordexample{diffusion of knowledge through books and lectures（通过书籍和讲课传播知识）}
  \wordexample{diffusion of gases and liquids（气体和液体的扩散）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{infuse}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FUS, FUT, FUND, FOUND；in'fju:z}
  \wordfamily{diffusion；infusion；diffuse；refuse；profuse（丰富的）}
  \wordsense{v. 注入（思想、活力等）；泡（茶、药等）；鼓舞 *}
  \wordphrase{infuse soldiers with fresh courage（让士兵们鼓足勇气）}
  \wordphrase{infuse herbs（泡药草）}
  \wordexample{infuse soldiers with fresh courage（让士兵们鼓足勇气）}
  \wordexample{infuse herbs（泡药草）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{infusion}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FUS, FUT, FUND, FOUND；in'fju:ʒn}
  \wordfamily{infuse；refuse；diffusion；refusal；profuse（丰富的）}
  \wordsense{n. 灌，注入；鼓舞；泡制；注入物 *}
  \wordphrase{an infusion（灌，注入）}
  \wordexample{infusion of new blood into old stock（（为了改良）给旧的品种注入新鲜的血液）}
  \wordexample{an infusion（灌，注入）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{refuse}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FUS, FUT, FUND, FOUND；rı'fju:z}
  \wordfamily{infusion；refusal；infuse；suffuse；profuse（丰富的）}
  \wordsense{v. 拒绝；不肯；不愿}
  \wordphrase{to refuse（拒绝）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "to refuse".（常见搭配：拒绝。）}
  \wordsense{n. 废物，垃圾}
  \wordphrase{to refuse（拒绝）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "to refuse".（常见搭配：拒绝。）}
  \wordsense{a. 废物的，扔掉的 *}
  \wordphrase{refuse dump（垃圾回收处）}
  \wordphrase{refuse consumer（垃圾焚烧器）}
  \wordphrase{refuse collector（垃圾工人）}
  \wordexample{refuse dump（垃圾回收处）}
  \wordexample{refuse consumer（垃圾焚烧器）}
  \wordexample{refuse collector（垃圾工人）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{refusal}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FUS, FUT, FUND, FOUND；rı'fju:zəl}
  \wordfamily{refuse；suffuse；infusion；transfuse；profuse（丰富的）}
  \wordsense{n. 拒绝；推辞；（pl.）优先取舍权，优先购买权 *}
  \wordphrase{give her a flat refusal（断然地拒绝她）}
  \wordexample{give her a flat refusal（断然地拒绝她）}
  \wordexample{a refusal（拒绝）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{suffuse}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FUS, FUT, FUND, FOUND；sə'fju:z}
  \wordfamily{profuse（丰富的）；effusive（过分热情的）；irrefutable（无法反驳的）}
  \wordsense{v. 遍布；（液体、色彩等）充满 *}
  \wordphrase{eyes suffused with tears（泪汪汪的眼睛）}
  \wordexample{eyes suffused with tears（泪汪汪的眼睛）}
  \wordexample{to suffuse（遍布）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{transfuse}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FUS, FUT, FUND, FOUND}
  \wordfamily{suffuse；transfusion；refusal；fuse；profuse（丰富的）}
  \wordsense{v. 【医】输血，注射（生理盐水等）；使（液体、色彩等）渗入；灌输（思想等）；倾注 *}
  \wordphrase{to transfuse（输血，注射（生理盐水等））}
  \wordexample{to transfuse（输血，注射（生理盐水等））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{transfusion}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FUS, FUT, FUND, FOUND；træns'fju:ʒn}
  \wordfamily{transfuse；fuse；suffuse；fusion；profuse（丰富的）}
  \wordsense{n. 倾注；输血 *}
  \wordphrase{transfusion}
  \wordexample{The injured man was given a blood transfusion.（给那个受伤的人输了血。）}
  \wordexample{transfusion}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{fuse}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FUS, FUT, FUND, FOUND；fju:z}
  \wordfamily{profuse（丰富的）；effusive（过分热情的）；irrefutable（无法反驳的）}
  \wordsense{v. 熔化；保险丝烧坏}
  \wordphrase{time fuse（定时导火索）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "time fuse".（常见搭配：定时导火索。）}
  \wordsense{n. （炮弹等的）导火索，（用于爆破等的）导火线；熔丝}
  \wordphrase{fuse wire（保险丝，熔丝）}
  \wordexample{The light has fused.（保险丝烧断了，灯不亮了。）}
  \wordexample{fuse wire（保险丝，熔丝）}
  \wordexample{to fuse（熔化）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{fusion}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FUS, FUT, FUND, FOUND；ˈfju:ʒn}
  \wordfamily{fuse；refund；transfusion；confound；profuse（丰富的）}
  \wordsense{n. 熔解；（政党等的）联合，合作；（核）聚变}
  \wordphrase{fusion of copper and tin（铜和锡的熔合）}
  \wordexample{fusion of races / political parties（种族 / 政党的联合）}
  \wordexample{fusion of copper and tin（铜和锡的熔合）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{refund}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FUS, FUT, FUND, FOUND；rı'fʌnd}
  \wordfamily{fusion；confound；fuse；confounded；profuse（丰富的）}
  \wordsense{v. 归还；偿还}
  \wordphrase{to refund（归还）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "to refund".（常见搭配：归还。）}
  \wordsense{n. 返还（物）；偿还（金） *}
  \wordphrase{refund the cost of postage（归还邮费）}
  \wordexample{obtain a refund of a deposit（得到返还押金）}
  \wordexample{refund the cost of postage（归还邮费）}
  \wordexample{to refund（归还）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{confound}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FUS, FUT, FUND, FOUND；kən'faund}
  \wordfamily{refund；confounded；fusion；foundry；profuse（丰富的）}
  \wordsense{v. 混淆；使混乱；挫败 *}
  \wordphrase{confound right and wrong（混淆了对与错）}
  \wordexample{confound right and wrong（混淆了对与错）}
  \wordexample{Don't confound the means with the ends.（不要混淆手段和目的。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{confounded}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FUS, FUT, FUND, FOUND；kən'faundid}
  \wordfamily{confoundedly（非常地）；profuse（丰富的）；effusive（过分热情的）}
  \wordsense{a. 狼狈的；困惑的；《口》讨厌的；无理的 *}
  \wordphrase{confounded lie（无理的谎言）}
  \wordexample{You're a confounded nuisance!（你真是个让人讨厌的家伙！）}
  \wordexample{confounded lie（无理的谎言）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{foundry}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FUS, FUT, FUND, FOUND；ˈfaundri}
  \wordfamily{profuse（丰富的）；effusive（过分热情的）；irrefutable（无法反驳的）}
  \wordsense{n. 铸造厂；玻璃厂}
  \wordphrase{a foundry（铸造厂）}
  \wordexample{a foundry（铸造厂）}
  \wordexample{foundry（[ˈfaundri]）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{confute}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FUS, FUT, FUND, FOUND；kən'fju:t}
  \wordfamily{confutation（驳斥，驳倒）；profuse（丰富的）；effusive（过分热情的）}
  \wordsense{v. 驳倒 *}
  \wordphrase{to confute（驳倒）}
  \wordexample{to confute（驳倒）}
  \wordexample{confute（[kən'fju:t]）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{refute}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FUS, FUT, FUND, FOUND；rı'fju:t}
  \wordfamily{refutation（驳斥，反驳）；refutable（可以驳斥的）；profuse（丰富的）}
  \wordsense{v. 驳斥，反驳 *}
  \wordphrase{to refute（驳斥，反驳）}
  \wordexample{refute an argument / an opponent（驳斥一个论点 / 对手）}
  \wordexample{to refute（驳斥，反驳）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{futile}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FUS, FUT, FUND, FOUND；ˈfju:tail}
  \wordfamily{profuse（丰富的）；effusive（过分热情的）；irrefutable（无法反驳的）}
  \wordsense{a. 无用的，无益的，无效果的；没出息的；愚蠢的}
  \wordphrase{the futility of life（人生的无用）}
  \wordphrase{futile attempt（徒劳的尝试）}
  \wordexample{the futility of life（人生的无用）}
  \wordexample{futile attempt（徒劳的尝试）}
\end{word}



\chapter{AL}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根AL 来自拉丁语的 al 意为 to nourish。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 nutri, troph。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{prolific}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 AL；给予养分使其向前的pro（=forth）+al（=nourish）；prailifik}
  \wordfamily{Thecomposerremainedprolific（那位作曲家在创作上依然硕果累累）；alimentary（营养的）；alimony（离婚赠养费）}
  \wordsense{a. 多产的；作品多的；肥沃的，丰富的}
  \wordphrase{be prolific（多产的）}
  \wordexample{prolific（a.多产的）}
  \wordexample{be prolific（多产的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{alimentary}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 AL；ali'mentri}
  \wordfamily{prolific（多产的）；alimony（离婚赠养费）；alma mater（母校）}
  \wordsense{a. 营养的}
  \wordphrase{alimentary canal（消化道）}
  \wordexample{alimentary canal（消化道）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{alimony}{'aelimani}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 AL}
  \wordsense{n.离婚赠养费；生活费}
  \wordfamily{alimentary}
  \wordfamily{alma mater}
  \wordfamily{prolific}
  \wordfamily{an alimony（离婚赠养费）}
  \wordphrase{an alimony（离婚赠养费）}
  \wordexample{an alimony（离婚赠养费）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{alma mater}{alma 'maita(r)}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 AL}
  \wordsense{n.《拉》母校；校歌}
  \wordfamily{alimony}
  \wordfamily{alimentary}
  \wordfamily{prolific}
  \wordphrase{an alma mater（《拉》母校）}
  \wordexample{an alma mater（《拉》母校）}

\chapter{CLIV}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根CLIV 来自拉丁语的 cliv 意为 to bend。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 clin/cli，flect/flex，cumb/cub 和来自盎格鲁-撒克逊语的 croach[croch，crouch，crotch，crutch，crook] 等。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{proclivity}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{向前弯曲pro（=forward）+cliv（=bend)；pra'klivati}
  \wordfamily{acclivity；declivity；alma mater（母校）}
  \wordsense{n. 倾向；癖性；气质}
  \wordphrase{proclivity for exaggeration}
  \wordexample{Don't believe everything she says; she has a proclivity for exaggeration. (她 说的话不要全信，她总爱夸大其词。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{acclivity}{a'klivati}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CLIV}
  \wordfamily{declivity；proclivity（倾向）；alma mater（母校）}
  \wordsense{n. 斜坡；上倾'ac(ad (= to, toward)}
  \wordphrase{an acclivity（斜坡）}
  \wordexample{an acclivity（斜坡）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{declivity}{di'kivati}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CLIV}
  \wordfamily{acclivity；proclivity（倾向）；alma mater（母校）}
  \wordsense{n. 下斜；斜坡 'de(=down)}
  \wordphrase{a declivity（下斜）}
  \wordexample{a declivity（下斜）}
\end{word}



\chapter{TEG, TECT}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根TEG, TECT 来自拉丁语的 teg，tect 意为 to hide，to cover。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 cond，cel[ceal，cul]，cover/cur 等和来自希腊语的 crypt。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}{protégé}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TEG, TECT}
  \wordsense{n. <法> 被保护人；门徒}
  \wordfamily{detect}
  \wordfamily{detection}
  \wordfamily{detective}
  \wordfamily{protect}
  \wordphrase{Mr Higgins treated me}
  \wordexample{Mr. Higgins treated me as his protégé during my first years with the firm.（在我进入公司最初几年的时间里，希金斯先生一直把我当成他的门生。）}
  \wordexample{Mr. Higgins treated me as his protege during my first years with the firm. (在 我进入公司最初几年的时间里，希金斯先生一直把我当成他的门生。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{detect}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TEG, TECT；di'tekt}
  \wordfamily{detectable（可察觉的）；detector（探测器）；protégé（<法> 被保护人）}
  \wordsense{v. 发现，察觉；探测；侦察 *}
  \wordphrase{to detect（发现，察觉）}
  \wordexample{to detect（发现，察觉）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{detection}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TEG, TECT；di'tekfn}
  \wordfamily{detectionofcrime（犯罪的侦查）；protégé（<法> 被保护人）；detect（发现）}
  \wordsense{n. 察觉，发现；探测；侦察*}
  \wordphrase{a detection（察觉，发现）}
  \wordexample{a detection（察觉，发现）}

\chapter{FORT, FORC}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根FORT, FORC 来自拉丁语的 fort，forc意为 strong。同义词根有来自拉丁语的
  \item bil,firm,rob，val/vail等。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{reinforce}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 FORT, FORC；【重新使里面变得坚实re（=again）+in（=in）+forc（=strong）] [riin'fos]；reinforce（【重新使里面变得坚实re（=again）+in（=in）+forc（=strong）] [riin'fos]}

  \wordsense{v. 强化，增强；增援}

  \wordexample{to reinforce（强化，增强）}
\end{word}

  \wordphrase{fortify the issue}
  \wordexample{My firstpolitical defeat fortifiedmyresolveto succeedthenexttimearound.（我第一次的从政失败增强了我下一轮必胜的 决心。）}
\end{word}

\chapter{TARD}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根TARD 来自拉丁语的tard意为slow。同义词根有来自益格鲁一撒克逊语的
  \item slo，反义词根有来自拉丁语的cel。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}{retard}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{因落后而慢re（=back）+tard（=slow)；ri'tad}
  \wordfamily{retardation（妨碍）；tardy（缓慢的）；fortitude（坚忍）}
  \wordsense{vt. 妨碍；拖延，推迟}
  \wordphrase{retard the spread}
  \wordexample{Firefighters use certain chemicals that retard the spread of a fire.（消防员使用某些化学物质来阻止火势蔓延。）}
  \wordexample{retardation妨碍；迟延，拖延Firefightersusecertainchemicalisthat retardthespreadofafire.（消防队员使用特定的化学物质阻止火势蔓延。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{tardy}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TARD；'tadi}
  \wordfamily{tardily（拖拖拉拉地，迟缓地）；tardyinofferinghelp（不情愿帮忙的）；tardyforschool；retard（妨碍）}

  \wordphrase{be tardyforschool}
  \wordexample{be tardyforschool（上学迟到）}
\end{word}



\chapter{HAB[HIB]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根HAB[HIB] 来自拉丁语的hab意为to live，tohave，tohold。它的元音弱化形
  \item 为hib。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{rehabilitate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 HAB[HIB]；【重新拥有re（=again）+hab（=have,live）] [ri:ha'biliteit]；rehabilitate（【重新拥有re（=again）+hab（=have,live）] [ri:ha'biliteit]}
  \wordfamily{inhabitant（居民）；habituate（使习惯于）；malady（疾病）}
  \wordsense{vt. 使（出狱的人或病愈）恢复正常生活；使复职，复位；使恢复名誉；复原（老建 筑等），修复；使复生}
  \wordphrase{rehabilitate the issue}
  \wordexample{Istrongly believe prisoners should be rehabilitated during their incarceration.（我坚持认 为，犯人们在监禁期间应该接受正确的管教。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{inhabitant}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 HAB[HIB]；［在里面生活的人in（=in）+hab（=live,have）] [in'haebitant]}
  \wordfamily{rehabilitate（使（出狱的人或病愈）恢复正常生活）；habituate（使习惯于）；malady（疾病）}
  \wordsense{n. 居民，居住者；（某地区）栖息的动物}
  \wordphrase{的}
  \wordexample{There are only 7 inhabitants in this small apartment building.（这座小公寓 楼里只住了7个人。）}
  \wordexample{的}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{habituate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 HAB[HIB]；［使生活hab（=live,have)】 [ha'bitjueit]}
  \wordfamily{inhabitant；malady；rehabilitate；inhibit}
  \wordsense{v. 使习惯于}
  \wordphrase{Bears have become habituated}
  \wordexample{Bears have become habituated to tourists as a primary food source. (熊 已经 习惯将游客当作食物的首要来源了。）}
  \wordexample{Bears have become habituated}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{malady}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 HAB[HIB]；【具有不好的状态、恶性mal（=bad）+ad<hab（=live,have)] ['maeladi]；malady（【具有不好的状态、恶性mal（=bad）+ad<hab（=live,have)] ['maeladi]}
  \wordfamily{habituate；inhibit；inhabitant；habitable；rehabilitate（使（出狱的人或病愈）恢复正常生活）}
  \wordsense{n. 疾病；（社会的）弊病}
  \wordphrase{the malady}
  \wordexample{My brother's a hypochondriac, always complaining of some malady or other （我的弟弟是个郁症患者，经常抱有这样或那样的病。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{inhibit}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 HAB[HIB]；［使不能拥有in（=not）+hib<hab（=have,hold）] [in'hrbit]】；kau'haebnt}
  \wordfamily{cohabitationpolitics（左右共治）；rehabilitate（使（出狱的人或病愈）恢复正常生活）；inhabitant（居民）}
  \wordsense{v. 抑制；禁止}
  \wordphrase{I hate tight clothing}
  \wordexample{I hate tight clothing becauseitinhibitsmymovement.（我讨厌紧身的衣服，因为它妨碍我活动。）}
  \wordexample{cohabit v.（尤指未婚男女）同居生活；共同存在‘co<com（=together）}
\end{word}

  \wordsense{a. 适合居住的}
  \wordphrase{be habitable（适合居住的）}
  \wordexample{Theold house isno longerhabitable.（这座老房子再也不适宜居住了。）}

\end{word}

  \wordsense{a. 习惯的；习以为常的，惯常的}

\end{word}

\chapter{SCIND, SCIS}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根 SCIND, SCIS 来自拉丁语的scind，scis意为to cut，同义词根有来自拉丁语的cis，
  \item cop/coup，sect，sever，tail，tons，trench和来自希腊语的tom。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{rescind}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{向后裁断，使之消失re（=back,away）+scind（=cut)；ri'sind}
  \wordfamily{scissors；pronunciamento（布告）；pronunciation（发音 pro(= forth)）}
  \wordsense{vt. 废止，使无效，取消}
  \wordphrase{rescind an employment offer}
  \wordexample{The company may rescind an employment offer if references don't check Out.（如果相关资料得不到验证，那么公司可能会取消聘用。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{scissors}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCIND, SCIS}
  \wordfamily{rescind；pronunciamento（布告）；pronunciation（发音 pro(= forth)）}
  \wordsense{n. 剪刀 【'sizaz]}
  \wordphrase{a scissors（剪刀 【'sizaz]）}
  \wordexample{a scissors（剪刀 【'sizaz]）}
\end{word}



\chapter{TAC[TIC]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根TAC[TIC] 来自拉丁语的tac意为silent，其元音弱化后变成tic。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{reticent}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TAC[TIC]；［几乎没有话的re（=intensive）+tic<tac（=silent)]］ [ retrsnt]；reticent（［几乎没有话的re（=intensive）+tic<tac（=silent)]］ [ retrsnt]}
  \wordfamily{reticence（沉默寡言，缄默）；tacit（无言的）；taciturn（无言的）}
  \wordsense{a. 沉默寡言的，缄默的}
  \wordphrase{be reticent}
  \wordexample{Employees are often reticent to report fraud for fearoflosingtheirjobs.（职员们常常因担心丢掉工作而不报告欺骗行为，）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{tacit}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TAC[TIC]}
  \wordsense{a.无言的，沉默的；默示的}
  \wordfamily{['taesit]}
  \wordfamily{tacitly（默默地）?tacitconsent/agreement（默许/默契，默示协议）}
  \wordfamily{be tacit（无言的，沉默的）}
  \wordphrase{be tacit（无言的，沉默的）}
  \wordexample{be tacit（无言的，沉默的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{taciturn}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TAC[TIC]}
  \wordfamily{tacit；reticent；scissors（剪刀 【'sizaz）}
  \wordsense{a. 无言的，沉默寡言的 taciturnity（无言，沉默）}
  \wordphrase{be taciturn（无言的，沉默寡言的 ['taesit3in] taciturnity（无言，沉默））}
  \wordexample{be taciturn（无言的，沉默寡言的 ['taesit3in] taciturnity（无言，沉默））}
\end{word}



\chapter{DUB [DOUB]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根 DUB [DOUB] 来自拉丁语的dub意为uncertain。其变形为doub。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{redoubtable}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 DUB [DOUB]；完全不确定或不明确的re（=intensive）+doub<dub（=uncertain）】 [rdautabl}
  \wordfamily{indubitable（不容置疑的）；dubious（可疑的）；dubiety（怀疑）}
  \wordsense{a. 可怕的；令人敬畏的}
  \wordphrase{be redoubtable}
  \wordexample{Many modern politicians continue to quote the redoubtable Winston Churchill.（很多现代政治家还在继续引用令人敬畏的温斯顿·丘吉尔的话。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{indubitable}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{不是不确定的in（=not）+dub（=uncertain)；in'dju:bitabl}
  \wordfamily{redoubtable；dubious；dubiety}
  \wordsense{a. 不容置疑的，明确的}
  \wordphrase{indubitable conclusion is that}
  \wordexample{The indubitable conclusion is that you're either drunk or stoned.（结论毫无 疑问：你喝醉了或已烂醉如泥。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{dubious}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 DUB [DOUB]；'dju:bias}
  \wordfamily{dubiouscompliment（明衰实贬的恭维）；dubiousblessing（令人怀疑的祝福）；dubiouscharacter（名声不好的人）；redoubtable（可怕的）}
  \wordsense{a. 可疑的；半信半疑的；未定的，无把握的；暧昧的，含糊的；名声不好的}
  \wordphrase{be dubious（可疑的）}
  \wordexample{be dubious（可疑的）}
  \wordexample{dubious（['dju:bias]）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{dubiety}{dju:"balati}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 DUB [DOUB]}
  \wordsense{n.怀疑}
  \wordfamily{dubious}
  \wordfamily{redoubtable}
  \wordfamily{a dubiety（怀疑）}
  \wordphrase{a dubiety（怀疑）}
  \wordexample{a dubiety（怀疑）}
\end{word}



\chapter{MONSTR}

  \item phen。

\begin{word}[**]{remonstrate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MONSTR；［重新、反对着展示re（=again,against）+monstr（=show）] ['remanstreit]}
  \wordfamily{remonstrance（抗议）；demonstrate（证明）；demonstration（证明）}
  \wordsense{v. 抗议，提出异议；忠告}
  \wordphrase{remonstrate with the referee}
  \wordexample{A rabid fan jumped from the stands to remonstrate with the referee.（一位狂热的球迷跳下看台，向裁判抗议。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{demonstrate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MONSTR}

  \wordphrase{to demonstrate（证明，论证）}
  \wordexample{to demonstrate（证明，论证）}

  \wordsense{a. 感情流露的；【语】指示的；明确的，结论性的；示范的 'de (= completely)}

  \wordexample{be demonstrative（感情流露的）}

  \wordbreakdown{词根 MONSTR}

\begin{word}[**]{monstrous}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MONSTR；'mpnstras}
  \wordfamily{monstrouscrimes（骇人听闻的罪行）；remonstrate（抗议）；demonstrate（证明）}
  \wordsense{a. 怪异的，似怪物的；巨大的；骇人听闻的；让人厌恶的；荒谬的}
  \wordphrase{monstrous sum ( 巨 额)}
  \wordexample{monstrous sum ( 巨 额)}
  \wordexample{It's perfectly monstrous that men should be paid more than women for the same job.（同--职业的男性的收入应该比女性的多，这简直太荒谬了。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{monster}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MONSTR}
  \wordfamily{monstrous；demonstrator；demonstrative；demonstrable；remonstrate（抗议）}

  \wordphrase{Mermaids, griffins and dragons are monsters（人鱼、狮身鹫首的怪兽和龙都是虚构的 怪物。）}
  \wordphrase{A five-legged dog is a monster（五条腿的狗是畸形动物，）}
  \wordphrase{monster of cruelty（极度凶残的人）}
  \wordexample{Mermaids, griffins and dragons are monsters.（人鱼、狮身鹫首的怪兽和龙都是虚构的 怪物。）}
  \wordexample{A five-legged dog is a monster.（五条腿的狗是畸形动物，）}
  \wordsense{a. 巨大的；像怪物的 Mermaids, griffins and dragons are monsters.（人鱼、狮身鹫首的怪兽和龙都是虚构的 怪物。）A five-legged dog is a monster.（五条腿的狗是畸形动物，）monster of cruelty （极度凶残的人）monster ship（大型船舶）monster tree（巨树）}
  \wordphrase{a monster（（一种虚构的非人非兽的)怪物）}
  \wordexample{a monster（（一种虚构的非人非兽的)怪物）}
  \wordexample{be monster（巨大的）}
\end{word}

\chapter{TRENCH}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根TRENCH 语的tail，来自拉丁语的cis，cop/coup，scind/scis，sect，
  \item sever,tons和来自希腊语的tom[tem]。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{retrench}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TRENCH；【向后截断re（=back）+trench（=cut)］ [ri'trent]}
  \wordfamily{retrenchment（紧缩）；entrench（用壕沟围住）；trenchant（（话语）锋利的）}
  \wordsense{v. 减少（费用），紧缩；节约}
  \wordphrase{retrench in light}
  \wordexample{The corporation was forced to retrench in light of falling revenues.（由于收入下降，那家公司不得不缩减开支。）}
  \wordexample{retrenchment紧缩；节约经费The corporation was forced to retrench in lightoffallingrevenues.（由于收益减少，那家公司不得不缩减开支，）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{entrench}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{在内部截断en（=in）+trench（=cut)；in'trent}
  \wordfamily{entrenchment（堑壕）；retrench（减少（费用））；trenchant（（话语）锋利的）}
  \wordsense{v. 用壕沟围住，以壕沟防护；确立}
  \wordphrase{entrenched in Japanese society}
  \wordexample{Approval of commercial whaling is deeply entrenched in Japanese society.（日本社会对商业捕鲸的认可已经根深蒂固。）}
  \wordexample{entrenchment 堑壕；挖壕沟Approval of commercial whaling is deeply entrenchedinJapanesesociety.（日本社会对商业性捕鲸活动的认可已经根深 蒂固。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{trenchant}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TRENCH；【裁断的trench（=cut）] ['trentfant]}
  \wordfamily{thesemeetingsafteryouretire（您退任之后，我们会怀念您在会议上所发表的 那些一-针见血的评论的）；retrench（减少（费用））；entrench（用壕沟围住）}
  \wordsense{a. （话语）锋利的；尖锐的；（政策）有力的；（轮廓)清晰的}
  \wordphrase{be trenchant}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of trenchant.（此处展示 trenchant 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. ）战壕，沟渠；海沟?trenchancy尖锐，锋}
  \wordphrase{the trenchant}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of trenchant.（此处展示 trenchant 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. ）战壕，沟渠；海沟}
  \wordphrase{利}
  \wordexample{trenchantly尖锐地，锋利地We'll missyourtrenchantcommentsat thesemeetingsafteryouretire.（您退任之后，我们会怀念您在会议上所发表的 那些一-针见血的评论的。）}
  \wordexample{利}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{trench}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 TRENCH}

  \wordsense{n. 堑壕，阵地；沟渠}
  \wordphrase{trench warfare（堑壕战）}
  \wordphrase{trench a field（在田地里挖沟渠）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "trench warfare".（常见搭配：堑壕战。）}
  \wordsense{v. 挖战壕；挖沟渠 trench warfare（堑壕战）trench a field（在田地里挖沟渠）}
  \wordphrase{a trench（堑壕，阵地）}
  \wordexample{a trench（堑壕，阵地）}

\chapter{CILI}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根CILI 来自拉丁语的cili意为eyelid。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{supercilious}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CILI；【将眉毛向上挑起来的super（=above）+cili（=eyelid)] ['sju:pə'siliəs]；supercilious（【将眉毛向上挑起来的super（=above）+cili（=eyelid)] ['sju:pə'siliəs]}
  \wordfamily{Hissuperciliousmannerisquiteoff-putting（他傲慢的态度真让人厌恶）；cilia（睫毛）；plus（加（→minus减））}
  \wordsense{a. 傲慢的，自大的}
  \wordphrase{be supercilious（傲慢的，自大的）}
  \wordexample{be supercilious（傲慢的，自大的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{cilia}{'silia}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CILI}
  \wordfamily{supercilious；plus（加（→minus减））；plurality（大多数）}
  \wordsense{n. 睫毛；纤毛}
  \wordphrase{a cilia（睫毛）}
  \wordexample{a cilia（睫毛）}
\end{word}



\chapter{MIT, MISS[MIS, MESS]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根MIT, MISS[MIS, MESS] 义词根有来自拉丁语的leg和来自希腊语的stle。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{surmise}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MIT, MISS[MIS, MESS]；关于、对于某事寄送sur（=over）+mis（=send)；sa'maiz}
  \wordfamily{demise；intermittent；noncommittal；remit}
  \wordsense{v. 推测；猜想}
  \wordphrase{surmise the issue}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of surmise.（此处展示 surmise 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 推测；猜想}
  \wordphrase{surmise}
  \wordexample{The Republicans in this district aren't as conservative as you might surmise （这个地区的共和党人不像你想的那么保守。）}
  \wordexample{surmise}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{demise}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MIT, MISS[MIS, MESS]；【远远地、完全地寄送de（=away.intensive）+mis（=send）] [di'maz]；demise（【远远地、完全地寄送de（=away.intensive）+mis（=send）] [di'maz]}
  \wordfamily{surmise；intermittent；noncommittal；remit}
  \wordsense{n. 去世，驾崩；转让；遗赠}
  \wordphrase{the demise}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of demise.（此处展示 demise 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. 死；转让；遗赠}
  \wordphrase{demise of a national}
  \wordexample{The demise of a national administration in the United States is never a crisis. （在美国，一个政府的消亡不会成为危机。）}
  \wordexample{demise of a national}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{emit}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{向外寄送e(ex（=out）+mit（=send)；i'mit}
  \wordfamily{surmise（推测）；demise（去世）；intermittent（间歇的）}
  \wordsense{vt. 散发（光、热、声音、气味等)；发出，发射；发行（货币等）}
  \wordphrase{to emit（散发（光、热、声音、气味等)）}
  \wordexample{to emit（散发（光、热、声音、气味等)）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{intermittent}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MIT, MISS[MIS, MESS]；【送向其间的inter（=between）+mit（=send] [;inta'mitant]}
  \wordfamily{intermit（暂停，间断）；intermission（中断）；intermittently（间歇地）；surmise（推测）}
  \wordsense{a. 间歇的；断断续续的}
  \wordphrase{be intermittent}
  \wordexample{intermittently 间歇地；断断续续地Oursmokealarmmakesanirritatingintermittentbeep thatwon'tstop.（我们家的火警器没完没了地发出烦人的间歇性的响声。）}
  \wordexample{Our smoke alarm makes an irritating intermittent beep that won't stop.（我们家的烟雾报警器没完没了地发出烦人的间歇性哔哔声。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{noncommittal}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MIT, MISS[MIS, MESS]；inpnka'miti}
  \wordfamily{intermittent；remit；demise；submissive；surmise（推测）}
  \wordsense{a. 态度不明朗的，不表态的}
  \wordphrase{noncommittal about selling the}
  \wordexample{She was noncommittal about selling the house. but I think she wants to. (尽 管她对卖房子一事的态度并不明朗，但我觉得她还是想卖的。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{remit}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MIT, MISS[MIS, MESS]；远远地、向后寄送re（=back，away）+mit（=send）] [ri'mit] v.汇款；宽恕；免除；缓解；【律；医}
  \wordfamily{缓解remittance汇款（额）；surmise（推测）；demise（去世）}
  \wordsense{n. 将诉讼打回下级法院}
  \wordphrase{the remit}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of remit.（此处展示 remit 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. 汇款；宽恕；免除；缓解；【律】将诉讼打回下级法院}
  \wordphrase{remit the issue}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of remit.（此处展示 remit 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. remittent弛}
  \wordphrase{the remit}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of remit.（此处展示 remit 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. remittent弛 张的}
  \wordphrase{张的}
  \wordexample{We'll ship the merchandise as soon as you remit payment in full. (你- 将货款全部汇到，我们就用船运送货物。）}
  \wordexample{张的}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{remiss}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{向后寄送的re（=back）+miss（=send)；ri'mis}
  \wordfamily{surmise（推测）；demise（去世）；emit（散发（光、热、声音、气味等)）}
  \wordsense{a. 怠慢的，疏忽的}
  \wordphrase{remiss not}
  \wordexample{I was remiss not to greet you as you came in.（我急慢了，在您进来时没跟 您打招呼。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{submissive}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MIT, MISS[MIS, MESS]；把自己向下运送的sub（=under）+miss（=send)］ [səb'misiv}
  \wordfamily{submit（服从，屈服）；surmise（推测）；demise（去世）}

  \wordexample{submission服从；投降Rather than being submissive or aggressive, it's healthier to be assertive.（比起服从或者挑衅，自 信坚定要更健康有益。）}

  \wordphrase{Long documents can easily be transmitted over the Internet（长篇幅的文件可以通过网络轻松传送。）}
  \wordexample{Long documents can easily be transmitted over the Internet.（长篇幅的文件可以通过网络轻松传送。）}

\begin{word}[***]{admit}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MIT, MISS[MIS, MESS]；ad'mit}
  \wordfamily{surmise（推测）；demise（去世）；emit（散发（光、热、声音、气味等)）}

  \wordexample{The theater admits about 300 persons.（那个剧场可以容纳大约 300 人。）}
  \wordexample{It is generally admitted that..（被普遍承认的是.）}
\end{word}

\chapter{FORM}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根FORM 来自拉丁语的form意为form。同义词根有来自希腊语的morph。
\end{itemize}

  \wordsense{vt. 使变形；改变}
  \wordphrase{TwoweeksinthehotsunonaMexicanbeachwill transformyou（在墨西哥海边炙热的阳光下度过两个星期会让你变样。）}

\end{word}

\chapter{SCEND[SCENT,SCENS,SCAN]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根SCEND[SCENT,SCENS,SCAN]
  \item 等。另外，来自拉丁语的scal有ladder的意思，含义相似。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{transcend}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{越过某物爬tran<trans（=over,beyond）+scend（=climb）；traen'send}
  \wordfamily{transcendence（/ transcendency卓越，超越）；condescend（屈尊）；ascend（攀登（山)）}
  \wordsense{v. 超越（人类的经验、理性、信仰、描述能力等的范围）；优于}
  \wordphrase{的}
  \wordexample{You can transcend your shynessbydoingtheseexercisesdaily.（你每天做这些练习可以克服羞涩。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{condescend}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SCEND[SCENT,SCENS,SCAN]；【和别人一样爬下来con<com（=together）+de（=down）+scend（=climb）] [.kpndr'send]；condescend（【和别人一样爬下来con<com（=together）+de（=down）+scend（=climb）] [.kpndr'send]}
  \wordfamily{ascend；ascendancy；ascendant；ascension；transcend（超越（人类的经验、理性、信仰、描述能力等的范围））}
  \wordsense{vi. 屈尊；带着优越感表示亲切}
  \wordphrase{condescend in practice}
  \wordexample{That instructor condescends to his students by treating them like babies （那个教练不摆架子，像对待孩子似的亲切地对待他的学生。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{ascend}{[]}

\part{第二部分 利用后缀扩充词汇之词根篇}

\chapter{HER}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根HER 来自拉丁语的her意为heir，注意不要和意为to stick的her/hes相混淆。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{heritage}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 HER}
  \wordfamily{heritable（会遗传的）；heredity（遗传）；hereditary（世袭的）}
  \wordsense{n. 遗产；继承物}
  \wordphrase{heritage}
  \wordexample{As the eldest son, the title of Duke is his heritage.（作为家里的长子，他继承了公爵爵位。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{heredity}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 HER}
  \wordsense{n.遗传；遗传的特征}
  \wordfamily{[ha'redati]}

  \wordfamily{a heredity（遗传）}
  \wordphrase{a heredity（遗传）}
  \wordexample{a heredity（遗传）}
\end{word}

  \wordbreakdown{词根 HER；ha'reditri}
  \wordfamily{heredity；hereditament；heritage；heritable}

  \wordphrase{hereditaryrulers/beliefs/diseases（世的统治者/世代相传的信仰/遗传性疾病）}
  \wordexample{hereditaryrulers/beliefs/diseases（世的统治者/世代相传的信仰/遗传性疾病）}
  \wordexample{be hereditary（世袭的，世代相传的）}
\end{word}

\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{heritable}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 HER}

  \wordfamily{hereditament}

  \wordphrase{inherit30,000dollars fromhisfather（从他父亲那里继承3万美元）}
  \wordphrase{inherither mother's good looksand herfather's bad temper（继承了她母亲的美貌和父亲的坏 脾气）}
  \wordphrase{to inherit（继承（财产、权力、爵位等））}

\end{word}



\chapter{BOND}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根 BOND 来自拉丁语，意为 to bind。
  \item 同义词根有来自拉丁语的 cinct、lig[li, leag, ly]、nect/nex、strain/strict/string/stress 等。
  \item 另外，注意不要和意为 to join 的 junct/join/jug 相混淆。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{bondage}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 BOND；【被捆绑的状态bond（=bind)] ［bondd3]}
  \wordfamily{bondholder；bondstone；inheritance（继承）}
  \wordsense{n. 农奴、奴隶的状态（身份)；束缚，奴役}
  \wordphrase{bondage to cocaine It's}
  \wordexample{The poor guy is in bondage to cocaine. It's all he thinks about. (那个可怜的 人成了可卡因的奴隶，他脑子里想的都是它。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{bondholder}{tbnd,houlda(r)}

  \wordphrase{a bondholder（债券持有者）}
  \wordexample{a bondholder（债券持有者）}
\end{word}

  \wordfamily{bondage}
  \wordfamily{a bondstone（束石）}
  \wordphrase{a bondstone（束石）}
  \wordexample{a bondstone（束石）}
\end{word}



\chapter{SALV[SAV]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根SALV[SAV] 来自拉丁语的salv意为safe。sav是它的变形。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{salvage}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{使安全的行为salv（=safe)；'saelvrd3}
  \wordfamily{salvation（拯救）；salve（安慰）；saviour（救助者）}
  \wordsense{n. 海难救助；抢救的财物，被救的船舶；救助酬金；废物利用}
  \wordphrase{the salvage}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of salvage.（此处展示 salvage 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{vt. 救助；打捞；利用（废品）}
  \wordphrase{salvage from our home}
  \wordexample{After the tornado, there was literally nothing to salvage from our home.（龙卷 风过后，我们家真的是没有任何值得抢救的东西了。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{salvation}{sael'veifn}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 SALV[SAV]}

\end{word}

\chapter{PROPR}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根PROPR 来自拉丁语的propr意为one's own。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{propriety}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{自身所固有的性质propr（=one's own；pra'praati}
  \wordfamily{appropriate（占用）；appropriation（私用）；expropriate（没收（财产））}
  \wordsense{n. （行为等的）得体；妥当，适当；礼貌；（pl.）礼仪，礼节}
  \wordphrase{彻底地}
  \wordexample{I question the propriety of his promoting his own son to V. P. at such anearlyage.（我怀疑他将自己如此年轻的儿子提升为副总裁是否妥当。）}
\end{word}

  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of appropriate.（此处展示 appropriate 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{a. a'praupriat]适合的，适当的}
  \wordphrase{be appropriate}
  \wordexample{appropriate\$500.000for thenewschoolbuildings（为建新校舍拨款50万美元）}
  \wordexample{Thelegislatureappropriatedthe funds for theuniversity.（州议会为那所大学拨了款。）}
  \wordexample{Sports clothes arenot appropriateforaformalwedding.（运动服不适合正式的婚礼，）}
\end{word}

  \wordbreakdown{词根 PROPR}

  \wordphrase{an appropriation（私用，挪用（资金））}
  \wordexample{an appropriation（私用，挪用（资金））}
\end{word}

\chapter{AUD[ED, EIS]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根AUD[ED, EIS] 来自拉丁语的aud意为to hear。其变形为ed，eis。同义词根为来
  \item 自希腊语的acou。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{audition}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{听听aud(= hear)；[o'difn］}
  \wordfamily{inaudible；obeisance；audible；audience}
  \wordsense{n. （歌手、演讲者等发声的）试听；听力，听觉；（对志愿艺人等的)面试(指试演、 试唱、试奏等）}
  \wordphrase{audition is}
  \wordexample{My acting audition is at 3:30, and my singing audition is at 5:15. (我的试演时 间是3点30分，试唱时间是5点15分。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{inaudible}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{听不见的in（=not）+aud（=hear)；ndal}
  \wordfamily{isinaudible（录音带有一部分听不见）；audition（（歌手、演讲者等发声的）试听）；obeisance（敬礼）}
  \wordsense{a. 听不懂的；听不见的}
  \wordphrase{One section on the tape recording}
  \wordexample{One section on the tape recording isinaudible.（录音带有一部分听不见。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{obeisance}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{很听话ob（= intensive）+eis(aud（=hear)；aubeisns}
  \wordfamily{inaudible；audible；audition；audience}
  \wordsense{n. 敬礼；尊敬；服从}
  \wordphrase{obeisance to an Indian}
  \wordexample{My sister pays devoted obeisance to an Indian guru,of all things.（我妹妹完 全忠实于一位印度教领袖。）}
  \wordexample{obeisance to an Indian}
\end{word}

\chapter{LET, LIV}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根LET, LIV 来自拉丁语的let，liv意为lime。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{oblivion}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LET, LIV；［逆着涂抹石灰ob（=over.against）+liv（=lime）] [ablrvian]；oblivion（［逆着涂抹石灰ob（=over.against）+liv（=lime）] [ablrvian]}
  \wordfamily{delete；indelible；deleterious}
  \wordsense{n. 忘却，被遗忘的状态；无意识；无意识的状态}
  \wordphrase{the oblivion}
  \wordexample{oblivious遗忘的，忘却的；不注意的Shecan'thearyou.She'sstoned intoobliviononheroin.（她听不到你说话。她吸食了海洛因，恍忧熄的，处于 无意识状态。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{delete}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LET, LIV；【在某物上撒石灰使本来的形态消失de（=away）+let（=lime）] [dilist]；delete（【在某物上撒石灰使本来的形态消失de（=away）+let（=lime）] [dilist]}
  \wordfamily{oblivion（忘却）；indelible（（痕迹、墨水等）擦不掉的）；deleterious（（对身心）有害的）}
  \wordsense{vt. sth+from+sth删除；消除}
  \wordphrase{delete the issue}
  \wordexample{Theeditordeletedabout50wordsfromtheletterIsentto themagazine.（编辑从我寄给杂志的信里删掉了大约50个字。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{indelible}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LET, LIV；用石灰无法消除的in（=not）+de（=away.from）+li<liv（=lime)】 [n'delabl}
  \wordfamily{delete；deleterious；oblivion}
  \wordsense{a. （痕迹、墨水等）擦不掉的；难以忘却的；（耻辱等）无法洗刷的 I have an indelibie memory of the day we got married.（结婚的那一天令我无 法忘怀。）}
  \wordphrase{I have an indelibie memory of the day we got married（结婚的那一天令我无 法忘怀。）}
  \wordphrase{be indelible（擦不掉的）}
  \wordexample{I have an indelibie memory of the day we got married.（结婚的那一天令我无 法忘怀。）}
  \wordexample{be indelible（擦不掉的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{deleterious}{idela'trarias}

  \wordfamily{indelible}
  \wordfamily{delete}
  \wordfamily{oblivion}
  \wordfamily{be deleterious（有害的）}
  \wordphrase{be deleterious（有害的）}
  \wordexample{be deleterious（有害的）}

\chapter{CUSS}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根CUSS 来自拉丁语的 `cuss`，意为 `to strike, to shake`。同义词根有来自拉丁语的 `bat`、`flict`、`fend/fens[fenc, fest]`、`lid/lis`、`plaud/plaus[plod, plos, plex]` 等，以及来自盎格鲁-撒克逊语的 `sla`。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{repercussion}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CUSS；［用力摇晃回去re（=back）+per（=intensive）+cuss（=shake）] [ripa'kafn]}
  \wordfamily{percussion；concuss；concussion；discuss}
  \wordsense{n. 弹回；（事件等的）影响；反响}
  \wordphrase{repercussions all over America}
  \wordexample{The high school shooting had repercussions all over America.（高中枪击事 件在全美国引起了反响。）}
  \wordexample{repercussions all over America}
\end{word}

  \wordexample{a percussion（冲击）}
\end{word}

\end{word}

  \wordexample{a concussion（震动）}

\chapter{OP}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根OP, OPT, OPHTHALM 来自希腊语的op，opt意为eye。同义词根有来自拉丁语
  \item 的ocul。此外，还有形态和意思均与op，opt相似的来自希腊
  \item 语的ophthalm。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{optional}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{选择的opt（=choose）+ion（=state,quality）+al（=nature of)；'ppfani}
  \wordfamily{myopic（近视的）；amblyopia（弱视‘ambly (=dull)）；ametropia（（眼睛的）屈光不正（如散光、远视、近视）*a(a）}
  \wordsense{a. 随意的，非强制的；可自由选择的}
  \wordphrase{optional in the recipe}
  \wordexample{opt选择?option选择权；可选择的东西；选修科目；买卖的特权?optionally 随意地The roasted pine nuts are optional in the recipe.（在食谱上，烤松仁 是可选放的材料。）}
  \wordexample{optionally 随意地The roasted pine nuts are optional in the recipe.（在食谱上，烤松仁 是可选放的材料。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{myopic}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{闭上眼睛的my（=close）+op(opt（=eye）；mai'ppik}
  \wordfamily{myopia（近视）；optional（随意的）；amblyopia（弱视‘ambly (=dull)）}
  \wordsense{a. 近视的；目光短浅的}
  \wordphrase{myopic attitude toward technology}
  \wordexample{He says I have a myopic attitude toward technology, as though that's a bad thing!（他说我对技术持有一种短视的态度，好像那很不好 似的！）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{amblyopia}{raembli'aupia}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 OP}
  \wordfamily{ametropia；autopsy；biopsy；diplopia；optional（随意的）}
  \wordsense{n. 弱视‘ambly (=dull)}
  \wordphrase{an amblyopia（弱视‘ambly (=dull)）}
  \wordexample{an amblyopia（弱视‘ambly (=dull)）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{ametropia}{ami'troupia}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 OP}

\chapter{PATR[PATER]}

\chapter{NOMIN, NOMEN}

\begin{itemize}

  \item 意为law，order，rule的nom与该词根形态、意思相似。

\begin{word}[**]{nominal}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{只是名字的、名目的nomin（=name)；'nminl}

  \wordsense{a. 名字的；名义上的，有名无实的；不重要的，微不足道的；【语】名词性的}
  \wordphrase{nominal authority but the}
  \wordexample{The chair has nominal authority, but the real person in power is the executive secretary.（主席只拥有名义上的职权，实际掌权的是 行政秘书。）}
\end{word}

  \wordsense{n. 说梦话（的习惯）}

  \wordexample{The children grew somnolent, staring into the campfire's dying flames.（孩子们看着将要熄灭的营火，渐渐地困倦起来。）}
  \wordexample{somnambulism n.梦游病ambul（=walk）}

\chapter{MORD,MORS}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根MORD,MORS 来自拉丁语的mord，mors意为to bite。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{mordant}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{撕咬的mord<mors（=bite)；'moidant；'ml；morsel+}
  \wordfamily{remorse（极度懊悔）；concomitant（伴随的）；municipal（市的）}
  \wordsense{a. 尖酸的，刻薄的，讽刺的}
  \wordphrase{be mordant}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of mordant.（此处展示 mordant 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. （特别指食物的）一块，一口；少量；佳肴；（美味）小吃}
  \wordphrase{mordant wit is always}
  \wordexample{His mordant wit is always amusing though sometimes offensive.（他的讥讽总 是很有趣，虽然有时有点无礼。）}
  \wordexample{There was not even a morsel of food.（没有留下-点食物。）}
  \wordexample{What a dainty / choice morsel!（真是美味的/精选的小吃！）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{remorse}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MORD,MORS；'mos}
  \wordfamily{remorseful（极度懊悔的）；mordant（尖酸的）；concomitant（伴随的）}
  \wordsense{n. 极度懊悔；自责}
  \wordphrase{in a fit of remorse（有些愧悔）}
  \wordexample{remorseless（无悔意的；无恻隐心的）?feel remorse for my failure to help her（我后悔没有帮成她）}
  \wordexample{in a fit of remorse（有些愧悔）}
\end{word}



\chapter{CLAR[CLAIR]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根CLAR[CLAIR] 来自拉丁语的clar意为clear。clair是它的变形。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{clairvoyant}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CLAR[CLAIR]；【看上去分明、透明clair(clar(=clear)+voy(=look)+ant(=having the quality of)] [klea'ant]；clairvoyant（【看上去分明、透明clair(clar(=clear)+voy(=look)+ant(=having the quality of)] [klea'ant]}
  \wordfamily{clairvoyance（超人的视力）；clarify（阐明（意义、见解等））；clarinet（单簧管）}
  \wordsense{a. 有超人的视力的；有洞察力的}
  \wordphrase{be clairvoyant}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of clairvoyant.（此处展示 clairvoyant 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 有超人的视力的人；有洞察力的人}
  \wordphrase{clairvoyant counselor}
  \wordexample{She billed herself as a clairvoyant counselor.（她在海报上宣传自己是一位极具洞察力的顾问，）}
  \wordexample{clairvoyant counselor}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{clarify}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{使清楚clar（=clear）；klaerafar}
  \wordfamily{clairvoyant；clarinet；clarity；declaration}
  \wordsense{vt. 阐明（意义、见解等）；澄清；净化 clarification净化；澄清；说明clarity明晰，明确；透明，清澈?Could you please clarify for me the guidelines you use for grading essays?（能否请您说明 下您给文章评分时所使用的标准？）}
  \wordphrase{to clarify（阐明（意义、见解等））}
  \wordexample{Could you please clarify for me the guidelines you use for grading essays?（能否请您说明 下您给文章评分时所使用的标准？）}
  \wordexample{to clarify（阐明（意义、见解等））}
\end{word}

\chapter{BA[BET,BIT]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根 BA[BET, BIT] 来自希腊语，意为 to go。
  \item 同义词根有来自拉丁语的 ced/cess[ceed, ceas]、grad/gred/gress、it、vad/vas 等，此外还有来自盎格鲁-撒克逊语的 fare。
  \item 另外，注意不要和前面意为 to strike, to beat 的 bat 混淆。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{arbitrary}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 BA[BET,BIT]；［向着某物进发的ar（=to）+bit<bat（=go)] ['a:bitrari]}
  \wordfamily{arbitrate；arbitration；arbitrator；acrobat}
  \wordsense{a. 任意的，随意的；独断的；专横的}
  \wordphrase{arbitrary}
  \wordexample{arbitrarily独断地；任意地I hate my sergeant. His punishments are both harsh and arbitrary.（我很讨厌我的中士。他的处罚既严厉又专横。）}
  \wordexample{arbitrary}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{arbitrate}{['a:bitreit]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 BA[BET,BIT]}
  \wordsense{v.（通常指应当事双方的要求）仲裁，调停‘ar（=to),bit<bat（=go）}
  \wordfamily{arbitrary}
  \wordfamily{arbitration}
  \wordfamily{arbitrator}
  \wordfamily{acrobat}
  \wordphrase{arbitrate the dispute / between the employers and their workers（调停争论/劳资 纠纷）}
  \wordexample{arbitrate the dispute / between the employers and their workers（调停争论/劳资 纠纷）}
\end{word}

  \wordphrase{refer a wage dispute to arbitration（把工资纠纷提请仲裁）}
  \wordphrase{The Union agreed to go to arbitration（工会同意提请仲裁。）}
  \wordphrase{court of arbitration（仲裁法庭）}
  \wordexample{The Union agreed to go to arbitration.（工会同意提请仲裁。）}
  \wordexample{court of arbitration（仲裁法庭））}
\end{word}

\chapter{MAND, MEND}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根 MAND, MEND 来自拉丁语的 mand, mend，意为 to order，to entrust。同义词根有
  \item 来自拉丁语的ordin，意为to order。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[**]{mandatory}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MAND.MEND；［和命令有关mand（=order)】 ['maendatari]；mandatory（［和命令有关mand（=order)】 ['maendatari]}
  \wordfamily{commandeer；remand；command；commanding}
  \wordsense{a. 命令的；受委托的；强制的，义务的}
  \wordphrase{be mandatory}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of mandatory.（此处展示 mandatory 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 受托管理国，受托管理者}
  \wordphrase{mandatory during basic training}
  \wordexample{The hour of morning exercise is mandatory during basic training.（在基础训 练期间必须保证晨练的时间。）}
  \wordexample{mandatory during basic training}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{commandeer}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MAND.MEND；kman'da(r)}
  \wordfamily{mandatory；remand；command；commanding}
  \wordsense{vt. 征用；强征入伍；霸占}
  \wordphrase{commandeered a private vehicle}
  \wordexample{The FBI agent commandeered a private vehicle to chase the suspect.（联邦 调查局特工为追击嫌疑人征用了一辆私家车。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{commend}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{完全委托com（=intensive）+mend（=entrust,order)；ka'mend}
  \wordfamily{mandatory（命令的）；commandeer（征用）；remand（将（犯人）还押候审）}
  \wordsense{vt. sb+for+sth; sb+to+sb 称赞；赞赏；委托，托付}
  \wordphrase{commended my young son}
  \wordexample{With some trepidation, I commended my young son to the boarding school's care.（把我儿子托付给寄宿学校，我很担心。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}{remand}{ri'ma:nd}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MAND.MEND}
  \wordfamily{command；commandeer；commanding；mandatory}
  \wordsense{v. 将（犯人）还押候审；将（案件）发回原审法院重审}
  \wordphrase{a remand（还押）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "a remand".（常见搭配：还押。）}
  \wordsense{n. 还押；发回重审 *}
  \wordphrase{a remand（还押）}
  \wordexample{A common collocation is "a remand".（常见搭配：还押。）}
  \wordsense{n. 还押；发回重审}
  \wordphrase{remanded to his prison}
  \wordexample{After having testified, the prisoner was remanded to his prison cell.（罪犯在法庭上作证后被押回牢房候审。）}
  \wordexample{remanded to his prison}
  \wordexample{to remand（将（犯人）还押候审）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{command}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MAND.MEND}
  \wordfamily{remand；commanding；commander；commandeer；mandatory（命令的）}
  \wordsense{v. 命令；指挥，统率；控制；博得；俯瞰}
  \wordphrase{command the air/ sea（拥有制空/制海权）}
  \wordphrase{Great men command our respect（伟人 值得我们尊敬。）}
  \wordphrase{The hill commands a fine view（这座小山的俯瞰视野很好。）}
  \wordexample{Great men command our respect.（伟人 值得我们尊敬。）}
  \wordexample{The hill commands a fine view.（这座小山的俯瞰视野很好。）}
  \wordexample{I am at your command.（我服从你的命令。）}

  \wordphrase{to command（命令）}
  \wordexample{to command（命令）}
\end{word}

\end{word}

\chapter{CAUST, CAUT}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根 CAUST, CAUT 来自希腊语，意为 to burn。
  \item 同义词根有来自拉丁语的 flagr/flam、arid、torr 和来自希腊语的 ether。
  \item 此外还有来自盎格鲁-撒克逊语的 burn/bran。
\end{itemize}

  \wordphrase{caustic and can ruin}
  \wordexample{Battery acid is quite caustic and can ruin your clothes.（电池里的酸性物质腐 蚀性很强，会毁坏衣服。）}
  \wordexample{caustic and can ruin}
\end{word}

\chapter{THET, THES[THEC, THEM]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根 THET, THES[THEC, THEM] 来自希腊语，意为 to place, to put。
  \item thec、them 是其变形。
  \item 同义词根有来自拉丁语的 sert、loc、pos/post/pon/pound、stal 等和来自希腊语的 top。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{hypothetical}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{放在下面的hypo（=under）+thet（=put)；haipaBetikl}
  \wordfamily{synthetic；photosynthesis；thesis；antithesis}
  \wordsense{a. 假设的，假定的 hypothesis假设，假定?hypothesize假设，假定?A hypothetical example will illustrate how the costs will be allocated.（一个假定的例子将说明 如何分配那笔费用。）}
  \wordphrase{A hypothetical example will illustrate how the costs will be allocated（一个假定的例子将说明 如何分配那笔费用。）}
  \wordexample{A hypothetical example will illustrate how the costs will be allocated.（一个假定的例子将说明 如何分配那笔费用。）}
  \wordexample{hypothesis假设，假定?hypothesize假设，假定?A hypothetical example will illustrate how the costs will be allocated.（一个假定的例子将说明 如何分配那笔费用。））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{synthetic}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{放在一起的syn（=together）+thet（=put)；sin'etik}
  \wordfamily{hypothetical（假设的）；photosynthesis（光合作用）；thesis（论文）}
  \wordsense{a. 综合的；合成的，人造的；【语】综合型的}
  \wordphrase{be synthetic}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of synthetic.（此处展示 synthetic 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. size综合，合成；综合处理}
  \wordphrase{synthesis综合；合成synthesizer音响合成器，电子合成器synthe- size综合，合成；综合处理}
  \wordexample{She refuses to wear clothing made from synthetic fibers.（她拒绝穿合成纤维面料的衣服。）}
  \wordexample{synthesis综合；合成synthesizer音响合成器，电子合成器synthe- size综合，合成；综合处理}
\end{word}

  \wordphrase{the panegyric}
  \wordexample{Read this panegyric to Elton John on this website. It's great!（读一读这个网 站上关于埃尔顿·约翰的赞词。真是太棒了！）}
\end{word}



\chapter{ECO[ECU,OCE]}

\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{ecumenical}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 ECO[ECU,OCE]；【拉回家的ecu(eco（=house）+men（=lead）］ [ikju'menikl]；ecumenical（【拉回家的ecu(eco（=house）+men（=lead）］ [ikju'menikl]}

  \wordexample{Local ministers formed an ecumenical youth group that's been quite popular.（当地牧师们成立了一个颇具人气的全基督教青年团体。）}
\end{word}

\chapter{LITH}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根LITH 来自希腊语的lith意为stone。同义词根有来自拉丁语的lapid和来
  \item 自希腊语的petr。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{monolithic}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LITH；形成一块石头的mono（=one）+ith（=stone）】 [imonalioik}
  \wordfamily{neolithic；paleolithic；lithography}
  \wordsense{a. 整体的；坚如磐石的}
  \wordphrase{monolith独块巨石}
  \wordexample{monolith独块巨石（如obelisk（方尖碑）等）?John Wayne had a monolithic presence in movies that few current stars can match.（约翰·韦恩在电影中的表 演综合而全面，这是当今影星中很少有人可以匹敌的。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{neolithic}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LITH；inira'lrerk}
  \wordfamily{neolithicman（新石器时代的人）；monolithic（整体的）；paleolithic（旧石器时代的）}
  \wordsense{a. 新石器时代的 *neo (= new)}
  \wordphrase{be neolithic（新石器时代的）}
  \wordexample{be neolithic（新石器时代的）}
  \wordexample{neolithic（[inira'lrerk]）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{paleolithic}{ipalialloik}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LITH}
  \wordfamily{neolithic；lithography；monolithic}
  \wordsense{a. 旧石器时代的}

  \wordexample{be paleolithic（旧石器时代的‘paleo（=old））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{lithography}{[]}

  \wordsense{n. 【印】平版印刷（术）}

\chapter{NARC}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根NARC 来自希腊语的narc意为numbness，stupor。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{narcissistic}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 NARC；【麻痹、无感觉的narc（=numbness,stupor)] [narsr'sistik]；希神}
  \wordfamily{narcolepsy；narcotic；lithography（印】平版印刷（术））}
  \wordsense{a. 自我陶醉的；自恋的}
  \wordphrase{be narcissistic}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of narcissistic.（此处展示 narcissistic 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 那喀索斯；（n\textasciitilde{}）水仙花}
  \wordphrase{He's so narcissistic thathe could never love}
  \wordexample{narcissism/narcism自我陶醉；自恋narcissist自我陶醉者cf.Narcissus anotherperson.（他太自恋了，以至于根本不可能爱上别人。）}
  \wordexample{narcissism/narcism自我陶醉；自恋narcissist自我陶醉者cf.Narcissus 【希神】那喀索斯；（n\textasciitilde{}）水仙花}
  \wordexample{He's so narcissistic thathe could never love}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{narcolepsy}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 NARC}
  \wordfamily{narcissistic；narcotic；lithography（印】平版印刷（术））}
  \wordsense{n. 嗜睡症"lep（=take） 【'na:kalepsi]}
  \wordphrase{a narcolepsy（嗜睡症"lep（=take） 【'na:kalepsi]）}
  \wordexample{a narcolepsy（嗜睡症"lep（=take） 【'na:kalepsi]）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{narcotic}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 NARC}
  \wordfamily{narcolepsy；narcissistic；lithography（印】平版印刷（术））}
  \wordsense{a. 麻醉的；与毒品有关的}
  \wordphrase{Opium is a narcotic drug（鸦片是一种毒品。）}
  \wordphrase{Theuse of narcotics by teenagersisnowaprobleminSouthKorea（现在韩国十几岁的青少年使用麻药的现象 很成问题。）}
  \wordexample{Opium is a narcotic drug.（鸦片是一种毒品。）}
  \wordexample{Theuse of narcotics by teenagersisnowaprobleminSouthKorea.（现在韩国十几岁的青少年使用麻药的现象 很成问题。）}
  \wordsense{n. 麻醉剂；起麻痹作用的事物 [na:"kptik] narcotism（麻醉作用（或状态））narcotist（麻醉剂上瘾者；吸毒成瘾者）narcotine （那可汀）?Opium is a narcotic drug.（鸦片是一种毒品。）Theuse of narcotics by teenagersisnowaprobleminSouthKorea.（现在韩国十几岁的青少年使用麻药的现象 很成问题。）}
  \wordphrase{be narcotic（麻醉的）}
  \wordexample{be narcotic（麻醉的）}
\end{word}



\chapter{PRAG, PRACT}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根PRAG, PRACT 来自希腊语的prag，pract意为todo，business。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{pragmatic}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PRAG, PRACT；［不管什么都做的prag（=do,business）] [praeg'maetrk ]}
  \wordfamily{pragmatism（实用主义）；pragmatist（实用主义者）；practiceteacher（（本人是学生的）实习教师）；practical（a.实际的）；practicalEnglish（实用英语）；practicalknowledge（实用知识）；ideasthatappealtopracticalminds（讲求实用主义的 想法）；practice（n.实行）}
  \wordsense{a. 实际的；实用主义的；实干的}
  \wordphrase{puta plan intopractice（将计划付诸实施）}
  \wordexample{pragmatism实用主义pragmatist实用主义者Thepragmaticdecisionis toforgothevacationandstudy.（务实的决定是放弃休假去学习。）}
  \wordexample{The pragmatic decision is to forgo the vacation and study.（务实的决定是放弃休假去学习。）}
  \wordexample{puta plan intopractice（将计划付诸实施）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{practicable}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PRAG, PRACT}
  \wordsense{a.适用的；能实行的；可通行的}
  \wordfamily{['praektikab]]}
  \wordfamily{methodsthatarenot}
  \wordfamily{practicably（可实行地）}
  \wordfamily{practicability（可行性）}
  \wordfamily{practicable（不可行的方法）}
  \wordfamily{methodsthatarenot practicable（不可行的方法）}
  \wordphrase{mountain pass that is practicable only in summer（只能在 夏天通行的山路）}
  \wordexample{mountain pass that is practicable only in summer（只能在 夏天通行的山路）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{practitioner}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 PRAG, PRACT；praek'tifana(r)}
  \wordfamily{generalpractitioner（缩写：GP）（普通医生（相对于专科医生而言））；symptom（症状）；pragmatic（实际的）}
  \wordsense{n. 开业者（尤指医生、律师等）；从业者；实践者；（基督教科学派的）信仰疗法术士}
  \wordphrase{the practitioner}
  \wordexample{Severe chest pain can be symptomatic of a heart attack or just simple indigestion.（严重的胸痛可能是心脏病发作的征兆，也有 可能只是单纯的消化不良。）}
\end{word}



\chapter{PTO}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根PTO 来自希腊语的pto意为to fall。同义词根有来自拉丁语的cid /cad/
  \item cas和laps。
  \item symptomatic ［【一起掉下来的sym<syn（=together）+pto（=fall)]］
  \item [isimpta'maetik] a.征候的；（有）症状的
  \item symptom 症状；征候。
  \item Severe chest pain can be symptomatic of a heart attack or just simple indigestion.（严重的胸痛可能是心脏病发作的征兆，也有可能只是单纯的消化不良。）
\end{itemize}

\chapter{NOT}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根NOT 来自拉丁语的not意为to know。同义词根有来自拉丁语的gno,scia
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{notorious}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{广为人知的not（=know)；na'orias}
  \wordfamily{notoriety（恶名）；notable（值得注意的）；nota bene（常用义项待统一核对）}
  \wordsense{a. 声名狼藉的，臭名昭著的}
  \wordphrase{notorious for shoplifting and}
  \wordexample{His daughter is notorious for shoplifting and petty thievery. （他的女儿因从商店偷东西等小偷小摸而臭名昭著。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{notable}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{可以知道not（=know)；'natabl}
  \wordfamily{notably（显著地）；notorious（声名狼藉的）；nota bene（常用义项待统一核对）}
  \wordsense{a. 值得注意的；显著的；著名的}
  \wordphrase{be notable}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of notable.（此处展示 notable 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. 显要人物，名人}
  \wordphrase{notable citizens}
  \wordexample{Your grandfather was one of the most notable citizens of this town.（你的祖父曾是这个镇上最著名的市民之一。）}
  \wordexample{notably显著地，明显地Your grandfather was one of the most notable citizens of this town.（你的祖父曾是这个镇上最出 名的人之一。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{nota bene}{'nauta'biini}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 NOT；imper.留心，注意（缩写：N.B.）“bene（=good）}

  \wordsense{n. 常用义项待统一核对}
  \wordphrase{nota bene（imper.留心，注意（缩写：N.B.）“bene（=good））}
  \wordexample{nota bene（imper.留心，注意（缩写：N.B.）“bene（=good））}
\end{word}

  \wordexample{a notary（公证人）}

\begin{word}[*]{note}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 NOT}

  \wordsense{n. 留言，备忘录；笔记；（外交）照会；简短的信；注解，注释；注明；票据；纸币；音 【naut] 符；（钢琴的）键；记号，符号；语调}
  \wordphrase{the note}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of note.（此处展示 note 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. 记录，做笔记；注意；为（书等)加注释}
  \wordphrase{take notes of（记录，做笔记）}
  \wordexample{Please note my words. （请注意我的话。）}
  \wordexample{take notes of（记录，做笔记）}
  \wordexample{He noted down the main points of the lecture.（他把演讲的要点记录了下来。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{noted}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 NOT；'nautrd}
  \wordfamily{notorious（声名狼藉的）；notable（值得注意的）；nota bene（常用义项待统一核对）}
  \wordsense{a. 著名的}
  \wordphrase{be noted}
  \wordexample{town noted for its pottery/as a health resort（以陶器/疗养地而闻名的城市）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{notice}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 NOT}
  \wordfamily{leavewithoutnotice（没有预先通知（无端）地离开）；notorious（声名狼藉的）；notable（值得注意的）}
  \wordsense{n. 通知，布告，告示；警告；注意；（报刊上对图书、戏剧、电影等的）简介；短评}
  \wordphrase{the notice}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of notice.（此处展示 notice 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{v. 注意，留意；通知，通告；短评（图书、戏剧、电影等）}
  \wordphrase{send a notice （下通知）}
  \wordexample{noticeable（显而易见的；值得注意的）?put up a notice（贴告示）}
  \wordexample{send a notice （下通知）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{notify}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 NOT；'nautifa；【'naujn] notional（（知识等）纯理论的；概念的）}
  \wordfamily{notion（n.概念）；notorious（声名狼藉的）；notable（值得注意的）}
  \wordsense{v. 通知，通报，报告}
  \wordphrase{common notion（一般概念）}
  \wordexample{notify the police of a loss（将失窃一事报警）}
  \wordexample{have no notion of what he means（完全搞不懂他是什么意思）}
  \wordexample{notifiable（必须报告卫生当局的）?We have been notified that\textasciitilde{}（我们收到了…通知。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{annotate}{[]}

  \wordphrase{annotated edition/text（注释版/有注释的课文）}
  \wordexample{annotated edition/text（注释版/有注释的课文）}
  \wordexample{to annotate（（给...)加注解*an<ad(=to,add)）}

\begin{word}[*]{connote}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 NOT；ka'naut}

  \wordsense{v. 意味着；暗示*}

  \wordexample{The word “Tropics” means the area between about 23N and 23S; it connotes heat.（热带指的是北纬23度和南纬23度之间的区域，这意味着那 个地区很热。）}
  \wordexample{The word “Tropics” means the area between about 23N}
\end{word}

\chapter{VOR}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根VOR 来自拉丁语的vor意为to eat。同义词根有来自拉丁语的ed/es和
  \item 来自希腊语的phag。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{voracious}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{多吃的vor（=eat)；va'reifas}
  \wordfamily{voraciously（狼吞虎咽地）；omnivorous（什么都吃的）；devour（狼吞虎咽地吃）}
  \wordsense{a. 狼吞虎咽的，暴食的，贪食的；贪婪的，如饥似渴的}
  \wordphrase{voracious appetites}
  \wordexample{Our two teenage boys have voracious appetites.（我们那两个十来岁的儿子胃口极大。）}
  \wordexample{voraciously狼吞虎咽地；贪婪地Our two teenage boys have voracious appetites.（我们那两个十来岁的儿子胃口极大。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{omnivorous}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VOR；【不论什么都吃的omni（=all）+vor（=eat)] [pm'nivaras]}
  \wordfamily{devour；carnivorous；carnivore；insectivorous；voracious（狼吞虎咽的）}
  \wordsense{a. 什么都吃的，杂食的；什么都读的}
  \wordphrase{omnivorous curiosity His favorite}
  \wordexample{Our 2-year-old baby has an omnivorous curiosity. His favorite word is “Why"（我们两岁的宝宝对什么都好奇。他最喜欢的词就是“为什么”。）}
  \wordexample{omnivorous curiosity His favorite}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{devour}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VOR}
  \wordfamily{omnivorous；carnivorous；carnivore；insectivorous；voracious（狼吞虎咽的）}
  \wordsense{v. 狼吞虎咽地吃；吞噬；如饥似渴地看（或听、读）；吸引的全部注意力 [drtvaua(r)] 'de (= intensive), vour(vor (= eat)}
  \wordphrase{devoured his dinner}
  \wordexample{The hungry boy devoured his dinner.（那个男孩饿了，他狼吞虎咽地吃了晚饭。）}
  \wordexample{The fire devoured twenty square miles of forest.（火灾吞噬了20平方英里的森林。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{carnivorous}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VOR}
  \wordfamily{devour；carnivore；omnivorous；insectivorous；voracious（狼吞虎咽的）}
  \wordsense{a. 食肉（性）的*carn（=flesh） carnivorous animals（食肉动物）}
  \wordphrase{carnivorous animals（食肉动物）}
  \wordphrase{be carnivorous（食肉（性）的*carn（=flesh） [ka:'nivaras] carnivorous animals（食肉动物））}
  \wordexample{be carnivorous（食肉（性）的*carn（=flesh） [ka:'nivaras] carnivorous animals（食肉动物））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{carnivore}{"kanivo(r}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VOR}
  \wordfamily{carnivorous；insectivorous；devour；herbivorous；voracious（狼吞虎咽的）}
  \wordsense{n. 食肉动物}
  \wordphrase{a carnivore（食肉动物‘carn（=flesh））}
  \wordexample{a carnivore（食肉动物‘carn（=flesh））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{insectivorous}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VOR；insek'tivaras}
  \wordfamily{voracious（狼吞虎咽的）；omnivorous（什么都吃的）；devour（狼吞虎咽地吃）}
  \wordsense{a. 食虫的"in(=into),sect(=cut）}
  \wordphrase{be insectivorous}
  \wordexample{Swallows are insectivorous.（燕子是食虫的动物。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{herbivorous}{h3:'bivaras}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 VOR}
  \wordfamily{insectivorous；carnivore；carnivorous；devour；voracious（狼吞虎咽的）}
  \wordsense{a. （动物）食草的}

  \wordexample{be herbivorous（食草的‘herb（=grass））}
\end{word}



\chapter{DEXTR}

  \wordphrase{be dextrous / dexterous（灵巧的，敏捷的）}
  \wordexample{be dextrous / dexterous（灵巧的，敏捷的）}

\begin{word}[**]{cavalry}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 CAVAL[CHIVAL]；【"kavlri]}

\chapter{MAGN}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根MAGN 来自拉丁语的magn意为great。同义词根有来自拉丁语的grand，maj，
  \item max和来自希腊语的coloss，maha，mega/megalo，metro等。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{magnanimous}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{有宽大的心的 magn(= great)+ anim（=mind)；mag'naenimas}
  \wordfamily{magnanimity（宽宏大量）；magnate（要人）；magnet（磁石）}
  \wordsense{a. 宽宏大量的，有雅量的}
  \wordphrase{magnanimous when our ball}
  \wordexample{She couldn't have been more magnanimous when our ball broke her windows.（我们的球打破她的窗户时，她表现得再宽容不过了。）}
  \wordexample{magnanimity宽宏大量，雅量She couldn't have been more magnanimous when our ball broke her windows.（我们的球打破她的窗户时，她表现得宽容 极了。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{magnate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MAGN}
  \wordfamily{magnet；magnetic；magnetism；magnanimous（宽宏大量的）}
  \wordsense{n. 要人，有权势的人；（工商界的）寡头，巨头，大王ate（=personwho） territorial magnates（大地主）oil magnate（石油大王）}
  \wordphrase{territorial magnates（大地主）}
  \wordphrase{oil magnate（石油大王）}
  \wordphrase{a magnate（要人，有权势的人）}
  \wordexample{a magnate（要人，有权势的人）}
\end{word}

  \wordbreakdown{词根 MAGN}
  \wordfamily{magnate；magnetic；magnetism；magnanimous（宽宏大量的）}
  \wordsense{n. 磁石，磁铁；有吸引力的人（或物） bar magnet（条形的磁铁）U/horseshoe magnet（U形/马蹄形磁铁）natural/ permanent magnet（天然磁铁）}
  \wordphrase{bar magnet（条形的磁铁）}
  \wordphrase{U/horseshoe magnet（U形/马蹄形磁铁）}
  \wordphrase{natural/ permanent magnet（天然磁铁）}
  \wordexample{a magnet（磁石，磁铁）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{magnetic}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 MAGN}

  \wordphrase{magnetic field（磁场）}
  \wordphrase{magnetic needle（磁针）}
  \wordphrase{magnetic tape（磁带）}

\end{word}

\chapter{GRAT, GRAC}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根GRAT, GRAC 来自拉丁语的grat意为thankful，pleasing等。grac是它的变形。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{gratuitous}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{作为感谢而做的grat(=thank,pleasing)；gra'tjuitas}
  \wordfamily{ingratitude；ingratiate；gratify；congratulate}
  \wordsense{a. 免费的，无报酬的；没有理由的}
  \wordphrase{gratuity遣散费}
  \wordexample{gratuity遣散费；（军人的）退役金Your gratuitous cruelty is abominable. I've had enough!（你那无端的残忍实在令人讨厌。我已经受够了！）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{ingratitude}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{不感谢 in(= not)+ grat（= thank, pleasing, favor)；in'graetitjuid}
  \wordfamily{ingrate（忘恩负义者）；gratuitous（免费的）；ingratiate（［～oneself]使满意）}
  \wordsense{n. 忘恩负义；不感恩图报}
  \wordphrase{the ingratitude}
  \wordexample{Because of her past ingratitude, I won't be giving her any more gifts.（因为她过去不知感恩，所以我不会送给她礼物了。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{ingratiate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{使对方感到好感in（=in）+grat（=thank,pleasing,favor)；in'greiiet}
  \wordfamily{ingratitude；gratify；gratuitous；congratulate}
  \wordsense{vt. ［～oneself]使满意，讨好，迎合}
  \wordphrase{Despite my lack of}
  \wordexample{Despite my lack of credentials, I ingratiated myself with the hiring manager. （虽然我不够资格，但我逢迎了招聘主管，使其对我满意。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{gratify}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{使高兴grat（=pleasing.thank)；'graetifa}
  \wordfamily{gratuitous（免费的）；ingratitude（忘恩负义）；ingratiate（［～oneself]使满意）}
  \wordsense{v. 使高兴，使满意}
  \wordphrase{gratifies me most is}
  \wordexample{What gratifies me most is that I got admitted without any help from my father. （最让我最高兴的是我没有借助爸爸的任何帮助就被录取了。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{congratulate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 GRAT, GRAC；kan 'graetfuleit}
  \wordfamily{congratulatory（祝贺的）；gratuitous（免费的）；ingratitude（忘恩负义）}
  \wordsense{v. 祝贺*con(com (= together)}
  \wordphrase{congratulatory letter/telegram（贺信/贺电）}
  \wordphrase{to congratulate（祝贺*con(com (= together)）}

  \item 来自希腊语的ech。

  \wordsense{n. 悦耳之音，和谐音}
  \wordphrase{the cacophonous}

\chapter{LUMIN[LUN]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根 LUMIN[LUN] 来自拉丁语，意为 light。
  \item lun 是它的变形。
  \item 同义词根有来自拉丁语的 luc/lux/lust、来自希腊语的 photo 以及来自盎格鲁-撒克逊语的 light。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[*]{luminous}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{充满光的、明白的lumin（=light】；uminas}
  \wordfamily{luminary（发光体）；illuminate（照亮）；illumination（照明）}
  \wordsense{a. 发光的，明亮的；清楚的，易懂的}
  \wordphrase{luminous sea creatures}
  \wordexample{We saw several luminous sea creatures on our deep dive.（我们深度潜水时看到了几个发光的海底生物。）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{luminary}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LUMIN[LUN]}
  \wordfamily{illuminate；illumination；lunatic；lunacy；luminous（发光的）}
  \wordsense{n. 发光体，天体（如太阳、月亮、星星等）；（在学识、道德或精神方面有影响力的） 杰出人物；才智出众的人}
  \wordphrase{a luminary（发光体，天体（如太阳、月亮、星星等））}
  \wordexample{a luminary（发光体，天体（如太阳、月亮、星星等））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[**]{illuminate}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LUMIN[LUN]}
  \wordfamily{luminary；illumination；lunatic；lunacy；luminous（发光的）}
  \wordsense{v. 照亮，照明；（作为祝贺的表示，在街道上）用灯装饰；阑明；启发；用金色、银色、 ["'luminet] 红色或图案等装饰（起首字母或手稿）i<in（=into） street illuminated by oil lamps（用油灯照明的街道）poorly illuminated rooms（照明 不好的房间）illuminate a difficult passage in a book（阐明书中令人费解的一段）}
  \wordphrase{street illuminated by oil lamps（用油灯照明的街道）}
  \wordphrase{poorly illuminated rooms（照明 不好的房间）}
  \wordphrase{illuminate a difficult passage in a book（阐明书中令人费解的一段）}
  \wordexample{illuminate a difficult passage in a book（阐明书中令人费解的一段）}
  \wordexample{to illuminate（照亮，照明）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{illumination}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 LUMIN[LUN]}
  \wordfamily{illuminate；lunatic；luminary；lunacy；luminous（发光的）}
  \wordsense{n. 照明，照亮；灯饰；（用金色或鲜明的色彩、图案）装饰（起首字母、手稿等）}
  \wordphrase{an illumination（照明，照亮）}
  \wordexample{an illumination（照明，照亮）}

  \wordbreakdown{词根 LUMIN[LUN]；lu:natk}

  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of lunatic.（此处展示 lunatic 的常见用法。）}

\chapter{MELL, MEL[MIL, MOL, MAR]}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根 MELL, MEL[MIL, MOL, MAR] 来自拉丁语，意为 honey。
  \item mil、mol、mar 是它们的变形。
\end{itemize}

  \wordbreakdown{词根 MELL, MEL[MIL, MOL, MAR]；me'lifluas}

  \wordphrase{mellifluous monotony}
  \wordexample{The mellifluous monotony of train wheels on tracks soothes me.（火车车轮碰到轨道发出的柔和而单调的声音让我感到平静。）}
  \wordexample{mellifluous monotony}
\end{word}

\begin{itemize}

  \item （男子）的andr/mascul/vir，以及意为woman（女子）的femin/gyn/virgo。
\end{itemize}

\end{word}

\begin{word}[*]{rationalize}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 RAT；【'raeJnalaiz]}
  \wordfamily{rationalization（合理化）；ratify（批准）；rate（率）}
  \wordsense{v. 使合理；合理地解释}
  \wordphrase{rationalize his fears/behavior（合理解释他的不安/行为）}
  \wordexample{rationalize his fears/behavior（合理解释他的不安/行为）}
\end{word}



\chapter{JUST}

\begin{itemize}
  \item 词根JUST 来自拉丁语的词根just意为right。同义词根有来自希腊语的orth（o）。
\end{itemize}

\begin{word}[***]{justify}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{使正确、正当just（=right】；'d3Astifa}
  \wordfamily{justice（正义）；injustice（不公正）；unjust（不公正的）}
  \wordsense{v. 证明正当；为……辩护}
  \wordphrase{justify the issue}
  \wordexample{This entry shows a common use of justify.（此处展示 justify 的常见用法。）}
  \wordsense{n. justification证明为正当；辩护}
  \wordphrase{justify space exploration when}
  \wordexample{justifiable可证明为正当的；可辩护的?How can we justify space exploration when starvation is rampant on Earth?（地球上 到处饥饿横行，怎么能说这时候进行宇宙探险是正当的呢？）}
  \wordexample{justify space exploration when}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{justice}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUST；'d3Astis}
  \wordfamily{justify（证明正当）；injustice（不公正）；unjust（不公正的）}
  \wordsense{n. 正义；公平；司法，裁判；法官}
  \wordphrase{Department of Justice（《美》司法部（部长是Attorney General））}
  \wordexample{Department of Justice（《美》司法部（部长是Attorney General））}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{injustice}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUST}
  \wordfamily{justice；unjust；unjustifiable；adjust；justify（证明正当）}
  \wordsense{n. 不公正；不公平‘in（=not） ?remedy injustice（消除不公正）}
  \wordphrase{remedy injustice（消除不公正）}
  \wordphrase{an injustice（不公正）}
  \wordexample{an injustice（不公正）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{unjust}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUST；An'd3Ast}
  \wordfamily{injustice；unjustifiable；justice；adjust；justify（证明正当）}
  \wordsense{a. 不公正的；不公平的；非正义的*}
  \wordphrase{unjust enrichment（不正当的发财）}
  \wordphrase{the just and the unjust（（无论正直与否的）所有人）}
  \wordexample{unjust enrichment（不正当的发财）}
  \wordexample{the just and the unjust（（无论正直与否的）所有人）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{unjustifiable}{An'd3Astifarabi}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUST}
  \wordfamily{unjust；adjust；injustice；adjustment；justify（证明正当）}
  \wordsense{a. 不合理的；无法辩护的*}
  \wordphrase{be unjustifiable（不合理的）}
  \wordexample{be unjustifiable（不合理的）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{adjust}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUST}
  \wordfamily{unjustifiable；adjustment；unjust；maladjusted；justify（证明正当）}
  \wordsense{v. 调整，调节；改变以适应；校准‘ad（=to） adjustable（可以调节的）well-adjusted（完全适应环境的）well-adjusted child（完全 适应环境的孩子）adjuster（（要求赔偿损失时）负责调整赔偿金额的理算员）}
  \wordphrase{well-adjusted（完全适应环境的）}
  \wordphrase{well-adjusted child（完全 适应环境的孩子）}
  \wordphrase{to adjust（调整，调节）}
  \wordexample{to adjust（调整，调节）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[***]{adjustment}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUST；a'd3Astmant}
  \wordfamily{adjust；maladjusted；unjustifiable；unjust；justify（证明正当）}
  \wordsense{n. 调节；调解；调整 ‘ad(=to)}
  \wordphrase{adjustment board（调解委员会）}
  \wordexample{adjustment board（调解委员会）}
  \wordexample{an adjustment（调节）}
\end{word}



\begin{word}[*]{maladjusted}{[]}
  \wordbreakdown{词根 JUST}
  \wordfamily{adjustment；adjust；unjustifiable；unjust；justify（证明正当）}
  \wordsense{a. 失调的；（尤指人）无法适应（环境等）的 ‘mal(=bad) maladjustment（失调；不适应）}
  \wordphrase{be maladjusted（失调的）}
  \wordexample{be maladjusted（失调的）}
\end{word}
